  net_generation_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total net electrical energy in megawatt-hours (MWh) delivered by the unit to the grid during the reporting period, after deducting station service and auxiliary loads.']
  net_maximum_capacity_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'The maximum net electrical output in megawatts (MW) that the unit can sustain over a specified period under normal operating conditions, accounting for station service loads.']
  number_of_forced_outages varchar [note: 'The total count of discrete forced outage events during the reporting period. Used to calculate forced outage frequency metrics.']
  number_of_starts varchar [note: 'The total count of unit synchronization events during the reporting period, including both successful and failed start attempts.']
  period_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of hours in the reporting period. Typically 720 hours for a 30-day month or 744 hours for a 31-day month. Used as the denominator for availability calculations.']
  planned_outage_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of hours the unit was unavailable due to scheduled maintenance, inspections, or overhauls that were planned in advance and approved by system operations.']
  primary_fuel_type varchar [note: 'The predominant fuel source used by the generating unit during the reporting period. Used for fuel-specific performance benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coal|natural_gas|nuclear|hydro|wind|solar|oil|biomass|geothermal|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  report_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this GADS report was reviewed and approved by authorized personnel before submission to NERC.']
  report_approver_name varchar [note: 'The name of the authorized individual who approved this GADS report for submission to NERC. Used for internal compliance audit trail.']
  report_preparer_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the individual who prepared this GADS report. Used for follow-up questions and validation issue resolution.']
  report_preparer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual who prepared this GADS report. Used for internal audit trail and quality assurance purposes.']
  report_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which this GADS report was submitted to NERC. Used to track compliance with NERC submission deadlines.']
  reporting_entity_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the utility or generation owner responsible for submitting this GADS report to NERC. May differ from the operating entity.']
  reporting_entity_nerc_code varchar [note: 'The unique NERC-assigned identifier for the reporting entity. Used to aggregate performance data across multiple generating units owned by the same entity.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the monthly reporting period covered by this GADS report. Typically the last day of the calendar month.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the monthly reporting period covered by this GADS report. Typically the first day of the calendar month.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'The date the generating unit was permanently retired from service. Null for active units. Determines the end of GADS reporting obligations.']
  service_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of hours during the reporting period that the unit was in active service and synchronized to the grid, regardless of output level.']
  starting_reliability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of attempted unit starts that were successful. Calculated as Successful Starts / Total Attempted Starts * 100. Applicable primarily to peaking and cycling units.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'The current status of this GADS report in the NERC submission and validation workflow. Tracks compliance with regulatory reporting requirements.']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'The primary technology classification of the generating unit as defined by NERC GADS taxonomy. Determines applicable performance metrics and cause codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fossil_steam|combined_cycle|combustion_turbine|nuclear|hydro_conventional|hydro_pumped_storage|wind|solar_pv|solar_thermal|biomass|geothermal — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  validation_error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error or warning messages returned by NERC validation logic. Provides guidance for correcting data quality issues before resubmission.']

  Note: 'NERC Generating Availability Data System (GADS) monthly performance report records submitted for each generating unit. Captures reporting period, equivalent availability factor (EAF), equivalent forced outage rate (EFOR), equivalent planned outage factor (EPOF), net generation (MWh), capacity factor (%), total outage hours by cause code, and NERC submission status. SSOT for NERC GADS compliance and generation fleet benchmarking.'
}

Table generation.fuel_contract {
  fuel_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel supply contract record. Primary key.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Fuel contracts generate AP invoices for payment processing. Required for three-way match (contract-receipt-invoice), accrual accounting, and fuel cost reconciliation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fuel contracts are assigned to cost centers for budget tracking and fuel budget variance reporting. Standard in utility fuel management.']
  fuel_power_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the generation facility or plant that this fuel contract serves.']
  fuel_price_assumption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.fuel_price_assumption. Business justification: Fuel contracts are negotiated and evaluated against fuel price forecasts for procurement strategy and hedging decisions. Contract pricing formulas reference forecast assumptions. Essential for fuel co']
  fuel_supplies_power_plant varchar [note: 'FK to generation.power_plant.power_plant_id — Fuel contracts are typically negotiated for delivery to specific plant locations. Without this FK, fuel supply planning cannot associate contracts with the plants they serve.']
  hedge_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.hedge_strategy. Business justification: Fuel procurement contracts are integrated into hedge strategies for spark spread hedging. Essential for coordinated fuel and power hedging programs in generation portfolio management.']
  power_plant_id varchar [note: 'FK to generation.power_plant.power_plant_id — Fuel supply contracts are typically executed for specific generation facilities (delivery point is plant-level). power_plant_id links contracts to the plants they supply.']
  primary_fuel_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel supplier in the vendor master system.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Fuel contracts generate purchase orders for scheduled deliveries. Procurement execution requires linking contracts to POs for delivery tracking, invoice verification, and contract compliance monitorin']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Major fuel contracts require FERC/PUC approval and inclusion in rate cases for prudence review and cost recovery. Essential for linking fuel procurement decisions to regulatory approval processes and ']
  base_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base or fixed price per unit of fuel as specified in the contract, expressed in the currency defined by price_currency_code.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL to the executed contract document and amendments.']
  contract_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the contract manager for operational communications regarding this fuel supply agreement.']
  contract_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the utility employee responsible for managing and administering this fuel supply contract.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the fuel supply contract for business reference and reporting.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the fuel supply contract, used in procurement systems and supplier communications.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fuel supply contract indicating its operational state.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the contract term expressed in months from effective start to end date.']
  contract_value_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated monetary value of the contract over its full term, expressed in price_currency_code. Used for CAPEX and budget planning.']
  contracted_volume_annual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual volume of fuel contracted for delivery, expressed in the unit specified by volume_unit_of_measure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fuel contract record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_point_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the fuel delivery point including street, city, state, and postal code.']
  delivery_point_name varchar [note: 'Named location or facility where fuel is delivered under the contract (e.g., plant gate, pipeline interconnect, rail siding).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the fuel supply contract expires or terminates, ending delivery obligations. Nullable for evergreen contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the fuel supply contract becomes legally binding and fuel delivery obligations commence.']
  environmental_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes on environmental compliance requirements related to this fuel supply, including emissions standards, sulfur limits, or renewable content mandates.']
  escalation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual percentage rate for price escalation or inflation adjustment built into the contract pricing terms.']
  force_majeure_provision varchar [note: 'Description of force majeure events and remedies that excuse non-performance under the contract (e.g., natural disasters, strikes, regulatory changes).']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Type of fuel covered by this supply contract, aligned with generation facility fuel requirements.']
  heat_content_mmbtu_per_unit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Guaranteed or average heat content of the fuel expressed in MMBtu per unit (ton, barrel, cubic meter) for heat rate and LCOE calculations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fuel contract record was last updated or modified.']
  maximum_take_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum volume of fuel that can be purchased annually under contract terms, expressed in volume_unit_of_measure.']
  minimum_take_obligation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum volume of fuel that must be purchased annually under take-or-pay provisions, expressed in volume_unit_of_measure.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this fuel contract record.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Approved method of payment to the supplier for fuel deliveries under this contract.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due to the supplier.']
  price_adjustment_formula varchar [note: 'Mathematical formula or description of how contract price adjusts based on indices, escalation factors, or other variables.']
  price_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract.']
  price_index_reference varchar [note: 'Name of the pricing index or benchmark used for index-based contracts (e.g., Henry Hub, NYMEX Natural Gas, API2 Coal Index).']
  price_structure_type varchar [note: 'Pricing mechanism used in the contract: fixed price, index-linked (e.g., Henry Hub, NYMEX), formula-based, hybrid, or negotiated per delivery.']
  quality_specification varchar [note: 'Technical specifications for fuel quality including heat content, sulfur content, ash content, moisture, or other relevant parameters.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory approval was granted for this fuel contract, if applicable.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fuel contract requires approval from state PUC or FERC for cost recovery in rate base.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to exercise or decline contract renewal option.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Type of renewal provision in the contract specifying how the agreement may be extended beyond the initial term.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Conditions and notice requirements under which either party may terminate the contract prior to expiration.']
  transportation_arrangement varchar [note: 'Party responsible for arranging and paying for fuel transportation from supplier to delivery point.']
  transportation_cost_responsibility varchar [note: 'Party responsible for bearing the cost of fuel transportation under contract terms.']
  volume_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for contracted fuel volume (MMBtu for gas, tons for coal/biomass, barrels for oil, cubic meters for gas, MWh thermal for nuclear).']

  Note: 'Master records for fuel supply contracts covering natural gas, coal, nuclear fuel, oil, and biomass procurement for generation facilities. Captures fuel type, supplier name, contract term, contracted volume (MMBtu/year or tons/year), price terms (fixed, index, formula), delivery point, transportation arrangement, force majeure provisions, minimum take obligation, and contract status. Supports fuel supply security and generation cost planning.'
}

Table generation.plant_vendor_agreement {
  plant_vendor_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this plant-vendor agreement record. Primary key.']
  power_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the power generation facility that is party to this vendor agreement.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor that is party to this plant-specific agreement.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this plant-vendor agreement. Active = in force, Pending = negotiated but not yet effective, Expired = past end date, Terminated = ended early, Suspended = temporarily inactive.']
  annual_spend_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual procurement spend in USD for this plant-vendor relationship. Used for vendor performance analysis and strategic sourcing decisions.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration or termination date of the plant-specific vendor contract. Used for contract renewal tracking.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the plant-specific vendor contract or service agreement.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary service or product category covered by this plant-vendor agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this plant-vendor agreement record was created.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor has preferred status for this specific plant. A vendor may be preferred at one plant but not another based on local performance, proximity, or specialized capabilities.']
  response_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Contractually committed response time in hours for emergency service calls or critical supply requests at this plant. Varies by plant criticality and vendor capability.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Description or reference to the service level agreement terms specific to this plant-vendor relationship. May include uptime guarantees, quality standards, or delivery commitments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this plant-vendor agreement record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual relationship between a power generation facility and its suppliers/service vendors. It captures plant-specific procurement contracts, service level agreements, and vendor performance tracking. Each record links one power plant to one vendor with commercial terms, service commitments, and spend tracking that exist only in the context of this specific plant-vendor relationship.. Existence Justification: Power plants maintain contractual relationships with multiple vendors for different services (fuel supply, parts, maintenance, emergency response), and vendors serve multiple generation facilities across the utility\'s portfolio. Each plant-vendor relationship has its own contract terms, service level agreements, response time commitments, and spend tracking that vary by plant criticality, location, and vendor capability. This is an operational relationship actively managed by plant operators and procurement teams.'
}

Table generation.unit_crew_assignment {
  unit_crew_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for unit_crew_assignment']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the unit crew assignment. Primary key.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the crew assigned to the unit']
  generating_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the generating unit being staffed']
  assignment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for this assignment when crew is assigned to multiple units. Used for dispatch decisions during emergencies or outages.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the assignment (active, planned, suspended, completed). Determines crew availability and scheduling.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'Required or actual certification level for this crew assignment to this unit type. Used for NERC personnel qualification tracking and work authorization.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the generation supervisor or workforce planner who created the assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was created in the system.']
  crew_role varchar [note: 'Role or function the crew performs for this generating unit (e.g., lead operator, auxiliary operator, maintenance crew, outage crew). Determines work authorization and NERC qualification requirements.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the crew assignment to the generating unit ends. Null for ongoing assignments. Used for rotation tracking and historical analysis.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of person who last modified the assignment record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of most recent modification to the assignment record.']
  last_qualification_date date [note: 'Date of most recent NERC qualification verification or training for this crew on this unit type. Used for compliance tracking and requalification scheduling.']
  nerc_qualification_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether NERC personnel qualification requirements have been verified for this crew-unit assignment. Required before crew can operate the unit.']
  next_qualification_due_date date [note: 'Date when NERC requalification is due for this crew-unit assignment. Triggers training scheduling and compliance alerts.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Shift rotation pattern for this assignment (e.g., 12-hour rotating, 8-hour fixed, day shift, night shift). Critical for 24/7 generation operations staffing.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the crew assignment to the generating unit begins. Used for shift scheduling and outage planning.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of field crews to generating units for operations, maintenance, and outage work. It captures the assignment lifecycle, crew role, shift pattern, and certification requirements for NERC personnel qualification tracking. Each record links one generating unit to one crew with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment relationship, supporting shift scheduling, outage planning, and regulatory compliance reporting.. Existence Justification: In energy utilities generation operations, generating units require multiple specialized crews rotating across shifts for 24/7 operations, planned maintenance, and outage execution. A single generating unit has multiple crews assigned simultaneously (e.g., day shift operators, night shift operators, maintenance crew, outage crew), and each crew is qualified to work on multiple generating units within the fleet. The business actively manages these assignments with specific roles, shift patterns, and NERC qualification tracking.'
}

Table generation.unit_certification_requirement {
  unit_certification_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this unit-certification requirement record. Primary key.']
  certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the certification that is required for this generating unit']
  generating_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the generating unit for which this certification is required']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this unit-certification requirement record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this certification requirement became or will become effective for this generating unit. Used to track regulatory changes and phase-in periods for new requirements.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this certification requirement expires or is superseded for this generating unit. Null if the requirement is ongoing. Used when unit modifications or regulatory changes eliminate a requirement.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last updated this requirement record.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this requirement record.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is absolutely mandatory (regulatory requirement) or recommended (best practice) for personnel working with this generating unit in the specified role.']
  nerc_cip_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification requirement is driven by NERC CIP (Critical Infrastructure Protection) standards for cyber security and physical access to bulk electric system assets. True when the generating unit is classified as a BES Cyber Asset and personnel require CIP-specific clearances.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulation, NERC standard, OSHA rule, or state PUC order that mandates this certification for this unit type or voltage class. Provides traceability for compliance audits.']
  required_for_role varchar [note: 'The operational role or job function for which this certification is required when working with this specific generating unit. Determines which personnel must hold this certification based on their interaction with the unit.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this certification requirement. ACTIVE = currently enforced, PENDING = future requirement, SUPERSEDED = replaced by newer requirement, WAIVED = exception granted, GRANDFATHERED = existing personnel exempt.']
  unit_type_applicability varchar [note: 'The generating unit technology types for which this certification is required. Comma-separated list (e.g., \'CCGT,COAL,NUCLEAR\' or \'ALL\'). Different unit types require different operator qualifications and safety certifications.']
  voltage_class_applicability varchar [note: 'The voltage class range for which this certification requirement applies. Certain certifications (e.g., high voltage safety, arc flash) are only required when the generating unit operates at or above specific voltage thresholds.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this requirement record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the regulatory and operational certification requirements between generating units and workforce certifications. It captures which certifications are mandatory for personnel operating, maintaining, or accessing specific generating units based on unit type, voltage class, fuel type, and NERC CIP classification. Each record links one generating unit to one certification with attributes that define the requirement context, applicability rules, and compliance tracking metadata.. Existence Justification: In energy utility operations, generating units require multiple certifications based on unit characteristics (unit type, voltage class, fuel type, NERC CIP classification), and each certification applies to multiple generating units across the fleet. The business actively manages these requirements through safety compliance and workforce qualification programs, tracking which certifications are mandatory for which unit types, voltage classes, and operational roles. This is an operational regulatory compliance relationship, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table generation.facility_audit_scope {
  facility_audit_scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this facility-audit scope record. Primary key.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance audit under which this facility is being examined']
  power_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the generation facility included in the audit scope']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date when audit activities were conducted at this specific facility (site visit, inspection, or evidence review). May differ from overall audit start/end dates when audit covers multiple facilities over time.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Current closure status for this facility\'s portion of the audit. Tracks whether findings for this specific facility have been addressed and closed by the auditing body. Status can vary by facility within a multi-facility audit.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical or severe findings identified specifically for this facility during this audit.']
  facility_closure_date date [note: 'Date when all findings and corrective actions for this specific facility were closed by the auditing body. May differ from overall audit closure date when facilities close at different times.']
  facility_coordinator_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the facility audit coordinator for this facility-audit scope.']
  facility_coordinator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the utility employee at this facility who coordinated audit activities, provided access, and gathered evidence for this facility\'s portion of the audit.']
  facility_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed specifically for violations identified at this facility during this audit. Penalties are often assessed per facility based on facility-specific violations.']
  findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings, violations, or observations identified specifically for this facility during this audit. Derived from child finding records but denormalized for reporting.']
  included_in_scope_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this facility was ultimately included in the final audit scope. Facilities may be initially listed but excluded during scoping phase.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the auditor from the auditing body who led the audit activities at this specific facility. May differ from the overall audit lead auditor when audit teams are divided across multiple facilities.']
  scope_added_date date [note: 'Date when this facility was added to the audit scope. Tracks when the facility-audit relationship was established.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what aspects of this specific facility are included in the audit scope (e.g., \'NERC CIP-002 through CIP-009 compliance for critical cyber assets\', \'EPA Title V air emissions permit compliance\', \'Generation unit 1 and 2 only\'). Scope varies by facility even within the same audit.']
  scope_removed_date date [note: 'Date when this facility was removed from the audit scope, if applicable. Null if facility remains in scope.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the audit scope relationship between power_plant and audit. It captures the specific inclusion of a generation facility within a compliance audit\'s scope, including facility-specific findings, audit activities, and closure status. Each record links one power_plant to one audit with attributes that exist only in the context of this facility being audited.. Existence Justification: In energy utilities compliance operations, audits routinely cover multiple generation facilities (a NERC CIP audit may examine 10+ plants, an EPA inspection may cover all coal plants in a region), and each facility undergoes multiple audits over time (NERC reliability, FERC market behavior, state PUC, EPA environmental). The business actively manages facility-specific audit scope, tracks findings per facility, assigns facility coordinators, and closes findings independently per facility even within the same audit.'
}

Table generation.plant_crew_assignment {
  plant_crew_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the plant-crew assignment record. Primary key.']
  assigned_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plant manager or operations supervisor who authorized this crew assignment. Used for audit trail and assignment approval workflow.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the field crew assigned to the generation facility']
  power_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the generation facility where the crew is assigned']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the crew assignment to the generation facility ended or is planned to end. Null for ongoing assignments. Critical for tracking temporary assignments during outages or mutual aid deployments.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about the assignment including special instructions, temporary restrictions, or coordination requirements with other crews.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the crew assignment to the generation facility became effective. Used for resource planning and historical tracking of crew deployments.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the crew assignment. Active assignments are used for dispatch and cost allocation. Suspended assignments indicate temporary unavailability (e.g., crew deployed elsewhere).']
  cost_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of crew labor costs allocated to this plant assignment. Used when a crew is split across multiple facilities. Sum of percentages across all assignments for a crew should equal 100%.']
  crew_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the crew function for this specific assignment. A crew may serve different functions at different plants. Determines work authorization, safety protocols, and regulatory compliance requirements.']
  emergency_response_role varchar [note: 'Defines the crew\'s role in emergency response scenarios at this facility. Critical for NERC CIP compliance, emergency action plan (EAP) execution, and mutual aid coordination during grid emergencies.']
  nerc_cip_compliance_verified_date date [note: 'Date when crew members\' NERC CIP personnel risk assessments were verified for this specific facility. Required for crews working in CIP-designated critical cyber assets or physical security perimeters.']
  primary_assignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the crew\'s primary plant assignment (true) or a secondary/backup assignment (false). Primary assignments determine home base for crew scheduling and cost allocation.']
  work_authorization_level varchar [note: 'Level of access and work authorization the crew has at this specific facility. Varies by NERC CIP security zone, plant type, and crew certification level. Determines which work orders can be assigned.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment between power_plant and crew. It captures the operational assignment of field crews to generation facilities for operations, maintenance, environmental compliance, security, and emergency response work. Each record links one power_plant to one crew with attributes that define the assignment period, crew function, assignment priority, and emergency response role. Critical for resource planning, mutual aid coordination, regulatory staffing compliance (NERC CIP personnel requirements), and outage work scheduling.. Existence Justification: In energy utilities generation operations, power plants require multiple specialized crews assigned simultaneously for different functions (operations, maintenance, environmental compliance, security, outage work). Crews are routinely assigned to multiple plants within a generation fleet, particularly in regions where plants are geographically clustered. The business actively manages these assignments with specific start/end dates, primary vs. backup designations, emergency response roles, and NERC CIP compliance tracking.'
}

Table customer.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the utility customer account record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for all downstream joins across billing, metering, and demand response domains.']
  account_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master customer (business partner / person) record that owns this account. Links the account to the customer identity, contact, and demographic data in the Customer domain SSOT.']
  billing_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Account should reference address for billing address normalization. However, examining the account attributes, I do NOT see embedded address fields (no address_line_1, city, state, etc. visible in the']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Customer accounts are assigned to control areas for energy settlement, interchange accounting, regulatory reporting, and balancing authority cost allocation. Required for billing and compliance operat']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Utility accounts are assigned to cost centers for internal cost allocation, segment profitability analysis, and management accounting. Essential for customer segment P&L reporting and regulatory cost-']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent account_id for consolidated billing hierarchies and corporate account structures. Null for top-level or standalone accounts. Enables traversal of multi-level corporate hierarchies for enterprise billing and reporting.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary service premise (physical location) associated with this account. A premise represents the physical address where electricity is delivered. Accounts may span multiple premises in consolidated billing scenarios.']
  primary_service_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Accounts track primary service delivery asset (transformer, service vault) for billing context, capacity planning, and outage impact analysis. Critical for customer-asset operational linkage in utilit']
  to_profile_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.profile.profile_id — Every account must reference the customer profile (person or organization) that owns it. This is the most fundamental FK in any CIS — account.profile_id → profile.profile_id. Without this, you cannot ']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Grouping classification that determines billing consolidation and hierarchy rules. Consolidated billing groups multiple premises under one bill; corporate parent denotes the apex of a corporate hierarchy; municipal aggregation and Community Choice Aggregation (CCA) denote aggregated load programs; franchise denotes accounts under a specific franchise territory agreement.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known utility account number as assigned by the Customer Information System (CIS). This is the number printed on customer bills and used in all customer-facing communications. Sourced from SAP IS-U FKKVK or Oracle CC&B ACCOUNT table.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the utility account. Governs service eligibility, billing generation, and collections processing. Transitions include: pending_activation (new account awaiting energization), active (energized and billed), suspended (temporarily halted per tariff or non-payment), disconnected (service terminated), written_off (uncollectable balance), reactivated (restored after disconnection).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the utility account by customer segment. Drives rate eligibility, billing rules, and regulatory reporting. Residential covers single-family and multi-family dwellings; commercial covers businesses; industrial covers large-load manufacturing and processing facilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|commercial|industrial|municipal|agricultural|street_lighting|transportation|government — promote to reference product]']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the account was first activated and service was energized. Represents the precise business event time of account creation and service commencement. Used for SLA tracking of new service connections.']
  ami_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account has an Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) smart meter installed, enabling automated interval reads, remote connect/disconnect, and real-time usage data. Drives eligibility for TOU rates, demand response programs, and enhanced billing analytics.']
  autopay_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in automatic payment (AutoPay), where the bill amount is automatically debited from the customer\'s bank account or charged to a payment card on the due date. Reduces collections risk and improves cash flow.']
  billing_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the billing cycle (meter reading and bill generation schedule) assigned to this account. Determines the monthly read date and bill due date. Managed in SAP IS-U Billing Order or Oracle CC&B Bill Cycle. Examples: BC01 (1st of month), BC15 (15th of month).']
  btm_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account has Behind-the-Meter (BTM) generation or storage assets installed at the premise. BTM assets include solar PV, battery storage, and combined heat and power (CHP) systems that are not directly visible to the grid operator. Drives load disaggregation and DER management workflows.']
  budget_billing_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in a budget billing (levelized payment plan) program, where monthly bills are averaged over a 12-month period to smooth seasonal fluctuations. Affects bill calculation and settlement processes in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B.']
  cis_account_key varchar [note: 'The native primary key or account identifier as stored in the source CIS (SAP IS-U contract account number VKONT, or Oracle CC&B account ID). Enables traceability back to the operational system of record for reconciliation, audit, and issue resolution.']
  cis_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational Customer Information System (CIS) from which this account record originates. Critical for data lineage, deduplication, and cross-system reconciliation in multi-CIS utility environments. Values: SAP_IS_U (SAP Industry Solution for Utilities), ORACLE_CCB (Oracle Customer Care and Billing), LEGACY_CIS (decommissioned legacy system).']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the service agreement or contract associated with this account is scheduled to expire or terminate. Null for open-ended tariff service. For fixed-term commercial or industrial contracts and PPAs, this date triggers renewal or renegotiation workflows.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the service agreement or contract associated with this account became effective. For residential accounts this typically aligns with move_in_date; for commercial and industrial accounts this may reflect a separately negotiated contract effective date, Power Purchase Agreement (PPA), or special contract term.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account record was first created in the source CIS system. Represents the audit creation time for data lineage and SOX compliance. Distinct from activation_timestamp which represents the business event of service energization.']
  credit_class varchar [note: 'Credit risk classification assigned to the account based on payment history, credit bureau data, and internal scoring. Governs deposit requirements, payment plan eligibility, and collections treatment. Managed within SAP IS-U FICA or Oracle CC&B Collections module.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of security deposit held by the utility for this account, as required by credit class or PUC tariff rules. Refundable upon account closure in good standing. Tracked in SAP IS-U FI-CA or Oracle CC&B.']
  ev_charging_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in a special Electric Vehicle (EV) charging rate or managed charging program. Drives eligibility for off-peak EV charging incentives and supports grid load forecasting for EV adoption planning.']
  last_bill_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent bill generated for this account. Used to identify unbilled accounts, support billing cycle management, and calculate days since last bill for collections and customer service workflows.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment posted to this account. Used in collections scoring, credit class review, and customer service inquiries. Sourced from SAP IS-U FI-CA payment history or Oracle CC&B payment records.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to account_status. Enables tracking of the current status duration and supports collections and reconnection SLA monitoring. Complements the account status history table for current-state queries.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this account record in the source CIS or Silver Layer. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail requirements for SOX and regulatory compliance.']
  life_support_equipment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer at this account relies on electrically-powered life support equipment (e.g., home dialysis, ventilator, oxygen concentrator). Triggers mandatory advance disconnection notice requirements and priority restoration during outages per PUC regulations.']
  move_in_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer initiated service at the associated premise, triggering account activation and the start of billing. Corresponds to the service start date in CIS. Critical for proration of first bill and regulatory reporting of new connects.']
  move_out_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer terminated service at the associated premise, triggering final bill generation and account closure. Null for currently active accounts. Corresponds to the service end date in CIS.']
  open_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding balance on the account as of the last billing or payment posting. Represents the net amount owed by the customer (positive) or credit due to the customer (negative). Sourced from SAP IS-U FI-CA or Oracle CC&B AR sub-ledger. Used for collections prioritization and credit risk management.']
  paperless_billing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in paperless (electronic) billing, receiving bills via email or online portal rather than postal mail. Supports sustainability reporting and customer engagement analytics.']
  preferred_contact_channel varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred channel for receiving utility communications including bills, outage notifications, and program offers. Drives multi-channel engagement routing in CRM and outage notification systems (OMS). Aligns with customer consent preferences.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the customer\'s preferred language for all communications. Drives bill language selection, IVR routing, and outage notification language. Examples: en, es, zh-TW, vi, ko.']
  rate_category varchar [note: 'The tariff rate schedule or rate class assigned to this account, determining how energy consumption is priced. Examples include residential flat rate, Time-of-Use (TOU), Critical Peak Pricing (CPP), industrial demand rate, and agricultural pumping rate. Drives bill calculation in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential_flat|tou_residential|tou_commercial|cpp|industrial_demand|agricultural|street_lighting|ev_charging — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (ISO 3166-2 subdivision) identifying the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) with regulatory authority over this account. Determines applicable tariff, consumer protection rules, and reporting obligations. Critical for multi-state utility operations.']
  renewable_energy_plan varchar [note: 'Code identifying the voluntary renewable energy plan or green tariff program in which this account is enrolled. Examples include 100% renewable, green power option, or Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) purchase programs. Supports Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|green_100pct|green_50pct|rec_purchase|community_solar|wind_option — promote to reference product]']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regulated service territory within which this account receives electric service. Determines applicable tariff schedules, regulatory jurisdiction, and franchise area. Aligns with PUC-approved service territory boundaries.']
  status_change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining the most recent account status transition. Examples include non_payment, customer_request, move_out, returned_check, medical_baseline, regulatory_order. Supports collections analytics and regulatory compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_payment|customer_request|move_out|returned_check|medical_baseline|regulatory_order|fraud|deceased — promote to reference product]']
  tou_rate_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled on a Time-of-Use (TOU) rate schedule, where energy prices vary by time of day and season to reflect grid conditions. Requires AMI metering. Supports demand flexibility analytics and rate design evaluation.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a utility customer account in SAP IS-U and Oracle CC&B. Captures the authoritative account record for residential, commercial, and industrial customers including account number, account type (residential/commercial/industrial), account status, account status history (with change timestamps, reason codes, and initiating events), credit class, move-in/move-out dates, service territory, rate category, NEM participation flag, BTM flag, DER enrollment status, CIS source system identifier, regulatory jurisdiction, parent account reference for consolidated billing and corporate hierarchy, and account group classification (consolidated billing, corporate parent, franchise, municipal aggregation, CCA). SSOT for all downstream billing, metering, and demand response domains. Supports lifecycle tracking of activation, suspension, disconnection, reconnection, write-off, and reactivation events.'
}

Table customer.profile {
  profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer profile record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for all downstream joins across billing, metering, and demand response domains.']
  mailing_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Profile has embedded mailing address fields that should be normalized to reference the address table. The profile table has mailing_address_line1, mailing_address_line2, mailing_city, mailing_state_pr']
  autopay_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in automatic payment (AutoPay), authorizing the utility to automatically debit the customer\'s bank account or payment card on the bill due date. Reduces collections risk and improves cash flow predictability.']
  budget_billing_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in a budget billing (levelized payment) program, where monthly charges are averaged over 12 months to smooth seasonal bill fluctuations. Drives levelized billing calculation in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B.']
  business_entity_type varchar [note: 'Legal structure of the commercial or industrial customer organization. Null for residential person customers. Used for credit assessment, tax treatment, and regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sole_proprietor|partnership|llc|corporation|non_profit|government|trust|cooperative — promote to reference product]']
  business_partner_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the customer in the source Customer Information System (CIS). Maps to SAP IS-U Business Partner (BP) number or Oracle CC&B Person ID. Used for cross-system reconciliation and customer-facing correspondence.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred channel for receiving utility communications including bills, outage notifications, demand response alerts, and program enrollment confirmations. Drives multi-channel engagement routing in CRM and OMS notification systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer profile record was first created in the source CIS system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Represents the customer\'s initial enrollment date with the utility. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format per model conventions.']
  credit_classification varchar [note: 'Internal credit risk classification assigned to the customer based on payment history, credit bureau data, and utility-specific scoring. Drives deposit requirements, payment plan eligibility, and collections treatment. Sourced from SAP IS-U FICA credit segment or Oracle CC&B credit rating.']
  credit_score varchar [note: 'Numeric credit score obtained from a third-party credit bureau or internal scoring model. Range typically 300–850 for FICO-based scores. Used for deposit waiver decisions, payment arrangement eligibility, and collections prioritization. Null if no credit history exists.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary market segment classification of the customer. Drives rate schedule eligibility, billing treatment, and regulatory reporting. Residential covers individual households; commercial covers small-to-medium businesses; industrial covers large-load manufacturing and processing facilities; agricultural covers farming operations; government covers public sector entities.']
  customer_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is a natural person (individual) or a legal organization (business entity). Determines which identity attributes are applicable and governs data handling obligations under privacy regulations.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the residential customer individual. Used for identity verification, age-based eligibility programs (e.g., senior discount rates), and credit assessment. Null for organization customers. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format per model conventions.']
  demand_response_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is actively enrolled in a Demand Response (DR) program, authorizing the utility or ISO/RTO to curtail or shift the customer\'s load during peak demand events. Drives DR dispatch eligibility in DERMS and EMS systems.']
  deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is required to pay a security deposit based on credit classification, payment history, or state PUC rules. True triggers deposit collection workflow in SAP IS-U FICA or Oracle CC&B.']
  der_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has one or more Distributed Energy Resources (DER) — such as rooftop solar, battery storage, or EV chargers — enrolled in the utility\'s DERMS platform. Enables participation in virtual power plant (VPP) programs and demand response dispatch.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has requested to be excluded from non-essential marketing and outreach communications. Does not suppress legally required notices (e.g., disconnection warnings, safety alerts). Enforced in CRM and outbound communication systems.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the customer used for electronic bill delivery, outage notifications, demand response program communications, and account alerts. Sourced from SAP IS-U BP email or Oracle CC&B Person email.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the residential customer individual. Null for commercial/industrial entity customers. Sourced from SAP IS-U BP first name or Oracle CC&B Person first name field.']
  government_id_masked varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the government-issued identification number, retaining only the last four digits (e.g., ***-**-1234 for SSN). Full ID is stored only in the source CIS system under restricted access. Used for identity confirmation without exposing the full identifier.']
  government_id_type varchar [note: 'Type of government-issued identification document provided by the customer for identity verification. SSN and ITIN apply to US individuals; EIN applies to business entities; passport and drivers_license apply to individuals. Paired with masked government_id_masked field.']
  identity_verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the customer\'s identity verification process. Verified indicates successful validation against government ID or third-party identity service; unverified indicates not yet validated; pending indicates verification in progress; failed indicates verification attempt was unsuccessful; waived indicates verification requirement was formally waived (e.g., for government accounts).']
  identity_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer\'s identity was most recently successfully verified. Used for compliance audit trails and periodic re-verification scheduling. Null if identity_verification_status is unverified or pending.']
  income_tier varchar [note: 'Income tier classification assigned to the customer based on self-reported or verified household income relative to the federal poverty level (FPL). Used for low-income rate program eligibility, LIHEAP enrollment, and CARE/FERA program qualification. Tier 1 represents lowest income bracket.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred language for all communications, bills, and notices. Stored as ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., en, es, zh, fr, vi). Drives multi-language bill rendering and outage notification language selection in CIS and OMS systems.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the residential customer individual. Null for commercial/industrial entity customers. Sourced from SAP IS-U BP last name or Oracle CC&B Person last name field.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the customer profile record in the source CIS system. Used for change data capture (CDC) processing in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline and audit trail compliance.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the customer as registered with the utility. For residential customers this is the individual\'s full name; for commercial/industrial customers this is the registered business entity name. Used for billing, regulatory correspondence, and identity verification.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the customer profile in its lifecycle. Active indicates a current utility customer; inactive indicates a former customer with no active service agreements; pending indicates a new customer application in progress; suspended indicates temporary hold due to credit or compliance issues; deceased indicates confirmed death of a residential customer; merged indicates the profile was consolidated into another record.']
  low_income_assistance_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been determined eligible for low-income energy assistance programs such as CARE (California Alternate Rates for Energy), FERA, LIHEAP, or equivalent state-specific programs. Drives discounted rate application in billing.']
  low_income_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific low-income assistance program the customer is enrolled in (e.g., CARE, FERA, LIHEAP, HEAP, REACH). Null if low_income_assistance_eligible is false. Used for rate discount application and regulatory reporting to state PUC.']
  marketing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications about energy efficiency programs, new rate plans, and utility products. Required under GDPR opt-in rules for EU-adjacent operations and CCPA for California customers.']
  medical_baseline_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer qualifies for a medical baseline allowance due to a life-threatening illness or condition requiring life-support equipment. Triggers additional baseline energy allocation at the lowest rate tier and special disconnection protections under state PUC rules.']
  mobile_phone_number varchar [note: 'Mobile telephone number for the customer, used specifically for SMS-based outage alerts, two-factor authentication, and demand response text notifications. Distinct from primary phone which may be a landline. Stored in E.164 format.']
  nem_participant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer participates in a Net Energy Metering (NEM) program, meaning they have behind-the-meter (BTM) generation (typically solar PV) and receive credits for excess energy exported to the grid. Drives bidirectional metering requirements and NEM tariff application.']
  paperless_billing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted into electronic (paperless) bill delivery, suppressing physical mail generation. Drives bill rendering workflow in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B billing engine.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the customer. Used for outage notifications, payment reminders, demand response alerts, and field crew coordination. Stored in E.164 international format.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this customer profile was sourced. SAP_IS_U indicates SAP Industry Solution for Utilities; ORACLE_CCB indicates Oracle Utilities Customer Care and Billing; MANUAL indicates manually entered records; MIGRATION indicates records loaded during a system migration event. Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number for commercial/industrial customers (EIN) or individual taxpayer identification for sole proprietors. Used for 1099 reporting, tax exemption validation, and regulatory filings. Masked in analytics layer; full value retained in source CIS.']
  third_party_data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has consented to sharing their energy usage and profile data with authorized third parties (e.g., solar installers, EV charging providers, energy management platforms) under Green Button Connect My Data or similar data access frameworks.']

  Note: 'Master entity capturing the identity and demographic attributes of a utility customer person or organization. Stores legal name, date of birth, government ID type and masked number, business entity type (sole proprietor, LLC, corporation), language preference, communication preference, income tier classification, low-income assistance program eligibility, and identity verification status. Supports both residential individuals and commercial/industrial entities. Sourced from SAP IS-U Business Partner and Oracle CC&B Person/Account.'
}

Table customer.premise {
  premise_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the physical service location (premise) within the utility\'s Customer Information System (CIS). Primary key for all premise-level metering, billing, and outage management references. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Premises must be assigned to balancing authority control areas for load forecasting, interchange scheduling, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and NERC compliance reporting. Essential for operati']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Premises are mapped to distribution cost centers for territorial cost allocation and service area profitability tracking. Critical for distribution cost management, rate design, and territorial P&L re']
  distribution_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder circuit supplying power to the premise, as modeled in the utility\'s Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS) and GIS. Critical for outage management, load balancing, Fault Location Isolation and Service Restoration (FLISR), and Volt-VAR optimization in GE PowerOn ADMS.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution substation supplying the feeder that serves this premise. Used for hierarchical network topology analysis, outage impact scoping, and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability metric calculation.']
  distribution_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution transformer bank serving the premise, as recorded in the utility\'s asset management system (Ventyx Asset Suite) and GIS. Used for transformer loading analysis, outage impact assessment, and asset maintenance planning.']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Premises must be mapped to forecast zones for distribution load planning, capacity requirement allocation, outage impact analysis, and DER penetration forecasting. Essential for translating zone-level']
  meter_reading_route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the meter reading route to which the premise is assigned for field meter reading operations. Used for route optimization, meter reader scheduling, and AMI walk-by/drive-by reading coordination in Oracle Utilities MDM.']
  primary_warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse. Business justification: Premises are assigned to primary warehouses based on service territory geography for emergency restoration material dispatch, routine maintenance supply, and storm response logistics. Critical for SAI']
  ami_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise is eligible for Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) smart meter deployment based on infrastructure readiness, service voltage, and utility AMI rollout program criteria. Drives meter deployment prioritization in Oracle Utilities MDM.']
  billing_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the billing cycle to which the premise is assigned, determining meter reading schedule and bill generation frequency. Sourced from SAP IS-U Billing Cycle or Oracle CC&B Bill Cycle configuration.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Detailed structural classification of the premise beyond the high-level premise type. Used for load profile modeling, infrastructure planning, and demand response program eligibility. Examples: single-family, multi-family, high-rise, substation-fed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_family|multi_family|high_rise|substation_fed|mobile_home|commercial_strip|warehouse|manufacturing|farm — promote to reference product]']
  climate_zone varchar [note: 'Climate zone designation assigned to the premise based on geographic location and DOE/ASHRAE climate classification. Used for energy efficiency program targeting, demand response (DR) program design, Integrated Resource Planning (IRP), and Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance analysis.']
  construction_year varchar [note: 'Year the premise structure was originally constructed. Used for energy efficiency program eligibility assessment, weatherization program targeting, load profile modeling, and infrastructure age analysis. Sourced from county assessor records or customer-provided data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the premise record was first created in the source CIS system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Provides audit trail for data governance, regulatory compliance, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  critical_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise is designated as a critical facility (e.g., hospital, emergency services, water treatment plant, telecommunications hub) requiring priority restoration during outages. Drives outage management system (OMS) prioritization and mutual aid planning.']
  der_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise has an active Distributed Energy Resource (DER) enrollment, including rooftop solar, battery storage, or other behind-the-meter (BTM) generation assets registered in the utility\'s Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS). Supports Virtual Power Plant (VPP) aggregation and demand response programs.']
  ev_charging_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise has a registered electric vehicle (EV) charging installation. Used for distribution load forecasting, EV rate schedule eligibility, demand response program targeting, and grid modernization planning.']
  floor_area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total conditioned floor area of the premise structure in square feet. Used for energy intensity benchmarking (kWh per square foot), energy efficiency program eligibility, demand response capacity estimation, and load profile normalization.']
  gis_parcel_reference varchar [note: 'Assessor\'s parcel number or GIS parcel identifier linking the premise to the county tax assessor\'s spatial parcel layer in Esri ArcGIS Utility Network. Enables land-use analysis, property boundary correlation, and infrastructure planning.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent utility field inspection of the premise\'s service entrance, metering equipment, or electrical infrastructure. Used for compliance tracking, safety program management, and asset maintenance scheduling in Ventyx Asset Suite.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the premise record in the source CIS system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance per SOX and NERC CIP requirements.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the physical service location as recorded in the utility\'s Geographic Information System (GIS). Used for outage management spatial analysis, field crew routing, and distribution network modeling in Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  life_support_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a registered life-support equipment user resides at the premise. Triggers disconnection protection, priority outage notification, and special field crew protocols per state PUC regulations. Distinct from medical_baseline_flag which governs billing treatment.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the premise within the utility\'s service portfolio. Drives eligibility for new service connections, billing activation, and outage management inclusion. Active premises are energized and receiving service; decommissioned premises have been permanently removed from service.']
  load_profile_class varchar [note: 'Utility-assigned load profile class code used to model the premise\'s expected energy consumption pattern for billing, forecasting, and settlement purposes. Applied to non-interval-metered customers for load research and wholesale market settlement. Sourced from Oracle Utilities MDM or SAP IS-U.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the physical service location as recorded in the utility\'s Geographic Information System (GIS). Used in conjunction with latitude for spatial analysis, outage polygon mapping, and DER interconnection planning.']
  medical_baseline_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise has a registered medical baseline allowance due to a life-support or medical necessity condition. Triggers special billing treatment, outage notification priority, and disconnection protection protocols per state PUC regulations.']
  meter_socket_count varchar [note: 'Number of meter sockets (meter points) installed at the premise. Multi-socket premises include multi-family dwellings, commercial complexes, and industrial facilities with multiple service points. Drives meter deployment planning in Oracle Utilities MDM and SAP IS-U Device Management.']
  nem_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise is eligible for Net Energy Metering (NEM) based on service territory rules, interconnection capacity, and applicable state PUC tariff provisions. Supports Distributed Energy Resource (DER) enrollment and behind-the-meter (BTM) generation tracking.']
  num_units varchar [note: 'Number of individual dwelling or tenant units within the premise. Applicable to multi-family, high-rise, and mixed-use premises. Used for per-unit load analysis, master-metered billing allocation, and demand response program enrollment capacity.']
  peak_demand_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Recorded or estimated peak electrical demand in kilowatts (kW) for the premise. Used for transformer sizing, distribution capacity planning, demand charge billing, and demand response (DR) program baseline establishment. Sourced from Oracle Utilities MDM interval data or SAP IS-U demand registers.']
  premise_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the premise as assigned by the utility\'s CIS (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Used in field operations, customer correspondence, and cross-system integration. Corresponds to SAP IS-U Premise Number or Oracle CC&B Premise ID.']
  premise_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the premise by customer segment and land use. Drives rate schedule eligibility, load profile assignment, and regulatory reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|commercial|industrial|agricultural|municipal|mixed_use — promote to reference product]']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Utility tariff rate schedule code applicable to the premise, as defined in the state PUC-approved tariff and configured in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B. Determines energy charge structure, demand charges, TOU periods, and applicable riders.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Premise has embedded address fields that should be normalized to reference the address table. The premise table has address_line1, address_line2, city, state_code, postal_code, county, country_code wh']
  service_end_date date [note: 'Date on which utility service was permanently terminated at the premise (e.g., demolition, decommissioning). Null for active premises. Used for premise lifecycle management and historical reporting.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Date on which utility service was first activated at the premise. Establishes the premise\'s service history baseline for billing, regulatory reporting, and infrastructure age analysis.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Utility-defined code identifying the franchised service territory zone in which the premise is located. Determines applicable tariff schedules, regulatory jurisdiction (PUC), and operational responsibility. Sourced from SAP IS-U Division or Oracle CC&B Service Territory.']
  service_voltage_class varchar [note: 'Nominal voltage class at which electric service is delivered to the premise. Determines applicable tariff rate schedule, metering configuration, and interconnection requirements. Critical for rate design and OATT compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: secondary_120v|secondary_240v|primary_4kv|primary_12kv|primary_25kv|transmission — promote to reference product]']
  tou_rate_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise qualifies for Time of Use (TOU) or Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) rate schedules based on metering capability (AMI), service class, and applicable PUC tariff provisions. Drives rate schedule assignment and customer enrollment workflows.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a physical service location (premise) where utility service is delivered. Captures premise address, premise type (residential, commercial, industrial, agricultural), GIS coordinates (latitude/longitude), service territory code, distribution feeder ID, transformer bank ID, meter socket count, premise classification (single-family, multi-family, high-rise, substation-fed), load profile class, climate zone, and premise construction year. SSOT for all premise-level metering, billing, and outage management references.'
}

Table customer.customer_service_agreement {
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer service agreement. Primary key for this entity.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Every service agreement is held by an account. This is a core CIS relationship — service_agreement.account_id → account.account_id. Without this, service agreements are orphaned.']
  customer_premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical premise where service is delivered under this agreement.']
  der_interconnection_point_id varchar [note: 'Interconnection agreement identifier for customers with DER (Distributed Energy Resource) such as solar, wind, or battery storage. Links to detailed interconnection application and technical specifications. Null for customers without DER.']
  ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.ppa_contract. Business justification: Behind-the-meter PPA agreements are service agreements requiring direct linkage for billing integration, rate schedule coordination, and customer account management. Critical for community solar and t']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.premise.premise_id — Every service agreement delivers service at a specific premise. service_agreement.premise_id → premise.premise_id. This is the core CIS triad: account ↔ service agreement ↔ premise.']
  primary_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that holds this service agreement.']
  service_delivery_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Service agreements reference specific service delivery assets (transformer, switchgear) for capacity allocation, load management, and contract demand enforcement. Required for distribution capacity pl']
  service_level_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_level_agreement. Business justification: Each customer service agreement is governed by a Service Level Agreement; adding FK captures this relationship and provides inbound link to service_level_agreement.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competitive energy supplier or Community Choice Aggregator (CCA) providing generation service in deregulated markets. Null for bundled service where utility provides both delivery and generation.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the service agreement, used in customer communications and billing statements.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service agreement. Draft indicates agreement is being prepared; pending approval awaiting credit or regulatory review; active means service is being delivered; suspended for temporary service interruption; terminated for completed agreements; cancelled for agreements that never went active.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service agreement. Standard represents typical utility service; NEM (Net Energy Metering) for customers with behind-the-meter generation; DER interconnection for Distributed Energy Resource participants; community solar for shared renewable programs; green tariff for voluntary renewable energy purchase; special contract for negotiated large customer agreements.']
  auto_pay_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic payment is enabled for this service agreement, allowing bills to be paid automatically from customer bank account or credit card.']
  billing_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Billing cycle assignment determining when meter reads occur and bills are generated. Typically represents a monthly cycle number (e.g., cycle 01 reads on 1st of month, cycle 15 reads on 15th).']
  budget_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in budget billing program, which averages annual usage to provide consistent monthly payment amounts.']
  care_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates enrollment in CARE or similar low-income assistance program providing discounted rates. CARE is California-specific; other states have equivalent programs (e.g., LIHEAP).']
  contract_demand_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Contracted maximum demand in kilowatts (kW) for large commercial and industrial customers with demand-based rate schedules. Customer pays for this capacity regardless of actual usage. Null for non-demand customers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service agreement record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of security deposit held for this service agreement, typically required for customers with poor credit or no credit history. Null if no deposit required.']
  deposit_waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why security deposit was waived if deposit amount is zero. Good credit for customers meeting credit score threshold; senior citizen, low income, or military for protected classes; prior customer for returning customers with good payment history; none if deposit was not waived.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when service agreement terminates and service delivery ends. Null for open-ended agreements. Populated when customer requests disconnection or agreement reaches contracted term end.']
  life_support_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether premise has registered life support or medical equipment requiring continuous electric service. Provides special protections against disconnection and priority restoration during outages.']
  load_profile_code varchar [note: 'Statistical load profile classification used for non-interval metered customers to estimate hourly usage patterns for settlement and forecasting. Based on customer class and historical usage patterns.']
  master_metered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement covers master-metered service where a single meter serves multiple dwelling units (e.g., apartment building) and the property owner sub-meters or allocates costs to tenants.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service agreement record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and compliance.']
  nem_agreement_type varchar [note: 'Specific NEM (Net Energy Metering) program version if customer has behind-the-meter generation.
