  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility service account under which the enrollment is registered. A customer may have multiple accounts (premises) with independent enrollments.']

Business justification: Enrollment represents program participation and should reference the specific service agreement under which the customer is enrolled.   der_registry_id varchar [note: 'The unique registration identifier assigned to the customer\'s DER asset in the utility\'s Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS). Used for dispatch, telemetry, and FERC Order 2222 aggregation tracking.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: Customer DR program enrollment requires proper FK to dr_program for tracking enrollment terms, incentive structures, and regulatory compliance. Currently uses denormalized program_code/name. Essential']
  financial_assistance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.assistance_program. Business justification: Low-income and medical baseline enrollments must reference the authoritative financial assistance program in billing to ensure correct discount application, verify eligibility criteria match, and supp']
  incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.incentive_program. Business justification: Customer enrollments in solar, storage, and renewable incentive programs require direct linkage for payment processing, compliance verification, budget tracking, and regulatory reporting. Essential fo']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Customer DER program enrollments (NEM, demand response, VPP) originate from interconnection applications; tracking the originating application enables program eligibility verification, capacity valida']
  interconnection_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: DER enrollments must reference the interconnection asset (inverter, generation equipment, storage system) for NEM export tracking, Rule 21 compliance, and grid impact studies. Mandated by interconnect']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: DER/NEM program enrollments require specific equipment installations (inverters, smart meters, interconnection hardware). Tracking approved equipment specifications ensures regulatory compliance, warr']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter. Business justification: NEM and DER program enrollments require specific meter configurations (bidirectional metering, interval data collection). Supports interconnection approval workflows, export credit calculations, and r']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical service premise (metered location) associated with this enrollment. Required for NEM, DER, and demand response programs tied to a specific service point.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account holder enrolled in the program. Links to the master customer record in the CIS (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B).']
  vpp_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.vpp_configuration. Business justification: Customer DER enrollments in VPP programs require tracking which VPP configuration the asset participates in for dispatch authorization, performance incentives, capacity payments, and ISO/RTO settlemen']
  additional_baseline_kwh_per_day decimal(8,3) [note: 'The approved additional daily energy allowance in kilowatt-hours (kWh/day) granted to the customer under the medical baseline program, above the standard baseline allocation. Used in billing rate calculations.']
  aggregator_name varchar [note: 'The name of the third-party aggregator managing the customer\'s DER asset within a Virtual Power Plant (VPP) or demand response aggregation program. Required for FERC Order 2222 compliance and aggregator settlement.']
  annual_true_up_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month (1–12) in which the customer\'s annual NEM true-up billing cycle is settled. Determines when net export credits are reconciled against consumption charges for the 12-month period.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the enrollment application was formally approved by the utility or regulatory authority. Relevant for NEM interconnection approvals, medical baseline certifications, and low-income program eligibility approvals.']
  arrearage_forgiveness_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of past-due balance forgiven under an arrearage management program. [Moved from assistance_program: Arrearage forgiveness is applied at enrollment time and varies by account based on outstanding balance, making it enrollment-specific.]']
  arrearage_forgiveness_date date [note: 'Date when the arrearage forgiveness was applied to the customer account. [Moved from assistance_program: The date arrearage forgiveness was applied is specific to each enrollment instance.]']
  arrearage_plan_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'The outstanding balance (in USD) enrolled in the customer\'s Arrearage Management Plan (AMP). Tracks the deferred debt amount being managed under the structured repayment program. Null for non-AMP enrollments.']
  certification_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the medical baseline or life-support certification expires and must be renewed. Used to trigger renewal reminder notifications and manage disconnection protection eligibility.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer enrolled in the program. Supports multi-channel engagement analytics and channel effectiveness reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|ivr|call_center|field_agent|mail|third_party|auto_enrolled — promote to reference product]']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'The communication protocol used for telemetry and dispatch signaling between the utility/DERMS and the customer\'s DER device. Determines interoperability requirements for demand response and VPP dispatch.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the enrollment record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage, regulatory compliance, and SOX internal controls.']
  cumulative_export_credits decimal(12,4) [note: 'The running balance of net energy export credits (in kWh) accumulated by the customer under the NEM program since the last annual true-up. Used for billing reconciliation and NEM settlement calculations.']
  demand_response_program varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Demand Response (DR) program in which this account is enrolled, such as interruptible load, direct load control, or automated demand response. Null if not enrolled. Drives DR dispatch eligibility and incentive payment calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: interruptible_load|direct_load_control|auto_dr|peak_time_rebate|emergency_dr|none — promote to reference product] [Moved from account: This code identifies a single DR program but does not support enrollment in multiple DR programs simultaneously, nor does it capture enrollment dates, participation level, or tier. Should be replaced by enrollment records linking to DR program records.]']
  der_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Enrollment status of this account in a Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS) or Virtual Power Plant (VPP) program. Enrolled accounts may have their DER assets dispatched by the utility or grid operator for demand response, frequency regulation, or capacity services. [Moved from account: This status field indicates DER program enrollment status but belongs to the specific account-program enrollment relationship, not to the account itself. An account may be enrolled in multiple DER programs with different statuses. Should be captured as enrollment.status for DER program enrollments.]']
  der_technology_type varchar [note: 'The technology type of the customer\'s Distributed Energy Resource (DER) asset enrolled in the program. Used for DERMS registration, VPP aggregation, and FERC Order 2222 compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pv_solar|wind|chp|battery_storage|ev_charger|smart_thermostat|generator|fuel_cell — promote to reference product]']
  disconnection_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s account is protected from service disconnection due to medical baseline, life-support, or low-income program enrollment. True = disconnection protection active; False = no protection. Critical for collections and field operations.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the customer bill under the assistance program (e.g., 20.00 for 20% discount). [Moved from assistance_program: While programs may have standard discount rates, the actual discount applied can vary by account based on rate class, usage tier, or household size, making it enrollment-specific.]']
  dispatch_authorization_level varchar [note: 'The level of dispatch authority granted by the customer for their DER asset in demand response or VPP programs. \'full_auto\' = utility can dispatch without customer action; \'semi_auto\' = customer notified before dispatch; \'manual\' = customer initiates; \'opt_in_only\' = customer must opt in per event.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the enrollment ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended enrollments. Used for term-limited programs, certification expirations, and contract end dates.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the enrollment becomes operationally effective and the customer begins receiving program benefits, rates, or obligations. May differ from enrollment_date due to billing cycle alignment or approval processing.']
  eligibility_verification_date date [note: 'Date when customer eligibility was verified for the assistance program. [Moved from assistance_program: Verification date varies by enrollment instance based on when the customer submitted documentation.]']
  eligibility_verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the customer eligibility verification process for the assistance program. [Moved from assistance_program: Verification status is specific to each account\'s enrollment application and documentation submission.]']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the customer submitted or was enrolled in the program. Represents the principal business event date (application/enrollment action date), distinct from the effective start date when benefits begin.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the enrollment record. \'pending\' = application submitted, awaiting approval; \'active\' = currently participating; \'suspended\' = temporarily paused (e.g., medical review); \'cancelled\' = voluntarily withdrawn; \'expired\' = past effective end date; \'rejected\' = application denied.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment expires and requires re-certification or renewal. [Moved from assistance_program: Enrollment expiration is specific to each account-program enrollment, not to the program definition.]']
  export_compensation_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The per-kilowatt-hour ($/kWh) rate at which the utility compensates the customer for net energy exported to the grid under the NEM tariff. Rate is locked at enrollment for grandfathered NEM versions. Null for non-NEM enrollments.']
  green_energy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the customer\'s energy supply sourced from renewable energy under a green energy program enrollment (0–100%). Used for Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) allocation and Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance reporting.']
  household_size varchar [note: 'Number of individuals in the customer household used for eligibility determination. [Moved from assistance_program: Household size is captured at enrollment time for eligibility determination and may change between recertifications, making it enrollment-specific.]']
  income_verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer\'s income eligibility was last verified for low-income assistance programs (CARE, FERA, LIHEAP). Used for periodic re-certification compliance and audit trails.']
  installed_capacity_kw_dc decimal(10,3) [note: 'The nameplate DC capacity in kilowatts (kW-DC) of the customer\'s installed distributed generation or storage system. Used for NEM eligibility sizing, export limit calculations, and DERMS registration. Applicable to NEM and DER/VPP enrollments.']
  interconnection_application_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer submitted the interconnection application for a distributed generation system (e.g., rooftop solar, battery storage). Applicable to NEM and DER enrollments. Required for FERC Order 2222 and state PUC interconnection tracking.']
  inverter_type varchar [note: 'The type of inverter installed with the customer\'s solar PV or battery storage system. Relevant for NEM technical review, grid interconnection safety assessment, and DERMS telemetry compatibility.']
  last_recertification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent eligibility recertification completed by the customer. [Moved from assistance_program: Recertification history is specific to each account\'s enrollment, not to the program.]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the enrollment record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trails, and downstream incremental processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  life_support_equipment_type varchar [note: 'The type of electrically powered life-support medical equipment used by the customer or household member. Determines disconnection protection rules and priority restoration status during outages. Null for non-life-support enrollments.']
  low_income_program varchar [note: 'Code identifying the low-income assistance program in which this account is enrolled, such as CARE (California Alternate Rates for Energy), FERA (Family Electric Rate Assistance), LIHEAP, or utility-specific low-income discount programs. Drives rate discounts and disconnection protections. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: care|fera|liheap|utility_discount|none — promote to reference product] [Moved from account: This code identifies a single low-income program (CARE/FERA) but does not support enrollment in multiple assistance programs, nor does it capture enrollment date, eligibility verification date, tier assignment, or recertification schedule. Should be replaced by enrollment records.]']
  med_certification_type varchar [note: 'The type of medical program certification for the customer. \'life_support\' = customer or household member depends on electrically powered life-support equipment; \'medical_baseline_allowance\' = customer qualifies for additional baseline energy allowance due to medical condition. Null for non-medical enrollments.']
  medical_baseline boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account has been approved for a Medical Baseline Allowance, providing additional low-cost energy allocation to customers with qualifying medical conditions that require life-support equipment or climate control. Governs disconnection protections and rate adjustments per PUC rules. [Moved from account: This boolean flag indicates medical baseline approval but does not capture approval date, expiration date, recertification schedule, or participation level. Should be replaced by enrollment records linking to the Medical Baseline Program with full lifecycle attributes.]']
  monthly_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly credit amount applied to the customer bill under the assistance program. [Moved from assistance_program: The monthly credit amount may vary by enrollment based on account characteristics, usage patterns, or tiered benefit structures.]']
  nem_participant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in a Net Energy Metering (NEM) program, meaning the customer has behind-the-meter generation (typically solar PV) and receives bill credits for excess energy exported to the grid. Drives bidirectional metering requirements and NEM tariff application. [Moved from account: This boolean flag indicates NEM program participation but does not capture which NEM program, enrollment dates, or participation level. The flag should be replaced by enrollment records in the enrollment association that link to specific NEM program records with full lifecycle tracking.]']
  nem_version varchar [note: 'The version of the Net Energy Metering tariff under which the customer is enrolled. Determines export compensation rate structure and true-up methodology. Null for non-NEM enrollments.']
  opt_out_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer voluntarily opted out of the program. Null if the customer has not opted out. Distinct from effective_end_date which may be system-driven.']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the customer\'s voluntary withdrawal from the program. Used for churn analysis, program improvement, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|moved|rate_change|financial_hardship|program_change|ineligible|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  permission_to_operate_date date [note: 'The date on which the utility granted Permission to Operate (PTO) for the customer\'s distributed energy resource (DER) system. Marks the official start of NEM or DER export eligibility.']
  physician_certification_date date [note: 'The date on which a licensed physician certified the customer\'s medical condition for the medical baseline allowance or life-support program. Required for PUC medical tariff compliance.']
  qualifying_condition varchar [note: 'The medical condition or diagnosis that qualifies the customer for the medical baseline allowance or life-support program. Protected Health Information (PHI) — classified as restricted. Null for non-medical enrollments.']
  recertification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer must complete eligibility recertification to maintain program enrollment. [Moved from assistance_program: Recertification cycles are specific to each enrollment instance based on initial enrollment date and program rules.]']
  renewal_reminder_date date [note: 'The date on which the utility should send a renewal reminder notification to the customer for medical baseline or life-support certification renewal. Typically set a defined number of days before certification_expiration_date.']
  telemetry_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data is available from the customer\'s DER device for DERMS monitoring and dispatch. True = telemetry enabled; False = no real-time telemetry (manual or estimated dispatch).']
  total_benefit_received_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total benefit amount the customer has received to date under the current enrollment. [Moved from assistance_program: This is a cumulative metric specific to each account\'s enrollment, tracking total benefits received to date for this enrollment instance.]']
  tou_rate_plan_code varchar [note: 'The specific Time-of-Use (TOU) or Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) rate plan code assigned to the customer under this enrollment. Maps to the tariff schedule in the billing system. Null for non-rate-plan enrollments.']
  vpp_program_code varchar [note: 'The utility-assigned code for the Virtual Power Plant (VPP) aggregation program in which the customer\'s DER is enrolled. Used for dispatch scheduling, settlement, and FERC Order 2222 reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity and SSOT for all customer program participation across the utility. Captures enrollments in demand response (DR), time-of-use (TOU) rate plans, critical peak pricing (CPP) tariffs, budget billing, auto-pay, paperless billing, Net Energy Metering (NEM 1.0/2.0/3.0/NEM-ST) including interconnection application date, approval date, permission-to-operate date, installed capacity (kW-DC), inverter type, technology type (PV, wind, CHP), export compensation rate, annual true-up month, and cumulative export credits. Captures DER/VPP aggregation program enrollments including DER asset type (rooftop solar, battery storage, EV charger, smart thermostat, generator), DER registration ID, DERMS system reference, aggregator name, VPP program code, telemetry availability flag, communication protocol (IEEE 2030.5, OpenADR, Modbus), and dispatch authorization level. Captures medical baseline allowance certifications including certification type (life support, medical baseline allowance), qualifying condition, physician certification date, certification expiration, renewal reminder date, approved additional baseline kWh per day, and life-support equipment type (oxygen concentrator, dialysis, ventilator). Captures low-income assistance program enrollments (LIHEAP, CARE, FERA), green energy programs, EV charging incentive programs, and arrearage management plans. Stores program code, enrollment date, enrollment channel, enrollment status, effective start and end dates, opt-out date and reason, and all program-specific eligibility and technical attributes. Supports FERC Order 2222 compliance, PUC medical baseline tariff compliance, disconnection protection rules, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table customer.move_order {
  move_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the move order record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_service_agreement. Business justification: Move order (move-in, move-out, transfer) should reference the service agreement being initiated or terminated. Currently move_order has account_id and premise_id but no service_agreement_id. This FK e']
  field_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field work order created in the asset/workforce management system (Ventyx Asset Suite) to execute the physical service connection, disconnection, or meter read associated with this move order. Links the CIS move order to the field operations domain.']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the meter device installed at the service point at the time of the move order. Used to schedule move-in/move-out meter reads and link final read values to the correct device record in the Metering domain.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service point (metering point / point of delivery) at the premise where service is being initiated or terminated. A premise may have multiple service points (e.g., electric and gas).']
  move_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Move orders initiate/terminate service for an account. move_order.account_id → account.account_id.']
  move_premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise (physical location) at which the move order is being executed. Central linkage to the premise master record for address, meter, and distribution asset context.']
  primary_move_prior_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the existing service account being closed or vacated as part of this move order. Populated for move-out and transfer order types. Used to trigger final bill generation and deposit disposition in the billing domain.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer party initiating the move order. Links to the customer master record in the Customer domain. For a transfer, this is the incoming customer (move-in party).']
  tertiary_move_prior_occupant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the account of the previous occupant at the premise, if known at the time of the move-in order. Used to coordinate final bill issuance for the departing customer and to identify any outstanding balance or deposit transfer obligations.']
  to_premise_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.premise.premise_id — Move orders reference the premise where service starts/stops. move_order.premise_id → premise.premise_id.']
  budget_billing_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in a budget billing (levelized payment) plan at the time of the move order. When true, the final bill calculation must account for any budget billing true-up balance upon move-out.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason explaining why the move order was cancelled or rejected. Populated only when order_status is \'cancelled\' or \'rejected\'. Used for operational quality analysis and customer service improvement.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The customer engagement channel through which the move order was submitted. Supports multi-channel analytics and CRM reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_portal|mobile_app|call_center|walk_in|field_crew|ivr|third_party — promote to reference product]']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the move order was fully completed and all downstream actions (meter read, billing, account activation/deactivation) were confirmed. Distinct from effective_date (the business date of service change) and submitted_timestamp (when the order was placed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this move order record was first created in the Silver layer data store. Distinct from submitted_timestamp which reflects the business event time.']
  credit_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a credit check was required for this move-in or transfer order based on the customer\'s credit classification or account history. When true, the move order may be held pending credit review and deposit determination per SAP IS-U FICA or Oracle CC&B Collections rules.']
  credit_check_status varchar [note: 'The outcome of the credit check performed for this move order. Not_required: credit check was not triggered. Passed: customer met credit criteria. Failed: customer did not meet criteria (deposit required). Pending: check in progress. Waived: credit check bypassed per business rules (e.g., existing customer in good standing).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts on this move order (deposit_amount, final_bill_amount). Typically USD for North American utilities. Supports multi-currency operations for utilities with international operations.']
  demand_response_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer or premise is enrolled in a Demand Response (DR) program at the time of the move order. When true, the DR program administrator must be notified to update enrollment records and avoid erroneous dispatch signals.']
  deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the security deposit associated with the prior or new account at the time of the move order. Used to determine deposit disposition (refund, transfer, or application to final bill balance) upon move-out or transfer.']
  deposit_disposition varchar [note: 'The outcome or planned treatment of the security deposit upon move-out or transfer. Refund: returned to customer. Applied_to_balance: offset against outstanding final bill. Transferred: moved to new account. Forfeited: retained by utility per tariff terms. Pending: disposition not yet determined.']
  der_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise associated with this move order has an active Distributed Energy Resource (DER) enrollment (e.g., battery storage, EV charger, demand response program). When true, triggers DERMS notification and DER agreement review upon move-out or transfer.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the service change (move-in, move-out, or transfer) became effective at the premise. Used as the billing boundary date for final bill generation and new account activation. May differ from requested_date due to scheduling constraints or field execution timing.']
  final_bill_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary amount of the final bill generated as a result of the move-out or transfer order. Represents the net amount owed by or to the customer after applying the final meter read, applicable tariff rates, taxes, and deposit disposition.']
  final_read_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The meter register reading (in kWh or applicable unit) captured at the time of the move order. For move-out, this is the closing read used to calculate the final bill. For move-in, this is the opening read establishing the new billing baseline.']
  landlord_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property owner or landlord must be notified of the service change per tariff rules or regulatory requirements. Relevant for rental properties where the landlord has a standing notification agreement with the utility.']
  landlord_notified_date date [note: 'The date on which the landlord or property owner was notified of the service change associated with this move order. Populated only when landlord_notification_required is true. Used for regulatory compliance documentation.']
  medical_baseline boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has an approved medical baseline allowance (life support or medical necessity designation) that requires special handling during service disconnection or move-out. When true, additional notification and regulatory compliance steps are required before service termination.']
  meter_read_date date [note: 'The date on which the meter was physically or remotely read in connection with this move order. Used as the billing cut-off date for the final bill (move-out) or the start-of-service read date (move-in). Sourced from Oracle Utilities MDM or SAP IS-U Device Management.']
  nem_participant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer associated with this move order is enrolled in a Net Energy Metering (NEM) program, typically due to rooftop solar or other behind-the-meter (BTM) generation. When true, triggers specialized NEM billing and interconnection review processes upon move-in or move-out.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the move order, assigned by the source system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Used for customer-facing correspondence and cross-system reconciliation.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the move order workflow. Pending: submitted but not yet processed. In_progress: actively being executed (meter read scheduled, field crew dispatched). Completed: service change fully effected. Cancelled: withdrawn before execution. Rejected: failed validation or business rules.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the service order: move_in (new service activation at a premise), move_out (service termination at a premise), transfer (simultaneous move-out and move-in for a change of occupant), reconnect (restoration of previously disconnected service), or disconnect (non-payment or voluntary disconnection). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: move_in|move_out|transfer|reconnect|disconnect — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'The tariff rate schedule code assigned to the new service account upon move-in or transfer. Determines the billing rate class (e.g., residential, small commercial, large industrial, TOU, CPP). Sourced from the applicable PUC-approved tariff.']
  read_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how the move-order meter read was obtained: actual (field technician physical read), estimated (calculated based on usage history when actual read is unavailable), AMI_remote (automated read via Advanced Metering Infrastructure), or customer_self_read (customer-reported reading).']
  read_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the final_read_value. Typically kWh for electric meters, but may be therms, CCF, or MCF for gas service points. Ensures correct interpretation of the meter read value across commodity types.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or exceptions associated with this move order. May include field crew instructions, customer-specific handling notes, or escalation details entered by customer service representatives.']
  requested_date date [note: 'The date the customer requested the service change to take effect. This is the customer-stated desired effective date, which may differ from the actual effective date after scheduling and field execution.']
  scheduled_read_date date [note: 'The date on which the meter read associated with this move order is scheduled to be performed. May differ from meter_read_date (actual read date) if the read is rescheduled or delayed. Used for field crew scheduling and MDM read request management.']
  service_class varchar [note: 'Customer service classification at the time of the move order, determining applicable tariff, billing rules, and regulatory reporting category. Residential, small commercial, large commercial, industrial, agricultural, or lighting.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the utility\'s regulated service territory within which the premise is located. Used for regulatory reporting to State PUCs, FERC Form 1 filings, and NERC reliability reporting. Determines applicable tariff jurisdiction.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this move order record was sourced. SAP_IS-U: SAP Industry Solution for Utilities. Oracle_CCB: Oracle Utilities Customer Care and Billing. Manual: entered directly into the data platform. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_order_reference varchar [note: 'The native primary key or order identifier from the originating operational system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Enables traceability back to the source system record for audit, reconciliation, and issue resolution.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the move order was first submitted into the CIS system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Represents the principal business event timestamp for order creation. Used for SLA tracking and order aging analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this move order record was most recently modified in the Silver layer data store. Used for change data capture and incremental processing.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing move-in, move-out, and transfer-of-service orders that initiate or terminate service at a premise. Stores order type (move-in, move-out, transfer), requested date, actual effective date, prior account reference, new account reference, premise reference, meter read date at move, final read value, deposit disposition, and order status. Sourced from SAP IS-U Move-In/Move-Out and Oracle CC&B Start/Stop Service. Triggers meter read scheduling and final bill generation in downstream domains.'
}

Table customer.third_party_authorization {
  third_party_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each third-party authorization or consent record. Primary key for the third_party_authorization data product within the customer domain SSOT.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Third-party authorizations are granted per account. third_party_authorization.account_id → account.account_id.']
  customer_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DER program enrollment record associated with this authorization. Populated when the authorization grants a DERMS provider or aggregator access to dispatch or monitor a customer\'s distributed energy resource (solar, battery, EV charger, etc.).']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the demand response program for which this authorization grants aggregator or DERMS provider access. Links the authorization to a specific DR or Virtual Power Plant (VPP) program managed by the utility or ISO/RTO.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or agent identifier of the utility customer service representative who processed or witnessed this authorization on behalf of the customer (e.g., for paper, verbal, or IVR-assisted consents). Null for fully self-service digital consents.']
  ferc_aggregator_id varchar [note: 'FERC-registered identifier for the demand response or DER aggregator authorized under FERC Order 2222. Required when authorization_type is \'aggregator\' or \'derms_provider\'. Enables regulatory traceability of aggregator participation in wholesale markets.']
  notification_preference_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.notification_preference. Business justification: Third‑party authorizations reference a notification preference code; replace with FK to notification_preference to normalize and give inbound link to notification_preference.']
  ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.ppa_contract. Business justification: Third-party PPA agreements require explicit customer authorization for data sharing, billing coordination, and account access. Critical for community solar subscriptions and third-party ownership mode']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise (physical location) covered by this authorization. Relevant for landlord, property manager, and aggregator authorizations scoped to a specific service address.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account holder who granted this authorization or consent. Links to the master customer record in the CIS (SAP IS-U / Oracle CC&B).']
  third_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific utility service account to which this authorization applies. A customer may have multiple accounts (residential, commercial, NEM, DER-enrolled) and authorizations are scoped at the account level.']
  to_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account.account_id — Authorizations and consent records are granted by a customer account. Required for FERC Order 2222 aggregator access, Green Button compliance, and CCPA/GDPR data sharing.']
  authorization_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this authorization record, used in customer correspondence, audit trails, and regulatory submissions. Formatted as AUTH- followed by a 10-digit sequence.']
  authorization_scope varchar [note: 'Textual description of the specific data, services, or actions covered by this authorization. Examples include: interval usage data, billing history, account balance, demand response enrollment, Green Button data, DER dispatch commands. Captures the granular scope agreed to by the customer.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the authorization record. Active indicates the authorization is in force; pending indicates awaiting customer confirmation; suspended indicates temporarily paused; revoked indicates customer-initiated withdrawal; expired indicates the authorization end date has passed.']
  authorization_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the access level or relationship type granted to the third party. Determines what actions the authorized party may perform: view_only (read-only data access), full_access (read and transact), notification_only (receive alerts), landlord (property owner access), property_manager (delegated property management), aggregator (demand response or VPP aggregator per FERC Order 2222), derms_provider (Distributed Energy Resource Management System provider access). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: view_only|full_access|notification_only|landlord|property_manager|aggregator|derms_provider — promote to reference product]']
  authorized_party_identifier varchar [note: 'External registration or license number identifying the authorized third party, such as a FERC-registered aggregator ID, state PUC license number, or business registration number. Used for regulatory verification and audit.']
  authorized_party_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the third party (individual, company, or organization) to whom access or authority has been granted. May be a landlord, property management company, demand response aggregator, DERMS provider, or other authorized entity.']
  authorized_party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the authorized third party entity type. Distinguishes between individual persons (e.g., family member, tenant), commercial companies (e.g., property management firm), aggregators (demand response or VPP aggregators per FERC Order 2222), government agencies, and non-profit organizations.']
  ccpa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this authorization record is subject to CCPA consumer rights obligations (right to know, right to delete, right to opt-out of sale). Relevant for California utility customers and California-regulated service territories.']
  consent_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or medium through which the customer provided consent or authorization. Captures: web_portal (online self-service), mobile_app (utility mobile application), ivr (Interactive Voice Response system), paper (physical form), written (written correspondence), electronic (email or e-sign), verbal (recorded phone call). Required for regulatory proof of consent method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_portal|mobile_app|ivr|paper|written|electronic|verbal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consent_text_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or URL pointing to the exact consent language text presented to the customer. Used for legal and regulatory audit to confirm the precise wording agreed to at the time of consent.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (with timezone) at which the customer provided consent or authorization. This is the principal real-world event time for the consent act, distinct from the record creation audit timestamp. Critical for GDPR, CCPA, and state PUC compliance audit trails.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consent event captured in this record. Covers: data_sharing (general data access consent), marketing (communications consent), green_button (Green Button Connect My Data consent), electronic_signature (e-sign agreement), aggregator (third-party aggregator authorization), der_enrollment (DER program enrollment consent), nem_participation (Net Energy Metering participation consent). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_sharing|marketing|green_button|electronic_signature|aggregator|der_enrollment|nem_participation|demand_response — promote to reference product]']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent text or terms presented to the customer at the time of consent capture. Enables tracking of which version of the consent language was agreed to, supporting regulatory audit and re-consent workflows when terms change.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this authorization record was first created in the system. Audit timestamp for data lineage and regulatory record-keeping. Distinct from consent_timestamp, which captures the real-world consent event time.']
  data_access_end_date date [note: 'The latest date through which data may be accessed by the authorized third party. Defines the forward boundary of the data sharing window. Distinct from effective_end_date, which governs the authorization itself; this field governs the data time range.']
  data_access_start_date date [note: 'The earliest date from which historical usage or account data may be accessed by the authorized third party under this authorization. Defines the lookback window for Green Button data sharing, aggregator access, and DERMS data feeds.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this authorization or consent expires and the authorized party\'s access terminates. Nullable for open-ended authorizations. When populated, the system must enforce access revocation on or after this date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this authorization or consent becomes effective and the authorized party may begin exercising the granted access or permissions. May differ from consent_timestamp if a future start date was agreed.']
  electronic_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the electronic signature record associated with this authorization, stored in the e-signature platform. Provides legal proof of customer agreement for electronically executed authorizations. Populated when consent_channel is \'electronic\' or \'electronic_signature\'.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this authorization record is subject to GDPR data subject rights obligations (right to access, right to erasure, right to portability). Relevant for utility customers in GDPR-applicable jurisdictions.']
  green_button_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this authorization enables Green Button Connect My Data (CMD) access for the authorized third party. When true, the authorized party may retrieve interval energy usage data via the Green Button API. Supports DOE Green Button initiative compliance.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device from which the customer submitted the consent or authorization via a digital channel (web portal or mobile app). Used for fraud detection, audit trail, and regulatory proof of digital consent. Null for non-digital channels (paper, IVR, verbal).']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the customer confirmed, reviewed, or re-consented to this authorization. Used to track the currency of consent and trigger re-consent workflows when the review interval has elapsed.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this authorization record was most recently modified in the system. Supports change data capture, audit trails, and incremental lakehouse processing.']
  marketing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications from the utility or its authorized partners. Distinct from data sharing consent. Required for GDPR Article 6 and CCPA opt-in/opt-out compliance.']
  nem_participant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this authorization is associated with a Net Energy Metering (NEM) enrolled customer account. When true, the authorization may cover behind-the-meter (BTM) generation data sharing with aggregators or DERMS providers.']
  paper_form_reference varchar [note: 'Document management reference or scan identifier for the physical paper authorization form submitted by the customer. Populated when consent_channel is \'paper\' or \'written\'. Enables retrieval of the original signed document for audit.']
  puc_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code identifying the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) jurisdiction governing this authorization. Determines which state-specific data privacy and customer authorization rules apply. Required for multi-state utility operations.']
  renewal_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required authorization renewals when renewal_required is true. Common values are 365 (annual) or 730 (biennial) per state PUC requirements. Drives automated renewal reminder workflows in the CIS.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this authorization requires periodic renewal by the customer. When true, the system must generate renewal notifications before the effective_end_date. Supports compliance with state PUC rules requiring periodic re-consent for ongoing data sharing.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer withdrew or revoked this authorization prior to its scheduled end date. Populated only when the customer initiates early termination. Triggers downstream access revocation in CIS, DERMS, and Green Button systems.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this authorization was revoked or withdrawn. Supports regulatory reporting and customer service analytics. Values: customer_request (voluntary withdrawal), account_closure (account terminated), fraud_reported (suspected unauthorized access), regulatory_order (PUC or FERC directive), system_expiry (automated expiry), other.']
  session_token varchar [note: 'Authenticated session token or transaction reference from the digital channel (web portal or mobile app) at the time of consent capture. Provides a cryptographic audit trail linking the consent event to a verified authenticated session. Null for non-digital channels.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this authorization record originated. Identifies whether the record was created in SAP IS-U, Oracle CC&B, the customer web portal, mobile app, IVR system, or via manual data entry. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_reference_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this authorization record in the originating operational system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the CIS system of record.']
  verbal_recording_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the call recording in which verbal consent was captured (e.g., IVR or agent-assisted phone call). Links to the call recording archive for regulatory audit. Populated only when consent_channel is \'verbal\' or \'ivr\'.']

  Note: 'Master entity and SSOT for all customer-granted authorizations, explicit consent events, and data-sharing permissions. Captures third-party access authorizations including authorized party name, authorization type (view-only, full access, notification-only, landlord, property manager, aggregator, DERMS provider), and authorization scope. Captures all consent events including consent for data sharing, marketing communications, Green Button data access, electronic signature agreements, and third-party aggregator authorization. Stores consent type and version, consent channel (web portal, mobile app, IVR, paper, written, electronic, verbal with recording reference), consent date and time, consent text version reference, IP address or session token, authorization start and end dates, revocation/withdrawal date and reason. Required for FERC Order 2222 DER aggregator access, Green Button data sharing compliance, GDPR, CCPA, and state PUC data privacy requirements.'
}

Table customer.incentive_enrollment {
  incentive_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this incentive program enrollment record. Primary key for the association.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the utility customer account enrolled in the incentive program']
  renewable_incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the renewable energy incentive program in which the account is enrolled']
  application_date date [note: 'Date when the customer submitted the application for this incentive program. Used for determining eligibility windows and processing timelines.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique application number assigned when the customer applied for this specific incentive program. Used for customer communication and regulatory tracking.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the incentive program application for this account-program enrollment. Tracks the application lifecycle from submission through final disposition.']
  approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of incentive funding approved for this specific account-program enrollment in USD. Represents the committed program budget allocation for this participant.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this account with the requirements of this specific incentive program. Tracks whether the participant is meeting ongoing program obligations such as REC transfer, interconnection standards, or reporting requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit tracking and data lineage.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the account was officially enrolled in the incentive program following application approval. Marks the start of the enrollment relationship.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this account\'s enrollment in this incentive program. Governs whether payments can be processed and compliance is being monitored.']
  installed_system_size_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual installed capacity in kilowatts (kW) of the renewable energy system for which this account is receiving incentives under this program. Used for calculating incentive amounts and verifying eligibility.']
  interconnection_approval_date date [note: 'Date when utility interconnection approval was granted for the renewable energy system associated with this enrollment. Required for programs where requires_interconnection_approval is true.']
  last_compliance_verification_date date [note: 'Date when compliance with program requirements was last verified for this enrollment. Used for tracking ongoing compliance monitoring schedules.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent incentive payment disbursed to this account for this program enrollment. Used for payment tracking and schedule management.']
  next_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the next incentive payment is scheduled to be disbursed to this account for this program enrollment, based on the payment_schedule and compliance_status.']
  payment_schedule varchar [note: 'Schedule on which incentive payments will be disbursed to this account for this program enrollment. Determines payment frequency and timing for this specific enrollment.']
  rec_transfer_agreement_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has signed the Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) transfer agreement required for this program enrollment. Required for programs where requires_rec_transfer is true.']
  remaining_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining dollar amount of approved incentive funding that has not yet been paid to this account for this program enrollment in USD. Calculated as approved_amount minus total_paid_amount.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Specific renewable energy technology type for which this account is receiving incentives under this program enrollment. Must match one of the eligible_technology_types for the program.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when this account\'s enrollment in this incentive program was terminated, either due to program completion, non-compliance, or voluntary withdrawal. Null for active enrollments.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why this enrollment was terminated. Examples include program_completed, non_compliance, customer_withdrawal, program_expired, system_decommissioned.']
  total_paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount of incentive payments that have been disbursed to this account for this program enrollment to date in USD. Used for budget tracking and payment reconciliation.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment contract between a utility customer account and a renewable energy incentive program. It captures the application, approval, payment tracking, and compliance verification lifecycle for each account\'s participation in each incentive program. Each record links one account to one incentive program with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific enrollment relationship, including application details, approved funding amounts, payment schedules, disbursement tracking, and ongoing compliance status.. Existence Justification: In utility renewable energy operations, a customer account can simultaneously participate in multiple incentive programs (e.g., a residential customer with solar PV enrolled in both an ITC rebate program and an SREC program, plus a separate battery storage incentive). Conversely, each incentive program serves hundreds or thousands of customer accounts. The utility actively manages these enrollments as operational business entities, tracking application workflows, approval processes, payment disbursements, compliance verification, and budget allocation for each account-program combination.'
}

Table customer.premise_asset_connection {
  premise_asset_connection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the premise_asset_connection association']
  metering_point_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific metering point or meter socket associated with this premise-asset connection. Links to metering infrastructure for billing and load monitoring']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the premise receiving utility service']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the utility asset providing service']
  capacity_allocation_kva decimal(12,3) [note: 'Allocated transformer capacity in kilovolt-amperes (kVA) reserved for this premise on this asset. Used for load planning and capacity management when multiple premises share a transformer']
  connection_date date [note: 'Date when this premise was physically connected to this specific asset for service delivery']
  connection_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this service connection (active and energized, inactive/disconnected, temporarily suspended, or pending activation)']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service connection relationship (primary service path, secondary/redundant path, backup connection, or temporary connection during maintenance)']
  disconnection_date date [note: 'Date when this premise was disconnected from this specific asset (due to asset retirement, premise reconfiguration, or grid topology changes). Null if connection is currently active']
  service_voltage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual voltage delivered at this specific premise-asset connection point in volts. May differ from premise service_voltage_class due to transformer configuration or multi-voltage service']

  Note: 'This association product represents the physical service connection between a premise and utility assets. It captures the operational topology of how distribution assets (transformers, poles, service vaults) deliver electric service to customer premises. Each record links one premise to one asset with connection-specific attributes including service voltage, capacity allocation, connection dates, and metering configuration that exist only in the context of this service relationship.. Existence Justification: In utility distribution operations, a single premise is routinely served by multiple physical assets: a primary distribution transformer, potentially a secondary/backup transformer for redundancy, service poles, service vaults, and switchgear. Conversely, a single distribution transformer typically serves 5-50 premises (residential) or multiple commercial premises. The service connection topology is actively managed by distribution engineering and outage management teams, with connection-specific data including service voltage, capacity allocation, connection/disconnection dates, metering point configuration, and connection status (active/backup/temporary).'
}

Table customer.premise_service_contract {
  premise_service_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this premise-vendor service contract record. Primary key.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the premise receiving vendor services under this contract']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor providing services under this contract']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service contract automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless explicitly cancelled. Used for contract management and renewal tracking.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the vendor invoices for services under this contract. Defines the billing cycle for this premise-vendor service relationship.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration or termination date of the service contract. Used for contract renewal tracking and vendor performance evaluation scheduling.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the service contract. Defines when vendor service obligations begin for this premise.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service contract. ACTIVE = contract in force, EXPIRED = contract term ended, TERMINATED = contract cancelled before end date, SUSPENDED = services temporarily halted, PENDING_RENEWAL = approaching expiration and under renewal negotiation.']
  contract_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the service contract in the vendor\'s currency. Represents the committed spend for vendor services at this premise over the contract period. Used for budget tracking and vendor spend analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service contract record was created in the system.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent service visit or maintenance activity performed by the vendor at this premise under this contract. Used for service frequency tracking and compliance monitoring.']
  next_scheduled_service_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned preventive maintenance or service visit by the vendor at this premise. Used for service scheduling and coordination.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor\'s primary service contact assigned to this premise. The technician or account manager responsible for service delivery at this specific location.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the vendor\'s primary service contact for this premise. Used for emergency service requests and routine coordination.']
  response_time_sla_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Service Level Agreement response time in hours. Defines the maximum time the vendor has to respond to a service request or emergency call for this premise. Used for SLA compliance monitoring.']
  service_contract_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the service contract as assigned by procurement or vendor management. Externally known contract reference number used in invoicing and service tickets.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service provided under this contract. Defines the scope of vendor responsibilities for this premise (e.g., HVAC maintenance, generator service, substation maintenance, emergency restoration).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service contract record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the service contract relationship between a premise and a vendor. It captures vendor-provided maintenance and service agreements for specific premises, including HVAC maintenance, generator service, substation maintenance, and emergency restoration services. Each record links one premise to one vendor with contract-specific terms, SLAs, service scope, and commercial terms that exist only in the context of this contractual relationship.. Existence Justification: In utility operations, large commercial and industrial premises routinely have multiple concurrent service contracts with different vendors (e.g., one vendor for HVAC maintenance, another for generator service, a third for substation maintenance). Simultaneously, each vendor serves multiple premises across the utility\'s service territory. The business actively manages these service contracts as operational entities with contract-specific terms, SLAs, service scopes, and performance tracking.'
}

Table customer.customer_dr_enrollment {
  customer_dr_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for customer_dr_enrollment']
  dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this demand response enrollment record. Primary key.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the demand response program in which the premise is enrolled']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the premise enrolled in the demand response program']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Premise-specific override of the baseline calculation methodology used for this enrollment. Allows individual premises to use different baseline methods than the program default when justified by meter configuration, load volatility, or regulatory requirements. If null, inherits from dr_program.baseline_calculation_method.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was created in the system']
  demand_response_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise is currently enrolled in a utility Demand Response (DR) or load control program. Supports DR dispatch eligibility, load curtailment planning, and RTO/ISO demand response market participation reporting. [Moved from premise: This boolean flag on premise indicates whether the premise is enrolled in ANY DR program. This is a derived/aggregated attribute that can be calculated from the existence of active dr_enrollment records. It does not belong on the premise master record—it should be derived from the enrollment association. Removing this flag enforces single source of truth in the enrollment table.]']
  enrolled_load_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Premise-specific committed load reduction capacity in kilowatts for this enrollment. Represents the load curtailment the premise has agreed to provide when DR events are dispatched. Aggregated across all enrollments to calculate program-level enrolled_capacity_actual_mw.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which this premise was enrolled in the DR program. Used for marketing attribution, enrollment cost analysis, and channel effectiveness measurement.']
  enrollment_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this premise enrollment becomes active and the premise is eligible for DR event dispatch and incentive payments. Must fall within the parent dr_program effective date range.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Date when this premise enrollment terminates. Null for open-ended enrollments. Used to track enrollment history and calculate active enrollment counts.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this enrollment. Active indicates premise is eligible for dispatch. Suspended indicates temporary ineligibility (e.g., equipment failure, customer request). Terminated indicates permanent end. Pending Activation indicates enrollment approved but not yet effective.']
  incentive_override_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Premise-specific incentive amount override. If populated, this value supersedes the program-level dr_program.incentive_amount for this enrollment. Used for negotiated rates with large commercial/industrial customers or pilot program participants. Null indicates use of program default.']
  participation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical participation rate percentage for this premise across past DR events in this program. Calculated as (number of events participated / number of events dispatched) * 100. Used for performance tracking, incentive adjustments, and enrollment retention analysis.']
  telemetry_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data (AMI interval data, DER telemetry, or SCADA signals) is enabled for this premise enrollment. Required for direct load control programs and VPP aggregation. Determines whether premise participates in automated dispatch or manual notification-based DR.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment contract between a premise and a demand response program. It captures premise-specific participation terms, load capacity commitments, telemetry configuration, and baseline methodology overrides. Each record links one premise to one DR program with attributes that exist only in the context of this enrollment relationship. SSOT for all premise-level DR participation tracking.. Existence Justification: In utility demand response operations, premises participate in multiple DR programs simultaneously (e.g., a commercial premise enrolled in both an interruptible tariff program and a behavioral DR campaign), and each DR program enrolls thousands of premises across the service territory. The enrollment relationship is an operational business entity that DR program managers actively create, monitor, and terminate, with premise-specific attributes like committed load capacity, telemetry configuration, and baseline methodology overrides.'
}

Table customer.case {
  case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for case']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual support agent assigned to the case.']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the utility meter associated with the case, if applicable.']
  parent_case_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on case (parent_case_id)']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the case.']
  amount_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary amount associated with the case (e.g., fees before taxes).']
  amount_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount after taxes and adjustments.']
  amount_tax decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the case monetary amount.']
  assigned_group varchar [note: 'Internal support group or department responsible for handling the case.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of files or documents attached to the case.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible alphanumeric case number assigned at creation.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the case.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date on which the case was officially closed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the case with relevant regulations.']
  cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final cost incurred to resolve the case.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost associated with resolving the case.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative provided by the customer describing the issue or request.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the case is expected to be resolved.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case has been escalated to a higher support tier.']
  estimated_restoration_time timestamp [note: 'Projected timestamp for service restoration communicated to the customer.']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from an external system (e.g., regulator, partner).']
  field_technician_notes varchar [note: 'Notes entered by field personnel during on‑site work.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case was initially opened.']
  outage_end timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the outage was restored.']
  outage_flag boolean [note: 'True if the case pertains to a service outage.']
  outage_start timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reported outage began.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority indicating urgency.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the case record.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'True if the case is subject to regulatory reporting or compliance.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of how the case was resolved or closed.']
  sla_actual_hours varchar [note: 'Actual time taken to resolve the case in hours.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement target resolution time in hours.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the case was submitted.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the case.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case involves warranty‑covered equipment.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for case. Referenced by case_id.'
}

Table customer.field_order {
  field_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for field_order']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the field work is to be performed.']
  assigned_crew_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the crew assigned to execute the field order.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing account to which charges are applied.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the field order.']
  follow_up_field_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on field_order (follow_up_field_order_id)']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified the field order.']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the meter associated with the field order, if applicable.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the field order.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset related to the field order.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time taken to complete the field work.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when field work was completed.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when field work commenced.']
  amount_adjustments decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of monetary adjustments (taxes, fees, discounts) applied to the field order.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary amount before adjustments for the field order.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net monetary amount after adjustments for the field order.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the field order.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field order was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the field order.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the field order complies with regulatory requirements.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center code for financial tracking of the field order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field order record was first captured in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  dispatch_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispatch the field order to a crew.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time required to complete the field work.']
  external_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an external order system identifier.']
  external_system_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from an external system linked to this field order.']
  field_order_version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the field order is billable to the customer.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Soft delete indicator for the field order record.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the field order is an emergency response.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes related to the field order.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the field order.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field order was created or placed.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the field order indicating urgency.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the field order.']
  required_permits varchar [note: 'List of permits required to perform the field work.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed safety risk level for the field work.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time for the field work.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date and time for the field work.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of service to be performed in the field order.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the field order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the field order record.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tasks to be performed.']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work order based on planning horizon.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for field_order. Referenced by field_order_id.'
}

Table customer.service_level_agreement {
  service_level_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for service_level_agreement']
  superseded_service_level_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on service_level_agreement (superseded_service_level_agreement_id)']
  availability_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target service availability expressed as a percentage.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance obligations tied to the SLA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA record was created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the SLA scope and purpose.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the SLA becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the SLA expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the escalation contact.']
  escalation_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact for SLA escalations.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the escalation contact.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often SLA performance is measured.']
  measurement_units varchar [note: 'Units used for SLA metrics (e.g., percent, seconds).']
  monitoring_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the monitoring contact.']
  monitoring_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person responsible for monitoring SLA performance.']
  monitoring_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the monitoring contact.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the SLA.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the SLA.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied for SLA breach.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the penalty amount.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the SLA.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA is subject to regulatory reporting.']
  resolution_time_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed time to resolve an incident.']
  response_time_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed time to respond to a service request.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Utility service type to which the SLA applies.']
  sla_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the SLA by the business.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SLA.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of service covered by the SLA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SLA record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for service_level_agreement. Referenced by service_level_agreement_id.'
}

Table customer.notification_preference {
  notification_preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for notification_preference']
  superseded_notification_preference_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on notification_preference (superseded_notification_preference_id)']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the notification is delivered.']
  code varchar [note: 'Compact alphanumeric code used to reference the notification preference in downstream systems.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the regulatory regime governing this notification preference.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the preference record was first created in the system.']
