  coverage_area_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic coverage area or feeder segments this crew is responsible for within the feeder territory. May reference mile markers, geographic boundaries, or circuit sections. Used when a feeder spans multiple crew territories or when specialized crews handle specific segments (e.g., underground vs overhead sections).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dispatch_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking for dispatch priority when multiple crews are assigned to the same feeder. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by OMS/ADMS to route emergency work orders to the most appropriate crew based on assignment type, crew location, and current workload.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness assessment.']
  last_work_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent work order completed by this crew on this feeder. Used to track crew activity levels, identify underutilized assignments, and ensure regular crew familiarity with feeder infrastructure.']
  primary_responsibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crew has primary operational responsibility for the feeder (true) or is a backup/secondary assignment (false). Only one crew should have primary responsibility for a feeder at any time for a given assignment type. Used for dispatch routing and accountability.']
  work_order_count_ytd varchar [note: 'Total number of work orders completed by this crew on this feeder in the current calendar year. Used for workload balancing, crew performance assessment, and assignment optimization.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of field crews to distribution feeders for maintenance, inspection, vegetation management, and emergency restoration work. It captures the assignment lifecycle, responsibility type (primary maintenance crew, backup crew, vegetation management crew), coverage area within the feeder territory, and assignment dates. Each record links one feeder to one crew with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment relationship. Critical for dispatch optimization in OMS/ADMS, storm restoration planning, crew workload balancing, and regulatory compliance reporting (vegetation management cycles, inspection frequency).. Existence Justification: In energy utility distribution operations, feeders require maintenance, inspection, and emergency restoration services from multiple specialized crews (line crews for electrical work, vegetation management crews for tree trimming, cable crews for underground sections), and each crew is assigned to multiple feeders within their service territory. The business actively manages these assignments with specific responsibility types (primary/backup), coverage areas, and assignment dates to optimize dispatch routing in OMS/ADMS, ensure regulatory compliance (vegetation management cycles), and coordinate storm restoration efforts. This is an operational assignment process, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table metering.meter {
  meter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the physical metering device. Primary key for the meter entity. System-of-record: Oracle Utilities MDM meter object.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Meters are capitalized assets requiring depreciation tracking per FERC Uniform System of Accounts (Account 370 - Meters). Essential for plant-in-service accounting, RAB valuation, depreciation expense']
  meter_ami_head_end_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.ami_head_end. Business justification: Meters are enrolled in and managed by specific AMI head-end systems. The meter table currently has ami_head_end_system as a STRING attribute, which should be normalized to a FK relationship. This is a']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Meters must comply with regulatory obligations for accuracy (ANSI C12.20), testing frequency, certification. Supports compliance tracking, audit evidence, regulatory reporting on meter accuracy progra']
  to_ami_head_end_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.ami_head_end.ami_head_end_id — Each smart meter is assigned to an AMI head-end system for communication management. Required for data collection scheduling and network management.']
  tou_rate_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.tou_rate_program. Business justification: Meters are configured with specific Time-of-Use (TOU) rate programs that define peak/off-peak periods, CPP event criteria, and interval data requirements. The meter table currently has tou_rate_progra']
  accuracy_class varchar [note: 'ANSI accuracy classification of the meter (e.g., Class 0.2, Class 0.5, Class 1.0, Class 2.0). Lower numbers indicate higher precision. Determines acceptable measurement error tolerance and testing requirements.']
  ami_network_segment varchar [note: 'Identifier of the AMI communication network segment or cell to which this meter is assigned. Used for network planning, troubleshooting, and load balancing across AMI infrastructure.']
  communication_module_serial varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the communication module. Tracked separately from meter serial to support independent module replacement without replacing the entire meter. Used for module-level warranty and troubleshooting.']
  communication_module_type varchar [note: 'Technology type of the embedded or attached communication module enabling remote meter reading. RF Mesh uses radio frequency mesh networks; PLC uses power line carrier; Cellular uses mobile networks; Fiber uses optical networks; None for non-communicating meters.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Application-layer protocol used for meter-to-head-end communication (e.g., ANSI C12.22, DLMS/COSEM, SEP 2.0, Modbus). Determines data format, security features, and interoperability with AMI systems.']
  configuration_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current operational configuration (interval length, TOU program, demand reset, channels) became active. Used for configuration versioning and historical data interpretation. Critical for billing accuracy during configuration changes.']
  configuration_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current operational configuration is scheduled to expire or be replaced. Nullable for open-ended configurations. Used for planned configuration change management and audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meter record was first created in the MDM system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and system integration reconciliation. Distinct from installation date.']
  current_rating_amps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum continuous current the meter can measure in amperes. Typical values: 100A, 200A, 320A. Used to ensure meter capacity matches service load requirements.']
  demand_reset_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'Programmed interval in minutes for resetting the demand register (e.g., 15, 30 minutes). Determines how frequently peak demand is calculated and recorded. Critical for demand billing accuracy.']
  disconnect_switch_status varchar [note: 'Current position of the remote disconnect switch for meters with disconnect capability. Connected indicates service is on; Disconnected indicates service is off; Armed indicates ready for remote command; Not Applicable for meters without disconnect.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the embedded software running on the meter device. Critical for security patching, feature enablement, and compatibility with head-end systems. Tracked for firmware upgrade campaigns.']
  form varchar [note: 'ANSI form designation describing the physical configuration and wiring arrangement of the meter (e.g., Form 2S, Form 12S, Form 16S). Determines socket compatibility and installation requirements.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the meter was physically installed at its current service location. Used for age tracking, warranty calculations, and lifecycle management. Distinct from manufacturing date.']
  interval_length_minutes varchar [note: 'Programmed interval duration in minutes for load profile data collection (e.g., 15, 30, 60 minutes). Determines granularity of consumption data for billing, demand response, and grid analytics. Configurable parameter tracked for versioning.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Assigned IP address for meters using IP-based communication protocols (cellular, fiber). Used for direct meter communication, remote diagnostics, and firmware updates. Confidential to prevent unauthorized access.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in this meter record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance. Updated automatically on any record modification.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the meter in its asset lifecycle. Installed indicates active deployment; In Stock indicates warehouse inventory; In Transit indicates shipment; Retired indicates end-of-life; Failed indicates defective; Under Test indicates lab testing.']
  load_profile_channels_enabled varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of load profile channels currently enabled for data collection (e.g., kWh Delivered, kWh Received, kVARh, Voltage, Current). Defines which measurement streams are actively recorded in interval data.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Hardware MAC address of the communication module network interface. Used for network device identification, AMI head-end provisioning, and communication diagnostics. Confidential to prevent network security risks.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the meter device (e.g., Landis+Gyr, Itron, Sensus, Elster, Aclara). Used for vendor management, warranty tracking, and firmware compatibility.']
  meter_type varchar [note: 'Classification of meter technology and capability level. AMI Smart Meter supports two-way communication and remote operations; Interval Meter records time-series consumption; Demand Meter tracks peak demand; Electromechanical is legacy analog; Electronic is digital non-communicating; Prepaid supports prepayment programs.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model designation identifying the specific meter product line and capabilities. Used for configuration management, firmware updates, and technical specifications lookup.']
  module_firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the firmware running on the communication module, tracked separately from meter firmware. Critical for communication security patches and protocol updates.']
  module_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the communication module. Active indicates normal operation; Inactive indicates disabled but functional; Failed indicates hardware failure; Replaced indicates module swap; Not Installed for non-AMI meters.']
  next_test_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required accuracy test or calibration. Calculated based on regulatory testing intervals (typically 5-15 years depending on meter type and jurisdiction). Used for test planning and compliance tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, field observations, or historical context not captured in structured fields. Used by field technicians and operations staff.']
  number_of_elements varchar [note: 'Count of independent metering elements within the device. Single-phase meters have 1 element; polyphase meters have 2 or 3 elements. Determines measurement capability for multi-phase services.']
  ownership_status varchar [note: 'Legal ownership classification of the meter asset. Utility Owned is standard for most deployments; Customer Owned for customer-purchased meters; Leased for rental arrangements; Third Party for meters owned by service providers.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number under which this meter was procured. Used for procurement tracking, warranty validation, and vendor management. Links to financial and supply chain systems.']
  register_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of the meter register setup including number of dials, multiplier, and measurement units (kWh, kVARh, kW demand). Defines what consumption data the meter captures and how it is displayed.']
  remote_disconnect_capable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the meter has remote service disconnect/reconnect capability. True enables remote service control for collections, move-in/move-out, and emergency response. False requires manual field operations.']
  removal_date date [note: 'Date the meter was removed from service location. Nullable for currently installed meters. Used for lifecycle tracking, inventory management, and historical service point analysis.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the tamper-evident seal applied to the meter cover. Used to detect unauthorized meter access, tampering, and theft. Recorded during installation and verified during inspections.']
  seal_status varchar [note: 'Current condition of the meter seal. Intact indicates no tampering; Broken indicates potential unauthorized access requiring investigation; Missing indicates seal loss; Not Applicable for meters without seals.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number physically stamped on the meter device. Used for asset tracking, warranty claims, and field verification. SSOT for device identity.']
  service_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.service_point. Business justification: A service point can host many meters; the many side (meter) gets a foreign key to the parent service_point. This eliminates the siloed service_point table and provides the needed inbound relationship.']
  signal_strength_baseline_dbm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Baseline communication signal strength measurement in dBm at time of installation or last network optimization. Used to detect communication degradation and plan network improvements. Typical range: -110 to -40 dBm.']
  test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent accuracy test or calibration performed on the meter. Used to track compliance with regulatory testing intervals and identify meters due for testing.']
  voltage_class varchar [note: 'Nominal voltage rating for which the meter is designed and certified. Determines appropriate deployment location (residential 120/240V, commercial 277/480V, primary voltage for large industrial).']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty coverage expires. Used for warranty claim eligibility, replacement planning, and total cost of ownership analysis. Nullable if warranty terms are not date-based.']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical metering device deployed or managed by the utility — smart meters (AMI), interval meters, demand meters, and legacy electromechanical units. Captures device identity (serial number, manufacturer, model), firmware version, meter form factor, voltage class, current rating, number of elements, register configuration, seal status, and AMI head-end system assignment. Includes embedded communication module attributes: module type (RF mesh/PLC/cellular), module serial number, MAC/IP address, signal strength baseline, module firmware version, communication protocol, assigned AMI network segment, and module status — supporting independent module replacement tracking without a separate entity. Also captures the active and historical programmed operational configuration: interval length setting (15/30/60 min), TOU rate program assignment, demand reset interval, load profile channels enabled, remote disconnect/reconnect capability, and configuration effective dates for versioning. SSOT for meter asset identity, communication endpoint, and device configuration within the metering domain; links to the asset domain for full lifecycle management.'
}

Table metering.meter_point {
  meter_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the meter point (service delivery point). This is the primary key representing the logical location where energy consumption is measured, persisting across meter device swaps.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Service points must be assigned to balancing authority control areas for load aggregation, interchange scheduling, ACE calculation, and NERC CPS1/CPS2 compliance reporting. Core operational requiremen']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service points are assigned to cost centers for operational expense tracking and budget allocation by service territory, voltage class, and operational area. Required for monthly O&M cost allocation a']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Service points host DER installations. NEM billing reconciliation, export limit enforcement, and interconnection compliance require linking meter_point to der_registry. Essential for behind-the-meter ']
  distribution_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder circuit serving this meter point. Critical for outage management, load balancing, and SAIDI/SAIFI reliability calculations.']
  distribution_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution transformer serving this meter point. Used for transformer loading analysis and secondary network modeling.']
  lmp_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.lmp_price. Business justification: Distribution planning and rate design require associating service points with their default pricing nodes to calculate location-based energy costs and design locational tariffs. Essential for cost-of-']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Service points subject to net metering obligations, interconnection standards, service quality requirements. Enables tariff compliance verification, regulatory reporting by service class, obligation t']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical premise where this meter point is located. Links to the customer domain premise entity.']
  service_point_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Service points reference physical infrastructure assets (poles, pad-mount equipment, service cabinets) where metering is installed. Field operations, outage restoration, and capital planning require l']
  ami_collector_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the AMI data collector or concentrator device that aggregates meter data from this service point.']
  ami_network_segment varchar [note: 'AMI communication network segment or cell serving this meter point. Determines data collection routing and network performance monitoring.']
  contracted_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Contracted or subscribed service capacity in kilowatts for this meter point. Applicable to large commercial and industrial customers.']
  cpp_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meter point is eligible for critical peak pricing programs. Used for demand response event targeting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meter point record was first created in the MDM system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite data quality score (0-100) reflecting completeness, accuracy, and consistency of meter point attributes. Used for data stewardship prioritization.']
  der_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Nameplate capacity in kilowatts of distributed generation or storage interconnected at this meter point. Used for hosting capacity analysis.']
  der_interconnection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a distributed energy resource is interconnected at this meter point. Triggers special metering and grid integration requirements.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the meter point in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, outage analysis, and field crew navigation.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the meter point in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, outage analysis, and field crew navigation.']
  interval_data_collection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meter point supports interval data collection (15-minute, 30-minute, or hourly). Required for TOU billing and demand response.']
  interval_length_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of each measurement interval in minutes for interval data collection (typically 15, 30, or 60). Determines data granularity for analytics.']
  last_meter_reading_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful meter reading for this service point. Used for billing cycle validation and data quality monitoring.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meter point record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  load_profile_class varchar [note: 'Customer segment classification determining consumption patterns, rate structures, and demand response eligibility. Critical for load forecasting and grid planning.']
  meter_reading_cycle varchar [note: 'Two-digit billing cycle code determining the scheduled meter reading date. Used for billing run scheduling and route optimization.']
  meter_reading_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which meter readings are collected for billing purposes. AMI-enabled points support continuous interval data collection.']
  net_metering_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether net energy metering is enabled for this service point due to behind-the-meter generation (solar, wind, CHP).']
  network_topology_node varchar [note: 'Logical node identifier in the electrical network topology model. Used by ADMS and DMS systems for network analysis and switching operations.']
  peak_demand_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum recorded demand in kilowatts at this meter point. Used for capacity planning, rate design, and demand charge billing.']
  phase_configuration varchar [note: 'Electrical phase configuration of the service connection. Determines metering channel requirements and load balancing considerations.']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Tariff rate schedule code applicable to this meter point. Determines pricing structure, TOU periods, and billing determinants.']
  service_activation_date date [note: 'Date when service was first activated at this meter point. Marks the beginning of the service point lifecycle.']
  service_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the meter point location. Used for field crew dispatch and outage management.']
  service_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number at the meter point location.']
  service_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the meter point is located. Used for jurisdictional rate determination and regulatory reporting.']
  service_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the meter point location. Determines applicable national regulatory frameworks.']
  service_deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when service was permanently deactivated at this meter point. Null for active service points.']
  service_point_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the service delivery point. Used in customer communications, billing, and field operations.']
  service_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the meter point. Active indicates the point is in service and generating consumption data.']
  service_point_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commodity delivered at this meter point. Determines applicable rate schedules and measurement requirements.']
  service_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code for the meter point service address. Used for geographic analysis and service territory validation.']
  service_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the meter point location. Determines applicable PUC jurisdiction and regulatory requirements.']
  service_voltage_level varchar [note: 'Voltage classification at which energy is delivered to this meter point. Determines metering accuracy requirements and applicable tariffs.']
  service_voltage_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage in volts at which service is delivered (e.g., 120V, 240V, 480V, 13.8kV). Used for power quality monitoring and equipment compatibility verification.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the authoritative source system for this meter point record. Supports multi-system data integration and master data governance.']
  tou_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether time-of-use rate pricing is active for this meter point. Requires interval data collection and TOU-capable metering.']
  vee_processing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether meter data from this point undergoes VEE processing for data quality assurance before billing and analytics.']

  Note: 'Represents the physical service delivery point (premise) at which a meter is installed — the logical location where energy consumption is measured. Captures service point identifier, premise address, GPS coordinates, service voltage level, load class (residential, commercial, industrial), distribution feeder and transformer association, network topology node, and AMI network segment. Distinct from the meter device itself: a meter point persists even when meters are swapped. SSOT for the measurement location concept in the metering domain.'
}

Table metering.meter_installation {
  meter_installation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the meter installation record. Primary key for the meter installation entity.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Installation work audited for safety compliance, metering accuracy, proper sealing per regulatory standards. Supports audit trail for regulatory inspections, SOX controls over revenue metering, eviden']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Installation labor and materials are charged to cost centers for field service cost tracking. Essential for FERC Form 1 regulatory reporting (Account 586 - Meter Expenses) and operational budget varia']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_service_agreement. Business justification: Meter installations are performed under specific service agreements that define rate schedules, metering requirements, and billing start dates. Supports billing determinism, regulatory compliance for ']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Physical meter installations at DER sites require bidirectional metering equipment. DER commissioning verification and permission-to-operate inspections link meter installation records to registered D']
  installation_capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: New meter installations are often part of capital projects (AMI deployment, service territory expansion, voltage upgrades). Required for capital project cost accumulation, WBS tracking, and rate case ']
  meter_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order that authorized and tracked the meter installation activity. Links to asset management and field service systems.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the premise where the meter is physically installed. Provides geographic and customer context.']
  primary_meter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field technician or crew who performed the meter installation. Used for quality tracking and accountability.']
  primary_meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical meter device that was installed at the meter point. Links to the meter asset registry.']
  primary_meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the meter point (service point) where the meter is installed. The logical location for energy measurement.']
  primary_meter_removal_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order that authorized and tracked the meter removal activity. Links to asset management and field service systems.']
  to_meter_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.meter.meter_id — Every installation record MUST reference the meter device being installed. This is the core association that enables meter-to-premise traceability. Without this FK, you cannot determine which meter is']
  to_meter_point_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.meter_point.meter_point_id — Every installation record MUST reference the meter point (location) where the meter is installed. This completes the meter-device-to-location association that is the foundation of the metering domain.']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.trade. Business justification: Behind-the-meter generation contracts and renewable PPAs require tracking which meter installation measures energy delivery for specific trade contracts. Critical for contract settlement, compliance v']
  accuracy_class varchar [note: 'Meter accuracy classification per ANSI C12.20 standard. Lower values indicate higher precision. Class 0.2 and 0.5 are used for revenue-grade metering.']
  ami_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meter installation supports AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure) remote reading and two-way communication. True for smart meters with network connectivity.']
  bidirectional_metering_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meter can measure energy flow in both directions (consumption and generation). Required for NEM (Net Energy Metering) and DER (Distributed Energy Resource) customers.']
  billing_end_date date [note: 'Date when billing ends for this meter installation. Used for final bill calculation and account closure processing.']
  billing_start_date date [note: 'Date when billing begins for this meter installation. May differ from installation_date due to service agreement terms or proration rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the meter installation record was first created in the MDM (Meter Data Management) system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  ct_ratio varchar [note: 'Current Transformer ratio in effect for this installation (e.g., 200:5, 400:5). Used for high-voltage metering where direct measurement is not feasible.']
  end_reading decimal(18,3) [note: 'Meter register reading captured at the time of removal. Used to calculate final consumption and validate billing for the installation period.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the meter was physically installed at the meter point. Marks the start of the installation period.']
  installation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for technician notes captured during installation. May include site conditions, access issues, or special configuration details.']
  installation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the meter installation record. Used for external reference and work order tracking.']
  installation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason for the meter installation. Used for program tracking, cost allocation, and regulatory reporting.']
  installation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the meter installation. Active indicates the meter is currently in service at the meter point.']
  installation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the meter installation was completed. Used for interval data alignment and VEE (Validation, Estimation, and Editing) processing.']
  installation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the installation based on its intended duration and purpose. Permanent installations are standard service; temporary installations support construction or events.']
  meter_location_code varchar [note: 'Physical location classification of the meter at the premise. Used for field service routing, safety planning, and AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure) signal strength analysis.']
  multiplier decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier factor applied to meter readings to calculate actual consumption. Used when Current Transformer (CT) or Potential Transformer (PT) ratios are in effect.']
  phase_configuration varchar [note: 'Electrical phase configuration of the service. Single-phase for residential; three-phase for commercial and industrial customers.']
  pt_ratio varchar [note: 'Potential Transformer ratio in effect for this installation (e.g., 7200:120). Used in conjunction with CT ratio for accurate high-voltage metering.']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Rate schedule in effect for this meter installation. Determines pricing structure (flat, TOU, CPP) and billing determinants.']
  reading_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the meter readings. Typically kWh (Kilowatt-Hour) for electric meters, therms or ccf for gas meters. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kWh|MWh|kW|kVAh|kVArh|therms|ccf|mcf — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  register_count varchar [note: 'Number of registers (dials) on the meter. Multi-register meters support TOU (Time of Use) and demand metering programs.']
  remote_disconnect_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meter has remote service disconnect/reconnect capability. Used for credit management and demand response programs.']
  removal_date date [note: 'Date when the meter was removed from the meter point. Marks the end of the installation period. Null for active installations.']
  removal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for technician notes captured during removal. May include meter condition, seal integrity, or reasons for discrepancies in final readings.']
  removal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason for the meter removal. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, failure analysis, and warranty claims.']
  removal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the meter was removed. Used for final interval data cutoff and back-billing calculations.']
  seal_number_1 varchar [note: 'Primary tamper-evident seal number applied to the meter at installation. Used for security and regulatory audit purposes.']
  seal_number_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary tamper-evident seal number applied to the meter at installation. Provides additional security for high-value or critical installations.']
  service_amperage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum service amperage rating at the meter point in amps. Determines meter sizing and load capacity.']
  service_voltage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal service voltage at the meter point in volts. Used for meter configuration, safety planning, and load analysis.']
  start_reading decimal(18,3) [note: 'Meter register reading captured at the time of installation. Used as the baseline for consumption calculations during this installation period.']
  test_date date [note: 'Date when the meter was last tested for accuracy before or after installation. Used for regulatory compliance and quality assurance.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent meter accuracy test. Passed indicates the meter meets accuracy standards; failed triggers meter replacement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the meter installation record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  vee_profile_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the VEE (Validation, Estimation, and Editing) rule set applied to interval data from this installation. Determines data quality thresholds and estimation algorithms.']

  Note: 'Tracks the active and historical association between a meter device and a meter point — the physical installation record. Captures installation date, removal date, meter reading at installation (start index), meter reading at removal (end index), installation technician, seal numbers applied, multiplier/CT ratio in effect, and installation status. Enables meter-to-premise traceability for billing, VEE, and regulatory audit. Supports meter swap history and back-billing calculations.'
}

Table metering.interval_reading {
  interval_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interval reading record. Primary key for raw interval energy consumption and demand data collected from AMI smart meters.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Interval consumption data feeds real-time control area load calculations for AGC signals, state estimation, and balancing authority settlement. Required for NERC compliance and operational balancing.']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing cycle to which this interval reading contributes. Links interval data to the billing period for invoice generation and revenue recognition.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Interval data from DER meters feeds REC issuance, curtailment verification, and generation forecasting. REC certificate generation process requires validated interval readings linked to specific DER f']
  interval_meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the smart meter device that generated this interval reading. Links to the physical AMI meter asset.']
  lmp_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.lmp_price. Business justification: Raw interval consumption data must be priced using LMP at the meter\'s pricing node for preliminary settlement calculations before VEE processing. Required for real-time cost allocation and interval bi']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service point (delivery location) where energy consumption was measured. Represents the physical location of energy delivery.']
  to_meter_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.meter.meter_id — Raw interval readings must reference the meter device that produced them. This is the fundamental data provenance link for all consumption data.']
  channel_number varchar [note: 'Channel identifier on the meter device. Multi-channel meters track multiple measurement types (kWh delivered, kWh received, kW demand, voltage, power factor). Typically 1-99.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the interval data was collected. AMI_PUSH = meter initiated transmission, AMI_POLL = head-end system requested, MANUAL = field reading, ESTIMATED = system generated, SCADA = real-time system, IMPORT = bulk file load.']
  collection_run_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch collection run or polling cycle that retrieved this reading. Groups readings collected in the same operational batch for traceability and reprocessing.']
  correction_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the reason for manual correction or substitution of the original reading value. Examples: METER_ERROR, COMM_FAILURE, CUSTOMER_DISPUTE, DATA_QUALITY. Empty if no correction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interval reading record was first inserted into the MDM system. Audit field for data lineage and record creation tracking.']
  data_completeness_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of expected interval data received for the associated collection period. 100.00 = complete, <100.00 = partial data. Used for data quality monitoring and VEE prioritization.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system that provided this interval reading. Examples: AMI_HEAD_END, SCADA_HISTORIAN, MANUAL_ENTRY, BILLING_SYSTEM. Supports multi-source MDM environments.']
  daylight_saving_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether daylight saving time was in effect during this interval. True = DST active, False = standard time. Critical for accurate time-of-use rate application and demand response event timing.']
  demand_response_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a demand response event was active during this interval. True = DR event in progress, False = normal operation. Used for curtailment measurement and settlement.']
  estimation_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the estimation algorithm applied if the reading was estimated. Examples: LIKE_DAY, LINEAR_INTERPOLATION, AVERAGE_USAGE, ZERO_FILL. Empty for actual meter readings.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the measurement interval in minutes. Common values: 15 (quarter-hourly), 30 (half-hourly), 60 (hourly). Supports variable interval lengths for different meter configurations.']
  interval_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the end of the measurement interval. Defines the close of the accumulation period for this reading.']
  interval_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the beginning of the measurement interval. Represents the real-world event time when the meter began accumulating energy for this interval. Typically aligned to 15-minute or 60-minute boundaries.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this interval reading record. Tracks modifications during VEE processing, manual corrections, or reprocessing events.']
  load_profile_segment varchar [note: 'Customer load profile classification segment for this service point. Used for load forecasting, rate design, and demand response targeting. Examples: RESIDENTIAL_STANDARD, COMMERCIAL_SMALL, INDUSTRIAL_LARGE.']
  meter_read_status varchar [note: 'Status of the meter read operation that produced this interval. SUCCESS = complete read, PARTIAL = incomplete data, FAILED = read failure, TIMEOUT = communication timeout, COMM_ERROR = network error.']
  net_metering_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service point is enrolled in net energy metering (solar or DER generation). True = NEM active, False = standard consumption-only metering. Affects billing credit calculations.']
  original_reading_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'Original raw reading value before any estimation, substitution, or manual correction. Preserved for audit trail and comparison with corrected values. Null if no correction occurred.']
  power_outage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a power outage was detected during this interval based on meter status flags or zero-consumption patterns. True = outage detected, False = normal operation.']
  quality_code varchar [note: 'Data quality indicator for the raw interval reading. RAW = actual meter reading, ESTIMATED = calculated by estimation algorithm, SUBSTITUTED = replaced due to validation failure, EDITED = manually corrected, REJECTED = failed validation, MISSING = no data received.']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Rate schedule or tariff code in effect for this service point at the time of the interval. Used to determine applicable TOU (Time of Use) or CPP (Critical Peak Pricing) rates for billing.']
  reading_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'Raw numeric value of the interval measurement as collected from the meter. Represents energy consumption (kWh) or demand (kW) depending on channel configuration. Immutable raw value before VEE processing.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the interval reading was received by the head-end system or MDM platform. Used for latency analysis and data freshness monitoring. Distinct from the interval measurement time.']
  reprocessing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interval reading has been reprocessed after initial load. True = reprocessed due to correction or VEE re-run, False = original processing. Supports data lineage tracking.']
  reverse_flow_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether energy flowed in reverse direction (customer generation exported to grid) during this interval. True = export/generation, False = import/consumption. Critical for NEM and DER settlement.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature at the service point location during the interval, if available from weather data integration. Used for weather normalization and load forecasting. Null if not available.']
  time_zone_offset varchar [note: 'UTC offset for the interval timestamps in ISO 8601 format (e.g., -05:00, +00:00). Ensures correct temporal alignment across service territories and daylight saving transitions.']
  tou_bucket varchar [note: 'Time-of-use pricing period classification for this interval based on rate schedule and timestamp. Determines the applicable energy rate tier for billing calculations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the reading value. kWh (kilowatt-hour) for energy consumption, kW (kilowatt) for demand, kVArh for reactive energy, kVAr for reactive demand, kVAh for apparent energy, kVA for apparent demand.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this interval reading record. Supports audit trail and accountability for manual edits or system corrections.']
  validation_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific validation rule or exception that flagged this reading during VEE processing. Examples: HIGH_USAGE, NEGATIVE_VALUE, MISSING_INTERVAL, SPIKE_DETECTED. Empty if no exceptions.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of VEE (Validation, Estimation, and Editing) processing for this raw reading. PENDING = awaiting validation, VALIDATED = passed validation rules, FAILED = validation exceptions detected, BYPASSED = validation skipped.']
  voltage_level varchar [note: 'Voltage classification of the service point where this interval was measured. LOW = residential/small commercial (<1kV), MEDIUM = commercial/industrial (1-35kV), HIGH = large industrial (35-230kV), TRANSMISSION = bulk power (>230kV).']

  Note: 'Raw interval energy consumption and demand data collected from AMI smart meters at sub-hourly or hourly granularity (typically 15-min or 60-min intervals). Stores channel identifier, interval start timestamp, interval end timestamp, raw kWh or kW value, quality code (raw/estimated/substituted), data collection method (AMI push, manual, estimated), head-end system receipt timestamp, and collection run reference. This is the foundational time-series dataset feeding Oracle Utilities MDM VEE processing, billing, demand response, and grid operations. Immutable raw record — validated versions are stored in validated_interval_reading. High-volume transactional entity; typical utility processes millions of interval records daily.'
}

Table metering.validated_interval_reading {
  validated_interval_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the validated interval reading record. Primary key for this entity representing a single VEE-processed meter reading interval.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Interval data validation processes audited for VEE rule compliance, estimation methodology, data quality standards. Provides audit evidence for billing accuracy, regulatory data quality reviews, SOX t']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: VEE-validated readings are the authoritative source for control area load profiles used in state estimation, load forecasting, and balancing authority financial settlement. Regulatory and operational ']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing cycle that this interval reading contributes to. Links interval data to the billing period for invoice generation.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: VEE-processed interval data from DER meters supports PPA settlement calculations, RPS compliance reporting, and curtailment event compensation. Monthly PPA settlements require validated generation dat']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand response event that was active during this interval, if applicable. Null if no DR event was in effect.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific outage event record if this interval was affected by a service interruption. Null if no outage occurred.']
  interval_reading_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source raw interval reading record from which this validated reading was derived. Establishes lineage from raw to validated data.']
  lmp_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.lmp_price. Business justification: Settlement systems match validated interval consumption with LMP at the same timestamp and pricing node to calculate accurate energy costs for billing and cost allocation. This join happens in every w']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical meter device that captured this interval reading. Links to the meter asset registry.']
  primary_interval_reading_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.interval_reading.interval_reading_id — Each validated interval reading must trace back to its source raw interval reading for audit trail and VEE processing lineage. Regulatory requirement for billing data integrity.']
  primary_meter_point_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.meter_point.meter_point_id — Validated interval data is associated with the meter point (not just the meter device) because billing and downstream consumers need location-based consumption. This is the SSOT consumption record key']
  primary_validated_employee_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the user or system process that performed manual editing on this reading. Null for automated VEE processing.']
  primary_vee_rule_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.vee_rule.vee_rule_id — Each validated reading should reference the VEE rule that was applied during validation/estimation. Required for regulatory audit of data quality processing.']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate schedule (tariff) that was active for this service point during this interval. Used to determine applicable TOU or CPP pricing periods.']
  validated_meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service delivery point (premise connection) where energy consumption was measured. The logical metering location.']
  validated_vee_rule_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.vee_rule.vee_rule_id — Each validated interval reading references the VEE rule that was applied during validation/estimation. Critical for audit trail and understanding how data quality was assured.']
  vee_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: VEE processing operations (meter data management, validation software, analyst labor) have associated costs allocated to cost centers. Required for MDM operational cost tracking, billing quality assur']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The workflow approval state for this validated reading. Indicates whether the reading has been reviewed and approved for downstream consumption by billing and analytics systems.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this validated reading was approved for billing and operational use. Null if not yet approved.']
  billing_quality_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this reading meets the quality standards required for billing purposes. True indicates the reading is approved for billing calculations.']
  channel_number varchar [note: 'The specific meter channel or register from which this reading was captured. Multi-channel meters track different measurement types on separate channels.']
  consumption_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'The validated energy consumption for this interval measured in kilowatt-hours. This is the authoritative billing-quality consumption value after VEE processing.']
  cpp_event_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this interval occurred during a declared Critical Peak Pricing event. True indicates CPP premium rates apply.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this validated interval reading record was first created in the MDM system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A composite quality score (0-100) calculated by the VEE engine based on validation rules, estimation confidence, and data completeness. Higher scores indicate higher quality.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system or channel from which the raw interval data was collected before VEE processing.']
  demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum instantaneous power demand recorded during this interval, measured in kilowatts. Used for demand-based billing and capacity planning.']
  edit_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text notes provided by the editor explaining the context or justification for manual edits to this reading.']
  edit_reason_code varchar [note: 'The business reason code explaining why this reading was manually edited. Examples include meter malfunction, data transmission error, or customer dispute resolution.']
  estimation_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A numerical score (0-100) indicating the confidence level in the estimated value. Higher scores indicate greater confidence in the estimation accuracy.']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'The algorithm or technique used to estimate this reading if the original data was missing or invalid. Set to \'none\' for non-estimated readings.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'The specific exception rule code that was triggered during VEE processing. Examples include high-usage threshold breach, negative consumption, or data gap detection.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this reading triggered any VEE exception rules requiring manual review. True indicates the reading requires analyst attention.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The length of the measurement interval in minutes. Common values include 15, 30, or 60 minutes depending on meter configuration and rate program requirements.']
  interval_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this measurement interval ended. Defines the end boundary of the consumption period.']
  interval_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this measurement interval began. Defines the start boundary of the consumption period.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this validated interval reading record was most recently updated or edited.']
  meter_read_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the meter reading was obtained: automatically via AMI, manually by field personnel, estimated by system, or provided by customer.']
  original_consumption_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'The raw, unmodified consumption value before VEE processing. Preserved for audit trail and comparison purposes. Null if no original value existed.']
  outage_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a power outage was recorded at this service point during this interval. True indicates zero consumption due to service interruption.']
  power_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'The ratio of real power to apparent power, ranging from 0 to 1. Indicates the efficiency of power usage and may trigger power factor penalties or credits.']
  reactive_power_kvar decimal(18,3) [note: 'The reactive power component measured in kilovolt-amperes reactive. Used for power factor calculations and grid stability analysis.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the meter physically recorded this interval data. May differ from interval_end_timestamp in cases of delayed meter reads or backfill scenarios.']
  temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'The ambient temperature in degrees Fahrenheit recorded at the associated weather station during this interval. Used for load forecasting and weather normalization.']
  tou_period_code varchar [note: 'The time-of-use pricing period classification for this interval (e.g., on-peak, off-peak, shoulder). Determines the rate tier applied for billing.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The measurement unit for the primary consumption value. Standardizes interpretation of numeric readings across different meter types and configurations.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'The quality status assigned by the VEE process indicating whether the reading is original valid data, was estimated, edited, substituted, flagged as suspect, or missing.']
  vee_processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the VEE process completed validation and transformation of this reading into its final billing-quality state.']
  vee_run_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the VEE processing batch run that produced this validated reading. Enables traceability of which VEE execution cycle processed this data.']
  voltage_volts decimal(10,2) [note: 'The average voltage level measured during this interval in volts. Used for power quality monitoring and voltage regulation analysis.']
  weather_station_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the weather station used for temperature normalization and weather-adjusted load analysis for this service point location.']

  Note: 'VEE-processed (Validation, Estimation, and Editing) interval data representing the authoritative, billing-quality energy consumption record for each meter point and interval. Stores validated kWh, kW demand, reactive power (kVAR), power factor, VEE rule applied, estimation method (if estimated), editor identifier, validation status (valid/estimated/edited/substituted), and VEE processing run reference. This is the SSOT for consumption data consumed by billing, demand response, and grid operations domains. Distinct from raw interval_reading by lifecycle stage and quality assurance.'
}

Table metering.meter_read {
  meter_read_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the meter read record. Primary key. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER (scalar meter reading event with lifecycle and scheduling governance).']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account responsible for this service point. Enables customer-level consumption reporting.']
  ami_event_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier from the AMI head-end system for this read event. Used to correlate the scalar read with interval data and AMI system logs. Null for non-AMI reads.']
  billing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing run that consumed this meter read for invoice calculation. Null if not yet billed. Used for billing audit and revenue assurance.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Aggregated meter reads feed control area load profiles for interchange accounting and NERC reporting. Standard practice for balancing authority operations and compliance.']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing cycle for which this read was captured. Links the read to the billing run and invoice generation process.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Register-based reads from DER meters support NEM annual true-up calculations, monthly generation reporting, and capacity factor verification. NEM true-up process requires cumulative meter reads linked']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical meter device from which this reading was captured. Links to the meter asset registry.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise where the meter is installed. Enables geographic and customer aggregation of reads.']
  primary_meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logical service point (delivery point) associated with this meter read. Used for billing and consumption analysis.']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate schedule (tariff) in effect for this service point at the time of the read. Used to determine Time-of-Use (TOU) periods, Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) applicability, and billing determinants.']
  reader_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (field technician or meter reader) who captured the reading. Null for automated AMI/AMR reads. Used for workforce productivity analysis and audit trails.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific register on the meter (e.g., kWh delivered, kWh received, kVArh). Multi-register meters have multiple read records per read event.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the meter reading route assignment for manual reads. Defines the geographic sequence and schedule for field crews. Null for automated reads.']
  to_meter_point_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.meter_point.meter_point_id — Scalar meter reads must reference the meter point where the read was taken. Essential for billing cycle consumption calculation.']
  vee_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VEE (Validation, Estimation, and Editing) rule that processed this read. Links to the validation rule configuration for audit and troubleshooting.']
  average_daily_usage decimal(18,3) [note: 'Consumption divided by consumption days. Expressed in kWh per day or MWh per day. Used for customer usage profiling, demand response eligibility, and anomaly detection.']
  billed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meter read has been used in a billing run and invoice generation. True if billed; False if pending or unbilled. Used to prevent duplicate billing and support billing cycle reconciliation.']
  consumption_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between the current read date and the previous read date. Used to normalize consumption for billing and to calculate average daily usage.']
  consumption_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated energy consumption for the period (current read value minus previous read value). Expressed in kilowatt-hours (kWh) or megawatt-hours (MWh). This is the billable quantity fed to the billing domain.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this meter read record was first created in the MDM (Meter Data Management) system. Used for data lineage and audit trails.']
  demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Peak demand recorded during the read period, expressed in kilowatts (kW) or megawatts (MW). Captured for commercial and industrial customers on demand-based rate schedules. Null for residential meters without demand registers.']
  demand_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the peak demand occurred during the read period. Used for Time-of-Use (TOU) and Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) rate application.']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or rule used to estimate the read value when actual read was unavailable. Examples: HIST_AVG (historical average), PREV_YEAR (same period prior year), PRO_RATA (prorated from partial period), ADL (average daily load). Null for actual reads.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any exception or anomaly detected during read capture or validation. Examples: HIGH_USAGE, LOW_USAGE, ZERO_CONSUMPTION, NEGATIVE_CONSUMPTION, TAMPER, COMM_FAILURE. Used to trigger investigation workflows.']
  multiplier decimal(10,4) [note: 'Constant multiplier applied to the raw register reading to calculate actual consumption. Used for current transformer (CT) and potential transformer (PT) ratios on high-voltage meters. Default is 1.0 for direct-connected meters.']
  previous_read_date date [note: 'Date of the prior valid read for this register. Used to calculate the consumption period length (days between reads) and validate read sequence.']
  previous_read_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'The register value from the prior valid read. Used to calculate consumption (current read minus previous read) and detect anomalies such as reverse flow or register rollover.']
  read_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the recurring read schedule pattern. Examples: M01 (monthly cycle 1), B15 (bi-monthly cycle 15), D (daily), W (weekly). Aligns with billing cycle and route planning.']
  read_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the meter reading was captured. Principal business event timestamp for billing cycle alignment and consumption period determination.']
  read_frequency varchar [note: 'The recurring interval at which reads are scheduled for this meter. Monthly: standard residential. Bi-monthly: some residential jurisdictions. Daily: large commercial/industrial or AMI. On-demand: special reads only.']
  read_method varchar [note: 'Technical method used to capture the reading. Optical: infrared port. RF: radio frequency. PLC: power line carrier. Cellular: mobile network. Visual: technician reads display. Handheld: portable reader device.']
  read_quality_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the quality or exception condition of the read. Examples: OK (normal), EST (estimated), REV (reverse flow), OVF (overflow), PWR (power outage), TMR (tamper detected). Aligns with ANSI C12.19 quality flags.']
  read_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason for the read event. Examples: BILL (billing cycle), MOVE (move-in/move-out), DISC (disconnect), RECON (reconnect), INSP (inspection), COMP (complaint investigation). Used for operational reporting and audit.']
  read_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the meter read, often formatted as meter number plus read date. Used for external reference and audit trails.']
  read_source varchar [note: 'Method by which the reading was obtained. AMI: Advanced Metering Infrastructure (two-way automated). AMR: Automated Meter Reading (one-way drive-by). Manual: field technician on-site. Estimated: calculated by VEE (Validation, Estimation, and Editing) process. Customer: self-reported. Remote: handheld or mobile device.']
  read_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the meter read. Valid: passed VEE (Validation, Estimation, and Editing) and accepted for billing. Estimated: calculated value pending actual read. Suspect: flagged for review. Failed: read attempt unsuccessful. Pending_validation: awaiting VEE processing. Corrected: replaced by subsequent correction read.']
  read_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the meter reading was captured, including time zone. Used for interval boundary alignment and AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure) event correlation.']
  read_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the read event. Scheduled: routine billing cycle read. Special: off-cycle read for move-in/move-out or investigation. Final: account closure. Initial: new service activation. Test: commissioning or validation. Correction: replacement of erroneous prior read.']
  read_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the read value. Common values: kWh (kilowatt-hour), MWh (megawatt-hour), kVArh (kilovolt-ampere reactive hour) for electric; CCF, MCF, Therms for gas; Gallons for water. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kWh|MWh|kVArh|MVArh|kW|MW|kVA|MVA|CCF|MCF|Therms|Gallons — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  read_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'The cumulative register value captured at the read timestamp. For energy meters, typically kilowatt-hours (kWh) or megawatt-hours (MWh). This is the raw scalar reading before consumption calculation.']
  register_digits varchar [note: 'Number of digits on the meter register display. Used to detect register rollover (e.g., a 5-digit register rolls from 99999 to 00000). Typical values: 5, 6, 7.']
  register_rollover_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the register rolled over during this read period (current read value is less than previous read value due to register capacity limit). True if rollover detected; False otherwise.']
  scheduled_read_date date [note: 'The planned date for this meter read as defined by the read schedule. Used to measure on-time read performance and identify late or early reads.']
  tou_period varchar [note: 'The TOU rate period for this register reading. On-peak: highest rate hours. Off-peak: lowest rate hours. Shoulder: mid-tier hours. Super-off-peak: ultra-low rate hours (e.g., overnight EV charging). Null for non-TOU registers.']
  tou_register_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this register captures Time-of-Use (TOU) segmented consumption (e.g., on-peak, off-peak, shoulder). True for TOU registers; False for cumulative registers. TOU meters have multiple read records per read event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this meter read record was last modified. Tracks VEE corrections, status changes, and data quality improvements.']

  Note: 'Scalar (register-level) meter reading record and associated read scheduling — capturing both the cumulative register value at a specific point in time and the recurring schedule governing when reads are collected. For readings: stores read date, read time, register identifier, read value, read type (scheduled/special/final/initial), read source (AMR/AMI/manual/estimated), reader employee reference, and read quality flag. For scheduling: defines read cycle (monthly/bi-monthly/daily), read method (AMI automated/manual route), scheduled read date, route assignment, billing cycle alignment, schedule code, and active status. Drives field crew routing for manual reads and AMI polling schedules for automated collection. Feeds billing domain consumption calculation. Distinct from interval_reading (which is time-series) — this is the periodic scalar snapshot and its collection governance.'
}

Table metering.vee_rule {
  vee_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VEE rule. Primary key for the VEE rule reference entity.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: VEE rules implement regulatory obligations for data quality, estimation limits, validation thresholds mandated by state PUC orders. Enables compliance mapping, regulatory justification for VEE algorit']
  algorithm_type varchar [note: 'The computational algorithm or method used by the rule to validate, estimate, or edit meter data (e.g., threshold-based, statistical analysis, linear regression, interpolation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: threshold|statistical|regression|interpolation|substitution|comparison|pattern_matching — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  applicable_meter_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of meter types to which this VEE rule applies (e.g., AMI electric, AMI gas, interval demand, TOU residential). Defines the scope of rule application within the meter population.']
  applicable_rate_schedules varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of rate schedule codes to which this VEE rule applies. Enables rate-specific validation and estimation logic for TOU and CPP programs.']
  applicable_service_types varchar [note: 'Type of utility service to which this VEE rule applies (electric, gas, water, steam). Ensures rules are applied to appropriate commodity data streams.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether changes to this VEE rule require formal approval before activation. True for rules impacting billing or regulatory compliance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved this VEE rule for production use. Applicable when approval_required_flag is true.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VEE rule was formally approved for production use. Supports governance and compliance documentation.']
  audit_trail_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed audit logging is enabled for this rule, capturing every application of the rule and its outcomes for compliance and troubleshooting.']
  billing_quality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data passing this VEE rule is considered billing-quality and suitable for revenue calculation. True if the rule ensures billing-grade accuracy.']
  confidence_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum confidence level (as a percentage) required for estimated values to be accepted as billing-quality. Used in statistical estimation algorithms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VEE rule record was first created in the MDM system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the VEE rule is no longer active. Nullable for open-ended rules. Supports rule lifecycle management and historical analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the VEE rule becomes active and is applied to meter data. Supports temporal rule management and regulatory compliance.']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'Specific estimation technique used when the rule type is estimation. Defines how missing or invalid interval data is calculated (e.g., linear interpolation, historical average, same-day-last-week). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear_interpolation|historical_average|same_day_last_week|same_day_last_year|regression|zero_fill|prior_value — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exception_handling_action varchar [note: 'Action taken when the rule detects a data quality exception: flag for review, automatically estimate, reject the data, route to manual review, or substitute with a default value.']
  execution_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the VEE rule is executed against incoming or historical meter data (real-time, hourly, daily, weekly, on-demand).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this VEE rule record. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VEE rule record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change management.']
  lookback_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of historical data the rule examines for validation or estimation purposes. Used by statistical and pattern-matching algorithms.']
  lower_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for validation rules. Interval data below this limit triggers a validation exception or editing action.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, implementation guidance, or special considerations related to this VEE rule. Supports knowledge transfer and operational documentation.']
  owner_group varchar [note: 'Business unit or team responsible for maintaining and governing this VEE rule (e.g., Metering Operations, Revenue Assurance, Data Quality Team).']
  priority_order varchar [note: 'Execution sequence priority for the rule within the VEE processing engine. Lower numbers execute first. Determines the order in which multiple rules are applied to the same interval data.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Citation of the regulatory requirement, tariff provision, or PUC order that mandates or justifies this VEE rule. Supports compliance documentation and audit trails.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the rule indicating the specific data quality issue it addresses (e.g., spike detection, zero consumption validation, missing data estimation).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the VEE rule, used for external reference and system integration. Typically a short alphanumeric code following utility naming conventions.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the VEE rule including its business logic, application context, and expected outcomes when applied to interval meter data.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the VEE rule describing its purpose and function within the meter data management process.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the VEE rule indicating whether it is actively applied to meter data processing or in a non-operational state.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule within the VEE process: validation (checks data quality), estimation (fills missing data), or editing (corrects erroneous data).']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold value (e.g., kWh, MWh, percentage, count, standard deviations) providing context for threshold interpretation.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric threshold parameter used by the rule algorithm to determine data quality exceptions. For example, spike tolerance percentage, maximum consumption limit, or minimum expected value.']
  upper_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for validation rules. Interval data above this limit triggers a validation exception or editing action.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the VEE rule configuration. Incremented with each modification to support change tracking and rollback capabilities.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this VEE rule record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']

  Note: 'Defines the business rules applied during the VEE (Validation, Estimation, and Editing) process in Oracle Utilities MDM. Each rule specifies the rule type (validation/estimation/editing), rule algorithm, threshold parameters (e.g., spike tolerance percentage, zero-consumption threshold), applicable meter types, priority order, effective date range, and regulatory basis. Governs data quality for billing-quality interval data. Reference entity managed by the metering operations team.'
}

Table metering.vee_exception {
  vee_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VEE exception record. Primary key for the VEE exception entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with the meter and service point for this VEE exception. Used for customer impact analysis and billing hold workflows.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: VEE exceptions identified as audit findings when indicating systematic data quality issues or control deficiencies. Supports audit evidence collection, corrective action tracking, regulatory reporting']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or system user who reviewed and resolved this VEE exception. Null if exception is still open or was auto-resolved without manual review.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: VEE analysts investigate whether missing/anomalous interval readings stem from power interruptions. Regulatory VEE reports require cross-referencing exceptions with outage events to justify estimation']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the meter that generated the interval data which triggered this VEE exception.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service point (delivery location) associated with the meter that generated the interval data for this exception. Used to link exceptions to customer accounts and premises.']
  primary_vee_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VEE rule that was triggered and caused this exception to be raised.']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate schedule (tariff) applicable to the service point at the time of the exception. Used to determine billing impact and priority for resolution, especially for Time-of-Use (TOU) and Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) customers.']
  to_interval_reading_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.interval_reading.interval_reading_id — VEE exceptions must reference the interval reading(s) that failed validation. Without this link, operators cannot identify and resolve data quality issues.']
  to_vee_rule_id varchar [note: 'FK to metering.vee_rule.vee_rule_id — Each VEE exception must reference the VEE rule that triggered it. This is essential for exception resolution workflows and rule tuning.']
  vee_interval_reading_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific interval data record that failed VEE validation and raised this exception.']
