  shoulder_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Energy consumption during shoulder (mid-peak) hours as defined by the Time of Use (TOU) rate schedule. Mid-tier pricing period between on-peak and off-peak.']
  temperature_normalized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this usage transaction has been adjusted for weather normalization to account for temperature variations. Used for load forecasting and customer comparisons.']
  total_interval_count varchar [note: 'Total number of interval data records aggregated to produce this usage transaction. Used for data quality assessment and completeness validation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the primary usage value in this transaction. Standardizes measurement units across different rate components and meter types.']
  usage_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The method used by MDM to calculate this usage transaction. Indicates whether the value was derived from interval data aggregation, register reads, estimation algorithms, proration, or manual adjustment.']
  usage_quality_code varchar [note: 'Data quality indicator code assigned by MDM to this usage transaction. Reflects confidence level in the calculated usage based on source data quality and VEE results.']
  usage_transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the usage transaction. Human-readable reference number used in billing and customer service interactions.']
  vee_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the VEE processing was completed for this usage transaction. Marks when the data became billing-ready.']
  vee_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the Validation, Estimation, and Editing (VEE) process for the interval data underlying this usage transaction. Indicates data quality and readiness for billing.']

  Note: 'The Oracle Utilities MDM usage transaction — the aggregated, billing-ready consumption record derived from validated interval data for a specific meter point, billing period, and rate component. Represents the output of MDM interval-to-usage processing: stores billing determinants (on-peak kWh, off-peak kWh, peak demand kW, reactive demand kVAR), billing period start/end, usage calculation method, and MDM processing run identifier. This is the primary handoff record from the metering domain to the billing domain for invoice generation.'
}

Table metering.metering_dr_enrollment {
  metering_dr_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for metering_dr_enrollment']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the third-party aggregator managing this enrollment, if enrollment_source is Aggregator. Null for utility-direct enrollments.']
  dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this meter enrollment in this DR program. Primary key.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the demand response program']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the enrolled meter device']
  baseline_calculation_method_override varchar [note: 'Enrollment-specific override of the baseline calculation method defined at the program level. Allows customer-specific baseline methodology when negotiated or required by customer characteristics. Null indicates use of program default method.']
  capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Committed load reduction capacity in kilowatts for this specific meter enrollment. Used for program capacity aggregation and ISO/RTO registration. May differ from meter peak demand based on customer commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this enrollment record was created in the DR management system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this enrollment terminates. Null for active open-ended enrollments. Populated for withdrawn or expired enrollments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this enrollment becomes effective for event dispatch and incentive eligibility. May differ from enrollment_date due to program rules or customer request.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Channel or mechanism through which this enrollment was created. Direct indicates utility direct enrollment; Aggregator indicates third-party aggregator enrollment; Auto-Enroll indicates automatic enrollment based on tariff or meter type; Tariff-Linked indicates enrollment required by rate schedule; ISO-RTO indicates wholesale market enrollment.']
  last_event_participation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent demand response event in which this meter participated. Used for engagement tracking and inactive enrollment identification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this enrollment record was last updated.']
  metering_dr_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the meter was enrolled into this demand response program. Used for eligibility determination and incentive calculation start date.']
  metering_dr_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this enrollment. Active indicates eligible for event dispatch; Pending indicates enrollment submitted but not yet effective; Suspended indicates temporarily ineligible; Withdrawn indicates customer-initiated termination; Expired indicates program end date reached.']
  opt_out_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of demand response events from which this meter opted out (if program allows opt-outs). Used for compliance tracking and penalty assessment.']
  participation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated participation performance metric representing the percentage of dispatched events in which this meter achieved the committed load reduction. Used for program performance reporting and customer engagement scoring.']
  total_incentive_payments_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of all incentive payments earned by this meter for participation in this DR program since enrollment. Used for settlement reconciliation and customer billing integration.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment relationship between a meter and a demand response program. It captures the operational enrollment lifecycle, participation tracking, and settlement data for each meter enrolled in each DR program. Each record links one meter to one DR program with enrollment-specific attributes including enrollment dates, status, enrolled capacity commitment, baseline calculation method override, participation performance metrics, opt-out history, and cumulative incentive payments earned. This is the SSOT for DR program enrollment administration, event dispatch eligibility, M&V settlement, and regulatory compliance reporting.. Existence Justification: In energy utilities operations, a single meter can simultaneously enroll in multiple demand response programs (e.g., ISO/RTO capacity market + utility peak-time rebate + behavioral DR campaign), and each DR program enrolls thousands of meters across the service territory. The business actively manages enrollments as operational entities with lifecycle status, capacity commitments, participation tracking, opt-out history, and settlement data. Enrollment is a recognized business concept with dedicated administration, event dispatch eligibility determination, M&V settlement processes, and regulatory compliance reporting obligations.'
}

Table metering.meter_reading_route {
  meter_reading_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for meter_reading_route']

  meter_route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_route. Business justification: Meter reading routes are specific instances of broader meter routes; the child route (meter_reading_route) should reference its parent meter_route.']

  assigned_region varchar [note: 'Region code (e.g., ISO‑3166‑2) where the route operates.']
  assigned_zone varchar [note: 'Operational zone or service area within the region.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the route complies with regulatory or internal standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the route record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the route, special instructions, or notes.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the route is retired or no longer used (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the route becomes valid for scheduling.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned time to complete the route, in minutes.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of the route.']
  max_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum travel distance covered by the route.']
  meter_reading_frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'Target interval between successive meter reads on this route.']
  next_scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start time for the next execution of the route.']
  number_of_meters varchar [note: 'Count of meters assigned to the route.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority of the route (1 = highest).']
  route_code varchar [note: 'External code or alphanumeric identifier assigned to the route.']
  route_creation_method varchar [note: 'How the route record was created.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the route used by field crews.']
  route_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the route in a larger schedule set.']
  route_shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the route is executed.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Category of customers served by the route.']
  route_vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Typical vehicle used to service the route.']
  route_version varchar [note: 'Version number for change tracking of the route definition.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the route is scheduled.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the route.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the route record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for meter_reading_route. Referenced by meter_reading_route_id.'
}

Table metering.register {
  register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for register']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the geographic location or asset where the register is installed.']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical meter to which this register belongs.']
  parent_register_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on register (parent_register_id)']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Billing frequency associated with the register.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the last calibration performed on the register.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next calibration of the register.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the register metadata.']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Physical connection type of the register to the meter.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the register record was first created in the system.']
  current_rating decimal(8,2) [note: 'Rated current for the register (amperes).']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Overall quality assessment of the register\'s data.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the register\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  edit_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the register\'s data can be manually edited.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the register became active for data collection.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the register is scheduled to be retired or become inactive (null if open‑ended).']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to estimate missing or corrupted readings.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'True if the register\'s readings are used for billing.']
  is_cumulative boolean [note: 'True if the register accumulates values over time (e.g., total kWh).']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'True if the register is a calculated/virtual register, false if physical.']
  last_reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent reading recorded for the register.']
  last_reading_value decimal(15,3) [note: 'Value of the most recent reading for the register.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Scheduled maintenance window description for the register.']
  max_capacity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum measurable capacity of the register (in its unit).']
  measurement_multiplier decimal(10,6) [note: 'Factor applied to raw readings to obtain engineering units.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the register\'s readings.']
  min_capacity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum measurable capacity of the register (in its unit).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes for operational or engineering remarks.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the register.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Phase configuration of the register.']
  rate_schedule varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate schedule (e.g., TOU, CPP) linked to the register.']
  reading_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places stored for each reading.']
  register_category varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of the register within the meter.']
  register_code varchar [note: 'External code or number assigned to the register by the utility.']
  register_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the register used in reports and UI.']
  register_type varchar [note: 'Category of measurement the register provides.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the register must be included in regulatory reports.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Whether the register uses net or gross settlement for billing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the register definition (e.g., Oracle Utilities MDM).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the register.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Code of the tariff schedule applied to the register\'s consumption.']
  uom_conversion_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Factor to convert raw device units to the engineering unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the register record.']
  validation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to validate raw interval data for this register.']
  voltage_rating decimal(8,2) [note: 'Rated voltage for the register (volts).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for register. Referenced by register_id.'
}

  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the route becomes operational and eligible for meter reads.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the route is retired or superseded; null for open‑ended routes.']

  number_of_meters varchar [note: 'Count of individual meters assigned to this route.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Logical region identifier used for operational planning and reporting.']
  route_classification varchar [note: 'Secondary classification indicating the route\'s priority tier within the distribution network.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible alphanumeric code used to reference the meter route in operational systems.']
  route_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manager overseeing the route.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the meter collection route.']
  route_owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the route\'s operation.']

  route_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the route.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the route based on the customer mix it serves.']
  schedule_end_time varchar [note: 'Planned daily end time for meter reads on this route (HH:MM, 24‑hour).']
  schedule_start_time varchar [note: 'Planned daily start time for meter reads on this route (HH:MM, 24‑hour).']
  total_route_length_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative length of the route measured in kilometers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the route record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for meter_route. Referenced by route_id.'
}

Table metering.service_point {
  service_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for service_point']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing cycle applied to the service point.']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the meter installed at the service point.']
  parent_service_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on service_point (parent_service_point_id)']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate plan governing charges.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the service point became active.']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Electrical connection configuration of the service point.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service point record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the quality of the interval data for this service point.']
  data_quality_status varchar [note: 'Categorical assessment of data quality.']
  deactivation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the service point was deactivated or retired.']
  demand_response_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the service point can participate in demand‑response programs.']
  external_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems or regulatory filings.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the meter and service point were installed.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance was performed.']
  last_read_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent validated meter reading.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the service point.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the service point.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the maintenance schedule.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the service point.']
  net_metering_capacity_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum generation capacity allowed under net‑metering.']
  net_metering_flag boolean [note: 'True if the service point is enrolled in net‑metering.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next maintenance activity.']
  outage_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the outage was resolved.']
  outage_flag boolean [note: 'True if the service point is currently experiencing an outage.']
  outage_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the current outage began.']
  rated_capacity_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical capacity the service point is authorized to deliver.']
  reading_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'Configured interval between meter reads.']
  renewable_energy_source_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the service point is supplied by renewable generation.']
  renewable_source_type varchar [note: 'Type of renewable energy associated with the service point.']
  service_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the service point.']
  service_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  service_city varchar [note: 'City where the service point is located.']
  service_country varchar [note: 'ISO‑3 country code of the service point location.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'Date the service point ceased delivering utility service, if applicable.']
  service_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the service point address.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Date the service point began delivering utility service.']
  service_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the service point location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the service point.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tariff applied to the service point.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the service point based on customer class or usage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the service point record.']
  voltage_level varchar [note: 'Design voltage classification for the service point.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for service_point. Referenced by service_point_id.'
}

Table billing.rate_schedule {
  rate_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rate schedule. Primary key for the rate schedule master reference entity.']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.irp_scenario. Business justification: Rate schedules are designed to recover costs projected in IRP scenarios. Regulatory rate cases reference specific IRP scenarios for cost recovery justification. Utilities track which IRP scenario info']
  lmp_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.lmp_price. Business justification: Dynamic retail tariffs indexed to real-time or day-ahead LMP require direct price reference for automated rate calculation, regulatory cost recovery validation, and customer bill transparency in real-']
  peak_demand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.peak_demand. Business justification: Demand charge rate schedules are designed to recover capacity costs allocated based on peak demand forecasts. Rate design documents reference specific peak demand forecasts (by customer class and seas']
  rate_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_case. Business justification: Rate schedules in billing are approved through regulatory rate case proceedings tracked in compliance. This is a fundamental regulatory relationship in utility operations - every tariff/rate schedule ']
  renewable_incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.incentive_program. Business justification: Rate schedules incorporate renewable incentive program terms for green tariff pricing, renewable energy riders, and regulatory compliance. Tariff design requires linking incentive program rates to bil']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Rate schedules are approved by regulatory jurisdiction tied to service territory. Required for tariff management, rate case filings, and ensuring customers are billed under correct jurisdictional rate']
  billing_cycle_type varchar [note: 'Standard billing frequency applicable to customers on this rate schedule.']
  budget_billing_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers on this rate schedule are eligible for budget billing or levelized payment plans.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this rate schedule record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rate schedule record was first created in the billing system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this rate schedule.']
  customer_charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly customer charge in dollars applicable under this rate schedule. Covers meter reading, billing, and basic service costs.']
  demand_charge_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Demand charge rate in dollars per kilowatt of peak demand. Null for rate schedules without demand charges.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this rate schedule ceases to be effective. Null for currently active rate schedules with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this rate schedule becomes effective and applicable to customer billing.']
  energy_charge_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Base energy charge rate in dollars per kilowatt-hour. Represents the primary volumetric charge component for energy consumption.']
  fuel_adjustment_clause_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a fuel adjustment clause or energy cost adjustment mechanism applies to this rate schedule.']
  has_seasonal_rates boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate schedule includes seasonal rate variations for summer and winter periods.']
  has_tiered_pricing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate schedule includes tiered or block pricing with different rates for different usage levels.']
  has_tou_periods boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate schedule includes time-of-use pricing periods with different rates for peak, off-peak, and shoulder periods.']
  interruptible_service boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate schedule provides interruptible or curtailable service with discounted rates in exchange for load reduction rights.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State or regulatory jurisdiction code where this rate schedule is approved and applicable. Uses standard state abbreviations or jurisdiction codes.']
  maximum_demand_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum demand threshold in kilowatts for customer eligibility under this rate schedule. Null if no maximum applies.']
  maximum_usage_kwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum monthly energy usage in kilowatt-hours for customer eligibility. Null if no maximum applies.']
  minimum_demand_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum demand threshold in kilowatts required for customer eligibility under this rate schedule. Null if no minimum applies.']
  minimum_power_factor decimal(4,3) [note: 'Minimum power factor threshold required to avoid penalties. Expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1. Null if power factor penalties do not apply.']
  minimum_usage_kwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum monthly energy usage in kilowatt-hours required for customer eligibility. Null if no minimum applies.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this rate schedule record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rate schedule record was last modified in the billing system.']
  net_metering_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers on this rate schedule are eligible for net energy metering programs for distributed generation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this rate schedule for internal reference.']
  power_factor_penalty_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether power factor penalties or adjustments apply to customers on this rate schedule for poor power factor performance.']
  prepay_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers on this rate schedule are eligible for prepaid metering programs.']
  rate_class varchar [note: 'Customer class or segment to which this rate schedule applies. Defines the primary customer category for rate applicability.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the rate schedule used in billing systems and customer communications. Externally-known unique code for this tariff rate schedule.']
  rate_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rate schedule including applicability, terms, and conditions as filed with regulatory authorities.']
  rate_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the rate schedule displayed on customer bills and tariff documents.']
  rate_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate schedule in the billing system.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Type of rate structure defining the pricing methodology. TOU = Time of Use, CPP = Critical Peak Pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|tou|cpp|tiered|demand|flat|seasonal|interruptible — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority approved this rate schedule for implementation.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the regulatory order or docket approving this rate schedule. Links to PUC or FERC rate case proceedings.']
  renewable_energy_rider_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether renewable energy riders or RPS compliance charges apply to this rate schedule.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of electrical service configuration applicable to this rate schedule.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source billing system from which this rate schedule record originated.']
  tariff_book_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the section and page number in the official tariff book where this rate schedule is published.']
  tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the published rates include applicable taxes or if taxes are added separately during billing.']
  voltage_level varchar [note: 'Voltage level at which service is delivered under this rate schedule. Determines applicable transmission and distribution charges.']

  Note: 'Master reference entity defining tariff rate schedules and their granular charge components applicable to customer accounts. At the schedule level: stores rate code, effective dates, rate class, voltage level, applicable customer segments, regulatory approval references, and jurisdiction. At the component level: captures individual charge elements including energy charges ($/kWh), demand charges ($/kW), customer charges, fuel adjustment clauses, distribution charges, transmission charges, and riders with component type, unit of measure, tier thresholds, seasonal applicability, time-of-use period mapping, and calculation formula. Sourced from SAP IS-U and Oracle CC&B rate configuration modules. SSOT for all rate definitions and charge component structures used in bill calculation. Supports rate case filings and regulatory tariff compliance.'
}

Table billing.rate_component {
  rate_component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rate component. Primary key.']
  capacity_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.capacity_requirement. Business justification: Capacity-related rate components (demand charges, capacity riders, PJM/ISO capacity cost recovery riders) are calculated to recover costs from capacity requirement forecasts. Tariff filings must refer']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: Specific rate components (demand charges, TOU energy charges) may be waived, modified, or have special calculation rules for DR program participants. Tariff administration, billing system configuratio']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: Energy charge rate components often include fuel adjustment clauses indexed to forecasted energy/fuel prices. Tariff sheets reference specific price forecast scenarios for automatic rate adjustments. ']
  fuel_price_assumption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.fuel_price_assumption. Business justification: Fuel adjustment clause rate components are calculated using specific fuel price assumptions filed with regulators. Utilities must track which fuel price forecast version was used for each rate compone']
  primary_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent rate schedule that contains this component.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Rate components must be documented in regulatory filings when utilities propose tariff changes or rate case adjustments. Links the rate component to the specific filing (rate case, tariff amendment) w']
  adjustment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this rate component value is adjusted or recalculated. Fixed components never change, monthly adjustments are common for fuel clauses, quarterly for purchased power adjustments, annually for base rate reviews, event-driven for critical peak pricing.']
  applicable_days varchar [note: 'Indicates which days of the week or calendar this rate component applies to. TOU rates often differ between weekdays and weekends.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory approval status of the rate component. Draft components are under development, pending_approval are submitted to PUC, approved are authorized for billing, rejected were denied, superseded were replaced by newer versions, retired are no longer in use.']
  bill_print_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number controlling the order in which rate components appear on printed customer bills. Lower numbers print first. Null if print_on_bill_flag is False.']
  billing_determinant varchar [note: 'The measured or calculated value that this rate component is applied to. Actual usage comes from meter reads, estimated usage from profiling, contracted demand from service agreements, metered demand from interval data, billing demand from demand ratchets.']
  calculation_formula varchar [note: 'Mathematical formula or expression used to calculate the charge when calculation_method is \'formula\'. May reference other rate components, meter data, or external indices.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate charges for this component. Flat applies a single rate, tiered uses progressive rate steps, block uses declining block rates, TOU (time-of-use) varies by time period, CPP (critical peak pricing) applies premium rates during peak events, real-time uses dynamic pricing, formula uses a custom calculation expression. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flat|tiered|block|tou|cpp|real_time|formula — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_category varchar [note: 'Billing category classification indicating whether this is a base tariff charge, a rider (supplemental charge), surcharge, credit (reduction), adjustment clause, or tax component.']
  component_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the rate component used in billing systems and tariff filings.']
  component_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the rate component, its purpose, and how it is calculated. Used for customer communications, tariff documentation, and internal training.']
  component_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the rate component as it appears on customer bills and tariff documents.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate component indicating the nature of the charge. Energy charges are based on kWh consumption, demand charges on peak kW usage, customer charges are fixed monthly fees, fuel adjustment clauses recover fuel cost variations, distribution charges cover local delivery infrastructure, and transmission charges cover high-voltage grid costs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rate component record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rate value (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  customer_class_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates which customer classes this rate component applies to. Rate components may be specific to residential, commercial, industrial, or other customer segments.']
  decimal_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places to retain in charge calculations for this component. Typically 2 for dollar amounts, but may be higher for per-unit rates or regulatory calculations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this rate component expires and is no longer applied to customer bills. Null indicates the component is currently active with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this rate component becomes effective and can be applied to customer bills. Aligns with PUC rate case approval dates.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA) where revenue from this rate component is posted for financial accounting.']
  index_reference varchar [note: 'External market index or benchmark used to calculate this component if it is formula-based or indexed (e.g., Henry Hub natural gas price, PJM LMP, CPI). Null for fixed-rate components.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rate component record was last updated in the system.']
  maximum_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum charge cap for this component. Used to limit exposure for certain rate components or to comply with regulatory caps.']
  minimum_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum charge amount that must be applied for this component regardless of usage. Common for customer charges and demand charges with minimum billing requirements.']
  print_on_bill_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate component should appear as a separate line item on customer bills. True for customer-visible charges, False for internal calculation components that roll up into other line items.']
  proration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to prorate charges for partial billing periods (e.g., when a customer starts or stops service mid-cycle). Daily proration divides by days in period, calendar uses actual calendar days, billing uses billing cycle days.']
  puc_docket_number varchar [note: 'Public Utility Commission docket or case number associated with the rate case that approved this component. Used for regulatory compliance tracking and audit trails.']
  rate_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The numeric rate or price applied per unit of measure. For example, $0.085 per kWh for energy charges, $15.00 per kW for demand charges, or $12.00 per account for customer charges.']
  regulatory_account_code varchar [note: 'FERC Uniform System of Accounts code or state regulatory accounting code used for revenue recognition and regulatory reporting of charges from this component.']
  revenue_class varchar [note: 'Classification of revenue type for financial and regulatory reporting. Aligns with FERC revenue categories and state regulatory reporting requirements.']
  rider_code varchar [note: 'Short code identifying the rider or surcharge if this component is a supplemental charge (e.g., FAC for Fuel Adjustment Clause, EEC for Energy Efficiency Charge, REC for Renewable Energy Charge). Null for base rate components.']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rounding method applied to calculated charges for this component. Round_half_up is standard commercial rounding, round_down always rounds down, round_up always rounds up, truncate drops decimals.']
  seasonal_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates which season or time period this rate component applies to. Many utilities have different rates for summer (high cooling demand) versus winter periods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: year_round|summer|winter|spring|fall|peak_season|off_peak_season — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tariff_sheet_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific tariff sheet number or section in the official tariff book where this rate component is documented. Used for regulatory compliance and customer inquiries.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate component is exempt from sales tax or other taxes. True if exempt, False if taxable.']
  tier_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number for tiered or block rate structures, indicating the order of rate tiers (1 for first tier, 2 for second tier, etc.). Null for non-tiered components.']
  tier_threshold_max decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum usage threshold for this tier in the specified unit of measure. Null indicates no upper limit (open-ended tier). Null for non-tiered components.']
  tier_threshold_min decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum usage threshold for this tier in the specified unit of measure. For example, tier 1 might be 0 kWh, tier 2 might be 500 kWh. Null for non-tiered components.']
  tou_end_time timestamp [note: 'End time for the TOU period in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). For example, on-peak might end at 21:00. Null for non-TOU components.']
  tou_period varchar [note: 'Time-of-use period classification for TOU rate structures. On-peak represents highest demand periods, off-peak represents lowest demand periods, critical-peak represents emergency high-demand events.']
  tou_start_time timestamp [note: 'Start time for the TOU period in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). For example, on-peak might start at 14:00. Null for non-TOU components.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for this rate component. Common values include kWh (kilowatt-hour) for energy consumption, kW (kilowatt) for demand, account for fixed customer charges, day/month for time-based charges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kWh|kW|MWh|MW|account|day|month|therm|ccf — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  voltage_level varchar [note: 'Voltage level at which service is delivered, affecting rate component applicability. Secondary is typical residential (120/240V), primary is distribution (4-35kV), sub-transmission (35-138kV), transmission (>138kV).']

  Note: 'Granular rate component definitions within a rate schedule, capturing individual charge elements such as energy charges ($/kWh), demand charges ($/kW), customer charges, fuel adjustment clauses, distribution charges, transmission charges, and riders. Stores component type, unit of measure, tier thresholds, seasonal applicability, and calculation formula. Enables precise bill calculation for all rate structures including tiered and TOU.'
}

Business justification: Service agreements track DER installations at customer premises for net metering billing, export compensation, and demand response programs. Essential for calculating NEM credits, managing export limi']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Service agreements govern specific service points (the contract-to-location binding). Rate schedules, service voltage, and billing cycles are service-point-specific. This link is essential for service']
  nem_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.nem_account. Business justification: Service agreements have 1:1 relationship with NEM accounts for net metering customers. Business justification: Special contract service agreements and non-standard tariff arrangements require regulatory approval and disclosure in filings. Links agreements to the filing where they were approved or reported for ']
  service_connection_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Field operations use this for capacity planning, service upgrades, and ou']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the service agreement, used in customer communications and billing statements.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing service agreement based on customer class and service characteristics. Determines applicable rate structures and regulatory treatment.']
  autopay_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in automatic payment program where invoices are paid automatically on due date.']
  billing_agreement_uses_rate_schedule varchar [note: 'FK to billing.rate_schedule.rate_schedule_id — Each service agreement is assigned a rate schedule that governs how the account is billed. This is a fundamental billing relationship — without it, the billing engine cannot determine which rates to a']
  billing_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the billing cycle schedule that determines invoice generation frequency and due dates for this agreement.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are generated for this service agreement.']
  billing_service_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing service agreement indicating whether it is actively generating invoices and accepting payments.']
  budget_billing_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly payment amount for customers enrolled in budget billing program. Recalculated periodically based on usage history.']
  budget_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in budget billing program that spreads annual energy costs into equal monthly payments.']
  contract_capacity_kw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Contracted maximum demand capacity in kilowatts for large commercial or industrial customers. Basis for capacity charges and penalties for exceeding contracted demand.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing service agreement record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service agreement is on credit hold due to payment delinquency or credit risk, potentially restricting service or requiring prepayment.']
  demand_metered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service agreement includes demand metering and demand charges based on peak kW usage.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of security deposit held for this service agreement. Subject to interest accrual per state PUC regulations.']
  deposit_date date [note: 'Date when the security deposit was received from the customer.']
  deposit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security deposit is required for this service agreement based on credit profile or regulatory requirements.']
  disconnect_pending_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a service disconnection order is pending for this agreement due to non-payment or other reasons.']
  dunning_block_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether collections and dunning activities are blocked for this service agreement due to payment arrangement, dispute, or special circumstances.']
  dunning_block_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why dunning activities are blocked, such as active payment plan, billing dispute, or regulatory protection.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the billing service agreement terminates and billing ceases. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Aligns with service disconnection or move-out date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the billing service agreement becomes active and billing begins. Aligns with service connection or move-in date.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this service agreement record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing service agreement record was last updated.']
  life_support_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise has registered life support medical equipment requiring continuous power. Provides regulatory protection from disconnection.']
  load_profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer load profile and associated rate structure. Determines whether Time of Use, Critical Peak Pricing, or Real Time Pricing applies.']
  low_income_assistance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer qualifies for and is enrolled in low income energy assistance programs such as LIHEAP or CARE.']
  nem_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service agreement participates in Net Energy Metering program for distributed generation customers with solar or other renewable energy systems.']
  nem_program_type varchar [note: 'Specific NEM program version and structure applicable to this agreement. Different versions have varying compensation rates and rules.']
  paperless_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted for electronic invoice delivery instead of paper bills.']
  payment_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has an active payment arrangement or installment plan for past due balances on this service agreement.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument the customer uses to pay invoices for this service agreement.']
  revenue_class varchar [note: 'Revenue accounting classification for financial reporting and regulatory compliance. Determines how revenue is recognized and reported to FERC and state PUCs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|small_commercial|large_commercial|industrial|agricultural|street_lighting|public_authority — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_voltage_level varchar [note: 'Voltage level at which electric service is delivered to the customer. Affects rate structure and delivery charges.']
  special_contract_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service agreement operates under a special negotiated contract with non-standard rates or terms, typically for large industrial customers.']
  special_contract_terms varchar [note: 'Description of special contractual terms, pricing arrangements, or service level commitments that deviate from standard tariff schedules.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the service agreement was terminated, such as customer move-out, non-payment disconnection, or service transfer.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing the contractual billing relationship between the utility and a customer account for energy delivery at a specific service point. Captures agreement type (residential, commercial, industrial, NEM), assigned rate schedule, effective and end dates, deposit requirements, budget billing enrollment status, special contract terms, and service point reference. SSOT for the active billing relationship that drives invoice generation, payment application, collections, and all downstream billing activity. Sourced from SAP IS-U contract account and Oracle CC&B service agreement modules.'
}

Table billing.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the utility invoice. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for which this invoice was generated.']
  billing_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_run. Business justification: Each invoice is created by a specific billing run execution (batch job). This FK enables tracing invoices back to the exact run that generated them, critical for exception handling, reprocessing, and ']
  billing_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_service_agreement. Business justification: An invoice is generated FOR a specific service agreement (the contractual billing relationship between utility and customer). While invoice currently has account_id and premise_id, it lacks the direct']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Each invoice is generated as part of a specific billing cycle execution. Currently invoice has billing_cycle_code (STRING) but no FK to the billing_cycle entity. This FK enables joining to retrieve cy']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Invoices post revenue to specific GL accounts for financial statement preparation and regulatory reporting. Every billed revenue transaction requires GL account assignment per FERC Uniform System of A']
  invoice_for_service_agreement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.billing_service_agreement.billing_service_agreement_id — Every invoice is generated for a specific service agreement. This is the core billing relationship — without it, invoices cannot be tied to the customer\'s contractual billing arrangement, rate schedul']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Invoices bill specific service points (meter_point). This link is fundamental for rate application, network cost allocation, and regulatory reporting by service location. Every invoice must identify w']
  nem_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.nem_account. Business justification: Invoices apply NEM credits from specific NEM accounts, track cumulative credit balances, and perform annual true-up settlements. Critical for accurate billing of net metering customers, ensuring prope']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise where energy was consumed during this billing period.']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Invoices require service territory reference for revenue allocation by jurisdiction, regulatory reporting (PUC filings), jurisdictional tax calculation, and franchise fee assessment. Critical for mult']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.trade. Business justification: Bilateral wholesale energy trades delivered outside ISO markets generate direct customer invoices requiring trade reference for settlement reconciliation, revenue recognition, and regulatory audit tra']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total adjustments applied to the invoice including credits, refunds, billing corrections, and dispute resolutions. Positive for credits, negative for additional charges.']
  auto_pay_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is enrolled in automatic payment processing from a bank account or credit card.']
  bill_message_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying special messages or notices printed on the invoice (e.g., disconnection warning, rate change notice, energy efficiency tips).']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'FK to billing.billing_cycle.billing_cycle_id — Every invoice is generated within a billing cycle run. Critical for billing operations monitoring and batch reconciliation.']
  billing_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the billing cycle schedule to which this invoice belongs (e.g., monthly cycle 01, cycle 15).']
  billing_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the billing period, used for prorating charges and calculating daily averages.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the billing period for which energy consumption and charges are calculated.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'First date of the billing period for which energy consumption and charges are calculated.']
  budget_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is part of a budget billing plan where monthly charges are levelized based on annual consumption estimates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the billing system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  current_charges_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total charges for the current billing period including energy, demand, customer charges, riders, and fees before taxes.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the invoice was delivered to the customer.']
  deposit_applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Customer security deposit amount applied to this invoice, typically at service termination or account closure.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is currently under dispute by the customer, requiring resolution before collections action.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be received to avoid late fees or service disconnection.']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total electric energy consumed during the billing period measured in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Calculated from meter readings or AMI interval data.']
  from_billing_cycle varchar [note: 'FK to billing.billing_cycle.billing_cycle_id — Invoices are generated as part of a billing cycle run. This FK supports billing operations monitoring, cycle-level revenue reconciliation, and workload management.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date when this invoice was posted to the general ledger accounts receivable.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was generated and issued to the customer. Principal business event timestamp for the transaction.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, customer-facing invoice number printed on the bill. Unique business identifier for the invoice.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the billing and collections workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|sent|paid|partially_paid|overdue|cancelled|disputed|adjusted|written_off — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice based on billing purpose: regular cycle bill, final bill at move-out, interim bill, adjustment bill, credit memo, or debit memo.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this invoice record was last updated, capturing adjustments, payment applications, or status changes.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Late payment fee assessed on this invoice for overdue balances from prior billing periods.']
  meter_read_end_date date [note: 'Date of the meter reading at the end of the billing period.']
  meter_read_start_date date [note: 'Date of the meter reading at the beginning of the billing period.']
  outstanding_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on this invoice after payments have been applied.']
  paperless_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted for electronic delivery of this invoice instead of paper mail.']
  payment_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is subject to a payment arrangement or installment plan for past-due balances.']
  payment_received_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total payments received against this invoice to date.']
  peak_demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum electric demand recorded during the billing period measured in kilowatts (kW). Used for demand-based rate calculations.']
  previous_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding balance carried forward from prior billing periods before current charges are applied.']
  print_date date [note: 'Date when the physical or electronic invoice document was generated and prepared for delivery to the customer.']
  rate_class varchar [note: 'Customer rate classification determining applicable tariff structures and regulatory treatment.']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Tariff rate schedule code applied to this invoice, defining the pricing structure for energy and demand charges.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date when revenue from this invoice is recognized in the general ledger for financial reporting purposes.']
  service_agreement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — Every invoice is generated for a specific service agreement. This is the core billing transaction-to-master relationship.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'Date when utility service ended for this billing period, relevant for disconnections or final bills.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Date when utility service began for this billing period, relevant for new connections or service resumptions.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total taxes applied to the invoice including sales tax, utility tax, and other regulatory taxes.']
  to_cycle varchar [note: 'FK to billing.billing_cycle.billing_cycle_id — Every invoice is generated within a billing cycle run. This FK supports billing operations monitoring and revenue cycle timing.']
  to_sa varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — Every invoice is generated for a specific service agreement. This FK is essential for tracing billed amounts back to the contractual billing relationship.']
  total_amount_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net total amount the customer must pay, calculated as previous balance plus current charges plus taxes plus adjustments.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of this invoice written off as uncollectible bad debt, subject to SOX financial controls and regulatory reporting.']

  Note: 'Core transactional entity representing a utility bill issued to a customer for a billing period, including header-level totals and granular line-item charge detail. At the header level: captures invoice number, billing period dates, due date, total amount due, energy consumed (kWh/MWh), demand (kW), prior balance, current charges, taxes, adjustments, and payment status. At the line level: captures individual charge components including energy charges, demand charges, customer charges, fuel adjustment clauses, distribution riders, taxes, and credits with rate component reference, quantity, unit rate, calculated amount, and applicable billing determinant. Generated from Oracle CC&B and SAP IS-U billing engine. SSOT for all customer-facing billing documents and their charge detail. Supports SOX AR controls, granular revenue recognition, rate component-level analysis, and regulatory rate case evidence.'
}

Table billing.invoice_line {
  invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the invoice line item. Primary key for the invoice line detail record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue allocation to cost centers enables internal performance reporting, cost-of-service studies for rate cases, and regulatory reporting by functional area. Essential for FERC Form 1 preparation an']
  dr_incentive_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_incentive_payment. Business justification: Individual invoice line items represent specific DR incentive credits at granular level. Detailed billing reconciliation, audit trails, dispute resolution, and regulatory reporting require line-level ']
  from_rate_component_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.rate_component.rate_component_id — Each invoice line item is calculated from a specific rate component. This FK enables rate-component-level revenue analysis and bill audit trails. Critical for regulatory rate case support.']
  invoice_rate_component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rate_component. Business justification: Each invoice line item is calculated using a specific rate component definition from the rate schedule. Currently invoice_line has rate_component_code (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Addin']
  original_line_invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice line item being reversed or corrected. Null for original charges. Maintains audit trail for billing adjustments.']
  primary_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent invoice header. Links this line item to the billing document.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue attribution to profit centers supports segment reporting, FERC functional classification (generation/transmission/distribution), and business unit profitability analysis. Required for regulato']
  sox_control_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sox_control_test. Business justification: SOX control testing samples specific invoice line items to verify billing accuracy controls and revenue recognition. Links the tested line item to the control test for audit evidence, sample documenta']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.trade. Business justification: Individual invoice line items may represent specific wholesale trade settlements, particularly for bilateral contracts with retail pass-through pricing where each trade leg generates a separate billin']
  adjustment_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the adjustment reason for customer communication and audit trail. Null for standard charges.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the reason for any adjustment applied to this line item (e.g., REBILL, CREDIT, DISPUTE, CORRECTION). Null for standard charges.']
  billing_determinant_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantity of the billing determinant used in the rate calculation (e.g., kWh consumed, peak kW demand, number of days). Precision supports interval meter data.']
  billing_determinant_type varchar [note: 'Type of billing determinant used to calculate this charge (e.g., kWh for energy consumption, kW for demand, days for fixed charges, percentage for adjustments). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kwh|kw|mwh|mw|days|units|flat|percentage — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the charge type for revenue recognition and financial reporting. Groups rate components into standard categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: energy|demand|customer|distribution|transmission|generation|tax|fee|adjustment|credit|rebate|penalty — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consumption_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Energy consumption in kilowatt-hours for this line item. Primary billing determinant for energy charges. Null for non-energy charges.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line item record was first created in the billing system. Supports audit trail and data lineage for SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the line item amounts. Supports multi-currency billing for international operations.']
  demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Peak demand in kilowatts for this line item. Primary billing determinant for demand charges. Null for non-demand charges.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is under customer dispute. True if disputed, false otherwise. Supports dispute resolution and collections workflows.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for revenue recognition posting. Maps line item to chart of accounts for financial reporting and SOX compliance.']
  line_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated charge amount for this line item before taxes and adjustments. Result of billing determinant quantity multiplied by unit rate, or flat charge amount.']
  line_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of line items within the invoice. Determines display order on customer bill.']
  meter_read_end_date date [note: 'Date of the ending meter read used to calculate consumption for this charge. May differ from service period end for estimated or prorated bills.']
  meter_read_start_date date [note: 'Date of the beginning meter read used to calculate consumption for this charge. May differ from service period start for estimated or prorated bills.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line item record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking for SOX compliance.']
  print_sequence varchar [note: 'Display order for this line item on the printed or electronic customer bill. Controls presentation layout for customer readability.']
  print_suppress_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item should be suppressed from customer bill presentation. True if suppressed (internal accounting only), false if displayed to customer.']
  proration_factor decimal(8,6) [note: 'Proration multiplier applied to this charge for partial billing periods (e.g., 0.5 for half-month service). Value of 1.0 indicates full period charge.']
  rate_component_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the rate component or charge type for customer bill presentation (e.g., \'Energy Charge\', \'Demand Charge\', \'Customer Charge\', \'Fuel Adjustment Clause\').']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tariff rate schedule applied to this line item (e.g., RES-1, COM-2, IND-3). Links to approved tariff structure.']
  rate_version varchar [note: 'Effective date version of the rate schedule applied to this line item. Supports rate change tracking and historical billing accuracy.']
  revenue_class varchar [note: 'Customer class for revenue reporting and regulatory compliance. Aligns with FERC and state PUC revenue classification requirements.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a reversal of a previous charge. True for reversal entries, false for original charges. Supports rebilling and correction workflows.']
  service_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the service period covered by this line item charge. Defines the end of the consumption or service window.']
  service_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the service period covered by this line item charge. Defines the beginning of the consumption or service window.']
  source_line_reference varchar [note: 'Original line item identifier from the source billing system. Maintains traceability to operational system of record for reconciliation and troubleshooting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source billing system that generated this invoice line item. Supports multi-system integration and data lineage tracking.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount applied to this line item. May include sales tax, utility tax, franchise fees, or other jurisdiction-specific taxes.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax jurisdiction for this line item. Determines applicable tax rates and rules. Null for non-taxable items.']
  taxable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is subject to sales tax or utility tax. True if taxable, false if exempt.']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Tier number for tiered rate structures (e.g., 1 for baseline, 2 for tier 2, 3 for tier 3). Null for non-tiered charges. Supports inclining block and declining block rate designs.']
  total_line_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount for this line item including taxes and adjustments. Sum of line amount and tax amount.']
  tou_period_code varchar [note: 'Time-of-use period classification for this charge. Identifies whether consumption occurred during on-peak, off-peak, or other TOU periods. Null for non-TOU charges.']
  unit_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Rate per unit applied to the billing determinant (e.g., dollars per kWh, dollars per kW, daily charge). High precision supports complex tariff calculations including TOU and CPP rates.']
  write_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item has been written off as uncollectible. True if written off, false otherwise. Supports bad debt accounting and SOX controls.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for each charge component on a utility invoice, including energy charges, demand charges, customer charges, fuel adjustment clauses, distribution riders, taxes, and credits. Stores rate component reference, quantity, unit rate, calculated amount, and applicable billing determinant. Enables granular revenue recognition and rate component-level analysis. Sourced from SAP IS-U FICA and Oracle CC&B billing line detail.'
}

Table billing.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment transaction record. Primary key for the payment entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account against which this payment or refund is applied. Links to the customer account master record in the billing system.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — Payments are applied against invoices (or account balances). This FK is essential for AR clearing, cash application, and payment status tracking. Note: in open-item accounting (SAP FICA), payments may']
  payment_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment arrangement or installment plan if this payment is part of a structured payment agreement. Null for standard payments.']
  payment_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific invoice being paid. Null for account-level payments or refunds not tied to a specific invoice.']
  to_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — Payments are applied against specific invoices (or account balances). This FK is critical for cash application and AR reconciliation.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the payment or refund transaction in the billing currency. Positive values for inbound payments, may be positive or negative depending on system convention for refunds.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether and how the payment has been applied to outstanding invoices or account balance. Tracks cash application workflow completion.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount that has been successfully applied to invoices or account balance. May differ from payment_amount if payment is partially applied or includes unapplied credit.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization or approval code returned by the payment processor or card network confirming the transaction was approved. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  auto_pay_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment was processed as part of an automatic payment enrollment. True for auto-pay transactions, false for manual payments.']
  bank_account_number_masked varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized bank account number for ACH payments. Only last 4 digits visible for PCI compliance and fraud prevention. Full account number stored in secure vault.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit American Bankers Association routing transit number identifying the financial institution for ACH or check payments. Confidential financial data.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the payment processing batch if this payment was processed as part of a batch run. Used for lockbox processing, bulk ACH imports, and batch reconciliation.']
  card_expiration_month varchar [note: 'Month component of the payment card expiration date (1-12). Used for recurring payment validation and auto-pay processing.']
  card_expiration_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit year component of the payment card expiration date. Used for recurring payment validation and auto-pay processing.']
  card_last_four_digits varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the payment card number for customer reference and dispute resolution. Full card number never stored per PCI DSS requirements.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Credit or debit card network brand for card-based payments. Null for non-card payment methods.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Customer interface or channel through which the payment was submitted. Distinguishes self-service digital channels from assisted or physical channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_portal|mobile_app|ivr|walk_in|mail|bank_draft|third_party_agent — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this payment record was first created in the billing system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment transaction. Typically USD for US-based utilities but may vary for international operations.']
  disbursement_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or tracking number for the refund disbursement. May be check number, ACH trace number, or wire transfer confirmation. Null for inbound payments.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'Date when the refund check was issued or electronic refund was transmitted. Null for inbound payments or pending refunds.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which this payment transaction is posted. Typically a cash or accounts receivable account per the utility chart of accounts.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this payment record. Part of the audit trail for SOX compliance and change management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this payment record was last updated. Tracks all modifications for audit trail and change tracking.']
  lockbox_number varchar [note: 'Bank lockbox identifier for payments received via lockbox processing service. Null for non-lockbox payments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the payment transaction. May include customer instructions, dispute details, or special handling notes entered by customer service representatives.']
  nsf_fee_assessed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee amount charged to the customer account for returned payment due to insufficient funds. Null if no NSF fee applies.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Business date when the payment was received or the refund was issued. For inbound payments, this is the date the payment was tendered by the customer. For outbound refunds, this is the disbursement date.']
  payment_invoice varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — Payments are applied against specific invoices. Critical for AR balance calculation and cash application.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Financial instrument or mechanism used to tender the payment. Identifies whether payment was made via check, electronic funds transfer, card payment, or cash. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: check|ach|credit_card|debit_card|cash|money_order|wire_transfer|online|auto_pay — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction. Tracks progression from initial receipt through clearing and application to customer account balance.']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the payment transaction was recorded in the billing system. Used for audit trail and transaction sequencing.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment transaction direction and purpose. Inbound payments represent cash receipts from customers; outbound refunds represent disbursements to customers.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Accounting date when the payment transaction was posted to the general ledger. May differ from payment_date due to batch processing timing or period-end cutoffs.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or confirmation code for the payment transaction. May include check number, ACH trace number, credit card authorization code, or online payment confirmation ID.']
  refund_approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow status for refund approval process. Tracks management review and authorization for outbound refund disbursements. Null for inbound payments.']
  refund_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was approved for disbursement. Part of the audit trail for financial controls. Null for inbound payments.']
  refund_approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the employee who authorized the refund disbursement. Required for SOX compliance and audit trail. Null for inbound payments.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the refund is disbursed to the customer. May be check, ACH transfer, account credit, or reversal to original payment method. Null for inbound payments.']
  refund_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for issuing a refund to the customer. Null for inbound payments. Used for refund analytics and regulatory reporting.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was reversed. Null if payment has not been reversed.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment transaction has been reversed due to NSF (non-sufficient funds), chargeback, or administrative correction. True if reversed, false otherwise.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for payment reversal. Includes NSF returns, chargebacks, disputes, and administrative corrections. Null if payment has not been reversed.']
  service_agreement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — Payments may be applied at the account/service agreement level when not tied to a specific invoice (e.g., prepayments, deposits).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system or module that originated the payment transaction record. Examples include SAP IS-U FICA, Oracle CC&B, payment gateway integration, or third-party payment processor.']
  to_sa varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — Payments are received for a customer service agreement. Needed when payments are applied at account level rather than invoice level.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment gateway or processor. Used for reconciliation, chargebacks, and technical support inquiries.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount that remains unapplied and held as account credit. Occurs when payment exceeds outstanding balance or when application is suspended pending dispute resolution.']
