  total_kwh_billed decimal(15,3) [note: 'Sum of all energy consumption in kilowatt-hours billed across all accounts in this billing run. Key operational metric for load forecasting and revenue analysis.']

  Note: 'Reference and operational entity defining billing cycle configurations and their execution history. At the cycle level: stores cycle code, read frequency (monthly, bi-monthly), scheduled read and bill generation dates, due date offset, and assigned account count. At the run level: captures batch billing run execution records including run date, billing cycle reference, accounts processed, invoices generated, total billed amount, run status (pending, in-progress, completed, error), exception count, and operator. Drives billing run scheduling, workload distribution, and revenue cycle timing. Sourced from SAP IS-U and Oracle CC&B billing engine. SSOT for billing cycle definitions and all batch billing run execution records.'
}

Table billing.billing_run {
  billing_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing run execution. Primary key for the billing run entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that initiated or executed the billing run, used for audit trail and accountability in billing operations.']
  reversed_run_billing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original billing run that is being reversed by this run, establishing audit trail for billing corrections and dispute resolution.']
  accounts_failed varchar [note: 'Number of customer accounts or service agreements that failed to complete billing due to data errors, validation failures, or system exceptions.']
  accounts_processed varchar [note: 'Number of customer accounts or service agreements that were successfully processed and completed billing calculations without errors.']
  accounts_targeted varchar [note: 'Total number of customer accounts or service agreements that were selected and targeted for billing in this run based on billing cycle criteria.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the billing run execution completed or terminated, used to calculate run duration and identify performance issues.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the billing run execution began, capturing the real-world event time for performance monitoring and audit purposes.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval state of the billing run results, indicating whether the run output has been reviewed and approved for invoice release and accounts receivable posting.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing run was approved for invoice release, establishing the audit trail for revenue recognition timing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the supervisor or manager who approved the billing run results for release, supporting SOX segregation of duties requirements.']
  batch_job_reference varchar [note: 'Technical identifier of the underlying batch job or process execution in the billing system, used for technical troubleshooting and system integration.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by this run, defining the conclusion of the consumption or service period being invoiced.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by this run, defining the beginning of the consumption or service period being invoiced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing run record was first created in the system, establishing the audit trail for billing operations tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this billing run, typically USD for US-based energy utilities.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from actual start to actual end of the billing run, used for performance analysis and capacity planning.']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Total number of critical errors encountered during billing run execution that prevented successful processing of accounts or invoice generation.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'Total number of billing exceptions, warnings, or validation issues encountered during the run that require manual review or correction.']
  execution_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which the billing run was triggered, indicating whether it was automatically scheduled, manually initiated, or executed on-demand.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Accounting date when the billing run results were posted to the general ledger for revenue recognition and accounts receivable booking.']
  gl_posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the financial posting to the general ledger, indicating whether accounts receivable and revenue entries have been successfully recorded.']
  invoice_due_date date [note: 'Standard payment due date applied to invoices generated in this billing run, used for accounts receivable management and collections planning.']
  invoices_generated varchar [note: 'Total number of billing invoices or statements successfully generated and created during this billing run execution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing run record was last updated, supporting change tracking and audit compliance for billing operations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or comments about the billing run execution, capturing special circumstances, manual interventions, or issues requiring follow-up.']
  print_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the print or output batch associated with this billing run, linking invoices to physical or electronic delivery processes.']
  rate_schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the rate schedules and tariff structures applied during this billing run, ensuring regulatory compliance and audit traceability.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this billing run is a reversal run intended to cancel or reverse invoices from a previous billing run due to errors or corrections.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing identifier for the billing run, typically formatted as a sequential or date-based code used for operational tracking and reference in billing operations.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing run execution indicating whether the batch process is pending, actively running, successfully completed, completed with exceptions, failed, or cancelled.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing run indicating whether it is a regular scheduled run, interim billing, final billing, correction run, reversal run, or special ad-hoc billing event.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the billing run was scheduled to begin execution, used for operational planning and SLA tracking.']
  total_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate sum of all billing adjustments, credits, and corrections applied during this billing run, including late fees, discounts, and dispute resolutions.']
  total_billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate sum of all invoice amounts generated in this billing run before adjustments, representing the gross revenue recognized for the billing period.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final aggregate net amount due across all invoices after applying taxes and adjustments, representing the total accounts receivable created by this billing run.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate sum of all tax charges calculated and applied across invoices in this billing run, supporting tax reporting and remittance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a batch billing run execution that generates invoices for a set of service agreements within a billing cycle. Captures run date, billing cycle, number of accounts processed, number of invoices generated, total billed amount, run status (pending, in-progress, completed, error), exception count, and operator. Sourced from SAP IS-U and Oracle CC&B billing engine execution logs. Supports billing operations monitoring and revenue cycle management.'
}

Table billing.revenue_recognition {
  revenue_recognition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for revenue_recognition']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: External and internal audits test revenue recognition entries for compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements. Links revenue entries to the audit engagement where they were tested']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account associated with this revenue recognition entry. Supports multi-account revenue tracking.']
  billing_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agreement for which revenue is being recognized. Links this revenue recognition entry to the underlying customer contract.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — Revenue recognition entries may reference the source invoice for billed revenue. Supports ASC 606 compliance and audit trail.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Revenue recognition entries create or reference journal entries for GL posting. Direct linkage ensures audit trail from billing subledger to general ledger, required for SOX compliance, external audit']
  original_entry_revenue_recognition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original revenue recognition entry being reversed. Populated only when reversal_flag is True. Maintains audit trail for reversals.']
  ppa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.ppa. Business justification: PPA revenue recognition requires contract-specific treatment for bundled energy and RECs under GAAP; direct PPA reference enables proper allocation between energy and environmental attribute revenue s']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rate_schedule. Business justification: Revenue recognition entries are calculated according to rate schedule rules and must be traceable to the rate schedule version in effect. Currently revenue_recognition has rate_schedule_code (STRING) ']
  renewable_ppa_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.ppa_settlement. Business justification: Revenue from PPA settlements must be recognized in correct accounting periods per ASC 606. Financial reporting requires linking settlement amounts to revenue recognition entries for matching generatio']
  revenue_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice document from which this revenue recognition entry was derived. Null for unbilled revenue accruals.']
  sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sox_control. Business justification: Revenue recognition is a key SOX control area for financial statement accuracy. Links specific revenue entries to the SOX control they satisfy, enabling control testing, evidence collection, and audit']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.trade. Business justification: Revenue from wholesale bilateral trades must be recognized per ASC 606 performance obligations; direct trade linkage enables proper revenue timing, contract modification tracking, and GAAP-compliant f']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal accounting period (YYYY-MM format) for which revenue is being recognized. Aligns with financial close calendar and regulatory reporting periods.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustments applied to revenue recognition for corrections, disputes, write-offs, or regulatory true-ups. Positive or negative value.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status of this revenue recognition entry. Ensures SOX-compliant segregation of duties for revenue posting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who approved this revenue recognition entry for posting to the general ledger.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this revenue recognition entry was approved for posting. Part of SOX audit trail.']
  billed_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of recognized revenue that has been invoiced to the customer. Sourced from SAP IS-U billing documents.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'The business area classification for this revenue (e.g., generation, transmission, distribution, retail). Supports segment reporting under GAAP.']
  consumption_kwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'The kilowatt-hours of energy consumption for which revenue is being recognized. Sourced from meter data management system.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The organizational cost center responsible for this revenue stream. Supports internal management accounting and profitability analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this revenue recognition entry record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this revenue recognition entry. Typically USD for US utilities.']
  deferred_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of billed revenue that has been collected but not yet earned. Represents liability for future service delivery under ASC 606.']
  demand_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The peak kilowatt demand for which demand charges are being recognized. Applicable to commercial and industrial rate schedules.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which this revenue recognition entry is posted in SAP S/4HANA FI. Maps to FERC account structure.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person or system process that last modified this revenue recognition entry record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this revenue recognition entry record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  meter_read_date date [note: 'The date of the meter reading that forms the basis for billed revenue in this entry. Null for unbilled accruals or non-metered charges.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context for this revenue recognition entry, including explanations for adjustments or special circumstances.']
  performance_obligation varchar [note: 'Description of the performance obligation satisfied by this revenue recognition entry under ASC 606 (e.g., electricity delivery, capacity reservation).']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date this revenue recognition entry was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. May differ from recognition_date due to period-end adjustments.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'The profit center to which this revenue is allocated for segment reporting and profitability analysis.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'The specific date on which revenue was recognized in the general ledger. Represents the business event timestamp for this revenue recognition transaction.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'The accounting method used to recognize this revenue under ASC 606. Indicates whether revenue is recognized at a point in time or over time.']
  recognized_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total revenue amount recognized in this accounting period for this service agreement and revenue category. Represents earned revenue under ASC 606.']
  regulatory_account_code varchar [note: 'The regulatory accounting code as defined by FERC or state PUC for revenue classification and reporting. Supports regulatory financial statements.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue type being recognized. Distinguishes between energy consumption charges, demand charges, fixed customer charges, regulatory riders, and ancillary services.']
  revenue_class varchar [note: 'Customer class for which revenue is being recognized. Aligns with rate class segmentation and regulatory reporting requirements.']
  revenue_invoice varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — Revenue recognition entries may reference specific invoices for billed revenue. Supports GL reconciliation.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this revenue recognition entry is a reversal of a previous entry. True for reversals, False for original entries.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for reversing this revenue recognition entry (e.g., billing error, customer dispute, regulatory adjustment). Populated only when reversal_flag is True.']
  revrec_to_sa varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — Revenue recognition entries are derived from billed/unbilled revenue for a specific service agreement and accounting period.']
  rider_code varchar [note: 'The regulatory rider or surcharge code applied to this revenue recognition entry (e.g., fuel adjustment clause, renewable energy surcharge).']
  service_agreement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — Revenue recognition entries are derived from billed/unbilled revenue for a specific service agreement. Required for ASC 606 compliance.']
  service_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the service period for which revenue is being recognized. Defines the end of the consumption or service delivery window.']
  service_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the service period for which revenue is being recognized. Defines the beginning of the consumption or service delivery window.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number in the source system that originated this revenue recognition entry (e.g., SAP IS-U billing document number, FI document number).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this revenue recognition entry was sourced (SAP IS-U FICA, Oracle CC&B, SAP S/4HANA FI, or manual entry).']
  tariff_version varchar [note: 'The version of the tariff rate schedule applied for this revenue recognition entry. Ensures auditability of rate changes.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount included in recognized revenue (sales tax, utility tax, franchise fees). Excluded from net revenue for regulatory reporting.']
  unbilled_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of recognized revenue that has been earned but not yet invoiced. Represents accrued revenue for consumption between last meter read and period end.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person or system process that created this revenue recognition entry record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing revenue recognition entries derived from billed and unbilled revenue for a service agreement and accounting period. Stores recognized revenue amount, deferred revenue amount, unbilled revenue accrual, revenue category (energy, demand, customer charge, rider), accounting period, GL account code, and recognition method. Supports SOX-compliant revenue recognition under ASC 606 and regulatory accounting requirements. Sourced from SAP IS-U FICA and SAP S/4HANA FI integration.'
}

Table billing.bill_dispute {
  bill_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bill dispute record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that initiated the dispute.']
  bill_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice being disputed by the customer.']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credit memo document issued to the customer account if a credit was granted as part of the resolution.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the customer service representative or analyst currently assigned to investigate and resolve the dispute.']
  grid_reliability_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_reliability_event. Business justification: Customers dispute bills due to outages or voltage issues caused by grid reliability events. Real business process: dispute investigation requires referencing the causal grid event to validate customer']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — Bill disputes reference a specific invoice being contested. This FK is essential for dispute resolution workflow and PUC complaint tracking.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Disputes often involve meter accuracy, AMI communication failures, or service point configuration issues. This link identifies the physical metering location for field investigation, meter test schedu']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount credited or debited to the customer account as a result of the dispute resolution. Positive values represent credits to customer; negative values represent additional charges.']
  assigned_to_team varchar [note: 'Name or code of the team or department responsible for handling the dispute (e.g., Billing Operations, Meter Services, Revenue Protection).']
  bill_dispute_to_invoice varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — Bill disputes are filed against a specific invoice or charge. This FK is essential for dispute investigation and resolution tracking.']
  bill_invoice varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — Bill disputes are raised against a specific invoice. Critical for dispute resolution workflow and PUC complaint tracking.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the system user or customer service representative who created the dispute record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dispute record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the disputed amount.']
  customer_satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Customer-provided satisfaction score for the dispute resolution process, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique dispute case number assigned by the Customer Information System (CIS) for tracking and customer communication.']
  dispute_open_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was formally opened and logged in the system. Represents the business event timestamp for dispute initiation.']
  dispute_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute record was created in the CIS, including time-of-day for SLA tracking.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the primary reason for the dispute as reported by the customer or identified during investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: estimated_read|meter_malfunction|rate_error|billing_calculation_error|unauthorized_charge|duplicate_charge|service_quality|high_bill_complaint|tariff_misapplication|proration_error|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation of the dispute reason provided by the customer or customer service representative.']
  dispute_source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer initiated the dispute. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: call_center|web_portal|mobile_app|email|mail|in_person|field_service|automated_system — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispute case in the resolution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|under_investigation|pending_customer_response|pending_meter_test|pending_field_inspection|resolved|closed|escalated_to_puc|withdrawn — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_to_sa varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — Bill disputes are associated with a service agreement for customer service tracking and PUC complaint reporting.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount in dispute as claimed by the customer. Represents the portion of the invoice the customer contests.']
  field_inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the field inspection was conducted at the customer premise.']
  field_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a field technician visit to the premise is required to investigate the dispute.']
  investigation_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes and findings documented during the dispute investigation process, including meter test results, field inspection outcomes, and billing recalculations.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal investigation of the dispute began.']
  meter_test_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical meter accuracy test is required as part of the dispute investigation.']
  meter_test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the meter accuracy test if performed, indicating whether the meter was found to be within acceptable tolerance.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the system user who last modified the dispute record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dispute record was last updated.']
  preventable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute was caused by a preventable error or process failure on the utility\'s part, used for quality metrics and process improvement.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification assigned to the dispute based on factors such as disputed amount, customer segment, regulatory risk, or service impact.']
  puc_complaint_number varchar [note: 'Official complaint case number assigned by the Public Utility Commission if the dispute was escalated to regulatory review.']
  puc_escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was formally escalated to the Public Utility Commission.']
  regulatory_escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute has been escalated to the Public Utility Commission (PUC) or other regulatory body for formal complaint review.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was formally resolved and closed.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the resolution decision, including justification for credit issuance, bill upholding, or other actions taken.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute status was changed to resolved or closed.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the dispute was resolved, indicating the action taken to close the case. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_issued|bill_upheld|partial_adjustment|rebill_issued|payment_plan_offered|service_correction|meter_replaced|dispute_withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying root cause identified during investigation, used for process improvement and quality tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: meter_issue|billing_system_error|rate_application_error|customer_misunderstanding|data_entry_error|estimation_error|system_integration_issue|process_failure|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_agreement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — Bill disputes are associated with a service agreement/account. Required for customer service case management.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute resolution exceeded the target SLA timeframe.']
  sla_target_resolution_date date [note: 'Target date by which the dispute must be resolved according to internal service level agreements or regulatory requirements.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a customer-initiated dispute against a billed amount or charge on an invoice. Captures dispute open date, disputed invoice, disputed amount, dispute reason (estimated read, rate error, meter malfunction, unauthorized charge), investigation status, resolution type (credit issued, bill upheld, partial adjustment), resolution date, and regulatory escalation flag. Managed within Oracle CC&B and SAP IS-U. Supports PUC complaint tracking and customer service SLAs.'
}

Table billing.nem_credit {
  nem_credit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for nem_credit']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that earned this NEM credit. Links to the customer billing account with behind-the-meter generation.']
  billing_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_service_agreement. Business justification: NEM credits are earned under a specific service agreement that has NEM tariff terms and interconnection agreement details. Currently nem_credit has account_id and premise_id but no direct link to the ']
  compliance_rec_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.rec_certificate. Business justification: Net energy metering credits for customer-sited generation are often tied to renewable energy certificates for RPS compliance tracking. Links NEM export credits to the REC issued for that generation fo']
  der_interconnection_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.der_interconnection_point. Business justification: NEM credits must reference the specific DER interconnection point for export metering validation, tariff rate application (location-specific export rates), interconnection agreement compliance, and IE']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: NEM credits originate from specific DER installations and must track source system for generation verification, capacity validation, and program eligibility. Required for REC tracking, interconnection']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: Emerging VPP programs allow DR participation for NEM customers with battery storage or controllable DER. Billing systems must track both NEM export credits and DR incentive payments for same customer.']
  generation_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Net metering credits are generated by specific distributed generation assets (solar, wind) registered in asset_registry. Interconnection compliance, regulatory reporting, credit validation, and capaci']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing invoice on which this NEM credit was applied or carried forward.']
  lmp_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.lmp_price. Business justification: NEM export compensation rates in market-based programs reference real-time or day-ahead LMP at premise location for avoided cost valuation; required for tariff compliance, customer bill calculation, a']
  nem_for_agreement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.billing_service_agreement.billing_service_agreement_id — NEM credits are earned by a specific service agreement with behind-the-meter generation. This FK links credits to the customer\'s NEM billing arrangement.']
  net_energy_metering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.net_energy_metering. Business justification: NEM credits are generated from specific DER installations (net_energy_metering records). This link tracks which solar/storage system produced the export, supports RPS compliance reporting, interconnec']
  original_credit_nem_credit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original NEM credit transaction that this record adjusts or corrects, establishing audit trail for credit modifications.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise where the behind-the-meter generation equipment (solar, battery storage) is installed.']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying retail rate schedule that determines the credit valuation methodology for exported energy.']
  rec_holding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.rec_holding. Business justification: Net metering programs bundling RECs with energy credits require tracking which REC inventory supports customer credits for RPS compliance verification, renewable portfolio standard reporting, and envi']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: NEM credits and distributed generation exports must be reported in RPS compliance filings and renewable energy program reports. Links NEM credits to the regulatory filing where they were reported for ']
  renewable_ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.ppa_contract. Business justification: Some NEM arrangements involve PPA contracts for pricing export compensation above standard tariff rates. Business model for community solar, virtual net metering, and third-party owned systems where P']
  adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit transaction is an adjustment or correction to a previously posted NEM credit.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the reason for adjustment to the NEM credit (e.g., meter read correction, tariff change, billing error correction).']
  billing_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the billing cycle schedule during which this NEM credit was calculated and posted.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system process identifier that created this NEM credit record, supporting audit and compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this NEM credit record was first created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of NEM credits earned during this period, calculated as exported_energy_kwh multiplied by credit_rate_per_kwh.']
  credit_applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of NEM credits applied to offset current billing period charges, reducing the customer\'s net invoice amount.']
  credit_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which unused NEM credits from this transaction will expire if not applied, per state NEM program rules (typically 12 months).']
  credit_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period during which the excess energy was exported to the grid and NEM credits were earned.']
  credit_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period during which the excess energy was exported to the grid and NEM credits were earned.']
  credit_rate_per_kwh decimal(12,6) [note: 'Dollar amount per kWh credited to the customer for exported energy, as defined by the applicable NEM tariff schedule.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NEM credit transaction in the billing and accounts receivable system.']
  credit_transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the NEM credit transaction, used for customer communication and dispute resolution.']
  credit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the NEM credit based on the value stream being compensated (energy, capacity, environmental attributes).']
  cumulative_credit_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Running total of unused NEM credits available on the customer account after this transaction, including prior period carryover.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this credit transaction (typically USD).']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has disputed the NEM credit calculation or application, triggering investigation and resolution workflow.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the reason for customer dispute of the NEM credit (e.g., incorrect meter reading, wrong tariff applied, calculation error).']
  exported_energy_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total kilowatt-hours of excess energy exported from the customer\'s behind-the-meter generation to the utility grid during the credit period.']
  generation_technology_type varchar [note: 'Type of behind-the-meter distributed energy resource (DER) technology generating the exported energy.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for posting NEM credit transactions to the financial accounting system, supporting revenue recognition and regulatory reporting.']
  installed_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Nameplate capacity in kilowatts of the customer\'s behind-the-meter generation equipment registered for NEM participation.']
  interconnection_agreement_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the utility interconnection agreement authorizing the customer\'s DER to export energy to the grid.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'State or regulatory jurisdiction code governing the NEM program rules and tariff provisions applicable to this credit.']
  meter_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bidirectional meter measuring both imported and exported energy at the customer premise.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system process identifier that last modified this NEM credit record, supporting audit and compliance requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this NEM credit record was last modified or updated.']
  nem_tariff_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific NEM tariff schedule version applicable to this credit transaction (e.g., NEM 1.0, NEM 2.0, NEM 3.0).']
  nem_tariff_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the NEM tariff schedule, reflecting regulatory changes and grandfathering provisions.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the NEM credit transaction was posted to the customer account in the billing system.']
  regulatory_account_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory accounting code for tracking NEM program costs and benefits in rate case filings and PUC compliance reports.']
  renewable_energy_certificate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether renewable energy certificates associated with the exported energy are retained by the customer or transferred to the utility.']
  rps_compliance_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exported energy qualifies for the utility\'s RPS compliance obligations under state renewable energy mandates.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Utility service territory code where the customer premise and DER interconnection are located.']
  tou_period_code varchar [note: 'Time-of-use period classification for exported energy, affecting credit rate when NEM tariff includes TOU differentiation.']
  true_up_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit transaction is part of an annual true-up reconciliation period where unused credits are settled or forfeited.']
  true_up_settlement_date date [note: 'Date of the annual true-up settlement when cumulative NEM credits are reconciled and excess credits are either paid out or forfeited per tariff rules.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of NEM (Net Energy Metering) credits earned by a customer with behind-the-meter generation (rooftop solar, battery storage) for excess energy exported to the grid. Captures credit period, exported kWh, credit rate ($/kWh), credit amount, cumulative credit balance, credit expiration date, and applicable NEM tariff version. Sourced from Oracle CC&B NEM billing module. Supports state RPS and NEM regulatory compliance reporting.'
}

Table billing.assistance_program {
  assistance_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for assistance_program']
  billing_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service agreement receiving the assistance program benefit.']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Equity-focused interconnection programs (SGIP equity resiliency budget, community solar for low-income) coordinate with utility financial assistance programs. Business process: Eligibility verificatio']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.irp_scenario. Business justification: Low-income assistance programs are designed and funded based on rate impact projections from IRP scenarios. Program budgets and eligibility thresholds reference specific IRP scenarios to ensure afford']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Low-income assistance programs require annual reporting to regulatory commissions on enrollment, costs, and effectiveness. Links the program to the regulatory filing where program performance and cost']
  renewable_incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.incentive_program. Business justification: Low-income assistance programs often stack with renewable incentives for solar installations, energy efficiency, and green tariffs. Regulatory coordination required to ensure customers receive combine']
  application_date date [note: 'Date the customer submitted the application for the assistance program.']
  application_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the customer applied for the assistance program.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the customer application was approved for enrollment in the assistance program.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the representative who approved the assistance program enrollment.']
  assistance_to_sa varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — After merging program_enrollment, the assistance_program entity needs a link to service_agreement to track which accounts are enrolled in which programs.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment is eligible for automatic renewal without re-application.']
  benefit_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial benefit provided by the assistance program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assistance program enrollment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD).']
  disenrollment_date date [note: 'Date the customer was disenrolled or removed from the assistance program.']
  disenrollment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for customer disenrollment from the assistance program (e.g., income exceeded threshold, failed recertification, customer request, account closed, moved out of service territory).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the assistance program benefits cease applying to the customer account. Null for open-ended enrollments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the assistance program benefits begin applying to the customer account.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of the eligibility requirements for the assistance program (e.g., income threshold, household size, participation in other programs).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the customer was enrolled in the financial assistance program.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the customer enrollment in the assistance program.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of funding for the assistance program benefits.']
  income_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual household income threshold for program eligibility.']
  income_threshold_percentage_fpl decimal(5,2) [note: 'Income threshold expressed as a percentage of the Federal Poverty Level (e.g., 200.00 for 200% of FPL).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the representative who last modified this assistance program enrollment record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assistance program enrollment record was last updated in the system.']
  maximum_benefit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total benefit amount the customer can receive under the assistance program during the enrollment period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the assistance program enrollment, eligibility determination, or special circumstances.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the assistance program type (e.g., LIHEAP, CARE, FERA, AMP).']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the financial assistance program (e.g., Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program, California Alternate Rates for Energy, Family Electric Rate Assistance).']
  program_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the program period or program year for which this enrollment is valid.']
  program_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the program period or program year for which this enrollment is valid.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assistance program benefit structure.']
  puc_docket_number varchar [note: 'PUC docket or proceeding number under which the assistance program was approved.']
  regulatory_mandate varchar [note: 'Regulatory requirement or mandate authorizing the assistance program (e.g., PUC decision number, state statute reference).']
  service_agreement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — Program enrollments (now within assistance_program) are linked to a specific service agreement. Required for benefit application and eligibility tracking.']

  Note: 'Reference and transactional entity defining utility-administered financial assistance programs and tracking individual customer enrollments. At the program level: captures program name (LIHEAP, CARE, FERA, arrearage management), eligibility criteria, benefit type (discount rate, bill credit, arrearage forgiveness), maximum benefit amount, funding source, regulatory mandate, and program period. At the enrollment level: captures enrollment date, enrolled service agreement, program type, eligibility verification status, benefit amount, enrollment expiration date, re-certification due date, disenrollment reason, and enrollment status. Sourced from Oracle CC&B program management and SAP IS-U. SSOT for all financial assistance program definitions and customer enrollment records. Supports PUC low-income program compliance reporting and customer affordability tracking.'
}

Table billing.billing_program_enrollment {
  billing_program_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer program enrollment record. Primary key for the program enrollment entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account enrolled in the program. Links to the customer account master entity.']
  billing_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the utility employee who approved the program enrollment application. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  billing_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this program enrollment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Customer program enrollments (demand response, energy efficiency, low-income assistance) are tracked under compliance programs for regulatory reporting and program administration. Links enrollment to ']
  demand_dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_enrollment. Business justification: Generic program enrollment table tracks all utility program participation including DR alongside low-income assistance, budget billing, etc. Customer service, program administration, and unified custo']
  green_tariff_subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.green_tariff_subscription. Business justification: Green tariff subscriptions are a specific type of program enrollment where customers purchase renewable energy blocks or percentages. Direct relationship required for billing premium charges, allocati']
  payment_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated payment arrangement for arrearage management program enrollments. Links to payment arrangement master entity.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise associated with this program enrollment. Links to the premise master entity.']
  renewable_incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.incentive_program. Business justification: Customers enroll in renewable incentive programs through utility billing systems for solar rebates, green tariffs, and DER programs. Enrollment tracking links customer accounts to program eligibility,']
  sgip_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.sgip_enrollment. Business justification: SGIP (Self-Generation Incentive Program) enrollment for battery storage requires coordinated enrollment in specific utility rate schedules (TOU rates for optimal dispatch). Business process: Program a']
  application_date date [note: 'Date the customer submitted the program enrollment application. Used for tracking application processing time and eligibility determination.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the program enrollment application was approved by the utility. Used for processing time metrics and audit trail.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program enrollment automatically renews upon expiration (True) or requires manual re-enrollment (False). Used for enrollment lifecycle management.']
  benefit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monthly or per-billing-cycle discount or credit amount provided to the customer under this program enrollment. Used for rate calculation and revenue impact analysis. Expressed in local currency.']
  benefit_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to eligible charges under this program enrollment (e.g., 20% discount on energy charges for CARE customers). Used when benefit is calculated as a percentage rather than fixed amount.']
  budget_billing_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly payment amount for budget billing program enrollments. Calculated based on historical usage and periodically adjusted. Expressed in local currency.']
  budget_billing_review_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date for budget billing amount review and adjustment. Typically occurs annually or semi-annually to true-up actual vs. budgeted usage.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which customer consent or authorization was obtained. Used for compliance tracking and audit trail.']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'Method by which customer consent was obtained: written signature, electronic acceptance, verbal authorization (recorded), or implied consent.']
  consent_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer consent or authorization was received for program enrollment (True/False). Required for regulatory compliance and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this program enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Type of discount calculation method applied: fixed amount (flat monthly credit), percentage (percent off eligible charges), tiered (discount varies by usage tier), usage-based (discount calculated from consumption).']
  disenrollment_date date [note: 'Date the customer was disenrolled or removed from the program. Null for active enrollments. Used for churn analysis and program retention metrics.']
  disenrollment_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Party that initiated the disenrollment: customer (voluntary opt-out), utility (administrative removal), automatic (system-triggered expiration), regulator (compliance action).']
  disenrollment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for program disenrollment: customer request (voluntary withdrawal), ineligibility (no longer qualifies), non-payment (payment arrangement breach), moved (service transfer), deceased, program ended (utility discontinued), failed re-certification (did not complete renewal). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|ineligibility|non_payment|moved|deceased|program_ended|failed_recertification — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the program enrollment expires or terminates. Null for open-ended enrollments. Used for automatic disenrollment processing and re-certification scheduling.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the program benefits and terms become effective for billing and rate calculation purposes. May differ from enrollment date due to billing cycle alignment.']
  eligibility_verification_date date [note: 'Date on which eligibility verification was completed and approved. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting to regulatory authorities.']
  eligibility_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify customer eligibility for the program: income documentation (pay stubs/tax returns), categorical eligibility (participation in other assistance programs), third-party verification (government database check), self-certification, or automatic enrollment.']
  eligibility_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the eligibility verification process for income-qualified or regulated programs: not verified (initial state), verified (eligibility confirmed), pending verification (under review), verification failed (did not meet criteria), exempt (no verification required).']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer enrolled in the program: online portal, phone call center, mail application, in-person at office, automatic enrollment by utility, or third-party agency.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the customer was officially enrolled in the program and benefits became effective. Key business event timestamp for program participation lifecycle.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique enrollment number assigned to this program participation record. Used for customer communication and service inquiries.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the program enrollment: pending (application submitted), active (enrolled and receiving benefits), suspended (temporarily inactive), expired (term ended), cancelled (customer or utility terminated), denied (application rejected).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Scheduled expiration date for time-limited program enrollments requiring periodic re-certification (e.g., annual CARE/FERA re-qualification). Triggers re-certification workflow.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this program enrollment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  last_recertification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful re-certification or eligibility verification. Used to calculate next re-certification due date and track compliance history.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the program enrollment. Used for documenting special circumstances, customer requests, or processing exceptions.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment has priority status for special handling (True/False). Used for life support, medical baseline, or critical care programs requiring expedited service.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the utility billing program type (e.g., CARE, FERA, BUDGET, AUTOPAY, PAPERLESS, ARREARAGE, TOU, CPP, NEM, DR).']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the utility billing program (e.g., California Alternate Rates for Energy, Family Electric Rate Assistance, Budget Billing Plan, Auto-Pay Enrollment, Paperless Billing, Arrearage Management Program).']
  program_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the program category: rate assistance programs (CARE/FERA), payment plans (budget billing/arrearage management), billing convenience (auto-pay/paperless), energy efficiency, demand response, net metering, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rate_assistance|payment_plan|billing_convenience|energy_efficiency|demand_response|net_metering|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  puc_approval_number varchar [note: 'PUC decision or resolution number authorizing this program. Required for regulatory compliance and audit documentation.']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Rate schedule or tariff code applicable to this program enrollment. Used for billing calculation and rate application. May differ from standard rate for discount programs.']
  recertification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer must complete re-certification to maintain program eligibility. Applicable to income-qualified and regulatory programs requiring periodic eligibility verification.']
  regulatory_program_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance program code assigned by the Public Utility Commission (PUC) or governing body. Used for regulatory reporting and cost recovery tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional master entity representing a customer account enrollment in a utility billing program such as CARE, FERA, budget billing, auto-pay, paperless billing, or arrearage management. Captures enrollment date, program type, eligibility verification status, benefit amount, enrollment expiration date, re-certification due date, and disenrollment reason. Managed within Oracle CC&B and SAP IS-U. SSOT for all customer program participation records in the billing domain.'
}

Table billing.ar_ledger {
  ar_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ar_ledger']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with this AR ledger entry. Links to the customer account master.']
  ar_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AR ledger entries directly map to GL accounts for subledger-to-GL reconciliation. Essential for month-end close, external audits, SOX compliance, and ensuring billing subledger balances tie to general']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice document that originated this AR ledger entry, if applicable. Null for non-invoice postings such as payments or adjustments.']
  collections_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated collections case if this AR ledger entry is part of an active collections effort. Null if no collections case exists.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — AR ledger postings reference specific invoices for open item management. Required for FICA clearing and SOX controls.']
  payment_arrangement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment arrangement plan if this AR ledger entry is covered by an installment agreement. Null if no payment arrangement exists.']
  primary_ar_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the authorized approver who approved this write-off. Null for non-write-off entries. Required for SOX internal controls and audit trail.']
  primary_invoice_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.invoice.invoice_id — AR ledger entries reference the source invoice document for open item management and clearing.']
  aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Classification of this AR ledger entry based on the number of days past due. Used for aging reports and collections prioritization. Calculated from posting date and due date.']
  ar_to_sa varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — AR ledger entries are posted for a specific service agreement. This FK is the backbone of the customer AR sub-ledger.']
  bad_debt_gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code for bad debt expense associated with this write-off. Null for non-write-off entries. Used for financial statement preparation.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'The date on which this AR ledger entry was cleared (fully paid or offset). Null for open items.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this AR ledger entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the amounts in this AR ledger entry. Typically USD for US utilities.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this AR ledger entry is a debit (increases receivable balance) or credit (decreases receivable balance). Invoices and adjustments are typically debits; payments and refunds are credits.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer initiated a dispute for this AR ledger entry. Null if no dispute exists.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this AR ledger entry is under dispute by the customer. True if disputed; false otherwise. Disputed items may be excluded from collections activity.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the customer dispute. Examples include billing error, meter reading issue, rate dispute. Null if no dispute exists.']
  document_date date [note: 'The business date of the underlying transaction or event that gave rise to this AR ledger entry. May differ from posting date for backdated or future-dated transactions.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique document number for this AR ledger entry as recorded in the financial system. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial document represented by this AR ledger entry. Determines the nature of the posting and its impact on the customer balance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: invoice|payment|adjustment|write-off|refund|credit_memo|debit_memo|reversal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment is expected for this AR ledger entry. Used for aging calculations and collections prioritization. Null for non-invoice postings.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which this AR ledger entry was posted. Used for monthly financial reporting and variance analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this AR ledger entry was posted. Used for period-based financial reporting and year-end closing processes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this AR ledger entry record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, explanations, or special handling instructions related to this AR ledger entry. Used for audit trail and customer service reference.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original posted amount of this AR ledger entry in the transaction currency. Represents the gross amount before any partial payments or adjustments.']
  outstanding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unpaid balance of this AR ledger entry in the transaction currency. Equals original amount minus any payments or offsets applied. Zero for cleared items.']
  payment_method_code varchar [note: 'The payment method used for this AR ledger entry if it represents a payment. Examples include check, credit card, ACH, cash. Null for non-payment entries.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the payment transaction, such as check number, credit card authorization code, or ACH trace number. Null for non-payment entries.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which this AR ledger entry was posted to the financial system. Used for period-based financial reporting and aging analysis.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the AR ledger entry. Open items represent outstanding receivables; cleared items have been fully paid or offset.']
  recovery_tracking_status varchar [note: 'Status of efforts to recover a written-off receivable. Tracks whether the utility is still pursuing collection or has abandoned recovery efforts. Not applicable for non-write-off entries.']
  revenue_class varchar [note: 'Classification of revenue type for regulatory and financial reporting purposes. Examples include residential, commercial, industrial, street lighting.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue associated with this AR ledger entry is recognized for financial reporting purposes. May differ from posting date based on revenue recognition policies.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this AR ledger entry is a reversal of a previous posting. True for reversal entries; false otherwise. Used for audit trail and error correction tracking.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the original AR ledger entry that this reversal entry is offsetting. Null for non-reversal entries. Establishes audit trail linkage.']
  service_agreement varchar [note: 'FK to billing.service_agreement.service_agreement_id — AR ledger postings are recorded against a service agreement/account. SSOT for customer AR balance.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the geographic service territory or regulatory jurisdiction associated with this AR ledger entry. Used for regulatory reporting and revenue allocation.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount written off as uncollectable bad debt. Null for non-write-off entries. Represents the reduction in accounts receivable and corresponding bad debt expense.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'The date on which this receivable was written off as uncollectable. Null for non-write-off entries. Used for bad debt expense tracking and PUC reporting.']
  write_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this AR ledger entry represents a write-off of uncollectable debt. True for write-off postings; false otherwise.']
  write_off_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for writing off this receivable. Used for bad debt analysis and regulatory reporting. Null for non-write-off entries.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity representing the accounts receivable sub-ledger for customer billing activity, capturing all open item postings including invoiced amounts, payments applied, adjustments, write-offs, balance carry-forwards, and bad debt entries. Stores posting date, document type (invoice, payment, adjustment, write-off, refund), debit/credit indicator, amount, clearing status, clearing date, and GL account. For write-off postings: captures write-off date, write-off amount, reason (bankruptcy, skip, hardship, deceased), approval authority, bad debt GL account, recovery tracking status, and associated collections case reference. Sourced from SAP IS-U FICA open item management and SAP S/4HANA AR sub-ledger. SSOT for customer AR balance, write-off tracking, and bad debt management supporting SOX financial controls and PUC uncollectable expense reporting.'
}

Table billing.refund {
  refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the refund transaction. Primary key for the refund product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that received the refund. Links to the billing account in the customer domain.']
  bill_dispute_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer dispute or complaint case that triggered this refund. Links to customer service resolution tracking.']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credit memo document that authorized this refund. Links to the billing adjustment that created the refundable credit.']
  deposit_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.deposit.deposit_id — Deposit refunds are a primary refund type. This FK links the refund disbursement back to the originating deposit for financial reconciliation. Note: not all refunds are deposit-related, so this is a n']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Refunds post to specific GL accounts to reverse revenue or record expense. Link enables proper financial statement presentation, cash flow tracking, and regulatory reporting of customer refunds and ov']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice that generated the credit or overpayment leading to this refund. May be null for deposit returns.']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment. Business justification: Refunds can be issued for overpayments, and linking to the original payment enables full audit trail and reconciliation. Currently refund has account_id and invoice_id but no link to the payment being']
  refund_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the employee who authorized the refund. Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  refund_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the employee who last modified this refund record. Required for SOX audit trail and change tracking.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.work_order. Business justification: Refunds often result from service quality issues, meter inaccuracies, or outage credits documented in work orders. Customer service, audit trails, and regulatory compliance require traceability from r']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross monetary value of the refund issued to the customer before any adjustments or withholdings.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the refund was formally approved by an authorized user. Establishes the point of financial commitment.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the refund approval workflow. Tracks whether the refund has passed required authorization controls.']
  approval_threshold_level varchar [note: 'The authorization level required for this refund based on amount thresholds and business rules. Supports tiered approval workflows.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'The customer bank account number for ACH or wire transfer refunds. Encrypted at rest per PCI-DSS requirements.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'The ABA routing transit number for the customer financial institution. Required for ACH and wire transfer processing.']
  cleared_date date [note: 'The date when the refund payment was confirmed as cleared or cashed by the customer or their bank. Used for cash flow reconciliation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The organizational cost center responsible for this refund. Used for internal financial reporting and budget tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when this refund record was first created in the billing system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund transaction. Typically USD for US-based utilities.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'The date when the refund payment was physically issued or electronically transmitted to the customer or their financial institution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when this refund record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  net_refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final amount disbursed to the customer after all adjustments, withholdings, and offsets have been applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, special instructions, or audit notes related to the refund transaction.']
  originating_credit_source varchar [note: 'The upstream transaction or business process that created the credit balance leading to this refund. Enables traceability to root cause.']
  payee_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the refund recipient. Used for check mailing and payment verification.']
  payee_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number. Optional address component for refund delivery.']
  payee_city varchar [note: 'City name for the refund recipient address. Required for payment processing and tax reporting.']
  payee_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the refund recipient address. Used for international payment processing and compliance.']
  payee_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the individual or entity receiving the refund. May differ from account holder for third-party payments.']
  payee_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the refund recipient address. Required for payment delivery and address validation.']
  payee_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the refund recipient address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination and regulatory reporting.']
  payment_instrument_number varchar [note: 'The check number, ACH trace number, wire confirmation number, or other instrument identifier for the disbursement. Used for reconciliation.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Detailed code identifying the specific reason for the refund. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement tracking.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable explanation of why the refund was issued. Provides context for customer service and audit purposes.']
  refund_date date [note: 'The business date when the refund was officially issued or approved for disbursement. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The disbursement instrument or mechanism used to deliver the refund to the customer. Determines processing time and cost.']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique refund document number used for customer communication and audit trails. Externally visible identifier.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the refund transaction. Tracks progression from approval through disbursement and clearing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|rejected|issued|cleared|cancelled|voided — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the refund based on the originating business reason. Determines accounting treatment and regulatory reporting category.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this refund must be included in regulatory filings to state PUC or FERC. True if reportable under utility commission rules.']
  revenue_class varchar [note: 'The customer class or rate category associated with this refund. Used for regulatory reporting and revenue analysis.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'The geographic service territory or jurisdiction where the refunded service was provided. Used for regulatory compliance reporting.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this refund is subject to SOX internal control testing and audit procedures. True for material transactions requiring enhanced controls.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the refund amount representing sales tax, utility tax, or other regulatory taxes being returned to the customer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a refund issued to a customer for overpayment, deposit return, credit balance, or billing error correction. Captures refund date, amount, method (check, ACH, account credit), reason code, originating credit source, approval status, and disbursement confirmation. Sourced from SAP IS-U FICA and Oracle CC&B. Supports cash disbursement controls and SOX compliance.'
}

Table billing.service_agreement_incentive_enrollment {
  service_agreement_incentive_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this enrollment record. Primary key for the association.']
  billing_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the billing service agreement participating in this enrollment.']
  renewable_incentive_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the renewable incentive program in which the service agreement is enrolled']
  service_agreement_billing_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the billing service agreement enrolled in the incentive program']
  application_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the program application or approval process for this enrollment.']
  approved_incentive_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total incentive amount approved for this service agreement under this specific program in USD. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this enrollment indicating whether the service agreement meets ongoing program requirements. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when incentive payments cease for this enrollment. Null for active enrollments with no scheduled end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when incentive payments begin for this enrollment. May differ from enrollment_date due to approval processing time.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the service agreement was enrolled into this specific incentive program. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this enrollment indicating whether it is actively receiving incentive payments.']
  last_compliance_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance verification for this enrollment.']
  next_payment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next incentive payment for this enrollment. Null for one-time payments already disbursed.']
  payment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment schedule for this enrollment: one_time, monthly, quarterly, annual, or per_kwh_generated. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  total_paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount paid to date for this specific enrollment in USD. Tracks actual payments made under this program-agreement combination. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment relationship between a billing service agreement and a renewable incentive program. It captures the operational enrollment of a specific service agreement into a specific incentive program, tracking approval, payment, and compliance status. Each record links one billing service agreement to one incentive program with attributes that exist only in the context of this enrollment relationship.. Existence Justification: In energy utilities operations, a single billing service agreement can simultaneously participate in multiple renewable incentive programs (e.g., a residential solar customer enrolled in both a solar rebate program and a green tariff program and a low-income assistance program). Conversely, each incentive program serves many service agreements across the utility\'s customer base. The business actively manages these enrollments as operational entities, tracking approval amounts, payment schedules, compliance status, and payment history for each program-agreement combination.'
}

Table billing.credit_memo {
  credit_memo_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for credit_memo']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Account to which the credit memo is applied.']
  original_credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on credit_memo (original_credit_memo_id)']
  original_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that the credit memo offsets.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the credit.']
  reversal_of_memo_id varchar [note: 'If this memo reverses a prior credit memo, reference to that memo.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Category of financial adjustment represented by the credit memo.']
  amount_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total credit amount before taxes, discounts, or adjustments.']
  amount_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final credit amount after tax and discount adjustments.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the credit memo.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the credit memo.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credit memo was approved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch run that created or processed the credit memo.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the credit memo.']
