  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the credit memo.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the credit must be applied or settled.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the credit memo to an external system (e.g., SAP, Oracle).']
  is_refund boolean [note: 'True if the credit memo results in a cash refund to the customer.']
  memo_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credit memo was issued to the customer.']
  memo_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the credit memo for customer and accounting purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the credit memo.']
  original_invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice number associated with the original charge.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment or refund was executed.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to process any refund associated with the credit memo.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Internal status of the credit memo processing workflow.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why the credit memo was issued (e.g., overpayment, service credit).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the reason for the credit.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the credit memo is a reversal of a previous transaction.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date the credit memo was settled against the account.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the credit memo.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component of the credit memo.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Applicable tax code used for tax calculation on the credit.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credit memo is exempt from tax.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the credit memo record.']
  write_off_flag boolean [note: 'True if the credit memo amount was written off as uncollectible.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the credit memo record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for credit_memo. Referenced by credit_memo_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for billing_period. Referenced by billing_period_id.'

  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the employee who approved the disconnect order.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the disconnect order record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the order.']
  deposit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Security deposit held for the account at the time of disconnect.']
  disconnect_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the service disconnect is being requested or mandated.']
  disconnect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the disconnect (e.g., voluntary customer request vs. involuntary for safety).']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'True if the disconnect order is under a customer dispute.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Projected time in minutes required to complete the disconnect.']
  gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount before any adjustments, taxes, or discounts.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special instructions.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the disconnect order for customer‑facing communication.']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the disconnect order was created or submitted by the customer.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Urgency level assigned to the disconnect order.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is subject to special regulatory reporting (e.g., SOX, NERC).']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the disconnect order was later reversed or reinstated.']
  scheduled_disconnect_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for the disconnection to occur.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the disconnect order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the disconnect order record.']
  write_off_flag boolean [note: 'True if the outstanding balance was written off as uncollectible.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for disconnect_order. Referenced by disconnect_order_id.'
}

Table billing.dunning_run {
  dunning_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for dunning_run']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary customer account targeted by the dunning run.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User responsible for initiating or overseeing the dunning run.']
  last_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the previous dunning run that this run may be a retry of.']
  previous_dunning_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dunning_run (previous_dunning_run_id)']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the schedule definition that triggered the run.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., 2023Q4) for which the dunning run is accounted.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total of discounts, fees, or write‑offs applied during the run.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'External batch identifier used by the processing engine.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred communication channel used for dunning (letter, email, phone, sms).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dunning run record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for monetary values.']
  deposit_flag boolean [note: 'True if the run processes deposit‑related dunning actions.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or notes for the run.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the outstanding balances should be collected.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Highest escalation level applied in this run (e.g., 1‑3).']
  dunning_strategy_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the collection strategy (e.g., standard, aggressive).']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dunning run completed or was terminated.']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Count of error records encountered during processing.']
  failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of accounts that failed processing.']
  financial_period varchar [note: 'Financial reporting period aligned with the run\'s revenue recognition.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'GL account code to which dunning write‑offs are posted.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of original invoice amounts before any adjustments.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount due after adjustments; used for accounting postings.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational comments captured during execution.']
  payment_plan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the run includes accounts on a payment arrangement.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the run for scheduling purposes.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of financial reconciliation for the run\'s postings.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region identifier (e.g., three‑letter ISO region) associated with the run.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the run has been re‑executed after failure.']
  run_comment varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comment captured at run completion.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential number assigned to the dunning run.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the run was generated automatically by the system or initiated manually.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start time for the dunning run as defined by scheduling logic.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dunning run processing began.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dunning run.']
  success_count varchar [note: 'Number of accounts successfully processed without errors.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification applied to adjustments within the run.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Sub‑regional sales or service territory linked to the accounts in the run.']
  total_accounts varchar [note: 'Number of distinct accounts included in this dunning run.']
  total_outstanding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount overdue across all accounts in the run.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last modified the dunning run record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dunning run record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the dunning run record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for dunning_run. Referenced by dunning_run_id.'
}

Table billing.dunning_schedule {
  dunning_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for dunning_schedule']
  superseded_dunning_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dunning_schedule (superseded_dunning_schedule_id)']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Method used to contact the customer for this dunning step.']
  amount_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum outstanding amount that must be met before this dunning step applies.']
  compliance_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance code associated with this dunning schedule (e.g., SOX control reference).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dunning schedule record was first created in the system.']
  days_before_action varchar [note: 'Number of days after the invoice due date before this dunning action is triggered.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the schedule purpose and any special handling notes.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Ordinal level of the dunning step (e.g., 1 = first reminder, 2 = second reminder).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the dunning schedule becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the dunning schedule expires; null for open‑ended schedules.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of how far the dunning process has escalated.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Additional days granted after the action before further escalation.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Interest rate applied to the overdue balance for this dunning step, expressed as a percentage.']
  max_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this dunning step may be repeated before moving to the next level.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal comments or audit notes related to the schedule.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the dunning notice.']
  penalty_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed penalty fee charged when this dunning step is executed.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the dunning schedule used for reporting and UI display.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule indicating whether it follows a standard, custom, or regulatory pattern.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dunning schedule record.']
  waive_interest_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether interest is waived for this dunning step (true = waived).']
  waive_penalty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the penalty fee is waived for this dunning step (true = waived).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for dunning_schedule. Referenced by schedule_id.'
}

Table grid.control_area {
  control_area_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the balancing authority control area. Primary key for the control area master record.']
  contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Control areas require designated responsible operator for NERC CPS1/CPS2 accountability and reliability coordinator communications. \'contact_name/phone/email\' denormalized fields should be FK to emplo']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Control areas are operational units that incur costs (staffing, SCADA, EMS maintenance). Finance tracks these costs by control area for FERC Form 1 reporting, rate case cost allocation, and operationa']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Control areas represent distinct operational/geographic segments for P&L reporting. Utilities track revenue and costs by control area for segment reporting, regulatory jurisdiction analysis, and RTO/I']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Each control area operates under a NERC Reliability Standards compliance program. This link tracks which compliance program governs the control area\'s operations, enabling audit scoping, program effec']
  ace_tolerance_limit_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable Area Control Error in megawatts before corrective action is required, used for automatic generation control and NERC BAL-001 compliance.']
  agc_mode varchar [note: 'Current operating mode of the automatic generation control system for this control area.']
  balancing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the balancing authority organization operating this control area.']
  contingency_analysis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time contingency analysis is active for N-1 and N-2 security assessment.']
  control_area_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code identifying the control area, typically 2-6 uppercase letters used in interchange scheduling and NERC reporting.']
  cps1_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target CPS1 compliance percentage (minimum 100%) measuring the control area\'s contribution to interconnection frequency error over a rolling 12-month period.']
  cps2_limit_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'CPS2 compliance limit percentage (minimum 90%) measuring the control area\'s ACE performance within defined bounds during 10-minute periods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this control area master record was first created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this control area record originated (e.g., EMS, SCADA, PI Historian, NERC Registry).']
  disturbance_control_standard_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Most severe single contingency loss in megawatts that the control area must be prepared to recover from within 15 minutes, per NERC BAL-002 standard.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this control area ceased operations or when the current configuration was superseded, null if currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this control area became operational or when the current configuration became effective.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the primary EMS platform used for real-time monitoring and control of this control area.']
  frequency_bias_setting_mw_per_hz decimal(10,2) [note: 'Frequency bias setting in MW per 0.1 Hz used in ACE calculation, representing the control area\'s expected response to frequency deviations.']
  geographic_boundary_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic and electrical boundaries defining the control area\'s operational footprint.']
  installed_generation_capacity_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total installed generation capacity in megawatts available within the control area boundaries.']
  interconnection_name varchar [note: 'Name of the major North American interconnection to which this control area belongs (Eastern, Western, Texas, or Quebec).']
  l10_limit_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'L10 parameter used in CPS1 (Control Performance Standard 1) calculation, representing the control area\'s frequency bias obligation in megawatts per 0.1 Hz.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this control area master record.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity responsible for reliability oversight of this control area (e.g., WECC, NPCC, MRO, SERC, RF, TRE). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|NPCC|MRO|SERC|RF|TRE|FRCC|SPP RE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nerc_registered_entity_code varchar [note: 'Official NERC registry identifier for the balancing authority entity responsible for this control area.']
  nominal_frequency_hz decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target system frequency for this control area in Hertz, typically 60.00 Hz in North America or 50.00 Hz in other regions.']
  non_spinning_reserve_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of non-spinning (offline) reserve in megawatts that can be deployed within 10 minutes for contingency response.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional operational notes, configuration details, or special instructions related to this control area.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the control area in the grid operations environment.']
  peak_load_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Historical or forecasted peak electrical load in megawatts for this control area, used for capacity planning and reserve calculations.']
  pi_historian_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'Standard tag prefix used in OSIsoft PI historian for all telemetry points associated with this control area.']
  regulation_reserve_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of regulation reserve in megawatts maintained for automatic generation control and frequency regulation.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory jurisdiction governing this control area (e.g., FERC, state PUC, provincial authority).']
  reserve_requirement_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum operating reserve requirement in megawatts that must be maintained for reliability and contingency response.']
  rto_iso_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the RTO or ISO market operator with which this control area is affiliated, if applicable (e.g., PJM, CAISO, MISO, SPP, NYISO, ISO-NE, ERCOT).']
  scada_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SCADA telemetry integration is active for real-time data collection from substations and generation facilities.']
  spinning_reserve_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of spinning (synchronized) reserve in megawatts available for immediate deployment in response to contingencies.']
  state_estimator_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EMS state estimator function is active for real-time network model solving and contingency analysis.']
  tie_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of transmission tie lines connecting this control area to adjacent control areas for interchange scheduling and metering.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Primary time zone for control area operations and scheduling, using standard abbreviations (e.g., EST, CST, MST, PST, EPT, CPT, MPT, PPT).']

  Note: 'Master record for a balancing authority control area — the fundamental geographic and electrical boundary within which the EMS/SCADA system maintains real-time generation-load balance, frequency regulation, and interchange scheduling. Captures control area identifier, NERC registered entity ID, balancing authority code, nominal frequency (60 Hz), ACE (Area Control Error) tolerance limits, tie-line list, interchange schedule references, and regulatory jurisdiction. SSOT for control area identity used across grid operations, trading, and NERC BAL compliance reporting.'
}

Table grid.ems_node {
  ems_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the electrical node (bus) in the EMS network model. Primary key for the node entity. This is the EMS model representation, distinct from physical substation assets.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — Every EMS bus/node belongs to a control area. Critical for determining which balancing authority is responsible for voltage and frequency at each node. Required for NERC TOP compliance scoping.']
  ems_control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the balancing authority responsible for this node. Used for coordination of generation control and frequency regulation.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: EMS nodes represent physical electrical equipment (buses, breakers, transformers) that must be tracked as capital assets for maintenance scheduling, depreciation accounting, and FERC/regulatory report']
  voltage_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the voltage control zone or area to which this node belongs. Used for coordinated voltage control and reactive power management.']
  agc_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether generation at this node participates in Automatic Generation Control (AGC) for frequency regulation and area control error correction.']
  base_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base power rating in megavolt-amperes (MVA) used for per-unit calculations in the EMS power flow model. Standardizes impedance and power values.']
  bus_number varchar [note: 'Numeric identifier assigned to the bus in the EMS power flow model. Used in state estimation and contingency analysis calculations.']
  contingency_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node is included in EMS contingency analysis studies. True if the node is monitored for N-1 and N-2 contingency violations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this node record was first created in the EMS system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this node configuration became effective in the EMS network model. Used for model version control and historical analysis.']
  ems_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the EMS network model in which this node definition exists. Tracks model updates and changes over time.']
  generation_mvar decimal(12,3) [note: 'Reactive power generation at this node in megavolt-amperes reactive (MVAR). Represents the reactive power injection from generators connected to this bus.']
  generation_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Real power generation at this node in megawatts (MW). Represents the active power injection from generators connected to this bus.']
  island_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the electrical island (connected network segment) to which this node currently belongs. Updated during topology processing after switching events.']
  last_state_estimation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful state estimation run that included this node. Used for data quality monitoring and operational awareness.']
  last_topology_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent topology processor update that affected this nodes connectivity or energization status. Used for real-time operations tracking.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the node location in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analysis and outage visualization.']
  load_mvar decimal(12,3) [note: 'Reactive power load at this node in megavolt-amperes reactive (MVAR). Represents the reactive power consumption at the bus in the EMS model.']
  load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Real power load at this node in megawatts (MW). Represents the active power consumption at the bus in the EMS model.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the node location in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analysis and outage visualization.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this node record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity jurisdiction for this node. Used for compliance reporting and reliability coordination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|TRE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the electrical node as defined in the EMS network model. Used by operators for identification and communication.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the node in the power flow model. Slack (reference bus), PV (voltage-controlled generator bus), PQ (load bus), or Isolated (disconnected from network).']
  nominal_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'The nominal voltage level of the node in kilovolts (kV). Represents the design voltage class for this bus in the transmission or distribution network.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or additional context about this node. Used by EMS engineers and operators for documentation.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the node in the EMS network model. Indicates whether the node is actively participating in state estimation and power flow calculations.']
  operator_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the entity responsible for operating and controlling this node. May differ from owner in cases of contracted operations.']
  owner_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the utility or entity that owns the physical assets associated with this node. Used for multi-utility coordination and cost allocation.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name for this nodes voltage or power measurements. Used for historical data retrieval and post-event analysis.']
  pnode_reference varchar [note: 'Locational Marginal Price (LMP) pricing node identifier used in energy market operations. Links the EMS node to wholesale market pricing and settlement.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or operational region code to which this node belongs. Used for regional coordination and reporting to Regional Transmission Organizations (RTOs) or Independent System Operators (ISOs).']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when this node was retired or removed from the EMS network model. Null for active nodes. Used for historical model reconstruction.']
  rto_iso_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the RTO or ISO market area in which this node operates. Used for market operations and LMP pricing coordination.']
  shunt_mvar decimal(12,3) [note: 'Reactive power provided by shunt devices (capacitors, reactors) at this node in megavolt-amperes reactive (MVAR). Used for voltage support and power factor correction.']
  state_estimation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node participates in the EMS state estimator calculations. True if the node has sufficient telemetry for state estimation.']
  topology_status varchar [note: 'Real-time topology status of the node as determined by the EMS network topology processor. Reflects current switching state and connectivity.']
  voltage_limit_high_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Upper voltage limit in kilovolts (kV) for this node. Used in contingency analysis and voltage violation detection.']
  voltage_limit_low_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Lower voltage limit in kilovolts (kV) for this node. Used in contingency analysis and voltage violation detection.']
  voltage_setpoint_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Target voltage setpoint in kilovolts (kV) for voltage-controlled nodes (PV buses). Used by automatic voltage regulators and reactive power control devices.']

  Note: 'Master record for every electrical node (bus) modeled in the Energy Management System (EMS) state estimator and network topology processor. Captures node name, bus number, nominal voltage (kV), substation reference, voltage zone, island membership, base MVA, and EMS model version. Serves as the topological anchor for SCADA measurements, LMP pricing nodes (PNodes), and contingency analysis. Distinct from the physical substation asset (owned by asset domain) — this is the EMS network model representation.'
}

Table grid.scada_point {
  scada_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SCADA telemetry point or PMU device. Primary key for the SCADA point master catalog.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — SCADA points belong to a specific control area\'s monitoring scope. Required for filtering telemetry by BA jurisdiction and for multi-area EMS configurations.']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — SCADA telemetry points are associated with specific EMS nodes or branches. This is the fundamental link between the measurement layer and the network model. Required for state estimation input mapping']
  nerc_cip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.nerc_cip_asset. Business justification: SCADA points monitoring BES Cyber Systems are CIP-reportable assets under NERC CIP-002. This link enables CIP-007 systems security management and CIP-010 configuration change management compliance by ']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: SCADA measurement points are physically installed on capital assets (transformers, breakers, capacitors). Operations and maintenance teams need to trace measurements back to asset records for predicti']
  scada_ems_node_id varchar [note: 'The EMS network model node identifier to which this telemetry point is associated. Used for state estimation and contingency analysis.']
  to_ems_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — SCADA points are associated with EMS nodes (buses) or branches in the network model. This FK is essential for mapping telemetry to the network topology for state estimation and contingency analysis.']
  agc_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this telemetry point participates in AGC for frequency regulation and area control error (ACE) calculation. True if AGC-enabled.']
  alarm_high_limit decimal(15,4) [note: 'The threshold above which an alarm is triggered. Used by EMS and SCADA alarm management systems.']
  alarm_low_limit decimal(15,4) [note: 'The threshold below which an alarm is triggered. Used by EMS and SCADA alarm management systems.']
  balancing_authority varchar [note: 'The NERC-registered balancing authority responsible for this telemetry point. Used for coordination and reliability compliance.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'The date this SCADA point was commissioned and placed into operational service.']
  contingency_analysis_monitored boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this telemetry point is monitored in EMS contingency analysis for N-1 and N-2 reliability assessments. True if monitored.']
  control_area varchar [note: 'The control area or operating region to which this telemetry point belongs. Used for AGC (Automatic Generation Control) and load balancing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SCADA point record was first created in the master catalog.']
  critical_infrastructure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this telemetry point is associated with critical infrastructure as defined by NERC CIP standards. True if subject to enhanced cybersecurity controls.']
  data_owner varchar [note: 'The organizational unit or individual responsible for the accuracy and maintenance of this telemetry point configuration (e.g., Grid Operations, Transmission Planning).']
  deadband_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum change in value required to trigger an update to the historian or EMS. Used to filter noise and reduce data volume. Expressed in engineering units.']
  ems_branch_reference varchar [note: 'The EMS network model branch identifier (transmission line, transformer) to which this telemetry point is associated. Null for nodal measurements.']
  engineering_units varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the telemetry value (e.g., MW, MVAR, kV, A, Hz, degrees, pu). Used for display, validation, and conversion.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'The type of equipment this telemetry point monitors (e.g., transformer, circuit breaker, transmission line, generator, capacitor bank, reactor).']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent calibration or accuracy verification for this telemetry point. Used for maintenance tracking and data quality assurance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SCADA point record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'The specific electrical or operational parameter being measured: MW (real power), MVAR (reactive power), kV (voltage), amps (current), breaker status, tap position, frequency, voltage angle, phasor magnitude, or phasor angle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MW|MVAR|kV|amps|breaker_status|tap_position|frequency|voltage_angle|phasor_magnitude|phasor_angle — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nerc_prc_002_registered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PMU is registered under NERC PRC-002 (Disturbance Monitoring and Reporting Requirements). True if registered for compliance. Null for non-PMU points.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next calibration or accuracy verification. Used for preventive maintenance planning.']
  normal_max_value decimal(15,4) [note: 'The expected maximum value for this measurement under normal operating conditions. Used for validation and alarm generation.']
  normal_min_value decimal(15,4) [note: 'The expected minimum value for this measurement under normal operating conditions. Used for validation and alarm generation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, configuration details, or special handling instructions for this SCADA point.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'The current operational status of the SCADA point: active (in service), inactive (out of service), testing (commissioning), maintenance (temporarily offline), or decommissioned (permanently retired).']
  pdc_assignment varchar [note: 'The PDC (Phasor Data Concentrator) to which this PMU streams data. Used for synchrophasor data aggregation and distribution. Null for non-PMU points.']
  pi_archive_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this point is archived in the OSIsoft PI historian. True if historical data is stored for analytics and compliance.']
  pi_compression_deviation decimal(10,4) [note: 'The compression deviation threshold for the PI tag. Values within this deviation are not archived. Expressed in engineering units or percentage.']
  pi_compression_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether exception-based compression is enabled for this PI tag to reduce storage volume while preserving data fidelity.']
  pmu_firmware_version varchar [note: 'The firmware version installed on the PMU device. Used for compliance tracking and troubleshooting. Null for non-PMU points.']
  pmu_gps_sync_source varchar [note: 'The GPS or time synchronization source for the PMU (e.g., GPS satellite, IRIG-B, NTP). Ensures time-aligned synchrophasor measurements. Null for non-PMU points.']
  pmu_manufacturer varchar [note: 'The manufacturer of the PMU device (e.g., SEL, GE, ABB). Null for non-PMU points.']
  pmu_model varchar [note: 'The model number of the PMU device. Null for non-PMU points.']
  pmu_reporting_rate_fps varchar [note: 'The reporting rate of the PMU in frames per second (e.g., 30, 60). High-speed synchrophasor data for wide-area monitoring. Null for non-PMU points.']
  point_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the telemetry point: analog (continuous measurements), digital (status/binary), calculated (derived values), or PMU synchrophasor (high-speed phasor measurements).']
  quality_flag_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quality flags (good, bad, questionable, substituted) are enabled for this point. True if quality validation is active.']
  rtu_address varchar [note: 'The communication address or channel identifier of the RTU/IED within the SCADA network (e.g., IP address, serial port, DNP3 address).']
  rtu_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the RTU or IED (Intelligent Electronic Device) that sources this telemetry point. Links to the physical field device.']
  scan_rate_seconds varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the SCADA system polls this point, measured in seconds. Typical values: 2-10 seconds for critical analog points, 30-60 seconds for status points.']
  state_estimator_observable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this telemetry point is used in the EMS state estimator for real-time network analysis. True if included in state estimation.']
  substation_name varchar [note: 'The name of the substation or generating station where this telemetry point is located. Used for geographic and operational context.']
  tag_name varchar [note: 'The unique tag identifier in the OSIsoft PI historian system. This is the canonical name used for real-time data retrieval and archival queries.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'The nominal voltage level of the equipment associated with this telemetry point, measured in kilovolts (kV). Used for network model alignment.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all telemetry sources and measurement points — SCADA analog/digital points from RTUs and IEDs, plus PMU synchrophasor devices — across the transmission and generation network. SCADA point attributes include tag name (OSIsoft PI tag), point type (MW, MVAR, kV, amps, breaker status, tap position), associated EMS node or branch, RTU/IED source, scan rate, engineering units, deadband, quality flags, and PI historian archive configuration. PMU device attributes include manufacturer, model, firmware version, GPS synchronization source, reporting rate (frames/second), PDC assignment, and NERC PRC-002 registration status. SSOT for all telemetry source definitions and PI tag configuration — the unified operational and archiving metadata catalog for grid telemetry devices.'
}

Table grid.grid_scada_measurement {
  grid_scada_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each grid telemetry measurement record. Primary key for the grid SCADA measurement event log. Inferred role: EVENT_LOG.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the Balancing Authority responsible for the control area where this measurement was captured. Links telemetry to the NERC-registered Balancing Authority for compliance reporting, interchange scheduling, and frequency regulation analysis. Critical for NERC BAL and TOP standards.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the substation where this measurement was captured. Links the telemetry to the physical grid location for geographic and network topology analysis.']
  lmp_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.lmp_price. Business justification: Real-time SCADA measurements at pricing nodes validate LMP calculations and market outcomes. Critical for market monitoring, price validation, dispute resolution, and FERC market surveillance. Enables']
  pmu_device_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the Phasor Measurement Unit that captured this synchrophasor measurement. Identifies the source PMU device for high-precision time-synchronized measurements. Null for SCADA-sourced measurements.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Real-time SCADA measurements trigger asset condition assessments and health index calculations. Linking measurements to asset records enables predictive maintenance algorithms, thermal aging models, a']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the SCADA point master registry that defines the telemetry point configuration, location, equipment association, and engineering metadata. Links this measurement to the grid asset or network element being monitored.']
  active_power_mw decimal(12,4) [note: 'The real power flow in megawatts (MW) measured at this telemetry point. Positive values typically indicate power flowing in the defined forward direction; negative values indicate reverse flow. Used for generation dispatch monitoring, transmission loading, and energy accounting. Applicable to both SCADA and PMU measurements.']
  agc_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement is used as an input to the Automatic Generation Control (AGC) system for real-time generation dispatch and frequency regulation. True indicates the measurement (typically tie-line flows, area control error, or frequency) is part of the AGC control loop. False indicates the measurement is not used for AGC. Critical for NERC BAL-001 compliance.']
  alarm_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement triggered an alarm condition in the SCADA or EMS (Energy Management System). True indicates the value exceeded a configured threshold (high/low limit, rate-of-change limit, or deviation alarm). False indicates normal operation. Used to filter alarm-worthy events for operator notification and post-event analysis.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this measurement if it triggered an alarm. Critical indicates immediate operator action required (e.g., equipment overload, voltage collapse risk). High indicates urgent attention needed. Medium indicates abnormal condition requiring review. Low indicates informational alarm. Null if alarm_flag is false.']
  breaker_status varchar [note: 'The operational status of a circuit breaker for digital SCADA measurements. Open indicates the breaker is open and the circuit is de-energized. Closed indicates the breaker is closed and the circuit is energized. Intermediate indicates a transitional state during switching. Unknown indicates communication loss or indeterminate status. Critical for outage management, switching order execution, and network topology determination. Null for analog measurements.']
  contingency_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement is monitored in the EMS contingency analysis application for N-1 and N-2 reliability assessments. True indicates the measurement is checked against post-contingency limits. False indicates the measurement is not part of contingency monitoring. Used to identify critical telemetry for NERC TPL (Transmission Planning) compliance.']
  current_angle_degrees decimal(10,4) [note: 'The phase angle of the current phasor in degrees relative to a common time reference. Used in conjunction with voltage angle to calculate power flow direction and power factor. Null for non-current SCADA measurements.']
  current_magnitude_amperes decimal(12,4) [note: 'The magnitude of the current phasor in amperes for PMU measurements. Represents the RMS current flowing through the monitored line or equipment. Used for real-time loading analysis and thermal limit monitoring. Null for non-current SCADA measurements.']
  disturbance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement was captured during a grid disturbance event (e.g., fault, generation trip, load rejection, frequency excursion). True indicates the measurement is part of a disturbance data set for NERC PRC-002 (Disturbance Monitoring and Reporting Requirements) compliance and post-event analysis. False indicates normal operation.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the engineering_value field. Common values include kV (kilovolt), MW (megawatt), MVAR (megavolt-ampere reactive), A (ampere), Hz (hertz), degrees (phase angle), percent (%). Essential for correct interpretation and aggregation of telemetry data.']
  engineering_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The measurement value converted to engineering units (e.g., kV for voltage, MW for power, Hz for frequency, degrees for phase angle). This is the business-ready value used for operational analytics, state estimation, and reporting. Derived from raw_value using the SCADA point or PMU configuration scaling factors.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'The type of grid equipment or network element that this measurement is monitoring. Transformer includes power transformers and autotransformers. Transmission Line includes overhead lines and underground cables. Generator includes generating units. Circuit Breaker includes all switching devices. Capacitor Bank and Reactor are reactive power devices. Bus represents substation buses. Tie Line represents interconnection points between control areas. Used for equipment-specific analytics and asset performance monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Transformer|Transmission Line|Generator|Circuit Breaker|Capacitor Bank|Reactor|Bus|Tie Line — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  frequency_hz decimal(10,6) [note: 'The measured electrical frequency in Hertz at the measurement point. Nominal frequency is 60 Hz in North America or 50 Hz in other regions. Frequency deviations indicate generation-load imbalance and are critical for NERC BAL (Balancing and Frequency) reliability standards compliance and automatic generation control (AGC). Captured by both SCADA frequency transducers and PMUs.']
  ingestion_timestamp_utc timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when this measurement record was ingested from the OSIsoft PI historian into the analytics lakehouse platform. Distinct from measurement_timestamp_utc (the source event time). Used for data lineage tracking, latency analysis, and identifying data pipeline delays.']
  limit_violation_type varchar [note: 'The type of limit violation that triggered an alarm, if applicable. High indicates the value exceeded the high threshold. Low indicates the value fell below the low threshold. High-High and Low-Low indicate emergency thresholds. Rate-of-Change indicates the value changed too rapidly. None indicates no violation. Used for categorizing alarm events and compliance reporting.']
  measurement_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the measurement type for filtering and analytics. Voltage includes voltage magnitude and angle. Current includes current magnitude and angle. Power includes active and reactive power. Frequency includes frequency and ROCOF. Status includes breaker and switch positions. Temperature and Pressure are auxiliary measurements for equipment monitoring. Facilitates domain-specific queries and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Voltage|Current|Power|Frequency|Status|Temperature|Pressure — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_source_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this measurement originated from SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) analog/digital telemetry or PMU (Phasor Measurement Unit) synchrophasor data. Determines the grain and attribute applicability of the record.']
  measurement_timestamp_utc timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when the telemetry measurement was captured at the source device (RTU or PMU). For SCADA measurements, precision is typically to the second or millisecond based on scan cycle. For PMU measurements, precision is to the microsecond per IEEE C37.118 synchrophasor standard. This is the principal event timestamp for the measurement.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'The NERC regional entity where this measurement was captured. WECC (Western Electricity Coordinating Council), ERCOT (Electric Reliability Council of Texas), MRO (Midwest Reliability Organization), NPCC (Northeast Power Coordinating Council), RF (ReliabilityFirst), SERC (SERC Reliability Corporation), SPP (Southwest Power Pool), TRE (Texas Reliability Entity). Used for regional compliance reporting and inter-regional coordination analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|TRE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pdc_reference varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the Phasor Data Concentrator that aggregated and time-aligned this PMU measurement before forwarding to the historian or wide-area monitoring system. PDCs collect data from multiple PMUs and provide synchronized data streams. Null for SCADA-sourced measurements.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI historian tag name that uniquely identifies this telemetry point in the PI system. This is the source-system identifier for SCADA measurements. Examples include substation voltage tags, transformer load tags, breaker status tags. Null for PMU-sourced measurements that may use a different naming convention.']
  pmu_data_quality_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IEEE C37.118 data quality flags for PMU measurements. Flags include: PMU_SYNC_LOST (GPS synchronization lost), PMU_ERROR (measurement error detected), DATA_SORTING (time-order issue), PMU_TRIGGER (event trigger active), CONFIG_CHANGE (PMU configuration changed). Empty string indicates no quality issues. Used to filter high-quality synchrophasor data for oscillation detection and wide-area monitoring. Null for SCADA measurements.']
  pmu_reporting_rate_hz varchar [note: 'The reporting rate in samples per second (Hz) for PMU synchrophasor measurements. Common values are 30 Hz or 60 Hz per IEEE C37.118 standard. Indicates the time resolution of the phasor data stream. Null for SCADA-sourced measurements.']
  quality_code varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI or SCADA system quality indicator for this measurement. Good indicates valid data from the source device. Substituted indicates the value was estimated or filled by the historian due to communication loss. Bad indicates invalid or out-of-range data. Questionable indicates data that passed validation but has uncertainty flags. Annotated indicates manually adjusted data. Critical for filtering reliable data in operational analytics and NERC compliance reporting.']
  raw_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The raw numeric value of the measurement as captured by the source device before any engineering unit conversion or scaling. For analog measurements, this is the unscaled sensor reading. For digital measurements, this is the binary state (0/1). For PMU measurements, this may represent the raw ADC output before phasor calculation.']
  reactive_power_mvar decimal(12,4) [note: 'The reactive power flow in megavolt-amperes reactive (MVAR) measured at this telemetry point. Critical for voltage control, VAR management, and power factor optimization. Monitored for NERC VAR-001 (Voltage and Reactive Control) compliance. Applicable to both SCADA and PMU measurements.']
  rocof_hz_per_second decimal(10,6) [note: 'The rate of change of frequency in Hertz per second, calculated from PMU synchrophasor data. A key indicator for detecting sudden generation loss, load rejection, or islanding events. High ROCOF values trigger under-frequency load shedding schemes and are monitored for NERC PRC-006 (Automatic Underfrequency Load Shedding) compliance. Null for SCADA measurements that do not calculate ROCOF.']
  rtu_reference varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the Remote Terminal Unit that collected and transmitted this SCADA measurement. Identifies the source device in the field for SCADA telemetry. Null for PMU-sourced measurements.']
  scan_cycle_seconds varchar [note: 'The scan cycle interval in seconds at which this SCADA point is polled by the RTU or SCADA master. Typical values range from 2 seconds (critical telemetry) to 60 seconds (slower-changing measurements). Null for PMU measurements which operate on continuous streaming at fixed reporting rates (e.g., 30 or 60 samples per second).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system that provided this measurement to the PI historian. Examples include EMS (Energy Management System), SCADA Master, PMU Network, ADMS (Advanced Distribution Management System). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting data quality issues by tracing back to the originating system.']
  state_estimator_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement is used as an input to the EMS (Energy Management System) state estimator application. True indicates the measurement is included in the state estimation model for real-time network analysis and contingency analysis. False indicates the measurement is for monitoring only. Critical for identifying the telemetry set used for NERC TOP reliability analysis.']
  switch_position varchar [note: 'The position status of a disconnect switch or sectionalizing switch for digital SCADA measurements. Open indicates the switch is open. Closed indicates the switch is closed. Grounded indicates the switch is in a grounding position (for maintenance safety). Unknown indicates communication loss. Used for network topology modeling and switching order verification. Null for analog measurements.']
  voltage_angle_degrees decimal(10,4) [note: 'The phase angle of the voltage phasor in degrees relative to a common time reference (typically GPS-synchronized). Critical for wide-area situational awareness, angle difference monitoring across transmission corridors, and oscillation detection. Null for non-voltage SCADA measurements.']
  voltage_level_kv varchar [note: 'The nominal voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the network element where this measurement was captured. Common values include 765, 500, 345, 230, 138, 115, 69, 34.5, 13.8, 4.16 kV. Used for segmenting telemetry by transmission vs. sub-transmission vs. distribution voltage classes and for voltage-specific analytics.']
  voltage_magnitude_kv decimal(12,4) [note: 'The magnitude of the voltage phasor in kilovolts (kV) for PMU measurements. Represents the RMS voltage at the measurement point. Used for wide-area voltage monitoring, oscillation detection, and voltage stability analysis. Null for non-voltage SCADA measurements.']

  Note: 'Time-series transactional record of all real-time and near-real-time grid telemetry readings — both SCADA analog/digital measurements and PMU synchrophasor data — ingested from OSIsoft PI historian into the analytics platform. SCADA attributes include PI tag reference, UTC timestamp, raw value, engineering-unit value, quality code (Good/Substituted/Bad), scan cycle, and source RTU. PMU attributes include voltage/current phasor magnitude and angle, frequency (Hz), ROCOF (Hz/s), microsecond-precision timestamp, and PDC data quality flags. Measurement source type discriminator (SCADA/PMU) distinguishes data origin and grain. SSOT for all grid telemetry time-series data supporting post-event review, NERC TOP reliability analysis, NERC PRC-002 disturbance monitoring, wide-area situational awareness, oscillation detection, and operational analytics. Distinct from interval meter data (owned by metering domain).'
}

Table grid.state_estimation_run {
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each execution cycle of the Energy Management System (EMS) state estimator. Primary key for the state estimation run record.']
  balancing_authority_control_area_id varchar [note: 'NERC-registered identifier of the Balancing Authority responsible for the control area where this state estimation run was executed. Used for inter-BA coordination and compliance reporting.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — State estimation runs execute within the context of a specific control area\'s network model. This link is required for multi-area EMS configurations and for scoping SE results to the correct balancing']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — State estimation results are per-node/per-branch. After merge of state_estimation_result into state_estimation_run, the result-level grain references ems_node. Required for post-event voltage analysis']
  state_ems_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — State estimation results (now merged into state_estimation_run) produce per-node voltage/angle estimates. The FK to ems_node is required to map results back to the network model for visualization and ']
  bad_data_detection_count varchar [note: 'Number of SCADA measurements flagged as bad data (outliers or erroneous readings) during the state estimation execution. High counts indicate telemetry quality issues requiring investigation.']
  closed_switching_device_count varchar [note: 'Number of circuit breakers, disconnects, and other switching devices in the closed (conducting) position during this state estimation run. Defines network connectivity paths.']
  contingency_pre_screening_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) signaling whether this state estimation solution was used as the base case for contingency analysis pre-screening to identify critical N-1 and N-2 outage scenarios.']
  control_center_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical or logical control center facility where the EMS state estimator executed this run. Used for operational audit trail and disaster recovery tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this state estimation run record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  de_energized_bus_count varchar [note: 'Total number of electrical buses (nodes) in the network topology that are de-energized (out of service) and excluded from the state estimation solution for this execution cycle.']
  ems_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the EMS network model (bus-branch topology, equipment parameters, ratings) used for this state estimation execution. Critical for model validation and change tracking.']
  energized_bus_count varchar [note: 'Total number of electrical buses (nodes) in the network topology that are energized and included in the state estimation solution for this execution cycle.']
  execution_duration_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from state estimation run initiation to solution completion or failure. Used for EMS performance monitoring and capacity planning.']
  iteration_count varchar [note: 'Number of iterative solver cycles executed before the state estimator reached convergence or divergence. High iteration counts may indicate network stress or measurement quality issues.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this state estimation run record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  mvar_mismatch_tolerance decimal(12,6) [note: 'The maximum allowable reactive power mismatch threshold in megavars (MVAR) used as the convergence criterion for the state estimation solver. Critical for voltage stability assessment.']
  mw_mismatch_tolerance decimal(12,6) [note: 'The maximum allowable active power mismatch threshold in megawatts (MW) used as the convergence criterion for the state estimation solver. Lower values indicate tighter solution accuracy requirements.']
  observable_island_count varchar [note: 'Number of electrically isolated network islands that have sufficient measurement coverage for state estimation. Each island requires independent solution processing.']
  open_switching_device_count varchar [note: 'Number of circuit breakers, disconnects, and other switching devices in the open (non-conducting) position during this state estimation run. Defines network connectivity and island boundaries.']
  operator_override_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) signaling whether a system operator manually overrode state estimation inputs, outputs, or solver parameters during this execution cycle. Used for audit trail and compliance evidence.']
  operator_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the system operator documenting the business justification for any manual override applied during this state estimation run. Required for NERC compliance and post-event review.']
  pi_historian_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI System tag prefix or namespace used to store time-series state estimation results (voltage, angle, flow) in the PI historian for operational analytics and post-event review.']
  pmu_measurement_count varchar [note: 'Total number of synchrophasor measurements from Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) available and processed as inputs to the state estimation algorithm. PMU data provides high-precision time-synchronized voltage and current phasors.']
  pseudo_measurement_count varchar [note: 'Number of pseudo-measurements (synthetic measurements derived from historical load profiles, generation schedules, or forecasts) injected into the state estimation to improve observability in areas with sparse SCADA coverage.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the state estimation solution indicating whether the iterative solver successfully converged to a valid network state, diverged without solution, started from flat conditions, failed due to error, was aborted by operator, or produced partial results.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the state estimation execution cycle was initiated by the EMS. This is the principal business event timestamp representing when the network state was computed.']
  scada_measurement_count varchar [note: 'Total number of SCADA telemetry measurements (voltage, current, MW, MVAR, breaker status) available and processed as inputs to the state estimation algorithm for this execution cycle.']
  solution_quality_indicator varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the state estimation solution quality based on residual magnitude, bad data detection count, and observability metrics. Used for operator confidence and post-event analysis.']
  system_frequency_hz decimal(8,4) [note: 'Measured or estimated electrical system frequency in Hertz (Hz) at the time of the state estimation run. Nominal frequency is 60 Hz in North America; deviations indicate generation-load imbalance.']
  thermal_violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of transmission lines, transformers, or other network branches where the estimated current or power flow exceeds thermal rating limits. Indicates potential equipment overload requiring operator intervention.']
  topology_change_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) signaling whether the network topology connectivity state changed since the previous state estimation run due to switching operations, outages, or equipment status changes.']
  topology_snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or hash representing the specific network topology configuration (bus connectivity, switching device states, equipment in-service status) captured at the time of this state estimation run. Used for topology reconstruction and post-event analysis.']
  total_generation_mw decimal(15,3) [note: 'Sum of active power generation in megawatts (MW) across all generating units included in the state estimation solution. Used for system-wide power balance verification and NERC BAL compliance reporting.']
  total_interchange_mw decimal(15,3) [note: 'Net scheduled active power interchange in megawatts (MW) with neighboring balancing authorities or control areas. Positive values indicate net export; negative values indicate net import.']
  total_load_mw decimal(15,3) [note: 'Sum of active power load in megawatts (MW) across all load buses included in the state estimation solution. Used for system-wide power balance verification and load forecasting validation.']
  total_losses_mw decimal(15,3) [note: 'Calculated active power losses in megawatts (MW) across the transmission network (lines, transformers, reactors) derived from the state estimation solution. Used for loss allocation and energy accounting.']
  unobservable_island_count varchar [note: 'Number of electrically isolated network islands that lack sufficient SCADA measurement coverage for state estimation. These islands cannot be solved and require operator attention.']
  voltage_violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of electrical buses where the estimated voltage magnitude falls outside acceptable operating limits (typically 0.95 to 1.05 per-unit). High counts indicate voltage stability concerns requiring corrective action.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each EMS state estimator execution cycle including topology connectivity state, per-node/per-branch electrical results, and network island configuration. Run-level attributes include timestamp, solution status (converged/diverged/flat-start), iterations, MW mismatch tolerance, bad-data detections, observable islands, operator overrides. Topology attributes include number of energized buses, open switching devices, topology change flag, and island membership. Result-level detail (grain: one row per node/branch per run) includes estimated voltage magnitude (kV), voltage angle (degrees), active power flow (MW), reactive power flow (MVAR), current (amps), and residual. SSOT for state estimation execution, topology snapshots, and electrical results — used for NERC TOP compliance evidence, post-event voltage profile and network reconstruction analysis, contingency pre-screening, NERC FAC-002 transfer capability studies, and EMS model validation.'
}

Table grid.state_estimation_result {
  state_estimation_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each state estimation result record. Primary key for the state estimation result entity.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'The SCADA telemetry point identifier that provided the measurement for this element. Links state estimation results to real-time data sources.']
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent state estimation run that produced this result. Links this node or branch result to the specific EMS state estimator execution.']
  active_power_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The estimated active power flow through this branch or injected/consumed at this node in megawatts. Represents real power transfer in the network.']
  apparent_power_mva decimal(12,3) [note: 'The estimated apparent power (vector sum of active and reactive power) in megavolt-amperes. Used for equipment loading and thermal limit assessment.']
  balancing_authority varchar [note: 'The balancing authority responsible for this network element. Identifies the control area for coordination and reliability compliance.']
  contingency_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this element would violate operating limits under contingency conditions. Used for pre-screening in contingency analysis.']
  control_area varchar [note: 'The control area to which this network element belongs. Used for area interchange control and automatic generation control (AGC).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this state estimation result record was created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  current_amps decimal(12,3) [note: 'The estimated current flow through this branch or element in amperes. Critical for thermal limit monitoring and equipment protection.']
  current_magnitude_pu decimal(8,6) [note: 'The estimated current magnitude expressed in per-unit relative to the element rating. Normalized representation for loading assessment.']
  element_identifier varchar [note: 'The unique identifier or name of the network element (node, branch, bus, line) in the EMS model. Corresponds to the SCADA point or network model object.']
  element_type varchar [note: 'The type of network element this result represents. Distinguishes between nodes (buses), branches (lines, transformers), generators, and loads in the EMS network model. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: node|branch|bus|line|transformer|generator|load — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  estimation_confidence decimal(6,4) [note: 'A confidence score (0.0 to 1.0) indicating the reliability of the state estimation for this element based on measurement redundancy and quality.']
  from_bus_name varchar [note: 'For branch elements, the name of the originating bus or node. Defines the topology of the power flow path.']
  loading_percentage decimal(8,3) [note: 'The percentage of the element\'s rated capacity being utilized based on the estimated power flow. Used for thermal limit and contingency analysis.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'The quality status of the measurement used for this element in the state estimation. Indicates whether the measurement was accepted, flagged as suspect, or rejected.']
  normalized_residual decimal(10,6) [note: 'The residual normalized by the measurement standard deviation. Used for bad data detection and measurement validation in state estimation.']
  observability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this network element was observable in the state estimation (sufficient measurements available to estimate its state). True if observable, False if unobservable.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI historian tag name for this measurement point. Enables integration with PI historian for operational analytics and post-event review.']
  power_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'The ratio of active power to apparent power at this element. Indicates the efficiency of power transfer and reactive power balance.']
  reactive_power_mvar decimal(12,3) [note: 'The estimated reactive power flow through this branch or injected/consumed at this node in megavolt-amperes reactive. Essential for voltage control and stability.']
  residual decimal(12,6) [note: 'The difference between the measured value and the estimated value for this element. Indicates measurement quality and potential bad data.']
  result_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this state estimation result was calculated by the EMS state estimator. Represents the real-world event time of the solved network state.']
  solution_convergence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state estimator solution converged for this element. False indicates numerical instability or insufficient measurements.']
  substation_name varchar [note: 'The name of the substation where this network element is located. Provides geographic and operational context for the result.']
  thermal_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the estimated current or power flow exceeds the thermal rating of this element. Triggers congestion management actions.']
  to_bus_name varchar [note: 'For branch elements, the name of the destination bus or node. Completes the topology definition of the power flow path.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this state estimation result record was last updated. Tracks modifications for audit and data quality purposes.']
  voltage_angle_degrees decimal(10,4) [note: 'The estimated voltage phase angle at this node in degrees. Critical for power flow analysis and system stability assessment.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'The nominal voltage level of the network element in kilovolts. Indicates whether this is transmission (e.g., 500kV, 345kV, 230kV) or sub-transmission (e.g., 138kV, 69kV).']
  voltage_magnitude_kv decimal(12,4) [note: 'The estimated voltage magnitude at this node or element in kilovolts. Core output of the state estimator representing the solved voltage level.']
  voltage_magnitude_pu decimal(8,6) [note: 'The estimated voltage magnitude expressed in per-unit (pu) relative to the nominal voltage level. Standard normalized representation for power system analysis.']
  voltage_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the estimated voltage at this element is outside acceptable operating limits. Triggers voltage control actions.']

  Note: 'Per-node and per-branch output record from each EMS state estimator run. Captures the estimated voltage magnitude (kV), voltage angle (degrees), active power flow (MW), reactive power flow (MVAR), current (amps), and residual for each EMS node or branch in the solved network model. Linked to a parent state_estimation_run. Enables post-event voltage profile analysis, contingency pre-screening, and NERC FAC-002 transfer capability studies.'
}

Table grid.contingency {
  contingency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contingency case definition. Primary key for the contingency master catalog.']
  contingency_state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.state_estimation_run.state_estimation_run_id — Each contingency analysis execution (now merged into contingency) is triggered by a converged state estimation run. This FK links the security assessment to the system state it evaluated — critical fo']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Contingencies (N-1 and N-2 scenarios) must be scoped to a specific control area for NERC compliance and operational context. Each contingency case is defined and analyzed within the boundaries of a ba']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Contingency definitions require audit trail of creator for NERC FAC-011 compliance and engineering change management. \'created_by_user\' text field should be FK to employee.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Each contingency definition implements specific NERC TPL standard requirements (N-1, N-2 criteria). This link documents which obligation mandates monitoring this contingency, enabling compliance repor']
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.state_estimation_run.state_estimation_run_id — Contingency analysis runs are triggered after each converged state estimation solution. The CA run references its parent SE run. Required for tracing security assessment results back to the network st']
  to_state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.state_estimation_run.state_estimation_run_id — Contingency analysis runs are triggered after each converged state estimation solution. The parent SE run provides the base-case power flow against which contingencies are evaluated. This is a critica']
  alarm_threshold_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether operator alarms are triggered when violations are detected for this contingency.']
  analysis_method varchar [note: 'Power flow solution method used for contingency analysis: full AC (alternating current) power flow, linear DC (direct current) approximation, or fast decoupled method.']
  compliance_evidence_retention_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that contingency analysis records and violation evidence must be retained for NERC compliance audits.']
  contingency_category varchar [note: 'NERC planning category indicating the number of simultaneous element outages (N-1 single contingency, N-2 double contingency, N-1-1 sequential, or extreme event).']
  contingency_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code or identifier used in EMS and SCADA systems to reference this contingency case.']
  contingency_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the contingency case (e.g., \'Line 345 Outage\', \'Generator Unit 2 Trip\', \'Transformer T-12 Loss\').']
  contingency_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contingency scenario by the type of element or event being simulated.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the corrective actions, remedial action schemes, or operator interventions modeled or recommended for this contingency.']
  corrective_action_scheme_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a remedial action scheme (RAS) or special protection system (SPS) is modeled or enabled for this contingency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contingency case record was first created in the EMS contingency library.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application from which this contingency case definition was ingested (e.g., GE ADMS, ABB EMS, manual entry).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this contingency case definition was retired or became inactive. Null for currently active contingencies.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this contingency case definition became active and eligible for inclusion in EMS contingency analysis runs.']
  elements_removed_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the transmission or generation elements that are removed or tripped in this contingency scenario (e.g., \'Transmission Line 345kV between Substation A and Substation B\', \'Generator Unit G2 at Plant XYZ\').']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the EMS platform where this contingency case is defined and executed (e.g., GE ADMS, ABB EMS, Siemens Spectrum).']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region, zone, or control area where the contingency elements are located.']
  last_analysis_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent EMS contingency analysis execution that evaluated this contingency case.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the EMS engineer or system operator who last modified this contingency case definition.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contingency case record was last modified or updated.']
  last_violation_detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent contingency analysis run that detected one or more violations for this contingency.']
  monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this contingency is actively monitored in real-time EMS contingency analysis runs.']
  nerc_standard_applicability varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific NERC reliability standard(s) that mandate or govern this contingency analysis (e.g., TPL-001-5, TPL-002-4, TPL-003-1, TPL-004-1, TOP-002-4).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, operational notes, or special instructions related to this contingency case.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contingency case definition in the EMS contingency library.']
  operator_acknowledgment_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether system operator acknowledgment is required when violations are detected for this contingency.']
  pi_historian_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'Tag prefix or naming convention used in the OSIsoft PI historian system for storing contingency analysis results and telemetry related to this contingency.']
  pre_contingency_generation_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total system generation in megawatts (MW) at the time of the base case state estimation, before the contingency is applied.']
  pre_contingency_load_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total system load in megawatts (MW) at the time of the base case state estimation, before the contingency is applied.']
  primary_element_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary transmission or generation asset involved in the contingency (line, transformer, generator, bus). References the asset registry or network model.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether violations or analysis results for this contingency must be reported to NERC, FERC, or state regulatory authorities.']
  scada_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether real-time SCADA telemetry is used as input for this contingency analysis.']
  secondary_element_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the secondary element involved in N-2 or multiple-element contingencies. Null for single-element (N-1) contingencies.']
  severity_classification varchar [note: 'Risk severity level assigned to this contingency based on potential impact to grid stability, load loss, or cascading failure risk.']
  thermal_overload_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of rated capacity that defines a thermal overload violation for transmission elements in this contingency (e.g., 100.0 for normal rating, 120.0 for emergency rating).']
  total_violations_detected_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of violations detected across all historical contingency analysis runs for this contingency case.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the transmission element(s) involved in the contingency.']
