  voltage_violation_high_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper voltage limit in kilovolts (kV) above which a voltage violation is flagged for this contingency.']
  voltage_violation_low_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower voltage limit in kilovolts (kV) below which a voltage violation is flagged for this contingency.']
  worst_case_violation_severity varchar [note: 'Highest severity level of any violation ever detected for this contingency across all historical analysis runs.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of defined contingency cases (N-1 and N-2 scenarios) with their analysis execution records and violation results from the EMS contingency analysis (CA) function. Master attributes include contingency name, type (line outage, generator trip, transformer loss, bus fault), elements removed, NERC standard applicability (TPL-001 through TPL-004), and severity classification. Analysis run attributes (grain: one row per CA execution) include run timestamp, parent state estimation run reference, contingencies evaluated count, violations detected, worst-case contingency, solution method (full AC/linear DC), and operator acknowledgment. Violation detail attributes (grain: one row per violation per run) include violated element, violation type (thermal overload %, voltage exceedance kV), pre/post-contingency values, applicable rating, and corrective action status. SSOT for contingency definitions, security assessment execution, and N-1/N-2 violation records used in NERC TPL/TOP compliance evidence and real-time operator situational awareness.'
}

Table grid.contingency_analysis_run {
  contingency_analysis_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each contingency analysis execution. Primary key for the contingency analysis run record.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control area or balancing authority area for which this contingency analysis was executed.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Contingency analysis is performed by specific grid operations teams whose costs are tracked in cost centers. Needed for activity-based costing of reliability analysis work, NERC compliance cost tracki']
  emergency_stock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.emergency_stock_event. Business justification: Contingency analysis detecting thermal/voltage violations may trigger emergency material deployment for corrective action schemes (capacitor banks, reactors, mobile transformers). Links analysis run t']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the system operator who acknowledged the contingency analysis results. Used for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: NERC TPL-001/TPL-002 standards mandate contingency analysis for transmission planning. Each analysis run must be traceable to the specific NERC obligation it satisfies for audit evidence and complianc']
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent state estimator solution that triggered this contingency analysis. Each CA run is executed after a converged state estimation solution.']
  worst_case_contingency_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contingency scenario that produced the most severe violations or highest risk to system reliability in this analysis run.']
  analysis_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contingency analysis execution context. Real-time runs support live operations; study mode supports planning; post-event supports incident investigation.']
  base_case_mvar_mismatch decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total MVAR mismatch in the base case power flow solution before contingency evaluation. Indicates quality of reactive power balance in the state estimation input.']
  base_case_mw_mismatch decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total MW mismatch in the base case power flow solution before contingency evaluation. Indicates quality of the state estimation input. Should be near zero for valid analysis.']
  convergence_tolerance decimal(12,8) [note: 'The numerical convergence tolerance threshold used for power flow solutions in this contingency analysis. Smaller values provide higher accuracy but longer execution time.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of recommended or required corrective actions to address violations detected in the contingency analysis. May include generation redispatch, switching orders, or load curtailment.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective actions are required based on the violations detected. Triggers operator response procedures when true.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contingency analysis run record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated this contingency analysis run record. Typically the EMS application name and version for data lineage tracking.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the EMS system that executed this contingency analysis run. Supports multi-EMS environments and system integration tracking.']
  execution_duration_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds for the contingency analysis execution from initiation to completion. Critical for monitoring EMS performance and real-time operational requirements.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contingency analysis run record was last modified. Tracks updates to operator acknowledgment, corrective actions, or status changes.']
  max_iterations varchar [note: 'Maximum number of iterations allowed for power flow convergence in each contingency scenario. Prevents excessive computation time for non-converging cases.']
  n1_contingencies_evaluated varchar [note: 'Number of single-element (N-1) contingencies evaluated in this run. N-1 represents loss of a single transmission element.']
  n2_contingencies_evaluated varchar [note: 'Number of double-element (N-2) contingencies evaluated in this run. N-2 represents simultaneous loss of two transmission elements.']
  nerc_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contingency analysis run meets NERC TPL compliance requirements for documentation and retention. Used for regulatory audit evidence.']
  non_converged_contingencies varchar [note: 'Number of contingency scenarios that failed to converge within the iteration limit. Non-convergence may indicate severe system stress or numerical issues.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this contingency analysis run. May include operator observations, special conditions, or analysis context.']
  operator_acknowledged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the system operator has reviewed and acknowledged the contingency analysis results and any violations detected.']
  operator_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the system operator acknowledged the contingency analysis results. Null if not yet acknowledged.']
  pi_historian_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'Tag prefix used for storing this contingency analysis run results in the OSIsoft PI historian for operational analytics and post-event review.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the contingency analysis run. Tracks the lifecycle from initiation through completion or failure.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this contingency analysis execution was initiated by the EMS system. Represents the real-world operational moment of the analysis.']
  scada_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the SCADA telemetry snapshot used as input to the state estimator that preceded this contingency analysis. Establishes data lineage for compliance.']
  solution_method varchar [note: 'The power flow calculation method used for this contingency analysis execution. Full AC provides highest accuracy; linear DC provides faster performance for screening studies.']
  stability_violations_detected varchar [note: 'Number of system stability violations detected including transient stability, voltage stability, or oscillatory stability issues.']
  thermal_violations_detected varchar [note: 'Number of thermal overload violations detected where transmission line or transformer loading exceeds emergency ratings under contingency conditions.']
  total_contingencies_evaluated varchar [note: 'The total number of contingency scenarios evaluated during this analysis run. Includes N-1, N-2, and other credible contingencies per NERC TPL standards.']
  total_violations_detected varchar [note: 'Total number of reliability violations detected across all contingencies evaluated. Includes thermal overloads, voltage violations, and stability issues.']
  voltage_violations_detected varchar [note: 'Number of bus voltage violations detected where voltage levels fall outside acceptable operating limits under contingency conditions.']
  worst_case_contingency_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the worst-case contingency scenario, typically describing the element(s) lost and the resulting system impact.']
  worst_case_severity_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numerical severity index for the worst-case contingency, typically representing the magnitude of the most severe violation (e.g., percent overload or per-unit voltage deviation).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each EMS contingency analysis (CA) execution. Captures run timestamp, parent state_estimation_run reference, number of contingencies evaluated, number of violations detected (thermal, voltage), worst-case contingency identifier, solution method (full AC / linear DC), and operator acknowledgment status. Supports NERC TPL compliance evidence and real-time operator situational awareness. Runs are triggered automatically after each converged state estimator solution.'
}

Table grid.contingency_violation {
  contingency_violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contingency violation record. Primary key for the contingency violation entity.']
  contingency_analysis_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent contingency analysis execution that produced this violation. Links to the specific analysis run timestamp and configuration.']
  contingency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific contingency scenario (N-1, N-2, etc.) that was simulated and resulted in this violation. Identifies the outage condition tested.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the balancing authority responsible for the violated element. Identifies the operational control area where the violation occurred.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation where the violated element is located. Links the violation to physical infrastructure location.']
  emergency_stock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.emergency_stock_event. Business justification: Contingency violations requiring physical corrective action (not just redispatch) trigger emergency stock mobilization. Essential for tracking material deployment decisions, FEMA reimbursement eligibi']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system operator or dispatcher who acknowledged or acted on the violation. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'SCADA telemetry point identifier for the violated element. Links the violation to real-time monitoring data in the SCADA system.']
  acknowledged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the violation alarm has been acknowledged by an operator. True if acknowledged, false if still requiring attention.']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the violation alarm was acknowledged by an operator. Null if not yet acknowledged.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the violation alarm in the EMS operator interface. Determines visual and audible alarm presentation to operators.']
  analysis_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the power flow or stability model used for the contingency analysis. Ensures traceability of analysis results to specific model configurations.']
  contingency_case_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the contingency scenario tested (e.g., \'Loss of Line 345-123\', \'Generator Unit 5 Trip\'). Provides operator-friendly identification of the simulated outage.']
  contingency_probability decimal(5,4) [note: 'Estimated probability of the contingency event occurring (0.0 to 1.0). Used for risk-based contingency ranking and prioritization.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective actions taken or planned to mitigate the violation (e.g., \'Reduce generation at Unit 3 by 50 MW\', \'Open breaker at Station A\').']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of operator corrective actions to resolve the violation. Tracks the lifecycle of violation mitigation from detection to resolution.']
  corrective_action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when corrective action was initiated or completed by the operator. Null if no action has been taken yet.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this violation record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application that generated this violation record (e.g., \'GE EMS\', \'Siemens Spectrum PowerOn\', \'OSI Monarch\'). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the EMS or contingency analysis application that generated the violation record (e.g., \'GE PSLF\', \'Siemens Spectrum\', \'OSI Monarch\').']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this violation record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']
  limit_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The applicable operating limit threshold that was exceeded (e.g., thermal rating in MW, voltage limit in kV). Defines the boundary of acceptable operation.']
  nerc_category varchar [note: 'NERC TPL planning event category classification (P0 through P7) that this contingency represents. Defines the severity and likelihood of the contingency scenario. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: P0|P1|P2|P3|P4|P5|P6|P7 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for operator comments, analysis notes, or additional context about the violation. Used for post-event review and documentation.']
  operator_action_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether immediate operator intervention is required to mitigate the violation. True if corrective action is needed, false if informational only.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name for the violated element measurement. Enables integration with PI historian for post-event analysis and trending.']
  post_contingency_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Simulated or calculated value of the monitored parameter after the contingency event. Represents the stressed system condition that triggered the violation.']
  pre_contingency_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Measured or estimated value of the monitored parameter (MW loading, voltage kV, etc.) before the contingency event. Baseline condition for comparison.']
  rating_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Time duration in minutes for which the applicable rating is valid (e.g., 15-minute emergency rating, 4-hour emergency rating). Null for continuous normal ratings.']
  rating_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the equipment rating that was violated. Identifies whether the violation is against normal, emergency, or extreme operating limits.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the violation was resolved and the system returned to secure operating limits. Null if violation is still active.']
  risk_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated risk score combining violation severity, probability, and impact. Used for prioritizing violations in operator displays and reports.']
  time_to_violation_seconds varchar [note: 'Simulated time in seconds from the contingency event initiation to the violation occurrence. Indicates how quickly the system degrades post-contingency.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the violation values (MW for thermal, kV for voltage, etc.). Provides context for interpreting pre/post-contingency and limit values.']
  violated_element_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the violated grid element (e.g., \'Line 345kV Smith-Jones\', \'Transformer T-123\'). Provides operator-friendly identification.']
  violated_element_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific grid element (transmission line, transformer, bus, generator) that experienced the limit violation in the post-contingency state.']
  violated_element_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the grid element that experienced the violation. Categorizes the equipment type for analysis and reporting purposes.']
  violation_magnitude decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute difference between the post-contingency value and the limit value. Quantifies the extent of the violation in native units (MW, kV, etc.).']
  violation_percentage decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage by which the post-contingency value exceeds the limit. Calculated as ((post_contingency_value - limit_value) / limit_value) * 100. Used for relative severity assessment.']
  violation_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the violation based on magnitude and operational impact. Used for prioritization of operator corrective actions.']
  violation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contingency violation was detected by the analysis engine. Represents the real-time or study timestamp of the violation occurrence.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the limit violation detected. Identifies whether the violation is thermal (loading), voltage (magnitude), or stability-related.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts of the violated element. Used for categorizing violations by voltage class (transmission vs sub-transmission).']

  Note: 'Individual violation record produced by a contingency analysis run for a specific contingency case. Captures the contingency reference, violated element (line, transformer, bus), violation type (thermal overload % of rating, voltage limit exceedance kV), pre-contingency and post-contingency values, applicable rating (normal/emergency/extreme), and operator corrective action status. SSOT for real-time N-1 security violations used in NERC TPL and TOP reliability reporting.'
}

Table grid.agc_signal {
  agc_signal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the AGC signal record. Primary key for the agc_signal product.']
  agc_control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the balancing authority control area issuing this AGC signal.']
  generating_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the generating unit receiving this AGC regulation signal.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: AGC operations directly implement NERC BAL-001 and BAL-002 standards for frequency control and CPS compliance. Each AGC signal execution must be traceable to the governing obligation for CPS1/CPS2 com']
  primary_control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — AGC regulation signals and ACE computations are performed for a specific balancing authority control area. This link is required for NERC BAL-001 CPS1/CPS2 compliance reporting scoped to the correct B']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'The SCADA system point identifier associated with this AGC signal measurement.']
  to_control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — AGC signals and ACE records (now merged) are computed for a specific balancing authority control area. This FK is essential for multi-area operations and NERC BAL compliance reporting.']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.trade. Business justification: AGC regulation deployment often fulfills ancillary service trades or regulation market awards. Linking enables real-time trade performance tracking, settlement quantity validation, and compliance veri']
  actual_frequency_hz decimal(8,5) [note: 'The actual measured system frequency in hertz (Hz) at the time of this AGC signal. Nominal frequency is 60.000 Hz in North America.']
  agc_mode varchar [note: 'The operational mode of the Automatic Generation Control system at the time of this signal. Automatic mode means AGC is actively controlling generation; manual mode means operators are controlling generation manually.']
  ancillary_service_type varchar [note: 'The type of ancillary service being provided by this AGC signal. Regulation services provide continuous balancing, while reserve services provide contingency response.']
  base_point_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The base generation setpoint in megawatts (MW) for the generating unit before AGC regulation adjustment.']
  cps1_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this AGC signal interval meets NERC CPS1 compliance criteria. CPS1 measures the balancing authority\'s ability to follow its own generation schedule on a one-minute basis.']
  cps2_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this AGC signal interval meets NERC CPS2 compliance criteria. CPS2 measures the balancing authority\'s ability to limit unscheduled power flows on a 10-minute average basis.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system from which this AGC signal record was sourced (e.g., OSIsoft PI, EMS, SCADA).']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'The name of the Energy Management System that generated this AGC signal.']
  filtered_ace_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The filtered Area Control Error in megawatts (MW) after applying smoothing algorithms to remove noise and transient spikes.']
  frequency_bias_setting_mw_per_0_1_hz decimal(10,3) [note: 'The frequency bias setting in megawatts per 0.1 hertz (MW/0.1 Hz) used in the ACE calculation. Represents the balancing authority\'s expected MW response to a 0.1 Hz frequency deviation.']
  interconnection_name varchar [note: 'The name of the North American electric interconnection to which this control area belongs. The four major interconnections are Eastern, Western, Texas (ERCOT), and Quebec.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'The NERC regional entity code responsible for reliability oversight of this control area (e.g., WECC, SERC, MRO, NPCC, RF, TRE).']
  net_interchange_actual_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The actual net interchange power flow in megawatts (MW) across all tie lines for the control area at this timestamp.']
  net_interchange_deviation_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The deviation between actual and scheduled net interchange in megawatts (MW). Calculated as (Actual Net Interchange - Scheduled Net Interchange).']
  net_interchange_scheduled_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The scheduled net interchange power flow in megawatts (MW) across all tie lines for the control area at this timestamp.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, annotations, or operational notes related to this AGC signal event.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI historian tag name from which this AGC signal data was sourced. Used for traceability back to the SCADA system.']
  raw_ace_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The unfiltered Area Control Error in megawatts (MW) computed at the same time grain as the AGC signal. Raw ACE = (Actual Net Interchange - Scheduled Net Interchange) - (Frequency Bias Setting) × (Actual Frequency - Scheduled Frequency).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this AGC signal record was first created in the data platform.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this AGC signal record was last updated in the data platform.']
  regulation_direction varchar [note: 'The direction of the regulation signal. Raise indicates the unit should increase generation, lower indicates decrease, neutral indicates no change.']
  regulation_reserve_deployed_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The amount of regulation reserve capacity deployed in megawatts (MW) in response to the AGC signal.']
  regulation_signal_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The AGC regulation signal in megawatts (MW) sent to the generating unit. Positive values indicate raise signal, negative values indicate lower signal.']
  response_rate_mw_per_min decimal(10,3) [note: 'The rate at which the generating unit responds to the AGC signal, measured in megawatts per minute (MW/min).']
  rto_iso_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the RTO or ISO responsible for coordinating this control area\'s operations (e.g., PJM, MISO, CAISO, ERCOT, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP).']
  scheduled_frequency_hz decimal(8,5) [note: 'The scheduled target system frequency in hertz (Hz). Typically 60.000 Hz for North American interconnections.']
  settlement_interval varchar [note: 'The settlement interval number within the operating day, used for ancillary services market settlement. Typically 5-minute or 15-minute intervals.']
  signal_quality_code varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for the AGC signal data. Good indicates reliable data, uncertain indicates questionable data, bad indicates invalid data, substituted indicates estimated or replaced data.']
  signal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when this AGC signal and ACE computation were recorded. Primary event timestamp for this transactional record.']
  ten_minute_average_ace_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The rolling 10-minute average of Area Control Error in megawatts (MW), used for NERC CPS2 compliance calculations.']
  unit_response_status varchar [note: 'The status of the generating unit\'s response to the AGC signal. Indicates whether the unit is following the signal as expected.']

  Note: 'Time-series transactional record of Automatic Generation Control (AGC) regulation signals and Area Control Error (ACE) computations for the balancing authority control area. AGC attributes include generating unit reference, base point (MW), regulation raise/lower signal (MW), regulation reserve deployed (MW), and response rate (MW/min). ACE attributes (same time grain) include raw ACE (MW), filtered ACE (MW), 10-minute average ACE, CPS1/CPS2 compliance indicators, frequency bias setting (MW/0.1 Hz), actual system frequency (Hz), scheduled frequency (Hz), and net interchange deviation (MW). Sourced from OSIsoft PI historian AGC and ACE tags. SSOT for all AGC regulation and ACE compliance data — used for NERC BAL-001 CPS1/CPS2 compliance evidence, NERC BAL-003 frequency response obligation, frequency regulation performance tracking, and ancillary services settlement.'
}

Table grid.ace_record {
  ace_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ACE record. Primary key for the time-series ACE dataset.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the balancing authority control area for which this ACE value was computed.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: ACE measurements are the foundation of NERC BAL-001 compliance for balancing authorities. Every ACE record serves as evidence for CPS1/CPS2 compliance calculations. This link enables automated complia']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'SCADA system point identifier for the ACE calculation output. Links this record to the underlying SCADA telemetry infrastructure.']
  ace_10min_avg_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Rolling 10-minute average of ACE values in megawatts. Critical metric for NERC CPS2 compliance evaluation, which requires the 10-minute average ACE to remain within L10 limits.']
  ace_filtered_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Filtered Area Control Error value in megawatts after applying smoothing algorithms to remove noise and transient spikes. Used for AGC control actions.']
  ace_raw_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Raw unfiltered Area Control Error value in megawatts as computed by the EMS. Represents the instantaneous imbalance between scheduled and actual interchange plus frequency bias contribution.']
  actual_frequency_hz decimal(8,5) [note: 'Actual measured interconnection frequency in hertz at the time of this ACE calculation. Nominal frequency is 60.000 Hz for North American interconnections (Eastern, Western, Texas) and 50.000 Hz for other regions.']
  agc_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether AGC was enabled and actively issuing control signals to generating units at the time of this ACE measurement.']
  agc_mode varchar [note: 'Operating mode of the AGC system at the time of this ACE measurement. Automatic mode indicates AGC is actively controlling generation; manual mode indicates operators are manually balancing; off indicates AGC is disabled; test indicates AGC is in test/simulation mode.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to compute this ACE value. Standard indicates normal EMS calculation; backup indicates calculation by backup system; manual indicates operator calculation; estimated indicates interpolated or reconstructed value.']
  cps1_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this ACE measurement contributes positively to CPS1 compliance. CPS1 measures the balancing authority\'s contribution to interconnection frequency error over a rolling 12-month period.']
  cps2_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the 10-minute average ACE is within the L10 limit for this measurement period. CPS2 requires the balancing authority to maintain its 10-minute average ACE within specific bounds at least 90% of clock-10-minute periods.']
  data_quality_code varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for this ACE measurement. Good indicates validated real-time data; suspect indicates potential data quality issues; bad indicates known invalid data; estimated indicates calculated/interpolated value; manual indicates operator-entered value.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system that provided this ACE record (e.g., EMS vendor name, PI historian instance, SCADA system identifier).']
  disturbance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a reportable disturbance event was occurring during this ACE measurement period. Disturbances may trigger special ACE evaluation rules under NERC standards.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the EMS system that computed this ACE value. Supports environments with multiple EMS instances or backup systems.']
  frequency_bias_setting_mw_per_0_1_hz decimal(10,3) [note: 'The frequency bias setting in megawatts per 0.1 Hz used in the ACE calculation for this measurement period. Represents the balancing authority\'s obligation to respond to frequency deviations. Negative value indicates the BA will increase generation when frequency drops.']
  frequency_deviation_hz decimal(8,5) [note: 'Difference between actual and scheduled frequency in hertz (actual minus scheduled). Positive values indicate over-frequency; negative values indicate under-frequency.']
  ime_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Interchange meter error correction term in megawatts applied to the ACE calculation to account for known metering inaccuracies on tie lines.']
  interconnection_name varchar [note: 'Name of the North American interconnection to which this balancing authority belongs. Eastern, Western, and Texas (ERCOT) are the three major asynchronous interconnections in North America; Quebec operates as a separate interconnection.']
  l10_limit_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'The L10 limit in megawatts for CPS2 compliance evaluation. Represents the maximum allowable 10-minute average ACE magnitude. Calculated as 1.65 times epsilon10 (the root-mean-square of the balancing authority\'s 10-minute average ACE over 12 months).']
  measurement_timestamp_utc timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when the ACE measurement was captured by the EMS. Represents the real-world event time for this ACE calculation cycle, typically at 4-6 second intervals per NERC requirements.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity code for the balancing authority. WECC=Western Electricity Coordinating Council, TRE=Texas Reliability Entity, MRO=Midwest Reliability Organization, NPCC=Northeast Power Coordinating Council, RF=ReliabilityFirst, SERC=SERC Reliability Corporation, SPP=Southwest Power Pool, FRCC=Florida Reliability Coordinating Council. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|TRE|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|FRCC — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  net_actual_interchange_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net actual interchange power flow in megawatts across all tie lines connecting this balancing authority to neighboring control areas. Positive values indicate net export; negative values indicate net import.']
  net_interchange_deviation_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Difference between net actual interchange and net scheduled interchange in megawatts (actual minus scheduled). A key component of the ACE calculation formula.']
  net_scheduled_interchange_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net scheduled interchange power flow in megawatts for this measurement period, including all confirmed interchange transactions and dynamic schedules.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, annotations, or explanations related to this ACE measurement. May include operator comments about unusual conditions, data quality issues, or special events.']
  pi_historian_tag varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name for this ACE measurement point. Enables traceability to the real-time data historian for post-event analysis and compliance reporting.']
  primary_frequency_response_adjustment_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Adjustment term in megawatts applied to ACE during primary frequency response events to account for the balancing authority\'s contribution to arresting frequency decline or rise.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ACE record was first inserted into the data historian or lakehouse. Distinct from measurement_timestamp_utc, which represents the real-world event time.']
  scheduled_frequency_hz decimal(8,5) [note: 'Scheduled interconnection frequency in hertz for this measurement period. Typically 60.000 Hz for North American grids, but may vary during time error correction periods.']
  state_estimator_solution_valid boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the EMS state estimator had a valid converged solution at the time of this ACE calculation. Invalid state estimator solutions may affect ACE accuracy.']
  tie_line_meter_count varchar [note: 'Number of tie line meters contributing to the net actual interchange calculation for this ACE measurement. Used for data quality assessment.']
  tie_line_meter_failures varchar [note: 'Number of tie line meters that were unavailable or failed at the time of this ACE calculation. Non-zero values may indicate degraded ACE accuracy.']
  time_error_correction_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the interconnection was in time error correction mode during this measurement period. Time error correction adjusts scheduled frequency to correct accumulated time error.']

  Note: 'Time-series record of Area Control Error (ACE) computed by the EMS for the balancing authority control area. Captures UTC timestamp, raw ACE (MW), filtered ACE (MW), 10-minute average ACE (MW), CPS1 compliance indicator, CPS2 compliance indicator, frequency bias setting (MW/0.1 Hz), actual system frequency (Hz), scheduled frequency (Hz), and net interchange deviation (MW). SSOT for NERC BAL-001 CPS1/CPS2 compliance evidence and frequency regulation performance.'
}

Table grid.interchange_schedule {
  interchange_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interchange schedule record. Primary key for the interchange schedule entity.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Energy interchange schedules (NERC e-Tags) require documented approver for audit trails, market settlement disputes, and FERC compliance. \'approved_by\' text field should be FK to employee.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the neighboring control area or counterparty entity involved in the interchange. Must be a NERC registered entity.']
  energy_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.energy_schedule. Business justification: Interchange schedules are created from or validated against energy schedules submitted by scheduling coordinators. Linking enables schedule reconciliation, e-Tag validation, and curtailment coordinati']
  interchange_control_area_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the balancing authority control area that owns this interchange schedule.']
  primary_control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — Interchange schedules define energy flows between control areas. The FK to control_area identifies which balancing authority is party to the schedule — required for NERC BAL-002 compliance.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Interchange schedules supporting market-based rate authority or transmission service must reference the authorizing FERC filing (MBR authorization, OATT tariff). This link enables rate compliance veri']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.trade. Business justification: Interchange schedules implement physical delivery of bilateral energy trades. Direct trade linkage enables trade-to-schedule reconciliation, settlement validation, and curtailment impact analysis. Ess']
  transmission_path_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transmission path or flowgate used for this interchange. Defines the physical route of energy transfer between control areas.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the interchange schedule was approved by the balancing authority or transmission operator.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this interchange schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  curtailment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the interchange schedule has been curtailed due to transmission constraints or reliability concerns. None indicates no curtailment; partial indicates reduced MW; full indicates complete curtailment.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated this interchange schedule record. Typically the EMS, energy trading system, or e-Tag system.']
  effective_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the interchange schedule ends and energy flow ceases. Marks the conclusion of the scheduled interchange period.']
  effective_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the interchange schedule becomes effective and energy flow begins. Marks the start of the scheduled interchange period.']
  etag_reference_number varchar [note: 'Electronic tag reference number assigned by the NERC tag authority for this interchange transaction. Links to the OASIS e-Tag system.']
  interchange_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of energy flow relative to the balancing authority. Import indicates energy flowing into the control area; export indicates energy flowing out.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this interchange schedule record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest change for audit and version control.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the market context for the interchange schedule. Day-ahead schedules are submitted in advance; real-time schedules are for immediate delivery; bilateral schedules are direct contracts between parties.']
  nerc_tag_reference varchar [note: 'NERC-assigned tag identifier for the interchange schedule. Used for compliance reporting and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or operational notes related to the interchange schedule. Used for context and communication between operators.']
  oasis_posting_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the OASIS posting associated with this interchange schedule. Links to the transmission reservation and available transfer capability posting.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for the interchange schedule used during curtailment events. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Typically ranges from 1 to 7 per NERC curtailment procedures.']
  ramp_rate_mw_per_minute decimal(8,3) [note: 'Maximum rate of change in megawatts per minute allowed for this interchange schedule. Used for ramping between hourly schedule values to maintain grid stability.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the interchange schedule. Externally visible schedule reference number used in operational communications and settlements.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interchange schedule. Tracks progression from submission through approval, execution, and completion or cancellation.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interchange schedule as firm (guaranteed delivery) or non-firm (interruptible). Firm schedules have priority during curtailment events.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_01 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 01:00. Positive values indicate export; negative values indicate import per NERC convention.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_02 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 02:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_03 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 03:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_04 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 04:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_05 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 05:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_06 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 06:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_07 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 07:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_08 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 08:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_09 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 09:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_10 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 10:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_11 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 11:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_12 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 12:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_13 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 13:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_14 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 14:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_15 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 15:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_16 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 16:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_17 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 17:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_18 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 18:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_19 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 19:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_20 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 20:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_21 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 21:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_22 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 22:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_23 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 23:00.']
  scheduled_mw_hour_24 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Scheduled interchange megawatt value for hour ending 24:00.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the interchange schedule was originally submitted for approval. Tracks the initial request time for compliance and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record of energy interchange schedules between the balancing authority and neighboring control areas or counterparties. Captures schedule ID, counterparty entity (NERC registered), interchange direction (import/export), scheduled MW by hour, e-Tag reference number, NERC TAG ID, effective start/end timestamps, schedule type (firm/non-firm), curtailment status, and OASIS posting reference. SSOT for interchange scheduling used in NERC BAL-002 compliance and energy trading settlement.'
}

Table grid.load_forecast {
  load_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for load_forecast']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — Operational load forecasts are produced for a specific control area. This link scopes forecasts to the correct BA for AGC and dispatch coordination.']
  forecast_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.model. Business justification: Grid load forecasts should link to the forecast model used for accuracy tracking, model governance, and regulatory compliance. Model metadata (version, type, accuracy metrics) is essential for forecas']
  forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.run. Business justification: Grid domain load forecasts should reference the forecast run metadata for traceability, model versioning, and forecast accuracy analysis. Operators need to know which forecast run produced the values ']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Grid load forecasts are spatially scoped to forecast zones for geographic aggregation, weather correlation, and substation-level planning. Forecast zones enable hierarchical load forecasting from feed']
  load_control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control area for which this load forecast was generated.']
  load_forecast_for_control_area varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — Load forecasts are produced for a specific control area. Required for AGC and dispatch operations scoping.']
  to_control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — Operational load forecasts are produced for a specific control area. This FK scopes the forecast to the correct balancing authority boundary.']
  actual_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The actual measured system load in megawatts (MW) for the target interval, populated post-interval from SCADA telemetry for forecast accuracy tracking and model tuning.']
  agc_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast is actively used by the Automatic Generation Control system for real-time generation dispatch and frequency regulation.']
  base_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The baseline load component in megawatts (MW) representing the minimum continuous demand independent of weather or time-of-day variations.']
  behind_the_meter_generation_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The estimated distributed energy resource generation in megawatts (MW) occurring behind customer meters (rooftop solar, battery storage) that reduces net load visible to the grid.']
  cloud_cover_input_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The cloud cover percentage used as input to the load forecasting model, influencing solar radiation and temperature-driven load patterns.']
  confidence_level_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The statistical confidence level of the forecast expressed as a percentage, indicating the model\'s certainty in the predicted load value.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this load forecast record was first created and persisted in the data platform.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system or application that originated this load forecast record (e.g., EMS vendor name, forecasting module identifier).']
  day_type varchar [note: 'The classification of the target day used in the forecast model to account for load pattern variations (weekday, weekend, holiday, or special event day).']
  demand_response_adjustment_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The expected load reduction in megawatts (MW) from active demand response programs during the target interval, subtracted from gross load to calculate net forecasted load.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the Energy Management System that generated this load forecast record.']
  forecast_error_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The difference between forecasted load and actual load in megawatts (MW), calculated as (actual_load_mw - forecasted_load_mw), used for accuracy metrics and model performance evaluation.']
  forecast_error_percentage decimal(6,3) [note: 'The forecast error expressed as a percentage of actual load, calculated as ((actual_load_mw - forecasted_load_mw) / actual_load_mw) * 100, used for model accuracy assessment.']
  forecast_horizon_type varchar [note: 'The temporal horizon category of the forecast indicating the planning timeframe (hour-ahead for AGC, day-ahead for unit commitment, week-ahead for resource planning).']
  forecast_lower_bound_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The lower confidence interval bound for the forecasted load in megawatts (MW), representing the low-end estimate for minimum generation and reserve planning.']
  forecast_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the forecast model was executed and this forecast record was produced by the EMS load forecasting function.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'The lifecycle status of the forecast record indicating whether it is preliminary, final, revised, superseded by a newer forecast, or archived.']
  forecast_upper_bound_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The upper confidence interval bound for the forecasted load in megawatts (MW), representing the high-end estimate for reserve planning and contingency analysis.']
  forecasted_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The predicted system load in megawatts (MW) for the target interval as calculated by the EMS load forecasting model.']
  humidity_input_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The relative humidity percentage used as input to the load forecasting model, influencing cooling load and weather-sensitive demand patterns.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this load forecast record was last modified or updated, typically when actual load values are populated post-interval.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, comments, or explanations regarding forecast anomalies, model adjustments, or special conditions affecting this forecast.']
  peak_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the target interval is expected to be a system peak load period, used for resource adequacy and reserve planning.']
  pi_historian_tag varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI historian tag name associated with this load forecast time series for integration with the real-time data historian and operational analytics.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'The sequential revision number for this forecast target interval, incremented each time a new forecast is issued for the same target timestamp.']
  scada_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast record is integrated with SCADA telemetry for real-time actual load comparison and AGC feedback.']
  season varchar [note: 'The meteorological season classification for the target interval, used to apply seasonal load pattern adjustments in the forecasting model.']
  target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The specific date and time for which the load forecast applies (the future interval being forecasted).']
  temperature_input_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'The temperature value in degrees Fahrenheit used as input to the load forecasting model, typically representing the control area weighted average or key city temperature.']
  weather_adjusted_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The forecasted load in megawatts (MW) after applying weather normalization adjustments based on temperature, humidity, and other meteorological inputs.']
  weather_sensitive_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The portion of forecasted load in megawatts (MW) that is driven by weather conditions (heating, cooling, and weather-dependent industrial processes).']
  wind_speed_input_mph decimal(5,2) [note: 'The wind speed in miles per hour used as input to the load forecasting model, affecting heating and cooling load calculations.']

  Note: 'Operational load forecast records produced by the EMS load forecasting function for the control area. Captures forecast run timestamp, forecast horizon type (hour-ahead, day-ahead, week-ahead), target hour, forecasted system load (MW), weather-adjusted load (MW), temperature input (°F), humidity input (%), forecast model version, and actual load (MW) populated post-interval for accuracy tracking. Distinct from the long-term capacity forecast (owned by forecast domain) — this is the real-time/short-term operational load forecast used by AGC and dispatch.'
}

Table grid.generation_dispatch {
  generation_dispatch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the generation dispatch instruction record. Primary key for the generation dispatch transaction.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — Dispatch instructions are issued within a control area\'s generation fleet. FK to control_area is needed for BAL compliance and to scope dispatch to the correct balancing authority.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the system operator who issued or approved this dispatch instruction. Populated for manual dispatches or operator overrides.']
  fuel_supply_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.fuel_supply_contract. Business justification: Economic dispatch optimization requires fuel contract terms (pricing index, take-or-pay obligations, delivery constraints) to calculate operating costs and ensure contract compliance during unit commi']
  generating_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the generating unit receiving this dispatch instruction within the control area.']
  generation_control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control area issuing this dispatch instruction.']
  generation_dispatch_within_control_area varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — Economic dispatch instructions are issued to generating units within a control area. Required for NERC BAL compliance scoping and dispatch optimization.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Economic dispatch decisions often relate to specific internal orders for fuel procurement, emissions compliance projects, or ancillary service provision. Links operational dispatch to financial commit']
  ppa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.ppa. Business justification: Many generation dispatch instructions fulfill PPA delivery obligations. Linking enables real-time PPA compliance monitoring, settlement validation, and curtailment tracking. Essential for renewable en']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SCADA point used to monitor the actual output of the generating unit in response to this dispatch instruction.']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.trade. Business justification: Dispatch instructions often execute physical delivery obligations from bilateral energy trades. Operators need trade context for dispatch compliance verification, settlement reconciliation, and audit ']
  actual_output_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'The actual real power output in megawatts (MW) achieved by the generating unit in response to this dispatch instruction, as measured by SCADA telemetry. Nullable until the unit responds.']
  agc_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispatch instruction is part of an Automatic Generation Control (AGC) signal for real-time frequency regulation. True if AGC-controlled, false if manual or economic dispatch.']
  ancillary_service_type varchar [note: 'The type of ancillary service this dispatch instruction is providing, if applicable. None indicates energy-only dispatch. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulation_up|regulation_down|spinning_reserve|non_spinning_reserve|voltage_support|black_start|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_tolerance_mw decimal(8,3) [note: 'The acceptable deviation in megawatts (MW) between the dispatch setpoint and actual output for the unit to be considered compliant with the dispatch instruction.']
  contingency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispatch instruction was issued in response to a contingency event (transmission outage, generation loss, or other reliability event). True if contingency-related, false otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this dispatch record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system from which this dispatch record originated. Typically the EMS system name or SCADA integration platform.']
  dispatch_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the generating unit complied with the dispatch instruction within the required tolerance and timeframe. True if compliant, false if non-compliant. Used for NERC BAL compliance reporting.']
  dispatch_mw_setpoint decimal(10,3) [note: 'The target real power output in megawatts (MW) that the generating unit is instructed to achieve. This is the primary dispatch instruction value.']
  dispatch_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the dispatch instruction, typically generated by the Energy Management System (EMS) for operational tracking and audit purposes.']
  dispatch_reason_code varchar [note: 'The operational reason code explaining why this dispatch instruction was issued. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: economic_optimization|frequency_regulation|voltage_support|congestion_management|reserve_activation|emergency_response|must_run_contract|renewable_curtailment|manual_override|load_following|spinning_reserve|non_spinning_reserve|black_start — promote to reference product]']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispatch instruction: issued (sent to unit), acknowledged (received by unit), active (unit ramping or at setpoint), completed (instruction fulfilled), superseded (replaced by new instruction), or cancelled (instruction voided).']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dispatch instruction was issued by the EMS to the generating unit. This is the principal business event timestamp for the dispatch transaction.']
  dispatch_type varchar [note: 'The classification of the dispatch instruction indicating the operational reason for the dispatch: economic (cost-optimized), emergency (reliability event), must-run (contractual or reliability requirement), regulation (frequency control), manual (operator override), or AGC (Automatic Generation Control).']
  economic_merit_order_rank varchar [note: 'The rank of this generating unit in the economic dispatch merit order at the time of dispatch, where 1 is the lowest-cost unit. Used for economic dispatch optimization and fuel cost minimization.']
  effective_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dispatch instruction expires or is superseded by a subsequent dispatch instruction. Nullable for open-ended dispatches.']
  effective_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dispatch instruction becomes effective and the generating unit is expected to begin ramping to the target setpoint.']
  emissions_rate_co2_lbs_per_mwh decimal(10,3) [note: 'The carbon dioxide emissions rate in pounds per megawatt-hour (lbs/MWh) for this generating unit at the dispatch setpoint. Used for environmental compliance and carbon accounting.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the Energy Management System (EMS) that issued this dispatch instruction. Used for multi-EMS environments and data lineage tracking.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'The primary fuel type of the generating unit receiving this dispatch instruction. Used for fuel cost tracking and emissions reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coal|natural_gas|nuclear|hydro|wind|solar|biomass|oil|geothermal — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this dispatch record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  locational_marginal_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'The locational marginal price in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh) at the generating unit\'s point of interconnection at the time of dispatch. Used for market settlement and economic dispatch optimization.']
  market_settlement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispatch instruction is subject to wholesale market settlement with the RTO/ISO. True if market-settled, false if bilateral or internal dispatch.']
  maximum_operating_limit_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'The maximum continuous operating output in megawatts (MW) for the generating unit. The dispatch setpoint must be at or below this limit.']
  minimum_operating_limit_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'The minimum stable operating output in megawatts (MW) for the generating unit. The dispatch setpoint must be at or above this limit when the unit is online.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, comments, or special instructions related to this dispatch instruction. Used for operator communication and post-event review.']
  operating_cost_per_mwh decimal(12,4) [note: 'The marginal operating cost in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh) for this generating unit at the time of dispatch. Includes fuel cost, variable O&M, and emissions costs. Used for economic dispatch calculations.']
  operator_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispatch instruction was manually overridden by a system operator, bypassing the automated economic dispatch algorithm. True if operator-initiated, false if system-generated.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI historian tag name associated with this dispatch instruction for time-series data archival and post-event analysis.']
  ramp_rate_mw_per_min decimal(8,3) [note: 'The maximum rate at which the generating unit can change its output in megawatts per minute (MW/min). Used by the EMS to calculate the time required to reach the dispatch setpoint.']
  response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The elapsed time in seconds from when the dispatch instruction was issued to when the generating unit reached the target setpoint within compliance tolerance. Nullable until the unit responds.']
  rto_iso_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the RTO or ISO market in which this dispatch instruction was issued, if applicable. Examples: PJM, CAISO, ERCOT, MISO, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of economic dispatch and unit commitment instructions issued by the EMS to generating units within the control area. Captures dispatch timestamp, generating unit reference, dispatch MW setpoint, economic merit order rank, operating cost ($/MWh), ramp rate (MW/min), minimum/maximum operating limits (MW), dispatch type (economic/emergency/must-run), and operator override indicator. Supports NERC BAL compliance, fuel cost optimization, and generation operations coordination.'
}

Table grid.grid_switching_order {
  grid_switching_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the grid switching order. Primary key for this entity.']
  affected_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder circuit impacted by the switching operation, if applicable.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Many switching orders support capital project execution (new substation commissioning, equipment upgrades). Links operational switching activity to capital project tracking for AFUDC calculation, capi']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the control area or balancing authority responsible for coordinating this switching order.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary substation where the switching operations will be performed.']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.environmental_permit. Business justification: Switching operations at substations with environmental permits (SF6 gas handling, oil containment systems) must comply with permit conditions. This link ensures switching orders respect environmental ']
  executing_crew_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the field crew or work group assigned to execute the switching order steps.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Switching orders for maintenance, capital projects, or emergency work are authorized against internal orders that track labor, materials, and contractor costs. Essential for work order cost settlement']
  primary_grid_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system operator or control room operator who created and issued the switching order.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary equipment asset (breaker, switch, transformer) that is the subject of the switching order.']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.safety_incident. Business justification: Switching orders can result in safety incidents (arc flash, equipment failure injuries). Linking enables OSHA recordkeeping correlation and switching procedure safety analysis for continuous improveme']
  staging_warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse. Business justification: Switching operations require material staging location for crew dispatch planning, especially for capital projects and emergency restoration. Warehouse proximity to target substation drives crew deplo']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the switching order execution was completed and verified, recorded for compliance audit and performance analysis.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the switching order execution began, recorded for compliance audit and performance analysis.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the switching order was cancelled or suspended, if applicable, used for operational review and lessons learned.']
  completed_step_count varchar [note: 'Number of switching steps that have been successfully completed and verified.']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any deviations, issues, or observations recorded during switching order execution, used for post-event review and compliance documentation.']
  contingency_analysis_performed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a contingency analysis was performed to assess system reliability impacts before approving the switching order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the switching order record was first created in the system.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether customer notifications are required due to planned service interruption resulting from the switching operation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this switching order record, typically the EMS, ADMS, or OMS platform.']
  ems_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the switching order is integrated with the Energy Management System for state estimation and contingency analysis updates.']
  estimated_customer_impact_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customers who may experience service interruption as a result of the switching operation.']
  estimated_load_impact_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated load in megawatts that will be interrupted or transferred during the switching operation, used for system balancing and contingency analysis.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the switching order record was most recently modified, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  nerc_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this switching order is subject to NERC TOP reliability standard compliance reporting and audit requirements.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the switching order from initial draft through approval, execution, and completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|issued|in_progress|completed|cancelled|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the switching order based on operational purpose: planned maintenance, emergency response, system restoration, equipment testing, or new asset commissioning.']
  outage_coordination_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this switching order requires coordination with neighboring utilities, Regional Transmission Organizations (RTO), or Independent System Operators (ISO).']
  pi_historian_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'Tag prefix used in the OSIsoft PI historian system for archiving switching order execution data and equipment status changes.']
  post_switching_network_state varchar [note: 'Description of the intended network topology and equipment status after the switching order is completed, used for verification and state estimation.']
  pre_switching_network_state varchar [note: 'Description of the network topology and equipment status before the switching order is executed, used for state verification and rollback planning.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the switching order based on system impact, safety considerations, and business urgency.']
  safety_clearance_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the associated safety clearance or work permit authorizing personnel to work on de-energized equipment.']
  scada_verification_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether SCADA telemetry is used to verify equipment status changes during switching order execution.']
  scheduled_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the switching order execution is scheduled to be completed and equipment returned to normal service.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the switching order execution is scheduled to begin.']
  switching_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the switching order, used by operators and field crews for reference and communication.']
  tagging_authority varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role authorized to place safety tags on equipment during the switching sequence.']
  target_equipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the equipment being switched: circuit breaker, disconnect switch, transformer, or other grid device. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: circuit_breaker|disconnect_switch|load_break_switch|transformer|capacitor_bank|reactor|bus_tie|recloser — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_step_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual switching steps defined in this switching order sequence.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a switching order — a formal, sequenced set of switching steps issued by the system operator to field crews for network reconfiguration, isolation, or restoration. Order-level attributes include switching order number, issuing operator, target substation and equipment, safety clearance reference, pre/post-switching network state, execution timestamps, and completion status. Step-level detail (grain: one row per step) captures sequence number, device identifier, action type (open/close/tag/verify), device pre/post-action status, executing crew member, execution timestamp, and step completion status. SSOT for switching order management including granular step-by-step audit trail used in NERC TOP compliance, outage coordination, and post-event review.'
}

Table grid.grid_switching_step {
  grid_switching_step_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the individual switching step within a switching order. Primary key for the grid switching step entity.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation where the switching device is located. Enables geographic and operational context for crew dispatch and coordination.']
  grid_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent switching order that contains this step. Links the step to its parent switching sequence for operational coordination and audit trail.']
  primary_grid_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the crew member or operator who executed this switching step. Used for accountability, training assessment, and incident investigation.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Individual switching steps target specific assets (circuit breakers, disconnects, reclosers). Work management and safety systems require asset-level traceability to retrieve equipment specifications, ']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SCADA telemetry point that monitors the status of the switching device. Enables automated status verification and real-time operational visibility.']
  spare_parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.spare_parts_catalog. Business justification: Individual switching steps may require specific spare parts (fuses, bushings, control components) for contingency scenarios. Catalog link enables pre-positioning of critical spares at substation befor']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Type of switching action to be performed on the device. Defines the specific operation required for this step in the switching sequence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|close|tag|verify|ground|remove_ground|rack_in|rack_out — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  actual_device_status varchar [note: 'Actual observed status of the device after the switching action was executed. Captured from SCADA telemetry or field crew verification for audit trail and discrepancy detection.']
  actual_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when this switching step was executed. Captured from SCADA system or field crew reporting for precise audit trail and sequence timing analysis.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this switching step was authorized for execution. Documents the authorization event for operational audit trail and compliance verification.']
  clearance_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the safety clearance or work permit authorizing this switching step. Links to safety management system for compliance verification.']
  contingency_step_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this step is a contingency or alternate step to be executed only if primary steps fail. True for contingency steps, false for primary sequence steps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this switching step record was first created in the system. Establishes the audit trail origin for the step record.']
  critical_step_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this step is critical to grid reliability or safety. True for steps that require enhanced verification or cannot be skipped without significant operational impact.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated this switching step record. Typically the Outage Management System (OMS), Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS), or Energy Management System (EMS).']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the switching device for operator recognition and communication. Typically includes substation name and device position identifier.']
  device_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the switching device (breaker, disconnect switch, recloser, or sectionalizing device) that is the target of this switching action. Links to asset registry or SCADA point configuration.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the switching device being operated. Determines operational procedures, safety protocols, and switching sequence constraints.']
  ems_control_command_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the EMS control command issued to execute this switching step remotely. Links to EMS audit logs for cyber security compliance and operational review.']
  executing_crew_member_name varchar [note: 'Name of the crew member or operator who executed this switching step. Provides human-readable identification for operational logs and audit trails.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason why the switching step failed or could not be completed as planned. Captured for root cause analysis and procedure improvement.']
  hold_tag_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical hold tag was applied to the device as part of this switching step. Used for lockout/tagout compliance and worker safety.']
  hold_tag_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical hold tag applied to the device. Enables tracking of lockout/tagout devices for safety compliance and tag removal coordination.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this switching step record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to the step record for audit trail and data quality monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, observations, or special conditions related to this switching step. Captures context not covered by structured fields.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name for the switching device status point. Enables retrieval of historical device status data for post-event analysis and compliance reporting.']
  post_action_device_status varchar [note: 'Expected status of the device after the switching action is completed. Used for verification that the switching action achieved the intended result.']
  pre_action_device_status varchar [note: 'Expected status of the device before the switching action is executed. Used for verification and safety checks to ensure proper switching sequence execution.']
  remote_operation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this switching step was executed remotely via SCADA or Energy Management System (EMS) rather than by field crew. True for remote operations, false for manual field operations.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this switching step was attempted before successful completion. Indicates operational challenges or equipment issues requiring investigation.']
  safety_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this switching step requires a safety clearance or work permit before execution. True for steps involving energized equipment or confined spaces.']
  scada_status_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device status change was confirmed via SCADA telemetry. True if SCADA confirmation received, false if manual verification only or SCADA unavailable.']
  scheduled_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for execution of this switching step. Used for crew scheduling, outage coordination, and operational planning.']
  step_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual elapsed time in minutes from step initiation to completion. Used for operational efficiency analysis, crew performance assessment, and future switching order planning.']
