  step_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this step within the parent switching order. Determines the execution order of switching actions to ensure safe and coordinated grid operations.']
  step_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of this switching step. Tracks the lifecycle state of the step from planning through completion for operational coordination and audit purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|completed|verified|failed|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  switching_authority varchar [note: 'Role or position of the person who authorized this switching step. Defines the level of operational authority required for the switching action.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the switching step completion was verified. Documents the verification event for compliance with two-person verification protocols.']
  verifying_crew_member_name varchar [note: 'Name of the crew member or operator who verified the completion of this switching step. Provides human-readable identification for verification audit trail.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts of the circuit or equipment being switched. Critical for safety protocols, crew qualifications, and switching procedure selection.']

  Note: 'Individual step within a switching order, representing a single discrete switching action (open or close a specific breaker, disconnect switch, or recloser). Captures step sequence number, parent switching order reference, device identifier, action type (open/close/tag/verify), device pre-action status, device post-action status, executing crew member, execution timestamp, and step completion status. Enables granular audit trail of switching sequence execution for NERC TOP compliance and post-event review.'
}

Table grid.voltage_control_action {
  voltage_control_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the voltage control action record.']
  contingency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingency analysis scenario that recommended or triggered this control action. Null for non-contingency-driven actions.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control area where the voltage control action was executed.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system operator who initiated or approved the control action. Null for fully automatic actions. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder affected by the voltage control action. Applicable for distribution-level voltage control actions.']
  primary_ems_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — Voltage control actions target a specific EMS bus (node) to maintain voltage within limits. The FK to ems_node identifies the target bus for the action.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SCADA telemetry point associated with the controlled device.']
  voltage_ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the EMS node (bus) where the voltage control action was targeted.']
  action_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Time in seconds from action initiation to completion or stabilization. Includes device response time and system settling time.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the voltage control action. Successful indicates action completed and achieved desired voltage change, failed indicates action did not execute or had no effect, partial indicates action executed but did not fully achieve target, aborted indicates action was cancelled mid-execution, pending verification indicates action executed but effectiveness not yet confirmed.']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the voltage control action was executed in the field. This is the principal business event timestamp representing when the physical control action occurred.']
  alarm_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a voltage alarm was triggered that led to this control action. True if an alarm condition existed, False if action was preventive or scheduled.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this voltage control action was taken to maintain compliance with NERC FAC-001 voltage profile requirements or other regulatory standards. True if compliance-driven, False otherwise.']
  control_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of voltage control action executed. Capacitor bank switching adds reactive power, reactor switching removes reactive power, transformer tap change adjusts voltage ratio, generator VAR dispatch adjusts generator reactive output, static VAR compensator provides dynamic reactive support, synchronous condenser provides rotating reactive reserve.']
  control_mode varchar [note: 'Operating mode under which the control action was executed. Automatic indicates system-initiated without operator intervention, manual indicates direct operator command, supervisory indicates operator-approved automatic action, emergency indicates action taken during system emergency, test indicates action performed during commissioning or testing.']
  controlled_device_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the physical device that was controlled (e.g., capacitor bank CB-123, transformer T-456, generator G-789).']
  controlled_device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the controlled device. Capacitor bank provides capacitive reactive power, shunt reactor provides inductive reactive power, load tap changer adjusts transformer voltage, generator provides dynamic VAR support, SVC and STATCOM provide fast dynamic reactive compensation, synchronous condenser provides rotating inertia and reactive support. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: capacitor_bank|shunt_reactor|load_tap_changer|generator|svc|statcom|synchronous_condenser — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this voltage control action record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated this voltage control action record (e.g., GE ADMS, OSIsoft PI, SCADA RTU).']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the EMS system that executed or recorded the voltage control action (e.g., GE ADMS, ABB Symphony Plus, Siemens Spectrum).']
  initiating_cause varchar [note: 'Source or trigger that initiated the voltage control action. Automatic Volt-VAR optimization indicates EMS algorithm-driven action, automatic voltage regulator indicates local device control, operator manual indicates human-initiated action, state estimator recommendation indicates action based on network state analysis, contingency analysis recommendation indicates preventive action, scheduled maintenance indicates planned operational adjustment.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this voltage control action record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  mvar_change decimal(10,2) [note: 'Change in reactive power in megavolt-amperes reactive resulting from the control action. Positive values indicate capacitive reactive power injection (voltage support), negative values indicate inductive reactive power absorption (voltage reduction).']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity jurisdiction where the voltage control action occurred. WECC=Western Electricity Coordinating Council, ERCOT=Electric Reliability Council of Texas, MRO=Midwest Reliability Organization, NPCC=Northeast Power Coordinating Council, RF=ReliabilityFirst, SERC=SERC Reliability Corporation, SPP=Southwest Power Pool, TRE=Texas Reliability Entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|TRE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator comments, special conditions, or additional context about the voltage control action.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name associated with this voltage control action for time-series data archival and retrieval.']
  post_action_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured voltage in kilovolts at the target bus after the control action was executed and the system stabilized. Used to assess the effectiveness of the control action.']
  power_factor_post_action decimal(5,4) [note: 'Power factor measured at the target bus after the control action and system stabilization. Used to assess improvement in reactive power balance.']
  power_factor_pre_action decimal(5,4) [note: 'Power factor measured at the target bus before the control action. Range -1.0 to 1.0, where values closer to 1.0 indicate better reactive power balance. Negative values indicate leading power factor (capacitive), positive values indicate lagging power factor (inductive).']
  pre_action_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured voltage in kilovolts at the target bus immediately before the control action was executed. Used to assess the voltage deviation that triggered the action.']
  rto_iso_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the RTO or ISO market operator responsible for the transmission system where the action occurred (e.g., CAISO, PJM, MISO, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP).']
  substation_name varchar [note: 'Name of the substation where the controlled device is located.']
  tap_position_change varchar [note: 'Number of tap positions changed for transformer tap control actions. Positive values indicate raise operations, negative values indicate lower operations. Null for non-tap control actions.']
  target_bus_name varchar [note: 'Name of the electrical bus where voltage control was targeted. This is the point in the network where voltage was being regulated.']
  target_bus_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts of the target bus where control action was applied.']
  voltage_deviation_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage deviation of pre-action voltage from the nominal voltage setpoint. Calculated as ((pre_action_voltage - nominal_voltage) / nominal_voltage) * 100. Positive values indicate overvoltage, negative values indicate undervoltage.']
  voltage_limit_high_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Upper voltage limit in kilovolts for the target bus as defined by operating procedures or NERC standards. Actions are triggered when voltage approaches or exceeds this limit.']
  voltage_limit_low_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Lower voltage limit in kilovolts for the target bus as defined by operating procedures or NERC standards. Actions are triggered when voltage approaches or falls below this limit.']
  voltage_setpoint_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Target voltage setpoint in kilovolts that the control action was attempting to achieve or maintain at the target bus.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of voltage control actions taken by the EMS Volt-VAR optimization or operator to maintain voltage within acceptable limits. Captures action timestamp, control type (capacitor bank switching, reactor switching, transformer tap change, generator VAR dispatch), controlled device reference, pre-action voltage (kV), post-action voltage (kV), MVAR change, initiating cause (automatic/operator), and target bus. Supports NERC FAC-001 voltage profile compliance and reactive power management reporting.'
}

Table grid.frequency_event {
  frequency_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the frequency excursion event. Primary key for the frequency event record.']
  corrective_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corrective action plan developed in response to this frequency event to prevent recurrence or mitigate future impacts.']
  emergency_stock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.emergency_stock_event. Business justification: Frequency excursion events causing generation/transmission equipment damage trigger emergency stock deployment for restoration. Essential for tracking material response to interconnection-wide disturb']
  ems_alarm_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the EMS alarm that was triggered when the frequency event was detected. Links the event to the real-time alarm sequence.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: Large outages trigger frequency events (generation/load imbalance). Linking enables analysis of frequency response, UFLS activation, and NERC BAL-003 compliance for disturbance control standard.']
  frequency_control_area_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the control area or balancing authority where the frequency event was detected.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary SCADA measurement point used to detect and monitor the frequency event, typically a system frequency measurement point.']
  to_control_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.control_area.control_area_id — Frequency events are detected within a specific control area\'s EMS. FK to control_area scopes the event for NERC BAL-003 frequency response obligation reporting.']
  agc_response_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether Automatic Generation Control (AGC) was active and responding during the frequency event to provide corrective generation adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this frequency event record was first created in the data system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that captured and recorded the frequency event data, typically the Energy Management System (EMS) or SCADA system.']
  disturbance_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Magnitude of the generation or load disturbance in Megawatts (MW) that caused the frequency event. Represents the MW imbalance introduced into the system.']
  event_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the frequency excursion event measured in seconds, from initial detection to restoration or stabilization.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the frequency excursion event concluded and system frequency returned to acceptable operating range or stabilized.']
  event_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the frequency event based on magnitude of frequency deviation, duration, and system impact. Used for prioritization and post-event analysis.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the frequency excursion event was first detected by the Energy Management System (EMS). Represents the moment system frequency deviated beyond normal operating thresholds.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the frequency event based on the direction of frequency deviation: under-frequency (frequency drop), over-frequency (frequency rise), or frequency oscillation (sustained fluctuation).']
  frequency_deviation_hz decimal(8,4) [note: 'Maximum deviation of system frequency from nominal frequency (typically 60 Hz in North America or 50 Hz elsewhere) during the event, measured in Hertz.']
  frequency_response_mw_per_hz decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured frequency response of the balancing authority in Megawatts per Hertz (MW/Hz), calculated as the change in generation output divided by the change in frequency. Key metric for NERC BAL-003 compliance.']
  generation_response_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total generation response in Megawatts (MW) provided by generating units through governor action, Automatic Generation Control (AGC), or manual dispatch to arrest frequency deviation.']
  interconnection_name varchar [note: 'Name of the North American interconnection where the frequency event occurred: Eastern Interconnection, Western Interconnection, Texas (ERCOT) Interconnection, or Quebec Interconnection.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this frequency event record was last modified or updated with additional information.']
  load_shed_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount of load shed in Megawatts (MW) through UFLS or manual operator actions to restore frequency balance during the event.']
  max_rocof_hz_per_second decimal(10,6) [note: 'Maximum rate of change of frequency observed during the event in Hertz per second (Hz/s). Peak ROCOF value used for worst-case analysis.']
  nadir_frequency_hz decimal(8,4) [note: 'Lowest system frequency in Hertz (Hz) reached during the under-frequency event. Critical metric for assessing severity of frequency decline and system stability.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity with jurisdiction over the balancing authority where the event occurred (e.g., WECC, SERC, MRO, NPCC, RF, TRE).']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by system operators documenting observations, actions taken, and contextual information about the frequency event.']
  peak_frequency_hz decimal(8,4) [note: 'Highest system frequency in Hertz (Hz) reached during an over-frequency event. Used for over-frequency disturbance analysis.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name for the frequency measurement time series data associated with this event. Used for detailed post-event analysis and trending.']
  pre_event_frequency_hz decimal(8,4) [note: 'System frequency in Hertz (Hz) immediately before the disturbance event occurred, representing baseline operating frequency.']
  recovery_frequency_hz decimal(8,4) [note: 'System frequency in Hertz (Hz) at the point of stabilization or restoration after the disturbance, indicating the settling point after corrective actions.']
  reportable_event_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the frequency event meets NERC EOP-004 criteria for mandatory reporting to regulatory authorities and the Electricity Reliability Organization (ERO).']
  restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when system frequency was fully restored to normal operating range and all corrective actions were completed.']
  rocof_hz_per_second decimal(10,6) [note: 'Rate of Change of Frequency measured in Hertz per second (Hz/s). Indicates how rapidly system frequency is changing and is critical for assessing system inertia and disturbance severity.']
  root_cause_analysis_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a formal root cause analysis has been completed for this frequency event.']
  triggering_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause or initiating event that triggered the frequency excursion. Common causes include generation unit trip, sudden load loss, transmission fault, system islanding, or tie-line trip.']
  ufls_activated_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether Under-Frequency Load Shedding (UFLS) schemes were activated during the event to arrest frequency decline and prevent system collapse.']
  ufls_stage_activated varchar [note: 'Identifier of the UFLS stage or stages that were activated during the event (e.g., Stage 1, Stage 2). Multiple stages may be activated in severe events.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a system frequency excursion event detected by the EMS. Captures event start timestamp, nadir frequency (Hz), recovery frequency (Hz), rate of change of frequency (ROCOF in Hz/s), duration (seconds), triggering cause (generation trip, load loss, islanding), under-frequency load shedding (UFLS) activation flag, MW of load shed, and restoration timestamp. SSOT for frequency disturbance events used in NERC BAL-003 frequency response obligation reporting and post-event analysis.'
}

Table grid.pmu_measurement {
  pmu_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the PMU synchrophasor measurement record. Primary key for the pmu_measurement product.']
  pmu_device_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PMU device that generated this measurement. Links to the physical PMU asset in the asset registry.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SCADA point configuration associated with this PMU measurement stream. Links to the SCADA point registry for metadata and configuration.']
  wams_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a WAMS disturbance or oscillation event that this measurement is associated with. Null for routine measurements outside of event windows.']
  active_power_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Real power flow in megawatts calculated from voltage and current phasors. Positive values indicate power flowing in the defined direction.']
  apparent_power_mva decimal(12,3) [note: 'Apparent power in megavolt-amperes, the vector sum of active and reactive power. Represents total power flow magnitude.']
  balancing_authority_code varchar [note: 'NERC-registered balancing authority code for the control area where this PMU is located. Used for jurisdictional reporting and inter-BA coordination.']
  current_angle_degrees decimal(10,6) [note: 'Phase angle of the current phasor in degrees relative to the system reference. Used to calculate power flow and detect phase imbalances.']
  current_magnitude_amps decimal(12,6) [note: 'Magnitude of the current phasor measured in amperes. Represents the RMS current flowing through the monitored circuit element.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for the PMU measurement data. Good indicates valid GPS-synchronized data; questionable indicates potential issues; invalid indicates data should not be used for analysis.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that provided this PMU measurement data. Typically the PDC system name or OSIsoft PI interface identifier.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of electrical equipment being monitored by this PMU measurement. Determines the interpretation and application of the phasor data.']
  frequency_hz decimal(10,6) [note: 'Measured system frequency in Hertz at the PMU location. Nominal frequency is 60 Hz in North America or 50 Hz in other regions. Frequency deviations indicate generation-load imbalances.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PMU measurement record was ingested into the Silver layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage and latency monitoring.']
  interconnection_name varchar [note: 'North American interconnection where this PMU is located. The four major interconnections operate asynchronously from each other.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the PMU device location in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial visualization and propagation analysis.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the PMU device location in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial visualization and propagation analysis.']
  measurement_timestamp_utc timestamp [note: 'Precise UTC timestamp of the synchrophasor measurement with microsecond resolution. GPS-synchronized time reference for wide-area situational awareness and event correlation.']
  negative_sequence_voltage_kv decimal(12,6) [note: 'Negative sequence component of the three-phase voltage in kilovolts. Indicates voltage imbalance and potential phase faults.']
  nerc_prc_002_registered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PMU measurement point is registered for NERC PRC-002 disturbance monitoring compliance. Registered points must retain data for reportable disturbances.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, data quality annotations, or event context related to this PMU measurement record.']
  oscillation_damping_ratio decimal(6,4) [note: 'Damping ratio of the detected oscillation mode. Values below 0.03 indicate poorly damped oscillations requiring operator attention. Null if no oscillation detected.']
  oscillation_detected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an inter-area or local oscillation was detected in this measurement window by the WAMS analytics engine. Used for modal analysis and stability assessment.']
  oscillation_frequency_hz decimal(8,4) [note: 'Dominant oscillation frequency detected in Hertz. Typical inter-area oscillations range from 0.1 to 1.0 Hz. Null if no oscillation detected.']
  pdc_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the PDC that aggregated and forwarded this PMU measurement. PDCs collect synchrophasor data from multiple PMUs for wide-area monitoring.']
  pi_archive_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PMU measurement stream is actively archived in the OSIsoft PI historian for long-term storage and analysis.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name where this PMU measurement stream is archived. Enables correlation with other SCADA and operational data in the PI system.']
  pmu_configuration_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the PMU configuration frame active when this measurement was captured. Used to track configuration changes over time.']
  pmu_sync_status varchar [note: 'Overall synchronization status of the PMU device. Synchronized indicates the PMU is GPS-locked and producing valid time-aligned measurements.']
  positive_sequence_voltage_kv decimal(12,6) [note: 'Positive sequence component of the three-phase voltage in kilovolts. Represents the balanced component of the voltage phasor.']
  power_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of active power to apparent power, ranging from -1.0 to 1.0. Indicates the efficiency of power transmission and the phase relationship between voltage and current.']
  reactive_power_mvar decimal(12,3) [note: 'Reactive power flow in megavolt-amperes reactive calculated from voltage and current phasors. Used for voltage control and power factor management.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this measurement record. Incremented if the record is corrected or reprocessed. Initial version is 1.']
  reporting_rate_fps varchar [note: 'PMU reporting rate in frames per second. Common rates are 30, 60, or 120 FPS. Higher rates provide better temporal resolution for transient event analysis.']
  rocof_hz_per_second decimal(10,6) [note: 'Rate of change of frequency measured in Hz per second. High ROCOF values indicate rapid frequency excursions and potential grid instability events requiring immediate response.']
  substation_name varchar [note: 'Name of the substation where the PMU device is installed. Provides geographic and operational context for the measurement.']
  time_quality_flag varchar [note: 'GPS time synchronization status. Locked indicates accurate GPS time; unlocked indicates loss of GPS signal; holdover indicates operating on internal clock.']
  total_harmonic_distortion_current_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Total harmonic distortion of the current waveform expressed as a percentage. Indicates non-linear load effects and power quality issues.']
  total_harmonic_distortion_voltage_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Total harmonic distortion of the voltage waveform expressed as a percentage. Measures power quality and the presence of harmonic frequencies.']
  voltage_angle_degrees decimal(10,6) [note: 'Phase angle of the voltage phasor in degrees relative to the system reference. Critical for detecting angular separation and stability issues across the wide-area grid.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage level of the bus or circuit where the PMU is measuring. Common transmission levels include 115, 230, 345, 500, and 765 kV.']
  voltage_magnitude_kv decimal(12,6) [note: 'Magnitude of the voltage phasor measured in kilovolts. Represents the RMS voltage at the PMU measurement point.']
  zero_sequence_voltage_kv decimal(12,6) [note: 'Zero sequence component of the three-phase voltage in kilovolts. Indicates ground faults and neutral voltage displacement.']

  Note: 'Time-series record of Phasor Measurement Unit (PMU) synchrophasor data ingested from the wide-area measurement system (WAMS) into the OSIsoft PI historian and Silver layer. Captures PMU device reference, UTC timestamp (microsecond precision), voltage phasor magnitude (kV) and angle (degrees), current phasor magnitude (amps) and angle, frequency (Hz), ROCOF (Hz/s), and PDC data quality flags. Supports wide-area situational awareness, oscillation detection, and NERC PRC-002 disturbance monitoring compliance.'
}

Table grid.pmu_device {
  pmu_device_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Phasor Measurement Unit device. Primary key for the PMU device master record.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the balancing authority or control area responsible for the grid region where this PMU is deployed.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation where the PMU device is physically installed and commissioned.']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — PMU devices monitor specific buses/lines in the EMS network model. FK to ems_node identifies which network element the PMU is measuring — required for WAMS data integration with state estimation.']
  nerc_cip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.nerc_cip_asset. Business justification: PMUs at control centers and critical substations are BES Cyber Assets under NERC CIP-002. Each PMU must be categorized and tracked in the CIP asset inventory for CIP-007 and CIP-010 compliance (system']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: PMU devices are capital assets requiring maintenance, calibration, warranty tracking, depreciation accounting, and spare parts inventory management. Asset management systems need direct linkage to tra']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the PMU device was commissioned and began operational data reporting in the WAMS network.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Network protocol used by the PMU to transmit synchrophasor data to the PDC (IEEE C37.118, IEC 61850-90-5, DNP3, or Modbus TCP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PMU device record was first created in the system.']
  critical_infrastructure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PMU device is designated as critical infrastructure requiring enhanced cybersecurity and physical security controls per NERC CIP standards.']
  data_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Typical end-to-end latency in milliseconds from PMU measurement to PDC receipt, critical for real-time wide-area monitoring applications.']
  data_quality_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable data quality percentage for this PMU device, below which alarms are triggered for investigation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application from which this PMU device master data originated (e.g., WAMS configuration system, asset registry, EMS).']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label assigned to the PMU device for operational identification and reference in WAMS configuration.']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the PMU hardware unit, used for warranty tracking and asset management.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PMU device form factor: standalone dedicated PMU, relay with embedded PMU functionality, meter with embedded PMU, or portable PMU for temporary deployment.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the PMU device, critical for compatibility and feature support in synchrophasor data collection.']
  gps_sync_source varchar [note: 'Time synchronization method used by the PMU to achieve microsecond-level timestamp accuracy required for synchrophasor measurements (GPS antenna, IRIG-B, Precision Time Protocol, Network Time Protocol, or internal clock).']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the PMU device was physically installed at the substation location, which may precede commissioning date.']
  interconnection_name varchar [note: 'Name of the major North American power grid interconnection where the PMU is deployed (Eastern, Western, Texas, or Quebec).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address assigned to the PMU device for communication with the PDC and WAMS applications.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration or accuracy verification test performed on the PMU device to ensure measurement quality.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PMU device record was most recently modified.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the PMU device installation location in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the PMU device installation location in decimal degrees.']
  maintenance_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the maintenance service contract covering this PMU device.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the PMU device (e.g., SEL, GE, ABB, Schweitzer Engineering Laboratories).']
  model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number or designation for the PMU device, identifying the specific product line and capabilities.']
  monitored_bus_name varchar [note: 'Name of the electrical bus or node that this PMU is monitoring for voltage phasor measurements.']
  monitored_line_name varchar [note: 'Name of the transmission line or branch that this PMU is monitoring for current phasor and power flow measurements.']
  nerc_prc_002_registered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PMU device is registered with NERC as part of the disturbance monitoring equipment required under PRC-002 reliability standard.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'North American Electric Reliability Corporation regional entity jurisdiction where the PMU is located (WECC, ERCOT, MRO, NPCC, RF, SERC, SPP, TRE). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|TRE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nerc_registration_code varchar [note: 'NERC-assigned registration identifier for this PMU device when registered under PRC-002 disturbance monitoring requirements.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration or accuracy verification test of the PMU device.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special considerations related to the PMU device configuration or operation.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the PMU device in the WAMS network, indicating whether it is actively reporting synchrophasor data.']
  operator_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the utility or organization responsible for operating and maintaining the PMU device, which may differ from the owner.']
  owner_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the utility or organization that owns the PMU device asset.']
  pdc_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Phasor Data Concentrator that aggregates and time-aligns synchrophasor data from this PMU device.']
  pdc_stream_reference varchar [note: 'Unique stream identifier or channel number assigned by the PDC for this PMU data feed.']
  pi_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'Prefix used for all OSIsoft PI historian tags associated with this PMU device, enabling integration with PI for synchrophasor data archival and analytics.']
  reporting_rate_fps varchar [note: 'Number of synchrophasor data frames transmitted per second by the PMU device, typically 30, 60, or 120 frames per second depending on application requirements.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the PMU device was decommissioned and removed from operational service in the WAMS network.']
  total_error_vector_limit_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable Total Error Vector percentage for synchrophasor measurements, per IEEE C37.118 accuracy requirements.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts of the electrical equipment being monitored by the PMU device.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty coverage for the PMU device expires.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Phasor Measurement Unit (PMU) deployed in the wide-area measurement system (WAMS). Captures PMU device name, manufacturer, model, firmware version, GPS synchronization source, reporting rate (frames/second), installed substation reference, monitored bus/line, PDC (Phasor Data Concentrator) assignment, commissioning date, and NERC PRC-002 registration status. SSOT for PMU device inventory used in WAMS configuration and synchrophasor data quality management.'
}

Table grid.operator_log {
  operator_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the operator log entry. Primary key for the operator log record.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Operator logs are primary evidence during NERC audits for TOP and IRO standards. Auditors review logs to verify operator actions during system events and compliance with operating procedures. This lin']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the system operator who created the log entry. Used for accountability, audit trails, and NERC compliance verification.']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the EMS node or bus associated with the log entry. Applicable when the entry relates to a specific electrical node in the network model.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: Operators log actions during outage events for audit trail, shift handover, and regulatory compliance. Linking enables comprehensive event reconstruction and NERC compliance documentation.']
  operator_scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SCADA telemetry point associated with the log entry. Links the entry to specific real-time measurement or control points.']
  primary_operator_control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control area or balancing authority area where the logged event occurred. Links the entry to the responsible operational jurisdiction.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Operators frequently log observations about specific equipment (unusual noise, oil leak, temperature anomaly, visual damage). These narrative entries feed asset condition assessments, health index cal']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.scada_point.scada_point_id — After merging ems_alarm and voltage_control_action into operator_log, alarm entries reference associated SCADA points. Required for alarm-to-measurement correlation.']
  shift_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the operational shift during which the log entry was created. Links entries to specific shift periods for crew accountability and handover analysis.']
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the state estimation solution run active at the time of the log entry. Links operator actions to the network state model.']
  abnormal_condition_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the abnormal condition when entry_type is abnormal_condition_noted. Categorizes the type of operational anomaly observed.']
  ace_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Area control error in megawatts at the time of the log entry. Key metric for balancing authority performance and CPS compliance.']
  agc_mode_at_entry varchar [note: 'Operating mode of the automatic generation control system at the time of the log entry. Documents the AGC state for operational context.']
  alarm_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alarm was acknowledged by the operator. Used for alarm response time analysis and operator performance metrics.']
  alarm_clearance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alarm condition was cleared or resolved. Marks the end of the alarm lifecycle for duration analysis.']
  alarm_message_text varchar [note: 'System-generated alarm message text when entry_type is alarm. Captures the exact alarm notification presented to the operator.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the alarm when entry_type is alarm. Indicates the operational severity and required response urgency.']
  alarm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alarm when entry_type is alarm. Categorizes the nature of the alarm condition for alarm management and performance tracking.']
  communication_method varchar [note: 'Method or channel used for the communication when entry_type is verbal_communication. Documents the communication medium for audit purposes.']
  communication_party varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the external party involved in the communication when entry_type is verbal_communication. May include other control areas, reliability coordinators, or field personnel.']
  contingency_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the log entry is related to a contingency analysis result or violation. Links operator actions to contingency assessment outcomes.']
  control_initiating_cause varchar [note: 'Classification of what triggered the voltage control action. Distinguishes between automatic system responses, manual operator interventions, scheduled actions, and emergency responses.']
  controlled_device varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the device that was controlled when entry_type is voltage_control_action. Specifies the equipment operated for voltage regulation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the operator log record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit field for data lineage tracking.']
  entry_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the operator action, observation, or event. Provides detailed context and operator commentary for post-event analysis and regulatory review.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operator log entry was recorded in the EMS system. This is the principal event timestamp for the log entry, critical for chronological reconstruction of grid operations and NERC TOP-001 compliance.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operator log entry type. Discriminates between operator actions, system alarms, voltage control activities, communications, abnormal conditions, and switching orders.']
  equipment_reference varchar [note: 'Free-text reference to the equipment, device, or facility associated with the log entry. May include breaker names, transformer IDs, line designations, or substation names.']
  frequency_hz decimal(6,3) [note: 'System frequency in hertz at the time of the log entry. Critical measurement for frequency regulation and NERC BAL standard compliance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the operator log record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit field for change tracking.']
  mvar_change decimal(10,2) [note: 'Change in reactive power in megavolt-amperes reactive resulting from the voltage control action. Quantifies the reactive power adjustment made.']
  nerc_reportable_event boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the logged event meets NERC EOP-004 criteria for mandatory event reporting. Used for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments added by operators or analysts during post-event review. Supplementary information for investigation and compliance documentation.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name associated with the log entry. Links the operator log to time-series telemetry data for integrated analysis.']
  post_action_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Voltage level in kilovolts measured after the voltage control action was completed. Result measurement for evaluating control effectiveness.']
  pre_action_voltage_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Voltage level in kilovolts measured before the voltage control action was taken. Baseline measurement for evaluating control effectiveness.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority or severity level assigned to the log entry. Used for filtering critical events and prioritizing post-event review activities.']
  reliability_coordinator_notified boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the reliability coordinator was notified of the event or condition. Critical for NERC TOP-001 compliance verification.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated or recorded the operator log entry. Typically the EMS, SCADA, or operator logging application.']
  switching_order_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the switching order when entry_type is switching_order_issued. Links the log entry to the formal switching procedure documentation.']
  to_switching_order varchar [note: 'FK to grid.switching_order.switching_order_id — Operator log entries frequently reference switching orders being issued or executed. This FK enables audit trail linkage between operator actions and switching operations for NERC TOP compliance.']
  voltage_control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the voltage control action when entry_type is voltage_control_action. Identifies the specific control mechanism employed.']

  Note: 'Chronological record of all system operator actions, EMS alarms, voltage control actions, and observations recorded during grid operations. Operator log attributes include entry timestamp, operator ID, shift reference, entry type (action taken, alarm, voltage control action, verbal communication, abnormal condition noted, switching order issued), free-text narrative, associated equipment or event reference, and priority level. Alarm attributes (entry_type=alarm) include alarm type (limit violation, equipment status change, communication failure, protection relay operation), alarm priority (critical/major/minor), associated SCADA point or EMS node, alarm message text, acknowledgment timestamp, and clearance timestamp. Voltage control attributes (entry_type=voltage_control) include control type (capacitor bank switching, reactor switching, transformer tap change, generator VAR dispatch), controlled device, pre/post-action voltage (kV), MVAR change, and initiating cause (automatic/operator). SSOT for the unified operational event log required by NERC TOP-001, NERC FAC-001 voltage profile compliance, alarm management performance tracking, post-event investigations, and regulatory audits.'
}

Table grid.ems_alarm {
  ems_alarm_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the EMS alarm record. Primary key for the alarm transaction.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: EMS alarms indicating limit violations or equipment failures can be audit findings under NERC TOP or FAC standards. Auditors review alarm response times and operator actions. This link enables finding']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control area or balancing authority responsible for the grid segment where the alarm originated. Supports multi-area alarm management and NERC reporting.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation where the alarm condition originated, enabling geographic and asset-based alarm analysis.']
  emergency_stock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.emergency_stock_event. Business justification: Critical EMS alarms (equipment failure, thermal overload) may trigger emergency material deployment for equipment replacement before catastrophic failure. Links alarm escalation to emergency stock mob']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or employee identifier of the operator who acknowledged the alarm. Supports accountability and audit trail for alarm handling.']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the EMS node (bus) associated with the alarm, if the alarm pertains to a specific electrical node in the power system model.']
  ems_scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SCADA point that triggered the alarm, if the alarm is associated with a specific telemetry measurement or control point.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: EMS alarms (voltage violations, equipment overloads) may trigger or result from outages. Linking enables alarm correlation, root cause analysis, and coordinated response between transmission and distr']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: EMS alarms often indicate asset-level issues (overheating transformer, breaker failure, capacitor bank malfunction). Linking alarms to asset records enables automated work order creation, asset health']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.scada_point.scada_point_id — EMS alarms are triggered by SCADA point limit violations or status changes. The FK to scada_point identifies which measurement or status point generated the alarm — essential for alarm management and ']
  acknowledged_by_operator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the operator who acknowledged the alarm. Provides human-readable audit trail for alarm management.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alarm was acknowledged by an operator. Null if the alarm has not yet been acknowledged. Used to track operator response time and alarm management performance.']
  alarm_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the alarm for filtering and reporting purposes. Groups alarms into operational, equipment, communication, protection, system, and security categories.']
  alarm_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration in seconds from alarm generation to clearance. Calculated field used for alarm management performance analysis and NERC TOP situational awareness metrics.']
  alarm_message varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the alarm condition generated by the EMS system. Provides context and specifics about the abnormal condition for operator situational awareness.']
  alarm_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the alarm, typically a sequential or formatted alarm number assigned by the EMS system for operator reference and tracking.']
  alarm_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the functional source or subsystem within the EMS that generated the alarm: SCADA telemetry, state estimator, contingency analysis, AGC, protection system, or manual operator entry.']
  alarm_state varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the alarm. Active indicates the condition persists and has not been acknowledged. Acknowledged means an operator has reviewed it. Cleared indicates the condition has resolved. Suppressed and shelved indicate operator-managed alarm filtering.']
  alarm_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the alarm condition was detected and generated by the SCADA/EMS system. This is the principal business event timestamp for the alarm.']
  alarm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alarm based on the nature of the abnormal condition detected. Includes limit violations, equipment status changes, communication failures, protection relay operations, state estimation failures, and topology changes.']
  balancing_authority varchar [note: 'Name or code of the balancing authority responsible for the area where the alarm occurred. Used for coordination and regulatory reporting.']
  clearance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alarm condition was resolved and the alarm was cleared. Null if the alarm is still active. Used to calculate alarm duration and system performance metrics.']
  contingency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alarm is related to a contingency analysis violation or post-contingency condition. True if the alarm resulted from contingency analysis.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action taken to resolve the alarm condition. Used for operational documentation and continuous improvement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this alarm record was first inserted into the data system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system that provided this alarm record, such as the specific EMS vendor system or SCADA master. Supports multi-system data integration and lineage tracking.']
  disturbance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alarm is associated with a system disturbance event requiring NERC reporting. True if the alarm is part of a reportable disturbance.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the EMS system that generated the alarm. Supports multi-EMS environments and system-specific alarm analysis.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the limit value and measured value, such as MW, MVAR, kV, Amps, Hz, or degrees. Essential for interpreting alarm magnitude.']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the specific equipment associated with the alarm, as known to operators and maintenance personnel.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment associated with the alarm condition, such as transformer, breaker, line, generator, capacitor bank, or relay. Supports equipment-specific alarm analysis.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this alarm record was last modified. Tracks changes to alarm state, acknowledgment, clearance, or operator notes.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'For limit violation alarms, specifies the type of limit that was exceeded: high, low, high-high (critical high), low-low (critical low), rate of change, or deadband violation.']
  limit_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The threshold value that was exceeded to trigger the alarm. Provides context for the severity of the limit violation.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual measured value at the time the alarm was triggered. Allows operators to assess the magnitude of the deviation from normal or limit conditions.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity code (e.g., WECC, ERCOT, NPCC, SERC, MRO, RFC, SPP, TRE) for the geographic region where the alarm originated. Required for NERC compliance reporting.']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alarm condition meets NERC criteria for mandatory event reporting under EOP-004 or other reliability standards. True if reportable to NERC.']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by operators regarding the alarm, including actions taken, root cause observations, or coordination with other entities. Supports knowledge capture and post-event review.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name associated with the alarm, enabling integration with PI data for post-event analysis and trending.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the alarm indicating the urgency of operator response required. Critical alarms require immediate action, major alarms require prompt attention, minor alarms are monitored, and informational alarms are logged for awareness.']
  related_alarm_count varchar [note: 'Number of related or cascading alarms that occurred within a defined time window of this alarm. Helps identify alarm floods and system-wide events.']
  response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Time in seconds from alarm generation to operator acknowledgment. Key performance indicator for alarm management and operator situational awareness.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the alarm condition, assigned during post-event analysis. Supports trending and reliability improvement initiatives.']
  rtu_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the RTU that reported the alarm condition, if applicable. Links alarm to field communication infrastructure.']
  suppression_rule varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the alarm suppression rule applied to this alarm, if any. Supports intelligent alarm management and reduction of alarm floods.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts of the equipment or node associated with the alarm. Critical for prioritizing alarms based on system criticality.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of EMS alarms generated by the SCADA/EMS system for abnormal conditions on the grid. Captures alarm timestamp, alarm type (limit violation, equipment status change, communication failure, protection relay operation), priority level (critical/major/minor), associated SCADA point or EMS node, alarm message text, operator acknowledgment timestamp, operator ID, and alarm clearance timestamp. Supports NERC TOP situational awareness requirements and alarm management performance tracking.'
}

Table grid.grid_reliability_event {
  grid_reliability_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the grid reliability event record. Primary key for the grid reliability event entity.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: NERC and regional entities audit reliability events (especially EOP-004 reportable events) to verify utility response and compliance. This link tracks which events were examined during compliance audi']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control area or balancing authority where the reliability event occurred.']
  emergency_stock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.emergency_stock_event. Business justification: NERC-reportable reliability events (OE-417 reports) trigger emergency procurement and mutual aid material requests. Essential for tracking restoration material costs, FEMA reimbursement documentation,']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: Grid reliability events (NERC reportable) often involve customer outages. Linking enables consolidated DOE OE-417 reporting, reliability metrics calculation, and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: NERC reliability events (OE-417 reports) require documented operator accountability. \'operator_on_duty\' text field should be FK to employee for regulatory audit trails and operator performance analysi']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.safety_incident. Business justification: NERC reliability events can involve personnel safety incidents. Linking enables correlation of NERC OE-417 reports with OSHA recordables for comprehensive event investigation and regulatory reporting.']
  affected_facilities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or free-text description of generation plants, substations, transmission lines, and other facilities impacted by the reliability event.']
  corrective_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Detailed description of immediate corrective actions and operator interventions taken to mitigate and resolve the reliability event. Includes switching orders, generation redispatch, and emergency procedures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reliability event record was first created in the system, in UTC. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer accounts impacted by the reliability event, if the event resulted in customer outages. Used for regulatory reporting and impact assessment.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application that created or last updated this reliability event record. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the EMS system that detected and recorded the reliability event. Used for data lineage and system integration.']
  event_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the reliability event from start to resolution, measured in minutes. Calculated from event start and end timestamps.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reliability event was resolved or system returned to normal operating conditions, in UTC. Null if event is still ongoing.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reliability event was first detected or initiated, in UTC. This is the principal business event timestamp for the reliability event.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reliability event in the investigation and resolution workflow.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reliability event according to NERC reporting categories. Determines which NERC standards and reporting requirements apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disturbance|emergency|unusual_occurrence|frequency_excursion|voltage_excursion|load_shed|generation_loss|transmission_loss|islanding — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  frequency_excursion_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of the frequency excursion from initial deviation to recovery within acceptable limits, measured in seconds. Used for NERC BAL-003 compliance assessment.']
  frequency_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event involved a system frequency excursion outside normal operating limits. True if frequency deviated beyond acceptable thresholds per NERC BAL-003.']
  frequency_excursion_trigger varchar [note: 'Specific triggering cause of the frequency excursion event. Categorizes the type of disturbance that caused frequency deviation.']
  initiating_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause or triggering event that initiated the reliability event. Free-text description of the primary causal factor (e.g., generator trip, transmission line fault, equipment failure, weather event).']
  initiating_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the root cause category for analytics and trend analysis. Aligns with NERC cause code taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|weather|human_error|cyber_security|physical_security|fuel_supply|load_forecast_error|unknown|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  interconnection_name varchar [note: 'North American interconnection in which the reliability event occurred. Determines applicable reliability standards and coordination requirements.']
  investigation_complete_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the root cause investigation and analysis of the reliability event has been completed. True when final investigation report is approved.']
  investigation_report_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the detailed investigation report document for this reliability event. Used for audit trail and knowledge repository access.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reliability event record was most recently modified, in UTC. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Summary of key lessons learned from the reliability event investigation. Used for knowledge management and continuous improvement of grid operations.']
  mw_impact decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total megawatt impact of the reliability event, representing generation loss, load shed, or transmission capacity reduction. Positive value indicates magnitude of impact.']
  nadir_frequency_hz decimal(6,3) [note: 'Lowest system frequency reached during a frequency excursion event, measured in Hertz. Applicable only when event_type is frequency_excursion. Critical metric for NERC BAL-003 frequency response obligation.']
  nerc_oe417_report_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the DOE OE-417 Electric Emergency Incident and Disturbance Report filed for this event, if applicable. Used to track regulatory submission.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity with jurisdiction over the control area where the event occurred. Determines regional compliance and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|TRE|SPP — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event meets NERC criteria for mandatory reporting to regulatory authorities. True if event must be reported per NERC EOP-004.']
  nerc_standard_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of applicable NERC reliability standards triggered by this event (e.g., EOP-004, BAL-003, TOP-001). Determines regulatory reporting obligations.']
  nerc_submission_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory report submission to NERC and other governing bodies. Tracks compliance with mandatory reporting timelines.']
  nerc_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event report was submitted to NERC or other regulatory authorities, in UTC. Used to verify compliance with reporting deadlines.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes and comments about the reliability event. Used for supplementary information not captured in structured fields.']
  pi_historian_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'Tag prefix in the OSIsoft PI historian system for retrieving detailed telemetry data associated with this reliability event. Enables post-event analysis and forensics.']
  preventive_actions_recommended varchar [note: 'Recommended preventive measures and long-term corrective actions to prevent recurrence of similar reliability events. Used for continuous improvement and reliability planning.']
  recovery_frequency_hz decimal(6,3) [note: 'System frequency after initial recovery and stabilization following a frequency excursion, measured in Hertz. Used to assess frequency response adequacy.']
  restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all affected facilities and customer load were fully restored to normal service following the reliability event, in UTC.']
  rocof_hz_per_second decimal(8,4) [note: 'Maximum rate of change of frequency during the excursion event, measured in Hertz per second. Critical parameter for assessing system inertia and frequency response performance.']
  rto_iso_name varchar [note: 'Name of the RTO or ISO responsible for grid operations in the affected area, if applicable. Used for coordination and reporting.']
  scada_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the SCADA system snapshot captured at the time of event detection, in UTC. Used to correlate event data with real-time telemetry.']
  severity_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated severity score for the reliability event based on MW impact, duration, frequency deviation, and customer impact. Used for prioritization and trend analysis.']
  ufls_activation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic under-frequency load shedding relays were activated during the event. True if UFLS protection operated to arrest frequency decline.']
  ufls_load_shed_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount of load automatically shed by UFLS relays during the frequency excursion, measured in megawatts. Null if UFLS was not activated.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at the time of the reliability event, if weather was a contributing factor. Includes temperature, wind, precipitation, and severe weather events.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of reportable reliability events as defined by NERC reliability standards (EOP, TOP, BAL), including frequency excursion events. General event attributes include event date/time, event type (disturbance, emergency, unusual occurrence, frequency excursion), NERC standard applicability, affected facilities, initiating cause, MW impact, duration, corrective actions taken, NERC OE-417 report reference, and regulatory submission status. Frequency excursion attributes (event_type=frequency_excursion) include nadir frequency (Hz), recovery frequency (Hz), ROCOF (Hz/s), excursion duration (seconds), triggering cause (generation trip, load loss, islanding), UFLS activation flag, MW of load shed, and restoration timestamp. SSOT for all NERC reliability event reporting including NERC BAL-003 frequency response obligation — distinct from outage records (owned by outage domain) which focus on customer impact.'
}

Table grid.operating_limit {
  operating_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the operating limit record. Primary key for the operating limit entity.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the balancing authority responsible for monitoring and enforcing this operating limit. Links to the control area entity for jurisdictional and operational responsibility.']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — Operating limits (thermal, voltage, stability) are defined for specific transmission elements and interfaces in the EMS network model, referenced by EMS node or branch. Required for contingency analys']
  operating_ems_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — Operating limits (now including real-time ratings) are defined for transmission elements referenced in the EMS network model. FK to ems_node anchors limits to the network topology.']
  operating_limit_for_ems_element varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — Operating limits and real-time ratings apply to specific transmission elements referenced by EMS nodes/branches. Required for contingency analysis limit checking and operator displays.']
  ambient_temperature_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient air temperature in degrees Fahrenheit used for ambient-adjusted or dynamic rating calculations. Real-time weather data input for AAR and DLR methodologies.']
  conductor_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual or calculated conductor temperature in degrees Celsius. For dynamic line ratings, this may be measured directly via fiber optic sensors or calculated from current flow and weather conditions. Maximum allowable conductor temperature determines thermal rating.']
  contingency_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this operating limit is monitored and enforced during EMS contingency analysis (N-1, N-2 studies). True means the limit is included in contingency screening and violation detection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operating limit record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and regulatory compliance.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that provided this operating limit data (e.g., EMS, DLR system, engineering study database). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and troubleshooting.']
  element_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the transmission element (e.g., line name, transformer designation, interface name) for operator displays and reporting.']
  element_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the transmission element (line, transformer, interface) to which this operating limit applies. References the network model element in the EMS system.']
  element_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transmission element type to which the operating limit applies. Determines the applicable limit categories and rating methodologies.']
  emergency_rating_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Emergency operating limit in MVA for the transmission element under post-contingency conditions. This higher rating is allowed for a limited duration (typically 15 minutes to 4 hours) following a system disturbance.']
  ems_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the EMS network model in which this operating limit is defined. Critical for change management, model synchronization, and audit trails.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the EMS system (e.g., GE ADMS, Siemens Spectrum, ABB Network Manager) that manages and enforces this operating limit. Used for multi-EMS environments and data lineage tracking.']
  from_bus_name varchar [note: 'Name of the originating bus or substation for transmission line or transformer elements. Defines the topology and network connectivity for EMS modeling.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operating limit record was last modified. Tracks changes to ratings, limits, or metadata for change management and audit purposes.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Category of operating limit being defined: normal MVA rating, emergency MVA rating, voltage upper limit, voltage lower limit, stability limit in MW, or thermal limit in MW. Determines the constraint type enforced by EMS contingency analysis.']
  max_conductor_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable conductor temperature in degrees Celsius before thermal damage, excessive sag, or loss of tensile strength occurs. Typically 75-100°C for ACSR conductors. Defines the thermal constraint for rating calculations.']
  nerc_fac_002_study_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the NERC FAC-002 facility interconnection study or FAC-008 facility ratings methodology study that established this operating limit. Provides traceability to engineering analysis and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity responsible for reliability oversight of this transmission element: WECC (Western), ERCOT (Texas), MRO (Midwest), NPCC (Northeast), RF (ReliabilityFirst), SERC (Southeast), SPP (Southwest), or TRE (Texas RE). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|SPP|TRE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  normal_rating_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'Normal continuous operating limit in MVA for the transmission element under normal system conditions. This is the baseline capacity used for day-to-day operations and pre-contingency analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special operating instructions, temporary limit adjustments, or engineering rationale for this operating limit. Used by operators and engineers for situational awareness.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the transmission element and its associated operating limits. In-service limits are actively enforced by EMS; out-of-service limits are inactive due to equipment outages.']
  operator_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the transmission operator responsible for real-time operation and monitoring of this element and its limits. May differ from owner in joint-use or RTO-operated facilities.']
  operator_display_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this operating limit is displayed on EMS operator screens and alarm systems. True means operators receive real-time visibility and alarms for limit violations.']
  owner_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the transmission asset owner responsible for the physical equipment and its ratings. Used for cost allocation, maintenance responsibility, and regulatory reporting.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name for archiving this operating limit time-series data. Enables historical trending, post-event analysis, and integration with asset performance analytics.']
  rating_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine the operating limit: static (fixed rating), seasonal (varies by season), ambient-adjusted (AAR based on weather), or dynamic (DLR with real-time conductor monitoring). Indicates the sophistication and real-time responsiveness of the rating.']
  rating_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operating limit or dynamic rating was calculated or became effective. For dynamic ratings, this represents the real-time calculation timestamp. Critical for time-series analysis and operational decision-making.']
  rto_iso_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the RTO or ISO market region in which this transmission element operates. Relevant for market-based congestion management and transmission capacity allocation.']
  season varchar [note: 'Season for which this operating limit is applicable. Seasonal ratings account for ambient temperature variations that affect conductor thermal capacity. Year-round indicates a single rating applies across all seasons.']
  solar_irradiance_w_per_m2 decimal(7,2) [note: 'Solar irradiance in watts per square meter incident on the conductor. Affects conductor heating and is a key input for dynamic line rating calculations, particularly during high solar exposure periods.']
  stability_limit_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum power transfer in MW constrained by system stability considerations (transient stability, voltage stability, or oscillatory stability). Derived from stability studies and enforced to prevent system instability following disturbances.']
  study_date date [note: 'Date when the engineering study establishing this operating limit was completed. Used for tracking study vintage and determining when re-studies are required per NERC standards.']
  to_bus_name varchar [note: 'Name of the terminating bus or substation for transmission line or transformer elements. Defines the topology and network connectivity for EMS modeling.']
  validity_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operating limit expires or is superseded by a new rating. For dynamic ratings, defines the end of the rating validity window. Null indicates an open-ended validity period.']
  validity_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operating limit becomes valid and enforceable in EMS operations. For dynamic ratings, defines the beginning of the rating validity window.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kV of the transmission element (e.g., 69 kV, 138 kV, 230 kV, 345 kV, 500 kV). Used for classification, reporting, and determining applicable standards.']
  voltage_lower_limit_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable voltage in kV for the transmission element or bus. Falling below this limit may cause voltage collapse, equipment malfunction, or customer service quality issues. Used by EMS voltage control and contingency analysis.']
  voltage_upper_limit_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable voltage in kV for the transmission element or bus. Exceeding this limit may cause equipment damage or insulation breakdown. Used by EMS voltage control and contingency analysis.']
  wind_speed_mph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Wind speed in miles per hour at the transmission element location. Critical input for dynamic line rating calculations as wind cooling significantly increases conductor thermal capacity.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record defining all operating limits for transmission elements and interfaces — static seasonal ratings, ambient-adjusted ratings (AAR), and dynamic line ratings (DLR) — within the EMS network model. Static/seasonal attributes include element reference (line, transformer, interface), limit type (normal/emergency MVA, voltage upper/lower kV, stability MW), applicable season, ambient temperature condition, and NERC FAC-002 study reference. Dynamic real-time rating attributes (grain: one row per element per rating timestamp) include ambient-adjusted or dynamic normal and emergency MVA, ambient temperature (°F), wind speed (mph), solar irradiance (W/m²), conductor temperature (°C), rating method (static/ambient-adjusted/dynamic), and validity window. SSOT for all operating limits and real-time ratings used by EMS contingency analysis, operator displays, NERC FAC-001/FAC-008 compliance, and transmission capacity optimization.'
}

Table grid.real_time_rating {
  real_time_rating_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each real-time rating record. Primary key for the real_time_rating product.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the balancing authority responsible for this transmission element. Links rating to operational control area.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the substation where this transmission element originates or terminates. Links rating to physical grid location.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the system operator who reviewed or overrode this rating. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Dynamic line ratings are calculated for specific transmission line and transformer assets. Asset management uses these ratings for thermal aging models, risk-based replacement prioritization, and capi']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SCADA measurement point providing real-time loading or temperature data for this element.']
