  ambient_temperature_f decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured or forecasted ambient air temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at the element location. Primary environmental input for thermal rating calculations.']
  circuit_reference varchar [note: 'Circuit identifier distinguishing parallel transmission paths between the same bus pair. Used in switching orders and outage coordination.']
  conductor_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured or calculated conductor surface temperature in degrees Celsius. Must not exceed maximum design temperature to prevent annealing and sag violations.']
  contingency_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rating is actively used in real-time contingency analysis. True for critical elements monitored for N-1 and N-2 contingencies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rating record was first inserted into the data platform. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application that generated this rating record (e.g., DLR system, EMS rating module, weather integration service).']
  element_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the transmission element as known in operational systems and switching orders.']
  element_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the transmission element (line, transformer, cable) being rated. References the asset or network element in the EMS model.']
  element_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission equipment being rated. Determines applicable rating methodology and thermal limits.']
  emergency_rating_mva decimal(12,3) [note: 'Real-time emergency thermal rating in megavolt-amperes. Maximum load the element can carry for a limited duration (typically 15 minutes to 4 hours) during contingency conditions.']
  ems_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the EMS or SCADA system that received and is using this real-time rating for state estimation and contingency analysis.']
  from_bus_name varchar [note: 'Name of the originating bus or node in the EMS network model. Defines one end of a transmission line or branch element.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rating record was last modified. Tracks changes to ratings due to recalculation or operator override.']
  load_shed_rating_mva decimal(12,3) [note: 'Short-term emergency rating used during system restoration or load shedding events. Highest permissible loading for very brief durations to prevent cascading outages.']
  max_conductor_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable conductor temperature in degrees Celsius per design specifications. Exceeding this limit risks permanent conductor damage and clearance violations.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity with jurisdiction over this transmission element. Determines applicable reliability standards and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|TRE|SPP — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  normal_rating_mva decimal(12,3) [note: 'Real-time normal (continuous) thermal rating in megavolt-amperes. Maximum load the element can carry indefinitely under current ambient conditions without exceeding design limits.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or operational notes related to this rating. Used for documentation and knowledge transfer.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the transmission element. Out-of-service elements do not require active rating updates.']
  operator_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a system operator manually overrode the calculated rating. True when operator judgment supersedes automated calculation.']
  operator_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the operator for manually overriding the calculated rating. Required for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'PI historian tag name where this rating value is archived for historical analysis, trending, and post-event review.']
  rating_calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rating calculation algorithm executed. May differ from rating_timestamp if calculation is performed ahead of validity period.']
  rating_confidence_level varchar [note: 'Overall confidence in the calculated rating based on data quality, sensor availability, and model accuracy. Low confidence may trigger operator alerts.']
  rating_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate the real-time rating. Static uses fixed seasonal values; ambient-adjusted incorporates weather; dynamic line rating (DLR) uses real-time sensor data.']
  rating_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this real-time rating was calculated and became effective. Represents the moment the rating applies to grid operations.']
  rating_validity_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rating expires and must be replaced by a new rating calculation. Ensures stale ratings are not used in operations.']
  rating_validity_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rating becomes valid and can be used by EMS for operational decisions and contingency analysis.']
  rto_iso_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the RTO or ISO market operator if this element participates in organized wholesale markets.']
  sag_clearance_ft decimal(8,3) [note: 'Calculated vertical clearance in feet between conductor at current temperature and ground or obstacles. Must maintain minimum clearances per NESC standards.']
  solar_irradiance_w_per_m2 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Solar radiation intensity in watts per square meter. Solar heating reduces available thermal capacity, especially for overhead conductors.']
  to_bus_name varchar [note: 'Name of the terminating bus or node in the EMS network model. Defines the other end of a transmission line or branch element.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage level of the transmission element in kilovolts. Critical for determining thermal and electrical limits.']
  weather_data_quality_code varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for weather inputs used in rating calculation. Poor quality data may require conservative rating adjustments or operator review.']
  weather_data_source varchar [note: 'Source of weather data used in rating calculation. Real-time sensors provide highest accuracy; forecasts or seasonal defaults are fallback options.']
  wind_direction_degrees decimal(5,2) [note: 'Wind direction in degrees (0-360) relative to true north. Wind angle relative to conductor affects cooling efficiency.']
  wind_speed_mph decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured or forecasted wind speed in miles per hour at conductor height. Wind cooling significantly increases thermal capacity of overhead lines.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of dynamic or ambient-adjusted real-time thermal ratings (AAR/DLR) for transmission lines and transformers, replacing static seasonal ratings in the EMS. Captures element reference, rating timestamp, real-time normal rating (MVA), real-time emergency rating (MVA), ambient temperature (°F), wind speed (mph), solar irradiance (W/m²), conductor temperature (°C), rating method (static/ambient-adjusted/dynamic), and validity window. Supports NERC FAC-001 compliance and transmission capacity optimization.'
}

Table grid.topology_snapshot {
  topology_snapshot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for topology_snapshot']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control area or balancing authority whose network topology is captured in this snapshot.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the control center operator who acknowledged or reviewed this topology snapshot.']
  state_estimation_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the state estimation run that triggered or is associated with this topology snapshot. Links the topological state to the electrical state analysis.']
  closed_switching_device_count varchar [note: 'Total number of breakers, switches, and other switching devices in the closed position at the time of this snapshot. Closed devices maintain network connectivity.']
  convergence_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the topology processor successfully converged to a valid network connectivity solution for this snapshot.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this topology snapshot record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0-100) representing the overall quality and completeness of the SCADA data used to generate this topology snapshot. Higher scores indicate better data quality.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source EMS or SCADA system that generated this topology snapshot (e.g., GE ADMS, ABB EMS, Siemens Spectrum).']
  de_energized_bus_count varchar [note: 'Total number of buses (nodes) that are de-energized or isolated from power sources in this topology snapshot.']
  de_energized_line_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transmission and distribution lines that are de-energized or out of service in this topology snapshot.']
  de_energized_transformer_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transformers that are de-energized or out of service in this topology snapshot.']
  disturbance_event_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator showing whether this topology snapshot was captured during or immediately following a grid disturbance event. Used for post-event reconstruction and NERC reporting.']
  ems_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the EMS network model used for this topology snapshot. Critical for ensuring consistency in post-event analysis and model validation.']
  energized_bus_count varchar [note: 'Total number of buses (nodes) that are energized and connected to an active power source in this topology snapshot.']
  energized_line_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transmission and distribution lines that are energized and in service in this topology snapshot.']
  energized_transformer_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transformers that are energized and in service in this topology snapshot.']
  execution_duration_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total time in seconds required to execute the topology processor and generate this snapshot. Used for performance monitoring and optimization.']
  interconnection_name varchar [note: 'Name of the North American electrical interconnection to which this topology snapshot applies (Eastern, Western, Texas, or Quebec).']
  island_count varchar [note: 'Total number of electrically isolated network islands detected in this topology snapshot. A single interconnected grid has island_count = 1; multiple islands indicate network separation.']
  iteration_count varchar [note: 'Number of iterations required by the topology processor to reach convergence or termination for this snapshot.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this topology snapshot record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  nerc_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator showing whether this topology snapshot meets NERC TOP-001 and TOP-002 requirements for transmission operations and real-time monitoring.']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'NERC regional entity code for the geographic region covered by this topology snapshot (e.g., WECC, SERC, MRO, NPCC, RF, TRE).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator comments, special conditions, or additional context related to this topology snapshot.']
  open_switching_device_count varchar [note: 'Total number of breakers, switches, and other switching devices in the open position at the time of this snapshot. Open devices create network separation.']
  operator_acknowledged boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator showing whether an operator has reviewed and acknowledged this topology snapshot.']
  operator_acknowledgment_required boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator showing whether this topology snapshot requires operator review and acknowledgment due to unexpected topology changes or alarm conditions.']
  operator_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when an operator acknowledged this topology snapshot, if applicable.']
  pi_historian_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag prefix used to archive this topology snapshot data for long-term storage and post-event analysis.']
  rto_iso_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the RTO or ISO responsible for the control area covered by this topology snapshot (e.g., PJM, CAISO, ERCOT, MISO, SPP, NYISO, ISO-NE).']
  scada_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the SCADA data snapshot used as input for this topology processor run. May differ slightly from snapshot_timestamp due to processing latency.']
  snapshot_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the topology snapshot indicating its validity and usability for analysis and compliance reporting.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the topology snapshot was captured by the EMS topology processor. Represents the real-world moment of the network connectivity state.']
  switching_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the switching order or operational instruction that caused the topology change captured in this snapshot, if applicable.']
  topology_change_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator showing whether the network topology changed compared to the previous snapshot. True indicates a switching operation or connectivity change occurred.']
  topology_processor_algorithm varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the topology processing algorithm or method used to generate this snapshot (e.g., depth-first search, breadth-first search, matrix-based).']
  topology_processor_run_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference identifier or sequence number assigned by the EMS topology processor for this specific run. Used for traceability and audit purposes.']
  validation_error_count varchar [note: 'Number of validation errors or inconsistencies detected in this topology snapshot during quality checks.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the validation checks performed on this topology snapshot to ensure consistency with physical network constraints and operational rules.']

  Note: 'Periodic snapshot of the EMS network topology model capturing the real-time connectivity state of the grid — which breakers and switches are open or closed, resulting in the active network islands and energized/de-energized sections. Captures snapshot timestamp, topology processor run reference, number of islands, number of energized buses, number of open switching devices, and topology change flag. Used for post-event network reconstruction, NERC TOP compliance, and EMS model validation. Complements state_estimation_run by capturing the topological (connectivity) state separately from the electrical (power flow) state.'
}

Table grid.protection_event {
  protection_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the protection system event record. Primary key for the protection event entity.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Protection system misoperations are auditable findings under NERC PRC-004. When auditors identify a misoperation during compliance reviews, the finding must link to the specific protection event for e']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the balancing authority control area responsible for the electrical system where the event occurred.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation where the protection event occurred. Links to the physical location of the protection system.']
  emergency_stock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.emergency_stock_event. Business justification: Protection relay trips causing equipment damage (fault current, breaker failure) trigger emergency material deployment for restoration. Links protection event to emergency procurement, mutual aid requ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control room operator who acknowledged or responded to the protection event.']
  ems_alarm_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the EMS alarm record generated by this protection event, linking to operator notification and acknowledgment workflow.']
  ems_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.ems_node.ems_node_id — Protection system operations occur at specific locations in the network model. Linking to EMS node enables correlation with state estimation results and contingency analysis for post-fault review.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: Protection relay operations cause customer outages. Essential for root cause analysis, misoperation investigation, NERC PRC-004 compliance reporting, and correlating transmission events with distribut']
  initiating_protection_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on protection_event (initiating_protection_event_id)']
  protection_device_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the protection device (relay, recloser, breaker) that detected and initiated the protection operation.']
  protection_scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SCADA telemetry point that captured the protection event data from the relay or RTU.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Protection relay operations are tied to specific relay assets. NERC PRC-004 misoperation investigations require linking events to asset maintenance records, inspection history, and firmware versions. ']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.safety_incident. Business justification: Protection events (relay trips, breaker failures) can cause or result from safety incidents. Linking enables equipment failure vs. human error root cause analysis for NERC PRC compliance.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'FK to grid.scada_point.scada_point_id — Protection relay operations are detected via SCADA status points. Linking protection events to their associated SCADA points enables correlation with telemetry data for fault analysis.']
  balancing_authority_code varchar [note: 'NERC-registered balancing authority code responsible for the area where the protection event occurred.']
  breaker_operation_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the circuit breaker operation command issued by the protection relay, indicating whether the breaker successfully interrupted the fault.']
  clearing_time_cycles decimal(8,3) [note: 'Time elapsed from fault initiation to circuit breaker interruption, measured in power system cycles (60 Hz = 16.67 ms per cycle). Critical for coordination studies.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions taken or planned to prevent recurrence of similar protection events.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective actions (relay setting changes, equipment repairs, vegetation clearance) are required based on investigation findings.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this protection event record was first created in the data system.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customer accounts that experienced a service interruption as a result of this protection event.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that provided the protection event data (e.g., GE PowerOn ADMS, SEL relay, ABB MicroSCADA, OSIsoft PI).']
  disturbance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this protection event was part of a larger system disturbance involving multiple facilities or widespread impact.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the protection system operation was initiated, typically synchronized to GPS time for accurate sequence-of-events analysis.']
  fault_current_magnitude_amps decimal(12,2) [note: 'Peak magnitude of the fault current measured by the protection relay at the time of the event, expressed in amperes.']
  fault_location_miles decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated distance from the relay terminal to the fault location, measured in miles along the protected line or feeder.']
  fault_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the electrical fault that triggered the protection operation, indicating the phases involved and whether ground was involved. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_line_to_ground|line_to_line|double_line_to_ground|three_phase|phase_to_phase|phase_a_to_ground|phase_b_to_ground|phase_c_to_ground|phase_ab|phase_bc|phase_ca — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  initiating_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause or trigger for the protection operation (e.g., lightning strike, equipment failure, vegetation contact, animal contact, insulator flashover, human error).']
  initiating_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause for analytics and trending purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|equipment_failure|vegetation|animal|human_error|unknown|planned_operation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  investigation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this protection event requires formal engineering investigation per utility protection system review procedures or NERC PRC-004 requirements.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the protection event investigation workflow, tracking progress from initiation to closure.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this protection event record was most recently modified, tracking investigation updates and status changes.']
  load_interrupted_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of electrical load that was interrupted by the protection operation, measured in megawatts (MW).']
  misoperation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the protection operation has been classified as a misoperation per NERC PRC-004 criteria (failure to operate, slow operation, or unnecessary operation).']
  misoperation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the misoperation if the misoperation flag is true, per NERC PRC-004 definitions.']
  nerc_prc_standard_applicability varchar [note: 'Identifies which NERC Protection and Control (PRC) reliability standards apply to this event (e.g., PRC-004, PRC-005, PRC-023, PRC-025).']
  nerc_region varchar [note: 'North American Electric Reliability Corporation regional entity with jurisdiction over the location of the event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WECC|ERCOT|MRO|NPCC|RF|SERC|TRE|SPP — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this protection event meets NERC criteria for mandatory reporting to the Electric Reliability Organization (ERO).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, operator observations, or engineering comments related to the protection event.']
  operator_acknowledged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a control room operator has reviewed and acknowledged the protection event in the EMS or SCADA system.']
  operator_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the control room operator acknowledged the protection event alarm.']
  outage_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the customer outage from protection event initiation to full service restoration, measured in minutes.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI historian tag name used to archive time-series data associated with this protection event for long-term analytics and post-event review.']
  protected_element_name varchar [note: 'Business name or identifier of the specific electrical equipment that was protected (e.g., line name, feeder ID, transformer designation).']
  protected_element_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the electrical equipment protected by the relay that operated (transmission line, distribution feeder, transformer, generator, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transmission_line|distribution_feeder|transformer|generator|bus|capacitor_bank|reactor|cable — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reclose_attempt_status varchar [note: 'Status of the automatic reclosing sequence following the initial trip, indicating whether the line was successfully re-energized or locked out.']
  reclose_shot_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the reclosing attempt (1 for first shot, 2 for second shot, etc.). Null if no reclose was attempted.']
  relay_function_code varchar [note: 'IEEE device function number identifying the specific protection function that operated (e.g., 21 for distance, 50 for instantaneous overcurrent, 51 for time overcurrent, 87 for differential).']
  relay_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the protection relay that operated, indicating the protection principle used (distance, overcurrent, differential, pilot scheme, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: distance|overcurrent|differential|pilot|ground_fault|directional|voltage|frequency|transformer|generator|motor|bus|breaker_failure — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when electrical service was fully restored following the protection event. Null if restoration is still in progress.']
  trip_zone varchar [note: 'The protection zone or stage that initiated the trip (e.g., Zone 1, Zone 2, Zone 3 for distance relays; instantaneous, time-delayed for overcurrent relays).']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage level of the protected equipment where the event occurred, expressed in kilovolts (kV).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of protection system operations (relay trips, recloser operations, breaker failure initiations) detected on the transmission and distribution network. Captures event timestamp, protection device reference, relay type (distance, overcurrent, differential, pilot), trip zone, fault type (single-line-to-ground, phase-to-phase, three-phase), fault current magnitude (amps), fault location (miles from terminal), clearing time (cycles), reclose attempt status, and associated NERC PRC standard applicability. SSOT for protection system event records used in NERC PRC-004 relay misoperation analysis, post-fault review, and protection coordination studies.'
}

Table grid.contingency_element {
  contingency_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this contingency-asset association record. Primary key for the contingency_element association.']
  contingency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the contingency case definition in the EMS contingency analysis catalog']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical asset (transmission line, generator, transformer) that is removed or affected in this contingency scenario']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether operator intervention or remedial action scheme (RAS) activation is required for this asset in this contingency scenario to maintain system reliability.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the grid operations engineer or system administrator who created this contingency element association. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contingency-asset association was first created in the EMS contingency catalog. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  criticality_rank varchar [note: 'Numerical ranking (1 = most critical) of this asset\'s importance within this specific contingency scenario based on its impact to system stability, voltage profile, and thermal limits. Rank is contingency-specific, not a global asset criticality score.']
  element_removal_order varchar [note: 'Classification of the removal order for this element in the contingency: PRIMARY (first element removed in N-1), SECONDARY (second element in N-2), TERTIARY (third element in N-3), or SIMULTANEOUS (removed at same time as other elements). Used for NERC category classification.']
  element_role_in_contingency varchar [note: 'Role of this asset in the contingency analysis: OUTAGED_ELEMENT (asset is removed), MONITORED_ELEMENT (asset is monitored for violations but not removed), AFFECTED_ELEMENT (asset experiences loading or voltage changes due to outage of other elements).']
  element_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order in which this asset is removed in the contingency scenario. Critical for N-2 and cascading outage analysis where removal order affects system response. Value 1 indicates primary element, 2 indicates secondary element in multi-element contingencies.']
  last_analysis_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent EMS contingency analysis execution that evaluated this specific asset in this contingency scenario. Used for data freshness validation and audit trail.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who most recently modified this contingency element association. Used for accountability and audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this contingency element record (sequence change, status update, criticality re-ranking). Used for change tracking and data lineage.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this asset\'s participation in this contingency definition: ACTIVE (included in analysis), INACTIVE (temporarily excluded), EXCLUDED (permanently removed from scenario), UNDER_REVIEW (pending engineering validation).']
  post_contingency_loading_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Loading level of this specific asset (if not removed) or affected neighboring assets as a percentage of rated capacity after the contingency event. Used to identify thermal overload violations and system stress points.']
  pre_contingency_loading_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Loading level of this specific asset as a percentage of its rated capacity immediately before the contingency event, based on the base case state estimation. Used to assess whether the asset was already stressed before removal.']
  violation_detected_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this specific asset experienced a thermal overload or voltage violation in the most recent contingency analysis run. Used for targeted corrective action planning.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation of specific transmission and generation assets in NERC-mandated contingency analysis scenarios. It captures the element-specific attributes that exist only in the context of a contingency case definition, including removal sequence, pre/post-contingency loading conditions, and criticality ranking. Each record links one contingency scenario to one physical asset that is removed or affected in that scenario, supporting NERC TPL compliance evidence and EMS contingency analysis execution.. Existence Justification: In utility grid operations, contingency analysis scenarios (N-1, N-2) are defined by specifying which physical assets (transmission lines, generators, transformers) are removed from service and in what sequence. A single contingency case removes multiple assets (e.g., N-2 removes two elements), and each physical asset participates in multiple contingency definitions across different scenarios. Grid operators actively manage these contingency-asset associations as part of NERC TPL compliance, updating element sequences, criticality rankings, and monitoring thresholds based on seasonal conditions and system topology changes.'
}

Table grid.control_area_obligation {
  control_area_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this control area-obligation applicability and compliance tracking record. Primary key for the association.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the balancing authority control area subject to this compliance obligation']
  mitigation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mitigation plan record if non-compliance or partial compliance has been identified for this control area-obligation pair and a formal mitigation plan is in place.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory compliance obligation that applies to this control area']
  applicability_determination varchar [note: 'Determination of whether this specific obligation applies to this specific control area, based on operational scope, asset type, and regulatory jurisdiction analysis. Values: applicable, not-applicable, conditionally-applicable, under-review.']
  applicability_rationale varchar [note: 'Business justification and regulatory analysis explaining why this obligation does or does not apply to this control area, including references to scope criteria, asset characteristics, and jurisdictional factors.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assess compliance with this obligation for this control area. Values: automated, manual, hybrid, third-party-audit.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this specific control area with respect to this specific obligation. Values: compliant, non-compliant, partial-compliance, not-assessed, not-applicable.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this control area-obligation applicability record was created in the compliance management system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this obligation became applicable to this control area, which may differ from the obligations general effective date based on control area commissioning or operational changes.']
  evidence_location varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location, document management system path, or evidence repository where compliance evidence for this control area-obligation pair is maintained for audit defense.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this obligation ceased or will cease to apply to this control area, due to control area decommissioning, regulatory changes, or scope modifications. Null if still applicable.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance assessment or audit for this control area-obligation pair.']
  last_audit_finding varchar [note: 'Summary of the most recent audit finding or assessment result for this control area-obligation pair, including any deficiencies identified or corrective actions required.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this control area-obligation compliance record was last updated.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date by which compliance evidence or reporting is due for this specific control area-obligation combination.']
  responsible_person varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the individual responsible for ensuring compliance with this obligation for this specific control area.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the applicability and compliance tracking relationship between a balancing authority control area and a specific regulatory obligation. It captures which NERC reliability standards, FERC requirements, and other regulatory obligations apply to each control area, along with the compliance status, assessment history, and evidence location for that specific control area-obligation pair. Each record links one control area to one obligation with attributes that exist only in the context of this applicability determination and compliance tracking relationship.. Existence Justification: In energy utility operations, each balancing authority control area must comply with multiple NERC reliability standards (BAL-001 through BAL-006 for balancing, TOP standards for transmission operations, IRO standards for reliability coordination, MOD standards for modeling), FERC regulations, and state PUC requirements. Conversely, each regulatory obligation applies to multiple control areas across the utility\'s operational footprint. The business actively manages this relationship through compliance tracking workflows, audit preparation, evidence collection, and regulatory reporting processes.'
}

Table grid.contingency_monitored_element {
  contingency_monitored_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this contingency-line monitoring relationship record. Primary key.']
  analysis_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific EMS contingency analysis execution run that produced this monitored element record. Links to the parent state estimation and analysis timestamp.']
  contingency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the contingency case definition under which this line is being evaluated']
  transmission_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the transmission line master record being analyzed in this contingency scenario']
  applicable_rating_mva decimal(10,2) [note: 'The thermal rating in MVA used to evaluate this line under this contingency scenario. May be normal, emergency, or load-dump rating depending on the contingency duration and severity classification.']
  contingency_severity_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numerical severity index quantifying the impact of this contingency on this specific transmission line. Calculated based on post-contingency loading, voltage deviation, and other performance metrics. Higher values indicate more severe impacts requiring operator attention or corrective action.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether operator corrective action or remedial action scheme (RAS) activation is required to mitigate violations on this line under this contingency.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this contingency monitored element record, typically corresponding to the latest EMS contingency analysis execution.']
  limiting_element_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this transmission line is the limiting element (most severely impacted) for this contingency scenario. The limiting element determines the severity classification of the contingency.']
  monitored_element_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this transmission line is designated as a monitored element in this specific contingency scenario. Monitored elements are tracked for violations during contingency analysis execution.']
  operator_acknowledged_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the system operator has acknowledged the violation or alarm associated with this contingency-line combination in the EMS interface.']
  post_contingency_flow_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Active power flow in megawatts (MW) on this transmission line immediately following the contingency event, calculated by the EMS contingency analysis function.']
  post_contingency_loading_percent decimal(7,2) [note: 'Calculated loading percentage of the transmission line immediately following the contingency event, expressed as a percentage of the applicable thermal rating (normal, emergency, or load-dump). Used to identify thermal overload violations.']
  pre_contingency_flow_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Active power flow in megawatts (MW) on this transmission line in the base case (pre-contingency) state, derived from the parent state estimation run.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the violation detected on this transmission line under this contingency scenario. Values include NONE (no violation), THERMAL_OVERLOAD (exceeds thermal rating), VOLTAGE_HIGH (voltage exceeds upper limit), VOLTAGE_LOW (voltage below lower limit), or STABILITY (transient or voltage stability issue).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational relationship between transmission lines and contingency scenarios in NERC-compliant security analysis. Each record captures a specific transmission line\'s behavior under a defined contingency case, including whether the line is a monitored element, a limiting element, post-contingency loading levels, violation types, and severity indices. This data is essential for real-time EMS contingency analysis, NERC TPL compliance evidence, and operator situational awareness during N-1 and N-2 security assessments.. Existence Justification: In NERC-compliant transmission operations, each contingency scenario (N-1, N-2) must be analyzed against multiple transmission lines to identify thermal overloads, voltage violations, and stability issues. Conversely, each transmission line must be evaluated under multiple contingency scenarios to determine its vulnerability and limiting conditions. The EMS contingency analysis function actively manages this many-to-many relationship, tracking which lines are monitored vs. limiting elements per contingency, post-contingency loading levels, violation types, and severity indices.'
}

Table grid.control_center {
  control_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for control_center']
  backup_control_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an alternate control center used for redundancy.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Control centers are organizational entities that belong to a specific control area; adding control_center.control_area_id creates the necessary parent relationship and eliminates the silo.']
  address varchar [note: 'Street address of the control center facility.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent audit.']
  capacity_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical power capacity the control center can manage, expressed in megawatts.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the control center is located.']
  code varchar [note: 'External business code or tag used to reference the control center in operational systems.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the control center became operational and entered service.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Primary protocol used for SCADA/EMS communications with the control center.']
  control_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the control center’s purpose, capabilities, and notable features.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the control center location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control center record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days operational data from the control center is retained in the lakehouse.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the control center was retired or taken out of service, if applicable.']
  emergency_shutdown_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates if the control center can initiate an emergency grid shutdown.']
  ems_system varchar [note: 'Energy Management System platform supporting the control center.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the control center facility was physically installed.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control center is designated as critical for grid reliability.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or security audit of the control center.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which scheduled maintenance was performed.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the control center (decimal degrees).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the control center (decimal degrees).']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Preferred time window for routine maintenance activities.']
  max_operating_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest ambient temperature at which the control center equipment is rated to operate.']
  min_operating_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest ambient temperature at which the control center equipment is rated to operate.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the control center.']
  nerc_bal_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status with NERC Balancing Authority Requirements.']
  nerc_top_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status with NERC Transmission Operator Performance standards.']
  operator varchar [note: 'Organization or business unit responsible for operating the control center.']
  power_flow_model varchar [note: 'Model type used for power flow calculations within the control center.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the primary operational contact of the control center.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the primary operational contact of the control center.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the control center is located or primarily serves.']
  scada_system varchar [note: 'SCADA platform used for telemetry collection at the control center.']
  security_classification varchar [note: 'Internal security classification of the control center data.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the control center location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control center.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'Time zone of the control center for scheduling and timestamp alignment.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the control center based on its functional role.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control center record.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(8,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage level of the network segments overseen by the control center, in kilovolts.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for control_center. Referenced by control_center_id.'
}

Table grid.dispatch_instruction {
  dispatch_instruction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for dispatch_instruction']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the dispatch instruction.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Dispatch instructions are issued within a control area context; adding dispatch_instruction.control_area_id links each instruction to its control area, providing the required inbound relationship.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the dispatch instruction record.']
  generating_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the generation asset or resource to which the instruction applies.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent update.']
  market_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (e.g., utility, balancing authority) that originated the dispatch instruction.']
  superseded_dispatch_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dispatch_instruction (superseded_dispatch_instruction_id)']
  transmission_path_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transmission corridor associated with the dispatch.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispatch instruction received formal approval.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed audit log entry for this instruction.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional notes or operator remarks.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the instruction meets applicable NERC BAL/TOP reliability standards.']
  created_location varchar [note: 'Plant or control center code where the instruction was originated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispatch instruction record was first captured in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  dispatch_price_usd_per_mwh decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price applied to the dispatched energy, expressed in US dollars per megawatt‑hour.']
  dispatch_quantity_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of electrical power, in megawatts, to be dispatched.']
  dispatch_status_detail varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form status information (e.g., \'awaiting acknowledgment\').']
  dispatch_total_cost_usd decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost of the dispatch instruction (quantity × price).']
  effective_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispatch instruction expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispatch instruction becomes operationally effective.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real‑world time when the dispatch instruction was generated or issued.']
  external_system_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the originating system (e.g., OSIsoft PI tag).']
  instruction_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the dispatch instruction by the market or operating entity.']
  instruction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the dispatch action, such as start, stop, ramp up, ramp down, or set point adjustment.']
  instruction_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the instruction for amendment tracking.']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the instruction is an emergency action (true) or routine (false).']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates if the instruction was manually overridden by an operator.']
  last_modified_location varchar [note: 'Control center or system code where the last modification occurred.']
  load_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic load zone or balancing area impacted by the instruction.']
  market_session varchar [note: 'Identifier of the market session (e.g., DA, RT) for the instruction.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'Market segment to which the dispatch instruction belongs.']
  max_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed duration for the instruction to remain active.']
  min_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum required duration for the instruction to be effective.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority level (higher value indicates higher urgency) used for instruction sequencing.']
  ramp_rate_mw_per_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed change in MW per minute for ramp‑up or ramp‑down.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the operational or market reason for the dispatch instruction.']
  revision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent revision to the instruction content.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'Planned end time for the dispatch action.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Planned start time for the dispatch action, may differ from effective start.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'System or process that generated the instruction.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the dispatch instruction.']
  target_frequency_hz decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired system frequency to be maintained by the instruction.']
  target_voltage_kv decimal(6,2) [note: 'Desired voltage level for the affected transmission zone.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the dispatch instruction record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for dispatch_instruction. Referenced by dispatch_instruction_id.'
}

Table trading.trade {
  trade_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the wholesale energy trade transaction. Primary key for the trade entity.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Specific trades are cited in audit findings for unauthorized trading, limit breaches, or confirmation failures. Audit findings reference specific trade IDs as evidence of control deficiencies, enablin']
  book_id varchar [note: 'FK to trading.trading_book']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Energy trades must be allocated to cost centers for internal P&L tracking and monthly financial close. Real business process: management accounting requires trade-level cost center assignment for vari']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'FK to trading.counterparty.counterparty_id — Every trade has a counterparty. This FK is essential for credit exposure calculation, ISDA netting, and FERC EQR reporting. Without it, credit risk management is impossible.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee or trading desk that executed the trade. Used for performance tracking and compliance monitoring.']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: Trades are priced and valued using forward LMP forecasts at delivery points. Hedging decisions, mark-to-market valuation, and trade approval workflows require linking trades to the price forecast scen']
  hedge_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.hedge_strategy. Business justification: Trades executed as part of board-approved hedging programs should reference the governing hedge_strategy to enable hedge effectiveness testing, regulatory reporting, and hedge accounting designation t']
  master_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing master trading agreement (e.g., ISDA, EEI, NAESB) under which this trade is executed. Defines legal terms and credit provisions.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Trades must comply with specific regulatory obligations (FERC EQR reporting, NERC reliability standards, market manipulation rules). Compliance officers trace trades to governing obligations for audit']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Trades must roll up to profit centers for segment reporting and executive dashboards. Real business process: quarterly earnings reporting aggregates trading P&L by profit center for investor disclosur']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Physical energy trades reference specific generation or transmission assets for delivery scheduling, settlement validation, and asset utilization tracking. Critical for reconciling financial trades wi']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Individual trades are reported in FERC EQR quarterly filings and other regulatory submissions. Regulatory teams must trace filed transactions back to source trade records for audit defense, filing ame']
  scheduling_coordinator_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Scheduling Coordinator (SC) responsible for submitting schedules to the ISO/RTO for this trade. Required for ISO/RTO market transactions.']
  to_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'FK to trading.counterparty.counterparty_id — Every trade has a counterparty. This is a fundamental master-transaction relationship required for credit exposure calculation, settlement, and FERC EQR reporting.']
  trade_broker_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the intermediary broker who facilitated the trade, if applicable. Null for direct bilateral or exchange trades.']
  trade_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the external party with whom the trade was executed. Links to the counterparty master for credit exposure and settlement tracking.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Physical commodity trades (coal, natural gas, uranium fuel) are procured from vendors. Links trading desk fuel purchases to vendor master for invoice reconciliation, payment processing, and vendor per']
  ancillary_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of ancillary service procured if the trade is for grid support services: regulation, spinning reserve, non-spinning reserve, voltage support, or black start. Null for energy-only trades. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulation|spinning_reserve|non_spinning_reserve|voltage_support|black_start|frequency_response|reactive_power — promote to reference product]']
  collateral_posted decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of collateral posted by the counterparty to secure this trade under the master agreement credit provisions.']
  commodity varchar [note: 'The energy commodity being traded: electric power, natural gas, Renewable Energy Certificate (REC), capacity, ancillary services, or Financial Transmission Right (FTR).']
  confirmation_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism used to confirm the trade with the counterparty: International Swaps and Derivatives Association (ISDA) agreement, broker confirmation, electronic platform, manual process, or exchange clearing.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the trade confirmation workflow: unconfirmed (awaiting counterparty acknowledgment), confirmed (both parties agree), disputed (discrepancy identified), or resolved (dispute settled).']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trade was confirmed by the counterparty. Null if trade remains unconfirmed.']
  counterparty_acknowledgment_received boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether formal acknowledgment of the trade has been received from the counterparty. True if received, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the trade record was first created in the ETRM system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current credit exposure to the counterparty resulting from this trade, calculated based on mark-to-market valuation and collateral posted. Used for credit risk management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the trade price and settlement amounts.']
  deal_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the trade as recorded in the Energy Trading and Risk Management (ETRM) system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and counterparty communication.']
  delivery_end_date date [note: 'The final date on which physical delivery or financial settlement of the commodity is completed.']
  delivery_point varchar [note: 'Physical or financial location where the commodity is delivered. For power: Pricing Node (PNode) or hub. For gas: pipeline interconnect or city gate.']
  delivery_start_date date [note: 'The first date on which physical delivery or financial settlement of the commodity begins.']
  discrepancy_details varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any discrepancies identified during the confirmation process, such as quantity mismatches, price differences, or delivery point errors. Null if no discrepancies exist.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the trade was executed in the trading system. Critical for Day-Ahead Market (DAM) and Real-Time Market (RTM) sequencing and audit trails.']
  ferc_reporting_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the trade for FERC Form 552 reporting purposes: short-term sales, long-term sales, purchases for resale, or other categories. Null if not subject to FERC reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: short_term_sales|long_term_sales|purchases_for_resale|transmission_services|ancillary_services|other — promote to reference product]']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal trading desk notes, strategy rationale, or special instructions related to the trade. Not shared with counterparties.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the trade record was last updated. Tracks amendments, status changes, and data corrections.']
  lmp_pricing_node varchar [note: 'The specific Pricing Node (PNode) used for Locational Marginal Price (LMP) calculation in ISO/RTO markets. Determines the settlement price for power trades.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the market in which the trade was executed: Day-Ahead Market (DAM), Real-Time Market (RTM), term contract, or spot market.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified the trade record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit price of the commodity. For power: dollars per MWh. For gas: dollars per MMBtu. Represents the agreed-upon price at trade execution.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Volume of the commodity traded. For power: Megawatt-hours (MWh). For gas: Million British Thermal Units (MMBtu). For RECs: certificate count.']
  rec_tracking_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier from the regional REC tracking system (e.g., WREGIS, M-RETS, PJM-GATS) for REC trades. Null for non-REC trades.']
  rec_vintage_year varchar [note: 'The year in which the renewable energy was generated for Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) trades. Used for Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance tracking. Null for non-REC trades.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this trade must be reported to regulatory authorities such as Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) or state Public Utility Commissions (PUCs). True if reporting required, False otherwise.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Indicates how the trade will be settled: physical delivery of the commodity, financial settlement based on market price, or cash payment.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the trade calculated as quantity multiplied by price. Represents the gross settlement amount before adjustments.']
  trade_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the trade was executed and agreed upon by both parties.']
  trade_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the trade: pending confirmation, confirmed by counterparty, amended after execution, cancelled before settlement, settled and closed, or voided due to error.']
  trade_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the utility is buying or selling the commodity in this transaction.']
  transaction_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the trade execution method: bilateral contract, exchange-traded, over-the-counter (OTC), Independent System Operator (ISO) or Regional Transmission Organization (RTO) market, or Power Purchase Agreement (PPA).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the trade quantity is expressed: Megawatt-hour (MWh), Million British Thermal Units (MMBtu), Megawatt (MW), certificates, or rights.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every wholesale energy trade executed by the utility, capturing bilateral, exchange-traded, and OTC transactions for power, gas, and RECs. Sourced from OpenLink Endur position management. Stores trade type (buy/sell), commodity, quantity in MWh/MMBtu, price, currency, trade date, execution timestamp, trader ID, book, counterparty, and trade status lifecycle (confirmed, amended, cancelled). Includes confirmation workflow tracking: confirmation method (ISDA, broker, electronic), confirmation status, discrepancy details, and counterparty acknowledgment. SSOT for all trade identity and confirmation state within the trading domain.'
}

Table trading.trade_leg {
  trade_leg_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the individual trade leg within a multi-leg wholesale energy transaction. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Complex structured trades require leg-level cost center allocation for accurate margin analysis. Real business process: monthly financial close allocates multi-leg transaction costs to different cost ']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the counterparty entity on the opposite side of this leg. Critical for credit exposure monitoring and settlement.']
  primary_trade_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent wholesale energy trade transaction that this leg belongs to. A single trade may contain multiple legs representing different delivery periods, pricing structures, or settlement mechanisms.']
  scheduling_coordinator_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the scheduling coordinator responsible for submitting energy schedules to the ISO/RTO for this leg. Required for physical delivery legs.']
  to_trade_id varchar [note: 'FK to trading.trade.trade_id — Every trade leg must reference its parent trade. This is the fundamental header→line relationship in the trading domain. Without this FK, trade legs are orphaned and cannot be aggregated to the trade ']
  ancillary_services_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg includes procurement of ancillary services such as regulation, spinning reserves, or voltage support required for grid reliability.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this trade leg was approved for execution by authorized personnel.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the supervisor or risk manager who approved this trade leg for execution.']
  booking_entity varchar [note: 'The legal entity or business unit within the utility that booked this trade leg. Used for regulatory reporting and internal profit/loss attribution.']
  collateral_requirement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of collateral required to be posted by the counterparty to secure performance on this leg, based on credit exposure and credit terms.']
  congestion_revenue_rights_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of Congestion Revenue Rights or Financial Transmission Rights associated with this leg, used to hedge transmission congestion costs.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the trader or system user who created this trade leg record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this trade leg record was first created in the Energy Trading and Risk Management (ETRM) system.']
  credit_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated credit exposure or mark-to-market value of this leg, used for counterparty credit risk management and collateral determination.']
  delivery_end_date date [note: 'The final date on which physical delivery or financial settlement obligations conclude for this leg. Used to determine the leg\'s active period.']
  delivery_point_hub varchar [note: 'The trading hub or aggregated pricing zone for delivery. Hubs represent weighted averages of multiple PNodes and are commonly used in bilateral contracts.']
  delivery_point_pnode varchar [note: 'The specific pricing node or delivery location where physical energy is delivered or financially settled. PNodes are defined by the ISO/RTO and determine locational pricing.']
  delivery_start_date date [note: 'The first date on which physical delivery or financial settlement obligations begin for this leg. Critical for scheduling and settlement calculations.']
  fixed_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The predetermined price per unit for fixed-price legs. Null for floating-price legs where price is determined by market index or formula.']
  heat_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'For tolling agreements or gas-fired generation legs, the heat rate (MMBtu per MWh) used to convert fuel costs to energy pricing. Represents generation efficiency.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this trade leg record was last updated or modified in the ETRM system.']
  leg_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this leg represents a buy, sell, receive, or pay position from the utility\'s perspective. Critical for position management and risk exposure calculation.']
  leg_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this leg within the parent trade. Used to maintain the chronological or logical order of legs in complex structured products.']
  leg_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trade leg indicating whether it is active, pending approval, executed, settled, cancelled, or expired.']
  leg_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trade leg indicating whether it represents a fixed-price obligation, floating-price exposure, physical energy delivery, financial cash settlement, option right, or swap exchange.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the market in which this leg was transacted: day-ahead market (DAM), real-time market (RTM), bilateral contract, forward market, or futures exchange.']
  notional_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The contracted volume or capacity for this leg, typically expressed in megawatt-hours (MWh) for energy transactions or megawatts (MW) for capacity products.']
  option_exercise_style varchar [note: 'Defines when the option can be exercised: American (any time before expiry), European (only at expiry), or Bermudan (specific dates). Null for non-option legs.']
  option_type varchar [note: 'For option legs, specifies whether this is a call option (right to buy), put option (right to sell), swaption (option on a swap), or collar (combination). Null for non-option legs.']
  premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The upfront payment made for option legs. Represents the cost of acquiring the option right. Null for non-option legs.']
  price_formula varchar [note: 'Mathematical formula or index reference used to calculate floating prices. May reference LMP (Locational Marginal Price) nodes, market indices, or custom calculation logic for structured products.']
  price_index_reference varchar [note: 'External market price index or benchmark used for floating-price legs. Examples include ISO day-ahead LMP, real-time LMP, or published hub indices.']
  price_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for pricing, indicating the currency and quantity basis. Typically USD per MWh for energy or USD per MW for capacity products.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'The type of wholesale product being traded in this leg: energy (MWh), capacity (MW), ancillary services, transmission rights, RECs, or financial instruments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: energy|capacity|ancillary_services|transmission|REC|CRR|FTR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the notional quantity. Common values include MWh (megawatt-hour) for energy, MW (megawatt) for capacity, and MMBtu for gas-fired generation fuel.']
  rec_vintage_year varchar [note: 'The year in which the renewable energy was generated, determining the vintage of associated RECs. Some RPS programs require specific vintage years.']
  renewable_energy_certificate_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The number of Renewable Energy Certificates associated with this leg. RECs represent the environmental attributes of renewable generation and may be bundled or unbundled from energy.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'The currency in which financial settlement for this leg will be executed. Typically USD for North American wholesale energy markets.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which financial settlement for this leg is completed and payment is exchanged between counterparties.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the leg settles through physical delivery of energy, financial cash settlement, or netting against other positions.']
  strike_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The predetermined price at which an option can be exercised. Applicable only to option legs; null for other leg types.']
  time_of_use_profile varchar [note: 'Defines the hours during which this leg is active. Common profiles include on-peak (weekday business hours), off-peak (nights and weekends), and flat (all hours). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: on_peak|off_peak|super_peak|flat|7x24|5x16|2x16H — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tolling_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg is part of a tolling agreement where one party provides fuel and the other provides generation capacity and conversion services.']
  transmission_rights_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg includes associated transmission capacity rights or Financial Transmission Rights (FTRs) for moving energy across the grid.']

  Note: 'Individual delivery leg or payment leg of a multi-leg wholesale energy trade. Captures leg type (fixed, floating, physical delivery, financial settlement), notional quantity, price formula, start and end delivery dates, delivery point (PNode/hub), and settlement currency. Supports complex structured products such as swaps, options, and tolling agreements managed in OpenLink Endur.'
}

Table trading.trading_position {
  trading_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the energy trading position record. Primary key for the position entity.']
  book_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trading book under which this position is held. Trading books organize positions by business unit, strategy, or portfolio.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying Power Purchase Agreement (PPA), bilateral contract, or master trading agreement governing this position. Nullable for ISO/RTO centrally cleared positions.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Trading positions are assigned to cost centers for risk capital allocation and performance attribution. Real business process: monthly risk reporting aggregates VaR and mark-to-market P&L by cost cent']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the counterparty entity for bilateral positions or the ISO/RTO for centrally cleared positions. Used for credit exposure tracking and settlement.']
  delivery_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical or financial delivery point (PNode, hub, zone, or interconnection point) where the commodity is delivered or settled.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: Trading positions track exposure to DR capacity commitments. Real business process: mark-to-market valuation of DR capacity positions for risk management and regulatory capital adequacy reporting (FER']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trader or scheduling coordinator responsible for managing this position. Used for attribution and accountability.']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: Position mark-to-market valuation and VaR calculations depend on forward price curves. Risk managers link positions to specific price forecast scenarios to calculate unrealized P&L and exposure metric']
  lmp_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trading.lmp_price. Business justification: Trading positions are marked-to-market using official LMP prices for the delivery point and snapshot timestamp. Linking position to the authoritative lmp_price record ensures valuation consistency and']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trading portfolio or strategy grouping to which this position belongs. Used for portfolio-level risk aggregation and performance reporting.']
  price_curve_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the forward price curve or index used for position valuation. Links to market data and pricing models.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Positions must roll up to profit centers for segment P&L and regulatory capital reporting. Real business process: quarterly segment reporting requires position-level profit center assignment for GAAP ']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating deal or transaction in the ETRM system that created this position. Links position to trade capture and deal management.']
  ancillary_service_type varchar [note: 'Type of ancillary service if this position represents capacity or ancillary services procurement (e.g., regulation up, regulation down, spinning reserve, non-spinning reserve, voltage support). Nullable for energy-only positions.']
  business_date date [note: 'Business date for which this position snapshot is reported. Used for end-of-day position reconciliation and historical trend analysis.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Type of energy commodity being traded. Defines the product category for the position. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: power|natural_gas|coal|renewable_energy_certificate|emissions_allowance|capacity|ancillary_services — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  congestion_revenue_right_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position includes Congestion Revenue Rights (CRRs) or Financial Transmission Rights (FTRs) used to hedge congestion costs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this position record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current credit exposure to the counterparty for this position. Represents the potential loss if the counterparty defaults. Used for credit risk management and collateral calculations.']
  delivery_end_date date [note: 'End date of the delivery period for the energy commodity. Defines when physical or financial settlement concludes.']
  delivery_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the delivery period for the energy commodity. Defines when physical or financial settlement begins.']
  hedge_designation varchar [note: 'Accounting hedge designation for the position under GAAP or IFRS hedge accounting rules. Determines financial reporting treatment.']
  iso_rto_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the ISO or RTO market jurisdiction where the position is held. Used for market-specific settlement and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|PJM|MISO|NYISO|ISO_NE|SPP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this position record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  mark_to_market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current market value of the position based on prevailing market prices (LMP, forward curves, or settlement prices). Represents the fair value of the position at the snapshot timestamp.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'Type of energy market in which the position is held. Distinguishes between Day-Ahead Market (DAM), Real-Time Market (RTM), forward markets, bilateral contracts, and ancillary services markets.']
  net_position_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Net long or short position quantity for the delivery period. Positive values indicate long positions, negative values indicate short positions. Measured in MWh for power or MMBtu for gas.']
  pnode_code varchar [note: 'ISO or RTO pricing node identifier used for Locational Marginal Price (LMP) settlement and valuation. Applicable for power positions.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position. Indicates whether the position is actively held, closed, or in settlement process.']
  rec_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) associated with this position, measured in MWh or certificate units. Nullable if rec_tracking_flag is false.']
  rec_tracking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position includes Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) that must be tracked separately for Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial settlement of the position. Typically USD for North American energy markets.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position snapshot was captured. Used for intraday position monitoring and end-of-day valuation time-series analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the net position quantity. Typically MWh (Megawatt-Hour) for power, MMBtu (Million British Thermal Units) for gas, or other commodity-specific units. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MWh|MMBtu|MW|GWh|metric_ton|allowance|certificate — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unrealized_pnl decimal(18,2) [note: 'Unrealized profit or loss on the position calculated as the difference between current mark-to-market value and original book value. Positive values indicate gains, negative values indicate losses.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the mark-to-market value of the position. Defines the pricing source and calculation approach.']
