  ferc_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the counterparty involved in the wholesale energy transactions reported in this EQR filing.']
  ferc_trade_id varchar [note: 'FK to trading.trade.trade_id — FERC EQR filings report specific wholesale transactions. The filing must reference the underlying trades for audit trail and compliance verification.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: FERC EQR filings must reference GL accounts for audit trail and reconciliation to financial statements. Real business process: external auditors trace EQR reported transactions to GL postings to verif']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.irp_scenario. Business justification: FERC EQR filings reference IRP scenarios to demonstrate that wholesale transactions align with prudent resource planning and regulatory commitments. Compliance officers link EQR filings to IRP scenari']
  original_filing_ferc_eqr_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original FERC EQR filing that this amendment corrects or updates. Null if this is an original filing.']
  preparer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FERC EQR filings require employee accountability for regulatory compliance. Preparer_name/contact fields are denormalized employee data. New FK enables workforce qualification tracking (FERC filing ce']
  primary_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'FK to trading.counterparty.counterparty_id — FERC EQR requires counterparty identification for every reported transaction. This FK ensures regulatory reporting accuracy.']
  primary_trade_id varchar [note: 'FK to trading.trade.trade_id — FERC EQR filings report wholesale transactions — each filing line must trace to the originating trade for audit compliance and amendment tracking.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: FERC EQR filings are a specific type of regulatory filing that should reference the master filing record. Regulatory affairs maintains a comprehensive filing log including EQR submissions alongside ra']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'FK to trading.trade.trade_id — FERC EQR filings report specific wholesale transactions. The FK to trade ensures audit trail from regulatory filing back to source transaction.']
  amendment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is an amendment to a previously submitted EQR filing (True) or an original filing (False).']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why this amendment was filed, including the nature of corrections or updates made to the original submission.']
  ancillary_services_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reported transactions include ancillary services procurement (e.g., regulation, spinning reserves, non-spinning reserves).']
  attestation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the authorized officer has attested to the accuracy and completeness of the information in this EQR filing, as required by FERC regulations.']
  attestation_officer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the authorized officer who attested to the accuracy and completeness of this EQR filing.']
  attestation_officer_title varchar [note: 'The job title of the authorized officer who attested to the accuracy and completeness of this EQR filing.']
  attestation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the authorized officer attested to the accuracy and completeness of this EQR filing.']
  congestion_revenue_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reported transactions involve or are hedged by Congestion Revenue Rights or Financial Transmission Rights.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'The type of contractual arrangement under which the reported transactions occurred (e.g., bilateral contract, ISO/RTO market participation, power purchase agreement).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this EQR filing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this filing. Typically USD for FERC EQR filings.']
  data_extraction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the transaction data was extracted from the source ETRM system for preparation of this EQR filing.']
  delivery_point_count varchar [note: 'The number of distinct delivery points or pricing nodes involved in the transactions reported in this EQR filing.']
  ferc_acceptance_date date [note: 'The date on which FERC officially accepted the EQR filing as complete and compliant.']
  ferc_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The confirmation number issued by FERC upon successful receipt of the EQR filing. Serves as proof of submission and regulatory acknowledgment.']
  filing_comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments or explanatory notes provided by the reporting entity to clarify or contextualize the information in this EQR filing.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'The official FERC-assigned filing number for this EQR submission. Serves as the external business identifier for regulatory tracking and correspondence.']
  filing_quarter varchar [note: 'The calendar quarter for which this EQR filing reports transactions (1=Q1, 2=Q2, 3=Q3, 4=Q4).']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the EQR filing in the regulatory submission workflow.']
  filing_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which this EQR filing reports transactions.']
  ftr_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total quantity of Financial Transmission Rights or Congestion Revenue Rights associated with the reported transactions, measured in megawatts.']
  iso_rto_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the ISO or RTO market in which the reported transactions occurred (e.g., CAISO, PJM, ERCOT, MISO, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this EQR filing record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'The type of wholesale energy market in which the reported transactions were executed (e.g., day-ahead market, real-time market, bilateral market, ancillary services market).']
  rec_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total quantity of Renewable Energy Certificates associated with the transactions reported in this EQR filing, if applicable.']
  rec_tracking_system varchar [note: 'The name of the REC tracking system or registry used to track renewable energy certificates associated with the reported transactions (e.g., WREGIS, M-RETS, NEPOOL GIS).']
  reporting_entity_ferc_code varchar [note: 'The FERC-assigned identifier for the utility or energy company submitting this EQR filing.']
  reporting_entity_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the utility or energy company submitting this EQR filing to FERC.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the Energy Trading and Risk Management (ETRM) system or other operational system from which the transaction data for this EQR filing was extracted (e.g., OpenLink Endur).']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The method or channel used to submit the EQR filing to FERC (e.g., eFiling portal, paper submission, electronic batch upload).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this EQR filing was officially submitted to FERC. Represents the principal business event timestamp for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  total_quantity_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total volume of energy transacted and reported in this EQR filing, measured in megawatt-hours.']
  total_transaction_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of all wholesale energy transactions reported in this EQR filing, expressed in US dollars.']
  transaction_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the wholesale energy transactions reported in this filing by contract duration (long-term: >1 year, short-term: 1 day to 1 year, spot: same-day or next-day).']
  validation_error_count varchar [note: 'The number of data quality validation errors identified during internal review of the EQR filing data.']
  validation_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting any data quality issues, validation errors, or exceptions identified during internal review of the EQR filing data.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'The status of internal data quality validation checks performed on the EQR filing data before submission to FERC.']
  weighted_average_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The weighted average price per megawatt-hour for all transactions reported in this EQR filing, expressed in US dollars.']

  Note: 'FERC Electric Quarterly Report (EQR) filing record capturing the utility\'s mandatory wholesale market transaction reporting to FERC. Captures filing period (quarter/year), transaction category (long-term, short-term, spot), counterparty, contract type, quantity (MWh), price ($/MWh), filing submission timestamp, FERC confirmation number, and amendment status. Supports FERC Order 2001 compliance.'
}

Table trading.hedge_strategy {
  hedge_strategy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hedge strategy record. Primary key.']
  book_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trading book in the ETRM system (OpenLink Endur) that executes trades under this hedge strategy.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_project. Business justification: Hedging strategies for capital projects (construction cost hedges, fuel hedges for new generation) must link to projects for cost-of-capital tracking. Real business process: IRP filings require disclo']
  compliance_sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sox_control. Business justification: Hedge strategies designated for hedge accounting must be tested for effectiveness under SOX controls. Internal audit tests hedge effectiveness quarterly as part of SOX 404 financial reporting controls']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or trader responsible for managing and executing this hedge strategy.']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: Hedge strategies are designed around price forecast scenarios. Risk managers link hedge strategies to price forecasts for hedge effectiveness testing (ASC 815), prospective/retrospective testing, and ']
  authorized_instruments varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of authorized derivative instruments that may be used to execute this hedge strategy (e.g., forwards, futures, swaps, options, collars).']
  board_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the board of directors approved this hedge strategy.']
  board_resolution_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the board resolution that authorized this hedge strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hedge strategy record was first created in the system.']
  cvar_target_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR) or Expected Shortfall limit in USD for tail risk management.']
  dedesignation_date date [note: 'Date on which the hedge accounting designation was terminated or de-designated, if applicable.']
  dedesignation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the hedge accounting designation was terminated.']
  dedesignation_trigger_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of conditions that would trigger de-designation of the hedge accounting relationship (e.g., effectiveness falls outside 80-125%, forecasted transaction no longer probable).']
  delivery_period_end date [note: 'End date of the delivery period covered by this hedge strategy.']
  delivery_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the delivery period covered by this hedge strategy.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this hedge strategy is scheduled to end or was terminated.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this hedge strategy became effective and operational.']
  effectiveness_test_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assess hedge effectiveness: dollar-offset, regression analysis, critical terms match, or VaR reduction.']
  ferc_reporting_category varchar [note: 'FERC reporting category or classification code applicable to this hedge strategy for regulatory filings.']
  hedge_accounting_designation varchar [note: 'ASC 815 hedge accounting designation type: fair value hedge, cash flow hedge, net investment hedge, or not designated for hedge accounting.']
  hedge_designation_date date [note: 'Date on which the hedge accounting designation was formally documented and became effective.']
  hedge_executed_in_book varchar [note: 'FK to trading.trading_book.trading_book_id — Hedge strategies are executed through specific trading books. This FK links board-approved hedging programs to the books that implement them.']
  hedge_horizon_months varchar [note: 'Time horizon in months over which the hedge strategy is executed and maintained.']
  hedge_objective varchar [note: 'Primary risk management objective of the hedge strategy: price risk, basis risk, volumetric risk, credit risk, operational risk, or regulatory compliance.']
  hedge_ratio_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target hedge ratio as a percentage of the underlying exposure that the strategy aims to hedge (0-100%).']
  hedge_to_book varchar [note: 'FK to trading.trading_book.trading_book_id — Hedge strategies are typically aligned to trading books for ASC 815 hedge accounting designation and effectiveness testing.']
  hedged_item_description varchar [note: 'Description of the underlying hedged item or forecasted transaction being protected by this strategy.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal comments, strategy rationale, or special instructions related to this hedge strategy.']
  irp_alignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hedge strategy is aligned with the utility\'s Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) as filed with the Public Utility Commission (PUC).']
  irp_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific IRP filing or docket number that supports this hedge strategy.']
  last_effectiveness_ratio_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Calculated effectiveness ratio from the most recent test, expressed as a percentage.']
  last_effectiveness_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent hedge effectiveness test performed for this strategy.']
  last_effectiveness_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent hedge effectiveness test: highly effective, effective, ineffective, or not tested.']
  prospective_test_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Threshold percentage for prospective hedge effectiveness testing (typically 80-125% range per ASC 815).']
  retrospective_test_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Threshold percentage for retrospective hedge effectiveness testing (typically 80-125% range per ASC 815).']
  risk_committee_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk committee approved this hedge strategy.']
  risk_committee_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hedge strategy has been reviewed and approved by the enterprise risk committee.']
  strategy_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the hedge strategy, used for external reference and reporting.']
  strategy_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the hedge strategy program as approved by the board.']
  strategy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hedge strategy: active, suspended, terminated, pending approval, or under review.']
  strategy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hedge strategy type: fixed-price forward, basis swap, collar, option, heat rate, CRR/FTR (Congestion Revenue Rights/Financial Transmission Rights), volumetric, or credit hedge. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fixed_price_forward|basis_swap|collar|option|heat_rate|crr_ftr|volumetric|credit — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_commodity varchar [note: 'The commodity or product being hedged: electricity, natural gas, coal, renewable energy certificate (REC), capacity, or ancillary services.']
  target_volume_mmbtu decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target volume in million British thermal units (MMBtu) to be hedged under this strategy for natural gas commodities.']
  target_volume_mwh decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target volume in megawatt-hours (MWh) to be hedged under this strategy for electricity commodities.']
  test_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which hedge effectiveness testing is performed: monthly, quarterly, annually, at inception, or event-driven.']
  to_trading_book varchar [note: 'FK to trading.trading_book.trading_book_id — Hedge strategies are executed through trading books. This FK links strategy definition to execution and enables hedge effectiveness tracking against actual positions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hedge strategy record was last modified.']
  var_confidence_level_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence level percentage used for VaR calculation (e.g., 95%, 99%).']
  var_target_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target Value at Risk (VaR) limit in USD that the hedge strategy aims to maintain for the portfolio.']

  Note: 'Hedge strategy master record defining the utility\'s board-approved hedging programs for managing commodity price risk, basis risk, volumetric risk, and credit risk in the wholesale energy portfolio. Captures strategy name, hedge type (fixed-price forward, basis swap, collar, option, heat rate, CRR/FTR), target commodity and delivery period, hedge ratio and target volume, hedge horizon, risk metric targets (VaR, CVaR, expected shortfall), authorized instruments, board approval date and reference, IRP alignment flag, and ASC 815 hedge designation (fair value, cash flow, net investment). Includes hedge effectiveness testing: prospective and retrospective test methods (dollar-offset, regression, critical terms match), effectiveness thresholds, test frequency, test results history, and de-designation triggers. Links to trading books that execute the strategy. Supports hedge accounting documentation, ASC 815/IFRS 9 compliance, effectiveness testing audit trail, and risk committee reporting.'
}

Table trading.capacity_obligation {
  capacity_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capacity obligation record in the ISO/RTO capacity market.']
  battery_storage_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.battery_storage_asset. Business justification: Battery storage provides capacity in ISO markets with specific performance requirements. Links capacity obligations to battery assets for availability tracking, discharge duration validation, and perf']
  bilateral_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the counterparty in a bilateral capacity purchase agreement, if applicable.']
  capacity_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.capacity_requirement. Business justification: Capacity obligations must satisfy ISO/RTO capacity requirements derived from resource adequacy assessments. Compliance officers link obligations to formal capacity requirements to demonstrate fulfillm']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capacity obligations and associated costs are allocated to cost centers for rate case cost-of-service calculations. Real business process: rate case testimony requires capacity costs by functional cos']
  demand_dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_enrollment. Business justification: Specific DR enrollments satisfy capacity obligations. Real business process: granular capacity resource tracking for ISO capacity market audits, performance measurement, and capacity payment allocatio']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary capacity resource (generation unit, demand response program, or import contract) committed to satisfy this obligation.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_program. Business justification: Capacity obligations met through DR program enrollment. Real business process: PJM/NYISO/ISO-NE capacity market compliance where load-serving entities use DR programs to satisfy resource adequacy requ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the capacity planning analyst or resource adequacy manager responsible for managing this obligation.']
  load_serving_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the load serving entity responsible for this capacity obligation.']
  market_bid_id varchar [note: 'FK to trading.market_bid.market_bid_id — Capacity obligations may be fulfilled through market participation (capacity auctions). This FK links the obligation to the market bid/award that satisfies it.']
  peak_demand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.peak_demand. Business justification: Capacity obligations are sized based on peak demand forecasts. Load-serving entities link capacity obligations to the peak demand forecasts used to determine required capacity purchases and demonstrat']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Capacity obligations impact profit center economics and must be tracked for segment profitability analysis. Real business process: monthly management reporting shows capacity costs by profit center fo']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Capacity obligations and procurement are reported in state IRP filings and resource adequacy compliance reports. Regulatory teams demonstrate capacity procurement meets state reliability requirements ']
  auction_date date [note: 'The date on which the capacity auction was conducted and clearing prices were established.']
  auction_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the ISO/RTO capacity auction in which the capacity was procured (e.g., BRA, incremental auction identifier).']
  auction_type varchar [note: 'Type of capacity procurement mechanism: base residual auction (BRA), incremental auction, reconfiguration auction, or bilateral capacity purchase.']
  bilateral_contract_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capacity was procured through a bilateral contract outside of the ISO/RTO auction (True) or through the auction mechanism (False).']
  bilateral_contract_number varchar [note: 'Contract reference number for bilateral capacity purchase agreements.']
  capacity_clearing_price_per_mw_day decimal(18,6) [note: 'The auction clearing price in dollars per megawatt-day for capacity in this zone and planning year, representing the market cost of capacity.']
  capacity_market_program varchar [note: 'Name of the specific capacity market program (e.g., PJM Reliability Pricing Model, MISO Planning Resource Auction, NYISO Installed Capacity Market, ISO-NE Forward Capacity Market).']
  capacity_performance_obligation_mw decimal(18,6) [note: 'The portion of committed capacity that is designated as Capacity Performance (CP) and subject to enhanced performance requirements and penalties during emergency conditions.']
  capacity_shortfall_mw decimal(18,6) [note: 'The difference between required capacity and committed capacity, representing unmet obligation exposure (negative values indicate surplus).']
  committed_capacity_mw decimal(18,6) [note: 'The total capacity in megawatts that has been committed through generation resources, demand response, imports, or bilateral contracts to satisfy the obligation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this capacity obligation record was first created in the system.']
  deficiency_charge_rate_per_mw_day decimal(18,6) [note: 'The penalty rate in dollars per megawatt-day that applies if the utility fails to meet its capacity obligation, typically set by ISO/RTO tariff.']
  deficiency_exposure_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The potential financial exposure in USD if the capacity shortfall is not resolved, calculated as capacity_shortfall_mw * deficiency_charge_rate_per_mw_day * days_in_period.']
  ferc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity obligation must be included in FERC Form 1 or other FERC regulatory filings (True) or is exempt (False).']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the capacity obligation and resource commitment were filed with the ISO/RTO for the planning year.']
  forced_outage_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The expected forced outage rate percentage applied to convert installed capacity (ICAP) to unforced capacity (UCAP) for the committed resources.']
  iso_rto_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the ISO or RTO capacity market in which this obligation exists (PJM RPM, MISO PRA, NYISO ICAP, ISO-NE FCM). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PJM|MISO|NYISO|ISO-NE|CAISO|ERCOT|SPP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this capacity obligation record was last modified, reflecting changes to committed resources, shortfalls, or status.']
  nerc_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capacity obligation and committed resources meet NERC reliability standards for resource adequacy (True) or have compliance gaps (False).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, comments, or special conditions related to the capacity obligation, resource procurement strategy, or compliance considerations.']
  obligation_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the capacity obligation period, typically May 31 of the following year for most ISO/RTO markets.']
  obligation_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the capacity obligation period, typically June 1 for most ISO/RTO markets.']
  obligation_reference_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the capacity obligation, used in ISO/RTO filings and internal tracking.']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity obligation: draft (planning), active (obligation period in effect), satisfied (fully committed), deficient (shortfall exists), or closed (period ended).']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Type of capacity obligation: ICAP (Installed Capacity), UCAP (Unforced Capacity), PLC (Peak Load Contribution), or CONE (Cost of New Entry).']
  peak_load_forecast_mw decimal(18,6) [note: 'The forecasted peak load in megawatts for the obligation period, used as the basis for calculating the capacity requirement.']
  performance_penalty_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committed capacity resources are subject to performance penalties during shortage events (True for capacity performance resources in PJM, False otherwise).']
  planning_year varchar [note: 'The planning year or delivery year for which the capacity obligation applies, typically formatted as YYYY/YYYY (e.g., 2024/2025).']
  puc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity obligation must be reported to the state Public Utility Commission for resource adequacy compliance (True) or not (False).']
  required_capacity_mw decimal(18,6) [note: 'The total capacity requirement in megawatts that the utility must procure or demonstrate to meet resource adequacy standards.']
  reserve_margin_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planning reserve margin percentage applied to determine the capacity obligation, representing the buffer above forecasted peak load.']
  total_capacity_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost in USD for procuring the committed capacity over the obligation period, calculated as committed_capacity_mw * capacity_clearing_price_per_mw_day * days_in_period.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Name of the capacity zone or locational deliverability area (LDA) where the obligation applies.']

  Note: 'Capacity market obligation and resource adequacy record capturing the utility\'s installed capacity (ICAP) or unforced capacity (UCAP) requirements and committed capacity resources in ISO/RTO capacity markets (PJM RPM, MISO PRA, NYISO ICAP, ISO-NE FCM). Captures obligation period, planning year, required MW, committed resource ID, capacity type (generation, demand response, energy efficiency, import), capacity price ($/MW-day), auction clearing reference, bilateral capacity purchase details, deficiency charge exposure, performance penalty risk, and NERC reliability standard compliance flag. Supports resource adequacy planning and capacity cost allocation.'
}

Table trading.settlement_statement {
  settlement_statement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for settlement_statement']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the power purchase agreement or bilateral contract associated with the settlement.']
  corrected_settlement_statement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on settlement_statement (corrected_settlement_statement_id)']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the counterparty (buyer or seller) involved in the settlement.']
  trade_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying trade that this settlement statement settles.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net adjustments (taxes, fees, penalties) applied to the gross amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement statement record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before adjustments, in settlement currency.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the invoice generated for this settlement.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'Market segment for which the settlement applies (e.g., day-ahead, real-time).']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after adjustments, in settlement currency.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the net settlement amount must be paid.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the settlement.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement statement was posted to the ledger.']
  price_per_mwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'Settlement price applied per megawatt-hour.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Energy product category settled by this statement.']
  quantity_mwh decimal(18,4) [note: 'Energy quantity settled, expressed in megawatt-hours.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes related to the settlement.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this settlement statement represents a reversal of a prior settlement.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement transaction is considered effective.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Process used to generate the settlement (automated, manual, or batch).']
  settlement_period_end date [note: 'End date of the settlement period covered by this statement.']
  settlement_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the settlement period covered by this statement.']
  settlement_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the settlement statement to support amendments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that produced the settlement data (e.g., OpenLink Endur).']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the settlement statement by the trading system.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement statement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement statement record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for settlement_statement. Referenced by settlement_statement_id.'
}

Table trading.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the counterparty organization associated with the contract.']
  master_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on contract (master_contract_id)']
  collateral_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary collateral posted to secure the contract.']
  collateral_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the collateral amount.']
  contract_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional details about the contract.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the contract for easy reference by traders and analysts.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the contract, used in trading and regulatory reporting.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract (e.g., active, pending, suspended, terminated).']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the agreement such as Power Purchase Agreement, Capacity Contract, Ancillary Service, or Financial Swap.']
  counterparty_credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned to the counterparty (e.g., A+, BBB).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure allowed for the counterparty under this contract.']
  credit_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the credit limit amount.']
  delivery_point_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the delivery location or node in the grid.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract expires or terminates, if applicable.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally binding and operational.']
  ferc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contract must be reported to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.']
  index_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Most recent value of the price index used for the contract.']
  index_price_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the index price, using ISO 4217 code.']
  is_exercisable boolean [note: 'True if the contract includes exercisable options (e.g., call/put).']
  is_firm boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is firm (obligatory) or non‑firm (optional).']
  is_hedge boolean [note: 'True if the contract is used for hedging market exposure.']
  is_indexed boolean [note: 'True if the contract price is tied to an external index.']
  is_renewable boolean [note: 'True if the contract pertains to renewable energy generation.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction governing the contract (state, province, or country).']
  market varchar [note: 'Electricity market or ISO/RTO where the contract is executed (e.g., CAISO, ERCOT).']
  naesb_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contract complies with NAESB standards.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Standard due date for each payment cycle.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often payments are due (e.g., monthly, quarterly).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of payment conditions (e.g., net 30, net 45).']
  price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount associated with the contract price per unit.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the contract price.']
  pricing_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate contract price (e.g., fixed, indexed, variable).']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Type of energy product covered (e.g., energy, capacity, ancillary service).']
  regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for mandatory regulatory filings (e.g., FERC schedule code).']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes an automatic renewal clause (e.g., none, optional, mandatory).']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the renewal period in months when a renewal option is exercised.']
  settlement_point_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement location used for financial settlement.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date the contract was signed by all parties.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contract is exempt from applicable taxes.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the contract was terminated before its scheduled end, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  volume_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total energy volume covered by the contract.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the contract volume (e.g., MWh, GWh).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for contract. Referenced by contract_id.'
}

Table trading.load_serving_entity {
  load_serving_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for load_serving_entity']
  parent_load_serving_entity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on load_serving_entity (parent_load_serving_entity_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the entity’s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the entity’s mailing address (optional).']
  capacity_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical capacity the entity can serve or transmit, expressed in megawatts.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the entity’s address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the entity is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the load serving entity record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the entity’s role, ownership, or special characteristics.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the entity became effective for operational purposes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the entity ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  entity_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the load serving entity (e.g., utility name, balancing authority).']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the entity by its functional role in the power system.']
  is_federal_entity boolean [note: 'True if the entity is a federal or government‑owned organization.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction (state or province) governing the entity.']
  lse_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the load serving entity by market operators or regulatory bodies.']
  market_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity participates in wholesale energy markets.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the entity’s address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact for the entity.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region where the entity operates (e.g., Northeast, Midwest).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the entity is subject to mandatory regulatory reporting (e.g., FERC, NAESB).']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the entity’s address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the entity within the utility\'s master data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for load_serving_entity. Referenced by load_serving_entity_id.'
}

Table trading.netting_agreement {
  netting_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for netting_agreement']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the primary counterparty to the netting agreement.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity that owns or signs the netting agreement.']
  superseded_netting_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on netting_agreement (superseded_netting_agreement_id)']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the netting agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the netting agreement by the trading system.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of netting agreement indicating the structure of parties involved.']
  amendment_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the record represents an amendment to an existing agreement.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the amendment applied to the original agreement.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit log entry that captures change history for this agreement.']
  collateral_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the posted collateral.']
  collateral_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the collateral amount.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'Form of collateral posted to support the netting agreement.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the agreement.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the agreement with regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the netting agreement record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure allowed under the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the netting agreement terminates; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the netting agreement becomes legally binding.']
  effective_version varchar [note: 'Version of the agreement reflecting the latest set of terms after amendments.']
  exposure_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum net exposure permitted for the agreement.']
  ftr_holding_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Quantity of FTRs held under the agreement, expressed in the holding unit.']
  ftr_holding_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the FTR holding quantity.']
  internal_reference_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used for cross‑system reference or tracking.']
  is_eligible_for_ftr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement qualifies for Financial Transmission Rights participation.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the regulatory jurisdiction governing the agreement.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement was last reviewed for compliance or operational relevance.']
  margin_requirement_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required margin as a percentage of the netted exposure.']
  netting_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for all monetary calculations in the agreement.']
  netting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often netting calculations are performed.']
  netting_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate net positions across the parties.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement is subject to mandatory regulatory reporting (e.g., FERC, NAESB).']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory agreement reviews.']
  settlement_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of settlement procedures, timing, and payment mechanisms.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the netting agreement.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the agreement was terminated, if earlier than the effective end date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the netting agreement record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for netting_agreement. Referenced by netting_agreement_id.'
}

Table trading.trading_desk {
  trading_desk_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for trading_desk']
  parent_trading_desk_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on trading_desk (parent_trading_desk_id)']
  capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum megawatt capacity the desk is authorized to trade.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the desk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the desk record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the desk\'s purpose and responsibilities.']
  desk_code varchar [note: 'External code or abbreviation used to reference the desk in trading systems.']
  desk_level varchar [note: 'Organizational seniority tier of the desk.']
  desk_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the trading desk.']
  desk_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the desk\'s operational mode.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the desk became operational or was officially recognized.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the desk ceased operations or was retired (null if still active).']
  ferc_registered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the desk is registered with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.']
  iso_rto varchar [note: 'Independent System Operator / Regional Transmission Organization the desk interacts with.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit for the desk.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City where the desk\'s primary office is located.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the desk\'s location.']
  location_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the desk\'s primary office.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the desk manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the desk manager.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the desk manager.']
  naesb_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with NAESB scheduling standards.']
  primary_market varchar [note: 'Main market segment the desk participates in.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region (e.g., North America, Europe) where the desk primarily trades.']
  reporting_group varchar [note: 'Grouping used for internal reporting and analytics.']
  risk_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary risk exposure allowed for the desk.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for settlements.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the desk.']
  trading_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary trading strategy employed by the desk.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the desk record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for trading_desk. Referenced by trading_desk_id.'
}

Table trading.market_participant {
  market_participant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for market_participant']
  parent_market_participant_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on market_participant (parent_market_participant_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the participant\'s primary address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the address, if applicable.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number for settlements.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Routing number of the settlement bank.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the primary address.']
  classification_code varchar [note: 'Internal classification code for reporting and segmentation.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status with regulatory requirements.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the participant\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the market participant record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure allowed for the participant.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned to the participant.']
  default_settlement_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for settling financial transactions.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet unique identifier.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the participant became active in the market.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the participant\'s market participation ends, if applicable.']
  ferc_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used for FERC market reporting.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the participant is currently active (true) or not (false).']
  iso_participant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the Independent System Operator.']
  last_credit_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent credit review.']
  market_participant_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used for trading system identification.']
  naesb_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used in NAESB reporting.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the market participant as registered.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks or notes about the participant.']
  participant_role varchar [note: 'Primary role of the participant in market transactions.']
  participant_type varchar [note: 'Category of the participant in the electricity market.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the primary address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person for the participant.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number assigned by regulatory authority.']
  reporting_entity_flag boolean [note: 'True if the participant is required to submit regulatory reports.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Internal risk classification based on credit and exposure.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for financial settlements with the participant.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used name for the participant.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the primary address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the participant.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax ID used for reporting and withholding.']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Tax status applicable to the participant.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the market participant record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for market_participant. Referenced by market_participant_id.'
}

Table trading.pnode {
  pnode_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pnode']
  aggregate_pnode_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pnode (aggregate_pnode_id)']
  capacity_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum generation or load capacity at the node in megawatts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the node record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the node\'s characteristics, location notes, or operational remarks.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the node became effective for market participation.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the node ceased to be effective; null if still active.']
  iso_rto varchar [note: 'Regional transmission organization or independent system operator that governs the node.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the node in decimal degrees.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the node in decimal degrees.']
  market_zone varchar [note: 'Market pricing zone to which the node belongs.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the node used in market and operational contexts.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code assigned to the node by the ISO/RTO or internal system.']
  node_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the entity that owns or operates the physical node.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the functional role of the node in the grid.']
  settlement_group varchar [note: 'Grouping used for settlement calculations, often aligned with market zones or pricing points.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the node.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the node record.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage level of the node expressed in kilovolts.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pnode. Referenced by source_pnode_id.'
}

Table trading.market_run {
  market_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for market_run']
  market_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary market participant associated with the run.']
  superseded_market_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on market_run (superseded_market_run_id)']
  ancillary_service_flag boolean [note: 'True if ancillary services (e.g., regulation, spinning reserve) were priced.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the run was formally approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User name or identifier of the person who approved the run results.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the run against market rules.']
  congestion_price_flag boolean [note: 'True if congestion pricing was applied in this run.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the market run record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the overall data quality of the run.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the market run data (e.g., OpenLink, Eikon).']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the market run execution finished.']
  forecast_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the run is a forecast (true) or actual market outcome (false).']
  iso_rto_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code identifying the ISO or RTO.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User who performed the most recent update to the record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  market_date date [note: 'The calendar date for which the market run prices apply.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Type of price produced by the run (e.g., Locational Marginal Price).']
  pricing_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate market prices.']
  region varchar [note: 'Regional transmission organization or independent system operator associated with the run.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the run data is required for regulatory reporting.']
  run_category varchar [note: 'Business categorization of the run for reporting purposes.']
  run_code varchar [note: 'System‑generated code used to reference the run in downstream systems.']
  run_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the run in minutes.']
  run_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the market run (e.g., "DA 2024‑05‑01").']
  run_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the run.']
  run_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to order runs when multiple are scheduled.']
  run_result_summary varchar [note: 'Brief textual summary of key outcomes from the run.']
  run_status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status, especially when failed or cancelled.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Category of the market run indicating the market horizon or purpose.']
  run_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the run logic or configuration.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which financial settlement for the run occurs.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the market run execution began.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the market run.']
  test_run_flag boolean [note: 'True if the run was executed for testing or validation purposes.']
  total_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the cleared energy, expressed in US dollars.']
  total_volume_mwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'Aggregate energy volume cleared by the run, measured in megawatt‑hours.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the market run record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User name or identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for market_run. Referenced by market_run_id.'
}

Table trading.transmission_constraint {
  transmission_constraint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for transmission_constraint']

  congestion_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of congestion cost attributable to the constraint.']
  constraint_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping such as capacity, ramp, must‑run, or reliability.']
  constraint_code varchar [note: 'External code or identifier used in market filings and operational systems.']
  constraint_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of power flow to which the constraint applies.']
  constraint_group varchar [note: 'Logical group identifier for related constraints (e.g., corridor group).']
  constraint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the transmission constraint.']
  constraint_owner varchar [note: 'Entity (e.g., utility, transmission operator) that owns or administers the constraint.']
  constraint_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used for conflict resolution when multiple constraints overlap.']
  constraint_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or ISO/RTO jurisdiction where the constraint applies.']
  constraint_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or application that supplied the constraint data (e.g., OpenLink Endur).']
  constraint_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason explaining the current status (e.g., maintenance, regulatory hold).']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Category of the constraint describing its physical or operational nature.']
  constraint_zone varchar [note: 'Specific market zone or congestion zone impacted by the constraint.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the constraint record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the constraint, including operational notes.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the constraint expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the constraint becomes effective.']
  is_regulatory boolean [note: 'True if the constraint is mandated by a regulator (e.g., FERC).']
  lmp_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the constraint directly influences Locational Marginal Pricing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or operational comments.']
  reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory reporting regime applicable to the constraint.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the constraint.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the constraint record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version used for change management and audit.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for transmission_constraint. Referenced by transmission_constraint_id.'
}

Table trading.price_curve {

  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the price curve ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']

  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the curve must be included in mandatory market or FERC reporting.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the curve is used for energy, capacity, or ancillary service settlement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system (e.g., OpenLink Endur) that supplied the curve data.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price curve.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'Time zone identifier (e.g., America/Los_Angeles) for timestamp alignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the curve record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the curve definition for change management.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for price_curve. Referenced by price_curve_id.'
}

Table trading.master_agreement {
  master_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for master_agreement']

  contract_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code used for monetary amounts in the agreement.']

  delivery_start_date date [note: 'First date on which the contracted energy delivery begins.']

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement becomes legally binding.']
  last_amended_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the most recent update.']

  price_per_mwh decimal(12,5) [note: 'Unit price of energy in the contract, expressed in the contract currency per megawatt‑hour.']

  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement must be included in mandatory market‑settlement or FERC reports.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes about the agreement.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement auto‑renews, requires manual renewal, or does not renew.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which financial settlement occurs for the contract.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agreement.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the party must notify the counter‑party before terminating the agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the master agreement record.']
  volume_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total energy volume committed under the agreement, measured in megawatt‑hours.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for master_agreement. Referenced by master_agreement_id.'
}

Table asset.registry {
  registry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset registry record. Primary key for the asset registry product.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: asset_registry currently has asset_class (STRING) attribute storing class name/code. This should be normalized to FK pointing to asset_class reference table. The asset_class product is the authoritati']
  manufacturer_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Assets have OEM manufacturers tracked as vendors for warranty claims, technical support, and spare parts procurement. Formalizes manufacturer field into vendor master for supplier performance tracking']
  parent_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent asset in a hierarchical asset structure (e.g., a relay\'s parent transformer, a component\'s parent assembly). Enables bill-of-materials and asset hierarchy navigation.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original purchase or construction cost of the asset in USD. Used for depreciation calculations and financial reporting.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the asset type based on utility functional area. Used for portfolio segmentation and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: generation|transmission|distribution|substation|protection|metering|fleet|facility — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asset_condition_rating varchar [note: 'Current physical and operational condition assessment of the asset based on inspection and testing. Drives maintenance prioritization and replacement planning.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the asset for identification and reporting purposes.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique asset identifier assigned by the utility for tracking and identification purposes. Used across maintenance, inventory, and financial systems.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Detailed asset type within the asset class (e.g., power transformer, circuit breaker, transmission tower, distribution pole, generation turbine, relay). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: power_transformer|circuit_breaker|transmission_tower|distribution_pole|generation_turbine|combustion_turbine|steam_turbine|relay|capacitor_bank|voltage_regulator|recloser|sectionalizer|switchgear|substation_battery|diesel_generator|service_vehicle|bucket_truck|operations_center|warehouse — promote to reference product]']
  book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current net book value of the asset after accumulated depreciation. Updated periodically based on depreciation schedules.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the rated capacity field (Megawatt, Megavolt-Ampere, Kilovolt, Kilovolt-Ampere, Ampere, Kiloampere).']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was placed into service and became operational. Used for depreciation calculations and lifecycle planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset registry record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  criticality_score varchar [note: 'Numerical score (typically 1-10) representing the asset\'s criticality to grid reliability and customer service. Used for maintenance prioritization and risk assessment.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that provided this asset record (e.g., Ventyx Asset Suite, SAP PM, GIS). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to calculate depreciation for this asset class. Typically prescribed by regulatory authority.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is subject to EPA environmental regulations (e.g., emissions monitoring, hazardous materials handling, PCB transformer tracking).']
  failure_risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk category for asset failure based on condition, age, and operational stress. Drives preventive maintenance and replacement strategies.']
  ferc_account_code varchar [note: 'FERC Uniform System of Accounts code for financial classification of the asset. Required for regulatory financial reporting and rate case filings.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was physically installed at its location, which may precede commissioning date.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal inspection or condition assessment performed on the asset.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent completed maintenance work order for this asset. Used to calculate next maintenance due dates.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset registry record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the asset location in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and outage analysis.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the asset\'s physical location (e.g., substation name, pole number, facility address).']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the asset location in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and outage analysis.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Assigned maintenance approach for this asset based on criticality, failure modes, and cost-benefit analysis. Determines work order generation rules.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model or part number for the asset. Used for spare parts procurement and technical specifications lookup.']
  nerc_critical_asset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is designated as a NERC Critical Cyber Asset or Critical Infrastructure Protection (CIP) asset, requiring enhanced security and compliance controls.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection based on regulatory requirements or maintenance strategy.']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next planned maintenance activity based on maintenance strategy and interval rules.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the asset in its lifecycle. Determines availability for grid operations and maintenance planning.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership classification of the asset. Determines financial treatment, maintenance responsibility, and regulatory reporting requirements.']
  rab_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is included in the Regulatory Asset Base for rate-making purposes. Critical for CAPEX planning and rate case preparation.']
  rated_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Nameplate capacity or rating of the asset in its primary unit of measure (MW for generation, MVA for transformers, kV for voltage equipment). Critical for grid planning and load management.']
  record_active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset registry record is currently active and valid. Used for soft deletes and historical record retention.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was permanently removed from service and retired. Triggers final depreciation and asset disposal processes.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific asset unit. Critical for warranty tracking and asset identity verification.']
  service_territory varchar [note: 'Utility service territory or operating region where the asset is located. Used for regulatory reporting and operational planning.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of the asset in years as determined by engineering standards or regulatory guidance. Used for depreciation and replacement planning.']
  voltage_class_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage class of the asset in kilovolts. Used for grid segmentation and equipment compatibility analysis.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty coverage expires. Important for maintenance cost planning and vendor claims.']

  Note: 'Master record for all utility physical assets including generation units, transmission towers, distribution poles, transformers, switchgear, protection relays, substation equipment, fleet vehicles, and facilities. Captures asset identity, classification, installation details, location (GIS coordinates), manufacturer, model, serial number, commissioning date, rated capacity, voltage class, asset condition rating, operational status, regulatory classification, and RAB (Regulatory Asset Base) inclusion flag. SSOT for asset identity across the enterprise, sourced from Ventyx Asset Suite EAM.'
}

Table asset.hierarchy {
  hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset hierarchy relationship record. Primary key for the asset hierarchy product.']
  child_asset_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the child asset in the hierarchy structure. Links to the asset registry for the lower-level asset (e.g., equipment, component, device).']
  hierarchy_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that created this hierarchy relationship record. Used for audit trail and data governance. Links to enterprise identity management system.']
  hierarchy_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that last modified this hierarchy relationship record. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Links to enterprise identity management system.']
  hierarchy_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent asset in the hierarchy structure. Links to the asset registry for the higher-level asset (e.g., substation, facility, network segment).']
  cost_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of OPEX and CAPEX costs allocated to this hierarchical relationship. Used when a child asset serves multiple parent facilities or network segments and costs must be apportioned. Value between 0.00 and 100.00. Supports regulatory cost allocation and rate case preparation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy relationship record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and regulatory compliance. Immutable after initial creation.']
  criticality_inherited_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child asset inherits the criticality classification from the parent asset in the hierarchy. True means child criticality is derived from parent (e.g., all equipment in a critical substation is critical). False means child has independent criticality assessment. Used for risk-based maintenance prioritization and outage impact analysis.']
  depth varchar [note: 'Total depth of the hierarchy tree from this relationship to the deepest leaf node. Used for hierarchy complexity analysis and query optimization. Updated when child relationships are added or removed.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchical relationship ended or was superseded. Null for currently active relationships. Supports historical hierarchy reconstruction for regulatory reporting, asset lifecycle analysis, and audit trails.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchical relationship became active. Supports temporal hierarchy tracking for asset relocations, network reconfigurations, and organizational restructuring. Used for point-in-time hierarchy queries and historical CAPEX/OPEX roll-up.']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'Structured functional location identifier from the enterprise asset management system representing the hierarchical path (e.g., NORTH-SUB132-BAY03-CB01). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance functional location structure and Ventyx Asset Suite location hierarchy. Used for work order assignment, maintenance planning, and cost center allocation.']
  geographic_zone_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or service territory zone identifier for the hierarchical relationship. Used for regional reporting, storm response planning, mutual aid coordination, and geographic cost allocation. Aligns with GIS-based service territory boundaries.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the hierarchy tree structure where level 1 is the top-most parent (e.g., network segment, region) and higher numbers represent deeper nesting (e.g., level 5 might be a component within equipment within a bay within a substation within a zone). Supports depth-based queries and roll-up aggregations.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hierarchical relationship type. Functional location represents organizational structure (e.g., substation > bay > equipment). Electrical connectivity represents electrical network topology. Linear asset represents sequential physical assets (e.g., transmission line spans). Spatial containment represents physical location nesting. Logical grouping represents business-defined asset groups. System subsystem represents system decomposition.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy relationship record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute changes. Used for audit trail, change detection, and incremental data processing.']
  maintenance_responsibility_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the organizational unit or crew responsible for maintenance of assets in this hierarchical relationship. Used for work order routing, preventive maintenance scheduling, and O&M cost tracking. May differ from parent asset responsibility for shared or outsourced maintenance scenarios.']
  network_segment_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the top-level network segment or zone in the hierarchy (e.g., Northern Transmission Zone, Metro Distribution Area). Used for regulatory reporting by network segment, RAB valuation aggregation, and CAPEX/OPEX planning at the network level.']
  outage_impact_propagation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an outage of the parent asset automatically impacts the child asset in this hierarchical relationship. True means child is dependent on parent (e.g., feeder outage when substation is out). False means child can operate independently. Used for outage management, customer impact analysis, and SAIDI/SAIFI calculation.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership or allocation of the child asset to this parent relationship. Used for jointly-owned assets, shared facilities, or cost allocation scenarios where a single asset may have multiple parent relationships with fractional ownership. Value between 0.00 and 100.00. Null for full ownership (100%).']
  path varchar [note: 'Materialized path representation of the full hierarchical lineage from root to this relationship (e.g., /REGION_NORTH/ZONE_132KV/SUB_NORTHSIDE/BAY_03/). Enables efficient ancestor and descendant queries without recursive joins. Updated when hierarchy structure changes.']
  planning_area_code varchar [note: 'Integrated resource planning area or load zone identifier for the hierarchical relationship. Used for CAPEX planning, load forecasting aggregation, and IRP scenario modeling. Aligns with transmission planning zones and distribution planning areas.']
  rab_allocation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchical relationship is used for RAB valuation roll-up and regulatory rate base calculations. True means the relationship is included in RAB aggregation. False means the relationship is excluded (e.g., non-regulated assets, assets under construction). Critical for regulatory financial reporting and rate case preparation.']
  relationship_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special conditions, exceptions, or business context for this hierarchical relationship. May include reasons for non-standard configurations, temporary arrangements during capital projects, or regulatory reporting exceptions.']
  relationship_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the child asset among siblings under the same parent. Used for ordered hierarchies such as linear assets (transmission tower sequence along a line) or bay numbering within a substation. Null for unordered hierarchies.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hierarchical relationship. Active indicates the relationship is currently in effect. Inactive indicates the relationship has been terminated. Pending indicates a planned future relationship. Superseded indicates the relationship was replaced by a newer configuration. Planned indicates a relationship scheduled for future capital projects.']
  scada_aggregation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SCADA measurements from child assets are aggregated to the parent level in this hierarchy. True means real-time data is rolled up (e.g., substation total load from individual feeders). False means no aggregation. Used for EMS and DMS real-time monitoring and control.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record that is the authoritative source for this hierarchy relationship (e.g., VENTYX_ASSET_SUITE, SAP_PM, ARCGIS_UTILITY_NETWORK). Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and master data management. Critical for multi-system integration scenarios.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Primary key or unique identifier of this hierarchy relationship record in the source operational system. Used for bidirectional traceability, data synchronization, and issue resolution. Enables drill-back to source system for detailed investigation.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts for electrical connectivity hierarchies. Represents the voltage class of the hierarchical relationship (e.g., 500kV transmission, 132kV sub-transmission, 11kV distribution). Used for network topology analysis and voltage-based asset segmentation. Null for non-electrical hierarchies.']

  Note: 'Defines the parent-child structural hierarchy of utility assets, enabling roll-up from component level (e.g., circuit breaker trip coil) to equipment level (e.g., 132kV circuit breaker) to functional location (e.g., substation bay 3) to facility (e.g., Northside 132kV Substation) to network segment (e.g., Northern Transmission Zone). Each hierarchy record captures parent asset reference, child asset reference, hierarchy level, hierarchy type (functional location, linear asset, electrical connectivity), and effective date range. Supports CAPEX/OPEX cost roll-up, condition aggregation, RAB valuation at any hierarchy level, and regulatory reporting by network segment. Aligned with Ventyx Asset Suite functional location structure, SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance hierarchy, and IEC 61968 CIM asset model.'
}

Table asset.class {
  class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset class record. Primary key.']
