  standard_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Asset classes define standard material types for procurement planning and inventory stocking strategies. Utilities standardize materials by asset class for economies of scale and interoperability—crit']
  annual_om_cost_per_unit_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard annual Operations and Maintenance cost per asset unit in this class. Includes routine maintenance, inspections, and minor repairs. Used for OPEX budgeting and lifecycle cost analysis.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level functional category of the asset class within the utility value chain. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks and operational management systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: generation|transmission|distribution|metering|fleet|facility|substation|protection|scada — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the asset class within the utility\'s asset taxonomy. Used for regulatory reporting and financial accounting groupings.']
  asset_class_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the asset class (e.g., \'Combined Cycle Gas Turbine\', \'Power Transformer 230kV\', \'Underground Distribution Cable\').']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Specific technology or equipment type within the asset category (e.g., \'CCGT\', \'Wind Turbine\', \'Overhead Line\', \'Underground Cable\', \'Circuit Breaker\', \'Relay\').']
  capacity_rating_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Nameplate capacity rating in megawatts for generation assets or power handling capacity for transmission/distribution equipment. Null for non-power assets.']
  capital_expenditure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether acquisition of assets in this class is typically capitalized (true) or expensed as Operations and Maintenance (OPEX) (false) per accounting policies.']
  class_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this asset class definition in the utility\'s asset taxonomy. Only active classes are available for new asset registration.']
  condition_assessment_method varchar [note: 'Primary technique(s) used for condition-based health assessment of assets in this class. Supports predictive maintenance and asset replacement prioritization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual_inspection|diagnostic_testing|dissolved_gas_analysis|thermography|partial_discharge|vibration_analysis|oil_analysis — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset class record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Standard criticality classification for assets in this class based on impact to grid reliability, customer service, and safety. Influences maintenance prioritization and spare parts stocking.']
  cybersecurity_classification varchar [note: 'NERC Critical Infrastructure Protection (CIP) cybersecurity impact classification for assets in this class. Determines required cyber controls and monitoring.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Standard accounting depreciation method applied to assets in this class for financial reporting and Regulatory Asset Base (RAB) valuation.']
  distributed_energy_resource_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class are Distributed Energy Resources requiring integration with Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS) and Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this asset class definition became effective for use in asset registry and capital planning. Supports historical asset classification tracking.']
  environmental_impact_category varchar [note: 'Classification of potential environmental impact from operation or failure of assets in this class. Drives EPA compliance requirements and environmental monitoring protocols.']
  expected_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Standard expected service life in years for this asset class. Used for depreciation calculations, capital planning, and replacement forecasting.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this asset class definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active asset classes. Supports asset taxonomy version control.']
  failure_mode_profile varchar [note: 'Typical failure modes and mechanisms for assets in this class (e.g., \'insulation degradation\', \'mechanical wear\', \'corrosion\'). Informs reliability-centered maintenance strategies.']
  ferc_account_code varchar [note: 'FERC Uniform System of Accounts code for capitalization and depreciation reporting. Maps asset class to regulatory accounting categories.']
  gis_layer_name varchar [note: 'Standard GIS feature class or layer name in Esri ArcGIS Utility Network where assets of this class are spatially represented for network modeling and outage analysis.']
  grid_modernization_category varchar [note: 'Classification of assets in this class within utility grid modernization and smart grid investment programs. Used for capital planning and regulatory cost recovery filings.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class contain or use hazardous materials (e.g., PCBs in transformers, SF6 in switchgear) requiring special handling and disposal procedures.']
  iec_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Applicable IEC international standard(s) governing design, testing, and operation of assets in this class (e.g., \'IEC 60076\' for power transformers).']
  ieee_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Applicable IEEE standard(s) for equipment specifications, testing, and maintenance practices (e.g., \'IEEE C57.12.00\' for transformers).']
  inspection_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Standard interval in months between scheduled inspections for assets in this class. Null if inspection frequency is condition-based rather than time-based.']
  interoperability_standard varchar [note: 'Communication protocol or interoperability standard for assets in this class (e.g., \'DNP3\', \'IEC 61850\', \'Modbus\', \'IEEE 2030.5\'). Ensures integration with utility control systems.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this asset class record. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Standard maintenance approach for assets in this class. Drives work order scheduling, inspection intervals, and Operations and Maintenance (O&M) cost planning.']
  manufacturer_diversity_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether reliability standards or utility policy require multiple manufacturers for assets in this class to mitigate common-mode failure risk.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Industry-standard or utility-specific average operating hours between failures for assets in this class. Used for reliability modeling and spare parts planning.']
  mean_time_to_repair_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time in hours required to restore an asset in this class to service following a failure. Includes diagnosis, parts procurement, repair, and testing time.']
  nerc_asset_type varchar [note: 'NERC-defined asset type classification for bulk electric system reliability reporting and Critical Infrastructure Protection (CIP) compliance.']
  obsolescence_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of technology obsolescence and spare parts availability risk for assets in this class. High risk indicates limited vendor support or discontinued product lines.']
  rab_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class are eligible for inclusion in the Regulatory Asset Base for rate recovery and Return on Equity (ROE) calculation.']
  renewable_energy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class are renewable energy generation or Distributed Energy Resource (DER) equipment eligible for Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) generation and Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance.']
  replacement_lead_time_months varchar [note: 'Typical procurement and installation lead time in months for new assets in this class. Critical for capital project planning and long-lead equipment identification.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Primary safety hazard category for work on assets in this class. Determines required safety training, personal protective equipment (PPE), and work clearance procedures.']
  scada_monitoring_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class require real-time SCADA monitoring and integration with Energy Management System (EMS) or Distribution Management System (DMS).']
  spare_parts_stocking_strategy varchar [note: 'Standard inventory management approach for spare parts supporting assets in this class. Balances inventory carrying costs against outage risk and restoration time.']
  standard_unit_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Baseline unit acquisition cost in USD for assets in this class. Used for capital budgeting, business case analysis, and Regulatory Asset Base (RAB) valuation. Excludes installation and commissioning costs.']
  technology_generation varchar [note: 'Technology vintage or generation for assets in this class (e.g., \'Gen 1\', \'Gen 2\', \'Legacy\', \'Current\', \'Next-Gen\'). Tracks technology evolution and obsolescence risk.']
  voltage_class_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal voltage rating in kilovolts for electrical assets. Null for non-electrical assets (fleet, facilities). Determines applicable safety and maintenance protocols.']

  Note: 'Reference classification taxonomy for utility asset types defining asset class codes, asset category (generation, transmission, distribution, metering, fleet, facility), voltage class, technology type (CCGT, wind turbine, overhead line, underground cable, power transformer, circuit breaker, etc.), expected useful life, standard depreciation method, and applicable NERC/IEC/IEEE standards. Drives depreciation schedules, maintenance strategies, and regulatory reporting groupings.'
}

Table asset.work_order {
  work_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work order. Primary key for the work order entity.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Every work order is executed against a specific asset. This is the most fundamental FK in any EAM system — work orders without asset references are unroutable and uncosted.']
  assigned_crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal utility crew or work group assigned to execute this work order. May be null if work is assigned to an external contractor.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Work orders are frequently driven by regulatory compliance obligations (NERC CIP maintenance requirements, environmental permit conditions, safety inspection mandates). Compliance-driven maintenance s']
  contractor_worker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external contractor or service provider assigned to execute this work order. May be null if work is performed by internal crews.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which this work order cost is allocated. Used for departmental budgeting, OPEX vs CAPEX classification, and regulatory reporting.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Maintenance and repair work orders issued against DER assets for O&M cost tracking, warranty claim documentation, and reliability metrics (SAIDI/SAIFI). Critical for performance guarantee compliance i']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or manager responsible for approving and overseeing this work order. Used for approval workflow and accountability.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution or transmission circuit affected by this work order. Used for outage impact analysis and load transfer planning.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for financial posting of work order costs. Aligns with FERC Uniform System of Accounts for utility financial reporting.']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Work orders are created to perform interconnection-related construction (meter installation, service upgrades, POI modifications) and inspection support activities. Essential for tracking utility labo']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.maintenance_plan.maintenance_plan_id — Preventive work orders are generated from maintenance plans. This FK enables tracking of PM compliance rates and plan execution history.']
  material_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Work orders often trigger direct material procurement for non-stock items or emergency purchases. Links work order to originating PO for cost tracking, capex/opex classification, and regulatory cost r']
  parent_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent work order if this is a child or sub-work order. Used for complex projects broken into multiple work packages.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility asset (generation unit, transformer, transmission tower, distribution pole, switchgear, protection relay, substation equipment, fleet vehicle, or facility) that is the subject of this work order.']
  to_maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.maintenance_plan.maintenance_plan_id — Preventive maintenance work orders are auto-generated from maintenance plans. The FK traces which plan triggered the work order for compliance tracking and schedule adherence reporting.']
  transmission_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.line. Business justification: Maintenance work orders on transmission lines (conductor replacement, tower repair, insulator replacement, vegetation management) require direct linkage to the specific line for work planning, outage ']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_substation. Business justification: Work orders for transmission substation maintenance (transformer servicing, breaker testing, relay calibration) require direct linkage to the specific transmission substation for work scheduling, outa']
  work_maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance schedule or plan that generated this work order. Used for PM compliance tracking and maintenance strategy optimization.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when work was completed and asset returned to service. Used for outage duration reporting, SAIDI/SAIFI calculation, and maintenance cycle analysis.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when work commenced on site. Used for labor tracking, outage duration calculation, and performance analysis.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the work order for execution. Used for audit trail and authorization verification.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was approved for execution. Used for approval cycle time analysis and audit trail.']
  asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Every work order must reference the asset it is performed against. This is the most fundamental FK in asset management — without it, maintenance costs cannot be attributed to assets, and asset history']
  capex_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order represents capital expenditure (true) or operational expenditure (false). Capital work orders contribute to RAB valuation and depreciation schedules.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was administratively closed and finalized. Used for cycle time analysis and completion reporting.']
  closure_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason and manner of work order closure. Used for completion analysis, deferral tracking, and maintenance effectiveness metrics.']
  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative notes entered by the crew or supervisor upon work order completion, documenting work performed, findings, issues encountered, and recommendations for future maintenance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order record was first created in the system. Used for work order aging analysis and backlog tracking.']
  equipment_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of specialized equipment, tools, and machinery rental or usage allocated to this work order. Includes bucket trucks, cranes, diagnostic equipment, and specialized tooling.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'For corrective and emergency work orders, describes the observed failure or defect mode (e.g., insulation breakdown, mechanical wear, corrosion, overheating, control malfunction) that triggered the work order.']
  labor_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total labor cost including wages, benefits, and contractor fees for this work order. Used for O&M cost tracking and rate case justification.']
  labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total labor hours expended on this work order across all crew members and contractors. Used for OPEX tracking, productivity analysis, and cost allocation.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the work site in decimal degrees. Used for crew dispatch optimization, GIS integration, and spatial analysis.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the work site location including street address, nearest intersection, substation name, or geographic landmark to assist crew dispatch and navigation.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the work site in decimal degrees. Used for crew dispatch optimization, GIS integration, and spatial analysis.']
  material_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of materials, spare parts, and consumables used in this work order. Sourced from inventory withdrawals and purchase orders. Used for OPEX and CAPEX tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  outage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order requires a planned outage or de-energization of the asset or circuit. Used for outage coordination and customer notification.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level indicating urgency and resource allocation precedence. Critical for emergency outages, high for reliability-impacting work, medium for routine preventive maintenance, low for deferred work.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order must be reported to regulatory authorities (PUC, NERC, FERC) due to safety incidents, major equipment failures, or compliance events.']
  requester_contact varchar [note: 'Phone number or email address of the work order requester for follow-up communication and status updates.']
  requester_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or department that originated the work order request. Used for accountability and follow-up communication.']
  safety_permit_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the safety work permit or clearance authorization required before work can commence. Ensures compliance with OSHA and utility safety protocols.']
  scheduled_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date when work is scheduled to be completed. Used for outage window planning and service restoration commitments.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when work is scheduled to begin. Used for resource planning, outage coordination, and customer notification.']
  to_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Every work order is raised against a specific asset. This is the most fundamental transactional-to-master FK in any EAM system. Without it, work orders are orphaned.']
  total_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of the work order including labor, materials, equipment, and contractor fees. Used for OPEX reporting, RAB valuation, and regulatory rate case filings.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order is covered under manufacturer or contractor warranty. Used for cost recovery and warranty claim tracking.']
  work_order_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the work to be performed, including scope, specific tasks, safety considerations, and any special instructions for the crew or contractor.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the work order, used for tracking and communication across systems and with contractors.']
  work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the work order. Draft (created but not approved), approved (authorized for scheduling), scheduled (crew assigned and date set), in_progress (work underway), on_hold (temporarily suspended), completed (work finished pending closure), closed (administratively finalized), cancelled (work not performed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|scheduled|in_progress|on_hold|completed|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work order by maintenance strategy: preventive (scheduled maintenance), corrective (repair of known defect), emergency (unplanned urgent repair), inspection (condition assessment), capital_project (major upgrade or replacement), or condition_assessment (detailed asset evaluation).']

  Note: 'Core transactional record for all planned and unplanned maintenance activities on utility assets. Captures work order number, type (preventive, corrective, emergency, inspection, capital project), priority, originating asset, work description, assigned crew/contractor, scheduled start and completion dates, actual start and completion dates, labor hours, material costs, total O&M cost, work order status, safety permit references, and closure codes. Sourced from Ventyx Asset Suite Work Management module and SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) orders.'
}

Table asset.maintenance_plan {
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the maintenance plan record. Primary key.']
  asset_class_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_class.asset_class_id — Maintenance plans are often defined at the asset class level (e.g., all 132kV power transformers get DGA every 2 years). This FK enables class-level maintenance strategy inheritance.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Maintenance plans are defined for specific assets or asset classes. The asset-level FK enables automatic work order generation for the correct asset.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset class or equipment category for which this maintenance plan is defined. Used for template-based maintenance strategies applied across multiple assets of the same type.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Maintenance plans implement regulatory requirements (NERC PRC-005 protection system maintenance, FERC gas pipeline integrity management, environmental permit conditions). Linking plans to obligations ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which maintenance costs (labor, materials, contractors) are charged. Used for OPEX tracking, departmental budgeting, and RAB (Regulatory Asset Base) cost allocation.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Preventive maintenance schedules for DER equipment (inverter inspections, battery health checks, panel cleaning) required for warranty compliance, performance guarantee tracking in PPAs, and interconn']
  distribution_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.transformer. Business justification: Transformer maintenance plans (oil sampling, dissolved gas analysis, thermography, load tap changer servicing) are mandated by IEEE/NETA standards and insurance requirements. Links preventive maintena']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Preventive maintenance plans are organized by feeder for patrol inspections, vegetation management, reliability-centered maintenance programs, and regulatory compliance. Enables automated work order g']
  primary_maintenance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance planner or engineer responsible for managing this maintenance plan, including plan updates, work order review, and execution oversight.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific asset (generation unit, transformer, transmission tower, distribution pole, switchgear, protection relay, substation equipment, fleet vehicle, or facility) to which this maintenance plan applies. Links to the asset registry.']
  responsible_crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance crew or work center assigned to execute work orders generated by this plan. Used for resource capacity planning and crew scheduling.']
  service_supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: Recurring maintenance services (vegetation management, transformer oil analysis, substation cleaning) are often contracted. Links maintenance plan to service contract for cost forecasting, contractor ']
  standard_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Preventive maintenance plans specify standard materials (oils, filters, consumables) for bill-of-materials planning and auto-generation of material requisitions. Essential for MRP planning, inventory ']
  transmission_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.line. Business justification: Transmission line maintenance plans (patrol inspections, ROW clearing, structure inspections, conductor monitoring) are line-specific. Utilities schedule preventive maintenance by line for NERC compli']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_substation. Business justification: Preventive maintenance plans for substation equipment (transformer oil analysis, breaker testing, relay calibration, battery testing) are organized by substation. Utilities schedule and track maintena']
  auto_generate_work_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether work orders are automatically created by the system when the maintenance plan becomes due (True) or require manual initiation by a planner (False).']
  compliance_mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan is legally or regulatorily mandated (True) or discretionary for asset optimization (False). Mandatory plans cannot be deferred without regulatory approval.']
  condition_trigger_parameter varchar [note: 'For condition-based maintenance plans, the specific asset health parameter or SCADA measurement that triggers maintenance (e.g., oil_dielectric_strength, vibration_amplitude, temperature_differential, partial_discharge_level). Empty for time-based plans.']
  condition_trigger_threshold decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric threshold value for the condition trigger parameter. When the monitored parameter exceeds (or falls below) this threshold, maintenance is triggered. Empty for time-based plans.']
  condition_trigger_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the condition trigger threshold (e.g., kV, ppm, mm/s, degrees_celsius, percent). Empty for time-based plans.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this maintenance plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cycle_interval_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maintenance interval: calendar units (days, weeks, months, years), operational hours, operational cycles, or energy production (MWh for generation assets). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: days|weeks|months|years|hours|cycles|mwh — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cycle_interval_value varchar [note: 'Numeric value representing the maintenance interval (e.g., 12 for annual, 5 for five-year). Used in conjunction with cycle_interval_unit to calculate next due date.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or trigger pattern for maintenance execution. Time-based cycles are calendar-driven; on-condition cycles are triggered by asset health thresholds; on-demand cycles are manually initiated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|biennial|five_year|ten_year|on_condition|on_demand — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this maintenance plan is scheduled to expire or be retired. Nullable for ongoing plans. Used for asset decommissioning or maintenance strategy transitions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this maintenance plan becomes active and begins generating work orders. Used for phased rollout of new maintenance strategies or asset commissioning.']
  estimated_contractor_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of external contractor services required for maintenance execution, expressed in US dollars. Used for OPEX budgeting and procurement planning.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated total labor hours required to complete all tasks defined in the maintenance plan. Used for crew scheduling, OPEX forecasting, and resource capacity planning.']
  estimated_material_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost of spare parts, consumables, and materials required for maintenance execution, expressed in US dollars. Used for OPEX budgeting and inventory planning.']
  functional_location varchar [note: 'Hierarchical location identifier in the asset management system representing the physical or logical location of the asset (e.g., substation, plant, transmission line section). Used for geographic and organizational reporting.']
  last_execution_date date [note: 'Date when maintenance activities under this plan were last completed. Used to calculate the next due date for time-based and usage-based maintenance cycles.']
  last_execution_work_order_number varchar [note: 'Work order number of the most recent maintenance execution under this plan. Provides traceability to historical maintenance records and completion documentation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this maintenance plan record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the scheduled due date that the work order should be generated, allowing time for planning, material procurement, crew scheduling, and outage coordination.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'High-level maintenance philosophy applied to the asset: preventive (scheduled), predictive (condition monitoring), reliability-centered (RCM analysis-driven), risk-based (prioritized by failure impact), or run-to-failure (no proactive maintenance).']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the next maintenance execution is scheduled or required. System-calculated based on last execution date, cycle interval, and usage/condition triggers.']
  next_scheduled_work_order_number varchar [note: 'Work order number of the upcoming maintenance execution that has been generated but not yet completed. Empty if no future work order has been created.']
  outage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether execution of this maintenance plan requires taking the asset out of service (True) or can be performed while the asset is energized or in standby (False). Critical for outage coordination and reliability planning.']
  plan_asset_class varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_class.asset_class_id — Maintenance plans are typically defined at the asset class level (all 132kV transformers get the same PM plan). This FK enables plan-to-class association.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the maintenance plan objectives, scope, and special instructions. Provides context for planners and technicians executing the work.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the maintenance plan for human identification and reporting purposes.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the maintenance plan, externally visible and used in work order generation and asset management reporting.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance plan. Active plans generate work orders automatically; suspended plans are temporarily paused; inactive plans are disabled but retained for historical reference; retired plans are archived.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance strategy: time-based (calendar-driven), condition-based (triggered by asset condition thresholds), usage-based (driven by operational hours or cycles), predictive (AI/ML-driven forecasting), regulatory (compliance-mandated), or corrective (reactive repair).']
  planning_plant_code varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for maintenance planning and execution. Aligns with SAP PM planning plant structure for multi-site utility operations.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level for maintenance execution. Critical plans address safety or regulatory compliance; high priority plans prevent major outages; medium and low priority plans optimize asset performance and lifecycle cost.']
  regulatory_driver varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory standard, code, or compliance requirement that mandates this maintenance plan (e.g., NERC FAC-003 Transmission Vegetation Management, NERC PRC-005 Protection System Maintenance, OSHA 1910.269 Electric Power Generation). Empty if not compliance-driven.']
  safety_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific safety protocols, personal protective equipment (PPE), lockout/tagout (LOTO) procedures, or clearance requirements mandated for maintenance execution under this plan. Ensures OSHA 1910.269 compliance.']
  scheduling_window_days varchar [note: 'Allowable time window (in days) after the due date within which maintenance can be performed without being considered overdue. Provides scheduling flexibility for resource optimization and outage coordination.']
  task_list_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code to the detailed task list or work instruction document that defines the specific maintenance activities, inspection points, and procedures to be executed under this plan.']
  to_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Maintenance plans are assigned to specific assets or asset classes. The FK to asset_registry enables plan-to-asset resolution for work order generation.']
  usage_trigger_parameter varchar [note: 'For usage-based maintenance plans, the operational metric that triggers maintenance (e.g., operating hours for turbines, breaker operations for circuit breakers, MWh generated for generators). Empty for time-based and condition-based plans.']
  usage_trigger_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold value for the usage trigger parameter. When cumulative usage since last maintenance reaches this threshold, maintenance is triggered. Empty for time-based and condition-based plans.']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of work orders automatically generated by this maintenance plan. Determines work order routing, approval workflows, and cost accounting treatment in Ventyx Asset Suite and SAP PM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preventive_maintenance|predictive_maintenance|inspection|calibration|testing|overhaul|refurbishment — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Preventive and predictive maintenance strategy definition for an asset class or individual asset. Captures maintenance plan type (time-based, condition-based, usage-based), maintenance cycle (e.g., annual, 5-year), task list reference, required materials, estimated labor hours, regulatory compliance driver (e.g., NERC FAC-003, OSHA), last execution date, next due date, and plan active status. Drives automatic work order generation in Ventyx Asset Suite and SAP PM.'
}

Table asset.failure_event {
  failure_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset failure event record. Primary key.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Failure events must reference the failed asset for reliability analysis, MTBF calculations, and NERC reporting. Core FK for RCM analysis.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: DER equipment failures (inverter trips, battery faults, turbine blade damage) tracked for reliability analysis, warranty claims, RPS compliance reporting, and NERC event reporting. Required for root c']
  distribution_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.transformer. Business justification: Transformer failures are major reliability events requiring detailed tracking for warranty claims, manufacturer defect analysis, fleet health assessment, and replacement prioritization. Essential for ']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.dr_event. Business justification: Asset failures may be triggered by DR events (transformer overload during rapid load restoration, relay misoperation during curtailment) or cause DR event cancellation. Reliability engineers analyze c']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated outage event if the failure caused a customer outage. Links to outage management system.']
  failed_component_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Failure events identify specific failed components for reliability analysis, warranty claims, and defect tracking. Links failure to material master for supplier quality management, mean-time-between-f']
  failure_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective maintenance work order created to address the failure.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Failure events must be attributed to feeders for IEEE 1366 reliability reporting (SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI), root cause analysis, worst-performing feeder identification, and targeted reliability improvement ']
  primary_work_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.work_order.work_order_id — Failure events typically generate corrective work orders. Linking failure to repair enables cost-of-failure analysis and RCM.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset that experienced the failure. Links to the asset registry in Ventyx Asset Suite.']
  self_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.self_report. Business justification: Certain asset failures trigger mandatory self-reporting to NERC (bulk electric system events) or state regulators (major outages, safety incidents). Direct link enables regulatory incident reporting w']
  to_work_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.work_order.work_order_id — Failure events typically generate corrective work orders. The FK links the failure to its remediation for root cause analysis and cost tracking.']
  transmission_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.line. Business justification: Equipment failures on transmission lines must be tracked to the specific line for reliability analysis (SAIDI/SAIFI metrics), root cause analysis, NERC reporting, and maintenance optimization. Essenti']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_substation. Business justification: Substation equipment failures require linkage to the specific substation for reliability metrics, outage analysis, root cause investigation, and regulatory reporting. Utilities track failure history b']
  affected_component varchar [note: 'Specific component or subsystem of the asset that failed (e.g., transformer winding, circuit breaker mechanism).']
  asset_age_years decimal(6,2) [note: 'Age of the asset in years at the time of failure. Used for age-based reliability analysis.']
  caidi_contribution_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Contribution of this failure event to the overall CAIDI reliability metric for the reporting period.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Description of preventive measures and process improvements to prevent recurrence of similar failures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this failure event record was first created in the asset management system.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers who experienced service interruption due to this failure. Used for SAIFI calculation.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the failure was detected by SCADA, protection relays, or field personnel.']
  energy_not_supplied_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total energy in megawatt-hours that could not be delivered to customers due to the failure.']
  failure_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Every failure event must reference the failed asset. Critical for reliability analysis, MTBF calculations, and NERC reporting.']
  failure_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the failure per utility taxonomy.']
  failure_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the failure cause including contributing factors and conditions.']
  failure_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of the failure including repair labor, materials, equipment replacement, and lost revenue.']
  failure_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the failure cost amount.']
  failure_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or component within the asset where the failure occurred.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of failure categorizing the physical mechanism of breakdown.']
  failure_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the failure event, typically auto-generated by the asset management system.']
  failure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the failure event record in the asset management workflow.']
  failure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the asset failure occurred. Critical for reliability analysis and SAIDI/SAIFI calculations.']
  failure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the failure event by operational impact and planning status.']
  investigated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the engineer or team responsible for investigating the failure.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance performed on the asset prior to failure.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this failure event record was most recently modified.']
  load_interrupted_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of electrical load in megawatts that was interrupted by the failure event.']
  nerc_report_submitted_date date [note: 'Date when the NERC reliability report was submitted for this failure event, if applicable.']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this failure event meets NERC reporting thresholds for reliability compliance.']
  operating_hours_at_failure decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative operating hours of the asset at the time of failure. Used for MTBF analysis.']
  outage_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the forced outage in minutes from failure to restoration. Used for SAIDI calculation.']
  puc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this failure event must be reported to the state Public Utility Commission.']
  repair_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action performed to restore the asset to service.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that reported the failure event.']
  restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the asset was restored to normal operation following repair.']
  root_cause_analysis_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal root cause analysis has been completed for this failure event.']
  root_cause_analysis_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of root cause analysis findings including identified systemic issues and contributing factors.']
  saidi_contribution_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Contribution of this failure event to the overall SAIDI reliability metric for the reporting period.']
  saifi_contribution decimal(10,6) [note: 'Contribution of this failure event to the overall SAIFI reliability metric for the reporting period.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification based on safety risk, customer impact, and asset damage extent.']
  to_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Failure events record which asset failed. Without this FK, reliability analysis (MTBF, failure rate by asset class) is impossible.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time of failure (e.g., storm, lightning, high wind, ice) if weather-related.']

  Note: 'Records of asset failures, forced outages, and equipment breakdowns including failure date and time, failure mode (electrical, mechanical, thermal, insulation, corrosion, external damage), failure cause, affected asset, associated outage reference, repair action taken, root cause analysis findings, failure cost, and SAIDI/SAIFI impact contribution. Supports reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis and NERC reliability reporting.'
}

Table asset.capital_project {
  capital_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capital expenditure project. Primary key for the capital project entity.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Capital projects create or refurbish assets. Linking project to resulting asset enables CAPEX-to-RAB traceability and in-service date tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capital projects track costs by cost center for budget control, variance analysis, and FERC account allocation in rate base filings. New FK needed; removes denormalized cost_center string.']
  cpcn_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.cpcn_application. Business justification: Major capital projects require Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity approval from state regulators. Tracking which CPCN application authorized each project is essential for regulatory compl']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_substation. Business justification: Substation capital projects (transformer additions, bus upgrades, protection modernization, capacity expansion) are major utility investments requiring detailed tracking for FERC/PUC reporting, RAB in']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Capital projects for feeder upgrades (reconductoring, capacity expansion, automation, undergrounding) require feeder attribution for investment planning, rate case justification, RAB tracking, and rel']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.irp_scenario. Business justification: Capital projects are planned and justified based on specific IRP scenarios. Project timing, scope, and budget are driven by IRP load growth, capacity needs, and retirement assumptions. Standard utilit']
  master_supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: Large capital projects often use frame contracts for multi-year procurement of standardized equipment (transformers, poles, conductors). Links project to master supply agreement for pricing validation']
  primary_equipment_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Capital projects specify primary equipment being installed (transformer model, circuit breaker type) for asset registry creation and depreciation setup. Links project to material master for standardiz']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Capital projects allocate to profit centers for business unit P&L tracking, rate base segmentation by service territory, and regulatory jurisdiction reporting. New FK needed for financial consolidatio']
  project_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the project manager, linking to the workforce management system.']
  rate_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.rate_case. Business justification: Capital projects are justified and approved for cost recovery in rate cases. Utilities must track which rate case authorized capital investments for regulatory accounting, prudency reviews, and rate b']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the capital project completed all construction and commissioning activities.']
  actual_in_service_date date [note: 'Actual date when the asset or infrastructure resulting from the capital project was placed into operational service and began providing utility function.']
  actual_spend_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual capital expenditure incurred and paid to date for the project, including all invoiced and settled costs.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the capital project execution activities commenced.']
  approved_capex_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capital expenditure budget approved for the project by the appropriate governance authority. Represents the authorized spending limit.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this capital project record.']
  capacity_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Rated capacity in megawatts for generation or transmission projects. Represents the power handling capability of the infrastructure being built or upgraded.']
  committed_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of capital expenditure committed through purchase orders, contracts, and other binding obligations, but not yet paid.']
  contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contractor or engineering-procurement-construction (EPC) firm executing the capital project.']
  cpcn_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity issued by the state Public Utility Commission authorizing the capital project. Required for major utility infrastructure investments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capital project record was first created in the system.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting depreciation method to be applied to the assets resulting from the capital project once placed in service.']
  estimated_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life in years for the assets resulting from the capital project, used for depreciation and asset management planning.']
  ferc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capital project must be reported to FERC for regulatory oversight and rate case proceedings.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of capital funding for the project: internal cash reserves, debt financing, equity issuance, government grants, customer contributions, or rate recovery mechanisms.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capital project record was last updated or modified.']
  nerc_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capital project is subject to NERC reliability standards and compliance requirements.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for the capital project to complete all construction and commissioning activities.']
  planned_in_service_date date [note: 'Target date for the asset or infrastructure resulting from the capital project to be placed into operational service.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for the capital project to commence execution activities.']
  project_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Capital projects create or refurbish assets. The link from project to resulting asset(s) is required for CAPEX-to-RAB traceability and commissioning workflows.']
  project_category varchar [note: 'High-level asset category that the capital project targets: generation units, transmission infrastructure, distribution network, substation equipment, facilities, fleet vehicles, or IT infrastructure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: generation|transmission|distribution|substation|facilities|fleet|it_infrastructure — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  project_closeout_date date [note: 'Date when the capital project was formally closed out, including final cost reconciliation, asset capitalization, and administrative closure.']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the capital project scope, objectives, deliverables, and expected benefits.']
  project_justification varchar [note: 'Primary business driver or justification for the capital project: reliability improvement, capacity expansion, regulatory compliance, safety enhancement, operational efficiency, customer service improvement, or environmental requirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reliability|capacity|compliance|safety|efficiency|customer_service|environmental — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the capital project describing its purpose and scope.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the capital project, typically assigned by the ERP system. Used for cross-system reference and reporting.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Current execution phase of the capital project within the project delivery lifecycle.']
  project_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the capital project for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capital project indicating its phase in the project delivery workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: feasibility|design|approved|procurement|construction|commissioning|closeout|cancelled|on_hold — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capital project by its primary purpose: new asset construction, asset replacement, system upgrade, major refurbishment, regulatory compliance, or network augmentation.']
  rab_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the project capital expenditure that is eligible for inclusion in the Regulatory Asset Base. May be less than 100% if only portions are rate-recoverable.']
  rab_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capital expenditure from this project is eligible for inclusion in the Regulatory Asset Base for rate recovery purposes.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment level for the capital project considering technical, financial, regulatory, and execution risks.']
  service_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic service territory or region where the capital project assets will be located and provide utility service.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which the capital project record originated: SAP Project System, Ventyx Asset Suite, SAP S/4HANA, or manual entry.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the capital project record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  sponsoring_business_unit varchar [note: 'The business unit or division that owns and sponsors the capital project, responsible for funding and business case justification.']
  to_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Capital projects create or refurbish assets. The FK links project spend to the resulting asset for RAB capitalization and commissioning tracking.']
  variance_to_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated variance between actual spend to date and approved CAPEX budget. Positive values indicate over-budget, negative values indicate under-budget.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,2) [note: 'Primary voltage level in kilovolts for electrical infrastructure projects. Applicable to transmission, distribution, and substation projects.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure project record for new asset construction, major refurbishment, or network augmentation. Captures project number, project name, project type (new build, replacement, upgrade, compliance), sponsoring business unit, project manager, approved CAPEX budget, committed spend, actual spend to date, project phase (feasibility, design, procurement, construction, commissioning, closeout), planned in-service date, actual in-service date, CPCN reference, and RAB eligibility flag. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Project System (PS) and Ventyx Asset Suite.'
}

Table asset.depreciation_schedule {
  depreciation_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the depreciation schedule record. Primary key for the depreciation schedule entity.']
  asset_class_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_class.asset_class_id — Depreciation methods and useful life defaults are driven by asset class. This FK enables class-level depreciation policy inheritance.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Depreciation schedules are defined per asset. Without this FK, depreciation charges cannot be allocated to specific assets for RAB valuation and financial reporting.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: depreciation_schedule has both asset_registry_id (for asset-specific schedules) and asset_class_code (STRING) for class-level depreciation schedules. The asset_class_code should be normalized to FK as']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or system account that created this depreciation schedule record.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or system account that last modified this depreciation schedule record.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility asset (generation unit, transformer, transmission tower, distribution pole, switchgear, protection relay, substation equipment, fleet vehicle, or facility) to which this depreciation schedule applies.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (month) within the fiscal year to which this depreciation schedule record applies. Typically 1-12.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recognized to date since the asset was placed in service. Increases each period by the depreciation charge.']
  accumulated_depreciation_gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for the accumulated depreciation contra-asset account on the balance sheet.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was acquired or placed in service. Marks the start of the depreciation period.']
  annual_depreciation_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation expense allocated to the asset under the selected depreciation method. For straight-line: depreciable base divided by useful life years.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area or operating segment (e.g., generation, transmission, distribution, retail) to which this asset and its depreciation expense are allocated.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset as capital expenditure (CAPEX) or operational expenditure (OPEX) for financial planning and regulatory reporting purposes. CAPEX assets are capitalized and depreciated; OPEX items are expensed immediately.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Company code or legal entity to which this depreciation schedule belongs. Supports multi-entity utility holding companies.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which depreciation expense is allocated for internal management accounting and OPEX tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this depreciation schedule record was first created in the source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this depreciation schedule (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  depreciable_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount subject to depreciation, calculated as original cost minus salvage value. Used as the basis for annual depreciation charge calculation.']
  depreciation_area_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the depreciation area or ledger (e.g., book depreciation, tax depreciation, regulatory depreciation) to which this schedule applies. Utilities often maintain parallel depreciation schedules for different reporting purposes.']
  depreciation_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Depreciation schedules must reference the asset being depreciated. Required for RAB valuation and financial reporting.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date on which depreciation calculation ends for this asset. Typically the date the asset is fully depreciated, retired, or disposed of.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation expense over the asset\'s useful life. Straight-line is most common for utility assets; units of production may be used for generation equipment; regulatory composite may be mandated by PUC for rate-regulated assets.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which depreciation calculation begins for this asset. Typically the placed-in-service date or the first day of the following month.']
  depreciation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the depreciation schedule. Active indicates ongoing depreciation; fully depreciated indicates the asset has reached its salvage value; suspended indicates temporary halt; retired/disposed indicates asset is no longer in service.']
  depreciation_to_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Each depreciation schedule is assigned to a specific asset for financial reporting, RAB valuation, and SOX compliance. This is a mandatory FK for fixed asset accounting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this depreciation schedule record applies. Enables year-over-year tracking of depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Functional area (e.g., operations, maintenance, administration) to which depreciation expense is allocated for cost analysis.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which depreciation expense is posted. Typically a depreciation expense account in the income statement.']
  impairment_indicator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset has been identified as potentially impaired (carrying value may exceed recoverable amount). Triggers impairment testing under GAAP ASC 360.']
  last_depreciation_run_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent depreciation calculation run for this asset. Used to track depreciation processing and ensure timely financial close.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this depreciation schedule record was last modified in the source system.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying value of the asset on the balance sheet, calculated as original cost minus accumulated depreciation. Also known as book value or carrying amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this depreciation schedule, including adjustments, regulatory guidance, or special circumstances.']
  original_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original acquisition or construction cost of the asset, including capitalized installation, engineering, and AFUDC (Allowance for Funds Used During Construction). Basis for depreciation calculation.']
  placed_in_service_date date [note: 'Date the asset was placed into operational service and began generating revenue or providing utility function. Depreciation typically begins on this date.']
  rab_depreciation_treatment varchar [note: 'Treatment of this asset\'s depreciation in the Regulatory Asset Base (RAB) for rate-making purposes. Determines whether depreciation expense is recoverable through customer rates.']
  rab_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is included in the Regulatory Asset Base (RAB) for rate-making purposes. True if included; false if excluded.']
  regulatory_depreciation_rate_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Depreciation rate (as a percentage of original cost) approved by the Public Utility Commission (PUC) for rate-making purposes. May differ from book depreciation rate.']
  remaining_useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated remaining useful life of the asset in years as of the current reporting period. Calculated as original useful life minus elapsed time since placed in service.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. Subtracted from original cost to determine depreciable base.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this depreciation schedule record originated (e.g., SAP_S4HANA, VENTYX_ASSET_SUITE).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this depreciation schedule record in the source system. Enables traceability and reconciliation.']
  tax_depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Depreciation method used for tax reporting purposes. May differ from book depreciation method. MACRS (Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System) is common in the US.']
  tax_useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Useful life of the asset for tax depreciation purposes, as defined by tax regulations. May differ from book useful life.']
  useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected useful life of the asset in years, as determined by engineering assessment and regulatory guidance. Used to calculate annual depreciation charge under straight-line or declining balance methods.']

  Note: 'Asset depreciation schedule defining the financial depreciation profile for each utility asset or asset class. Captures depreciation method (straight-line, declining balance, units of production), useful life (years), salvage value, annual depreciation charge, accumulated depreciation, net book value, regulatory depreciation rate (as approved by PUC), and RAB depreciation treatment. Supports CAPEX/OPEX planning, regulatory asset base (RAB) valuation, and SOX-compliant financial reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Fixed Asset Accounting (FI-AA).'
}

Table asset.spare_parts_inventory {
  spare_parts_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the spare parts inventory record. Primary key for the spare parts inventory product.']
  asset_class_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_class.asset_class_id — Spare parts are stocked for specific asset classes (e.g., bushings for 132kV transformers). This FK enables parts applicability lookup during work order planning.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: spare_parts_inventory has asset_class_applicability (STRING) which appears to store which asset class(es) the spare part is applicable to. This should be normalized to FK asset_class_id → asset_class.']
  direct_load_control_device_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to demand.direct_load_control_device. Business justification: Spare parts inventory for DLC devices (communication modules, control relays, thermostats) supports DR program reliability. Warehouse management links spare parts to device models for reorder point ca']
  primary_spare_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the person who created the spare parts inventory record in the enterprise asset management system.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'Inventory classification based on value and usage frequency. A items are high-value or high-usage requiring tight control and frequent review, B items are moderate-value with standard controls, C items are low-value with minimal controls. Used for cycle counting frequency and inventory management prioritization.']
  bin_location varchar [note: 'Specific bin, shelf, or rack location within the stocking location where the material is stored. Supports warehouse picking and cycle counting operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spare parts inventory record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  criticality_classification varchar [note: 'Business criticality rating of the spare part based on impact to operations if unavailable. Critical items are essential for emergency restoration and grid reliability, high items support major assets, medium items are standard maintenance spares, and low items are non-essential consumables.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost valuation (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, GBP).']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous and requires special handling, storage, and disposal procedures per OSHA and EPA regulations. True if hazardous, False if non-hazardous.']
  last_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the material was most recently issued from inventory to a work order or maintenance activity. Used for inventory turnover analysis and obsolescence management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spare parts inventory record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date when the last physical cycle count or annual inventory audit was performed for this material at this stocking location. Supports inventory accuracy and SOX compliance requirements.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date when the material was most recently purchased via a purchase order. Used for inventory aging analysis and procurement pattern tracking.']
  last_purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Most recent purchase order number used to procure this material. Provides audit trail and reference for pricing, lead time, and supplier performance analysis.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of calendar days from purchase order placement to material receipt at the stocking location. Used for reorder point calculation and emergency procurement planning.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) or supplier who produces the spare part. Used for warranty tracking, quality management, and supplier performance analysis.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original equipment manufacturer\'s part number or catalog number for the spare part. Used for cross-referencing, procurement, and ensuring correct part substitution.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the spare part or consumable item including technical specifications, manufacturer details, and usage context.']
  material_group_code varchar [note: 'Hierarchical classification code grouping similar materials for procurement, reporting, and spend analysis. Examples include electrical components, mechanical parts, safety equipment, or consumables.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Unique material master identifier assigned to the spare part or consumable item in the enterprise asset management system. This is the externally-known business identifier used across procurement, inventory, and maintenance operations.']
  material_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material master record. Active materials are available for procurement and issue, inactive materials are temporarily blocked, obsolete materials are phased out, pending approval materials are under review, and discontinued materials are no longer supported by the manufacturer.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory item by usage category. Spare parts are replaceable components for assets, consumables are items used up during maintenance, critical spares are high-value low-turnover items essential for emergency restoration, tools are reusable equipment, safety equipment includes PPE and protective gear, and bulk materials are commodities purchased in volume.']
  maximum_stock_level decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum inventory quantity threshold to avoid overstocking and excess capital tied up in inventory. Used for inventory optimization and storage capacity planning.']
  minimum_stock_level decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum inventory quantity threshold below which replenishment should be triggered. Set based on lead time, usage rate, and criticality to ensure availability for planned and emergency maintenance.']
  obsolete_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material has been marked as obsolete and should no longer be procured or issued. True if obsolete, False if active. Obsolete materials may still have on-hand inventory for legacy asset support.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the organizational plant or business unit to which this inventory record belongs. Used for multi-site inventory management and cost allocation across generation, transmission, and distribution operations.']
  preferred_supplier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the preferred or primary supplier from whom this material should be procured. Links to vendor master data in the enterprise resource planning system.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(15,3) [note: 'Current physical inventory quantity available in stock at the stocking location. Measured in the material\'s unit of measure.']
  reorder_point decimal(15,3) [note: 'Inventory level at which a purchase requisition or replenishment order should be automatically generated. Calculated based on lead time demand plus safety stock.']
  serial_number_tracking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual units of this material are tracked by unique serial numbers for warranty, calibration, or asset lifecycle management. True if serial-tracked, False if batch-tracked or non-serialized.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Maximum number of months the material can be stored before it expires or degrades and must be disposed of. Applicable to consumables, lubricants, chemicals, and time-sensitive spare parts. Null for non-perishable items.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the operational system of record from which this inventory data originated. VENTYX for Ventyx Asset Suite Inventory module, SAP_MM for SAP S/4HANA Materials Management, SAP_PM for SAP Plant Maintenance, MANUAL for manually entered records.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or primary key of this inventory record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and bidirectional synchronization.']
  spare_part_applicability varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_class.asset_class_id — Spare parts are stocked for specific asset classes. This FK enables identification of which parts serve which equipment types for inventory optimization.']
  spare_parts_to_asset_class varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_class.asset_class_id — Spare parts are stocked for specific asset classes (e.g., transformer bushings for 132kV power transformers). This FK enables bill-of-materials resolution and critical spare identification.']
  stocking_location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the warehouse, storeroom, or facility where the spare part inventory is physically held. May reference central warehouses, regional depots, or substation storerooms.']
  stocking_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the warehouse or storeroom where the inventory is held.']
  storage_condition_code varchar [note: 'Required environmental storage conditions for the material. Ambient for standard warehouse conditions, climate controlled for temperature and humidity sensitive items, refrigerated for cold storage, outdoor for weather-resistant bulk materials, and hazmat storage for hazardous materials requiring special containment.']
  total_inventory_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the on-hand inventory calculated as quantity on hand multiplied by unit cost. Used for Regulatory Asset Base (RAB) valuation, financial reporting, and Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) / Operational Expenditure (OPEX) tracking.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit of measure for the material. Used for inventory valuation, work order costing, and Operations and Maintenance (O&M) expense tracking.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the material quantity is tracked and issued. EA=each, FT=feet, M=meters, KG=kilograms, LB=pounds, GAL=gallons, L=liters, BOX=box, ROLL=roll, SET=set. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|FT|M|KG|LB|GAL|L|BOX|ROLL|SET — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Accounting classification code used to assign inventory value to specific general ledger accounts for financial reporting. Links material inventory to chart of accounts for CAPEX, OPEX, and balance sheet reporting.']
  warranty_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of manufacturer warranty coverage from the date of purchase or installation. Used for warranty claim management and total cost of ownership analysis. Null if no warranty applies.']

  Note: 'Inventory management for spare parts, consumables, and critical spares held for utility asset maintenance and emergency restoration. Captures material master data (number, description, asset class applicability, criticality classification), stocking details (location, quantity on hand, min stock, reorder point, lead time), valuation (unit cost, total inventory value), and supplier references. Also records material issue and return transactions against work orders including issue date, quantity, receiving technician, and cost allocation. Supports O&M material cost tracking, inventory replenishment triggers, and critical spare availability assurance. Sourced from Ventyx Asset Suite Inventory module and SAP S/4HANA Materials Management (MM).'
}

Table asset.inspection {
  inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset inspection record. Primary key.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Asset inspections are mandated by specific regulatory obligations (FERC dam safety, NERC vegetation management, state PUC equipment inspection requirements). Direct link enables compliance reporting, ']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Regular inspections of DER installations for safety compliance, performance verification, and interconnection agreement requirements. Mandated by most jurisdictions for NEM programs, feed-in tariffs, ']
  distribution_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.transformer. Business justification: Transformer inspections (visual, oil sampling, dissolved gas analysis, thermography) are mandated by NFPA 70B, IEEE C57 standards, and insurance requirements. Links inspection results to specific tran']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Feeder inspections (patrol, infrared thermography, detailed climbing inspections) are regulatory requirements (NERC FAC standards, state PUC mandates) and reliability best practices. Links inspection ']
