  inspection_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervisor or engineer who reviewed and approved the inspection findings and recommendations.']
  inspection_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee, contractor, or assessor who performed the inspection.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the utility asset being inspected (generation unit, transmission tower, distribution pole, transformer, switchgear, protection relay, substation equipment, fleet vehicle, or facility).']
  transmission_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.line. Business justification: Transmission line inspections (aerial patrols, ground inspections, LiDAR surveys, drone inspections) are line-specific and required for NERC FAC standards compliance, vegetation management, and struct']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_substation. Business justification: Substation inspections (thermographic surveys, equipment condition assessments, oil sampling, visual inspections) are performed at specific substations and must be tracked for NERC compliance, mainten']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work order in Ventyx Asset Suite that authorized or scheduled this inspection activity.']
  ambient_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of inspection, relevant for temperature-sensitive diagnostic tests (e.g., thermography, oil analysis).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection record was reviewed and approved by a supervisor or engineer.']
  asset_load_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of rated capacity at which the asset was operating during the inspection, relevant for load-dependent diagnostic tests.']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative condition rating corresponding to the overall condition score.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection record was first created in the system.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the defects, anomalies, or conditions observed during the inspection.']
  defect_severity varchar [note: 'Highest severity classification of defects identified: critical (immediate safety/reliability risk), major (near-term failure risk), minor (long-term degradation), or informational (observation only).']
  defects_identified_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects, anomalies, or non-conformances identified during the inspection.']
  document_attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents (test reports, certificates, checklists) attached to the inspection record.']
  duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total time in hours required to complete the inspection, including travel, setup, testing, and documentation.']
  estimated_remediation_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in USD to remediate identified defects or perform recommended actions. Used for CAPEX/OPEX planning and maintenance budget forecasting.']
  frequency_months varchar [note: 'Interval in months between scheduled inspections for this asset, based on regulatory mandates, asset criticality, or condition-based maintenance strategy.']
  health_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite health index score (0-100 scale) calculated from inspection results, test data, and condition indicators. Feeds asset health analytics and predictive maintenance models.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date on which the inspection or condition assessment was performed.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'The technical method or technology used to perform the inspection or diagnostic test. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual|infrared_thermography|ultrasonic|vibration|oil_analysis|electrical_test|drone_aerial|manual_measurement|scada_online_monitoring — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the inspection, typically generated by the work management system or field inspection application.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection record in the work management system.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection activity: routine patrol (visual), post-storm assessment, vegetation management inspection, scheduled condition assessment, or advanced diagnostic test type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: routine_patrol|post_storm|vegetation_management|scheduled_condition_assessment|thermographic_survey|dissolved_gas_analysis|partial_discharge_test|oil_sampling|vibration_analysis|ultrasonic_testing — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the inspector or assessor who conducted the inspection.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection record was last updated or modified.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next inspection or condition assessment based on regulatory requirements, manufacturer recommendations, or condition-based maintenance triggers.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, context, or comments recorded by the inspector during the inspection.']
  overall_condition_score varchar [note: 'Overall asset condition rating on a 1-5 scale per IEEE/IEC standards (1=excellent, 2=good, 3=fair, 4=poor, 5=very poor/critical).']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the test result meets acceptance criteria or threshold limits defined by manufacturer specifications or industry standards.']
  photo_attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of photos or images attached to the inspection record for documentation purposes.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Recommended follow-up action based on inspection findings: repair, replace, continue monitoring, no action required, further investigation needed, or emergency repair.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection is mandated by regulatory requirements (e.g., NERC FAC-003 vegetation management, OSHA confined space entry, state PUC inspection mandates).']
  regulatory_standard_code varchar [note: 'Code or reference to the specific regulatory standard or requirement that mandates this inspection (e.g., NERC FAC-003, OSHA 1910.269, IEEE Std 62.2).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score based on condition assessment results, asset criticality, and consequence of failure. Used for risk-based maintenance prioritization.']
  scada_data_source_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inspection data was sourced from SCADA-integrated online monitoring systems (true) or from manual field inspection (false).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which the inspection data originated (Ventyx Asset Suite, SCADA, field inspection mobile app, OSIsoft PI, or GE PowerOn ADMS).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the inspection record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  test_result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the test result value (e.g., ppm, pC, mm/s, kV, degrees Celsius, ohms).']
  test_result_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric result of the diagnostic test or measurement (e.g., dissolved gas concentration in ppm, partial discharge magnitude in pC, vibration amplitude in mm/s, oil dielectric strength in kV).']
  threshold_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'The acceptance threshold or limit value against which the test result is compared to determine pass/fail status.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at the time of inspection, relevant for outdoor asset inspections (e.g., clear, rain, snow, high wind, extreme temperature).']

  Note: 'Periodic, event-driven, or regulatory-mandated evaluation of utility asset condition encompassing routine patrol inspections (visual, post-storm, vegetation management), scheduled condition assessments, and advanced diagnostic tests (thermographic survey, dissolved gas analysis, partial discharge test, oil sampling, vibration analysis, ultrasonic testing). Records inspection/assessment date, method/type, inspector or assessor identity, overall condition score (1-5 scale per IEEE/IEC standards), individual test results with pass/fail thresholds, defects identified with severity classification (critical, major, minor), recommended follow-up actions (repair, replace, monitor, no action), estimated remediation cost, next assessment due date, and regulatory compliance flags (e.g., NERC FAC-003 vegetation management, OSHA confined space). Feeds asset health indices, risk-based maintenance prioritization, reliability-centered maintenance analysis, and condition-based maintenance triggers. SSOT for all asset condition evaluation data. Sourced from Ventyx Asset Suite Condition Monitoring, field inspection mobile apps, and SCADA-integrated online monitoring systems.'
}

Table asset.valuation {
  valuation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset valuation record. Primary key for the asset valuation entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this valuation for regulatory submission or financial reporting. Provides audit trail for governance.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility asset being valued (generation unit, transmission tower, distribution pole, transformer, switchgear, protection relay, substation equipment, fleet vehicle, or facility).']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total depreciation accumulated on the asset from its in-service date to the valuation date. Calculated based on approved depreciation rates and asset age.']
  appraiser_certification_number varchar [note: 'Professional certification or license number of the appraiser, demonstrating qualifications to perform utility asset valuations. Required for regulatory acceptance.']
  appraiser_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or firm that performed the valuation. Required for independent appraisals and regulatory submissions.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this valuation was formally approved for use in regulatory filings or financial reporting.']
  basis varchar [note: 'The source or basis for the valuation: independent appraisal (external certified appraiser), internal estimate (utility staff assessment), regulatory formula (PUC-prescribed calculation), indexed cost (inflation-adjusted historical cost), engineering assessment (technical evaluation), or third party valuation (consultant report).']
  condition_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the physical condition of the asset at the valuation date, typically on a scale of 1-10 or 1-100. Influences depreciation adjustments and replacement prioritization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this valuation record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this valuation record. Typically USD for US utilities.']
  depreciation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The annual depreciation rate applied to this asset, expressed as a percentage. Approved by the PUC and used to calculate accumulated depreciation and remaining life.']
  economic_obsolescence_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Adjustment factor (0 to 1) representing the impact of external economic factors on asset value, such as changes in market demand, regulatory environment, or competitive conditions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this valuation ceases to be effective, typically when superseded by a new valuation or when the asset is retired. Null for current valuations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this valuation becomes effective for regulatory and financial reporting purposes. May differ from valuation date for prospective valuations.']
  fair_market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated price at which the asset would change hands between a willing buyer and willing seller in an arm\'s length transaction. Used for financial reporting and M&A activities.']
  functional_obsolescence_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Adjustment factor (0 to 1) representing the degree of functional obsolescence due to technological advances or changes in operational requirements. Applied to reduce replacement cost for outdated assets.']
  gross_replacement_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated cost to replace the asset with a new equivalent asset at current prices, before any depreciation. Represents the full reproduction cost of the asset in today\'s dollars.']
  inflation_adjustment_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'The cumulative inflation factor applied to convert historical asset costs to current replacement costs. Derived from the selected inflation index over the asset\'s life.']
  inflation_index_applied varchar [note: 'The inflation index used to trend historical costs to current replacement costs, such as Handy-Whitman Index, GDP deflator, or CPI. Specifies the index name and base year.']
  insurance_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value used for insurance coverage determination, representing the cost to replace the asset in the event of loss or damage. May include additional costs for expedited replacement and business interruption.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this valuation record was most recently updated. Tracks changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'The primary methodology used to determine the asset value: cost approach (replacement cost basis), market approach (comparable sales), income approach (discounted cash flows), hybrid (combination of methods), index trending (inflation-adjusted historical cost), or engineering estimate (technical assessment).']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current book value of the asset calculated as original cost minus accumulated depreciation. Represents the accounting value on the balance sheet.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, limitations, or special considerations related to this valuation. May include references to supporting documentation or regulatory guidance.']
  odrc_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The optimised depreciated replacement cost representing the current cost of replacing the asset with its modern equivalent, adjusted for technological improvements and optimised capacity. Critical for RAB determination in regulatory proceedings.']
  puc_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the PUC regulatory filing or rate case submission that includes this valuation. Critical for tracking regulatory approval and RAB determination.']
  rab_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset valuation is included in the Regulatory Asset Base for rate-making purposes. True if the asset is rate-base eligible and approved by the PUC.']
  rate_case_docket_number varchar [note: 'The official docket number assigned by the PUC for the rate case proceeding in which this valuation was submitted. Used for regulatory tracking and compliance.']
  remaining_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'The estimated number of years of useful service life remaining for the asset at the valuation date. Used for depreciation calculations and capital planning.']
  report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the detailed valuation report or appraisal document supporting this valuation record. Links to document management system.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life, representing the amount recoverable through sale or scrap. Used in depreciation calculations and retirement planning.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this valuation record originated, such as Ventyx Asset Suite, SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting, or external appraisal system.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this valuation record in the source system. Enables traceability and reconciliation with operational systems.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The date on which the asset valuation was performed or is effective. Critical for regulatory reporting and RAB determination.']
  valuation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the valuation record, typically assigned by the valuation process or external appraiser. Used for tracking and referencing in regulatory filings.']
  valuation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the valuation record: draft (initial preparation), under review (internal validation), approved (management sign-off), submitted to PUC (regulatory filing), accepted (PUC approval), rejected (requires revision), or superseded (replaced by newer valuation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|submitted_to_puc|accepted|rejected|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'The type or purpose of the valuation: RAB (Regulatory Asset Base) for rate case filings, insurance replacement cost for coverage determination, fair value for financial reporting, depreciated replacement cost for asset management, market value for disposal decisions, or liquidation value for decommissioning scenarios.']

  Note: 'Periodic valuation record for utility assets supporting RAB (Regulatory Asset Base) determination, insurance valuation, and fair value assessment. Captures valuation date, valuation type (RAB, insurance replacement cost, fair value, depreciated replacement cost), gross replacement cost, accumulated depreciation, optimised depreciated replacement cost (ODRC), valuation methodology, valuation basis (independent appraisal, internal estimate), appraiser reference, and PUC/regulatory submission reference. Critical for IRP and rate case filings.'
}

Table asset.retirement {
  retirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset retirement event record. Primary key for the asset retirement data product.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the capital project under which the asset retirement was executed, if part of a planned capital program. Links to capital project management for CAPEX tracking and program reporting.']
  disposal_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or contractor who performed the asset disposal or environmental remediation services. Links to vendor master for procurement and payment tracking.']
  distribution_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.transformer. Business justification: Transformer retirements must be tracked for RAB removal, gain/loss on disposal accounting, salvage value recovery, environmental compliance (PCB disposal), and replacement asset linkage. Required for ']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.environmental_permit. Business justification: Asset retirements must comply with environmental permits governing decommissioning, hazardous material disposal, and site remediation. Tracking which permit governs each retirement ensures environment']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.feeder. Business justification: Feeder retirements (decommissioning, reconfiguration, voltage conversion) require tracking for RAB removal, regulatory accounting (FERC Form 1), salvage value recovery, and system planning updates. Es']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.irp_scenario. Business justification: Asset retirements are planned within IRP scenarios. Retirement schedules are key IRP inputs and outputs. Tracking which IRP scenario drove a retirement decision is required for regulatory filings, rat']
  retirement_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the asset retirement transaction. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  retirement_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who initiated the asset retirement transaction. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  retirement_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset being retired. Links to the master asset registry for generation units, transmission towers, distribution poles, transformers, switchgear, protection relays, substation equipment, fleet vehicles, and facilities.']
  retirement_replacement_asset_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the new asset that replaced the retired asset, if applicable. Links to the master asset registry for tracking asset replacement programs and capital project execution.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order that executed the asset retirement and decommissioning activities. Links to Ventyx Asset Suite work management for labor, materials, and cost tracking.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the asset retirement was approved by management or regulatory authority. Part of the retirement lifecycle audit trail.']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'FERC or utility-specific asset class code for the retired asset. Used for financial reporting, depreciation studies, and regulatory rate case filings.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center to which retirement and decommissioning costs are charged. Used for Operations and Maintenance (O&M) cost tracking and financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this asset retirement record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  decommissioning_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost incurred to decommission and remove the asset from service, including labor, equipment, environmental remediation, and disposal fees. Expressed in the utility\'s functional currency.']
  depreciation_reserve_released decimal(18,2) [note: 'The accumulated depreciation reserve amount released upon asset retirement. Used for financial reconciliation and regulatory reporting.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'The date on which the physical disposal or transfer of the retired asset was completed. May differ from retirement date if asset remained in inventory or storage after retirement from service.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the retired asset was disposed of or removed from inventory. Values include scrap (recycled or scrapped for materials), sale (sold to third party), transfer (transferred to another entity or business unit), donation (donated to external organization), write-off (financial write-off with no physical disposal), environmental disposal (specialized disposal for hazardous materials such as PCB transformers or asbestos-containing equipment).']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the asset retirement and disposal process complied with all applicable environmental regulations. True indicates full compliance with EPA standards for hazardous material disposal (e.g., PCB transformers, asbestos-containing equipment, SF6 gas handling).']
  environmental_disposal_permit_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the environmental disposal permit or certification obtained for hazardous material disposal. Required for assets containing PCBs, asbestos, lead, or other regulated substances.']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'The functional location or network position where the retired asset was installed. Used for geographic and network topology analysis of retirement patterns.']
  gain_loss_on_disposal decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated financial gain or loss on asset disposal, computed as salvage value realized minus net book value at retirement minus decommissioning cost. Positive values indicate gain, negative values indicate loss.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which the retirement transaction is posted. Used for financial accounting and FERC Uniform System of Accounts compliance.']
  insurance_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for any insurance claim filed related to the asset retirement, particularly for catastrophic failure or damage events. Used for insurance recovery tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this asset retirement record was last modified. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  net_book_value_at_retirement decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net book value of the asset at the time of retirement, calculated as original cost less accumulated depreciation. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal and for regulatory asset base (RAB) adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special circumstances related to the asset retirement. Used for capturing context not covered by structured fields.']
  rab_removal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount removed from the regulatory asset base upon asset retirement. Used for rate case filings and regulatory reporting to state Public Utility Commissions (PUCs) and FERC.']
  rab_removal_date date [note: 'The date on which the asset was removed from the regulatory asset base for rate-making purposes. Critical for regulatory compliance and revenue requirement calculations. May differ from retirement date based on regulatory approval timing.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Categorical code indicating the primary reason for asset retirement. Values include end of life (asset reached expected useful life), catastrophic failure (unrecoverable damage or failure), obsolescence (technology or standards obsolete), regulatory mandate (compliance requirement), network reconfiguration (grid modernization or topology change), replacement (planned upgrade or substitution), performance degradation (no longer meets operational standards). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: end_of_life|catastrophic_failure|obsolescence|regulatory_mandate|network_reconfiguration|replacement|performance_degradation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the specific circumstances and business justification for the asset retirement. Provides context beyond the categorical reason code for audit, regulatory reporting, and asset management analysis.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which regulatory approval for asset retirement was granted by FERC, state PUC, or other governing body. Null if regulatory approval was not required.']
  regulatory_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether regulatory approval was required prior to asset retirement. True for assets subject to Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity (CPCN) or other regulatory oversight.']
  regulatory_notification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory notification or filing submitted to FERC, state PUC, or other governing body regarding the asset retirement. Required for major transmission and generation asset retirements.']
  retirement_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the retired asset by utility functional area. Used for portfolio analysis and regulatory reporting segmentation.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'The date on which the asset was officially retired from service. This is the principal business event timestamp for the retirement transaction and marks the end of the asset\'s operational lifecycle.']
  retirement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the retirement transaction. Externally-known reference number used in regulatory filings, financial reporting, and audit trails.']
  retirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the retirement transaction. Values include planned (scheduled for future retirement), in progress (decommissioning underway), completed (retirement and disposal finalized), cancelled (retirement cancelled), pending approval (awaiting regulatory or management approval).']
  salvage_value_realized decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual monetary value recovered from the disposal of the retired asset through sale, scrap, or other means. Expressed in the utility\'s functional currency. Used for financial reconciliation and variance analysis against estimated salvage value.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which the retirement record originated. Primary sources are SAP S/4HANA FI-AA for financial asset retirement and Ventyx Asset Suite for operational retirement tracking.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this retirement record in the source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and troubleshooting.']

  Note: 'Record of asset decommissioning, disposal, or retirement events. Captures retirement date, retirement reason (end of life, failure, regulatory, network reconfiguration, replacement), disposal method (scrap, sale, transfer, write-off), salvage value realised, environmental disposal compliance flag (e.g., PCB transformer disposal, asbestos), decommissioning cost, RAB removal date, and regulatory notification reference. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AA asset retirement and Ventyx Asset Suite.'
}

  warranty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset warranty record. Primary key for the asset warranty product.']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Manufacturer warranties on DER equipment (inverters 10-25 years, batteries 10 years, turbines 5-10 years) critical for O&M cost forecasting, replacement planning, and failure cost recovery. Required f']
  provider_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Warranties are contractual agreements with vendors (OEMs or third-party providers). Formalizes manufacturer_name into vendor master for warranty claim processing, vendor performance evaluation, and tr']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility asset covered by this warranty. Links to the asset registry for generation units, transformers, switchgear, protection relays, substation equipment, transmission towers, distribution poles, fleet vehicles, and facilities.']
  warranty_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the warranty record in the system. Used for audit trail and data governance.']
  warranty_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the warranty record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  claim_documentation_required varchar [note: 'Description of documentation required to support warranty claims, such as failure reports, inspection records, maintenance logs, photographs, or condition monitoring data from SCADA or OSIsoft PI.']
  claim_submission_method varchar [note: 'Preferred or required method for submitting warranty claims to the manufacturer. Online portals provide fastest processing; email and phone are common alternatives; fax and mail are legacy methods.']
  claims_approved_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims approved and settled to date. Used to calculate claim approval rate and supplier responsiveness metrics.']
  claims_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate claim amount in USD allowed during the coverage period. Nullable if unlimited claim value is permitted. Used for financial exposure management.']
  claims_limit_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of warranty claims allowed during the coverage period. Nullable if unlimited claims are permitted. Used to track claim utilization and prevent warranty abuse.']
  claims_rejected_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims rejected by the manufacturer to date. Used to identify warranty coverage disputes and supplier performance issues.']
  claims_submitted_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims submitted to date for this warranty agreement. Used to track claim utilization against claims limit and for supplier performance analysis.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the master service contract or extended warranty agreement. Used to link warranty records to contract management systems.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what components, failures, and conditions are covered under the warranty. Specifies inclusions such as parts, labor, travel, and exclusions such as normal wear, misuse, or environmental damage.']
  covered_components varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or detailed enumeration of specific asset components covered by the warranty. For transformers may include windings, bushings, tap changers; for generators may include rotor, stator, bearings, exciter.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warranty record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance reporting.']

  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digital warranty certificate, terms and conditions document, or service agreement. Provides quick access to warranty documentation for claim processing.']
  duration_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of warranty coverage expressed in months. Standard manufacturer warranties typically range from 12 to 60 months; extended warranties may exceed 120 months for critical utility assets.']
  end_date date [note: 'Expiration date when warranty coverage terminates. Used for proactive warranty expiry management and planning for post-warranty maintenance strategies. Nullable for lifetime warranties.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Detailed description of conditions, failures, or circumstances explicitly excluded from warranty coverage. Common exclusions include normal wear and tear, misuse, unauthorized modifications, environmental damage, and force majeure events.']
  labor_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether labor costs for repair or replacement are covered under the warranty. True if labor is included; false if parts-only warranty.']
  last_claim_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent warranty claim submission. Used to track claim frequency and identify assets with recurring failures.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warranty record was last updated. Audit trail field for tracking warranty status changes, claim updates, and data corrections.']
  maintenance_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of preventive maintenance activities required to maintain warranty validity. May include inspection frequency, lubrication schedules, condition monitoring, and use of OEM parts. Non-compliance may void warranty.']
  manufacturer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the manufacturer warranty contact. Business-confidential organizational contact information used for claim correspondence.']
  manufacturer_contact_name varchar [note: 'Primary contact person at the manufacturer or supplier for warranty administration and claim processing.']
  manufacturer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the manufacturer warranty contact. Business-confidential organizational contact information.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional warranty information, special terms, claim history notes, or internal comments. Used for warranty administration and knowledge management.']
  parts_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether replacement parts are covered under the warranty. True if parts are included; false if labor-only warranty.']
  performance_guarantee_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty includes performance guarantees such as minimum efficiency, capacity factor, or availability. Common for generation units and renewable energy assets under Power Purchase Agreements (PPA).']
  prorated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether warranty coverage or claim value is prorated based on asset age or usage. True if prorated; false if full coverage throughout warranty period.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the asset acquisition and warranty agreement. Links warranty to procurement records in SAP S/4HANA Materials Management (MM).']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the warranty was registered with the manufacturer. Some warranties require registration within a specified period after installation to activate coverage.']
  repair_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum time in hours for the manufacturer to complete repair or replacement under warranty. Service Level Agreement (SLA) metric impacting System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI) for critical assets.']
  response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum time in hours for the manufacturer to respond to a warranty claim or service request. Service Level Agreement (SLA) metric for critical utility infrastructure.']
  start_date date [note: 'Effective start date when warranty coverage begins. Typically aligned with asset commissioning date or installation date. Critical for determining coverage eligibility and expiry calculation.']
  total_claims_recovered_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount in USD recovered through approved warranty claims to date. Used for Operational Expenditure (OPEX) cost recovery tracking and supplier performance analysis.']
  total_warranty_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the warranty coverage in USD. Represents the maximum potential claim recovery or the insured value of covered components. Used for financial risk assessment and Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) planning.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty can be transferred to a new owner if the asset is sold or relocated. Relevant for fleet vehicles, mobile equipment, and assets subject to ownership changes.']
  travel_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether technician travel and mobilization costs are covered under the warranty. Particularly relevant for remote utility assets such as transmission towers and rural substations.']
  warranty_number varchar [note: 'Unique warranty certificate or agreement number issued by the manufacturer or supplier. Business identifier for external reference and claim submission.']
  warranty_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the warranty agreement. Active warranties are in force and eligible for claims; expired warranties have passed their coverage period; cancelled warranties were terminated early; suspended warranties are temporarily inactive; pending activation warranties are registered but not yet effective.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of warranty coverage. Manufacturer standard warranties are included with equipment purchase; extended warranties provide coverage beyond standard terms; service contracts cover ongoing maintenance; performance guarantees ensure operational specifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturer_standard|extended|service_contract|performance_guarantee|parts_only|labor_only|comprehensive — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Warranty records and claim management for utility assets and equipment. Covers manufacturer warranty terms, extended warranty agreements, warranty start and expiry dates, covered components, and supplier/OEM contact details. Also captures warranty claim transactions including claim date, failure description, claim status (submitted, acknowledged, accepted, rejected, settled), replacement or repair outcome, credit value, and resolution date. Enables proactive warranty expiry management, claim cost recovery tracking, and supplier performance analysis. Sourced from Ventyx Asset Suite and SAP S/4HANA MM contract management.'
}

Table asset.asset_risk_assessment {
  asset_risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset risk assessment record.']
  assessor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Risk assessments determine asset replacement priorities and capital investment decisions worth millions. Regulatory prudency reviews require documented qualified assessor credentials. This link provid']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Risk assessments evaluate specific assets. This FK is essential for risk-based maintenance prioritization and IRP investment justification.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Risk assessments evaluate compliance with regulatory standards (NERC TPL transmission planning, distribution reliability requirements, FERC dam safety). Linking assessments to obligations enables comp']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Risk scoring for DER assets considering technology obsolescence, performance degradation rates, grid integration challenges, and stranded asset risk. Supports investment planning, rate case prudency d']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.irp_scenario. Business justification: Risk assessments inform IRP scenario development. High-risk assets identified in assessments feed into IRP capacity expansion and replacement plans. Essential for risk-informed integrated resource pla']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility asset being assessed in the asset registry.']
  assessment_confidence_level varchar [note: 'Confidence level in the risk assessment results based on data quality, methodology rigor, and subject matter expert validation.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment was performed.']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and observations from the risk assessment including assumptions, limitations, and special considerations.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk assessment record.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of risk assessment performed indicating the trigger or purpose of the assessment.']
  benefit_cost_ratio decimal(7,2) [note: 'Cost-benefit ratio calculated as risk reduction benefit divided by intervention cost estimate, used for investment justification and IRP (Integrated Resource Plan) prioritization.']
  consequence_of_failure_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative consequence of failure rating band derived from the aggregate CoF score.']
  consequence_of_failure_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate consequence of failure score combining safety, environmental, reliability, financial, and regulatory impact categories.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk assessment record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality score for the input data used in the risk assessment including asset condition data, failure history, loading data, and environmental factors. Scale 0-100.']
  environmental_consequence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Impact score for environmental consequences of asset failure including emissions, spills, contamination, and EPA violations.']
  financial_consequence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Impact score for direct financial consequences including replacement cost, emergency repair cost, lost revenue, and regulatory penalties.']
  intervention_cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in USD to execute the recommended intervention including materials, labor, outage costs, and project management.']
  intervention_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for executing the recommended intervention based on risk-informed maintenance planning.']
  irp_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk assessment and recommended intervention are included in the utility\'s Integrated Resource Plan for PUC review and approval.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk assessment record was last modified in the lakehouse.']
  nerc_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk assessment is required for NERC CIP (Critical Infrastructure Protection) compliance or other NERC reliability standards.']
  net_present_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net present value in USD of the recommended intervention considering risk reduction benefits, intervention costs, and discount rate over the planning horizon.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic risk assessment based on asset criticality and risk band.']
  overall_risk_score decimal(7,2) [note: 'Composite risk score calculated as the product or weighted combination of probability of failure and consequence of failure scores per the selected risk methodology.']
  payback_period_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated payback period in years for the intervention investment based on annual risk reduction benefits.']
  probability_of_failure_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative probability of failure rating band derived from the quantitative PoF score.']
  probability_of_failure_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative probability of failure score based on asset age, condition, loading, and environmental factors. Scale typically 0-100 or 0-1 depending on methodology.']
  puc_prudency_evidence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk assessment serves as prudency review evidence for capital investment justification to the Public Utility Commission.']
  rab_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recommended intervention will impact the Regulatory Asset Base valuation and rate base calculations.']
  recommended_intervention varchar [note: 'Recommended risk mitigation intervention strategy based on the risk assessment results. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: replace|refurbish|upgrade|increase_monitoring|condition_assessment|accept_risk|no_action — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_consequence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Impact score for regulatory and compliance consequences including NERC CIP violations, FERC penalties, PUC sanctions, and service level agreement breaches.']
  reliability_consequence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Impact score for system reliability consequences measured by customer interruptions, SAIDI (System Average Interruption Duration Index), SAIFI (System Average Interruption Frequency Index), and load at risk.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment was reviewed and approved.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the senior engineer or manager who reviewed and approved the risk assessment.']
  risk_assessment_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Risk assessments evaluate specific assets. The FK is required for risk-based maintenance prioritization and investment planning.']
  risk_assessment_to_asset varchar [note: 'FK to asset.asset_registry.asset_registry_id — Risk assessments are performed on specific assets. The FK enables risk-ranked asset registers and investment prioritization for IRP submissions.']
  risk_band varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification band derived from the overall risk score used for prioritization and investment planning.']
  risk_methodology varchar [note: 'Industry-standard risk assessment methodology applied (risk matrix, Monte Carlo simulation, Weibull analysis, Fault Tree Analysis, Bow-Tie, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis).']
  risk_rank varchar [note: 'Relative ranking of this asset risk within the asset portfolio or asset class for prioritization purposes.']
  risk_reduction_benefit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary benefit in USD from risk reduction achieved by the recommended intervention, calculated as avoided consequence cost weighted by probability reduction.']
  safety_consequence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Impact score for public and worker safety consequences of asset failure including potential injuries, fatalities, and OSHA reportable incidents.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system that generated this risk assessment record (e.g., Ventyx Asset Suite Risk module, enterprise risk management system).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this risk assessment record in the source system for traceability and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Risk-based assessment record for utility assets evaluating probability of failure (PoF), consequence of failure (CoF), and overall asset risk score using industry-standard methodologies. Captures assessment date, risk methodology (risk matrix, Monte Carlo simulation, Weibull analysis), probability of failure rating based on age, condition, loading, and environment, consequence categories (safety, environment, reliability/SAIDI-SAIFI impact, financial, regulatory/compliance), overall risk score, risk band (critical, high, medium, low), recommended intervention (replace, refurbish, increase monitoring, accept risk), cost-benefit analysis of intervention, and risk-informed maintenance priority ranking. Supports risk-based asset management (RBAM) per ISO 55000, IRP investment justification, and PUC prudency review evidence. Sourced from Ventyx Asset Suite Risk module and enterprise risk management systems.'
}

Table asset.work_order_material_issue {
  work_order_material_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the material issue transaction record. Primary key for the association.']
  issued_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storeroom clerk, technician, or employee who physically issued the material from inventory. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  spare_parts_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the spare parts inventory item that was issued. Identifies which material master record was consumed.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the work order that consumed the material. Identifies which maintenance activity the spare part was issued against.']
  cost_center_charged varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which this material consumption cost is allocated. May differ from the work order cost center if material is charged to a different organizational unit.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for financial posting of the material cost. Aligns with chart of accounts for O&M expense or capital project accounting.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the material was issued from inventory to the work order. Used for inventory transaction history and cost period allocation.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the material issue transaction was recorded in the EAM system. Provides audit trail and chronological sequencing of material movements.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier for the material issue transaction in the EAM or ERP system (e.g., SAP Material Document, Maximo Issue Record). Provides audit trail and cross-system reconciliation.']
  quantity_issued decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the spare part or material issued from inventory to the work order. Measured in the unit of measure defined in the spare parts inventory record. This is the primary consumption metric.']
  quantity_returned decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the spare part or material returned to inventory after work order completion (unused or excess material). Used to adjust actual consumption and inventory levels.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Material reservation or requisition number that pre-allocated this inventory item to the work order. Links the issue transaction to the planning phase reservation record.']
  return_date date [note: 'Calendar date when unused or excess material was returned to inventory from the work order. Null if no return occurred.']
  unit_cost_at_issue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit at the time the material was issued. Captures the cost basis for work order cost allocation, which may differ from current inventory unit cost due to price changes over time.']
  work_order_line_number varchar [note: 'Line item sequence number within the work order material list. Used for organizing and referencing multiple material issues on the same work order.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Material Issue Transaction between work_order and spare_parts_inventory. It captures the consumption of spare parts, materials, and consumables against maintenance work orders in utility asset management operations. Each record links one work order to one spare part inventory item with transactional attributes including quantities issued and returned, costs at time of issue, timestamps, material document references, and cost allocation codes. This is a core operational entity in EAM/CMMS systems (Ventyx Asset Suite, SAP PM, Maximo) that enables material cost tracking, inventory consumption analysis, and work order cost rollup.. Existence Justification: In utility asset management operations, maintenance work orders consume multiple spare parts and materials (transformers, insulators, cables, tools, consumables), and each spare part in inventory is issued to multiple work orders over its lifecycle. Material issue transactions are actively created by storeroom clerks and technicians during work execution, with each transaction recording quantities, costs, timestamps, and document references. This is a recognized operational entity in EAM systems called Material Issue, Parts Consumption, or Material Document.'
}

Table asset.asset_program_enrollment {
  asset_program_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for asset_program_enrollment']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the demand response program']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this asset-program enrollment record. Primary key.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the enrolled asset in the asset registry']
  average_performance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling average of actual load reduction achieved versus committed enrolled capacity across all dispatch events for this asset-program combination. Used for performance tier assignment.']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'The specific baseline calculation methodology applied to this asset for this program. May differ from program default if asset has unique operational characteristics requiring customized M&V approach.']
  cumulative_dispatch_hours_ytd decimal(8,2) [note: 'Year-to-date cumulative hours this asset has been dispatched under this program. Used to monitor compliance with max_event_hours_per_year program limits.']
  enrolled_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The committed load reduction capacity in kilowatts for this specific asset under this DR program. Aggregated across all asset enrollments to calculate program-level enrolled_capacity_actual_mw.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when this specific asset was enrolled into this DR program. Used for eligibility calculations and incentive accrual start date.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Date when this asset\'s enrollment in this DR program ended or is scheduled to end. Null for active ongoing enrollments.']
  incentive_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The specific incentive payment rate ($/kW or $/kWh) applicable to this asset for this program. May vary from program base rate based on performance tier, asset criticality, or negotiated terms.']
  last_dispatch_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent DR event dispatch where this asset was called to reduce load under this program. Used for performance tracking and compliance monitoring.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this asset\'s participation in this DR program. Active indicates asset is eligible for dispatch. Suspended indicates temporary exclusion. Withdrawn indicates permanent removal. Pending indicates enrollment awaiting approval.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Performance classification for this asset within this program based on historical dispatch compliance and load reduction achievement. Used to determine incentive rates and dispatch priority.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for asset withdrawal from program (e.g., asset retired, performance non-compliance, program requirements changed, operational constraints). Populated when participation_status is Withdrawn.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment relationship between physical utility assets and demand response programs. It captures the operational participation of specific assets (substations, feeders, transformers) in DR programs, including capacity commitments, performance tracking, and settlement parameters. Each record links one asset to one DR program with attributes that exist only in the context of this enrollment relationship.. Existence Justification: In energy utility operations, physical distribution assets (substations, feeders, transformers) are enrolled in multiple demand response programs simultaneously to serve different grid reliability and market participation objectives. A single substation may participate in ISO capacity markets, emergency DR programs, economic dispatch programs, and voltage support programs concurrently. Each enrollment has distinct capacity commitments, performance metrics, baseline methodologies, and settlement rates that are managed independently by the demand response operations team.'
}

Table asset.asset_compliance {
  asset_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for asset_compliance']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this asset-obligation compliance tracking record. Primary key.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory compliance obligation being verified']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical utility asset being tracked for compliance']
  remediation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the remediation or mitigation plan if this asset-obligation pair is in non-compliance or conditional status.']
  applicability_determination_date date [note: 'Date when it was determined that this obligation applies to this asset, based on asset characteristics (voltage class, criticality, location) and obligation scope.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this specific asset with respect to this specific obligation. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset-obligation compliance tracking record was created in the system.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or location of compliance evidence documentation for this asset-obligation pair (e.g., inspection report ID, test result document, audit file reference). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  last_audit_finding varchar [note: 'Most recent audit finding specific to this asset\'s compliance with this obligation (pass, minor-finding, major-finding, observation).']
  last_compliance_verification_date date [note: 'Date when compliance of this asset with this obligation was most recently verified through inspection, testing, or audit. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  next_compliance_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date by which this asset must be verified for compliance with this obligation, based on the obligation\'s compliance frequency and asset-specific factors. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  non_compliance_risk_score varchar [note: 'Calculated risk score for this asset-obligation pair based on asset criticality, obligation VRF/VSL, and current compliance status. Used for audit prioritization.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or organizational unit responsible for ensuring this asset\'s compliance with this obligation. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance record was last updated, reflecting changes in status, verification dates, or responsible parties.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance tracking relationship between physical utility assets and regulatory obligations. It captures the compliance status, verification history, and evidence management for each asset-obligation pair. Each record links one asset to one regulatory obligation with attributes that track compliance verification dates, responsible parties, and audit evidence specific to that asset\'s compliance with that particular obligation.. Existence Justification: In energy utilities operations, individual physical assets (transformers, transmission towers, substations, protection relays) are each subject to multiple regulatory obligations from different governing bodies (NERC CIP cybersecurity standards, EPA emissions limits, OSHA safety mandates, FERC reliability requirements). Simultaneously, each regulatory obligation applies to many assets across the utility\'s asset base based on asset characteristics like voltage class, criticality score, and operational function. Compliance teams actively manage this many-to-many relationship by tracking verification dates, collecting evidence, assigning responsible parties, and preparing for audits on each asset-obligation pair.'
}

Table asset.ppa_allocation {
  ppa_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this asset-PPA allocation record. Primary key.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical generation asset being allocated to this PPA contract']
  renewable_ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the renewable PPA contract receiving this asset allocation']
  settlement_meter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue-quality meter used to measure generation from this asset for settlement under this PPA contract. Critical for multi-PPA scenarios where different meters or meter channels track output allocated to different contracts.']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing business context for this allocation, such as contract amendment references, reallocation justifications, or special settlement terms applicable to this asset-PPA pairing.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the asset\'s generation capacity or output allocated to this specific PPA contract. Allows partial allocation where a single asset serves multiple contracts or where portfolio PPAs aggregate multiple assets. Sum of allocation percentages across all PPAs for a given asset may equal or be less than 100%.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this asset-PPA allocation: active (currently generating and settling), pending (approved but not yet effective), suspended (temporarily halted due to maintenance or curtailment), terminated (ended early), expired (reached contract_end_date). Drives settlement processing and REC transfer eligibility.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date when this asset\'s allocation to this PPA contract terminates. May differ from the PPA contract\'s overall effective_end_date if assets are removed from the contract early due to retirement, reallocation, or contract amendment.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date when this specific asset began contributing generation to this PPA contract. May differ from the PPA contract\'s overall effective_start_date if assets are added to the contract over time or if the asset was commissioned after contract inception.']
  contracted_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Portion of the asset\'s rated capacity (in MW) that is contractually committed to this specific PPA. For portfolio PPAs, this represents the asset\'s contribution to the total contracted capacity. Used for capacity payment calculations and contract compliance tracking.']
  contracted_price_per_mwh decimal(12,4) [note: 'Energy price in USD per MWh applicable to this specific asset-PPA pairing. May differ from the PPA\'s base_price_per_mwh if the contract has asset-specific pricing tiers, vintage-based pricing, or technology-specific rates. Used for settlement calculations.']
  rec_allocation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) generated by this asset under this allocation are transferred to the utility per the PPA\'s rec_ownership terms. Supports scenarios where energy and RECs are sold separately or where only a portion of an asset\'s RECs are allocated to a specific PPA.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the allocation relationship between physical generation assets and renewable power purchase agreement contracts. It captures the contractual assignment of specific assets (or portions thereof) to PPA contracts for renewable attribute tracking, settlement, and RPS compliance. Each record links one asset to one PPA contract with allocation percentage, contracted capacity, pricing terms, and effective dates that exist only in the context of this specific asset-contract pairing.. Existence Justification: In utility-scale renewable energy procurement, portfolio PPAs routinely allocate generation from multiple physical assets (e.g., individual wind turbines, solar array sections, or entire generation facilities) to a single contract to aggregate capacity and meet volume commitments. Conversely, a single asset can participate in multiple PPAs simultaneously through time-slicing (baseload energy sold to one contract, renewable attributes to another), partial capacity allocation, or sequential contract assignments. The allocation relationship itself carries critical operational data including allocation percentage, asset-specific pricing, effective dates, and settlement meter assignments that belong to neither the asset nor the PPA alone.'
}

Table asset.impact_assessment {
  impact_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the asset_impact_assessment association']
  analyzed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility engineer who performed the technical impact analysis for this asset-application combination. Used for accountability, quality assurance, and workload tracking.']
  cluster_study_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cluster study group that includes this asset impact assessment, if applicable. Under FERC Order 2023, multiple interconnection applications that impact the same assets may be grouped into cluster studies for coordinated analysis and cost allocation.']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the interconnection application being reviewed']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the utility asset being impacted by the interconnection application']
  affected_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of asset capacity in kilowatts that is affected or consumed by the proposed DER interconnection. Used to determine hosting capacity impacts and whether the asset has sufficient remaining capacity for additional interconnection applications in the queue.']
  analysis_date date [note: 'Date when the technical impact analysis for this asset-application combination was performed by the utility review engineer. Used to track review timeline compliance with regulatory deadlines and to determine whether re-analysis is required if grid conditions change.']
  analysis_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the impact analysis for this asset-application combination. Tracks progression through the technical review workflow and identifies when supplemental studies are required.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in USD to upgrade or replace this asset to accommodate the proposed DER interconnection. Used for cost allocation across applications in cluster studies and to determine applicant financial responsibility under interconnection agreements.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Engineering assessment of the severity of impact the proposed DER has on this asset. Determines review tier escalation, supplemental study requirements, and priority for upgrade planning.']
  impact_type varchar [note: 'Category of technical impact the proposed DER has on this specific asset (thermal loading, voltage regulation, protection coordination, fault current contribution, stability, power quality). Determines the type of engineering analysis required and potential mitigation measures.']
  mitigation_required varchar [note: 'Description of mitigation measures required to address the impact on this asset, such as protection relay upgrades, voltage regulator adjustments, transformer replacement, or operational restrictions on DER output.']
  upgrade_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset requires upgrade or replacement to accommodate the proposed DER interconnection. Drives cost allocation and determines whether the application triggers network upgrade costs that may be shared across multiple applications in a cluster study.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the technical impact assessment between a utility asset and an interconnection application. It captures the engineering analysis of how a proposed distributed energy resource (DER) interconnection affects specific grid assets, including thermal loading, voltage regulation, protection coordination, and required upgrades. Each record links one asset to one interconnection application with impact-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship. This is the foundation for cluster study formation, cost allocation, and queue management under FERC Order 2023 and state interconnection rules.. Existence Justification: In utility interconnection operations, a single interconnection application routinely impacts multiple grid assets (transformer, feeder, substation, protection devices) that must be analyzed for thermal, voltage, and protection impacts. Conversely, a single asset can be affected by multiple pending interconnection applications in the queue, requiring coordinated analysis to determine cumulative impacts and shared upgrade costs. Utilities actively manage these asset-application impact assessments as part of the technical review process, tracking impact type, severity, upgrade requirements, and cost allocation for each asset-application combination.'
}

Table asset.der_connection {
  der_connection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this specific connection between a utility asset and a DER system. Primary key for the association.']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the distributed energy resource system that is physically connected to the utility asset']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the utility asset (transformer, meter, disconnect switch, protection relay) that serves as a connection point for the DER system']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when this specific DER-asset connection was commissioned and placed into operational service. Marks the point when the physical connection was energized and the DER system began operating in coordination with the utility asset.']
  connection_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum power capacity in kilowatts approved for this specific DER-asset connection point. May be lower than the DER system nameplate capacity if the utility asset has capacity constraints or if multiple DER systems share the same asset.']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical connection point type between the DER system and the utility asset. Identifies the specific electrical interface and connection configuration used for the interconnection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this connection record was created in the system.']
  disconnect_switch_accessible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the utility-accessible disconnect switch for this DER-asset connection is readily accessible to utility crews for maintenance and emergency operations. Critical for outage response and safety procedures.']
  export_limit_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum power export limit in kilowatts allowed for this specific DER-asset connection. May be constrained by asset capacity, feeder capacity, or interconnection agreement terms. Null if no export is permitted.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inspection or testing of this DER-asset connection point. Used for maintenance tracking and compliance verification.']
  metering_configuration varchar [note: 'Specification of the metering arrangement and measurement configuration for this specific DER-asset connection. Defines how energy flow is measured and recorded for billing, settlement, and grid monitoring purposes.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection or testing of this DER-asset connection based on utility maintenance standards and regulatory requirements.']
  operating_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this specific DER-asset connection. Indicates whether the connection is actively energized, temporarily suspended, under maintenance, or permanently decommissioned. Used for outage management and operational coordination.']
  poi_voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts at the specific point where the DER system connects to this utility asset. Critical for protection coordination and equipment specification.']
  protection_scheme varchar [note: 'Description of the protection coordination scheme and relay settings applied to this specific DER-asset connection. Captures the protective relay configuration, trip settings, and coordination with upstream/downstream protection devices to ensure safe operation and fault isolation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this connection record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the physical electrical connection between a utility asset and a distributed energy resource (DER) system. It captures the technical configuration, protection coordination, metering arrangement, and operational status of each connection point. Each record links one asset_registry entry to one der_system with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific interconnection configuration. Essential for distribution operations, protection coordination, outage management, and maintenance planning of DER-integrated grid infrastructure.. Existence Justification: In utility distribution operations, a single DER system (solar PV, battery storage, EV charger) physically connects to multiple utility assets at the point of interconnection - typically a service transformer, revenue meter, utility-accessible disconnect switch, and protection relay. Conversely, a single utility asset such as a distribution transformer or secondary service panel can serve multiple DER systems installed at different customer premises or even multiple DER systems at the same location. Each connection has unique technical attributes including metering configuration, protection coordination settings, connection capacity limits, and operational status that belong to the specific DER-asset pairing, not to either entity alone.'
}

Table asset.project_permit {
  project_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this project-permit compliance relationship. Primary key.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the capital project requiring environmental permit compliance']
  compliance_environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the environmental permit governing this project']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the capital project with respect to this specific permit and condition. Tracks whether the project meets permit requirements.']
  environmental_permit_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for environmental permits required for the capital project, issued by EPA or state environmental agencies. [Moved from capital_project: This attribute in capital_project appears to reference a single permit, but in reality capital projects require multiple permits. This attribute should be removed from capital_project as the relationship is properly modeled through the project_permit association, which can link to multiple permits and track permit-specific compliance details.]']
  mitigation_measure_description varchar [note: 'Description of specific mitigation measures, controls, or corrective actions implemented for this project to comply with this permit condition. May include engineering controls, monitoring protocols, or operational restrictions.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next regulatory inspection or compliance verification for this project-permit combination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or documentation references related to permit compliance for this specific project.']
  permit_application_date date [note: 'Date when the permit application was submitted for this specific project. Relevant for project-specific permits or permit modifications required for the capital project.']
  permit_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the permit was approved or modified to cover this capital project. Critical for tracking project schedule dependencies on regulatory approvals.']
  permit_condition_number varchar [note: 'Specific permit condition number or reference that applies to this capital project. Each permit may have multiple conditions, and projects must track compliance with specific applicable conditions.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or team responsible for ensuring compliance with this permit condition for this specific project. May be project manager, environmental coordinator, or compliance officer.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when compliance with this permit condition was last verified by environmental compliance staff or regulatory inspectors.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the regulatory compliance relationship between capital projects and environmental permits. It captures permit-specific compliance tracking, verification activities, and mitigation measures required for each project-permit combination. Each record links one capital project to one environmental permit with attributes that exist only in the context of this regulatory compliance relationship.. Existence Justification: Capital projects in energy utilities require multiple environmental permits (EPA Title V air permits, NPDES water discharge permits, wetlands permits, endangered species authorizations) depending on project scope and location. Each permit applies to multiple projects over its validity period. Environmental compliance officers actively manage project-permit relationships by tracking compliance with specific permit conditions, conducting verification activities, documenting mitigation measures, and coordinating regulatory inspections for each project-permit combination.'
}

Table renewable.der_registry {
  der_registry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DER registration record. Primary key for the DER registry.']
  commissioning_technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: DER commissioning requires licensed electricians to perform final inspections, sign-off, and permission-to-operate certification. Tracks responsible technician for regulatory compliance, warranty clai']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: DERs operate within specific control areas for balancing authority coordination, ACE calculation, frequency regulation, and interchange scheduling. Control area operators need visibility of all DER ca']
