Should be modeled as a service_contract_type=\'epc_contractor\' record in the association, allowing multiple contractors to be tracked if the asset had phased construction or multiple EPC firms involved.]']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent service performed by this vendor on this battery asset. Used to track service frequency and schedule preventive maintenance.']
  next_scheduled_service_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled preventive maintenance or inspection by this vendor for this battery asset. Used for service planning and outage coordination.']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Vendor performance score specific to this battery asset, based on SLA compliance, response time, service quality, and asset availability impact. Used for vendor management and contract renewal decisions.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the vendor contact responsible for this battery asset. Used for service request submission and SLA tracking.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor representative responsible for servicing this specific battery asset. May differ from the vendor master contact for large vendors serving multiple sites.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number for the vendor contact responsible for this battery asset. Used for emergency response and service coordination.']
  response_time_sla_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contractually committed response time in hours for this vendor to respond to service requests for this battery asset. SLA varies by vendor type and asset criticality.']
  service_contract_type varchar [note: 'Type of service contract this vendor provides for the battery asset. Identifies the vendor\'s role in the multi-vendor service ecosystem (battery OEM for cell warranty, inverter supplier for power conversion equipment, BMS provider for control systems, O&M contractor for ongoing operations).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service agreement record was last updated.']
  warranty_coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of warranty coverage this vendor provides for the battery asset. Battery OEMs typically provide capacity guarantees (e.g., 80% capacity after 10 years), while other vendors may provide equipment replacement or performance guarantees.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the service contract relationship between battery storage assets and their vendors. Each battery storage system requires multiple vendors for different service types (battery OEM, inverter supplier, BMS provider, O&M contractor), and each vendor provides services to multiple battery assets. Each record captures vendor-specific contract terms, warranty coverage, and SLA commitments that exist only in the context of this specific asset-vendor relationship.. Existence Justification: In energy utilities operations, battery storage assets require multiple specialized vendors simultaneously: the battery cell OEM provides capacity warranty, the inverter supplier maintains power conversion equipment, the BMS provider supports control systems, and O&M contractors handle daily operations. Each vendor relationship has distinct contract terms, SLA commitments, warranty coverage types, and performance metrics. This is an operational M:N relationship that asset managers actively maintain, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table renewable.der_service_assignment {
  der_service_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the der_service_assignment association']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the field crew performing the service']
  der_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the DER installation being serviced']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order authorizing this service assignment. Links to work management system for cost tracking and compliance documentation.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when the crew was assigned to service this DER site. Corresponds to maintenance_assignment_date from detection data.']
  compliance_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service includes regulatory compliance inspection activities (IEEE 1547 testing, interconnection agreement verification, safety certification).']
  crew_specialization_match_score varchar [note: 'Calculated score (0-100) indicating how well the crew\'s certifications and equipment match the DER technology requirements. Solar PV crews score higher for solar sites, storage-certified crews for battery systems. Used for optimal crew assignment.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Date when the crew most recently completed service at this DER site. Used to calculate next service due date and track service history. Identified in detection data.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service activity performed. Preventive maintenance includes scheduled inspections and routine servicing. Corrective maintenance addresses identified issues. Emergency repair for unplanned outages. Storm restoration for weather-related damage. Identified in detection data.']
  next_scheduled_service_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next service visit by this crew to this DER site. Used for maintenance schedule optimization and crew dispatch planning. Identified in detection data.']
  service_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost for this service assignment including labor, equipment, materials, and travel. Used for DER maintenance cost tracking and budget management.']
  service_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual or estimated duration in hours for the service activity. Used for crew utilization tracking and labor cost allocation.']
  service_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval in days between service visits for this DER-crew pairing. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling optimization. Identified in detection data.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the service assignment. Tracks lifecycle from scheduling through completion.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the service assignment relationship between distributed energy resources and field crews. It captures the operational assignment of crews to DER sites for maintenance, inspection, emergency repair, and storm restoration activities. Each record links one DER installation to one crew service event with attributes that track service history, crew specialization matching, and maintenance schedule optimization. SSOT for DER field service history and crew utilization tracking.. Existence Justification: In utility DER operations, field crews are assigned to service portfolios of distributed generation sites on rotating schedules driven by maintenance type, technology specialization, and geographic proximity. A single DER installation receives service from multiple crews over its lifecycle (preventive maintenance crews, emergency repair crews, storm restoration crews, commissioning support crews). Each crew services many DER sites across their service territory. The business actively manages these service assignments with work orders, tracks service history per DER asset for compliance, and optimizes crew-to-DER matching based on technology specialization (solar vs storage vs wind).'
}

Table renewable.ppa_vendor_participation {
  ppa_vendor_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this vendor participation record in a PPA contract. Primary key for the association.']
  renewable_ppa_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the renewable energy PPA contract. Each participation record is associated with exactly one PPA contract.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor master record. Each participation record is associated with exactly one vendor playing a specific role in the PPA.']
  contact_email_for_role varchar [note: 'Email address of the role-specific contact person for this PPA contract. Used for contract-specific communications, settlement inquiries, and operational coordination for this vendor\'s role in this PPA.']
  contact_person_for_role varchar [note: 'Name of the specific individual at the vendor organization who serves as the primary contact for this PPA contract and role. May differ from the vendor\'s primary_contact_name in the vendor master record because large vendors assign different account managers to different contracts or roles.']
  contact_phone_for_role varchar [note: 'Phone number of the role-specific contact person for this PPA contract. Used for urgent operational issues, curtailment notifications, and real-time coordination specific to this vendor\'s role in this PPA.']
  contract_role varchar [note: 'The specific role this vendor plays in the PPA contract. Counterparty = the IPP/generator, scheduling_coordinator = entity managing energy schedules with ISO/RTO, transmission_provider = entity providing transmission service, rec_administrator = entity managing REC tracking and transfer, credit_support_provider = entity providing letters of credit or guarantees, metering_agent = entity responsible for meter data, settlement_agent = entity calculating and processing payments. A single PPA typically involves 3-6 vendors in different roles.']
  credit_support_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of credit support (letter of credit, cash collateral, parent guarantee) required from or provided to this vendor for their role in this specific PPA contract. Counterparties typically provide credit support to the utility; transmission providers may require credit support from the utility. Amount is specific to this PPA contract and vendor role combination.']
  credit_support_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of credit support mechanisms required from the counterparty, such as letters of credit, parent guarantees, or performance bonds, to mitigate counterparty credit risk. [Moved from ppa_contract: Credit support requirements vary by vendor and role within a PPA. The counterparty provides credit support to the utility, but the utility may need to provide credit support to the transmission provider. The amount and form of credit support is vendor-role-specific, not a single contract-level attribute. Should be modeled as credit_support_amount in the association.]']
  participation_end_date date [note: 'Date when this vendor ceased performing their role in this PPA contract. NULL if vendor is currently active in this role. Populated when vendors are replaced or when the PPA terminates. Supports historical tracking of vendor role changes.']
  participation_start_date date [note: 'Date when this vendor began performing their role in this PPA contract. May differ from the PPA effective_start_date if vendors are added or replaced during the contract term. Supports vendor role transition tracking and historical vendor participation analysis.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this vendor\'s participation in their PPA contract role. Active = currently performing role, suspended = temporarily not performing (e.g., during dispute resolution), terminated = role ended, replaced = vendor was substituted by another vendor in the same role. Supports operational tracking of multi-party PPA administration.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Vendor-specific payment terms for this PPA contract role. Defines payment schedule, invoice requirements, and settlement timing specific to this vendor\'s role. May differ from the vendor\'s standard payment terms in their master record because PPA contracts often have specialized payment structures (e.g., monthly energy payments to counterparty, quarterly REC transfer payments to administrator).']
  performance_guarantee_terms varchar [note: 'Vendor-specific performance guarantee obligations for this PPA contract role. Defines availability targets, response time requirements, service level agreements, and penalty/liquidated damages provisions specific to this vendor\'s role. Counterparties have energy delivery guarantees, scheduling coordinators have scheduling accuracy requirements, REC administrators have transfer timing requirements.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which payments and obligations are settled with this specific vendor for their role in the PPA. Energy counterparties typically settle monthly, REC administrators quarterly, transmission providers monthly. This is contract-role-specific and may differ from standard vendor terms.']
  vendor_specific_pricing_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed pricing or fee structure for this vendor\'s services in their PPA contract role. Scheduling coordinators charge per-MWh scheduling fees, REC administrators charge per-REC-transferred fees, transmission providers charge per-MW-month reservation fees. These are contract-specific and may differ from standard vendor pricing.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual participation of vendors in renewable energy Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) contracts. Each record links one PPA contract to one vendor with a specific role (counterparty/IPP, scheduling coordinator, transmission provider, REC administrator, credit support provider). Captures vendor-specific contract terms including payment terms, settlement frequency, credit support obligations, and performance guarantees that vary by vendor role within the same PPA. Supports multi-party PPA administration, vendor role management, and contract-specific commercial terms tracking.. Existence Justification: In renewable energy PPA operations, a single PPA contract involves multiple vendors in distinct roles: the counterparty/IPP who generates the energy, scheduling coordinators who manage ISO/RTO schedules, transmission providers who deliver the power, REC administrators who track renewable attributes, and credit support providers. Conversely, a single vendor (especially large IPPs or specialized service providers) participates in multiple PPA contracts across different projects. The utility actively manages these multi-party relationships with contract-role-specific terms, payment schedules, and performance obligations.'
}

Table demand.dr_program {
  dr_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response program. Primary key.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: DR programs are designed within control area operational boundaries to meet reserve requirements and reliability standards. Needed for capacity planning, NERC reserve margin calculations, and coordina']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: DR programs are managed as cost centers for budget tracking and expense allocation. Program_budget_annual and operational costs require cost center assignment for financial reporting and regulatory co']
  equipment_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: DR programs contract with vendors for equipment/services (smart thermostats, load control devices). Business process: vendor performance tracking, procurement management, warranty administration for p']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: DR programs are designed for specific geographic zones with zone-specific load characteristics, peak demand patterns, and capacity requirements. Essential for program design, enrollment targeting, and']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: DR programs require DER interconnection approval before enrollment eligibility. ISO/RTO capacity market registration tracking needs interconnection status verification. Program managers validate inter']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: DR programs must comply with specific regulatory obligations (NERC reliability standards, FERC Order 2222, state PUC DR requirements). Essential for compliance tracking, audit evidence, and regulatory']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: DR programs generate revenue through capacity payments and incentive structures that must be allocated to profit centers for P&L reporting, FERC Form 1 functional classification, and rate case cost-be']
  program_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: DR programs are managed by specific utility employees who design incentive structures, manage budgets, coordinate with ISO/RTOs, and ensure regulatory compliance. Program_manager_name denormalizes emp']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: DR programs are designed, approved, and operated within specific service territories due to regulatory jurisdiction, franchise boundaries, tariff structures, and eligibility rules. Essential for progr']
  transmission_planning_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.planning_study. Business justification: Transmission planning studies evaluate DR programs as non-wires alternatives to transmission upgrades. FERC Order 1000 and state IRP processes require utilities to assess DR capacity in transmission p']
  ami_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer must have Advanced Metering Infrastructure (smart meter) installed to participate in this demand response program. True for programs requiring interval data; False for programs using deemed savings or aggregated measurements.']
  baseline_adjustment_cap_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage adjustment allowed to baseline calculation to account for day-of variations. Prevents gaming and ensures baseline accuracy.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Specific algorithm used to calculate customer baseline load for measurement and verification. High 10 of Last 10 averages the highest 10 days from the last 10 similar days; Average 5 of 10 averages middle 5 of last 10 days; Day Matching uses same day-of-week; Regression uses statistical model; Weather Normalized adjusts for temperature.']
  behavioral_dr_delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel used to deliver behavioral demand response messages and engagement content to customers. Applicable only to behavioral DR program types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|mobile_app|web_portal|home_energy_report|in_home_display|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  behavioral_dr_engagement_scoring_parameters varchar [note: 'Parameters and weights used to calculate customer engagement scores in behavioral demand response programs, including message open rates, web portal logins, mobile app usage, survey responses, and behavioral action completion.']
  behavioral_dr_gamification_rules varchar [note: 'Description of gamification elements used in behavioral demand response campaigns, including points systems, leaderboards, badges, challenges, and rewards structures designed to drive sustained engagement and load reduction.']
  behavioral_dr_peer_comparison_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to compare customer energy usage to peers in behavioral demand response campaigns. Neighbor Comparison uses geographic proximity; Similar Home Comparison uses home characteristics; Percentile Ranking shows position in distribution. None if peer comparison not used.']
  cost_effectiveness_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Calculated benefit-cost ratio for this demand response program using the specified cost-effectiveness test method. Values greater than 1.0 indicate program is cost-effective. Updated annually based on actual performance.']
  cost_effectiveness_test_method varchar [note: 'Regulatory cost-effectiveness test used to evaluate this demand response program. Total Resource Cost (TRC) test includes all costs and benefits; Program Administrator Cost (PAC) test uses utility perspective; Ratepayer Impact Measure (RIM) test evaluates rate impacts; Participant Cost Test (PCT) evaluates customer economics; Societal Cost Test (SCT) includes externalities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demand response program record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the demand response program is scheduled to terminate. Null for open-ended programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the demand response program becomes active and eligible for customer enrollment and event dispatch.']
  eligible_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment(s) eligible to enroll in this demand response program. Segmentation based on rate class and usage characteristics.']
  enrolled_capacity_actual_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Current actual aggregate enrolled load reduction capacity in megawatts across all active customer enrollments in this program.']
  enrolled_capacity_target_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Target aggregate load reduction capacity in megawatts that the utility aims to enroll in this demand response program. Used for resource adequacy planning and regulatory performance reporting.']
  event_notification_lead_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum number of hours advance notice provided to customers before a demand response event is dispatched. Regulatory requirement varies by program type and jurisdiction.']
  incentive_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base incentive amount paid to customers. Units depend on incentive_structure_type: dollars per year for fixed payment, dollars per kWh for per-kWh curtailed, dollars per kW-month for capacity payment.']
  incentive_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for incentive payments. USD for United States utilities.']
  incentive_structure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how customer incentives are calculated and paid. Fixed Payment provides flat annual amount; Per kWh Curtailed pays based on measured load reduction; Capacity Payment compensates for availability; Energy Payment compensates for actual curtailment; Bill Credit reduces customer bill; Tiered Incentive varies payment by performance level.']
  iso_rto_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ISO or RTO where this demand response program is registered for wholesale market participation. None indicates retail-only program. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|MISO|PJM|NYISO|ISO_NE|SPP|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  iso_rto_registration_number varchar [note: 'Registration identifier assigned by the ISO or RTO for wholesale market participation. Required for programs that bid demand response capacity into day-ahead or real-time markets.']
  max_event_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable duration in hours for a single demand response event under this program. Protects customers from excessive curtailment.']
  max_event_hours_per_year decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative hours of demand response events that can be dispatched in a calendar year. Protects customers from excessive annual curtailment burden.']
  max_events_per_year varchar [note: 'Maximum number of demand response events that can be called in a calendar year under this program. Regulatory limit to ensure customer participation remains voluntary and reasonable.']
  measurement_verification_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate baseline load and measure actual load reduction during demand response events. Customer Baseline uses historical usage patterns; Meter Before-After compares pre-event and event-period readings; Control Group compares participants to non-participants; Regression Analysis uses statistical models; Deemed Savings applies fixed reduction values.']
  minimum_load_requirement_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum peak demand in kilowatts that a customer must have to be eligible for enrollment in this demand response program. Null if no minimum applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demand response program record was last modified in the system.']
  opt_out_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers are allowed to opt out of individual demand response events without penalty. True for voluntary programs; False for firm interruptible tariffs.']
  opt_out_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial penalty assessed to customers who opt out of a demand response event when opt-out is allowed but discouraged. Null if no penalty applies.']
  performance_reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which program performance metrics must be reported to ISO/RTO and PUC. Event-based reporting required for wholesale market programs; periodic reporting for retail programs.']
  performance_reporting_obligation varchar [note: 'Description of specific performance metrics and reporting obligations required by regulators and market operators, including enrolled capacity, event performance, customer participation rates, and measurement and verification results.']
  program_budget_annual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual budget allocated for this demand response program, including incentive payments, marketing, administration, measurement and verification, and technology costs.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the DR program used in regulatory filings, ISO/RTO registration, and customer communications.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the demand response program as marketed to customers and reported to regulators.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand response program. Draft indicates program design phase; Pending Approval awaits PUC/FERC authorization; Active means program is enrolling customers and dispatching events; Suspended indicates temporary halt; Closed means no new enrollments but existing customers remain; Retired indicates program termination.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand response program type. Direct Load Control (DLC) allows utility to remotely control customer devices; Interruptible Tariff provides bill credits for load curtailment; Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) charges higher rates during peak events; Time of Use (TOU) varies rates by time period; Behavioral DR uses engagement and gamification; Virtual Power Plant (VPP) aggregates distributed resources; Wholesale Market DR participates in ISO/RTO markets; Emergency DR responds to grid emergencies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_load_control|interruptible_tariff|cpp|tou|behavioral_dr|vpp_aggregation|wholesale_market_dr|emergency_dr — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the PUC or FERC issued final approval for this demand response program to commence operations.']
  regulatory_authorization_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory authorization under which this demand response program operates. PUC Tariff for state-approved retail programs; FERC Tariff for wholesale programs; Pilot Program for experimental initiatives; Emergency Authorization for grid reliability programs; Market Rule for ISO/RTO market products.']
  regulatory_docket_number varchar [note: 'PUC or FERC docket number under which this demand response program was approved. Used for regulatory compliance tracking and audit trail.']
  vpp_aggregation_model varchar [note: 'Business model for aggregating distributed energy resources into a virtual power plant. Utility-Owned means utility directly controls customer devices; Third-Party Aggregator uses external aggregator; Hybrid combines both models. None if not a VPP program.']
  vpp_dispatch_protocol varchar [note: 'Communication protocol used to dispatch demand response signals to distributed energy resources in virtual power plant programs. OpenADR is open standard; IEEE 2030.5 is smart grid standard; Proprietary uses vendor-specific protocol. None if not applicable.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Demand Response program offered by the utility, including direct load control, interruptible tariff, CPP, TOU, behavioral DR (with delivery channel, peer comparison methodology, gamification rules, and engagement scoring parameters), VPP aggregation, and wholesale market DR products. Captures program type, ISO/RTO registration, enrolled capacity targets, incentive structure, measurement and verification methodology, regulatory authorization (PUC/FERC), program lifecycle status, performance reporting obligations, and behavioral campaign parameters. SSOT for all DR program definitions including behavioral DR campaign configurations.'
}

Table demand.demand_dr_enrollment {
  demand_dr_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response enrollment record. Primary key for the DR enrollment entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account enrolled in the demand response program. Links to the customer account master record in the Customer Information System (CIS).']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party demand response aggregator managing this customer enrollment on behalf of the utility. Null for direct utility-managed enrollments. Links to the aggregator master record defining aggregator contracts, settlement terms, and performance obligations.']
  customer_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_service_agreement. Business justification: DR enrollment must reference the specific service agreement governing rate schedule, service class, and contractual terms. Critical for tariff compliance, billing integration, and ensuring enrolled lo']
  der_interconnection_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.der_interconnection_point. Business justification: Premises with DER assets (solar, battery storage) enrolled in DR/VPP programs require linkage to their interconnection point for telemetry, dispatch control, baseline calculation, and settlement. Esse']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: DR enrollment tracks specific DER system providing curtailment capability. Measurement & verification requires system nameplate capacity, inverter specifications, and export limits. Baseline calculati']
  distribution_transformer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.transformer. Business justification: DR enrollments must track the distribution transformer serving the enrolled premise for capacity planning, hosting capacity analysis, and assessing grid impact during curtailment events. Essential for']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific demand response program in which the customer is enrolled. Links to the DR program master record defining program rules, incentives, and dispatch protocols.']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Enrollments are aggregated by zone for ISO/RTO reporting, baseline calculation, and capacity market participation. Zone-level aggregation is required for settlement, performance reporting, and resourc']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Customer DR enrollment requires completed interconnection for behind-the-meter DER. Enrollment verification validates interconnection approval status and export capacity limits. ISO/RTO registration a']
  interruptible_tariff_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the interruptible tariff agreement contract that governs the terms, conditions, and compensation for load curtailment. Links to the tariff agreement master record defining rate discounts, curtailment obligations, and penalty provisions.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Enrollments track specific equipment installed at customer premises (thermostats, control switches). Business process: asset tracking, warranty management, spare parts planning, equipment lifecycle ma']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) meter device used to measure interval load data for baseline calculation and measurement and verification (M&V). Links to the meter device master record in Meter Data Management (MDM).']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical service delivery point where demand response load curtailment will be measured. Links to the service point master record in the Customer Information System (CIS).']
  average_curtailment_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The average actual load curtailment in kilowatts (kW) achieved by the customer across all participated DR events during the current enrollment period. Used for program performance analysis and capacity planning.']
  baseline_adjustment_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether day-of or morning-of baseline adjustments are enabled for this enrollment to account for real-time load variations. True: adjustments are applied. False: unadjusted baseline is used for M&V.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the customer baseline load (CBL) for measurement and verification (M&V) of load curtailment performance. High X of Y: average of highest X days in prior Y days. Mid X of Y: average of middle X days in prior Y days. Regression: statistical regression model. Control Group: comparison to similar non-participating customers. Meter Before Meter After: direct comparison of meter readings.']
  capacity_market_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment is registered to participate in the ISO/RTO capacity market and is eligible to receive capacity payments for committed load reduction. True: participating in capacity market. False: energy-only or retail program participation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this enrollment record was first created in the data platform. System-generated audit timestamp for data lineage and compliance.']
  direct_load_control_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the utility has direct load control capability to remotely curtail customer load via smart thermostats, water heaters, or other controllable devices. True: DLC enabled. False: behavioral or manual curtailment only.']
  enrolled_load_capacity_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The committed load reduction capacity in kilowatts (kW) that the customer has enrolled to curtail during demand response events. This is the System of Record (SSOT) for enrolled capacity reported to ISO/RTO for capacity market settlements and to PUC for program performance audits.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the customer enrolled in the demand response program. Web Portal: customer self-service online. Mobile App: enrollment via utility mobile application. Call Center: enrollment via customer service representative. Field Enrollment: enrollment by field agent or installer. Aggregator: enrollment through third-party demand response aggregator. Auto Enrollment: automatic enrollment based on rate class or program rules. Utility Initiated: proactive enrollment by utility for targeted customers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_portal|mobile_app|call_center|field_enrollment|aggregator|auto_enrollment|utility_initiated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the demand response enrollment becomes active and the customer becomes eligible to participate in DR events and receive incentive payments. Must align with program enrollment windows and ISO/RTO capacity commitment deadlines.']
  enrollment_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the demand response enrollment expires and the customer is no longer eligible for DR event dispatch or incentive payments. Null for open-ended enrollments subject to annual renewal or customer opt-out.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique enrollment number assigned to the customer for this DR program participation. Used in customer communications, billing statements, and regulatory reporting. Format: DR-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  enrollment_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system of record from which this enrollment record originated. Examples: SAP IS-U, Oracle CC&B, proprietary DR management system, aggregator platform.']
  enrollment_source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this enrollment record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage traceability and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand response enrollment. Pending: enrollment submitted but not yet active. Active: enrollment is in effect and customer is eligible for DR events. Suspended: temporarily inactive due to non-compliance or customer request. Terminated: enrollment ended by utility or customer. Expired: enrollment period ended. Opted Out: customer exercised opt-out right.']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer enrollment was submitted and recorded in the system. Represents the business event time of enrollment initiation, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  iso_rto_registration_number varchar [note: 'The unique registration identifier assigned by the ISO or RTO for this demand response resource enrollment. Used for wholesale market participation, capacity market bidding, and settlement reconciliation with the ISO/RTO.']
  last_opt_out_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent opt-out event when the customer declined to participate in a dispatched DR event. Null if the customer has never opted out during the current enrollment period.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this enrollment record was most recently modified in the data platform. System-generated audit timestamp for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  notification_lead_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The minimum advance notice time in minutes required to notify the customer before a demand response event dispatch. Defined by program rules and customer agreement. Typical values range from 15 minutes to 24 hours.']
  notification_preference varchar [note: 'The customer preferred method for receiving demand response event notifications and dispatch alerts. Email: email notification. SMS: text message. Phone: automated phone call. Mobile App: push notification via utility mobile app. None: no notification requested.']
  opt_out_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of times the customer has exercised their opt-out right to decline participation in a dispatched DR event during the current enrollment period. Used to track customer engagement and enforce program opt-out limits.']
  participation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of dispatched DR events in which the customer successfully participated and achieved the required load curtailment during the current enrollment period. Calculated as (successful events / total dispatched events) * 100. Used for program performance reporting and customer incentive tier determination.']
  puc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment must be included in Public Utility Commission (PUC) demand response program participation and performance reports. True: include in PUC reporting. False: exclude from regulatory reporting.']
  telemetry_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data from the customer meter or behind-the-meter (BTM) devices is enabled for this enrollment to support real-time dispatch verification and settlement. True: telemetry enabled. False: interval data only.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the enrollment was terminated. Null for active, pending, or suspended enrollments. Populated when enrollment status transitions to terminated.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for enrollment termination when enrollment status is terminated. Customer Request: customer voluntarily withdrew. Non-Compliance: customer failed to meet program requirements. Program Ended: DR program was discontinued. Account Closed: customer account was closed. Moved: customer moved out of service territory. Aggregator Change: customer switched to different aggregator. Other: other termination reason. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|non_compliance|program_ended|account_closed|moved|aggregator_change|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_incentive_payments_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cumulative total of all incentive payments in United States Dollars (USD) paid to the customer for demand response participation during the current enrollment period. Includes capacity payments, energy payments, and performance bonuses.']
  vpp_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment is part of a Virtual Power Plant (VPP) aggregation that dispatches distributed energy resources (DER) as a single coordinated resource. True: enrolled in VPP. False: standalone DR enrollment.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing a customer account\'s enrollment in a specific DR program, including enrolled load capacity (kW), service point, meter, enrollment effective and expiration dates, enrollment channel, opt-out history, interruptible tariff agreement reference, and current enrollment status. SSOT for enrolled load capacity by customer and program. Supports ISO/RTO capacity reporting and PUC program participation audits.'
}

Table demand.dr_event {
  dr_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response event. Primary key for the DR event record.']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party demand response aggregator managing this event on behalf of the utility or ISO/RTO, if applicable.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: DR events are subject to performance audits by ISO/RTO and regulatory bodies to verify curtailment delivery and baseline accuracy. Required for audit evidence collection, performance verification, and']
  congestion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.congestion_event. Business justification: DR events are dispatched in direct response to transmission congestion events. Linking DR dispatch to the triggering congestion event enables cost-benefit analysis, settlement reconciliation, and regu']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: DR events are dispatched to manage control area ACE and frequency deviations. Operators must track which control area initiated each event for NERC CPS1/CPS2 compliance reporting and real-time balanci']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: DR events incur operational costs (dispatch, settlement, M&V) that must be charged to cost centers. Total_incentive_payment represents expense requiring cost center assignment for budget variance anal']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: DR event dispatch targets specific DER systems for curtailment. VPP aggregation and DERMS integration require system-level identification for telemetry and control commands. Event performance measurem']
  dispatch_operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: DR events are dispatched by control room operators (employees) who make real-time decisions to trigger load curtailment based on grid conditions, market signals, or emergency situations. Critical for ']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific distribution substation targeted by this DR event, if geographic_scope is substation-level.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Every DR event is dispatched under a specific program. Required for program-level performance reporting and settlement.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific distribution feeder targeted by this DR event, if geographic_scope is feeder-level.']
  iso_rto_event_id varchar [note: 'The external event identifier assigned by the Independent System Operator (ISO) or Regional Transmission Organization (RTO) for market-based DR events, used for settlement reconciliation.']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Event dispatch decisions require load forecasts to determine timing, magnitude, and necessity of curtailment. Operators compare forecasted peak load against available capacity to trigger DR events for']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: DR events must comply with dispatch protocols mandated by regulatory obligations (e.g., NERC EOP-011 emergency operations, ISO/RTO market rules). Essential for event compliance validation, audit trail']
  primary_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent demand response program under which this event was dispatched (e.g., CPP, TOU, direct load control, interruptible tariff).']
  supporting_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: DR events may require vendor support services (dispatch coordination, technical support, equipment maintenance). Business process: vendor performance evaluation during events, service level agreement ']
  to_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Every DR event is dispatched under a specific program. This FK is required for program-level performance reporting and ISO/RTO settlement.']
  transmission_outage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_outage. Business justification: DR events are called during transmission outages to manage system loading and prevent cascading failures. Emergency DR dispatch during N-1 or N-2 contingencies is standard grid operations. Links DR re']
  transmission_path_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.path. Business justification: DR events are dispatched to relieve congestion on specific transmission paths. Operators call DR to manage path loading, avoid TLR procedures, and maintain ATC. Critical for congestion management and ']
  triggering_outage_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: DR events are frequently dispatched in response to grid emergencies that manifest as outage risks or actual supply shortfalls. Emergency operations centers coordinate DR dispatch with OMS to manage sy']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific load zone or pricing zone targeted by this DR event, if geographic_scope is zone-level.']
  actual_curtailment_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The actual load reduction in megawatts (MW) achieved during this DR event, calculated through baseline comparison and M&V processes.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual duration of the DR event in minutes, calculated from actual_start_timestamp to actual_end_timestamp, used for settlement.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when load curtailment ended, used for M&V and settlement calculations.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when load curtailment began, used for measurement and verification (M&V) and settlement calculations.']
  baseline_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The calculated baseline load in megawatts (MW) representing what the load would have been without the DR event, used as the reference for curtailment measurement.']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'The calculation method used to determine the baseline load for this event (e.g., 10-of-10, 5-of-10, regression-based, customer-specific), critical for M&V accuracy and settlement.']
  cancellation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this DR event was cancelled before execution. True indicates the event was cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the DR event was cancelled, used for operational review and reporting.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the DR event was cancelled, if cancellation_flag is True.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this DR event met all regulatory and contractual compliance requirements (notification timing, curtailment delivery, reporting).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this DR event record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dispatch_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated the DR event dispatch (e.g., ADMS, DERMS, EMS, manual dispatch console), used for system integration tracking.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the DR event was dispatched or issued to participants. This is the official event start trigger time.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The planned duration of the DR event in minutes, calculated as the difference between scheduled_start_timestamp and scheduled_end_timestamp.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the DR event, used for external communication and reporting to ISO/RTO and customers.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DR event: scheduled (future event), active (currently dispatched), completed (finished successfully), cancelled (terminated before start), aborted (terminated during execution), or pending (awaiting approval).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DR event based on its purpose and trigger: emergency (grid reliability), economic (price-driven), test (program validation), scheduled (planned curtailment), voluntary (opt-in), or mandatory (contractual obligation).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic extent of the DR event: system_wide (entire service territory), zone (load zone or pricing zone), substation (specific substation), feeder (distribution feeder), or premise (individual customer site).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this DR event record was last updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  lmp_at_dispatch decimal(10,2) [note: 'The locational marginal price in dollars per MWh at the relevant pricing node (PNode) at the time of event dispatch, used for economic DR event valuation.']
  market_product_type varchar [note: 'The wholesale market product associated with this DR event (e.g., day-ahead energy, real-time energy, regulation, spinning reserve, non-spinning reserve), if dispatched through ISO/RTO markets.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special circumstances, or additional context about the DR event execution and outcomes.']
  notification_lead_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The advance notice period in minutes provided to participants between notification and event start, measured as the difference between notification_timestamp and dispatch_timestamp.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when participants were notified of the upcoming DR event, used to calculate notification lead time compliance.']
  opted_out_count varchar [note: 'The number of enrolled customers who opted out of this specific DR event, tracked for program compliance and customer behavior analysis.']
  participant_count varchar [note: 'The total number of customers or premises that participated in this DR event, used for program performance reporting.']
  performance_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'The ratio of actual curtailment to target curtailment expressed as a percentage (0-100), used to measure event effectiveness and participant compliance.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when load curtailment is scheduled to end for this DR event.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when load curtailment is scheduled to begin for this DR event.']
  settlement_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The final price in dollars per MWh used for customer incentive payment settlement for this DR event, may differ from LMP based on program rules.']
  target_curtailment_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The target load reduction in megawatts (MW) requested for this DR event across all participating customers and assets.']
  temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'The average ambient temperature in degrees Fahrenheit during the DR event, used for weather normalization in M&V calculations.']
  total_incentive_payment decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total incentive payment amount in dollars paid to all participants for this DR event, calculated based on actual curtailment and settlement price.']
  triggering_entity varchar [note: 'The organization or system that initiated the DR event: utility (internal dispatch), ISO/RTO (market operator), aggregator (third-party DR provider), customer (self-curtailment), or system (automated trigger).']
  vpp_dispatch_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this DR event was dispatched as part of a virtual power plant (VPP) aggregation strategy.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Description of the prevailing weather conditions during the DR event (e.g., extreme heat, cold snap, normal), used for baseline adjustment and event analysis.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each DR dispatch event issued by the utility, ISO/RTO, or aggregator, including event type (emergency, economic, test), triggering entity, dispatch timestamp, notification lead time, target curtailment MW, geographic scope (zone, feeder, substation), event duration, cancellation flag, and post-event status. Core operational record for DR event lifecycle management and OMS/ADMS integration.'
}

Table demand.dr_event_participant {
  dr_event_participant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response event participant record. Primary key for this transactional entity linking a customer, aggregated resource, or Direct Load Control (DLC) device to a specific DR event.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account participating in the DR event. Used for billing, incentive payment processing, and customer-level performance aggregation.']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party aggregator managing this participant resource. Used for wholesale market DR programs and aggregated DER portfolios. Null for utility-direct programs.']
  caused_interruption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.interruption. Business justification: Direct load control actions during DR events can cause brief service interruptions to participating customers (cycling equipment, load shedding). Business process: Reconciling DR-induced interruptions']
  demand_dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer enrollment record that authorized participation in this DR event. Links to the active enrollment in the DR program at the time of event dispatch.']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Participant-level performance measurement requires DER system technical specifications for baseline calculation and curtailment verification. Device command type and target setpoint validation depend ']
  direct_load_control_device_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Direct Load Control (DLC) device or Distributed Energy Resource (DER) that was dispatched during the event. Applicable for DLC programs, smart thermostats, battery storage, and VPP resources. Null for behavioral DR programs.']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event.dr_event_id — Core header-line relationship. Every participant record must reference the event it responds to. Required for event-level curtailment aggregation.']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Compliance reporting and settlement require linking participant response to approved interconnection application for capacity verification. Incentive payment eligibility validation checks interconnect']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Participant-level performance measurement requires comparing actual response against forecasted baseline load for the event window. Essential for M&V, settlement calculation, and performance ratio det']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical premise location where the DR event participation occurred. Used for geographic analysis, load zone aggregation, and distribution system impact assessment.']
  primary_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent DR event that this participant record is associated with. Links to the specific dispatch event across all DR program types (DLC, behavioral, interruptible, VPP, wholesale market).']
  to_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event.dr_event_id — Every participant response record must reference the event it responds to. This is the core header-to-line relationship for DR event performance tracking.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the participant device or system acknowledged receipt of the dispatch command. Used to measure communication latency and confirm successful command delivery for DLC and VPP resources.']
  actual_load_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured actual load in kilowatts consumed by the participant during the DR event window. Sourced from Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) interval data or SCADA telemetry for real-time measurement.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate the baseline load for this participant. Common methods include high X of Y days, regression-based, or control group comparison. Must align with program rules and regulatory requirements for Measurement and Verification (M&V).']
  baseline_load_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Calculated baseline load in kilowatts representing what the participant would have consumed absent the DR event. Used as the reference point for measuring actual curtailment performance. Calculated using the baseline methodology specified in the program rules.']
  committed_curtailment_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Load reduction in kilowatts that the participant committed to provide during the DR event. Used for interruptible tariff, VPP, and wholesale market programs where participants bid or contract specific curtailment amounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this participant event record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  curtailment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes that the participant actively curtailed load during the DR event. Used for calculating energy reduction (kWh) and validating minimum participation requirements.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the quality and reliability of the meter data used for performance measurement. Flags estimated or missing interval data that may affect settlement accuracy and require manual review.']
  device_command_type varchar [note: 'Type of control command issued to the DLC device or DER during the event. Applicable for direct load control programs. Null for behavioral and interruptible tariff programs.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the DR event dispatch command was issued to this participant. Represents the moment the utility or aggregator initiated the curtailment request or device control signal.']
  energy_reduction_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total energy reduction in kilowatt-hours achieved by the participant during the DR event, calculated as measured curtailment (kW) multiplied by duration (hours). Used for program impact reporting and wholesale market settlement.']
  engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated engagement score for behavioral DR participants based on notification interaction (open, click, response). Used for segmentation, targeted messaging, and gamification strategies to improve participation rates.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for participant non-response or under-performance during the DR event. Used for root cause analysis, device maintenance prioritization, and program improvement initiatives.']
  incentive_payment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated incentive payment amount in local currency earned by the participant for this DR event based on measured curtailment, program rate structure, and performance ratio. Subject to final settlement and adjustment.']
  incentive_payment_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the incentive payment amount. Supports multi-currency operations for utilities operating across international jurisdictions.']
  incentive_payment_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this participant performance qualifies for incentive payment based on program rules, minimum performance thresholds, and compliance requirements. False indicates disqualification due to non-performance or rule violation.']
  measured_curtailment_kw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual measured load reduction in kilowatts achieved by the participant during the DR event, calculated as baseline load minus actual load. This is the verified performance used for incentive payment calculation and program reporting.']
  mv_method varchar [note: 'Measurement and Verification method applied to validate the participant curtailment performance. Determines the data sources and calculation approach used for performance settlement and regulatory reporting.']
  non_performance_penalty_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a penalty applies to this participant for failing to meet committed curtailment obligations. Applicable for interruptible tariff and wholesale market programs with contractual performance requirements.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to notify or dispatch this participant. For behavioral DR: notification method. For DLC: control protocol. Used for engagement analysis and communication effectiveness tracking.']
  notification_delivery_status varchar [note: 'Status of the event notification delivery to the participant for behavioral DR programs. Tracks email, SMS, or mobile app notification delivery confirmation and engagement.']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Participant-provided reason for opting out of the DR event. Used for customer satisfaction analysis and program design refinement to reduce opt-out frequency.']
  opt_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the participant exercised their opt-out right to decline participation in this specific DR event. Applicable for programs allowing per-event opt-out within contractual limits.']
  participant_to_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Links event participation back to the enrolled resource. Required to validate that participants are enrolled and to track per-enrollment performance history.']
  participant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DR participant type indicating the program mechanism and dispatch method. Determines which performance metrics and measurement methods apply to this participation record.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this participant record within the DR event. Tracks progression from dispatch through acknowledgment, response, and completion or failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dispatched|acknowledged|responding|completed|failed|opted_out|non_responsive — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  peer_comparison_percentile varchar [note: 'Percentile ranking of this participant performance relative to similar participants in the same program cohort. Used for behavioral DR feedback and social comparison messaging (e.g., You performed better than 75% of your neighbors).']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated penalty amount in local currency assessed against the participant for non-performance or under-performance during the DR event. Applied per contractual terms for interruptible and wholesale market programs.']
  performance_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of measured curtailment to committed curtailment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.95 for 95% performance). Used to assess participant compliance with contractual obligations and determine penalty or bonus payment eligibility.']
  response_classification varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the participant response quality based on performance ratio thresholds. Used for behavioral segmentation, program effectiveness analysis, and targeted engagement strategies.']
  response_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the participant response concluded, either by event completion, restore command, or participant opt-out. Marks the end of the performance measurement window.']
  response_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the participant began actively responding to the DR event, as measured by load reduction or device state change. Marks the beginning of the performance measurement window.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the financial settlement process for this participant event record. Tracks progression from initial calculation through approval, payment, and any dispute resolution.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the financial settlement for this participant event was finalized and approved for payment processing. Used for payment reconciliation and audit trail.']
  target_setpoint_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target setpoint value. Ensures correct interpretation of device control parameters across different resource types.']
  target_setpoint_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target setpoint value commanded to the device during the DR event (e.g., thermostat temperature in degrees, battery discharge rate in kW). Applicable for DLC and VPP resources with adjustable setpoints.']
  to_dr_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Every participant response must reference the enrollment that authorized participation. Required for incentive eligibility validation and capacity reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this participant event record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes to performance metrics, settlement status, or data quality adjustments.']

  Note: 'Transactional record linking an enrolled customer, aggregated resource, or DLC device to a specific DR event, serving as the single authoritative response record across ALL DR program types. For direct load control: captures device commands (shed/restore/cycle with target setpoints), acknowledgment status, actual device response, load shed achieved, dispatch duration, and failure reasons. For behavioral DR: captures notification delivery confirmation, peer comparison data, engagement scoring, pre-event baseline consumption, actual consumption during event window, calculated reduction, and response classification. For interruptible tariff, VPP, and wholesale market dispatch: captures committed curtailment (kW), actual measured curtailment (kW), performance ratio, incentive payment eligibility, non-performance penalty flag, and M&V method applied. SSOT for per-resource event performance tracking across all DR program types including DLC device-level response and behavioral DR customer engagement.'
}

Table demand.load_baseline {
  load_baseline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the load baseline calculation record. Primary key for the load baseline entity.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Load baselines must be calculated within control area boundaries for accurate curtailment measurement in ISO/RTO settlements. Control area load forecasts and actual interchange schedules are used to v']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Baseline calculation for behind-the-meter DER requires system capacity and export capability from interconnection approval. ISO/RTO baseline methodology depends on interconnection configuration (expor']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific DR event for which this baseline is applicable. Null if this is a standing baseline not tied to a specific event.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Demand Response program under which this baseline is calculated. Links to the DR program master record.']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Baselines are calculated using historical load patterns and must align with load forecasts for accuracy validation, adjustment factor calculation, and weather normalization. Critical for ISO/RTO basel']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) meter device used to capture interval data for baseline calculation.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise where the baseline load is measured. Links to the premise master record.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer for whom this baseline load profile is calculated. Links to the customer master record in the Customer Information System (CIS).']
  superseded_by_baseline_load_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the baseline record that supersedes this one. Null if this is the current active baseline. Used to maintain baseline version history.']
  adjustment_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to the baseline to account for day-of conditions (e.g., weather, operational changes). Typically ranges from 0.8 to 1.2. A value of 1.0 indicates no adjustment.']
  adjustment_method varchar [note: 'The method used to adjust the baseline for day-of conditions. Options include none (no adjustment), additive (add/subtract a fixed amount), multiplicative (apply a scaling factor), weather-normalized (adjust based on weather data), or pre-event-hours (adjust based on load in hours immediately before the event).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline was approved for use. Required for regulatory audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who approved this baseline for use in M&V calculations. Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  baseline_calculation_date date [note: 'The date on which this baseline load profile was calculated. Used for version control and audit trail purposes.']
  baseline_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this baseline load profile becomes effective for Measurement and Verification (M&V) purposes.']
  baseline_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this baseline load profile expires and is no longer valid for M&V calculations. Null if the baseline remains valid until superseded.']
  baseline_load_profile_json varchar [note: 'JSON-encoded array of baseline load values (kWh) for each interval within the event window. Each element represents the expected load for a specific time interval. Format: [{"interval_start":"2024-07-15T14:00:00","load_kwh":125.4},{"interval_start":"2024-07-15T14:15:00","load_kwh":128.2}].']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the customer baseline load. Common methods include 10-of-10 (average of 10 most recent similar days), high-10-of-10 (highest 10 days), mid-8-of-10 (middle 8 of 10), regression-based, weather-adjusted, or customer-specific algorithms approved by the ISO/RTO or PUC.']
  baseline_source_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of dates (YYYY-MM-DD) used as source data for calculating this baseline. Provides traceability to the historical load data used in the calculation.']
  baseline_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the baseline record. Indicates whether the baseline is in draft, pending approval, approved for use, actively in use, superseded by a newer version, or rejected.']
  baseline_to_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Baselines are calculated per enrolled resource. Required for M&V — you cannot measure curtailment without linking baseline to the enrolled customer/resource.']
  baseline_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this baseline calculation. Increments with each recalculation or adjustment. Used for audit trail and historical comparison.']
  calculation_engine varchar [note: 'Name and version of the software system or calculation engine used to compute this baseline (e.g., Oracle MDM Baseline Calculator v3.2, Custom Python Engine v1.5). Used for audit and reproducibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality score (0-100) indicating the reliability of the input meter data used for baseline calculation. Based on completeness, accuracy, and validation status of interval data.']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to estimate missing interval data in the baseline calculation. Options include none (no estimation), linear interpolation, similar-day substitution, regression-based estimation, or carry-forward from previous interval.']
  event_window_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The end timestamp of the DR event window for which this baseline applies. Defines the conclusion of the curtailment measurement period.']
  event_window_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The start timestamp of the DR event window for which this baseline applies. Defines the beginning of the curtailment measurement period.']
  exclusion_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of dates (YYYY-MM-DD) that were excluded from the baseline calculation due to holidays, prior DR events, or anomalous load conditions.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The duration of each interval in the baseline load profile, typically 15 or 60 minutes. Aligns with AMI meter reading intervals.']
  iso_rto_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline was submitted to the ISO/RTO for settlement. Null if not yet submitted.']
  iso_rto_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this baseline has been submitted to the ISO/RTO for settlement purposes. True if submitted, False otherwise.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system process that last modified this baseline record. Required for audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baseline record was last modified. Updated whenever any field in the record changes.']
  missing_interval_count varchar [note: 'Number of intervals with missing or invalid meter data that were estimated or excluded from the baseline calculation. Used to assess baseline reliability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, manual adjustments, or exceptions applied during baseline calculation. Used for audit trail and operational transparency.']
  peak_baseline_demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'The maximum demand (kW) observed in the baseline load profile during the event window. Used for capacity-based DR program settlement.']
  puc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this baseline is included in PUC program performance reporting. True if included, False otherwise.']
  temperature_sensitivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer\'s load is temperature-sensitive and requires weather-adjusted baseline methodology. True if temperature-sensitive, False otherwise.']
  to_dr_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Baselines are calculated per enrolled resource. Without this FK, you cannot match baselines to the correct customer enrollment for M&V calculations.']
  total_baseline_energy_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'The sum of all interval baseline load values across the entire event window, expressed in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Used for aggregate curtailment measurement.']
  weather_station_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the weather station used for weather-adjusted baseline calculations. Links to external weather data sources (e.g., NOAA, local meteorological services).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system process that created this baseline record. Required for audit trail.']

  Note: 'Calculated customer baseline load profile used as the reference point for measuring curtailment during DR events. Stores baseline methodology (e.g., 10-of-10, regression, weather-adjusted), baseline interval values (kWh per 15-min or hourly), calculation date, applicable event window, adjustment factors, and version history. SSOT for M&V baseline data required by ISO/RTO settlement and PUC program reporting.'
}

Table demand.curtailment_measurement {
  curtailment_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the curtailment measurement record. Primary key for post-event measurement records capturing actual load reduction achieved during a Demand Response (DR) event interval.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Measurement & verification data serves as primary audit evidence for DR performance audits. Essential for M&V audit support, compliance documentation, and providing granular interval data to auditors ']
  curtailment_dr_event_participant_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event_participant.dr_event_participant_id — Curtailment measurements are interval-level detail records for a participant\'s event performance. Required for M&V settlement calculations.']
  curtailment_load_baseline_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.load_baseline.load_baseline_id — Each curtailment measurement references the baseline used for comparison. Required for auditable M&V calculations.']
  demand_dr_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enrolled DR resource (customer premise, aggregated load zone, or DER asset) that participated in the curtailment event.']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Measurement & verification of curtailment performance requires DER system nameplate capacity, inverter specifications, and export limits from interconnection approval. Settlement quantity calculation ']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent DR event during which this curtailment was measured. Links to the DR event dispatch record that triggered the load reduction.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that validated this measurement record. Used for audit trail and accountability in the M&V process.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Curtailment measurements drive financial settlements with incentive_amount and penalty_amount requiring GL account posting for journal entries. Essential for accrual accounting, month-end close, and r']
  lmp_price_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the LMP pricing node or zone where this curtailment resource is located. Used for wholesale market settlement and valuation of the load reduction at the nodal or zonal LMP.']
