  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: M&V processes compare actual curtailment against forecasted load to calculate performance and settlement quantities. Required for ISO/RTO settlement, incentive payment calculation, and regulatory comp']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the meter or metering point from which interval data was sourced for this measurement. May be an AMI meter, SCADA point, or aggregated virtual meter.']
  to_dr_event_participant_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event_participant.dr_event_participant_id — Curtailment measurements are taken per participant per event. This FK links measured load reduction to the specific participant response record.']
  to_load_baseline_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.load_baseline.load_baseline_id — Each curtailment measurement references the baseline used to calculate the reduction. Required for M&V audit trail.']
  baseline_adjustment_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to the baseline to account for day-of conditions (weather, operational changes). Typically ranges from 0.8 to 1.2. A value of 1.0 indicates no adjustment.']
  baseline_demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated baseline power demand in kilowatts (kW) representing what the resource would have demanded absent the DR event. Used as the reference for curtailment calculation.']
  baseline_load_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated baseline energy consumption in kilowatt-hours (kWh) representing what the resource would have consumed absent the DR event. Calculated using the baseline methodology specified in the DR program rules.']
  baseline_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the baseline calculation methodology applied for this measurement. Common methods include High X of Y (e.g., average of highest 3 of last 10 similar days), Mid X of Y, regression-based, control group, meter-before, or customer-provided baseline.']
  bid_quantity_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'The bid quantity in kilowatts (kW) submitted by the resource or aggregator for this DR event interval. May differ from enrolled_capacity_kw if the resource bid a different amount for this specific event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this curtailment measurement record was first created in the system. Represents the initial calculation or data ingestion time.']
  curtailment_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated load curtailment achieved during the interval in kilowatts (kW). Computed as baseline_demand_kw minus metered_demand_kw. Positive values indicate successful load reduction; negative values indicate load increase.']
  curtailment_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated energy curtailment achieved during the interval in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Computed as baseline_load_kwh minus metered_load_kwh. Used for settlement and incentive payment calculation.']
  data_quality_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the quality status of the metered data. ACTUAL indicates raw meter data; ESTIMATED indicates VEE-filled gaps; VALIDATED indicates passed quality checks; SUSPECT indicates potential data issues; MISSING indicates no data received; REJECTED indicates failed validation.']
  data_source_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source of the metered load data. Values include AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure), SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition), MANUAL (manual meter read), ESTIMATED (VEE-estimated), AGGREGATED (sum of multiple meters), or TELEMETRY (real-time sensor data).']
  enrolled_capacity_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'The enrolled or nominated load reduction capacity in kilowatts (kW) for this resource during the DR event. Represents the committed curtailment target used for performance evaluation and settlement.']
  incentive_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated incentive payment amount in currency for this measurement interval. Computed as settlement_quantity_kw multiplied by incentive_rate_per_kwh and interval duration. Subject to program caps and adjustments.']
  incentive_rate_per_kwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'The incentive payment rate in currency per kilowatt-hour ($/kWh) applicable to this measurement interval. Rate may vary by time-of-use period, event type, or performance tier.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the measurement interval in minutes. Common values are 5, 15, or 60 minutes depending on ISO/RTO market settlement rules and meter data granularity.']
  interval_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the measurement interval during the DR event. Defines the boundary of the measurement period for load curtailment calculation.']
  interval_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the measurement interval during the DR event. Typically aligned to 5-minute, 15-minute, or hourly intervals per ISO/RTO settlement rules.']
  iso_rto_market_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the ISO/RTO wholesale market to which this curtailment measurement is reported for settlement. Values include CAISO, ERCOT, ISONE, MISO, NYISO, PJM, SPP, or NONE for retail-only programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|ISONE|MISO|NYISO|PJM|SPP|NONE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lmp_price_per_mwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'The Locational Marginal Price in currency per megawatt-hour ($/MWh) at the resource location during this interval. Used to value the curtailment for wholesale market settlement or avoided cost calculation.']
  measurement_status_code varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the curtailment measurement record. PRELIMINARY indicates initial calculation; VALIDATED indicates passed M&V review; SETTLED indicates used for payment calculation; DISPUTED indicates under review; ADJUSTED indicates corrected after dispute; FINAL indicates locked for regulatory reporting.']
  metered_demand_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual power demand measured during the interval in kilowatts (kW). Represents the average or peak demand during the measurement period depending on metering configuration.']
  metered_load_kwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual energy consumption measured during the interval in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Sourced from AMI meter data, SCADA telemetry, or estimated via Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) process.']
  mv_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Measurement and Verification (M&V) protocol applied to validate the curtailment calculation. Common protocols include IPMVP Options A-D (International Performance Measurement and Verification Protocol) or utility-specific custom methods.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Penalty amount in currency assessed for under-performance or non-compliance during this interval. Applied when actual curtailment falls below minimum performance thresholds defined in the DR program agreement.']
  performance_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of actual curtailment achieved to the enrolled capacity or bid quantity for this resource during the interval. Calculated as curtailment_kw divided by enrolled_capacity_kw. Values above 1.0 indicate over-performance; below 1.0 indicate under-performance.']
  reported_to_iso_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement has been reported to the ISO/RTO for wholesale market settlement. True indicates the record was included in the ISO/RTO performance report; False indicates retail-only or not yet reported.']
  reported_to_iso_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement was submitted to the ISO/RTO for wholesale market settlement. Used for tracking reporting deadlines and settlement cycles.']
  reported_to_puc_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this measurement has been included in regulatory reporting to the state Public Utility Commission (PUC). True indicates the record was included in DR program performance reports; False indicates not yet reported.']
  settlement_quantity_kw decimal(18,3) [note: 'The final settled curtailment quantity in kilowatts (kW) used for incentive payment calculation. May differ from curtailment_kw after applying performance thresholds, caps, or adjustments per program rules.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this curtailment measurement record was last modified. Tracks revisions due to data corrections, dispute resolution, or re-calculation with updated baseline methods.']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement record was validated and approved for settlement. Marks the transition from preliminary to validated status in the measurement lifecycle.']
  validation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting validation findings, data quality issues, adjustments applied, or dispute resolution details for this measurement record. Used for audit trail and M&V documentation.']
  vee_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the metered data was processed through Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) logic. True indicates VEE was applied to fill gaps or correct suspect data; False indicates raw meter data was used.']
  vee_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the VEE estimation method applied when metered data was missing or suspect. Common methods include linear interpolation, historical average, similar day substitution, regression-based estimation, or NONE if no VEE was applied.']
  weather_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at the resource location during the measurement interval. Used for baseline adjustment in weather-sensitive load profiles.']
  weather_temperature_f decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at the resource location during the measurement interval. Used for baseline adjustment and performance analysis. May be sourced from weather station data or utility weather service.']

  Note: 'Post-event measurement record capturing actual load curtailment achieved by an enrolled resource during a DR event interval. Stores interval-level metered load (kWh/kW), baseline load, calculated curtailment (kW), data source (AMI, SCADA, estimated), validation status, VEE flags, and M&V method applied. Feeds DR settlement, incentive payment calculation, and ISO/RTO performance reporting.'
}

Table demand.dr_incentive_payment {
  dr_incentive_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response incentive payment transaction. Primary key for the DR incentive payment record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with this incentive payment for billing integration.']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand response aggregator managing the customer enrollment and receiving payment on behalf of enrolled customers.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Incentive payments are audited for accuracy and regulatory compliance, especially for cost recovery in rate cases. Required for financial audit trail, settlement verification, and demonstrating pruden']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Incentive payments are expenses charged to cost centers for budget tracking and variance analysis. Utility_net_cost requires cost center assignment to enable budget-to-actual reporting and cost alloca']
  dr_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event.dr_event_id — Incentive payments and settlements are calculated per event (or per settlement period covering events). This FK links financial obligations to the triggering event for reconciliation.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Payments are issued under program-specific incentive structures. Required for program-level financial reporting.']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: Payment calculations for energy-based DR programs use LMP forecasts for valuation and settlement pricing. Essential for economic dispatch decisions, participant compensation, and cost-effectiveness an']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Incentive payments must post to specific GL accounts (typically FERC 565 - Customer Assistance Expenses or similar) for financial statement preparation, journal entry creation, and regulatory reportin']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Payment eligibility verification requires confirmed interconnection approval status. Capacity payments are tied to interconnection-approved export capacity limits. ISO/RTO settlement reconciliation va']
  primary_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific demand response event that triggered this incentive payment.']
  primary_dr_event_participant_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event_participant.dr_event_participant_id — Incentive payments are calculated based on participant performance in events. Required for financial reconciliation and customer payment accuracy.']
  primary_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand response program under which this payment is made (e.g., CPP, TOU, direct load control, interruptible tariff).']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving the incentive payment. May be null if payment is made to an aggregator on behalf of multiple customers.']
  to_dr_event_participant_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event_participant.dr_event_participant_id — Incentive payments are calculated based on participant event performance. This FK links financial records to the performance data that generated them.']
  to_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Financial settlements must reference the program for rate structure, incentive rules, and regulatory reporting.']
  actual_load_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The measured actual load in kilowatts during the demand response event. Used to calculate curtailment performance.']
  aggregator_share_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the payment allocated to the aggregator based on the aggregator share percentage.']
  aggregator_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the gross payment retained by the aggregator as commission for program management and customer enrollment services.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incentive payment was approved for disbursement by authorized personnel.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who approved this incentive payment for disbursement.']
  baseline_load_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The calculated baseline load in kilowatts representing the customers expected consumption without demand response curtailment. Used for measurement and verification (M&V) calculations.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the calculation methodology used for this payment, including baseline calculation method, M&V approach, and rate application logic.']
  capacity_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The calculated capacity payment amount in dollars based on enrolled capacity and capacity rate. Represents payment for availability.']
  capacity_rate_per_kw decimal(12,4) [note: 'The contracted capacity payment rate in dollars per kilowatt for the enrolled demand response capacity. Basis for capacity payment calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incentive payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this payment record (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  curtailment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes that the customer maintained load curtailment during the demand response event.']
  customer_net_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net payment amount in dollars disbursed to the customer after aggregator share and penalty deductions.']
  disbursement_date date [note: 'Date on which the incentive payment was disbursed to the customer or aggregator.']
  disbursement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the payment disbursement transaction (e.g., check number, wire confirmation, EFT trace number) for reconciliation with financial systems.']
  energy_curtailed_kwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total energy curtailed in kilowatt-hours during the settlement period, calculated as measured curtailment multiplied by duration. Used for energy payment calculations.']
  energy_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The calculated energy payment amount in dollars based on measured curtailment and energy rate or LMP. Represents payment for actual performance.']
  energy_rate_per_kwh decimal(12,6) [note: 'The contracted energy payment rate in dollars per kilowatt-hour for verified load curtailment. Used for retail-level energy payment calculations.']
  enrolled_capacity_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The committed demand response capacity in kilowatts that the customer or aggregator enrolled for this settlement period. Basis for capacity payments.']
  gross_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross incentive payment amount in dollars before aggregator share and deductions. Sum of capacity, energy, and bonus payments.']
  iso_rto_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ISO or RTO wholesale market from which this payment originates (e.g., PJM, CAISO, ERCOT, NYISO, ISO-NE, MISO, SPP). Null for retail-only programs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this incentive payment record was last updated, reflecting the most recent change to payment status, amounts, or reconciliation status.']
  lmp_price_per_mwh decimal(12,4) [note: 'The locational marginal price in dollars per megawatt-hour at the pricing node (PNode) during the demand response event. Used for wholesale market energy payment calculations.']
  measured_curtailment_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The verified load reduction in kilowatts achieved during the demand response event, calculated as baseline load minus actual load. Basis for energy payments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding special circumstances, adjustments, disputes, or other relevant information about this incentive payment.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the incentive payment is disbursed: bill credit applied to customer account, direct deposit to bank account, physical check, wire transfer, or settlement through aggregator.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for this incentive payment transaction, used for customer communication and payment tracking.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incentive payment: pending calculation (awaiting M&V), pending approval (awaiting management review), approved (ready for disbursement), disbursed (payment issued), reconciled (matched with wholesale settlement), disputed (under review), or cancelled (voided). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_calculation|pending_approval|approved|disbursed|reconciled|disputed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_to_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Payments are issued to enrolled customers/aggregators. This FK links the payment to the enrollment record for customer identification and payment routing.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incentive payment type: capacity payment for enrolled load, energy payment for actual curtailment, performance bonus for exceeding targets, penalty deduction for non-performance, reconciliation adjustment for settlement corrections, or aggregator commission for program management.']
  penalty_deduction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Penalty amount in dollars deducted from payment for non-performance, failure to curtail, or non-compliance with program requirements.']
  performance_bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional incentive payment in dollars awarded for exceeding curtailment targets or achieving exceptional performance metrics.']
  pnode_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier for the pricing node in the wholesale market where the demand response resource is located. Used for LMP-based settlement calculations.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of reconciliation between retail incentive payment and wholesale market settlement: not reconciled, reconciled (matched), variance identified (discrepancy found), under review (investigating), or resolved (variance corrected).']
  settlement_period_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the settlement period covered by this incentive payment. Defines the billing cycle or market settlement window.']
  settlement_period_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the settlement period covered by this incentive payment. Aligns with ISO/RTO settlement intervals for wholesale market payments.']
  settlement_statement_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to the ISO/RTO wholesale market settlement statement from which this payment originates. Used for reconciliation with wholesale market settlements.']
  utility_net_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net cost to the utility in dollars for this demand response incentive payment after accounting for wholesale market revenue offsets and ISO/RTO settlements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all DR financial settlements and incentive payments, serving as the single authoritative financial record for the demand domain. Covers wholesale market settlement (ISO/RTO capacity and energy payments, LMP-based calculations, performance adjustments, settlement statement references, settlement period reconciliation) and retail-level incentive disbursements (capacity payments, energy payments, performance bonuses, penalty deductions). Captures settlement period, calculation basis, enrolled capacity, measured curtailment, gross and net amounts, aggregator share, utility net cost, payment approval status, disbursement reference, reconciliation status, and ISO/RTO settlement statement cross-reference. SSOT for ALL DR financial obligations — wholesale settlement, retail incentives, aggregator payments, customer disbursements, and settlement reconciliation with wholesale markets.'
}

Table demand.aggregator {

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Aggregator relationships incur administrative and settlement costs that must be tracked by cost center. Performance_bond_amount and contracted_capacity_mw represent financial obligations requiring cos']
  der_installer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_installer. Business justification: Aggregators partner with certified DER installers for customer acquisition and enrollment pipeline development. Installer performance metrics (application completeness rate, average time to PTO) infor']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Aggregators operate in specific geographic zones/service territories for capacity aggregation and ISO/RTO market participation. Zone assignment is required for local capacity requirements, transmissio']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Aggregators must comply with FERC Order 2222 registration obligations and regional entity operational requirements. Essential for aggregator compliance tracking, registration validation, and ensuring ']
  primary_contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Aggregator relationships are managed by specific utility account managers (employees) who coordinate contracts, performance monitoring, and settlement. Primary_contact fields denormalize employee data']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Aggregators are registered as vendors for payment processing and contract management. Business process: financial settlement, invoice verification, payment terms administration, performance bond track']
  aggregation_platform varchar [note: 'Name or description of the technology platform or system used by the aggregator to manage and dispatch aggregated customer load.']
  aggregator_code varchar [note: 'Short business code or abbreviation used internally to identify the aggregator in operational systems and reports.']
  aggregator_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the third-party demand response aggregator or curtailment service provider as registered with regulatory authorities.']
  aggregator_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the aggregator relationship with the utility, indicating whether the aggregator is authorized to aggregate customer load.']
  aggregator_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the aggregator based on its primary business model and service offering in the demand response ecosystem.']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'Approved methodology used to calculate customer baseline load for measurement and verification of demand response performance (e.g., high-X-of-Y, regression-based).']
  business_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the aggregators primary business address, typically including street number and street name.']

  business_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the aggregators primary business address.']
  capacity_payment_rate_per_mw decimal(10,4) [note: 'Monthly or annual capacity payment rate in dollars per megawatt of enrolled demand response capacity, paid to the aggregator for maintaining availability.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the aggregator with respect to regulatory requirements, performance obligations, and contractual terms.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date or expiration date of the aggregator service agreement, after which the contract must be renewed or terminated.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the aggregator service agreement with the utility, marking the beginning of the contractual relationship.']
  contracted_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total contracted demand response capacity in megawatts that the aggregator has committed to provide under the service agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the aggregator record was first created in the system, capturing the initial registration date.']
  dispatch_notification_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to notify the aggregator of demand response event dispatch instructions (e.g., API, email, phone).']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number for the aggregator organization, used for business identity verification and credit assessment.']
  enrolled_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Current enrolled demand response capacity in megawatts from customer accounts aggregated by this provider, representing actual load available for curtailment.']
  ferc_registration_number varchar [note: 'FERC-issued registration identifier for the aggregator entity, required for participation in wholesale demand response markets.']
  incentive_rate_per_mwh decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard incentive payment rate in dollars per megawatt-hour of verified demand response performance delivered by the aggregator.']
  iso_rto_registration_number varchar [note: 'ISO or RTO market participant identifier assigned to the aggregator for wholesale demand response market participation and settlement.']
  last_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance review or audit conducted to assess the aggregators adherence to regulatory and contractual requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update or modification to the aggregator record, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  maximum_event_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable duration in hours for a single demand response event under the aggregator service agreement.']
  maximum_events_per_year varchar [note: 'Maximum number of demand response events that can be called per calendar year under the aggregator service agreement.']
  measurement_verification_protocol varchar [note: 'Specific measurement and verification protocol or standard applied to validate demand response performance and calculate incentive payments.']
  minimum_notice_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum advance notice time in hours required before a demand response event can be dispatched to the aggregator.']
  next_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review or audit of the aggregators operations and performance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the aggregator relationship and operations.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms for incentive payments and settlements with the aggregator, such as net-30 or net-60 days.']
  performance_bond_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar value of the performance bond or financial guarantee posted by the aggregator to ensure compliance with contractual capacity commitments and performance obligations.']
  performance_bond_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current performance bond or financial guarantee, after which the bond must be renewed or replaced.']
  performance_penalty_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Financial penalty rate applied per MW of underperformance when the aggregator fails to deliver committed demand response capacity during an event.']
  service_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic service territory or jurisdiction where the aggregator is authorized to operate and aggregate customer demand response load.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which demand response performance settlements and incentive payments are calculated and paid to the aggregator.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number (EIN or TIN) for the aggregator legal entity, used for financial reporting and tax compliance.']
  telemetry_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'Frequency in minutes at which telemetry data must be transmitted from aggregated customer sites to the utility or ISO/RTO (e.g., 5-minute intervals).']
  telemetry_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data from aggregated customer sites is required for dispatch and settlement purposes.']

  Note: 'Master record for third-party demand response aggregators and curtailment service providers (CSPs) registered to aggregate customer load for ISO/RTO wholesale DR markets. Captures aggregator legal entity, FERC/ISO registration ID, service territory, aggregation technology platform, contractual capacity commitment (MW), performance bond, compliance status, and active program associations. SSOT for aggregator identity and contractual management.'
}

Table demand.interruptible_tariff_agreement {
  interruptible_tariff_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interruptible service tariff agreement. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account enrolled in this interruptible tariff agreement. Links to the customer account that holds the contractual relationship.']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Interruptible tariff agreements are negotiated and managed by key account managers (employees) who work with large commercial/industrial customers on contract terms, compliance testing, and curtailmen']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party demand response aggregator managing this interruptible load resource on behalf of the customer. Nullable if the utility manages the resource directly.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tariff agreements create bill credits (revenue reductions) that must post to specific GL accounts for revenue recognition. Bill_credit_rate_per_kw drives monthly journal entries requiring GL account m']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Interruptible tariff enrollment for behind-the-meter generation requires completed interconnection approval. Tariff bill credits are calculated based on interconnection-approved export capacity. Compl']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Tariff agreements must comply with approved tariff schedules filed with PUCs. Essential for tariff compliance validation, rate case support, and ensuring interruptible rate offerings match regulatory ']
  peak_demand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.peak_demand. Business justification: Interruptible tariffs are designed to reduce peak demand; agreements reference peak demand forecasts for capacity credit calculation, rate design, and avoided capacity cost determination. Essential fo']
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: Interruptible tariff customers have contractual notification requirements and bill credit terms for planned interruptions. Business process: Coordinating planned maintenance outages with interruptible']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service premise where the interruptible load is located. Identifies the physical location subject to load curtailment.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the interruptible tariff agreement. Used in customer communications, billing statements, and regulatory filings.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interruptible service agreement based on firmness and curtailment conditions. Determines the priority and conditions under which load may be interrupted.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at expiration unless either party provides notice of termination. True for evergreen agreements, false for fixed-term agreements.']
  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the customer\'s baseline load for measurement and verification (M&V) of curtailment performance. Determines how actual load reduction is measured against expected load.']
  bill_credit_rate_per_kw decimal(12,4) [note: 'The monthly bill credit rate in dollars per kilowatt ($/kW) that the customer receives for enrolling contracted interruptible capacity. Compensates the customer for maintaining load curtailment capability.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the customer with respect to their interruptible tariff obligations. Reflects whether the customer has successfully curtailed load during recent dispatch events.']
  contracted_interruptible_load_kw decimal(15,3) [note: 'The committed load capacity in kilowatts (kW) that the customer agrees to curtail upon utility request. This is the baseline interruptible capacity used for billing credits and compliance measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interruptible tariff agreement record was first created in the system. Establishes the record creation audit trail.']
  dispatch_priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for dispatching this interruptible load resource relative to other demand response resources. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for dispatch.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the interruptible tariff agreement becomes binding and the utility gains the contractual right to curtail load. Marks the start of the agreement term.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the interruptible tariff agreement expires and the utility no longer has the right to curtail load under this agreement. Nullable for evergreen agreements subject to annual renewal.']
  interruptible_tariff_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interruptible tariff agreement. Indicates whether the agreement is currently in force and eligible for dispatch.']
  interruption_notice_period_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum advance notice time in minutes that the utility must provide before initiating a load curtailment event. Defines the customer\'s preparation window.']
  iso_rto_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interruptible load capacity is enrolled in an ISO (Independent System Operator) or RTO (Regional Transmission Organization) wholesale demand response program. True if enrolled in wholesale market programs.']
  iso_rto_resource_code varchar [note: 'The unique resource identifier assigned by the ISO (Independent System Operator) or RTO (Regional Transmission Organization) for this interruptible load in wholesale market programs. Nullable if not enrolled in ISO/RTO programs.']
  last_compliance_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance test or actual curtailment event used to verify the customer\'s ability to meet their contracted interruptible load obligation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this interruptible tariff agreement record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  maximum_annual_interruption_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The maximum total number of hours per year that the utility may interrupt service under this agreement. Protects the customer from excessive curtailment and defines the upper bound of utility dispatch rights.']
  maximum_curtailment_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The maximum duration in hours for a single load curtailment event. Protects the customer from excessively long interruptions.']
  maximum_events_per_year varchar [note: 'The maximum number of discrete curtailment events that the utility may dispatch in a calendar year under this agreement. Limits the frequency of customer disruption.']
  minimum_curtailment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The minimum duration in minutes for which a load curtailment event must be sustained once initiated. Ensures operational effectiveness of demand response dispatch.']
  non_compliance_penalty_rate_per_kw decimal(12,4) [note: 'The penalty rate in dollars per kilowatt ($/kW) assessed when the customer fails to curtail the contracted load during a dispatch event. Enforces contractual compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional terms, conditions, special provisions, or operational notes related to this interruptible tariff agreement.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the interruptible tariff agreement was executed by both the utility and the customer. Establishes the legal binding date of the contract.']
  tariff_agreement_to_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Interruptible tariff agreements formalize the enrollment of large C&I customers. Required to link contractual obligations to program participation.']
  tariff_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific interruptible service tariff schedule under which this agreement operates. References the PUC (Public Utility Commission) approved rate schedule.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the agreement. Defines the notice period for voluntary termination.']
  to_dr_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — Interruptible tariff agreements are linked to specific DR enrollments for the customer. Required for tariff compliance tracking.']
  year_to_date_event_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of discrete curtailment events dispatched under this agreement in the current calendar year. Tracked against the maximum events per year limit.']
  year_to_date_interruption_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Cumulative total hours of load interruption dispatched under this agreement in the current calendar year. Tracked against the maximum annual interruption hours limit.']

  Note: 'Master record for interruptible service tariff agreements between the utility and large commercial/industrial customers, capturing tariff schedule reference, contracted interruptible load (kW), maximum annual interruption hours, interruption notice period, bill credit rate ($/kW), penalty for non-compliance, agreement effective and expiration dates, and current compliance status. SSOT for interruptible tariff obligations and load curtailment rights.'
}

  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: DLC devices are deployed and controlled by geographic zone for targeted load management during peak periods. Zone-level control enables localized demand reduction for distribution constraint managemen']

  baseline_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate the customer baseline load for this device. Determines how curtailment is measured and verified for incentive payments.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Primary communication protocol used by the DLC device to receive control signals and report status. Determines network infrastructure requirements and latency characteristics.']
  control_strategy varchar [note: 'The control algorithm applied to this device during DR events. Defines how the device modulates load to achieve curtailment while minimizing customer comfort impact.']
  controlled_load_type varchar [note: 'Type of electrical load that the DLC device controls. Used for load forecasting, curtailment planning, and customer comfort impact assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac_cooling|hvac_heating|water_heater|ev_charger|pool_pump|battery_storage|general_appliance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DLC device record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the DLC device was permanently removed from service. Used for asset lifecycle tracking and program capacity reporting.']
  decommission_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the device was decommissioned. Used for failure analysis, vendor performance evaluation, and program improvement.']
  device_ownership varchar [note: 'Indicates who owns the physical DLC device. Determines maintenance responsibility, asset capitalization, and end-of-life disposition.']

  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment status of the device in the DR program. Determines whether the device can be dispatched during DR events and is eligible for incentive payments.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the DLC device. Critical for security patch management, feature availability, and protocol compatibility.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the DLC device was physically installed at the customer premise. Used for warranty period calculation and device lifecycle tracking.']
  iso_rto_resource_code varchar [note: 'Resource identifier assigned by the ISO/RTO for wholesale market participation. Used for market settlement and capacity credit tracking.']
  last_communication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful communication between the DLC device and the utility control system. Used for device health monitoring and connectivity issue detection.']
  last_control_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the device last responded to a DR control signal. Used for performance verification and device responsiveness tracking.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance or service activity performed on the device. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling and device reliability tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DLC device record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  max_curtailment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum number of consecutive minutes the device can be curtailed during a single DR event. Enforces customer comfort limits and program rules.']
  network_address varchar [note: 'IP address, MAC address, or protocol-specific network identifier for the DLC device. Used for device provisioning, network troubleshooting, and security monitoring.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the next preventive maintenance activity is scheduled for the device. Used for field service planning and resource allocation.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the DLC device. Determines whether the device is available for DR event dispatch and eligible for incentive payments.']
  opt_out_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is permitted to opt out of individual DR events. Determines event dispatch logic and customer notification requirements.']
  opt_out_count_ytd varchar [note: 'Number of times the customer has opted out of DR events in the current calendar year. Used to enforce program opt-out limits and track customer engagement.']
  rated_load_kw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nameplate rated power consumption of the controlled load in kilowatts. Used for calculating total enrolled capacity and expected curtailment during DR events.']
  security_certificate_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the digital security certificate used for encrypted communication. Critical for maintaining secure command and control of DLC devices.']
  telemetry_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Frequency in seconds at which the device reports status and operational data. Determines data granularity for M&V and real-time monitoring.']
  to_dr_enrollment varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_enrollment.dr_enrollment_id — DLC devices are installed at customer premises under a specific DR enrollment. Required for device-to-program mapping during event dispatch.']
  total_curtailment_hours_ytd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative hours of load curtailment delivered by this device in the current calendar year. Used for incentive calculation and program impact reporting.']
  total_events_dispatched_ytd varchar [note: 'Total number of DR events this device has been dispatched for in the current calendar year. Used for program performance reporting and customer communication.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty for the DLC device expires. Used for maintenance planning and replacement cost forecasting.']

  Note: 'Master record for utility-owned or utility-managed direct load control (DLC) devices installed at customer premises, including smart thermostats, load control switches, water heater controllers, and EV charger controllers. Captures device type, manufacturer, firmware version, installation date, controlled load type (HVAC, water heater, EV), rated load (kW), communication protocol (AMI, RF, cellular), operational status, and last communication timestamp.'
}

Table demand.vpp_dispatch {
  vpp_dispatch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VPP dispatch instruction. Primary key for the VPP dispatch transaction.']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party aggregator or utility entity managing the VPP portfolio and coordinating DER dispatch.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: VPP dispatch operations are audited for market participation compliance and anti-manipulation rules (FERC market behavior surveillance). Required for market behavior audit trail, dispatch compliance v']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: VPP dispatches provide regulation reserves and frequency response within specific control areas. Link required for AGC signal coordination, ACE management, and balancing authority settlement. Control ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: VPP dispatch operations incur costs (DERMS platform, settlement processing, telemetry) that must be allocated to cost centers. Settlement_amount represents expense requiring cost center assignment for']
  der_interconnection_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.der_interconnection_point. Business justification: VPP dispatch commands target specific DER interconnection points for real-time control, telemetry validation, and settlement. Critical for DERMS operations, IEEE 1547 compliance, and market participat']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: VPP dispatch commands target specific DER systems for aggregated response. DERMS integration requires system-level interconnection specifications, telemetry configuration, and communication protocol d']
  dispatch_operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: VPP dispatches are executed by control room operators (employees) who coordinate distributed energy resources in real-time for grid services. Dispatch_operator is text field denormalizing employee dat']
  dispatch_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to generation.dispatch_schedule. Business justification: Virtual power plant dispatches are coordinated with generation dispatch schedules by ISO/RTO for integrated grid balancing. Operators track both supply-side and demand-side dispatch instructions for r']
  dr_capacity_registration_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_capacity_registration.dr_capacity_registration_id — VPP dispatches target registered DER portfolios. This FK links the dispatch instruction to the capacity registration for performance tracking and settlement.']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.energy_price. Business justification: VPP dispatch optimization uses energy price forecasts to maximize revenue and minimize costs in wholesale markets. Essential for economic dispatch decisions, market bidding strategies, and real-time d']
  forecast_generation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.forecast_generation. Business justification: VPP dispatch coordinates with generation forecasts (especially renewable DERs) to balance supply-demand and optimize bidding. Critical for VPP portfolio optimization, renewable integration, and ancill']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: VPP dispatch decisions require real-time and short-term load forecasts to optimize DER dispatch and market participation. Essential for economic dispatch, grid services provision, and wholesale market']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: VPP dispatch targets specific distribution assets (substations, feeders, transformers) for locational demand response to defer capital upgrades, manage thermal constraints, and provide voltage support']
  to_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event.dr_event_id — VPP dispatches may be coordinated with or triggered by DR events. This FK enables correlation of VPP grid service dispatches with DR event lifecycle.']
  transmission_substation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transmission.transmission_substation. Business justification: VPP dispatches address transmission substation constraints by reducing load in specific service territories. Grid operators coordinate VPP dispatch with transmission operations to manage substation th']
  vpp_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the aggregated portfolio of Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) that received this dispatch instruction.']
  vpp_dr_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_event.dr_event_id — VPP dispatches occur in the context of DR events or market signals. Required to correlate VPP actions with overall DR event management.']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the aggregator or DERMS platform acknowledged receipt of the VPP dispatch instruction. Used to track communication latency and dispatch readiness.']
  actual_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual measured power response in megawatts (MW) delivered by the VPP portfolio during the dispatch period. Used for measurement and verification (M&V) and settlement calculations.']
  baseline_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Calculated baseline power level in megawatts (MW) representing what the VPP portfolio would have consumed or generated without the dispatch instruction. Used for measurement and verification (M&V) to calculate actual response.']
  bidirectional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the VPP dispatch instruction utilized bidirectional DER capabilities (both charging and discharging). True for dispatches involving energy storage or vehicle-to-grid (V2G) resources; false for unidirectional load curtailment.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the VPP dispatch instruction was cancelled. Examples include grid condition change, market price change, or operational constraint. Null if the dispatch was not cancelled.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction was cancelled before completion. Null if the dispatch was not cancelled.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction was completed and DER resources returned to normal operation. Marks the end of the dispatch lifecycle.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the VPP dispatch met the minimum performance requirements for the contracted service type. True if performance ratio exceeded the compliance threshold; false otherwise. Used for capacity accreditation and penalty assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this VPP dispatch record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  der_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual DER assets (batteries, electric vehicles, smart inverters, controllable loads) that participated in this VPP dispatch instruction.']
  der_response_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of DER assets in the VPP portfolio that successfully responded to the dispatch instruction, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.92 for 92% response rate). Used to assess portfolio reliability.']
  derms_integration_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier linking this VPP dispatch to the corresponding instruction or transaction in the Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS). Enables traceability between utility dispatch systems and DER control platforms.']
  dispatch_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the VPP dispatch instruction in minutes. Calculated as the difference between dispatch end time and dispatch start time.']
  dispatch_end_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction expires and DER resources should return to normal operation.']
  dispatch_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the VPP dispatch instruction, formatted as VPP- followed by 10 digits. Used for external communication with ISO/RTO and aggregators.']
  dispatch_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the VPP dispatch instruction. Emergency dispatches override normal operations; high priority dispatches support critical grid conditions; normal and low priority dispatches support economic optimization.']
  dispatch_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or operational reason for issuing the VPP dispatch instruction. Examples include peak demand management, renewable integration, frequency response, voltage support, or market arbitrage.']
  dispatch_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or entity that initiated the VPP dispatch instruction. Distinguishes between utility Energy Management System (EMS), ISO/RTO market dispatch, aggregator platform, Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS), or manual operator dispatch.']
  dispatch_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction becomes effective and DER resources should begin responding.']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the VPP dispatch instruction. Tracks progression from issuance through execution to completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|issued|acknowledged|active|completed|cancelled|failed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction was issued to the aggregated DER portfolio. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.']
  iso_rto_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the ISO or RTO market region where the VPP dispatch is being executed. Examples include CAISO, ERCOT, MISO, PJM, NYISO, ISO-NE, and SPP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|MISO|PJM|NYISO|ISO-NE|SPP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lmp_energy_price decimal(10,4) [note: 'Locational Marginal Price for energy at the pricing node during the dispatch interval, expressed in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh). Used for settlement and revenue calculation.']
  market_product varchar [note: 'Specific wholesale market product or ancillary service that the VPP dispatch is fulfilling. Examples include Day-Ahead Market (DAM) energy, Real-Time Market (RTM) energy, regulation up/down, or operating reserves. Maps to ISO/RTO product definitions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about the VPP dispatch. Used to capture non-standard conditions or operator observations.']
  performance_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of actual MW response to target MW dispatch, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.95 for 95% performance). Used to assess VPP portfolio compliance and reliability for capacity accreditation.']
  pricing_node varchar [note: 'Locational Marginal Price (LMP) node or pricing point where the VPP portfolio is registered and settled in the wholesale market. Used for financial settlement and locational value calculation.']
  ramp_rate_mw_per_min decimal(8,3) [note: 'Required rate of change in power output in megawatts per minute (MW/min). Specifies how quickly the VPP portfolio must transition from current state to target dispatch level.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of grid service requested from the VPP portfolio. Includes energy arbitrage, capacity provision, frequency regulation, spinning reserve, non-spinning reserve, and voltage support. Determines the operational mode and response requirements for the DER fleet.']
  settlement_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total financial settlement amount in dollars for the VPP dispatch performance. Calculated based on actual MW response, LMP energy price, and applicable market product rates. Used for aggregator and customer incentive payments.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amount. Typically USD for North American energy markets.']
  settlement_interval varchar [note: 'Time granularity for measuring and settling the VPP dispatch performance. Aligns with ISO/RTO market settlement intervals (typically 5-minute, 15-minute, or hourly).']
  target_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Target power output or curtailment in megawatts (MW) requested from the VPP portfolio. Positive values indicate generation or discharge; negative values indicate load reduction or charging.']
  telemetry_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Frequency in seconds at which real-time telemetry data was collected from the VPP portfolio during the dispatch. Typical values range from 1 to 60 seconds depending on service type and ISO/RTO requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this VPP dispatch record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a Virtual Power Plant (VPP) dispatch instruction issued to an aggregated portfolio of DERs (batteries, EVs, smart inverters, controllable loads) to provide grid services beyond traditional DR curtailment. Captures dispatch timestamp, service type (energy, capacity, frequency regulation, spinning reserve, voltage support), target MW, ramp rate, dispatch duration, ISO/RTO market product, settlement interval, actual response (MW), DERMS integration reference, and dispatch source (utility, ISO/RTO, aggregator). Distinct from standard DR events as VPP dispatches coordinate bidirectional DER resources for wholesale market products and ancillary services.'
}

Table demand.dr_capacity_registration {
  dr_capacity_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DR capacity registration record. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual customer for single-customer registrations. Null for aggregator portfolios or VPP resource pools.']
  aggregator_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.aggregator.aggregator_id — Aggregator portfolios register capacity with ISO/RTOs. After merging aggregator_portfolio into dr_capacity_registration, the aggregator FK is essential.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Capacity registrations require management approval before ISO/RTO submission due to financial obligations and performance penalties. Approved_by is text field. Essential for internal controls, authori']
  capacity_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.capacity_requirement. Business justification: DR capacity registrations must align with ISO/RTO capacity requirements for resource adequacy planning. Registrations are validated against capacity requirements to ensure sufficient installed capacit']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capacity registrations have administrative costs (ISO/RTO fees, compliance testing, certification) and generate revenue (annual_capacity_payment_usd) that must be tracked by cost center for budget man']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Capacity registration portfolio composition requires DER system technical specifications from interconnection approval. Performance testing uses interconnection-approved nameplate capacity and inverte']
  dr_aggregator_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the curtailment service provider or aggregator managing this DR capacity portfolio. Null for utility-managed or individual customer registrations.']
  dr_capacity_registration_to_aggregator varchar [note: 'FK to demand.aggregator.aggregator_id — Many capacity registrations are submitted by aggregators. This FK links the registration to the aggregator entity for portfolio management and FERC Order 2222 compliance.']
  dr_capacity_registration_to_program varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Capacity registrations are made under specific DR programs for ISO/RTO market participation. This FK links the registered capacity to the program for reporting and settlement.']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Capacity is registered under specific DR programs for ISO/RTO markets. Required for program-level capacity reporting.']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Capacity registrations are zone-specific for local capacity requirements and transmission constraint management. Zone assignment is required for ISO/RTO capacity market participation, zonal capacity a']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: ISO/RTO capacity market registration requires proof of interconnection approval for DER resources. Registered capacity cannot exceed interconnection-approved export capacity. Certification and complia']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Capacity registrations must comply with ISO/RTO market rules and FERC Order 719 demand response obligations. Essential for capacity market compliance, registration validation, and ensuring DR resource']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service premise for single-premise registrations. Null for multi-premise portfolios.']
  primary_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the utility DR program under which this capacity is enrolled. Links to retail program structure and incentive rules.']
  to_aggregator_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.aggregator.aggregator_id — Aggregator portfolio registrations must reference the aggregator entity. Required for aggregator performance tracking and settlement.']
  to_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Capacity registrations are made under specific DR programs for ISO/RTO market participation. Required for capacity reporting.']
  annual_capacity_payment_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual capacity payment in USD for this registration, calculated as registered_capacity_mw × capacity_payment_rate_per_mw_day × days_in_season. Used for revenue forecasting and financial reporting.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration was approved by the ISO/RTO or utility. Marks the transition to certified status.']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'The ISO/RTO-approved baseline methodology used for measuring demand reduction performance (e.g., High 5 of 10, Mid 8 of 10, Regression-based). Determines how capacity performance is calculated during events.']
  capacity_obligation_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The firm capacity obligation in megawatts (MW) that must be delivered during performance assessment hours. May differ from registered capacity due to derating factors or performance adjustments.']
  capacity_payment_rate_per_mw_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'The capacity payment rate in dollars per megawatt per day ($/MW-day) for this registration, as determined by the ISO/RTO capacity auction clearing price for the zone and season.']
  capacity_season varchar [note: 'The capacity delivery year or season for which this registration is effective (e.g., 2024/2025, Summer 2024). Aligns with ISO/RTO capacity auction and delivery periods.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the DR capacity registration was certified by the ISO/RTO as meeting all technical, metering, and performance requirements.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification status of the DR capacity registration with the ISO/RTO. Certified status is required for capacity payments and market participation.']
  compliance_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent ISO/RTO compliance test or capacity performance verification test for this registration.']
  compliance_test_result_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Measured demand reduction in megawatts (MW) achieved during the most recent compliance test. Used to validate registered capacity and adjust capacity credits.']
  compliance_test_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the most recent compliance test. Failed tests may result in capacity derating or registration suspension.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  iso_rto_market varchar [note: 'The ISO/RTO market in which this DR capacity is registered. Determines market rules, capacity auction structure, and performance obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PJM|NYISO|ISO-NE|CAISO|MISO|SPP|ERCOT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  iso_rto_registration_number varchar [note: 'External registration identifier assigned by the ISO/RTO capacity market for this DR resource or portfolio. This is the business identifier used in wholesale market communications and settlements.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this registration record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special conditions, exceptions, or operational details related to this DR capacity registration. Used for operational coordination and issue tracking.']
  performance_penalty_rate_per_mwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Penalty rate in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh) applied for non-performance during ISO/RTO performance assessment hours. Varies by market and performance tier.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Performance classification tier assigned by the ISO/RTO based on historical performance, resource type, and telemetry capabilities (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2, Emergency-Only). Affects capacity credit and payment rates.']
  portfolio_composition_json varchar [note: 'JSON structure containing detailed composition of the DR portfolio, including individual customer IDs, premise IDs, meter IDs, resource types, and individual capacity contributions. Used for portfolio management and settlement allocation.']
  portfolio_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation for the DR capacity portfolio. May represent an aggregator portfolio, utility program portfolio, or Virtual Power Plant (VPP) resource pool.']
  puc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this registration must be included in state Public Utility Commission (PUC) demand response program reporting and Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance filings.']
  registered_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total registered demand response capacity in megawatts (MW) committed to the ISO/RTO capacity market. This is the capacity obligation that must be available during performance periods.']
  registration_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this DR capacity registration becomes effective and capacity obligations begin. Typically aligns with the start of the capacity delivery season.']
  registration_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this DR capacity registration expires and capacity obligations end. Typically aligns with the end of the capacity delivery season.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DR capacity registration. Active status is required for capacity payments and dispatch eligibility.']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DR capacity registration structure. Determines reporting obligations, settlement processes, and performance measurement methodologies.']
  resource_type_mix varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or JSON structure describing the mix of resource types in this registration (e.g., load_curtailment, battery_storage, solar_der, ev_charging, chp). Captures the composition of the portfolio for capacity credit calculations.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration was submitted to the ISO/RTO capacity market. Used for tracking registration timelines and auction participation.']
  submitted_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who submitted this registration to the ISO/RTO. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  telemetry_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Required telemetry reporting interval in seconds (e.g., 4, 10, 60) for real-time performance monitoring. Null if telemetry is not required.']
  telemetry_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time telemetry data submission to the ISO/RTO is required for this registration. Typically required for Tier 1 performance resources and VPP registrations.']

  Note: 'Master record of DR capacity registered with an ISO/RTO or utility capacity market, representing individual customer resources, aggregator portfolios, and VPP resource pools. Captures registration ID, ISO/RTO market, portfolio name, resource type mix (load curtailment, DER, battery, EV), capacity season, registered MW, geographic zone, performance tier and obligation, capacity payment rate ($/MW-day), compliance test results, certification status, registration effective date, portfolio composition details, and aggregator portfolio metadata (ISO/RTO registration ID, resource type mix, total registered capacity). SSOT for wholesale DR capacity positions, aggregator portfolio registrations, VPP resource pools, and ISO/RTO capacity reporting obligations.'
}

Table demand.dr_compliance_report {
  dr_compliance_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand response compliance report record. Primary key.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Compliance reports are submitted as audit evidence and may trigger follow-up audits if performance thresholds are not met. Required for audit documentation, regulatory submission tracking, and linking']
  capacity_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.capacity_requirement. Business justification: Compliance reports validate DR capacity against capacity requirements for resource adequacy assessments. Required for ISO/RTO capacity market compliance, NERC reliability standards, and state PUC reso']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Compliance reporting is a cost center activity with direct costs (auditor fees, M&V, staff time) and potential penalties (penalty_amount_usd). Cost center assignment enables budget tracking and regula']
  dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Compliance reports are filed per DR program. This FK links the regulatory filing to the program for audit trail and reporting obligation tracking.']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Regulatory compliance reporting requires linking enrolled DR capacity to approved interconnection applications for audit trail. Submitted capacity verification validates against interconnection-approv']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.irp_scenario. Business justification: DR compliance reports feed into IRP scenarios as validated demand-side resource capacity for long-term planning. Actual DR performance data informs IRP assumptions on achievable demand response capaci']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Compliance reports document adherence to specific regulatory obligations (e.g., annual DR performance reporting to PUC, ISO/RTO compliance filings). Essential for regulatory reporting, obligation fulf']
  primary_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand response program for which this compliance report is submitted.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: DR compliance reports are often submitted as regulatory filings to PUCs or FERC (e.g., annual DR program performance reports, RPS compliance filings). Essential for filing documentation, regulatory su']
  submitted_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Compliance reports to PUCs and ISO/RTOs must be submitted by authorized utility employees with regulatory filing authority. Tracks accountability for regulatory submissions, audit trails, and attestat']
  to_dr_program_id varchar [note: 'FK to demand.dr_program.dr_program_id — Compliance reports are filed per program. This FK is required for regulatory reporting workflows.']
  acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory body or ISO/RTO formally accepted this compliance report as complete and compliant.']
  actual_events_called varchar [note: 'Actual number of demand response events dispatched or called during the reporting period, including both economic and emergency events.']
  audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the compliance report was audited and validated for accuracy and completeness.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party auditor or internal compliance officer who reviewed and validated this compliance report prior to submission.']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'The baseline calculation methodology used for measurement and verification (M&V) in this compliance report (e.g., \'High 5 of 10\', \'High 4 of 5\', \'Meter Before/Meter After\', \'Regression-based\').']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination for the reporting period. compliant = met all performance and reporting requirements; non_compliant = failed to meet minimum thresholds; conditional_compliance = compliant with conditions or waivers; under_investigation = compliance determination pending; penalty_assessed = non-compliance with financial penalties imposed.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the utility representative responsible for this compliance report.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the utility representative or program manager responsible for this compliance report and available for regulatory inquiries.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the utility representative responsible for this compliance report.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this compliance report record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Composite data quality score (0.0000 to 1.0000) reflecting completeness, accuracy, and timeliness of the underlying meter data and baseline calculations used in this compliance report.']
  enrolled_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total enrolled demand response capacity in megawatts (MW) committed for the reporting period, representing the baseline program commitment.']
  iso_rto_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ISO or RTO market operator receiving this report (e.g., PJM, CAISO, ERCOT, NYISO, ISO-NE, MISO, SPP).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this compliance report record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this compliance report record was last updated or modified.']
  measurement_verification_method varchar [note: 'The measurement and verification approach used to calculate demand response performance (e.g., \'Interval Meter Data\', \'Aggregated AMI Data\', \'Direct Load Control Telemetry\', \'Stipulated Load Reduction\').']
  methodology_attestation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the utility has formally attested that the baseline and performance measurement methodology complies with ISO/RTO and regulatory requirements. True = attestation provided; False = not attested.']
  minimum_performance_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'Minimum required performance ratio (e.g., 0.7500 for 75%) that the program must achieve to remain compliant and avoid penalties.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or explanatory information regarding this compliance report, including any special circumstances, waivers, or clarifications provided to the regulatory body.']
  penalty_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial penalty amount in US dollars assessed for non-compliance or underperformance during the reporting period. Null if no penalty assessed.']
  penalty_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of the basis for any financial penalty assessed, including specific performance shortfalls or reporting violations.']
