  voltage_level_kv decimal(8,3) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the circuit or bus associated with the target device. Determines applicable safety clearance distances, PPE requirements, and qualified worker certifications required for this step.']

  Note: 'Individual step within a switching order defining a discrete device operation (open, close, tag, verify). Tracks step sequence number, device identifier, action type, required safety state, completion status, actual execution timestamp, and executing crew member. Enables granular audit trail of switching sequences for NERC compliance and post-event analysis.'
}

Table outage.outage_crew_dispatch {
  outage_crew_dispatch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each crew dispatch record within the outage management system. Primary key for the crew_dispatch data product.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Crew dispatches to transmission-connected substations affect control area operations. Coordination between field crews and control center operators is essential for safe switching and maintaining syst']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Crew labor, vehicle, and equipment costs are allocated to cost centers for O&M expense tracking, budget variance analysis, and FERC account classification. Required for labor cost accounting and regul']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field crew assigned to this dispatch. Links to the workforce domain crew master record without duplicating workforce data. Crew composition and qualifications are owned by the workforce domain.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outage event that triggered this crew dispatch. Links the dispatch record to the parent outage lifecycle managed in the Outage Management System (OMS).']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Crew dispatches are assigned to forecast zones for resource tracking, mutual aid coordination, and operational planning. Utilities use zone-level crew deployment data for storm response planning, reso']
  grid_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_switching_order. Business justification: Crew dispatches execute grid switching orders. Linking enables tracking of switching order execution status, crew assignment, and field crew coordination with control center operators.']
  restoration_action_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific restoration action or switching order this dispatch is fulfilling. Enables traceability from dispatch to restoration workflow step.']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_record. Business justification: Crew dispatches to critical infrastructure or NERC CIP assets require verification that crew members have completed required compliance training (NERC CIP personnel risk assessments, safety certificat']
  actual_arrival_at timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the crew arrived at the outage location and confirmed on-site presence. Captured via mobile device check-in or OMS field update. Used to measure travel time actuals vs. estimates.']
  actual_restoration_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when power was actually restored to affected customers following crew repair work. Used as the definitive restoration time for SAIDI (System Average Interruption Duration Index) and CAIDI (Customer Average Interruption Duration Index) calculations.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for dispatch cancellation when dispatch_status is \'cancelled\'. Captures why the crew was stood down before completing the assignment (e.g., outage restored via FLISR/remote switching, duplicate dispatch, crew reassigned to higher priority). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: duplicate_dispatch|outage_restored_remotely|crew_reassigned|weather_stand_down|customer_cancelled|other — promote to reference product]']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized cause code assigned by the crew upon arrival or completion, classifying the root cause of the outage (e.g., equipment failure, tree contact, animal contact, weather, vehicle accident). Aligns with IEEE 1366 cause code taxonomy for reliability reporting.']
  cleared_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the crew was formally released from the outage site and cleared for reassignment. May differ from work_completed_at if post-work safety checks, documentation, or switching order closeout is required.']
  contractor_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external contractor company providing the crew, when crew_type is \'contractor\' or mutual_aid_flag is true. Used for contractor performance tracking, invoice reconciliation, and mutual aid cost recovery.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the crew dispatch record was first created in the Outage Management System (OMS). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  crew_identifier varchar [note: 'Human-readable operational identifier for the dispatched crew (e.g., crew badge number, radio call sign, or crew unit code) as used by dispatchers and field supervisors in OMS communications.']
  crew_size varchar [note: 'Number of personnel included in the dispatched crew. Used for resource planning, storm response staffing analytics, and mutual aid tracking.']
  crew_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dispatched crew by functional role and capability. Determines the type of work the crew is qualified to perform at the outage site. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: line_crew|troubleshooter|contractor|tree_crew|substation_crew|damage_assessment — promote to reference product]']
  critical_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outage site or affected area includes a critical facility (e.g., hospital, emergency services, water treatment plant, military installation). Drives elevated dispatch priority and regulatory notification requirements.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer service points affected by the outage event at the time of dispatch. Used for prioritization, regulatory reporting, and SAIFI (System Average Interruption Frequency Index) calculations.']
  damage_assessment_code varchar [note: 'Field crew assessment of infrastructure damage severity at the outage site. Used for storm response resource planning, mutual aid requests, and post-event damage reporting to regulators.']
  dispatch_location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the outage site location provided to the crew at dispatch time, including landmarks, access instructions, or hazard notes. Supplements GIS coordinates for field navigation.']
  dispatch_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the dispatch record, used by field crews, dispatchers, and OMS operators for cross-system reference and communication. Format: DSP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  dispatch_priority varchar [note: 'Priority tier assigned to this dispatch, reflecting urgency based on outage impact (e.g., critical infrastructure, hospital, large customer count). Drives restoration sequencing and resource allocation decisions.']
  dispatch_source varchar [note: 'Originating channel or system through which the dispatch was initiated. Distinguishes automated OMS-driven dispatches from manual dispatcher assignments, storm center activations, or mutual aid requests.']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the crew dispatch record, tracking progression from initial assignment through field completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|dispatched|en_route|on_site|work_in_progress|completed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  dispatched_at timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp when the crew was formally dispatched from the operations center to the outage location. Used as the primary business event time for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability metric calculations.']
  dispatcher_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the dispatcher at the time of crew assignment, including special instructions, hazard warnings, customer contact information, or access restrictions for the outage site.']
  estimated_arrival_at timestamp [note: 'Calculated timestamp for when the crew is expected to arrive on-site at the outage location. Derived at dispatch time from dispatched_at plus estimated_travel_time_min. Used for customer ETR (Estimated Time to Restore) communications.']
  estimated_restoration_at timestamp [note: 'Estimated Time to Restore (ETR) as assessed by the crew or dispatcher after on-site evaluation. Used for customer notification updates and regulatory ETR reporting requirements. Distinct from estimated_arrival_at.']
  estimated_travel_time_min varchar [note: 'Estimated travel time in minutes from the crew\'s current location or staging area to the outage site at the time of dispatch. Used for restoration time estimation and customer notification ETAs.']
  field_crew_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the field crew upon arrival or completion, documenting observed conditions, equipment findings, repair actions taken, or follow-up work required. Captured via mobile OMS interface.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the crew identified additional work required beyond the initial restoration scope (e.g., permanent repair after temporary restoration, equipment replacement, vegetation management). Triggers follow-up work order creation.']
  mutual_aid_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispatched crew is a mutual aid crew provided by another utility or contractor under a mutual assistance agreement. Used for storm cost tracking, mutual aid settlement, and NERC EOP-011 compliance reporting.']
  oms_incident_number varchar [note: 'The incident or trouble call number assigned by the Outage Management System (OMS) that initiated this dispatch. Provides cross-system traceability between the OMS incident record and the dispatch record.']
  operating_district_code varchar [note: 'Internal operating district or division code for the area where the outage occurred. Used for crew assignment routing, regional performance reporting, and SAIDI/SAIFI district-level aggregation.']
  safety_clearance_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the safety clearance or hold tag issued to the crew before commencing energized work. Required for OSHA lockout/tagout compliance and NERC CIP physical security documentation.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the utility service territory or operating district in which the outage site is located. Used for regulatory reporting to State Public Utility Commissions (PUCs) and for regional resource allocation.']
  site_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the outage site to which the crew is dispatched. Sourced from GIS or OMS outage location data for field navigation and spatial analytics.']
  site_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the outage site to which the crew is dispatched. Sourced from GIS or OMS outage location data for field navigation and spatial analytics.']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispatch is associated with a declared storm or major event. When true, the dispatch may be excluded from standard SAIDI/SAIFI calculations per IEEE 1366 major event day (MED) exclusion criteria.']
  switching_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the switching order issued to the crew for safe isolation and restoration of the affected circuit segment. Links to the switching order management workflow in the ADMS.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the crew dispatch record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vehicle_identifier varchar [note: 'Operational identifier for the assigned vehicle (e.g., fleet unit number, license plate, or radio unit ID) as used by dispatchers for field tracking and resource management.']
  vehicle_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle assigned to this dispatch from the fleet management system. Links to the asset management domain vehicle master record.']
  work_completed_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the crew completed all assigned repair or restoration work at the outage site and confirmed task closure in the OMS. Used for SAIDI/CAIDI calculation and restoration performance reporting.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated work order in the asset management or ERP system (Ventyx Asset Suite or SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance) that governs the repair or restoration task assigned to this crew.']
  work_start_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the crew began active repair or restoration work at the outage site. Distinct from arrival time — accounts for safety setup, hazard assessment, and switching order receipt before work commences.']

  Note: 'Record of a field crew assignment dispatched to an outage location for investigation, repair, or restoration. Captures dispatched crew identifier, dispatch timestamp, estimated travel time, on-site arrival time, work start and completion times, crew type (line crew, troubleshooter, contractor), vehicle assignment, dispatch priority tier, and dispatch status lifecycle. Links to outage events and restoration actions without duplicating workforce master data owned by the workforce domain.'
}

Table outage.restoration_action {
  restoration_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each discrete restoration action record within the outage management system. Primary key for the restoration_action data product.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Restoration actions change load/generation balance within control areas, requiring AGC adjustments and interchange schedule updates. Essential for coordinating distribution restoration with transmissi']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Restoration work costs (switching operations, load transfers, FLISR actions) are charged to cost centers for O&M expense allocation and variance analysis against distribution operations budgets. Stand']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field crew assigned to execute this restoration action. Populated only for manual crew-dispatched actions; null for fully automated FLISR actions.']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: FLISR and manual restoration sequences must account for DER presence on segments (anti-islanding protection, voltage regulation impact, fault current contribution). Restoration logic checks DER status']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the substation serving the network segment affected by this restoration action. Used for substation-level outage aggregation and reliability reporting.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage event that this restoration action is associated with. Links the discrete action to the broader outage lifecycle managed in the OMS.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder circuit on which this restoration action was performed, as defined in the GIS and ADMS network model. Used for feeder-level reliability performance tracking.']
  outage_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the switching order that authorized or directed this restoration action, if applicable. Switching orders govern the sequence of switching operations for safe restoration.']
  primary_restoration_network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution network segment (feeder section, lateral, or zone) affected by this restoration action. Identifies the portion of the grid being isolated, sectionalized, or re-energized.']
  scada_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: Restoration actions involve SCADA-monitored devices (reclosers, switches). Linking enables real-time status verification, FLISR automation, and coordinated distribution automation with EMS operations.']
  sectionalizing_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the switching device (recloser, sectionalizer, circuit breaker, or fuse) operated to isolate the faulted segment during this action. References the device asset in the GIS or ADMS network model.']
  action_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the restoration action was confirmed complete and verified by the crew or automated system. Used to calculate action duration and assess restoration efficiency.']
  action_duration_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elapsed time in minutes from action initiation (action_timestamp) to action completion (action_completed_timestamp). Used in CAIDI calculations and crew performance benchmarking.']
  action_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or crew notes describing the specific conditions, complications, or observations encountered during this restoration action. Supports post-event analysis and lessons-learned documentation.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier for this restoration action as recorded in the OMS or switching order log (e.g., RA-2024-00451). Used by field crews, control room operators, and regulators to reference specific actions.']
  action_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number of this action within the parent outage event\'s restoration workflow. Enables step-by-step reconstruction of the restoration sequence for post-event analysis and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reporting.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the restoration action. Pending: scheduled but not yet started. In_progress: crew or automation is actively executing. Completed: action successfully finished. Failed: action attempted but unsuccessful. Cancelled: action voided before execution.']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world timestamp when this restoration action was executed or initiated in the field or by the automated system. Used as the primary event time for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculations and restoration timeline analysis.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of restoration action performed. Isolation: de-energizes a faulted segment. Sectionalizing: divides a feeder to limit the outage zone. Load transfer: moves load to an alternate feeder. Temporary bypass: restores power via a temporary path. Permanent repair: physical repair restoring normal configuration. FLISR automated: Fault Location Isolation and Service Restoration (FLISR) algorithm-driven action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this restoration action record was first created in the OMS or data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  crew_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field crew arrived on-site to begin executing this manual restoration action. Used to calculate crew travel time and on-site restoration duration for workforce performance analytics.']
  crew_dispatched_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field crew was dispatched from the control room or dispatch center to execute this restoration action. Used to measure dispatch-to-arrival interval and overall crew response time.']
  customer_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification (outage alert, ETR update, or restoration confirmation) was sent to affected customers as a result of this restoration action. Supports compliance with state PUC customer communication requirements.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer service points that were de-energized or affected by the segment targeted by this action at the time of action execution. Provides context for partial vs. full restoration assessment.']
  customers_requiring_manual varchar [note: 'Number of customers within the affected segment that could not be restored by FLISR automation and required manual crew dispatch for restoration. Highlights FLISR coverage gaps.']
  customers_restored_automatically varchar [note: 'Number of customers restored through fully automated Fault Location Isolation and Service Restoration (FLISR) self-healing actions without manual crew intervention. Key metric for FLISR performance analytics.']
  customers_restored_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer service points restored to power as a direct result of this specific restoration action. Used in SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI index calculations and regulatory reliability reporting.']
  estimated_restoration_time timestamp [note: 'Estimated Time to Restore (ETR) for the remaining affected customers at the time this action was recorded. Updated as each restoration action is completed to refine the ETR communicated to customers and regulators.']
  execution_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the restoration action was executed manually by a field crew or control room operator, fully automated by the FLISR algorithm, or semi-automated (system-initiated but operator-confirmed).']
  fault_detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fault triggering this restoration action was first detected by SCADA, FLISR, or field crew. Distinct from action_timestamp; used to calculate fault-to-isolation and fault-to-restoration intervals.']
  flisr_algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Fault Location Isolation and Service Restoration (FLISR) algorithm that executed this automated restoration action. Enables performance benchmarking across algorithm versions and supports software change management.']
  flisr_isolation_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Number of automated switching operations performed by the FLISR algorithm to isolate the faulted segment during this restoration action. Supports FLISR performance analytics and switching operation audits.']
  flisr_load_transfer_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Number of automated load transfer switching operations performed by the FLISR algorithm to restore power to non-faulted segments during this action. Key metric for FLISR self-healing effectiveness.']
  flisr_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the FLISR automated restoration attempt for this action. Success: FLISR fully restored the affected segment. Partial_success: FLISR restored a portion; manual intervention required for remainder. Failure: FLISR could not restore; full manual restoration required. Not_applicable: action was manual, not FLISR-driven.']
  is_major_event_day boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restoration action occurred during a Major Event Day (MED) as defined by IEEE 1366 beta method. MED events are typically excluded from standard SAIDI/SAIFI calculations and reported separately to regulators.']
  is_partial_restoration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this action resulted in a partial restoration (True) — restoring power to a subset of affected customers — or a full restoration of the affected segment (False). Drives ETR refinement and customer notification logic.']
  is_planned_outage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restoration action is associated with a planned maintenance outage (True) or an unplanned/forced outage (False). Planned outages are tracked separately for SAIDI/SAIFI reporting and customer notification compliance.']
  load_transfer_source_feeder varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source feeder or substation bus from which load is transferred during a load transfer restoration action. Populated when action_type is load_transfer or flisr_automated.']
  load_transfer_target_feeder varchar [note: 'Identifier of the target feeder or substation bus receiving the transferred load during a load transfer restoration action. Used to assess feeder loading and capacity constraints post-transfer.']
  oms_action_reference varchar [note: 'Source system reference key for this restoration action as recorded in the GE PowerOn ADMS Outage Management System (OMS). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational OMS.']
  outage_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized cause code for the fault or outage condition that necessitated this restoration action (e.g., equipment failure, tree contact, animal contact, storm, vehicle accident, planned maintenance). Aligns with IEEE 1366 cause code taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|tree_contact|animal_contact|storm|vehicle_accident|planned_maintenance|unknown|other — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this restoration action is associated with an outage event that must be reported to a regulatory body (NERC, FERC, State PUC, or DOE). Drives inclusion in regulatory reliability reports and OE-417 emergency incident filings.']
  scada_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this restoration action to the corresponding SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) event or alarm in OSIsoft PI historian. Enables correlation between OMS restoration records and real-time grid telemetry.']
  to_crew varchar [note: 'FK to outage.crew_dispatch.crew_dispatch_id — Each restoration action is performed by a dispatched crew. Links action to the crew that executed it for accountability and performance tracking.']
  to_crew_dispatch varchar [note: 'FK to outage.crew_dispatch.crew_dispatch_id — Restoration actions are performed by dispatched crews. This FK links the action to the crew that executed it, enabling crew performance tracking and accountability.']
  to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Every restoration action is performed within the context of an outage event. Required for step-by-step restoration tracking and ETR refinement.']
  transferred_load_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Magnitude of electrical load in Megawatts (MW) transferred from the faulted segment to an alternate feeder during a load transfer action. Used for grid capacity analysis and post-event load flow validation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this restoration action record was last modified. Tracks updates to status, customer counts, or FLISR outcome fields throughout the restoration lifecycle.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(8,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the network segment on which this restoration action was performed (e.g., 4.16, 12.47, 34.5, 69, 115 kV). Used to classify the action within transmission vs. distribution tiers.']
  weather_condition_code varchar [note: 'Weather condition code at the time of the outage event and restoration action. Used for storm-related reliability analysis, SAIDI/SAIFI major event day (MED) exclusion calculations, and regulatory storm reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: normal|wind|ice|lightning|flood|extreme_heat|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Discrete restoration action taken during an outage event to restore power to a portion of affected customers, covering both manual and automated (FLISR) restoration. Records action type (isolation, sectionalizing, temporary bypass, permanent repair, load transfer, automated FLISR), execution mode (manual crew action vs automated grid self-healing), affected segment, customers restored by this action, action timestamp, crew performing the action (if manual), partial vs. full restoration flag, FLISR-specific attributes (fault detection timestamp, faulted segment, isolation actions taken automatically, load transfer actions, FLISR algorithm version, success/failure outcome, customers restored automatically vs requiring manual intervention). Enables step-by-step restoration tracking, ETR refinement, and FLISR performance analytics.'
}

Table outage.cause {
  cause_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each outage cause code record in the reference classification table. Primary key. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a reference/lookup table of cause codes; per-role minimum categories do not apply.]']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Specific outage cause codes trigger different regulatory reporting obligations (e.g., vegetation management failures vs. equipment failures have different NERC reporting rules). Compliance teams map c']
  asset_class_applicability varchar [note: 'Identifies the class(es) of utility assets to which this cause code applies (e.g., \'Overhead Distribution\', \'Underground Transmission\', \'Substation Equipment\', \'Generation Unit\'). Supports asset-cause correlation in reliability and asset management analytics.']
  cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the outage cause into standard industry categories. Drives SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI segmentation in reliability analytics and PUC regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|weather|vegetation|animal_contact|human_error|third_party_damage|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the outage cause within the classification system. Used as the business-facing key in OMS, regulatory filings, and reliability reports. Sourced from GE PowerOn ADMS cause code configuration.']
  cause_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the outage cause, including typical failure modes, contributing conditions, and examples. Used for training, documentation, and regulatory submissions.']
  cause_group varchar [note: 'Operational grouping of cause codes for internal reporting and benchmarking purposes (e.g., \'Overhead T&D\', \'Underground T&D\', \'Substation\', \'Generation\', \'External\'). Supports drill-down analysis in reliability dashboards.']
  cause_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable short name for the outage cause (e.g., \'Tree Contact\', \'Equipment Failure - Transformer\', \'Animal Contact\'). Displayed in OMS dashboards and regulatory reports.']
  cause_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cause code record. Active codes are available for OMS event classification; deprecated codes are retained for historical reporting only; pending_review codes are under regulatory or operational review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cause code record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and change management for the reference classification table.']
  crew_type_required varchar [note: 'Identifies the type of field crew typically required to restore outages of this cause (e.g., \'Line Crew\', \'Cable Crew\', \'Substation Technician\', \'Tree Crew\', \'Damage Assessment\'). Supports automated crew dispatch recommendations in OMS.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outages with this cause trigger mandatory proactive customer notification obligations (e.g., via IVR, SMS, or email). Supports compliance with PUC customer communication requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this cause code is valid and available for use in outage event classification. Supports temporal versioning of the cause code reference table for historical reporting accuracy.']
  epa_cause_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the outage cause has environmental reporting implications under EPA regulations (e.g., spill events, emissions exceedances triggered by unplanned generation outages). Used for environmental compliance tracking.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this cause code is no longer valid for new outage event classification. Null indicates the code is currently active with no planned expiry. Supports cause code lifecycle management and historical data integrity.']
  ieee_1366_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the outage cause under IEEE Standard 1366 for electric power distribution reliability indices. Determines how interruptions associated with this cause are counted in SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI calculations.']
  insurance_claimable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outage events attributed to this cause are eligible for insurance claim submission (e.g., storm damage, third-party damage). Supports financial recovery tracking and CAPEX/OPEX classification.']
  is_controllable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outage cause is considered controllable by the utility (i.e., preventable through operational or maintenance actions). Used in reliability improvement program targeting and PUC performance benchmarking.']
  is_doe_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outage events with this cause require submission to the U.S. Department of Energy via the OE-417 Electric Emergency Incident and Disturbance Report. True = DOE OE-417 filing required.']
  is_med_exclusion boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outage events attributed to this cause qualify for exclusion from SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI baseline calculations as a Major Event Day (MED) under IEEE 1366. True = MED-eligible exclusion; False = included in standard reliability indices.']
  is_momentary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cause code is associated with momentary interruptions (duration less than 5 minutes per IEEE 1366). Momentary events are tracked separately in MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) calculations.']
  is_nerc_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outage events attributed to this cause trigger mandatory NERC event reporting obligations (e.g., EOP-004 threshold events). True = NERC reporting required; False = not required.']
  is_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cause code applies to planned (scheduled) outages rather than unplanned interruptions. Planned outages are excluded from SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculations per IEEE 1366. True = planned; False = unplanned.']
  is_puc_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outage events with this cause must be reported to the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) under applicable regulatory reporting requirements. True = mandatory PUC notification required; False = not required.']
  is_weather_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outage cause is classified as weather-related. Drives storm response analytics, MED determination, and insurance/force-majeure reporting. Distinct from the cause_category field as some sub-causes may be weather-adjacent.']
  nerc_cause_category varchar [note: 'NERC-defined top-level cause category label corresponding to the nerc_cause_code. Supports direct mapping to NERC event reporting taxonomy without requiring a separate lookup. Examples: \'Electrical\', \'Environmental\', \'Human Error\', \'Other\'.']
  nerc_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized cause code as defined by NERC for transmission and bulk electric system outage reporting. Required for NERC event reporting (EOP-004) and DOE OE-417 submissions. Maps the internal cause code to the NERC taxonomy.']
  oms_cause_code varchar [note: 'The native cause code identifier as configured in the GE PowerOn ADMS/OMS system. Used for direct system integration, data lineage tracing, and reconciliation between the silver layer and the source OMS.']
  puc_cause_code varchar [note: 'The cause code as defined in the applicable state Public Utility Commission (PUC) reporting taxonomy. Required for annual reliability reports and service quality filings. May differ from internal cause_code.']
  regulatory_reporting_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing specific regulatory reporting instructions, exceptions, or conditions associated with this cause code. Used by compliance teams to document PUC, NERC, or DOE reporting nuances for this cause.']
  reliability_index_impact varchar [note: 'Identifies which reliability performance indices (SAIDI, SAIFI, CAIDI, MAIFI) are impacted by outage events attributed to this cause. Drives automated index calculation logic in the reliability analytics platform.']
  saidi_weight_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Numeric weighting factor applied to SAIDI calculations for outages of this cause type. Allows regulatory-mandated cause-specific weighting adjustments in reliability index computations. Default is 1.0 (no adjustment).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of cause codes in OMS dropdown lists, regulatory report templates, and analytics dashboards. Lower values appear first.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this cause code was sourced or synchronized (e.g., GE PowerOn ADMS, SAP IS-U). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks silver layer.']
  sub_cause_code varchar [note: 'Secondary classification code providing finer granularity within a cause category (e.g., within \'equipment_failure\': transformer, cable, switch, fuse). Enables detailed root-cause analysis and asset failure trending.']
  sub_cause_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the sub-cause code (e.g., \'Overhead Conductor\', \'Underground Cable Fault\', \'Fuse Operation\'). Used in OMS event classification and crew dispatch workflows.']
  switching_order_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outages attributed to this cause typically require a formal switching order to be issued before restoration work can begin. Drives workflow automation in OMS switching order management.']
  third_party_liability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outages attributed to this cause may involve third-party liability (e.g., contractor damage, vehicle contact, excavation damage). Triggers claims management and legal notification workflows.']
  typical_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Historical average or benchmark restoration duration in minutes for outages attributed to this cause. Used for crew dispatch planning, customer estimated restoration time (ERT) communications, and CAIDI benchmarking.']
  typical_restoration_priority varchar [note: 'Default restoration priority level associated with outages of this cause type. Used to pre-populate restoration prioritization workflows in OMS and crew dispatch systems. Overridable at the individual outage event level.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this cause code record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking revisions to the cause code definition, category mapping, or regulatory alignment. Supports audit trail and change management for the reference classification table.']
  voltage_level_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates the voltage level(s) at which this cause code is applicable: transmission (>= 115 kV), sub-transmission, distribution (< 35 kV), or all levels. Ensures correct cause code assignment during OMS event classification.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of outage cause codes used to categorize the root cause of interruption events. Includes cause category (equipment failure, weather, vegetation, animal contact, human error, third-party damage, unknown), sub-cause code, NERC cause code mapping, PUC reportable flag, and whether the cause qualifies as a Major Event Day (MED) exclusion under IEEE 1366. Drives regulatory reporting and reliability analytics.'
}

Table outage.planned_outage_window {
  planned_outage_window_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planned_outage_window']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the substation from which the affected feeder or circuit originates. Used for switching coordination, crew dispatch, and geographic impact analysis.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary distribution feeder or circuit segment to be de-energized during the planned outage window. Sourced from the GIS network model (Esri ArcGIS Utility Network) and used for switching order generation.']
  interconnection_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.application. Business justification: Tariff rules require notifying customers with pending interconnection applications of planned outages affecting their POI. Coordination prevents construction conflicts and ensures applicants can sched']
  oms_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding event record in the Outage Management System (OMS), such as GE PowerOn ADMS. Provides traceability between the planned outage window record and the operational OMS event for reconciliation and audit.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.premise. Business justification: Planned maintenance windows often target specific premises for equipment upgrades, service line work, or meter replacements. Utilities must track affected premises for advance customer notification (r']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual duration of the de-energization in minutes, recorded upon restoration. Used to compute duration variance against planned_duration_minutes and to feed SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability indices.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which the network segment was re-energized and service restored. Used to compute actual outage duration and variance against planned duration for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI performance reporting.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time at which the network segment was de-energized. Compared against planned_start_timestamp to compute schedule variance for reliability reporting and SAIDI/SAIFI tracking.']
  advance_notice_period_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum advance notice period in hours required before the planned outage commences, as mandated by state PUC tariff rules or internal SLA policy. Drives the customer notification scheduling workflow.']
  affected_customer_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customer service points (meters) that will experience interruption during the planned outage window. Used for customer notification prioritization, regulatory reporting, and SAIFI impact assessment.']
  alternate_feed_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an alternate supply path exists to maintain service to some or all affected customers during the planned outage window. Informs restoration strategy and customer notification messaging.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the planned outage window, tracking whether the request has been reviewed and authorized by the appropriate operations or grid control authority before execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the operations supervisor, grid control authority, or system operator who authorized the planned outage window. Required for audit trail and NERC compliance documentation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the planned outage window received formal authorization from the approving authority. Part of the approval audit trail required for NERC and PUC compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the planned outage window record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, workflow SLA tracking, and advance notice compliance verification.']
  critical_customer_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical or life-support customers (medical baseline, hospitals, emergency services) within the affected area. Drives mandatory advance notification requirements and restoration prioritization per PUC rules.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer advance notification is required for this planned outage window based on PUC tariff rules, affected customer count thresholds, or presence of critical customers.']
  customer_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required customer advance notification has been dispatched for this planned outage window. Used for compliance tracking and audit trail against PUC notification obligations.']
  duration_variance_minutes varchar [note: 'Difference between actual and planned outage duration in minutes (actual_duration_minutes minus planned_duration_minutes). Positive values indicate overrun; negative values indicate early restoration. Key metric for reliability performance reporting.']
  estimated_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated peak load in megawatts (MW) that will be interrupted during the planned outage window. Used for grid stability assessment, load transfer planning, and RTO/ISO notification thresholds.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or district within the service territory where the planned outage is located (e.g., North District, Urban Core, Rural East). Used for crew dispatch routing and regional reliability reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the planned outage window record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and Silver layer incremental processing.']
  load_transfer_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether load transfer switching is required to maintain service to customers via alternate feed paths during the planned outage window. Triggers switching order generation and crew coordination.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel used to deliver the planned outage advance notification to affected customers (e.g., IVR automated call, SMS, email, postal mail, online portal, or field visit for critical customers).']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the customer advance notification was dispatched. Used to verify compliance with the required advance_notice_period_hours and to support PUC audit inquiries.']
  outage_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized cause code classifying the reason for the planned de-energization (e.g., scheduled maintenance, capital project, vegetation management, equipment replacement). Aligns with IEEE 1366 cause code taxonomy for reliability reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled_maintenance|capital_project|vegetation_management|equipment_replacement|inspection|testing|new_connection — promote to reference product]']
  outage_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the planned outage window, used in customer notifications, regulatory filings, and OMS records. Format: POW-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  outage_window_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the planned outage window through its approval and execution workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through approval, active execution, and final completion or cancellation.']
  outage_window_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planned outage window by the nature of the work activity requiring de-energization. Drives notification requirements, crew dispatch, and regulatory filing obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: maintenance|construction|upgrade|inspection|testing|emergency_planned|commissioning|decommissioning — promote to reference product]']
  planned_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the de-energization window in minutes, derived from planned start and end timestamps at scheduling time. Stored explicitly for scheduling, crew planning, and customer notification purposes.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time at which the network segment is planned to be re-energized and service restored. Used to calculate planned outage duration and customer notification windows.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time at which the network segment is planned to be de-energized to begin the maintenance or construction activity. Used for customer advance notification and crew scheduling.']
  planned_window_to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — When a planned outage window is executed, it creates an outage event. Links planned scheduling to actual execution for variance tracking.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or docket number of the regulatory filing submitted to the PUC, NERC, or FERC for this planned outage. Populated only when regulatory_filing_required is true.']
  regulatory_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this planned outage window requires a formal regulatory filing with the state PUC, NERC, or FERC prior to execution (e.g., for high-voltage transmission outages or outages affecting critical infrastructure).']
  requested_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the employee, crew supervisor, or project manager who submitted the planned outage window request. Used for accountability tracking and workflow routing.']
  requesting_department varchar [note: 'Name or code of the business unit or department (e.g., T&D Engineering, Asset Management, Construction) that initiated the planned outage window request. Used for resource allocation and OPEX/CAPEX tracking.']
  rto_iso_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planned outage must be reported to the Regional Transmission Organization (RTO) or Independent System Operator (ISO) due to transmission-level impact or market implications under NERC/FERC requirements.']
  rto_iso_outage_code varchar [note: 'Outage coordination identifier assigned by the RTO or ISO (e.g., MISO, PJM, CAISO) when the planned outage is registered in the transmission outage coordination system. Populated only when rto_iso_notification_required is true.']
  saidi_contribution_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contribution of this planned outage window to the annual System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI) in customer-minutes per customer served. Calculated as (actual_duration_minutes × affected_customer_count) / total_customers_served. Used for IEEE 1366 reliability reporting.']
  saifi_contribution decimal(10,6) [note: 'Contribution of this planned outage window to the annual System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI), calculated as affected_customer_count / total_customers_served. Used for IEEE 1366 reliability performance reporting to PUC.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regulated service territory within which the planned outage window occurs. Used for PUC jurisdictional reporting, rate case filings, and geographic performance analysis.']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this planned outage window is associated with a declared storm or major event, which may qualify for IEEE 1366 major event day (MED) exclusion from SAIDI/SAIFI reliability index calculations.']
  switching_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the switching order issued in the ADMS or OMS to execute the de-energization and re-energization sequence for this planned outage window. Links to the operational switching workflow.']
  to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Planned maintenance windows result in planned outage events. This FK links the scheduling/approval workflow to the actual outage event record.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the network segment being de-energized. Determines safety protocols, crew qualifications, and regulatory notification thresholds.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the maintenance, construction, or upgrade activity to be performed during the planned outage window. Provides context for crew briefings, customer communications, and regulatory filings.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated work order in the asset management or ERP system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA PM or Ventyx Asset Suite) that drives the maintenance, construction, or upgrade activity requiring de-energization.']

  Note: 'Scheduled planned outage window for maintenance, construction, or upgrade activities requiring deliberate de-energization of network segments. Captures planned start and end times, affected feeder/circuit, work order reference, customer notification requirement, advance notice period, regulatory filing requirement, and actual vs. planned duration variance. Distinct from unplanned outage events — has its own approval and notification workflow.'
}

Table outage.affected_customer {
  affected_customer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the affected customer association record linking a customer service point to an outage or planned maintenance event.']
  billing_service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_service_agreement. Business justification: Affected customer records need service agreement link to determine applicable credit/adjustment rules, verify eligibility for outage compensation programs, and ensure credits are posted to correct bil']
  compliance_exception_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.exception. Business justification: Critical care customers experiencing outages may trigger compliance exceptions if notification protocols weren\'t followed per regulatory requirements. Compliance monitors critical customer outages for']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account record in the Customer Information System (CIS). Sourced from SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B customer account master.']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Tracks DER-owning customers experiencing outages for NEM credit adjustments (energy not exported during outage), performance guarantee calculations, and regulatory reporting (CPUC Rule 21 requires tra']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the substation serving this customer service point at the time of the outage. Used for substation-level reliability performance reporting and asset management correlation.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage or planned maintenance event that caused this customer interruption. Links to the outage event record in the Outage Management System (OMS).']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder circuit serving this customer service point at the time of the outage. Used for feeder-level reliability analysis and SAIDI/SAIFI segmentation.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: When customers are affected by outages, credits are applied to specific invoices; direct link streamlines adjustment posting, enables invoice-level outage credit tracking for regulatory reporting, and']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the meter installed at the affected service point. Used to correlate outage impact with Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) last-gasp signals and meter data.']
  outage_crew_dispatch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crew dispatch or work order assigned to restore power at this customer service point. Links to the field crew assignment in the OMS or Work Management System.']
  outage_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the switching order or planned maintenance work order that caused this customer interruption. Populated for planned outages; null for unplanned events.']
  service_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service point (premise delivery point) associated with this customer interruption. Corresponds to the service point or premise identifier in the CIS and GIS.']
  trouble_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the customer trouble call or service request that reported this outage, as recorded in the Customer Information System (CIS) or CRM. Populated when customer_reported_flag is True.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known customer account number as assigned by the Customer Information System (CIS). Used for customer-facing communications and regulatory impact reporting.']
  advance_notice_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours of advance notice provided to this customer prior to a planned interruption. Used to verify compliance with PUC minimum advance notification requirements for planned outages (typically 48–72 hours).']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized cause code classifying the root cause of the outage affecting this customer (e.g., equipment failure, tree contact, animal contact, weather, vehicle accident). Sourced from OMS cause code taxonomy aligned with EEI/NERC reporting categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|tree_contact|animal_contact|weather|vehicle_accident|scheduled_maintenance|unknown|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this affected customer association record was first created in the system, typically when the outage event was declared and customer impact was identified by the OMS.']
  critical_care_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer at this service point is registered as a critical care or life support customer (e.g., medical baseline, home oxygen, dialysis). Critical care customers receive priority restoration and mandatory advance notification per PUC regulations.']
  critical_care_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of critical care or life support designation for the affected customer. Populated only when critical_care_flag is True. Classified as restricted health information per HIPAA and state PUC medical baseline program rules.']
  customer_class varchar [note: 'Regulatory and rate classification of the customer affected by the outage. Used for segmented reliability reporting and prioritization of restoration efforts per PUC requirements.']
  customer_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outage was identified through a customer trouble call or self-reported via web/app, as opposed to being detected automatically via AMI last-gasp, SCADA, or OMS prediction. Used for outage detection source analysis.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational source system from which this affected customer record was ingested into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit purposes.']
  der_customer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affected customer has a registered Distributed Energy Resource (DER) such as rooftop solar, battery storage, or a Net Energy Metering (NEM) arrangement. Relevant for assessing behind-the-meter (BTM) resilience and grid impact during outages.']
  estimated_restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The estimated date and time of power restoration communicated to this customer. Corresponds to the Estimated Restoration Time (ERT) published in customer-facing outage communications and OMS crew dispatch workflows.']
  interruption_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the power interruption at this customer service point in minutes, calculated from interruption start to end timestamp. Used directly in SAIDI (System Average Interruption Duration Index) and CAIDI (Customer Average Interruption Duration Index) regulatory reporting.']
  interruption_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when power supply was restored at this customer service point. Null if restoration has not yet occurred. Used in SAIDI/CAIDI duration calculations.']
  interruption_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when power supply was interrupted at this customer service point. Used as the principal business event timestamp for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations and regulatory reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this affected customer association record was most recently modified, such as when restoration status was updated, notification was sent, or duration was recalculated.']
  major_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer interruption is associated with a declared Major Event Day (MED) per IEEE 1366 criteria. Major event interruptions are excluded from standard SAIDI/SAIFI calculations and reported separately to regulators.']
  momentary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interruption is classified as a momentary interruption (duration under 5 minutes per IEEE 1366 definition). Momentary interruptions are counted in MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) rather than SAIDI/SAIFI.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to deliver the outage notification to this customer (e.g., SMS text, email, Interactive Voice Response (IVR) call, mobile app push notification). Supports multi-channel outage communication tracking.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an outage notification has been sent to this customer for the associated outage event. Used to track compliance with PUC customer notification requirements.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the outage notification was sent to this customer. Used for regulatory compliance reporting on customer notification timeliness.']
  oms_customer_ref varchar [note: 'External customer reference identifier as recorded in the Outage Management System (OMS), used for cross-system reconciliation between OMS and CIS records.']
  outage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outage event type affecting this customer. \'unplanned\' = unexpected fault-driven interruption, \'planned\' = scheduled maintenance window, \'momentary\' = interruption under 5 minutes per IEEE 1366, \'major_event\' = declared major event excluded from standard reliability indices.']
  planned_interruption_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled end time of the planned maintenance interruption for this customer service point. Populated only for planned outages. Compared against actual interruption_end_timestamp for schedule adherence reporting.']
  planned_interruption_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled start time of the planned maintenance interruption for this customer service point. Populated only for planned outages. Compared against actual interruption_start_timestamp for schedule adherence reporting.']
  regulatory_exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code indicating why this customer interruption is excluded from standard IEEE 1366 reliability index calculations. \'major_event\' = IEEE 1366 MED exclusion, \'planned\' = scheduled maintenance exclusion, \'momentary\' = under 5-minute threshold, \'none\' = included in standard indices.']
  restoration_confirmation_method varchar [note: 'The method by which power restoration was confirmed at this customer service point. \'ami_signal\' = AMI meter power-on event, \'crew_verification\' = field crew confirmation, \'customer_callback\' = customer reported power restored, \'scada\' = SCADA system indication.']
  restoration_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether power restoration at this customer service point has been confirmed, either via AMI last-gasp/power-on signal, crew field verification, or customer callback. Distinguishes crew-reported restoration from confirmed restoration.']
  restoration_status varchar [note: 'Current restoration lifecycle status for this customer service point. \'interrupted\' = power out, \'restored\' = power confirmed back, \'partial\' = partial restoration, \'pending_confirmation\' = crew reports restored but not yet confirmed, \'excluded\' = excluded from reliability indices per IEEE 1366 major event criteria.']
  saidi_contribution_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'This customer\'s individual contribution to the SAIDI reliability index in customer-minutes. Equals the interruption duration in minutes for this customer record. Aggregated across all affected customers to compute system-level SAIDI per IEEE 1366.']
  saifi_contribution decimal(10,6) [note: 'This customer\'s individual contribution to the SAIFI reliability index (value of 1 per sustained interruption, 0 for momentary). Aggregated across all affected customers to compute system-level SAIFI per IEEE 1366.']
  service_point_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the customer service point (premise delivery point) where the interruption occurred. Used in field crew dispatch and restoration confirmation.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regulated service territory in which this customer service point is located. Used for jurisdictional reliability reporting to the applicable State Public Utility Commission (PUC).']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer interruption occurred during a declared storm or major weather event. Used for storm response performance reporting and potential major event day exclusion analysis.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a specific customer service point to an outage event or planned maintenance window, capturing the customer-level impact. Records customer account reference, service point identifier, interruption start and end times, duration, notification sent flag, notification channel, critical care flag (medical baseline, life support), and restoration confirmation. Enables customer-facing outage communication and regulatory customer impact reporting.'
}

Table outage.customer_notification {
  customer_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer notification record in the outage management system. Primary key for the customer_notification data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account receiving this notification. Sourced from the Customer Information System (CIS) in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B.']
  customer_preference_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer\'s communication preference profile that governed channel selection and opt-in/opt-out status at the time this notification was generated. Enables preference-driven communication auditing.']
  etr_revision_id varchar [note: 'FK to outage.etr_revision.etr_revision_id — ETR update notifications are triggered by ETR revisions. This FK enables audit trail from revision to customer communication.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outage event that triggered this notification. Links the notification to the specific unplanned interruption or planned maintenance window in the Outage Management System (OMS).']
  message_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved message template used to compose this notification. Templates are pre-approved for regulatory compliance and brand consistency. Enables template performance analytics and A/B testing of communication effectiveness.']
  service_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service delivery point (premise) affected by the outage for which this notification was issued. Enables geographic and network-level analysis of notification coverage.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer acknowledged receipt of this notification. Null if no acknowledgment was received. Used for life-support welfare check compliance and two-way communication analytics.']
  advance_notice_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours of advance notice provided to the customer prior to a planned outage, calculated as the difference between notification sent timestamp and planned outage start timestamp. Used to verify compliance with state PUC minimum advance notice requirements (typically 48–72 hours).']
  cause_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the outage cause as communicated to the customer in this notification (e.g., \'equipment failure on Main St. feeder\', \'storm damage to transmission line\'). Supports customer transparency and regulatory reporting.']
  cis_account_number varchar [note: 'The customer account number as recorded in the Customer Information System (CIS) — SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B — at the time of notification. Provides a direct cross-reference to the billing and service account for regulatory audit and dispute resolution.']
  contact_address varchar [note: 'The specific contact address (email address, phone number, or device token) used for this notification delivery attempt, as resolved from the customer\'s preference profile. Stored for audit and delivery confirmation purposes. Classified as restricted PII.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp when this notification record was first created in the outage management system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and regulatory record-keeping requirements.']
  customer_acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer explicitly acknowledged receipt of this notification (e.g., via IVR keypress, SMS reply, or mobile app confirmation). True = acknowledged. Supports two-way communication tracking and life-support customer welfare checks.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customers affected by the associated outage event at the time this notification was generated. Included in the notification for context and used in SAIFI (System Average Interruption Frequency Index) reporting.']
  delivered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when delivery confirmation was received from the channel provider, confirming the notification reached the customer\'s device, inbox, or IVR endpoint. Null if delivery has not been confirmed or failed.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the notification delivery attempt. pending: queued for dispatch; sent: transmitted to channel provider; delivered: confirmed receipt by customer device or inbox; failed: delivery attempt unsuccessful; bounced: message returned undeliverable; opted_out: customer has opted out of this channel.']
  etr_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The Estimated Time to Restore (ETR) communicated to the customer in this notification. Represents the utility\'s best estimate of when power will be restored to the affected service point. Critical for customer satisfaction and regulatory ETR accuracy reporting.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for notification delivery failure when delivery_status is \'failed\' or \'bounced\'. Supports channel quality monitoring, contact data hygiene initiatives, and SLA root cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: invalid_contact|channel_unavailable|provider_error|customer_opted_out|number_disconnected|mailbox_full|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) of the language in which the notification was composed and delivered. Supports multilingual customer communication compliance requirements mandated by state PUCs in diverse service territories.']
  last_retry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent delivery retry attempt for this notification. Null if no retries have been attempted. Used in conjunction with retry_count to assess delivery persistence and channel failure patterns.']
  life_support_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is registered as a life support or medical baseline customer at the time of this notification. True = life support customer. Triggers priority notification handling and regulatory reporting obligations under state PUC medical baseline rules.']
  message_body varchar [note: 'Full text content of the notification message as delivered to the customer, including outage details, Estimated Time to Restore (ETR), and restoration instructions. Stored for regulatory audit and dispute resolution.']
  message_subject varchar [note: 'Subject line or headline of the notification as delivered to the customer. Applicable primarily to email and mobile app push notifications. Stored for audit, regulatory review, and communication effectiveness analysis.']
  notification_batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch or campaign in which this notification was grouped and dispatched. Multiple customer notifications for the same outage event are typically dispatched in batches. Supports batch-level delivery performance analysis and storm response coordination.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel through which the notification was dispatched to the customer. IVR: Interactive Voice Response automated call; SMS: text message; email: electronic mail; mobile_app: push notification via utility mobile application; web_portal: in-portal alert; postal_mail: physical letter for regulatory advance notice.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this notification record. Used in customer correspondence, regulatory audit trails, and SLA compliance tracking. Format: NTF- followed by 10-digit sequence.']
  notification_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this notification, governing dispatch queue order and retry escalation logic. Critical priority is used for life-support customers and medical baseline accounts. Supports regulatory requirements for prioritized restoration communication.']
  notification_to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Customer notifications are triggered by outage events (alerts, ETR updates, restoration confirmations). Must link to the event for communication audit trail.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the notification purpose. outage_alert: initial unplanned outage alert; etr_update: Estimated Time to Restore (ETR) revision; restoration_confirmation: power restored confirmation; planned_outage_notice: advance notice of scheduled maintenance; safety_alert: safety-related communication; demand_response_event: Demand Response (DR) program event notice.']
  oms_notification_ref varchar [note: 'External reference identifier assigned by the Outage Management System (OMS), specifically GE PowerOn ADMS, to this notification record. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational OMS source system.']
  opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer had opted out of this notification channel or notification type at the time of dispatch. True = customer opted out; notification may have been suppressed or sent via alternate channel per preference rules. Critical for TCPA and state PUC opt-out compliance.']
  outage_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the outage event start as communicated to the customer in this notification. Provides the customer with context on when the interruption began. Used in SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations.']
  outage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outage event type associated with this notification. Distinguishes between unplanned interruptions, scheduled maintenance windows, storm-related events, equipment failures, and transmission faults. Supports SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability metric segmentation.']
  planned_outage_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'For planned maintenance outage notifications, the scheduled end time of the planned interruption communicated to the customer. Null for unplanned outage notifications. Supports advance notice compliance and customer impact duration communication.']
  planned_outage_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'For planned maintenance outage notifications, the scheduled start time of the planned interruption communicated to the customer. Null for unplanned outage notifications. Supports advance notice compliance tracking required by state PUCs.']
  regulatory_notice_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this notification fulfills a specific regulatory advance notice obligation (e.g., state PUC-mandated 48-hour advance notice for planned outages). True = this notification is a regulatory compliance notice. Supports regulatory reporting and audit evidence.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of delivery retry attempts made for this notification after an initial failure. Used to track delivery persistence, diagnose channel reliability issues, and support SLA compliance reporting.']
  sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp when the notification was dispatched from the utility\'s outage communication platform to the delivery channel provider. Used as the primary business event time for SLA measurement and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this notification was dispatched within the required Service Level Agreement (SLA) target time. True = SLA met; False = SLA breached. Supports regulatory SLA compliance reporting to state PUCs and internal performance dashboards.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'The Service Level Agreement (SLA) target time in minutes within which this notification type must be dispatched following the triggering outage event. Defined by state PUC regulations and internal communication standards. Used as the benchmark for SLA compliance measurement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this notification record. Primarily GE PowerOn ADMS for OMS-driven notifications, but may include SAP IS-U, Oracle CC&B for billing-related outage notices, or manual OMS entries during storm response.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the utility\'s service territory or operating division within which the affected customer and service point are located. Supports territory-level SLA compliance reporting to individual state PUCs and regional storm response performance analysis.']
  to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Notifications are sent in the context of an outage event or planned outage. Required for communication SLA compliance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp of the most recent modification to this notification record, such as a delivery status update or retry attempt. Supports change tracking and SLA audit.']

  Note: 'Record of outage-related notifications sent to customers via IVR, SMS, email, or mobile app. Captures notification type (outage alert, ETR update, restoration confirmation, planned outage advance notice), channel, delivery timestamp, delivery status, customer preference reference, and opt-out flag. Supports regulatory requirements for advance notice of planned outages and tracks communication SLA compliance.'
}

Table outage.event_status_history {
  event_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for event_status_history']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the field crew associated with this status transition, particularly relevant for CREW_DISPATCHED and PARTIAL_RESTORE transitions. Links to the crew or work order management record in Ventyx Asset Suite or SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the specific dispatcher, crew member, or automated system process that triggered this status transition. References the workforce or system actor record. Used for accountability auditing and OMS workflow traceability.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage event whose lifecycle this status record belongs to. Links each status transition to the originating outage event in the OMS. Serves as the EVENT_SOURCE_REFERENCE for this event log entity.']
  outage_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the switching order executed as part of this status transition, applicable when the OMS issues switching instructions to isolate faults or restore service segments. Links to the switching order record in GE PowerOn ADMS.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order created or updated in conjunction with this status transition. Links the outage event status history to the maintenance and restoration work order in Ventyx Asset Suite or SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance for integrated O&M tracking.']
