  actual_restoration_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time at which service was fully restored, populated only when status_code transitions to RESTORED. Used as the definitive restoration timestamp for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculations and regulatory outage duration reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  affected_customer_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers without power at the time of this status transition. Updated at each transition to reflect partial restorations. Used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculations, customer notification prioritization, and regulatory outage reporting to State PUCs and NERC.']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized cause classification code assigned or updated at this status transition point, following IEEE 1366 or utility-specific cause code taxonomy. May be refined as investigation progresses (e.g., initially UNKNOWN, later updated to EQUIPMENT_FAILURE or TREE_CONTACT). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UNKNOWN|EQUIPMENT_FAILURE|TREE_CONTACT|ANIMAL_CONTACT|WEATHER|VEHICLE_ACCIDENT|SCHEDULED_MAINTENANCE|OVERLOAD|THIRD_PARTY — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this status history record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Distinct from transition_timestamp (the business event time). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion monitoring. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  critical_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more critical facilities (hospitals, emergency services, water treatment plants, NERC CIP-designated assets) are among the affected customers at this status transition. Triggers escalation protocols and mandatory regulatory notification timelines.']
  ert_confidence_level varchar [note: 'Dispatcher or system assessment of the confidence level in the estimated_restoration_time at this transition. Supports customer communication tiering (e.g., only publish ERT to customers when confidence is HIGH or MEDIUM) and ERT accuracy analytics.']
  estimated_restoration_time timestamp [note: 'The estimated date and time by which full service restoration is expected, as assessed at the time of this status transition. ERT may be revised at each transition as field conditions evolve. Used for customer notifications, dispatcher planning, and regulatory ERT accuracy reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  flisr_activated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GE PowerOn ADMS Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration (FLISR) automation was activated during this status transition. FLISR activations represent automated restoration actions and are tracked separately for reliability performance and automation benefit reporting.']
  major_event_day_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition occurred during a declared IEEE 1366 Major Event Day (MED), where system-wide outage levels exceed the 2.5 beta threshold. MED-flagged records are excluded from normalized SAIDI/SAIFI regulatory reporting.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The primary communication channel used to notify affected customers of this status transition. Populated when notification_sent_flag is True. Supports multi-channel notification analytics and State PUC communication compliance reporting.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification (SMS, IVR, email, or app push) was dispatched to affected customers as a result of this status transition. Supports notification compliance tracking and customer communication audit trails per State PUC notification requirements.']
  oms_event_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known OMS event reference number assigned by GE PowerOn ADMS at the time of outage event creation. Carried on each status history record to enable direct cross-reference with OMS reports, dispatcher logs, and regulatory filings without requiring a join to the parent event table.']
  outage_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Elapsed duration in minutes from the initial outage report timestamp to this status transition timestamp. Provides a running duration metric at each lifecycle stage without requiring a join to the parent event. Used for real-time SAIDI contribution monitoring and SLA tracking.']
  outage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outage event type at the time of this status record. Distinguishes unplanned interruptions (equipment failure, fault), planned maintenance windows, storm-related events, and emergency outages. Denormalized from the parent event for efficient status history reporting and SAIDI/SAIFI segmentation.']
  previous_status_code varchar [note: 'The OMS workflow state code that was active immediately before this transition occurred. Enables reconstruction of the full state machine path and supports regression analysis of outage event progressions. Null for the initial status record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: same set as status_code — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Restoration priority classification assigned at this status transition, reflecting the urgency of restoration based on affected customer types (e.g., hospitals, critical infrastructure), customer count, and storm response protocols. Drives crew dispatch sequencing and resource allocation.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition triggers a mandatory regulatory notification obligation to NERC, FERC, State PUC, or other governing bodies based on outage severity thresholds (e.g., NERC EOP-004 event reporting, State PUC major outage notification). Drives compliance workflow automation.']
  regulatory_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required regulatory notification has been submitted to the applicable governing body (NERC, FERC, State PUC) as of this status transition. Supports compliance audit trails and ensures no mandatory notifications are missed.']
  restored_customer_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of customers whose service has been restored as of this status transition. Enables tracking of partial restoration progress and supports incremental SAIDI/SAIFI credit calculations. Particularly relevant for PARTIAL_RESTORE status transitions.']
  scada_alarm_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the SCADA alarm or event in OSIsoft PI or GE PowerOn ADMS that triggered or is associated with this status transition. Enables cross-system traceability between the OMS event lifecycle and real-time SCADA/EMS alarm data.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this status transition record. Primarily GE PowerOn ADMS for automated OMS transitions, but may include SAP IS-U, Oracle CC&B, or MANUAL for dispatcher-entered records. Supports data lineage and Silver layer reconciliation.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier assigned by the source system (e.g., GE PowerOn ADMS internal status log ID) for this status transition. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver layer and the operational system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  status_code varchar [note: 'Standardized OMS workflow state code representing the current lifecycle stage of the outage event at the time of this transition. Drives the OMS state machine and is used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI milestone tracking. Serves as the EVENT_TYPE_OR_CHANNEL discriminator for this event log. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: REPORTED|INVESTIGATING|CREW_DISPATCHED|PARTIAL_RESTORE|RESTORED|CANCELLED|ON_HOLD|ESCALATED|PENDING_VERIFICATION — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  status_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable label or narrative description corresponding to the status_code at the time of this transition. Provides dispatcher-facing context for the OMS workflow state and supports customer notification messaging. Serves as the EVENT_PAYLOAD_OR_VALUE for this event log.']
  status_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the dispatcher, crew, or system at the time of this status transition. Captures contextual information such as field conditions, equipment findings, partial restoration scope, or escalation reasons. Supports OMS audit trail and post-event analysis.']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outage event is classified as part of a declared storm or major event at the time of this status transition. Storm-classified events are typically excluded from IEEE 1366 SAIDI/SAIFI calculations per the Major Event Day (MED) exclusion methodology.']
  to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Status history records belong to a parent outage event. This is the core audit trail FK for OMS workflow state machine.']
  transition_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number of this status transition within the parent outage event lifecycle, starting at 1 for the initial reported status. Used to order status history records chronologically and to identify the current (latest) status without relying solely on timestamps.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (with timezone offset) at which the outage event transitioned into this status. This is the principal EVENT_TIMESTAMP for the log record and is used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI duration calculations and OMS audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  triggering_actor_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the dispatcher, crew member, or automated system process that triggered this status transition. Retained for audit trail readability independent of workforce record changes. For system-triggered transitions, contains the system or process name (e.g., \'GE PowerOn ADMS Auto-Restore\').']
  triggering_actor_type varchar [note: 'Category of the actor (person, crew, or automated system) that initiated this status transition. Distinguishes between manual dispatcher actions, field crew updates, automated OMS/SCADA system triggers, customer-initiated reports, and supervisory overrides. Supports workflow accountability and automation rate analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this status history record was last modified in the Silver layer data product. Supports change detection, incremental processing, and audit trail requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  volt_var_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GE PowerOn ADMS Volt-VAR Optimization (VVO) actions were taken in conjunction with this status transition to manage voltage and reactive power during the outage or restoration. Supports grid operations analytics and ADMS performance reporting.']

  Note: 'Lifecycle status history of an outage event, capturing each state transition from initial report through investigation, crew dispatch, partial restoration, and full restoration. Records status code, transition timestamp, triggering actor (dispatcher, system, crew), and status notes. Enables full audit trail of outage event progression and supports OMS workflow state machine.'
}

Table outage.storm_event {
  storm_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the storm event record in the Outage Management System (OMS). Primary key for the storm_event entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Storm response costs are tracked to dedicated cost centers for PUC storm cost recovery filings, insurance claims, FEMA disaster reimbursement, and major event day (MED) cost exclusion from reliability']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Storm events impact specific forecast zones. Utilities track zone-level storm impacts for Major Event Day (MED) threshold calculations, mutual aid resource allocation, and regulatory reporting (IEEE 1']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Major storm events trigger specific regulatory reporting obligations (NERC EOP-004 emergency operations, state PUC emergency reporting requirements). Compliance teams track which obligations are activ']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary service territory affected by the storm event. Links to the geographic service area definition used for regulatory reporting and customer impact scoping.']
  affected_distribution_circuits varchar [note: 'Number of distribution circuits (below 69kV) that experienced outages or damage during this storm event. Key metric for distribution storm damage assessment and restoration prioritization.']
  affected_region_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the geographic area impacted by the storm, including counties, cities, or distribution circuits affected. Supplements the service territory reference for regulatory filings and public communications.']
  affected_transmission_lines varchar [note: 'Number of transmission lines (69kV and above) that experienced outages or damage during this storm event. Used for NERC reliability reporting and transmission damage assessment.']
  after_action_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document management reference number or URL for the post-storm After-Action Report (AAR) filed following storm demobilization. Used for continuous improvement tracking and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this storm event record was first created in the OMS or data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  critical_facilities_affected varchar [note: 'Number of critical facilities (hospitals, emergency services, water treatment plants, shelters) that experienced power interruptions during this storm event. Drives restoration prioritization and regulatory reporting.']
  customer_notification_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proactive customer notifications (via IVR, SMS, email, or web portal) were issued for this storm event. Supports PUC customer communication compliance tracking.']
  declared_end_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the storm event was officially closed and storm response operations were demobilized. Null if the storm is still active.']
  declared_start_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the storm event was officially declared by the utility\'s emergency operations center, triggering storm response activation. Principal business event timestamp for the storm lifecycle.']
  emergency_operations_center_activated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the utility\'s Emergency Operations Center (EOC) was formally activated for this storm event. EOC activation triggers escalated staffing, executive involvement, and enhanced regulatory reporting obligations.']
  eoc_activation_time timestamp [note: 'Date and time the Emergency Operations Center (EOC) was formally activated for this storm event. Null if EOC was not activated.']
  eoc_deactivation_time timestamp [note: 'Date and time the Emergency Operations Center (EOC) was formally deactivated following storm response demobilization. Null if EOC is still active.']
  estimated_storm_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost of storm response operations in US dollars, including labor (internal and mutual aid), materials, equipment, and contractor costs. Used for regulatory cost recovery filings and executive reporting. Actual costs tracked in SAP S/4HANA.']
  external_crews_deployed varchar [note: 'Actual number of external line crews that arrived and were deployed during this storm event. Compared against requested count for mutual aid effectiveness analysis.']
  external_crews_requested varchar [note: 'Number of external line crews requested through mutual aid for this storm event. Used for resource planning, cost tracking, and post-storm mutual aid reconciliation.']
  fema_disaster_declaration_number varchar [note: 'FEMA federal disaster declaration number associated with this storm event, if applicable. Required for federal cost reimbursement claims and regulatory filings when a presidential disaster declaration is issued.']
  first_customer_notification_time timestamp [note: 'Date and time the first proactive customer notification was issued for this storm event. Used for PUC compliance reporting on customer communication timeliness.']
  incident_commander varchar [note: 'Name of the designated Incident Commander responsible for overall storm response operations under the Incident Command System (ICS). Used for accountability, communications, and post-storm after-action review.']
  is_major_event_day boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storm event qualifies as a Major Event Day (MED) per IEEE 1366-2012 statistical threshold methodology. When True, outages during this event are excluded from SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations for regulatory reporting.']
  is_mutual_aid_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mutual aid from other utilities or the regional mutual aid group was formally requested for this storm event. Triggers mutual aid tracking, cost allocation, and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this storm event record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  med_threshold_saidi decimal(10,4) [note: 'The calculated SAIDI threshold value (in minutes per customer) used to determine MED qualification for this event year, per IEEE 1366-2012 beta method. Stored for audit and regulatory verification purposes.']
  meteorological_end_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time the weather event physically ended impacting the service territory per NWS or internal meteorological data. Used for IEEE 1366 MED exclusion period boundary calculations.']
  meteorological_start_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time the weather event physically began impacting the service territory, as reported by the National Weather Service or internal meteorological data. May precede the declared start time.']
  mutual_aid_coordinator varchar [note: 'Name of the regional mutual aid coordinator organization or agreement (e.g., \'EEI Mutual Aid\', \'Southeast Mutual Aid Group\') through which external crews were requested.']
  mutual_aid_request_time timestamp [note: 'Date and time the formal mutual aid request was submitted to the regional mutual aid coordinator or neighboring utilities. Null if no mutual aid was requested.']
  nerc_event_type varchar [note: 'NERC event classification code for mandatory regulatory reporting. EEA1/EEA2/EEA3 indicate Emergency Energy Alert levels; MEA indicates a Misoperation Event Alert; OE417 indicates a DOE Electric Emergency Incident and Disturbance Report filing requirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EEA1|EEA2|EEA3|MEA|OE417|other|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nws_storm_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the National Weather Service (NWS) to the weather event, used to cross-reference official meteorological data, storm tracks, and NWS storm reports for regulatory and insurance purposes.']
  oe417_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storm event meets the threshold requiring a DOE OE-417 Electric Emergency Incident and Disturbance Report filing. Triggered when 50,000+ customers are affected or critical infrastructure is impacted.']
  oe417_filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the DOE OE-417 Electric Emergency Incident and Disturbance Report was submitted for this storm event. Null if filing not required or not yet submitted.']
  peak_customers_affected varchar [note: 'Maximum number of customers simultaneously without power at any point during the storm event. Key metric for regulatory reporting, mutual aid justification, and public communications. Sourced from OMS outage aggregation.']
  puc_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storm event requires formal notification to the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) per state-specific emergency reporting thresholds (e.g., >10% of customers affected or extended outage duration).']
  puc_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the formal notification was submitted to the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) for this storm event. Null if notification not required or not yet submitted.']
  response_activation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level (1–5) indicating the severity of the storm response activation, where Level 1 is routine and Level 5 is maximum emergency mobilization. Determines staffing, resource deployment, and executive notification thresholds.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the storm response operation. Drives workflow routing in OMS and determines whether mutual aid, crew dispatch, and cost tracking are active.']
  storm_cost_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this storm event to the corresponding cost collection object in SAP S/4HANA (e.g., WBS element, internal order, or cost center) for storm cost tracking, regulatory cost recovery filings, and OPEX reporting.']
  storm_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the storm event (e.g., \'Hurricane Ida\', \'Winter Storm Uri\', \'Derecho 2023-07\'). Used for communications, regulatory reporting, and historical reference.']
  storm_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes and situational awareness comments entered by storm coordinators during the event lifecycle. Captures key decisions, escalations, and observations not captured in structured fields.']
  storm_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the storm event, used in OMS, regulatory filings, and mutual aid coordination. Format: STM-{YYYY}-{NNNNN}.']
  storm_to_outage_events varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — A storm event groups multiple outage events under coordinated response. This parent-child link is essential for storm response management and MED calculations.']
  storm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the weather event type driving storm response operations. Determines response protocols, mutual aid triggers, and IEEE 1366 Major Event Day (MED) classification criteria. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hurricane|ice_storm|derecho|tornado|heat_event|winter_storm|thunderstorm|flood|wildfire|other — promote to reference product]']
  total_customer_interruptions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of individual customer interruption events associated with this storm, accounting for customers who experienced multiple outages. Used in SAIFI calculation inputs for IEEE 1366 reporting.']
  total_outage_events varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete outage events (trouble calls, switching events, fault isolations) grouped under this storm event in the OMS. Used for storm complexity assessment and resource planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for a declared storm or major weather event that triggers storm response operations. Captures storm name, storm type (hurricane, ice storm, derecho, tornado, heat event), declared start and end times, affected service territory, Major Event Day (MED) declaration flag per IEEE 1366, storm response activation level (e.g., Level 1-5), mutual aid request flag, total estimated affected customers at peak, and storm cost tracking reference. Parent entity for grouping multiple outage events and mutual aid requests under a single coordinated storm response.'
}

Table outage.mutual_aid_request {
  mutual_aid_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the mutual aid request record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Mutual aid costs (crew deployment, equipment, lodging) are charged to cost centers for reimbursement tracking, interstate billing, and regulatory reporting. Required for cost recovery from requesting ']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outage event that triggered this mutual aid request. Links the aid request to the specific storm, major outage, or emergency event driving the need for external resources.']
  mutual_storm_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to outage.storm_event.storm_event_id — Mutual aid requests are issued in response to storm events. Required for storm cost tracking and NERC EOP mutual aid reporting.']
  primary_storm_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to outage.storm_event.storm_event_id — Mutual aid requests are issued in context of a storm event. Must link to parent storm for coordination and cost tracking.']
  regulatory_correspondence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_correspondence. Business justification: Mutual aid coordination often involves regulatory notifications and correspondence with state commissions or NERC regarding emergency response. Emergency operations centers document regulatory communi']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the responding utility formally acknowledged receipt of the mutual aid request. Used to measure response time SLA compliance.']
  actual_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost in US dollars incurred for the mutual aid deployment upon demobilization, as submitted by the responding utility for reimbursement. Populated after demobilization and cost reconciliation.']
  affected_customers_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers without power at the time the mutual aid request was issued. Provides context for the scale of the outage event and justifies the level of external resource request.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the responding utility\'s crews arrived at the staging area or work location within the requesting utility\'s service territory and became available for restoration work.']
  commitment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the responding utility formally committed to providing the requested resources, transitioning the request from acknowledged to committed status.']
  cost_reimbursement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requesting utility has agreed to reimburse the responding utility for costs incurred during the mutual aid deployment. True = reimbursable; False = non-reimbursable (goodwill aid). Drives cost tracking and invoice generation workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this mutual aid request record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  crew_count_committed varchar [note: 'Number of crew units actually committed by the responding utility. May be less than crew_count_requested if the responding utility has limited available resources.']
  crew_count_deployed varchar [note: 'Actual number of crew units that physically arrived and performed restoration work in the requesting utility\'s territory. May differ from committed count due to last-minute changes or attrition during transit.']
  crew_count_requested varchar [note: 'Number of crew units (typically crew trucks or work teams) requested from the responding utility. A crew unit typically consists of a truck and associated personnel per ESOP standards.']
  crew_type_committed varchar [note: 'Classification of crew type actually committed by the responding utility. May differ from crew_type_requested if the responding utility can only provide a partial or alternative resource match. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: line_crew|tree_crew|damage_assessment|substation|transmission|logistics — promote to reference product]']
  crew_type_requested varchar [note: 'Classification of crew type requested by the requesting utility (e.g., line crew, tree trimming crew, damage assessment crew, substation crew, transmission crew, logistics crew). Drives resource matching in ESOP coordination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: line_crew|tree_crew|damage_assessment|substation|transmission|logistics — promote to reference product]']
  demobilization_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the responding utility\'s crews completed their assignment and were released to return to their home utility. Marks the end of active mutual aid deployment.']
  equipment_count_committed varchar [note: 'Number of equipment units of the specified type actually committed by the responding utility.']
  equipment_count_requested varchar [note: 'Number of equipment units of the specified type requested from the responding utility.']
  equipment_type_requested varchar [note: 'Type of specialized equipment requested alongside crews, such as bucket trucks, digger derricks, cable pullers, generators, or mobile command units. Supplements crew type in resource matching.']
  esop_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Emergency Support Operations Plan (ESOP) or mutual aid agreement under which this request is issued. Ties the request to the governing bilateral or regional aid compact.']
  estimated_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in US dollars for the mutual aid deployment, including labor, equipment, lodging, and travel expenses. Used for budget planning and pre-authorization of reimbursable aid.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the weather or emergency event that triggered the mutual aid request. Used for post-event analysis, NERC regulatory reporting, and storm response benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hurricane|ice_storm|tornado|wildfire|flood|earthquake|major_outage|other — promote to reference product]']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number issued by the responding utility to the requesting utility for cost reimbursement of mutual aid services. Links to the accounts receivable/payable process in SAP S/4HANA.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the cost reimbursement invoice associated with this mutual aid request. Tracks the financial settlement lifecycle from invoice submission through payment or dispute resolution.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this mutual aid request record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and Silver Layer incremental processing.']
  mobilization_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date on which the committed crews and resources depart from the responding utility and begin travel to the requesting utility\'s service territory.']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this mutual aid request must be reported to NERC under EOP-005-3 or other applicable reliability standards. True = reportable event requiring NERC submission.']
  nerc_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this mutual aid event was submitted to NERC for regulatory reporting. Null if nerc_reportable_flag is False or submission is pending.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or coordination details associated with the mutual aid request. May include staging instructions, safety briefing requirements, or special equipment needs.']
  oms_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the Outage Management System (GE PowerOn ADMS) for the outage event associated with this mutual aid request. Enables traceability between the aid request and the OMS event record.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the mutual aid request, reflecting the severity of the outage event and urgency of resource need. Drives dispatch sequencing when multiple requests compete for limited responding utility resources.']
  regional_mutual_aid_group varchar [note: 'Name of the regional mutual aid compact or group under which this request is coordinated (e.g., Southeast Electric Exchange, Mid-Atlantic Mutual Aid Group, Midwest Mutual Aid Group). Identifies the regional coordination framework.']
  request_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the mutual aid request was formally submitted by the requesting utility. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the request lifecycle.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the mutual aid request, used in inter-utility communications, ESOP documentation, and regulatory reporting. Format: MAR-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the mutual aid request, tracking progression from initial submission through crew mobilization, active deployment, and final demobilization or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|committed|active|demobilized|cancelled|closed — promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this utility is the requesting party (outbound — this utility is seeking aid from a neighbor) or the responding party (inbound — this utility is providing aid to a neighbor).']
  requesting_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary operational contact at the requesting utility responsible for coordinating the mutual aid request and managing incoming crews.']
  requesting_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number for the primary operational contact at the requesting utility. Used for real-time coordination during storm response.']
  requesting_utility_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the utility that is requesting mutual aid resources. Identifies the party in need of crew or equipment support during a major outage or storm event.']
  requesting_utility_nerc_code varchar [note: 'NERC-assigned registered entity identifier for the requesting utility, used for regulatory reporting and inter-utility coordination under NERC EOP standards.']
  responding_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary operational contact at the responding utility responsible for coordinating crew dispatch and resource commitment.']
  responding_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number for the primary operational contact at the responding utility. Used for real-time coordination during crew mobilization.']
  responding_utility_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the utility that is committing resources in response to the mutual aid request. Identifies the aid-providing party.']
  responding_utility_nerc_code varchar [note: 'NERC-assigned registered entity identifier for the responding (aid-providing) utility, used for regulatory reporting and inter-utility coordination.']
  service_territory_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the primary service territory where restoration work will be performed. Used for jurisdictional compliance, state PUC reporting, and logistics planning.']
  staging_location varchar [note: 'Name or address of the staging area designated for incoming mutual aid crews within the requesting utility\'s service territory. Used for logistics coordination and crew check-in management.']

  Note: 'Record of a mutual aid request issued to or received from neighboring utilities during large-scale storm response or major outage events. Captures requesting utility, responding utility, request date, crew type and count requested, crew type and count committed, mobilization date, demobilization date, ESOP (Emergency Support Operations Plan) reference, and cost reimbursement tracking flag. Supports NERC EOP mutual aid obligations and storm recovery coordination.'
}

Table outage.reliability_index_period {
  reliability_index_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for reliability_index_period']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reporting is mandated by specific PUC obligations with defined performance thresholds and filing deadlines. Regulatory affairs maps each reporting period to the governing obligation ']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for amending or recomputing this reliability index period record (e.g., data correction from OMS, reclassification of MED events, customer count correction). Required for version_number greater than 1. Supports regulatory audit trail and PUC amendment filing documentation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the authorized utility employee (typically a Reliability Engineer or Director of Grid Operations) who approved this reliability index period record for regulatory filing. Supports SOX internal control documentation and regulatory audit requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reliability index period record was formally approved for regulatory filing. Supports SOX internal control audit trails and regulatory submission documentation.']
  caidi_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'Customer Average Interruption Duration Index expressed in minutes per interruption. Computed as total_customer_minutes_interrupted divided by total_customer_interruptions (equivalently, SAIDI divided by SAIFI). Represents the average duration of each interruption experienced by affected customers. Used to assess restoration performance.']
  caidi_normalized_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'CAIDI value computed after excluding Major Event Day (MED) interruptions per IEEE 1366 normalization methodology. Derived from normalized SAIDI and SAIFI values. Used for PUC performance standard compliance and restoration performance benchmarking.']
  computation_method varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific computational methodology applied to derive the reliability indices in this record. IEEE_1366_STANDARD applies the full IEEE 1366 methodology including MEDs; IEEE_1366_MED_EXCLUDED applies the normalized MED-excluded methodology; PUC_CUSTOM applies a state-specific PUC-mandated variation; NERC_BENCHMARK applies NERC benchmarking methodology.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reliability index period record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX internal control requirements.']
  customers_served varchar [note: 'Total number of customers served within the reporting entity boundary during the reporting period. This is the denominator used in SAIDI and SAIFI index calculations per IEEE 1366. Typically represents the average or end-of-period customer count as defined by the applicable PUC methodology.']
  equipment_failure_outage_events varchar [note: 'Number of outage events during the reporting period caused by equipment failure (excluding storm and third-party causes). Supports asset reliability analysis, maintenance planning, and capital investment justification for aging infrastructure programs.']
  filed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which this reliability index period record was submitted to the applicable regulatory authority. Null if not yet filed. Used to verify on-time filing compliance and support regulatory audit trails.']
  filing_due_date date [note: 'Deadline date by which this reliability index period record must be submitted to the applicable regulatory authority (PUC or NERC). Used to track filing compliance and trigger workflow notifications. Determined by PUC tariff or NERC standard reporting schedule.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reliability index period record in the regulatory submission workflow. DRAFT indicates preliminary computation; UNDER_REVIEW indicates internal QA; APPROVED indicates sign-off for submission; FILED indicates submission to PUC or NERC; AMENDED indicates a corrected re-submission; SUPERSEDED indicates replaced by a newer version.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reliability index period record was most recently modified. Tracks amendments, recomputations, and corrections to the reliability indices. Supports audit trail and regulatory amendment documentation.']
  maifi_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index computed as total momentary customer interruptions divided by customers served. Measures the frequency of brief power interruptions (five minutes or less) per customer. Increasingly required by PUCs as a power quality metric alongside SAIDI and SAIFI.']
  med_event_count varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days during the reporting period that qualified as Major Event Days (MEDs) per the IEEE 1366 beta + 2.5 sigma threshold methodology. Used to assess storm and extreme weather impact on reliability performance.']
  med_excluded_customer_interruptions varchar [note: 'Total customer interruptions attributable to Major Event Days (MEDs) that are excluded from the normalized IEEE 1366 index calculations. MEDs are days where the daily SAIDI exceeds a statistically derived threshold (beta + 2.5 sigma). Tracking this separately enables both total and MED-excluded reporting as required by most PUCs.']
  med_excluded_customer_minutes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total customer minutes interrupted attributable to Major Event Days (MEDs) that are excluded from the normalized IEEE 1366 index calculations. Used to compute MED-normalized SAIDI and SAIFI values required for PUC regulatory filings and NERC benchmarking.']
  med_threshold_saidi_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'The computed daily SAIDI threshold (beta + 2.5 sigma) used to classify a calendar day as a Major Event Day for this reporting period. Derived from the five-year historical daily SAIDI distribution per IEEE 1366 methodology. Documents the MED classification boundary applied to this period.']
  momentary_interruption_count varchar [note: 'Total number of momentary interruptions (duration five minutes or less per IEEE 1366 definition) experienced by customers during the reporting period. Used to compute MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) and assess power quality performance.']
  nerc_registered_entity_code varchar [note: 'NERC Compliance Registry identifier for the utility entity responsible for this reliability report. Required for NERC reliability standard compliance submissions and benchmarking participation. Aligns with the NERC Transmission Planner, Distribution Provider, or Load-Serving Entity registration.']
  oms_source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Outage Management System (OMS) instance from which the underlying outage event data was sourced for this reliability index computation. Supports data lineage tracing back to GE PowerOn ADMS or equivalent OMS. Critical for audit and regulatory filing validation.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date marking the inclusive end of the reliability reporting period. Combined with period_start_date to define the exact window over which SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI indices are computed.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date marking the inclusive start of the reliability reporting period. Combined with period_end_date to define the exact window over which SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI indices are computed.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reporting period granularity. Determines the aggregation window for SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI index calculations. MONTHLY is used for operational monitoring; QUARTERLY and ANNUAL are used for PUC regulatory filings; ROLLING_12_MONTH is used for NERC benchmarking.']
  planned_interruption_customer_minutes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Customer minutes interrupted attributable to planned (scheduled maintenance) outage events during the reporting period. Some PUC jurisdictions require separate reporting of planned vs. unplanned interruptions. Enables decomposition of total CMI into planned and unplanned components.']
  prior_period_saidi_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'SAIDI value from the immediately preceding equivalent reporting period (e.g., prior month, prior quarter, prior year) for the same reporting entity. Stored on the record to support period-over-period variance analysis in regulatory filings without requiring a self-join.']
  prior_period_saifi_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'SAIFI value from the immediately preceding equivalent reporting period for the same reporting entity. Stored on the record to support period-over-period variance analysis in regulatory filings without requiring a self-join.']
  puc_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code plus PUC suffix identifying the state Public Utility Commission jurisdiction under which this reliability index period record is filed (e.g., CA-PUC, NY-PUC, TX-PUC). Determines applicable performance standards, reporting formats, and filing deadlines.']
  puc_saidi_performance_target decimal(10,4) [note: 'Regulatory SAIDI performance target (in minutes) established by the applicable state PUC for the reporting period and reporting entity. Used to assess compliance with performance-based rate mechanisms and potential penalty or incentive triggers. Null if no PUC target applies to this reporting level.']
  puc_saifi_performance_target decimal(10,4) [note: 'Regulatory SAIFI performance target established by the applicable state PUC for the reporting period and reporting entity. Used to assess compliance with performance-based rate mechanisms and potential penalty or incentive triggers. Null if no PUC target applies to this reporting level.']
  puc_target_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the utility met all applicable PUC reliability performance targets (SAIDI and SAIFI) for this reporting period and reporting entity. True if all applicable targets were met; False if one or more targets were missed. Null if no PUC targets apply. Drives performance-based rate adjustment calculations.']
  reporting_entity_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the specific entity (circuit ID, substation code, PUC territory code, operating area code, or system-level identifier) at which the reliability indices are reported. Corresponds to the reporting_level classification. For SYSTEM level, this is the utility NERC registered entity identifier.']
  reporting_entity_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the reporting entity (e.g., circuit name, substation name, PUC territory name, operating area name). Used for display in regulatory filings and reliability performance dashboards.']
  reporting_level varchar [note: 'Organizational or geographic granularity at which the reliability indices are computed and reported. SYSTEM represents the full utility service territory; CIRCUIT and SUBSTATION represent distribution-level granularity; PUC_TERRITORY aligns with regulatory jurisdiction boundaries; OPERATING_AREA represents internal operational divisions.']
  reporting_period_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the reporting period, encoding the period type and calendar reference (e.g., MONTHLY-2024-03, QUARTERLY-2024-Q1, ANNUAL-2024). Used as the primary business key for regulatory filings and cross-system reconciliation.']
  saidi_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'System Average Interruption Duration Index expressed in minutes per customer. Computed as total_customer_minutes_interrupted divided by customers_served. Represents the average total duration of interruptions experienced per customer during the reporting period. Primary reliability metric for PUC regulatory filings and NERC benchmarking.']
  saidi_normalized_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'SAIDI value computed after excluding Major Event Day (MED) interruptions per IEEE 1366 normalization methodology. Computed as (total_customer_minutes_interrupted minus med_excluded_customer_minutes) divided by customers_served. This is the primary metric used for year-over-year performance benchmarking and PUC performance standard compliance.']
  saifi_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'System Average Interruption Frequency Index expressed as interruptions per customer. Computed as total_customer_interruptions divided by customers_served. Represents the average number of interruptions experienced per customer during the reporting period. Key reliability metric for PUC regulatory filings.']
  saifi_normalized_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'SAIFI value computed after excluding Major Event Day (MED) interruptions per IEEE 1366 normalization methodology. Computed as (total_customer_interruptions minus med_excluded_customer_interruptions) divided by customers_served. Used for PUC performance standard compliance and NERC benchmarking.']
  storm_related_outage_events varchar [note: 'Number of outage events during the reporting period that were classified as storm-related (weather-caused). Supports storm impact analysis, MED classification validation, and regulatory storm reporting requirements. Sourced from OMS event cause codes.']
  third_party_outage_events varchar [note: 'Number of outage events during the reporting period caused by third parties (e.g., vehicle accidents, dig-ins, animal contacts, vegetation encroachment by non-utility parties). Some PUC jurisdictions allow exclusion of third-party caused outages from performance standard calculations.']
  total_customer_interruptions varchar [note: 'Total number of customer interruptions (sum of customers affected across all outage events) during the reporting period. This is the numerator for SAIFI calculation. Each customer affected by each distinct outage event counts as one interruption.']
  total_customer_minutes_interrupted decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of the product of customers affected multiplied by outage duration in minutes across all outage events in the reporting period. This is the numerator for SAIDI calculation, expressed in customer-minutes. Also referred to as Customer Minutes of Interruption (CMI) in regulatory filings.']
  total_outage_events varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct outage events (interruption incidents) that occurred within the reporting entity boundary during the reporting period. Provides context for interpreting SAIDI and SAIFI values and supports frequency-based reliability analysis.']
  unplanned_interruption_customer_minutes decimal(18,2) [note: 'Customer minutes interrupted attributable to unplanned (forced/fault) outage events during the reporting period. Unplanned interruptions are the primary driver of reliability index degradation and are the focus of most PUC performance standards.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number incremented each time this reliability index period record is amended or recomputed. Version 1 represents the initial filing; subsequent versions represent amendments. Supports regulatory amendment tracking and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Periodic reliability performance record capturing computed SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI index values for a defined reporting period (monthly, quarterly, annual) at a given reporting level (system, circuit, substation, PUC territory). Stores total customer minutes interrupted, total interruptions, customers served, MED-excluded values, and IEEE 1366 normalized values. This is the authoritative operational record — NOT an analytics aggregate — used for PUC regulatory filings and NERC reliability reporting.'
}

Table outage.prediction {
  prediction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the outage prediction record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  circuit_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary distribution circuit predicted to be affected by the outage. Sourced from the GIS network model and used for crew routing and switching order preparation.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the substation associated with the predicted outage scope. Used for crew dispatch routing and switching order coordination.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system operator or dispatcher who created or last modified this prediction record when prediction_source is \'dispatcher_manual\'. Provides accountability and audit trail for manually-entered predictions.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder predicted to be affected. A feeder is a primary distribution line segment fed from a substation bus. Used for network topology analysis and switching order preparation.']
  load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.load. Business justification: Predictive outage management systems integrate load forecasts to estimate customer impact (count, MW interrupted) and prioritize response. Advanced OMS platforms reference specific load forecast runs ']
  oms_event_id varchar [note: 'Externally-known identifier assigned by the Outage Management System (OMS) to the predicted outage event. Used to correlate this prediction record with the OMS event lifecycle and downstream actual outage records.']
  predictive_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the predictive analytics model version that generated this prediction, when prediction_source is \'predictive_model\'. Enables model performance tracking, auditability, and retraining feedback loops.']
  scada_alarm_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) alarm that triggered or corroborated this outage prediction. Provides traceability back to the real-time operational data historian (OSIsoft PI) or ADMS alarm log.']
  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulated service territory within which the predicted outage falls. Used for regulatory reporting to State Public Utility Commissions (PUCs) and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI performance tracking.']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storm or major weather event record associated with this prediction, if applicable. Links the prediction to the storm response management workflow for resource mobilization and regulatory major event day (MED) classification.']
  weather_input_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.weather_input. Business justification: Outage prediction models consume weather forecast inputs (wind speed, precipitation, temperature) to predict storm-related outage likelihood and severity. Utilities use this linkage for storm prepared']
  ami_meter_count varchar [note: 'Number of Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) smart meters within the predicted outage scope that contributed last-gasp or ping-failure signals to the prediction. Indicates the AMI signal density supporting the prediction confidence.']
  confidence_level_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0.00–100.00) representing the model\'s or system\'s certainty in the predicted outage scope, cause, and affected area. Higher values indicate greater confidence. Used to prioritize crew dispatch and ETR communication decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this outage prediction record was first persisted in the data platform. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crew_preplacement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether field crews have been pre-positioned based on this outage prediction prior to field confirmation of the actual outage. When True, crew dispatch records are linked to this prediction in the workforce management system.']
  critical_customer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the predicted outage scope includes one or more critical customers (e.g., hospitals, emergency services, life-support customers, large industrial accounts with SLA obligations). When True, triggers priority restoration protocols and mandatory customer notification.']
  customer_call_count varchar [note: 'Number of inbound customer outage calls received and clustered to generate or corroborate this prediction. Used to assess the customer-reported signal strength and prioritize crew dispatch.']
  der_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the predicted outage scope includes Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) such as rooftop solar, battery storage, or microgrids that may be islanded or require isolation procedures. When True, DERMS coordination is required.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time after which this prediction is considered stale and should no longer be used for crew dispatch or ETR communication without refresh. Typically set to a configurable window (e.g., 2 hours) after prediction_timestamp. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  flisr_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the predicted outage scope is within a network segment eligible for automated Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration (FLISR) execution via the ADMS. When True, automated switching sequences may be initiated without manual dispatcher intervention.']
  major_event_day_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this prediction is associated with a Major Event Day (MED) as defined by IEEE 1366. MED events are excluded from standard SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculations and reported separately to state PUCs and NERC.']
  medical_baseline_customer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the predicted outage scope includes customers enrolled in a medical baseline or life-support program. When True, triggers mandatory proactive notification and priority restoration per state PUC regulations.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an outage notification has been sent to affected customers based on this prediction. When True, the notification timestamp and channel are tracked in the customer notification log. Supports state PUC notification compliance reporting.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the first customer notification was dispatched based on this outage prediction. Used for state PUC compliance reporting on notification timeliness. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  outage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the predicted outage by its nature. \'unplanned\' = unexpected equipment or system failure; \'planned\' = scheduled maintenance window; \'storm\' = weather-driven event; \'momentary\' = predicted to be a brief interruption (< 5 minutes per IEEE 1366); \'sustained\' = predicted to exceed the momentary threshold.']
  predicted_affected_segments varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of distribution circuit segment identifiers predicted to be de-energized. Sourced from the GIS network model topology analysis. Used for switching order preparation and crew routing.']
  predicted_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized cause code representing the predicted root cause of the outage at time of initial assessment (e.g., equipment failure, tree contact, animal contact, weather, vehicle accident, unknown). Aligned with IEEE 1366 cause code taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|tree_contact|animal_contact|weather|vehicle_accident|planned_maintenance|unknown|overload|lightning|ice_loading — promote to reference product]']
  predicted_cause_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the predicted cause of the outage as assessed at the time of prediction creation. Supplements the structured cause code with dispatcher or model-generated context.']
  predicted_customer_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customers predicted to be without power based on the initial assessment. Used for crew pre-positioning, ETR communication, and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI impact estimation. Distinct from the confirmed customer count on the actual outage event record.']
  predicted_etr timestamp [note: 'Initial estimated date and time by which power is predicted to be restored to affected customers. Communicated to customers and field crews at the time of prediction creation. Distinct from the revised ETR updated during active restoration. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  predicted_fault_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'WGS-84 decimal latitude coordinate of the predicted fault location. Used for GIS-based crew dispatch and outage mapping.']
  predicted_fault_location varchar [note: 'Predicted geographic or network location of the fault, expressed as a GIS asset identifier, address, or coordinate reference. Used to direct first-responder crews to the most probable fault point before field confirmation.']
  predicted_fault_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'WGS-84 decimal longitude coordinate of the predicted fault location. Used for GIS-based crew dispatch and outage mapping.']
  predicted_load_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated megawatts (MW) of load predicted to be interrupted based on the affected circuit segments and customer mix. Used for grid operations impact assessment and load restoration prioritization.']
  predicted_outage_duration_min varchar [note: 'Estimated duration of the outage in minutes as assessed at the time of prediction creation. Derived from historical restoration times for similar cause codes and circuit configurations. Used for CAIDI estimation and crew resource planning.']
  prediction_source varchar [note: 'The originating signal or mechanism that triggered the outage prediction. \'ami_ping\' = Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) last-gasp or ping failure; \'scada_alarm\' = SCADA system alarm from field device; \'customer_call_cluster\' = clustering of inbound customer outage calls; \'predictive_model\' = ML/analytics model output; \'dispatcher_manual\' = manually entered by a system operator or dispatcher; \'smart_meter_outage_notification\' = direct outage notification from smart meter.']
  prediction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the outage prediction record. \'pending\' = generated but not yet dispatched; \'active\' = in use for crew pre-positioning and ETR communication; \'confirmed\' = field crew has validated the predicted scope; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer prediction; \'cancelled\' = prediction voided before confirmation; \'expired\' = prediction window elapsed without field confirmation.']
  prediction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the outage prediction was system-generated or dispatcher-created. Represents the principal real-world event time for this prediction record. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  predictive_model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the predictive analytics model that generated this prediction. Used alongside predictive_model_id for model governance, auditability, and performance benchmarking.']
  protection_zone varchar [note: 'The protection zone or relay zone within which the predicted fault is located. Used by switching order coordinators to identify the correct protective device (recloser, fuse, breaker) to operate for isolation and restoration.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this outage prediction record. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this outage prediction record was last modified in the data platform. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  voltage_level varchar [note: 'Voltage classification of the network segment predicted to be affected. Determines the severity tier, crew qualification requirements, and regulatory reporting category.']
  weather_condition_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the weather conditions at the time of prediction creation (e.g., clear, wind, ice, lightning, flood). Used for cause analysis, storm classification, and reliability reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|wind|ice|lightning|flood|snow|extreme_heat|fog|tornado|hurricane — promote to reference product]']
  wind_speed_mph decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded or forecast wind speed in miles per hour at the predicted fault location at the time of prediction creation. Used for storm severity assessment and major event day (MED) classification.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a system-generated or dispatcher-created predicted outage scope at the time of initial event creation, capturing estimated number of affected customers, predicted cause, predicted affected circuit segments, confidence level, and prediction source (AMI ping, SCADA alarm, customer call clustering, predictive model). Distinct from the actual outage event — represents the initial assessment used for crew pre-positioning and ETR communication before field confirmation.'
}

Table outage.etr_revision {
  etr_revision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each ETR revision record within the outage management system. Primary key for the etr_revision data product.']
  affected_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary distribution feeder affected by the outage event at the time of this ETR revision. Supports feeder-level reliability analysis and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dispatcher or control room operator who issued this ETR revision. Supports dispatcher performance review and accountability tracking.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage event for which this ETR revision was issued. Links the revision to its originating outage lifecycle record in the OMS.']
  outage_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the switching order associated with the restoration effort at the time of this ETR revision. Links the ETR revision to the specific switching sequence being executed. Null if no switching order was active.']
  regulatory_correspondence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_correspondence. Business justification: ETR revisions for major outages may require regulatory notifications or explanations to PUC regarding restoration delays. Customer service and regulatory affairs teams document regulatory communicatio']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field work order created for the restoration effort associated with this ETR revision. Links to the asset management and workforce management system for crew and material tracking.']
  auto_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ETR revision was automatically generated by the OMS/ADMS system (e.g., via predictive restoration algorithms or rule-based triggers) rather than manually entered by a dispatcher. True if system-generated; False if manually entered.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Dispatcher or system-assigned confidence level for the revised ETR at the time of issuance. Reflects the degree of certainty based on available information (crew on-site, damage assessment status, resource availability). Used in ETR accuracy analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this ETR revision record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit trail creation timestamp for the Silver layer record. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crew_dispatched boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a field crew had been dispatched to the outage location at the time this ETR revision was issued. True if crew dispatch had occurred; False if crew was not yet dispatched. Supports correlation between crew availability and ETR accuracy.']
  crew_on_site boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a field crew had arrived on-site at the outage location at the time this ETR revision was issued. True if crew was confirmed on-site; False otherwise. Supports analysis of ETR accuracy improvement after crew arrival.']
  customer_notification_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ETR revision triggered an outbound customer notification (SMS, IVR, email, or web portal update). True if notifications were dispatched; False if the revision was internal only. Supports customer communication audit trail.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer service points affected by the outage event at the time this ETR revision was issued. May change between revisions as the outage scope is refined. Used for prioritization and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability metric calculations.']
  customers_restored_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of customer service points that had been restored at the time this ETR revision was issued. Enables tracking of restoration progress across the outage lifecycle and supports CAIDI calculation.']
  damage_assessment_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal damage assessment had been completed prior to this ETR revision. True if damage assessment was finalized; False if the revision was issued before full assessment. ETR revisions post-assessment are typically more accurate.']
  dispatcher_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes entered by the dispatcher at the time of this ETR revision. May include field crew communications, equipment status updates, or coordination details not captured in structured fields. Supports dispatcher performance review and post-event analysis.']
  estimated_mw_interrupted decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated load in megawatts (MW) interrupted by the outage event at the time of this ETR revision. Used for NERC EOP-004 regulatory reporting thresholds and for grid impact assessment.']
  etr_shift_minutes varchar [note: 'Signed integer representing the change in estimated restoration time in minutes between the previous ETR and the revised ETR. Positive values indicate a later restoration estimate; negative values indicate an earlier estimate. Derived at ingestion for analytical convenience — not a KPI aggregate.']
  mutual_aid_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mutual aid from neighboring utilities or contractors had been requested for this outage event at the time of this ETR revision. True if mutual aid was requested; False otherwise. Mutual aid requests often correlate with extended ETR windows.']
  nerc_event_type varchar [note: 'NERC EOP-004 event type classification applicable to the outage at the time of this ETR revision. Populated only when regulatory_reportable is True. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LOSS_OF_LOAD|TRANSMISSION_LOSS|GENERATION_LOSS|VOLTAGE_DEVIATION|FREQUENCY_DEVIATION|CYBER_SECURITY|PHYSICAL_SECURITY|NATURAL_DISASTER — promote to reference product]']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which customer notifications were dispatched for this ETR revision. Null or NONE if customer_notification_triggered is False. Supports multi-channel communication audit and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which customer notifications were dispatched following this ETR revision. Null if no notification was triggered. Enables measurement of lag between ETR revision and customer communication. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  oms_incident_number varchar [note: 'External incident number assigned by the GE PowerOn ADMS Outage Management System to the parent outage event. Serves as the cross-system business identifier for traceability between the OMS and downstream reporting systems.']
  outage_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized cause code for the outage event as known at the time of this ETR revision. May be updated across revisions as field crews refine the root cause. Aligns with IEEE 1366 cause code taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WEATHER|EQUIPMENT_FAILURE|TREE_CONTACT|ANIMAL_CONTACT|VEHICLE_ACCIDENT|PLANNED_MAINTENANCE|UNKNOWN|THIRD_PARTY|OVERLOAD|LIGHTNING — promote to reference product]']
  outage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outage event type at the time of this ETR revision. UNPLANNED covers unexpected equipment failures; PLANNED covers scheduled maintenance windows; STORM covers major weather events; EMERGENCY covers safety-driven de-energizations.']
  partial_restoration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a partial restoration of service had been achieved for a subset of affected customers prior to this ETR revision being issued. True if partial restoration was in effect; False if no customers had been restored yet.']
  previous_etr timestamp [note: 'The ETR value that was in effect immediately before this revision was applied. Null for the initial ETR issuance (revision_sequence_number = 1). Enables before/after comparison for ETR accuracy analysis. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Restoration priority assigned to the outage event at the time of this ETR revision. CRITICAL typically covers medical baseline customers, hospitals, or critical infrastructure; HIGH covers large commercial/industrial customers; MEDIUM and LOW cover standard residential and commercial segments.']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the parent outage event meets the threshold for mandatory regulatory reporting to NERC, FERC, or State PUC at the time of this ETR revision. True if reportable thresholds (e.g., customer count, duration, MW lost) are met; False otherwise.']
  revised_etr timestamp [note: 'The new ETR value established by this revision. Represents the dispatcher\'s best estimate of when service will be restored to affected customers at the time of revision. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  revision_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for this ETR revision. Used for root-cause analysis of ETR accuracy and dispatcher performance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: INITIAL_ESTIMATE|CREW_ASSESSMENT|SCOPE_CHANGE|RESOURCE_DELAY|WEATHER_UPDATE|RESTORATION_PARTIAL|EQUIPMENT_COMPLEXITY|SAFETY_HOLD|MUTUAL_AID_DELAY|DAMAGE_REASSESSMENT — promote to reference product]']
  revision_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative provided by the dispatcher explaining the specific circumstances that necessitated this ETR revision. Supplements the structured reason code with operational context.']
  revision_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number of this revision within the outage event, starting at 1 for the initial ETR issuance. Enables chronological ordering of all ETR revisions for a given outage and supports ETR accuracy trend analysis.']
  revision_source varchar [note: 'Indicates the originating source of this ETR revision — whether it was manually entered by a dispatcher, automatically generated by the OMS/ADMS system, submitted by a field crew, escalated by a supervisor, or issued from a storm operations center.']
  revision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this ETR revision record. ACTIVE indicates the revision is the current operative ETR; SUPERSEDED indicates it has been replaced by a later revision; CANCELLED indicates the revision was voided; FINAL indicates the outage has been restored and this was the last ETR.']
  revision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (with timezone offset) at which this ETR revision was recorded in the OMS. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the revision lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  saidi_contribution_minutes decimal(12,6) [note: 'Incremental SAIDI contribution in customer-minutes attributable to this outage event as projected at the time of this ETR revision, calculated as (customers_affected_count × projected_duration_minutes) / total_customers_served. Supports real-time reliability index tracking.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regulated service territory within which the outage event occurred. Used for jurisdictional regulatory reporting to State PUCs and for territory-level reliability benchmarking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this ETR revision record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and Silver layer provenance in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ETR revision was issued during an active declared storm event or major weather emergency. True if a storm event declaration was active; False for normal operations. Supports storm vs. non-storm ETR accuracy benchmarking.']
  to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Every ETR revision is for a specific outage event. Required for ETR accuracy tracking and customer communication audit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this ETR revision record was last modified in the data platform. Supports audit trail and change tracking for the Silver layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the affected circuit or equipment at the time of this ETR revision. Distinguishes transmission-level (>69 kV) from distribution-level (<69 kV) outages for regulatory classification and restoration complexity assessment.']
  weather_condition_code varchar [note: 'Standardized weather condition code recorded at the time of this ETR revision. Used to correlate ETR accuracy and outage duration with prevailing weather conditions for storm hardening and reliability planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CLEAR|WIND|RAIN|ICE|SNOW|LIGHTNING|EXTREME_HEAT|FLOOD — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Record of each revision to the Estimated Time of Restoration (ETR) for an outage event. Captures revision sequence number, previous ETR, revised ETR, revision timestamp, revision reason code, issuing dispatcher, and whether customer notifications were triggered by the revision. Enables ETR accuracy tracking, customer communication audit trail, and dispatcher performance review.'
}

Table outage.call {
  call_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inbound customer trouble call or AMI-generated outage report record in the Outage Management System (OMS). Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility customer account associated with the caller or AMI device that originated this outage report. Links to the Customer Information System (CIS) account record.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution substation serving the reported outage location, resolved from the GIS network model. Used for geographic grouping of calls, outage event scoping, and storm response coordination.']
  duplicate_of_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original (first-received) outage call record of which this call is a duplicate. Populated only when is_duplicate = True. Enables deduplication lineage tracing in OMS call clustering.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the call center agent who received and logged this trouble call. Populated only when call_source_type = AGENT. Used for quality assurance, workforce management, and call handling performance reporting.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage event record once this call has been matched or clustered to a confirmed outage event in the OMS. Null until the call is correlated to an event.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution circuit (feeder) serving the reported outage location, resolved from the GIS network model based on the service address or premise. Used for OMS call clustering by circuit and outage event correlation.']
  meter_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the smart meter or AMI endpoint that generated an automated outage ping or last-gasp signal, if the call source is AMI-initiated rather than a human caller.']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility service premise (physical delivery point) associated with the reported outage location. Links to the GIS/network model for spatial outage clustering and distribution circuit identification.']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the declared storm or major weather event record associated with this outage call, when weather_event_flag = True. Links to the storm event management entity for storm response coordination and MED exclusion processing.']
  affected_customers_reported varchar [note: 'Number of customers the caller reports as being affected by the outage (e.g., \'my whole street is out\'). This is a caller-provided estimate, not a system-confirmed count. Used as an early indicator for outage scope assessment and crew dispatch prioritization.']
