  ami_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the AMI endpoint (smart meter) that generated an automated last-gasp outage notification or power-off ping. Populated only when call_source_type = AMI_PING. Used to correlate AMI-detected outages with customer account and network topology.']
  ami_signal_type varchar [note: 'Type of automated signal received from the AMI endpoint that triggered this outage report. LAST_GASP = meter-detected power loss notification; POWER_RESTORE = meter-detected power restoration; TAMPER = tamper event; BLINK = momentary interruption blink count; POWER_QUALITY = voltage sag/swell event. Populated only when call_source_type = AMI_PING.']
  call_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outage call record within the OMS workflow. RECEIVED = call logged, not yet processed; PENDING_MATCH = awaiting correlation to an outage event; MATCHED = successfully linked to an outage event; DUPLICATE = identified as a duplicate of an existing call or event; CLOSED = call resolved and closed; CANCELLED = call withdrawn or invalid.']
  caller_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who placed the trouble call, as provided at time of call. May differ from the account holder name if a third party is reporting on behalf of the customer. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR/CCPA.']
  caller_phone_number varchar [note: 'Phone number provided by or captured from the caller at time of call receipt. Used for call-back confirmation and customer notification of restoration. Classified as restricted PII per GDPR/CCPA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this outage call record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer ingestion timestamp). Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and regulatory record-keeping. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  customer_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer associated with this outage call by service type. Used for restoration prioritization, regulatory reporting, and SAIDI/SAIFI index segmentation per IEEE 1366 and PUC requirements.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the data quality status of this outage call record as assessed during Silver Layer ingestion and validation. VALID = passed all validation rules; SUSPECT = passed ingestion but has anomalies requiring review; INVALID = failed critical validation; PENDING_REVIEW = awaiting manual data quality review.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the final outcome or resolution action taken on this call (e.g., EVENT_CREATED, ADDED_TO_EVENT, DUPLICATE_CLOSED, NO_OUTAGE_FOUND, REFERRED_TO_CREW, CUSTOMER_CALLBACK_SCHEDULED). Sourced from OMS disposition reference table. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EVENT_CREATED|ADDED_TO_EVENT|DUPLICATE_CLOSED|NO_OUTAGE_FOUND|REFERRED_TO_CREW|CUSTOMER_CALLBACK_SCHEDULED|INVALID — promote to reference product]']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the customer call in seconds from answer to disconnect, for calls received via IVR or live agent channels. Used for call center operational reporting, SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance, and customer experience analytics.']
  estimated_restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time of power restoration communicated to the customer at the time of call handling or subsequent notification. Sourced from OMS restoration forecasting. Used for customer expectation management and SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  hazard_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the caller reported a safety hazard associated with the outage (e.g., downed power line, sparking equipment, fire, flooding near electrical equipment). True = hazard reported; False = no hazard reported. Triggers priority escalation in OMS crew dispatch and public safety notification workflows.']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of safety hazard reported by the caller when hazard_indicator = True. Used to determine emergency response protocol, crew safety briefing requirements, and coordination with emergency services (fire, police). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DOWNED_LINE|SPARKING|FIRE|FLOODING|EQUIPMENT_DAMAGE|TREE_ON_LINE|VEHICLE_ACCIDENT|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this call has been identified as a duplicate report of an outage already captured by a prior call or AMI notification for the same event and location. True = duplicate; False = unique first report. Used by OMS call clustering logic to avoid double-counting in reliability metrics.']
  ivr_session_code varchar [note: 'Unique session identifier assigned by the Interactive Voice Response (IVR) system for calls received through the automated phone channel. Used for call traceability, IVR system reconciliation, and customer experience analytics. Populated only when call_source_type = IVR.']
  matched_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this call was successfully matched or correlated to a confirmed outage event in the OMS. Populated when call_status transitions to MATCHED. Used for OMS processing latency analysis and reliability reporting timelines. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  medical_baseline_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affected customer account is registered on the utility\'s medical baseline or life-support program, requiring priority restoration and proactive notification per PUC regulations. True = medical baseline customer; False = standard customer. Classified as confidential due to sensitivity of health-related program enrollment.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an outage acknowledgement or estimated restoration time (ERT) notification has been sent to the customer associated with this call. True = notification sent; False = not yet sent. Supports PUC customer notification compliance tracking.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the outage acknowledgement or estimated restoration time (ERT) notification was dispatched to the customer. Populated when notification_sent_flag = True. Used for PUC notification compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  oms_call_cluster_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the OMS call cluster to which this call has been assigned during the outage prediction and event correlation process. Multiple calls from the same geographic area or circuit are grouped into a cluster to predict the probable outage location and initiate crew dispatch before a formal event is confirmed.']
  outage_start_reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the caller reports the outage began, as stated by the customer or detected by the AMI device. May differ from the OMS-confirmed outage start time. Used as an input to outage event timeline reconstruction and SAIDI/CAIDI calculations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Restoration priority level assigned to this outage call based on hazard indicators, medical baseline status, customer class, and outage scope. CRITICAL = life-safety or critical infrastructure; HIGH = medical baseline or large commercial/industrial; MEDIUM = standard residential or small commercial; LOW = non-urgent or duplicate. Drives OMS crew dispatch sequencing.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the outage call or AMI-generated outage report was received by the utility system. This is the principal business event timestamp used for OMS call clustering, outage prediction, and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to this trouble call at receipt, used for customer-facing acknowledgement (e.g., IVR confirmation code, agent ticket number, web portal confirmation). Sourced from the CIS or OMS call intake module.']
  reported_symptom_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the symptom or problem reported by the caller or detected by the AMI system (e.g., FULL_OUTAGE, PARTIAL_OUTAGE, FLICKERING, LOW_VOLTAGE, DOWNED_LINE, BURNING_SMELL). Sourced from the OMS symptom code reference table. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FULL_OUTAGE|PARTIAL_OUTAGE|FLICKERING|LOW_VOLTAGE|DOWNED_LINE|BURNING_SMELL|SPARKING|BUZZING — promote to reference product]']
  reported_symptom_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the outage symptom or problem as reported by the caller or transcribed by the call center agent. Supplements the structured symptom code with verbatim customer language for OMS analysis and crew dispatch context.']
  service_address varchar [note: 'Street address of the service location reported as experiencing the outage, as provided by the caller or resolved from the account record. Used for geographic clustering in the OMS and crew dispatch routing.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the utility\'s regulated service territory or operating division in which the reported outage is located. Used for regulatory reporting segmentation to State PUCs and NERC reliability reporting.']
  source_system_call_code varchar [note: 'Original call or ticket identifier assigned by the source operational system (e.g., OMS call number from GE PowerOn ADMS, CIS interaction ID from SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer data product and the system of record.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Channel or mechanism through which the outage report was received. IVR = Interactive Voice Response automated phone system; AGENT = live call center agent; AMI_PING = Advanced Metering Infrastructure last-gasp or outage notification; MOBILE_APP = utility mobile application; WEB_PORTAL = online self-service portal; FIELD_CREW = field crew-initiated report.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this outage call record was last modified in the data platform, reflecting any status updates, event matching, or data corrections applied during the call lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  weather_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outage reported in this call is associated with a declared weather event (storm, hurricane, ice storm, heat wave, etc.). True = weather-related; False = non-weather. Relevant for IEEE 1366 Major Event Day (MED) exclusion determination and storm response activation.']

  Note: 'Record of an inbound customer trouble call or AMI-generated outage report received by the utility, used to initiate or corroborate an outage event. Captures call receipt timestamp, source type (IVR, agent, AMI ping, mobile app, web portal), reported symptom, caller account reference, associated outage event (once matched), call disposition, and whether the call was a duplicate of an existing event. Feeds OMS call clustering and outage prediction logic.'
}

Table outage.segment_impact {
  segment_impact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for segment_impact']
  cause_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outage cause classification record describing the root cause of the de-energization event for this segment.']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Network segment analysis must identify DER on affected segments for hosting capacity recalculation, protection coordination verification, and restoration priority determination. Engineering studies po']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution substation that supplies this network segment. Used for substation-level outage impact aggregation and transmission-distribution boundary analysis.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage event that caused this segment to be de-energized. Links the segment impact to the originating outage lifecycle record.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution feeder circuit that this segment belongs to. Used for feeder-level outage aggregation, reliability reporting, and ADMS topology navigation. Sourced from Esri ArcGIS Utility Network feeder model.']
  nerc_cip_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.nerc_cip_asset. Business justification: Network segment impacts may affect NERC CIP-designated BES Cyber Systems, triggering CIP incident reporting obligations. Cybersecurity teams assess whether outages impacted CIP assets to determine CIP']
  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution network segment (feeder section, lateral, transformer zone) affected by the outage. Sourced from the GIS network model in Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  outage_crew_dispatch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crew dispatch assignment record for field crews assigned to restore this segment. Links to workforce and work order management in Ventyx Asset Suite.']
  sectionalizing_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the switching device (recloser, sectionalizer, fuse, breaker) that performed the isolation of this segment. Sourced from the GIS network model and ADMS device registry. Used for asset performance tracking in Ventyx Asset Suite.']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storm or major weather event record associated with this segment impact. Null for non-storm outages. Used for storm response coordination, MED exclusion processing, and FEMA/state emergency reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment impact record was first created in the OMS or lakehouse system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record provenance tracking.']
  critical_infrastructure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment serves critical infrastructure facilities (hospitals, emergency services, water treatment, telecommunications). When true, triggers elevated restoration priority and mandatory regulatory notification.']
  customers_on_segment varchar [note: 'Total number of customer service points (meters) located on this network segment at the time of the outage event. Used as the customer count denominator for SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI reliability index calculations per IEEE 1366.']
  customers_partially_restored varchar [note: 'Number of customers on this segment restored during partial restoration switching operations prior to full segment restoration. Used to accurately credit partial restoration in SAIDI/CAIDI calculations per IEEE 1366.']
  customers_restored varchar [note: 'Number of customer service points on this segment that have been restored to service. Incremented as partial restoration switching operations are completed. Enables real-time tracking of restoration progress.']
  de_energization_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the network segment was fully restored to energized state. Null if the segment has not yet been restored. Used with de_energization_start_timestamp to compute segment outage duration for SAIDI/CAIDI calculations.']
  de_energization_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the network segment was de-energized (lost power) as recorded by the OMS or SCADA system. This is the principal business event timestamp used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations per IEEE 1366.']
  doe_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment impact is part of an event requiring reporting to the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) via Form OE-417 (Electric Emergency Incident and Disturbance Report).']
  estimated_restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time when the segment is expected to be restored to service. Published to customer notification systems and field crews. Updated dynamically as restoration progresses. Supports ETR (Estimated Time to Restore) commitments to regulators and customers.']
  fault_location_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the fault or damage location on this segment as identified by field crews or FLISR analysis (e.g., \'Pole 47 on Oak Street\', \'Underground cable section between manholes MH-12 and MH-14\'). Supports crew navigation and post-event analysis.']
  flisr_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration (FLISR) automated action applied to this segment. Indicates whether the segment was handled by automatic FLISR logic or required manual crew intervention.']
  flisr_automated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FLISR (Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration) action for this segment was executed automatically by the ADMS system (true) or required manual crew switching (false). Used to measure ADMS automation effectiveness.']
  gis_segment_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for this network segment in the Esri ArcGIS Utility Network geospatial model. Enables spatial analysis, outage mapping, and topology-based impact assessment.']
  impact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment impact record. Tracks the progression from initial de-energization through isolation, partial restoration, and full restoration. Drives OMS workflow state transitions in GE PowerOn ADMS.']
  is_med_exclusion boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment impact qualifies for Major Event Day (MED) exclusion from SAIDI/SAIFI reliability index calculations per IEEE 1366-2012 beta method. MED exclusions apply during catastrophic storm or disaster events.']
  is_momentary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment impact qualifies as a momentary interruption (typically less than 5 minutes per IEEE 1366 definition). Momentary interruptions are counted in MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) rather than SAIDI/SAIFI.']
  is_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment de-energization was a planned maintenance outage (true) or an unplanned/forced interruption (false). Planned outages are typically excluded from SAIDI/SAIFI calculations per IEEE 1366 and state PUC rules.']
  isolation_method varchar [note: 'The switching or protective device operation method used to isolate this segment from the energized network. Critical for FLISR (Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration) workflow analysis and switching order documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automatic_switch|manual_switch|fuse_operation|recloser|sectionalizer|breaker_trip — promote to reference product]']
  life_support_customers varchar [note: 'Number of customers on this segment registered as life-support or medical baseline customers. Elevates restoration priority and triggers mandatory notification protocols per state PUC regulations.']
  load_mw_at_outage decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated electrical load in megawatts (MW) on this segment at the time of de-energization. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI historian data. Used for load loss reporting, demand response impact assessment, and grid restoration planning.']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment impact contributes to a reportable event under NERC reliability standards (e.g., EOP-004 disturbance reporting thresholds). Drives mandatory NERC event reporting workflows.']
  oms_segment_ref varchar [note: 'Source system reference identifier for this segment impact record as assigned by the GE PowerOn ADMS Outage Management System. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the OMS and the lakehouse silver layer.']
  outage_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the de-energization event for this segment in minutes, calculated from de_energization_start_timestamp to de_energization_end_timestamp. Null if segment is not yet restored. Used directly in SAIDI and CAIDI reliability index calculations per IEEE 1366.']
  partial_restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when partial restoration of this segment was achieved (i.e., a subset of customers on the segment were restored via switching operations before full restoration). Null if no partial restoration occurred.']
  puc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment impact must be included in mandatory reliability reporting submissions to the state Public Utility Commission (PUC). Determined by customer count thresholds, duration thresholds, and critical infrastructure involvement.']
  restoration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to restore this segment to service. Distinguishes between switching-based restoration (no repair needed), physical repair of damaged equipment, temporary bypass, or load transfer to alternate feeder.']
  restoration_sequence_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank assigned to this segment for restoration sequencing within the outage event. Lower values indicate higher priority. Drives crew dispatch order and switching plan execution. Considers life-support customers, critical infrastructure, and load size.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the distribution network segment as maintained in the GIS and ADMS systems (e.g., feeder section code, lateral identifier). Used for cross-system reconciliation with GE PowerOn ADMS and Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the impacted distribution network segment (e.g., \'Oak Street Lateral 3\', \'Feeder 12A Section 4\'). Used in operational dashboards and crew dispatch communications.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution network segment topology type. Determines the scope of impact and applicable FLISR (Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration) switching logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: feeder_section|lateral|transformer_zone|substation_bus|service_point_group|tie_line — promote to reference product]']
  switching_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the switching order issued to field crews for isolating or restoring this segment. Links to the switching order management workflow in GE PowerOn ADMS. Required for audit trail of all switching operations.']
  to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Network segment impacts are recorded per outage event. Required for topology-based outage analysis and FLISR workflow tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment impact record was last modified. Tracks updates to restoration status, customer counts, ETR, and other operational fields throughout the outage lifecycle.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage of the impacted network segment in kilovolts (kV). Used to classify the segment within the distribution voltage hierarchy (e.g., 4kV, 12kV, 25kV, 34.5kV) and determine applicable switching procedures.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the time of the segment de-energization. Used for storm-related outage analysis, MED exclusion determination, and regulatory storm reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: normal|wind|ice|lightning|flood|heat|snow|hurricane|tornado — promote to reference product]']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Work order number from the asset management system (Ventyx Asset Suite or SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance) associated with the restoration or repair activity for this segment impact.']

  Note: 'Record of a specific distribution network segment (feeder section, lateral, transformer zone) impacted by an outage event. Captures segment identifier, segment type, de-energization start and end timestamps, isolation method, number of customers on segment, and restoration sequence priority. Enables network topology-based outage analysis and supports FLISR (Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration) workflow tracking.'
}

Table outage.outage_flisr_event {
  outage_flisr_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each automated FLISR execution event record in the distribution network self-healing system.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Automated FLISR operations on critical infrastructure are audited for compliance with operational reliability standards. Compliance audits review FLISR algorithm performance, operator override decisio']
  cause_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outage cause classification record that categorizes the root cause of the fault triggering this FLISR event (e.g., equipment failure, tree contact, animal contact, lightning). Links to the outage_cause reference product for NERC/PUC cause code reporting.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation from which the faulted feeder originates. Used for geographic and operational scoping of the FLISR event.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage event that triggered this FLISR execution. Links the automated self-healing action to the broader outage lifecycle record in the Outage Management System (OMS).']
  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific distribution network segment (line section, cable section, or switch zone) determined by the FLISR algorithm to contain the fault. Sourced from the GIS/Utility Network model.']
  primary_outage_feeder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution feeder on which the fault was detected and FLISR was triggered. Identifies the primary circuit affected by the fault event.']
  sectionalizing_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the normally-open tie switch operated by the FLISR algorithm to transfer load to the alternate feeder during restoration. Sourced from the GIS/Utility Network model. Critical for post-event switching order reconciliation.']
  tie_switch_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: FLISR (Fault Location, Isolation, Service Restoration) events use tie switches to transfer load between feeders. Linking to asset_registry enables tracking tie switch operation counts, maintenance nee']
  adms_event_ref varchar [note: 'Native event reference identifier assigned by the GE PowerOn ADMS system for this FLISR execution. Enables cross-system traceability between the data lakehouse record and the operational ADMS source system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this FLISR event record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  customers_affected_total varchar [note: 'Total number of customer service points de-energized as a result of the fault event prior to any FLISR restoration actions. Used as the baseline for SAIFI and SAIDI reliability index calculations per IEEE 1366.']
  customers_requiring_manual varchar [note: 'Number of customer service points within the faulted segment that could not be restored automatically by FLISR and require manual crew dispatch and switching operations for restoration. Drives crew dispatch prioritization.']
  customers_restored_auto varchar [note: 'Number of customer service points successfully restored to service by the FLISR automated switching and load transfer actions, without requiring manual crew intervention. Key metric for measuring FLISR effectiveness and SAIDI/CAIDI improvement.']
  doe_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FLISR event is associated with an outage that meets DOE OE-417 reporting thresholds (e.g., affecting 50,000+ customers or critical infrastructure). Triggers mandatory federal emergency reporting.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FLISR execution event. \'initiated\' = algorithm triggered; \'in_progress\' = isolation/restoration actions underway; \'completed\' = full automated restoration achieved; \'failed\' = algorithm could not restore; \'partial\' = some customers restored automatically, remainder require manual intervention; \'cancelled\' = FLISR aborted before completion.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason code or message explaining why the FLISR execution failed or was aborted. Populated only when outcome is \'failure\' or \'aborted\'. Examples: capacity constraint on alternate feeder, communication failure to remote switch, operator override, protection relay lockout.']
  fault_detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fault on the distribution network was first detected by the ADMS/FLISR system, triggering the automated response sequence. This is the principal business event timestamp for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations per IEEE 1366.']
  fault_distance_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated distance in meters from the substation or reference point to the calculated fault location on the feeder, as determined by the FLISR impedance-based fault location algorithm. Used for crew dispatch and asset inspection prioritization.']
  fault_location_confidence varchar [note: 'Confidence level assigned by the FLISR algorithm to its fault location determination. Influences whether automated restoration proceeds or is held pending manual verification. High confidence enables full automation; low confidence may trigger manual review.']
  fault_type varchar [note: 'Electrical classification of the fault detected on the distribution network. Determines the FLISR algorithm\'s isolation and restoration strategy. Phase-to-ground is the most common distribution fault type.']
  flisr_algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the FLISR algorithm deployed in the ADMS at the time of this event execution. Critical for reproducibility analysis, algorithm performance benchmarking, and post-event audit of automated decision logic.']
  flisr_event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for this FLISR execution event, as assigned by the Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS). Used in operational reports, regulatory filings, and cross-system correlation.']
  is_momentary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fault event was classified as a momentary interruption (duration ≤ 5 minutes per IEEE 1366) rather than a sustained outage. Momentary events are tracked under MAIFI rather than SAIFI/SAIDI for reliability reporting.']
  isolation_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the FLISR algorithm completed all automated isolation switching actions to contain the faulted segment. Marks the end of the isolation phase and the start of the restoration phase.']
  isolation_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in seconds from fault detection to completion of all automated isolation switching actions. Key performance indicator for FLISR speed and ADMS response time benchmarking.']
  isolation_switch_count varchar [note: 'Total number of automated switching operations executed by the FLISR algorithm during the isolation phase to contain the faulted segment. Used for asset wear tracking and switching order audit.']
  life_support_customers_affected varchar [note: 'Number of registered life support or medical baseline customers within the affected area of this FLISR event. Used to prioritize restoration sequencing and trigger mandatory customer notification per state PUC medical baseline rules.']
  load_transferred_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total electrical load in megawatts (MW) transferred to alternate feeders or tie switches by the FLISR algorithm during the restoration phase. Used to assess grid capacity utilization and validate load transfer feasibility.']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FLISR event is associated with an outage that meets NERC reporting thresholds and must be reported to NERC under applicable reliability standards (e.g., EOP-004). Drives regulatory reporting workflow.']
  oms_incident_number varchar [note: 'Incident number assigned by the Outage Management System (OMS) to the outage event associated with this FLISR execution. Enables correlation between automated FLISR actions and the broader outage incident record for regulatory reporting.']
  operator_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a dispatcher or system operator manually intervened to override, modify, or abort the FLISR automated execution sequence. True = operator intervention occurred; False = fully automated execution without human intervention.']
  operator_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason a dispatcher or operator overrode the FLISR automated execution. Populated only when operator_override_flag is True. Used for post-event analysis and FLISR algorithm improvement.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the FLISR execution event. \'success\' = all restorable customers restored automatically; \'partial_success\' = some customers restored, remainder require manual intervention; \'failure\' = FLISR could not execute any restoration; \'aborted\' = execution halted due to constraint violation or operator override.']
  pre_fault_load_mw decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total electrical load in megawatts (MW) on the affected feeder immediately prior to the fault event, as recorded by the ADMS/SCADA historian. Used to assess load transfer feasibility and post-restoration capacity margin.']
  protection_relay_operated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protection relay (recloser, sectionalizer, or breaker) operated to initiate the fault isolation sequence that triggered FLISR. True = relay operation confirmed; False = FLISR triggered by other detection method (e.g., SCADA alarm, voltage sag detection).']
  recloser_operations_count varchar [note: 'Number of automatic recloser operations that occurred on the faulted circuit prior to FLISR lockout and isolation. Used to distinguish momentary interruptions from sustained outages for IEEE 1366 MAIFI vs SAIFI classification.']
  restoration_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the FLISR algorithm completed all automated load transfer and service restoration actions. Customers restored after this timestamp required manual crew intervention.']
  restoration_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in seconds from completion of fault isolation to completion of all automated load transfer and service restoration actions. Used to measure FLISR restoration speed and its contribution to CAIDI reduction.']
  restoration_switch_count varchar [note: 'Total number of automated switching operations executed by the FLISR algorithm during the load transfer and service restoration phase. Distinct from isolation switch count — represents the re-energization actions.']
  scada_communication_status varchar [note: 'Status of SCADA communication to the affected field devices (switches, reclosers, sensors) at the time of FLISR execution. \'degraded\' or \'failed\' communication may limit FLISR automation capability and is a key factor in partial or failed outcomes.']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FLISR event occurred during a declared major event or storm. Major event exclusions per IEEE 1366 may apply to SAIDI/SAIFI calculations when this flag is True.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this FLISR event record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports audit trail and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage in kilovolts (kV) of the distribution circuit on which the FLISR event occurred. Determines applicable switching procedures, safety clearances, and regulatory reporting thresholds.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition recorded at the time of the fault event (e.g., clear, wind, lightning, ice, flooding). Used for outage cause analysis, storm event classification, and reliability reporting under IEEE 1366 Major Event Day criteria. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|wind|lightning|ice|flooding|snow|fog|extreme_heat|unknown — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Record of an automated FLISR (Fault Location, Isolation, and Service Restoration) execution triggered by a fault on the distribution network. Captures fault detection timestamp, faulted segment, isolation actions taken automatically, load transfer actions, customers restored automatically vs. requiring manual intervention, FLISR algorithm version, and success/failure outcome. Distinct from manual switching orders — represents automated grid self-healing actions.'
}

Table outage.safety_clearance {
  safety_clearance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the safety clearance record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the safety_clearance data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete, lifecycle-managed safety authorization event issued to protect field crews working on de-energized equipment.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor, lead dispatcher, or safety officer who approved the issuance of this safety clearance, where a dual-authorization workflow is required. May be the same as the issuing dispatcher in single-authority configurations.']
  compliance_sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sox_control. Business justification: Safety clearance processes for critical infrastructure work may be subject to SOX internal controls over operational safety and asset protection. Internal audit tests safety clearance controls as part']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation where the de-energized equipment covered by this clearance is located. Used for geographic scoping of the clearance and for SAIDI/SAIFI reliability reporting.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outage event during which this safety clearance was issued. Links the clearance to the associated unplanned interruption or planned maintenance outage driving the switching and restoration work.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution feeder or circuit segment on which the safety clearance applies. Enables feeder-level aggregation of active clearances for dispatcher situational awareness and restoration sequencing.']
  grid_switching_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.grid_switching_order. Business justification: Safety clearances are issued for grid switching orders to protect field crews. Linking enables safety compliance tracking, OSHA documentation, and lockout/tagout procedure enforcement.']
  primary_safety_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control room dispatcher or system operator who formally issued this safety clearance. The issuing authority is accountable for verifying equipment de-energization and isolation before granting clearance. Satisfies PARTY_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary electrical equipment asset (transformer, circuit breaker, switch, line segment, cable, bus section) covered by this safety clearance. Sourced from the asset registry in Ventyx Asset Suite or Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.']
  tertiary_safety_release_authorized_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dispatcher or supervisor who authorized the release of this safety clearance after confirming all crew members are clear of the work zone and equipment is ready for re-energization. May differ from the original issuing dispatcher.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance or restoration work order in the asset management system (Ventyx Asset Suite / SAP S/4HANA PM) that this safety clearance supports. Enables traceability between safety clearances and planned work activities.']
  adms_switching_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the GE PowerOn Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS) linking this safety clearance to the associated switching sequence or network model state. Enables traceability to the ADMS network topology at the time of clearance issuance.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason this safety clearance was cancelled before use or suspended mid-work. Populated only when clearance_status is \'cancelled\' or \'suspended\'. Required for safety audit trail and incident investigation.']
  clearance_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable alphanumeric identifier for the safety clearance, as assigned by the OMS or switching management system (e.g., GE PowerOn ADMS). Used by dispatchers, field crews, and control room operators to reference the clearance in verbal and written communications. Satisfies BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  clearance_purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which the safety clearance was issued. Outage Restoration: unplanned outage repair. Planned Maintenance: scheduled preventive or corrective maintenance. Emergency Repair: urgent unplanned repair outside normal outage workflow. Inspection: equipment inspection requiring de-energization. Construction: new construction or capital project work. Storm Response: clearance issued during declared storm event.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the safety clearance. Requested: clearance has been requested but not yet issued. Issued: clearance has been formally issued to the holding crew member. Active: crew is actively working under the clearance. Suspended: clearance temporarily suspended (e.g., weather hold). Released: clearance has been formally released and equipment may be re-energized. Cancelled: clearance voided before use. Expired: clearance lapsed without formal release. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|issued|active|suspended|released|cancelled|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  clearance_to_switching_order varchar [note: 'FK to outage.switching_order.switching_order_id — Safety clearances are issued in context of switching orders to protect crews. Must link for safety compliance and clearance release workflow.']
  clearance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the safety clearance instrument issued to protect field crew. Hold Tag: prevents re-energization while crew is on equipment. Clearance Tag: formal authorization to work on de-energized equipment. Ground Placement: physical grounding of conductors for crew protection. Danger Tag: prohibits operation of tagged device. Caution Tag: advisory restriction. Equipment Tag: equipment-specific isolation marker. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hold_tag|clearance_tag|ground_placement|danger_tag|caution_tag|equipment_tag — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this safety clearance record was first created in the OMS or switching management system. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crew_count varchar [note: 'Number of field crew members working under this safety clearance. Used for crew accountability tracking and to ensure all crew members are clear of the work zone before the clearance is released and equipment is re-energized.']
  crew_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the field crew holding this safety clearance. Line Crew: distribution line workers. Substation Crew: substation maintenance technicians. Transmission Crew: high-voltage transmission workers. Contractor Crew: third-party contracted crew. Mutual Aid Crew: crew deployed under a mutual aid agreement from another utility.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers de-energized as a result of the switching and isolation actions associated with this safety clearance. Used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculation and customer notification prioritization.']
  equipment_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the electrical equipment covered by this safety clearance, including equipment name, type, and location identifier as recorded in the OMS or GIS. Supplements the equipment_id for dispatcher and crew reference.']
  grounds_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether physical protective grounds have been applied to the de-energized conductors or equipment covered by this clearance. True = grounds applied; False = no grounds applied. Grounding is mandatory for certain voltage levels and work types per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.269.']
  grounds_location_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the specific locations where protective grounds have been applied (e.g., \'North side of pole 47A, phase A-B-C\'). Required when grounds_applied is True. Used for crew safety verification and clearance release sequencing.']
  hazard_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing specific hazard conditions, safety warnings, or special precautions associated with this clearance (e.g., proximity to energized conductors, confined space entry, asbestos presence, wildlife hazards). Communicated to field crew at time of clearance issuance.']
  isolation_point_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the electrical isolation boundary for this safety clearance, identifying all open points, switches, and disconnects that define the de-energized zone. Critical for crew safety verification and dispatcher situational awareness.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the safety clearance was formally issued to the holding crew member by the issuing dispatcher. This is the principal real-world business event time for the clearance lifecycle. Satisfies BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  life_support_customers_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any customers dependent on life support equipment (medical baseline, life support registry) are within the de-energized zone covered by this safety clearance. True = life support customers affected. Triggers priority restoration and mandatory customer notification per PUC rules.']
  lockout_tagout_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number for the associated Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) procedure document or energy control program record, as required by OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 for control of hazardous energy. Links the safety clearance to the formal LOTO documentation package.']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the switching and clearance activity associated with this safety clearance is subject to NERC reliability standard reporting requirements (e.g., FAC-001, FAC-002, or CIP-related switching). True = NERC reportable.']
  network_segment_description varchar [note: 'Description of the electrical network segment (feeder section, bus section, transmission line segment) covered by this safety clearance, including circuit identifiers and geographic boundaries. Used for dispatcher situational awareness and restoration sequencing.']
  oms_clearance_ref varchar [note: 'Native clearance or safety tag identifier as recorded in the source Outage Management System (GE PowerOn ADMS). Used for cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer record and the operational OMS source record.']
  osha_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all applicable OSHA 29 CFR 1910.269 electrical safety requirements have been verified and documented prior to issuing this clearance, including minimum approach distances, PPE requirements, and lockout/tagout procedures. True = OSHA compliance verified.']
  planned_outage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety clearance was issued in support of a planned (scheduled) outage rather than an unplanned interruption. True = planned outage; False = unplanned/emergency outage. Used for SAIDI/SAIFI planned vs. unplanned reliability index segregation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for this safety clearance, reflecting the urgency of the associated restoration or maintenance work. Critical: life safety or critical infrastructure. High: large customer impact or time-sensitive restoration. Normal: standard planned or unplanned work. Low: minor maintenance with minimal customer impact.']
  regulatory_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for regulatory compliance notes associated with this safety clearance, including NERC FAC, OSHA, or PUC-specific documentation requirements, special reporting obligations, or compliance exceptions noted at time of issuance.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the safety clearance was formally released by the holding crew member and acknowledged by the issuing dispatcher, signaling that the equipment may be re-energized. Null if the clearance has not yet been released. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  requested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the field crew or work planner submitted the request for this safety clearance to the control room. Used to measure dispatcher response time and clearance issuance latency for safety performance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  safety_state_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issuing dispatcher has formally verified that the equipment is in a safe de-energized state (confirmed open isolation points, absence of voltage, and grounding where required) prior to issuing the clearance. True = verified; False = not yet verified. Required for OSHA and NERC FAC compliance.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when field work under this safety clearance is expected to be completed and the clearance released. Used for outage duration estimation and customer notification ETR (Estimated Time of Restoration). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when field work under this safety clearance is scheduled to begin. Used for planned maintenance windows and pre-scheduled outage coordination. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety clearance was issued during a declared major event or storm restoration. True = storm event clearance. Used to segregate storm-related clearances for major event day (MED) exclusion in IEEE 1366 reliability reporting.']
  to_switching_order varchar [note: 'FK to outage.switching_order.switching_order_id — Safety clearances are issued in conjunction with switching orders. Required for OSHA/NERC FAC compliance — every clearance must trace to the switching activity it protects.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this safety clearance record was last modified in the source system. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version counter for this safety clearance record, tracking revisions such as scope extensions, crew changes, or re-issuance after suspension. Supports audit trail requirements and change history for regulatory compliance.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the equipment covered by this safety clearance. Determines the applicable OSHA electrical safety standards, required personal protective equipment (PPE), and minimum approach distances for field crew.']

  Note: 'Formal safety clearance (hold tag, clearance tag, or ground placement) issued to protect field crews working on de-energized equipment during outage restoration or planned maintenance. Captures clearance type, issuing authority, equipment covered, clearance issue and release timestamps, crew member holding the clearance, and safety state verification. Required for OSHA and NERC FAC compliance during switching and restoration operations.'
}

Table outage.puc_outage_report {
  puc_outage_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each PUC outage report record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying outage event that triggered this PUC regulatory report. Links the formal filing to the operational outage record in the Outage Management System (OMS).']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: PUC outage reports are a specific type of regulatory filing with docket numbers, submission tracking, and approval workflows. Compliance teams manage outage reports within the broader regulatory filin']
  superseded_by_report_puc_outage_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer puc_outage_report_id that supersedes this record when a corrected or amended report has been filed. Null if this is the current active version. Enables version chain traversal for audit purposes.']
  actual_filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this report was actually submitted to the state PUC. Compared against filing_deadline_timestamp to determine on-time compliance. This is the principal business event timestamp for the report transaction.']
  affected_counties varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of county names within the utility service territory that experienced customer interruptions during this outage event. Required by many state PUCs for geographic impact assessment and emergency management coordination.']
  affected_service_territory varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic service territory area impacted by the outage, such as county names, city names, or distribution circuit identifiers. Used in PUC reports to communicate the geographic scope of the event to regulators.']
  cause_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the root cause of the outage event as submitted in the PUC report. Provides detailed explanation beyond the cause category code, including contributing factors, sequence of events, and any relevant environmental or equipment conditions.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the corrective action plan committed to in the final PUC report. Used for regulatory follow-up tracking and compliance monitoring.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective actions taken or planned to prevent recurrence of the outage event. Required in final PUC reports. Includes infrastructure improvements, maintenance program changes, vegetation management, and operational procedure updates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this PUC outage report record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Represents the audit trail creation timestamp for data governance and lineage tracking.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer accounts that experienced an interruption during this outage event. Used to determine whether the event meets PUC reportable customer threshold (e.g., >50,000 customers) and for System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI) calculations.']
  customers_restored_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer accounts that have been restored to service as of the time this report was filed. For final reports, this should equal customers_affected_count. For initial and update reports, tracks restoration progress.']
  estimated_restoration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time by which all affected customers are expected to be restored, as reported in initial and update PUC filings. The Estimated Time of Restoration (ETR) is a key metric for customer communication and regulatory scrutiny during major events.']
  filing_deadline_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which this report type (initial, update, or final) must be submitted to the state PUC. Calculated based on outage_start_timestamp and the applicable PUC reporting rules for the jurisdiction. Used for compliance monitoring and SLA tracking.']
  is_filed_on_time boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the report was submitted to the PUC at or before the filing_deadline_timestamp. Used for regulatory compliance tracking, internal audit, and PUC performance reporting.']
  is_major_event_day boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outage occurred on a day classified as a Major Event Day (MED) per IEEE 1366 beta method. MED events are typically excluded from SAIDI/SAIFI baseline calculations but must still be reported to the PUC separately.']
  is_nerc_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outage event also meets NERC EOP-004 reporting thresholds and has been or must be filed with NERC in addition to the state PUC. Enables cross-regulatory compliance tracking.']
  is_storm_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outage was caused by or associated with a declared storm event. Storm-related outages may have different PUC reporting requirements, extended restoration time allowances, and separate cost recovery treatment.']
  life_support_customers_affected varchar [note: 'Number of customers registered on the utility\'s life support or medical baseline program who were affected by this outage. PUC regulations typically require special notification and prioritized restoration for life support customers, and this count is a required field in many state PUC outage reports.']
  mutual_aid_activated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mutual aid assistance from other utilities was requested and activated in response to this outage event. Mutual aid activation is a reportable fact in many state PUC major event reports and NERC EOP-004 filings.']
  nerc_event_report_number varchar [note: 'NERC-assigned event report number for the corresponding NERC EOP-004 filing, when applicable. Enables cross-referencing between the state PUC report and the federal NERC submission for the same outage event.']
  oms_incident_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the corresponding incident record in the Outage Management System (OMS), specifically GE PowerOn ADMS. Enables traceability between the regulatory PUC report and the operational OMS incident record.']
  outage_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level cause category for the outage event as reported to the PUC. Derived from the OMS outage cause classification and mapped to PUC-required cause taxonomy. Distinct from the detailed cause code in the outage_cause reference product.']
  outage_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the outage event in minutes, measured from outage_start_timestamp to outage_end_timestamp. Used to assess whether the event meets PUC reportable duration thresholds (e.g., >8 hours) and for SAIDI/CAIDI reliability index calculations.']
  outage_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the last affected customer was restored and the outage event was officially closed. Used to calculate total outage duration for SAIDI/CAIDI reporting and to confirm restoration completion.']
  outage_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reportable outage event began, as recorded in the Outage Management System (OMS). This is the principal real-world event time used to calculate outage duration and filing deadline compliance.']
  outage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outage by the level of the electric grid affected. Transmission-level events typically have lower PUC reporting thresholds than distribution events. Used to route reports to the appropriate regulatory body and apply correct reporting rules.']
  prepared_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the utility employee responsible for preparing and submitting this PUC outage report. Required for regulatory accountability and as the point of contact for PUC inquiries about the filing.']
  prepared_by_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number of the utility employee who prepared this PUC outage report. Provided to the PUC as the contact number for follow-up questions regarding the filing.']
  prepared_by_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the utility employee who prepared this PUC outage report (e.g., \'Manager, Regulatory Affairs\', \'Director, Grid Operations\'). Required by some PUCs to confirm appropriate organizational authority for the filing.']
  puc_acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the PUC\'s formal acknowledgment or acceptance of this report filing. \'pending\' indicates awaiting PUC response; \'acknowledged\' indicates receipt confirmed; \'accepted\' indicates formally accepted without deficiency; \'rejected\' indicates returned with deficiency notice requiring resubmission.']
  puc_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the state PUC formally acknowledged or responded to this report filing. Null if acknowledgment has not yet been received.']
  puc_docket_number varchar [note: 'Official docket or case number assigned by the state PUC upon receipt of the outage report. Used for tracking the regulatory proceeding and all subsequent correspondence with the commission.']
  puc_jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code identifying the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) to which this report is filed. Determines applicable reporting thresholds, deadlines, and format requirements.']
  puc_rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason provided by the PUC for rejecting or returning this report filing. Populated only when puc_acknowledgment_status is \'rejected\'. Used to guide corrective resubmission.']
  puc_report_to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — PUC reports are filed for specific qualifying outage events. Must link to the event(s) being reported for regulatory traceability.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable report identifier assigned by the reporting utility and submitted to the state PUC. Used as the official reference number in all regulatory correspondence and acknowledgment tracking (e.g., PUC-2024-OUT-00123).']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this regulatory report filing. \'draft\' indicates preparation in progress; \'submitted\' indicates filed with PUC; \'acknowledged\' indicates PUC receipt confirmed; \'accepted\' indicates PUC formally accepted; \'rejected\' indicates PUC returned for correction; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a subsequent filing.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the filing stage of this report submission: \'initial\' (first notification within required timeframe), \'update\' (interim progress report), or \'final\' (complete post-restoration report with root cause and corrective actions). Multiple report types may exist per outage event.']
  report_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this report record, incremented each time the report is revised and resubmitted to the PUC. Version 1 represents the original submission; subsequent versions represent amendments or corrections.']
  reporting_utility_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the electric utility entity filing this PUC outage report. Required on all regulatory submissions to identify the responsible regulated entity.']
  reporting_utility_nerc_code varchar [note: 'NERC-assigned registered entity identifier for the reporting utility. Used to cross-reference NERC EOP-004 filings and to uniquely identify the utility across multi-state regulatory submissions.']
  saidi_contribution_minutes decimal(10,4) [note: 'This outage event\'s contribution to the utility\'s System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI), calculated as (customer_interruption_minutes / total_customers_served). Reported to the PUC as part of annual reliability performance metrics.']
  saifi_contribution decimal(10,6) [note: 'This outage event\'s contribution to the utility\'s System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI), calculated as (customers_affected / total_customers_served). Reported to the PUC as part of annual reliability performance metrics.']
  to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — PUC reports are filed for specific qualifying outage events. Required for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this PUC outage report record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(10,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the highest-voltage facility involved in the outage event. Used to classify the outage as transmission-level (typically ≥69 kV) or distribution-level, which determines applicable PUC and NERC reporting thresholds.']

  Note: 'Formal regulatory outage report filed with the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) for qualifying outage events meeting reportable thresholds (e.g., >50,000 customers, >8 hours, transmission-level events). Captures report type (initial, update, final), filing deadline, actual filing timestamp, reporting utility, affected customer count, outage duration, cause narrative, corrective action plan, and PUC acknowledgment status. Supports NERC EOP-004 and state PUC reporting obligations.'
}

Table outage.damage_assessment {
  damage_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the field damage assessment record. Primary key for the damage_assessment data product in the outage domain.']
  compliance_environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.environmental_permit. Business justification: Damage assessments may identify environmental permit violations (oil spills from damaged transformers, hazardous material releases). Environmental compliance officers review damage reports to assess p']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Damage assessment labor and vehicle costs are allocated to cost centers for O&M expense tracking and storm cost accounting. Essential for separating assessment costs from restoration costs in regulato']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field crew that performed this damage assessment. Used for crew accountability, workload tracking, and mutual aid resource estimation.']
  distribution_substation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the substation serving the damaged location. Used for outage scope analysis, switching order coordination, and reliability reporting at the substation level.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outage event that triggered this damage assessment. Links the assessment to the outage lifecycle for restoration planning and OMS integration.']
  feeder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution circuit (feeder) on which the damage was assessed. Used for outage scope determination, SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculations, and restoration sequencing by feeder.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Damage assessments identify specific materials needed for repairs (poles, transformers, wire). Field crews use this to requisition parts from inventory. The materials_required text field should be rep']
  primary_damage_assessor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the field crew member who performed and submitted the damage assessment. Used for accountability, quality review, and training program analysis. References the workforce management system.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific utility infrastructure asset record in the asset management system that was assessed as damaged. Links to Ventyx Asset Suite and Esri ArcGIS Utility Network for asset lifecycle and geospatial tracking.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the damage assessment record, formatted as DA-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used in OMS incident tracking, insurance claims, regulatory damage reports, and mutual aid coordination.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the damage assessment record. Drives restoration planning workflow: draft (field entry in progress), submitted (crew submitted for review), verified (supervisor confirmed), approved (cleared for work order creation), closed (repair completed), cancelled (assessment voided).']
  assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the field crew performed the physical damage assessment at the site. Principal real-world event time used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations and storm response timeline reporting.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Type of utility infrastructure asset that sustained damage. Used for asset class reporting, material procurement planning, and reliability performance analysis by asset category. Aligns with Esri ArcGIS Utility Network asset classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: conductor|transformer|pole|crossarm|insulator|fuse|switch|cable|substation_equipment|meter|service_drop|other — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized cause code identifying the root cause of the damage (e.g., wind, lightning, vehicle contact, equipment failure, vegetation). Aligns with IEEE 1366 and NERC cause code taxonomy for reliability reporting. References outage.outage_cause for full cause hierarchy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the damage assessment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking.']
  crew_count_required varchar [note: 'Number of crew members estimated to be required to complete the repair. Used for resource planning, mutual aid request sizing, and restoration workforce management during major storm events.']
  critical_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the damage location affects a critical facility such as a hospital, emergency services, water treatment plant, or life-support customer. Triggers elevated restoration priority and regulatory notification requirements.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers estimated to be without power due to the damage at this assessed location. Used for restoration prioritization, SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI index calculations, and regulatory reporting thresholds.']
  damage_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the damage observed by the field crew. Captures details not covered by structured fields such as extent of damage, contributing factors, and site conditions. Used in insurance claims and regulatory damage reports.']
  damage_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the assessed damage. Used for restoration prioritization, mutual aid resource estimation, and insurance/regulatory damage reporting. Critical indicates immediate public safety risk or bulk power system impact.']
  damage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary infrastructure damage identified during assessment. Drives material procurement, crew skill requirements, and restoration sequencing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broken_conductor|damaged_transformer|downed_pole|equipment_failure|vegetation_contact|damaged_crossarm|fuse_blown|insulator_damage|underground_cable_fault|substation_damage|meter_damage|service_drop_damage — promote to reference product]']
  doe_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the damage event meets DOE OE-417 reporting thresholds (e.g., affecting 50,000+ customers or critical infrastructure). Triggers DOE emergency reporting workflow.']
  estimated_repair_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Field crew estimate of the time in hours required to complete repairs for the assessed damage. Used for estimated time of restoration (ETR) calculation, customer notification, and restoration planning prioritization.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Feature identifier from Esri ArcGIS Utility Network corresponding to the damaged asset or network segment. Enables geospatial analysis, damage heatmap generation, and network topology impact assessment.']
  insurance_claimable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the damage is eligible for an insurance claim (e.g., third-party vehicle contact, natural disaster coverage). Triggers insurance claim initiation workflow and cost recovery tracking.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the damage location recorded by the field crew. Enables geospatial mapping in GIS, storm damage heatmaps, and precise crew dispatch routing.']
  life_support_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the damage location affects one or more customers registered on the life support or medical baseline program. Triggers immediate escalation and priority restoration per state PUC requirements.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the damage location including street address, landmark references, or cross-street information. Supplements GPS coordinates for field crew navigation and customer communication.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the damage location recorded by the field crew. Enables geospatial mapping in GIS, storm damage heatmaps, and precise crew dispatch routing.']
  nerc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the damage event meets NERC reporting thresholds (e.g., bulk electric system impact, transmission-level damage). Triggers NERC EOP-004 event reporting workflow.']
  oms_incident_number varchar [note: 'Incident number from the GE PowerOn ADMS Outage Management System associated with this damage assessment. Enables bidirectional traceability between the field assessment record and the OMS outage incident for restoration coordination.']
  photo_evidence_ref varchar [note: 'Reference path or URL to photographic evidence captured by the field crew during the damage assessment. Stored in the enterprise document management system. Used for insurance claims, regulatory damage reports, and contractor work verification.']
  pole_number varchar [note: 'Utility pole identifier at or nearest to the damage location. Standard field reference used by field crews, GIS systems, and OMS for precise location identification on the distribution network.']
  puc_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the damage event meets state Public Utility Commission reporting thresholds for major outage or infrastructure damage notification. Triggers state regulatory reporting workflow.']
  repair_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the repair identified in this damage assessment was completed and the asset was returned to service. Used for actual vs. estimated repair time analysis, SAIDI/CAIDI calculations, and post-storm performance reporting.']
  repair_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the repair approach required based on the damage assessment. Drives work order type creation in SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance and material procurement in Ventyx Asset Suite.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the utility service territory or operating district where the damage occurred. Used for regulatory reporting by jurisdiction, mutual aid resource allocation, and storm damage aggregation by territory.']
  specialized_equipment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair requires specialized equipment such as a digger derrick, aerial lift, cable puller, or transformer handling equipment. Used for resource dispatch planning and mutual aid equipment requests.']
  storm_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this damage assessment is associated with a declared storm or major event. Used for Major Event Day (MED) exclusion from SAIDI/SAIFI calculations and storm cost recovery regulatory filings.']
  third_party_liability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the damage was caused by a third party (e.g., vehicle accident, contractor damage, excavation). Triggers third-party liability claim process and cost recovery actions.']
  to_outage_event varchar [note: 'FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Damage assessments are performed during outage response. Required for restoration planning and insurance/regulatory damage reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the damage assessment record was last modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data synchronization with OMS and work management systems.']
  verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the damage assessment was verified by a supervisor or field operations manager. Used for quality assurance tracking, SLA compliance, and audit trail of the assessment lifecycle.']
  voltage_level_kv decimal(8,3) [note: 'Nominal voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the damaged infrastructure. Determines crew safety requirements, switching order complexity, restoration priority, and NERC/FERC reportability thresholds.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather condition observed at the damage site at the time of assessment. Used for storm damage correlation, MED classification, insurance claims, and predictive analytics for future storm hardening investments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|wind|rain|ice|snow|lightning|hurricane|tornado|flood|extreme_heat|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance work order number created as a result of this damage assessment. Links the assessment to the repair execution workflow for cost tracking, O&M reporting, and CAPEX/OPEX classification.']

  Note: 'Field damage assessment record capturing infrastructure damage identified during outage response or storm patrol. Records assessment timestamp, assessed location (GPS coordinates, circuit, pole number), damage type (broken conductor, damaged transformer, downed pole, equipment failure, vegetation contact), severity rating, estimated repair time, materials required, crew performing assessment, and photographic evidence reference. Feeds restoration planning, material procurement, mutual aid resource estimation, and insurance/regulatory damage reporting.'
}

Table outage.storm_der_impact {
  storm_der_impact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this storm-DER impact assessment record. Primary key for the association.']
  der_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the distributed energy resource system record. Identifies which specific DER installation experienced this storm impact.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility staff member or system user who performed or validated this storm impact assessment. Used for accountability and data quality tracking.']
  storm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the storm event record. Identifies which declared storm or major weather event this DER impact assessment belongs to.']
  der_availability_during_storm varchar [note: 'Operational availability classification of the DER system during the storm event. Indicates whether the system remained online, went offline, or operated in islanded mode. Used for resilience analysis and DER program evaluation.']
  grid_support_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DER system provided measurable grid support services during the storm event, such as voltage support, frequency regulation, or black start capability. Used for regulatory filings and DER value assessment.']
  impact_assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this storm-DER impact assessment record was created or last updated by operations staff. Used for audit trail and data quality verification.']
  islanding_attempts_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the DER system attempted to island (disconnect from grid and serve local load independently) during the storm event. Relevant for advanced inverter systems with IEEE 1547-2018 capabilities. Used to assess microgrid and resilience functionality.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the DER system\'s overall performance during the storm event based on availability, grid support contribution, and reconnection behavior. Used for after-action reports and DER program evaluation.']
