  grid_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether transmission or distribution grid constraints are expected to limit renewable generation dispatch during this interval. True indicates constraints are forecasted, False indicates no constraints expected.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The duration of the forecast interval in minutes. Common values include 5, 15, 30, or 60 minutes depending on operational requirements and forecast granularity.']
  locational_marginal_price_usd_per_mwh decimal(10,2) [note: 'The forecasted locational marginal price at the renewable asset point of interconnection in USD per MWh. Reflects local grid congestion and transmission constraints. Used for economic dispatch optimization.']
  market_clearing_price_usd_per_mwh decimal(10,2) [note: 'The forecasted or actual wholesale electricity market price in USD per MWh for this interval. Used to calculate expected revenue and inform economic curtailment decisions. May be null for non-market forecasts.']
  net_generation_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'The predicted net generation output after accounting for curtailment. Calculated as forecasted_generation_mw minus forecasted_curtailment_mw. Represents the actual expected dispatch to the grid.']
  persistence_forecast_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'A simple baseline forecast assuming current generation continues unchanged. Used as a benchmark to evaluate the skill and value-add of advanced forecasting models.']
  ramp_rate_mw_per_minute decimal(10,3) [note: 'The predicted rate of change in generation output in megawatts per minute from the previous interval. Critical for grid stability analysis and reserve requirement calculations. Positive values indicate increasing generation, negative values indicate decreasing generation.']
  renewable_interval_to_renewable_forecast varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.renewable_forecast.renewable_forecast_id — Every renewable forecast interval MUST reference its parent renewable forecast. Required for real-time renewable integration and grid balancing.']
  renewable_technology_type varchar [note: 'The type of renewable generation technology for which this forecast applies. Different technologies require different forecasting methodologies and meteorological inputs.']
  weather_data_source varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the meteorological data provider used as input for this forecast (e.g., NOAA, ECMWF, proprietary weather service). Critical for forecast quality assessment and vendor management.']

  Note: 'Interval-level renewable generation forecast values for a parent renewable forecast run. Stores forecasted MW output per renewable asset or aggregate zone at each time interval, including irradiance (W/m²), wind speed (m/s), capacity factor, curtailment MW, and probabilistic confidence bands. Enables real-time renewable integration management and grid balancing decisions by grid operators.'
}

Table forecast.peak_demand {
  peak_demand_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for peak_demand']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Peak demand forecasts drive control area capacity planning, reserve margin calculations, and resource adequacy assessments. Balancing authorities use peak forecasts for transmission planning and gener']
  forecast_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.model. Business justification: Peak demand forecasts are produced by registered forecasting models. The model table is the authoritative registry for model metadata, versioning, and accuracy metrics. Adding forecast_model_id FK all']
  peak_forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Peak demand forecasts are spatially scoped to forecast zones. The zone table defines geographic boundaries and aggregation levels. Adding forecast_zone_id FK allows retrieval of zone_code, zone_name, ']
  peak_zone_id varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.zone.zone_id — Peak demand forecasts are scoped to system or zone level. FK enables zone-level capacity planning.']
  planning_assumption_id varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.planning_assumption.planning_assumption_id — Peak demand forecasts use planning assumptions (load growth rates, EV adoption, DR credits). FK enables assumption traceability.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this peak demand forecast for use in resource adequacy and capacity planning.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this peak demand forecast was approved by planning or management for use in resource adequacy and capacity planning.']
  btm_solar_reduction_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Forecasted behind-the-meter (BTM) solar generation reduction credit in megawatts (MW) that will reduce the net peak demand seen by the grid.']
  capex_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast triggers capital expenditure (CAPEX) investment decisions for transmission and distribution (T&D) infrastructure (True if triggers investment, False otherwise).']
  confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level of the forecast expressed as a percentage (e.g., 90.0 for 90% confidence interval). Indicates forecast reliability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this peak demand forecast record was first created in the system.']
  dr_reduction_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Forecasted demand response (DR) program reduction credit in megawatts (MW) that will reduce the gross peak demand. Used for resource adequacy calculations.']
  energy_efficiency_reduction_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Forecasted energy efficiency program reduction credit in megawatts (MW) that will reduce the gross peak demand due to efficiency measures.']
  forecast_error_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Expected forecast error or uncertainty range in megawatts (MW). Represents the plus/minus range around the forecasted peak demand.']
  forecast_horizon varchar [note: 'Time horizon classification for the forecast (short-term operational, seasonal, annual, multi-year, or long-term capacity planning).']
  forecast_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for this peak demand forecast (e.g., \'Summer 2024 System Peak\', \'Winter 2025 Coincident Peak\').']
  forecast_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes, assumptions, or commentary related to this peak demand forecast. May include special conditions, data quality issues, or planning considerations.']
  forecast_scenario varchar [note: 'Planning scenario or case assumption underlying this forecast (base case, high growth, low growth, extreme weather, conservation, electrification).']
  forecast_season varchar [note: 'Seasonal period for which the peak demand is forecasted (summer, winter, spring, fall, or annual).']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the peak demand forecast (draft, submitted to RTO/ISO, approved by planning, revised, or archived).']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the peak demand forecast by scope and aggregation level (system-wide, coincident across zones, non-coincident, zonal, substation-level, or feeder-level).']
  forecast_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the peak demand is forecasted.']
  forecasted_temperature decimal(6,2) [note: 'Forecasted temperature (in degrees Fahrenheit or Celsius) at the time of peak demand. Used for weather-correlated load forecasting.']
  irp_filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the IRP containing this forecast was filed with the state Public Utility Commission.']
  irp_filing_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast is included in an Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) filing to the state Public Utility Commission (True if included, False otherwise).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person or system that last modified this peak demand forecast record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this peak demand forecast record was last modified or updated.']
  mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Forecasted system peak demand in megawatts (MW). This is the principal quantitative value representing the maximum expected load.']
  net_peak_demand_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net forecasted peak demand in megawatts (MW) after applying all reduction credits (demand response, BTM solar, energy efficiency). This is the load the utility must serve.']
  normal_temperature decimal(6,2) [note: 'Normal or baseline temperature assumption (in degrees Fahrenheit or Celsius) used as the reference point for temperature sensitivity calculations.']
  peak_date date [note: 'Forecasted date on which the peak demand is expected to occur.']
  peak_hour_end varchar [note: 'Ending hour (0-23) of the forecasted peak demand window. Used for operational dispatch planning.']
  peak_hour_start varchar [note: 'Starting hour (0-23) of the forecasted peak demand window. Used for operational dispatch planning.']
  required_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total required generation capacity in megawatts (MW) calculated as net peak demand plus reserve margin. Used for resource adequacy and capacity procurement planning.']
  reserve_margin_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Planning reserve margin percentage applied to the net peak demand to determine required generation capacity. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.0 for 15%).']
  rto_iso_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which this forecast was submitted to the RTO/ISO for capacity planning and reliability assessments.']
  rto_iso_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast has been submitted to the RTO/ISO for capacity planning and reliability assessments (True if submitted, False otherwise).']
  temperature_sensitivity_mw_per_degree decimal(10,4) [note: 'Temperature sensitivity coefficient representing the change in peak demand (MW) per degree Fahrenheit or Celsius change from normal. Used for weather-adjusted forecasting.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for temperature sensitivity (Fahrenheit or Celsius).']
  weather_station_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the weather station or meteorological data source used for temperature and weather correlation in this forecast.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person or system that created this peak demand forecast record.']

  Note: 'Master entity capturing seasonal and annual peak demand forecasts used for resource adequacy planning, transmission planning, and IRP. Records forecasted system peak MW, coincident peak date/time window, temperature sensitivity assumptions, demand response reduction credits, and reserve margin calculations. Submitted to RTO/ISO for capacity planning and NERC reliability assessments. Supports CAPEX investment decisions for T&D infrastructure.'
}

Table forecast.weather_input {
  weather_input_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for weather_input']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the geographic zone or service territory to which this weather forecast applies. Links to the zone definition used in load and generation forecasting models.']
  atmospheric_pressure_hpa decimal(7,2) [note: 'Forecasted atmospheric pressure at sea level in hectopascals (hPa). Used in advanced weather correlation models and can influence wind patterns and storm tracking.']
  cloud_cover_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted total cloud cover as a percentage (0-100). Primary driver of solar irradiance reduction and solar generation output forecasting.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assurance flag indicating the reliability of this forecast record. Values include valid (passed all checks), suspect (anomaly detected), missing (data gap), interpolated (filled from adjacent data), estimated (model-derived).']
  dew_point_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted dew point temperature in degrees Celsius. Used to calculate humidity and assess cooling load and comfort index impacts on energy demand.']
  ensemble_member_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the ensemble member or scenario within an ensemble forecast system (e.g., ECMWF EPS member 1, 2, 3). Null for deterministic forecasts. Enables probabilistic forecasting and uncertainty quantification.']
  forecast_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence or quality score assigned to this forecast record by the NWP provider or internal validation process. Higher scores indicate greater reliability. Used to weight ensemble forecasts.']
  forecast_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the NWP model run was issued or published by the provider. This is the model initialization time, critical for tracking forecast vintage and lead time.']
  forecast_lead_time_hours varchar [note: 'The number of hours between the forecast issue time and the valid time. Indicates forecast horizon and is a key driver of forecast accuracy degradation.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast methodology: deterministic (single outcome), ensemble (multiple scenarios), probabilistic (distribution-based), or nowcast (very short-term, 0-6 hours).']
  forecast_update_cycle varchar [note: 'The NWP model run cycle identifier (e.g., 00Z, 06Z, 12Z, 18Z for GFS). Indicates which daily model run produced this forecast, critical for tracking model version and update frequency.']
  forecast_valid_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time for which this forecast is valid. Represents the target hour or interval that the weather variables predict. Used to align weather inputs with load and generation forecast horizons.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this weather forecast record was ingested into the lakehouse from the external NWP provider or data feed. Used for data lineage and latency tracking.']
  is_historical_reforecast boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this record is a historical reforecast (hindcast) used for model training and validation, rather than a real-time operational forecast.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, anomalies, or special conditions associated with this forecast record (e.g., model outage, data correction, extreme weather event).']
  nwp_model_source varchar [note: 'The numerical weather prediction model provider or system that generated this forecast data (e.g., GFS, ECMWF, NAM, HRRR, WRF). Critical for model performance tracking and ensemble forecasting.']
  precipitation_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Forecasted precipitation accumulation in millimeters for the forecast interval. Impacts hydro generation availability, solar output, and load patterns during storm events.']
  precipitation_probability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted probability of precipitation occurrence as a percentage (0-100). Used in probabilistic forecasting and risk assessment for renewable generation and load variability.']
  relative_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted relative humidity as a percentage (0-100). Influences cooling load, HVAC efficiency, and customer comfort-driven energy consumption.']
  snow_depth_cm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Forecasted snow depth on the ground in centimeters. Relevant for winter peak load forecasting, transmission line loading, and hydro reservoir inflow modeling.']
  solar_irradiance_w_per_m2 decimal(7,2) [note: 'Forecasted global horizontal irradiance (GHI) in watts per square meter. Direct input to solar photovoltaic generation forecasting models and renewable energy integration planning.']
  source_file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the source file or data feed from which this forecast record was extracted (e.g., GRIB2 file name, API endpoint). Supports data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  spatial_resolution_km decimal(6,2) [note: 'The spatial grid resolution of the NWP model in kilometers (e.g., GFS 13km, ECMWF 9km). Indicates the granularity of the forecast and impacts accuracy for localized weather phenomena.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted air temperature in degrees Celsius at the zone or station level. Primary driver of heating and cooling load in energy demand forecasting.']
  temporal_resolution_minutes varchar [note: 'The time interval between consecutive forecast data points in minutes (e.g., 60 for hourly, 15 for sub-hourly). Defines the granularity of the forecast time series.']
  visibility_km decimal(6,2) [note: 'Forecasted horizontal visibility in kilometers. May be used in operational safety assessments and can correlate with fog-related load patterns.']
  weather_station_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the weather station or observation point from which this forecast data is sourced or interpolated.']
  wind_direction_degrees decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted wind direction in degrees (0-360, where 0/360 is North, 90 is East, 180 is South, 270 is West). Used in wind farm output modeling and turbine-level generation forecasting.']
  wind_gust_speed_mps decimal(6,2) [note: 'Forecasted maximum wind gust speed in meters per second. Important for wind turbine curtailment decisions and grid stability planning during high-wind events.']
  wind_speed_mps decimal(6,2) [note: 'Forecasted wind speed in meters per second at the zone or station level. Critical input for wind generation forecasting and renewable intermittency prediction.']

  Note: 'Weather forecast data ingested from NWP providers (GFS, ECMWF, NAM) as primary inputs to load, generation, and renewable forecasting models. Stores temperature, humidity, wind speed/direction, cloud cover, precipitation, solar irradiance, and dew point at zone or weather station level. Tracks NWP model source, forecast issue time, and validity period. Critical driver for all forecast accuracy and the primary exogenous variable in energy demand and renewable output prediction.'
}

Table forecast.model {
  model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for model']
  accuracy_metric_mae decimal(12,4) [note: 'Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of the model on validation data. Measures average forecast error magnitude.']
  accuracy_metric_mape decimal(8,4) [note: 'Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the model on validation data. Primary accuracy metric for forecast quality assessment.']
  accuracy_metric_rmse decimal(12,4) [note: 'Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of the model on validation data. Measures forecast deviation in absolute units (MW, MWh, etc.).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the model was formally approved for production use by the model governance committee or designated authority.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the model was last calibrated or re-trained with updated historical data. Critical for model performance tracking and refresh scheduling.']
  champion_challenger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this model is part of a champion/challenger evaluation workflow. True if the model is being compared against an incumbent champion model.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model record was first created in the registry. Used for audit trail and model lifecycle tracking.']
  deployment_environment varchar [note: 'Environment where the model is currently deployed: development, staging, production, or sandbox. Used for change management and release control.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to detailed model documentation including methodology, assumptions, validation results, and usage guidelines.']
  execution_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the model generates forecasts: real-time, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, or on-demand.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of forecast output produced by this model: load forecasting, generation forecasting, renewable intermittency, demand response, price forecasting, or capacity planning.']
  forecast_horizon varchar [note: 'Temporal scope of the forecast: real-time (minutes), short-term (hours to days), day-ahead, medium-term (weeks to months), seasonal, or long-term (years) for IRP planning.']
  framework varchar [note: 'Software framework or library used to implement the model (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn, R forecast package, proprietary EMS module).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage of the forecast model (e.g., service territory, balancing authority area, RTO region, specific substation, or renewable site).']
  hyperparameters varchar [note: 'JSON or text representation of model hyperparameters (e.g., learning rate, number of layers, regularization coefficients, ensemble weights). Used for model reproducibility and tuning.']
  input_feature_set varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of input features used by the model (e.g., temperature, humidity, day-of-week, historical load, wind speed, solar irradiance).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model record was last updated. Tracks configuration changes, recalibration, or metadata updates.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the forecast model used in operational references and reporting. Unique across all registered models.']
  model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the model purpose, methodology, use cases, and key assumptions. Provides business context for model selection and interpretation.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the forecast model describing its purpose and scope (e.g., \'Summer Peak Load LSTM\', \'Wind Generation Ensemble\').']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the model: development (under construction), testing (validation phase), champion (production model), challenger (competing alternative), retired, or deprecated.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecasting algorithm or methodology used (statistical regression, neural network, ensemble, persistence, ARIMA, LSTM, random forest). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: statistical_regression|neural_network|ensemble|persistence|arima|lstm|random_forest — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  output_granularity varchar [note: 'Temporal resolution of the forecast output: 5-minute, 15-minute, hourly, daily, monthly, or annual intervals.']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the model owner for operational communication and governance escalation.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual responsible for the model. Primary point of contact for model questions and governance.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the business unit or team responsible for developing, maintaining, and governing this forecast model (e.g., Load Forecasting, Renewable Analytics, Grid Operations).']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this model is used for regulatory reporting or compliance purposes (e.g., NERC resource adequacy, FERC market reporting, IRP filings). True if regulatory-critical.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the model was retired from active use. Null for active models.']
  training_end_date date [note: 'End date of the historical data period used to train the model. Defines the conclusion of the training window.']
  training_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the historical data period used to train the model. Defines the beginning of the training window.']
  validation_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the out-of-sample validation period used to assess model accuracy.']
  validation_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the out-of-sample validation period used to assess model accuracy. Distinct from training period.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the model configuration following semantic versioning (e.g., v1.2.0). Incremented when model structure, hyperparameters, or training data change.']
  weather_data_source varchar [note: 'Name of the weather data provider or service used as input to the model (e.g., NOAA, Weather Underground, proprietary SCADA sensors).']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a registered forecasting model configuration used to produce load, generation, or renewable forecasts. Captures model type (statistical regression, neural network, ensemble, persistence), model version, training data period, input feature set, hyperparameters, calibration date, and owning team. Provides model governance and lineage for all forecast runs. Supports model comparison and champion/challenger evaluation workflows.'
}

Table forecast.accuracy {
  accuracy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for accuracy']
  accuracy_forecast_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.model. Business justification: Forecast accuracy records measure the performance of registered forecasting models. The model table is the authoritative registry for model configuration, hyperparameters, and baseline accuracy metric']
  accuracy_model_id varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.model.model_id — Accuracy records measure performance of a specific forecast model. FK enables model performance governance and champion/challenger comparison.']
  forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific forecast run being evaluated for accuracy. Links to the forecast execution that produced the predictions being measured.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Forecast accuracy records track performance at specific service points or aggregations. Forecasters identify which meter points drive forecast errors to refine models. Real business process: granular ']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific transmission or distribution zone for which accuracy is measured. Null for system-level measurements.']
  absolute_error_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'The absolute difference between forecasted and actual values, calculated as |forecasted_value_mw - actual_value_mw|. Used in Mean Absolute Error (MAE) calculations.']
  actual_value_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual observed value for the interval, measured in megawatts. This is the realized load, generation, or other metric that occurred and is used as ground truth for accuracy calculation.']
  benchmark_comparison varchar [note: 'Comparison of the forecast accuracy against a benchmark model or target threshold. Indicates whether the forecast performed better, worse, or equivalent to the benchmark.']
  bias_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'The signed difference between forecasted and actual values, calculated as forecasted_value_mw - actual_value_mw. Positive bias indicates over-forecasting, negative bias indicates under-forecasting.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(10,4) [note: 'The lower bound of the confidence interval for the accuracy metric. Provides statistical confidence range for the measured accuracy.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(10,4) [note: 'The upper bound of the confidence interval for the accuracy metric. Provides statistical confidence range for the measured accuracy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this accuracy record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the quality of the actual data used for accuracy calculation. Flags whether the actual values were validated, suspect, estimated through VEE (Validation Estimation and Editing), or missing.']
  day_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the day as weekday, weekend, or holiday. Used to segment accuracy analysis by day-of-week patterns that affect load profiles.']
  for_run varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.run.run_id — Accuracy records are computed for a specific forecast run. FK enables traceability from accuracy metrics to the exact run and input data.']
  forecast_horizon_hours varchar [note: 'The time horizon of the forecast being evaluated, measured in hours from the forecast issuance time. Common values include 1-hour ahead, 24-hour ahead, 168-hour ahead (weekly), etc.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'The category of forecast being evaluated. Indicates whether this accuracy measurement applies to load forecasting, generation forecasting, renewable intermittency prediction, demand response, price forecasting, or reserve requirements.']
  forecasted_value_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'The predicted value from the forecast model, measured in megawatts. This is the value that was forecasted for the interval and is being compared against actuals.']
  granularity_level varchar [note: 'The geographic or organizational level at which the forecast accuracy is measured. System-level represents total utility territory, zone represents transmission zones, substation and feeder represent distribution levels.']
  improvement_opportunity_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator flagging forecast intervals or conditions where significant accuracy improvement opportunities exist. Used to prioritize model tuning and enhancement efforts.']
  interval_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The ending timestamp of the forecast interval being evaluated. Represents the end of the period for which the forecast was made and is now being measured against actuals.']
  interval_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The beginning timestamp of the forecast interval being evaluated. Represents the start of the period for which the forecast was made and is now being measured against actuals.']
  mae_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Mean Absolute Error for the forecast interval or aggregation period, measured in megawatts. Represents the average magnitude of forecast errors in absolute terms.']
  mape decimal(10,4) [note: 'The Mean Absolute Percentage Error for the forecast interval or aggregation period. Represents the average magnitude of forecast errors as a percentage of actual values. Industry standard accuracy metric for forecast performance.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the forecast accuracy measurement was calculated and recorded. Represents when actuals were compared against forecasted values.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the forecasting model that produced the forecast being evaluated. Enables tracking of model performance across versions and model updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing contextual information about the accuracy measurement, including explanations for anomalies, data quality issues, or special conditions affecting the forecast.']
  peak_error_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum absolute error observed during the forecast period. Critical for evaluating forecast performance during peak demand or generation periods.']
  peak_error_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the peak error occurred. Identifies the specific interval with the largest forecast deviation.']
  percentage_error decimal(10,4) [note: 'The error expressed as a percentage of the actual value, calculated as ((forecasted_value_mw - actual_value_mw) / actual_value_mw) * 100. Used in Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) calculations.']
  regulatory_threshold_met boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the forecast accuracy met regulatory or contractual accuracy thresholds. Used for compliance reporting to FERC, NERC, or RTO/ISO requirements.']
  rmse_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Root Mean Squared Error for the forecast interval or aggregation period, measured in megawatts. Penalizes larger errors more heavily than MAE and is sensitive to outliers.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'The number of individual forecast intervals included in the accuracy calculation. Relevant when accuracy metrics are aggregated across multiple intervals or data points.']
  season varchar [note: 'The meteorological season during which the forecast interval occurred. Used to track accuracy performance by seasonal patterns and weather regimes.']
  squared_error decimal(24,8) [note: 'The square of the difference between forecasted and actual values, calculated as (forecasted_value_mw - actual_value_mw)^2. Used in Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) calculations.']
  time_of_day_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast interval by time-of-day load pattern. Used to evaluate accuracy during different daily load shapes and operational conditions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this accuracy record was last modified. Used for tracking changes to accuracy measurements as actuals are revised or data quality improves.']
  weather_regime varchar [note: 'The weather condition classification during the forecast interval. Used to evaluate forecast accuracy under different weather scenarios that impact load and renewable generation.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing post-hoc forecast accuracy measurements comparing forecasted values against actuals for a completed forecast run. Stores MAPE (Mean Absolute Percentage Error), MAE (Mean Absolute Error), RMSE, bias, and peak error metrics at system, zone, and interval levels. Tracks accuracy by forecast horizon, season, and weather regime. Used for model performance governance, regulatory reporting, and continuous improvement of forecasting processes.'
}

Table forecast.irp_scenario {
  irp_scenario_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IRP scenario. Primary key for the irp_scenario product.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: IRP scenarios model future control area configurations, load growth, generation mix, and transmission expansion. Utilities plan generation and transmission investments within control area operational ']
  irp_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent IRP filing submission to the state Public Utility Commission (PUC). Links this scenario to the regulatory filing docket.']
  owning_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: IRP scenarios are developed and owned by specific planning departments/organizational units for territorial and functional accountability. Required for regulatory filings (PUC dockets) to identify res']
  parent_scenario_irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent scenario if this is a sensitivity or variant scenario. Links sensitivity scenarios back to their base case for comparison. Null for base case scenarios.']
  planning_assumption_id varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.load_growth_assumption.load_growth_assumption_id — IRP scenarios are built on specific planning assumptions (load growth, fuel price). Must link for scenario reproducibility.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or committee that approved this scenario for inclusion in the IRP filing. Provides accountability and governance trail.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scenario was formally approved for inclusion in the IRP filing. Marks transition from draft to official status.']
  battery_storage_capacity_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned battery energy storage system (BESS) capacity additions over the planning horizon for this scenario, measured in megawatts (MW). Critical for renewable integration and grid flexibility.']
  capacity_market_assumption varchar [note: 'Assumed market structure for capacity procurement. None means vertically integrated utility; energy_only means no separate capacity payments; capacity_market means RTO/ISO capacity auction; hybrid means mixed approach.']
  carbon_policy_assumption varchar [note: 'Carbon emissions policy framework assumed in this scenario. None means no carbon constraint; state_target follows state renewable portfolio standard (RPS) or clean energy goals; federal_mandate assumes national policy; carbon_tax models explicit carbon pricing; cap_and_trade models emissions trading system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scenario record was first created in the system. Audit trail for scenario development lifecycle.']
  demand_response_credit_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Assumed demand response (DR) program capacity credit in megawatts (MW) that can be counted toward resource adequacy. Represents load reduction capability from DR programs during peak periods.']
  discount_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted average cost of capital (WACC) or discount rate used for net present value (NPV) calculations in this scenario, expressed as a percentage. Reflects utility cost of capital for investment analysis.']
  distributed_energy_resource_penetration_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assumed penetration of distributed energy resources (rooftop solar, battery storage, microgrids) as a percentage of total capacity by end of planning horizon. Impacts load forecast and grid planning.']
  economic_growth_assumption varchar [note: 'Assumed economic growth trajectory for the service territory. Low assumes slow GDP growth; reference uses consensus forecasts; high assumes robust expansion; recession models economic downturn impacts on load.']
  electrification_assumption varchar [note: 'Assumed rate of transportation and building electrification (electric vehicles, heat pumps) over the planning horizon. Low assumes slow adoption; reference uses market forecasts; high assumes policy-driven acceleration.']
  energy_efficiency_savings_gwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative annual energy savings from energy efficiency programs by the end of the planning horizon, measured in gigawatt-hours (GWh). Reduces forecasted energy requirements.']
  filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IRP containing this scenario was filed with the state Public Utility Commission (PUC). Establishes regulatory record date.']
  fossil_capacity_retirement_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned fossil fuel generation capacity retirements (coal, natural gas, oil) over the planning horizon for this scenario, measured in megawatts (MW). Reflects decarbonization strategy and asset lifecycle management.']
  irp_uses_peak_demand varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.peak_demand_forecast.peak_demand_forecast_id — IRP scenarios reference peak demand forecasts for capacity planning. Critical for resource adequacy analysis.']
  load_growth_assumption_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average annual load growth rate assumption for this scenario expressed as a percentage (e.g., 1.50 for 1.5% annual growth). Key driver for capacity planning and resource adequacy analysis.']
  modeling_software varchar [note: 'Name and version of the capacity expansion or production cost modeling software used to develop this scenario (e.g., PLEXOS, Aurora, PROMOD, Encompass). Important for model transparency and reproducibility.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scenario record was last modified. Tracks iterative refinement during IRP development process.']
  natural_gas_price_trajectory varchar [note: 'Assumed natural gas price path over the planning horizon. Low assumes sustained low prices; reference uses EIA or market consensus; high assumes supply constraints or carbon pricing; volatile models price uncertainty.']
  peak_demand_growth_assumption_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average annual peak demand (MW) growth rate assumption for this scenario expressed as a percentage. May differ from energy growth due to load shape changes, electrification, or demand response programs.']
  planning_horizon_end_year varchar [note: 'Final year of the planning horizon covered by this scenario (typically 10-20 years from start). Four-digit year (e.g., 2043).']
  planning_horizon_start_year varchar [note: 'First year of the planning horizon covered by this scenario (typically current year or next year). Four-digit year (e.g., 2024).']
  puc_docket_number varchar [note: 'State PUC docket or case number assigned to the IRP filing containing this scenario. Links scenario to regulatory proceeding for tracking and reference.']
  reliability_metric_lole decimal(8,4) [note: 'Loss of Load Expectation (LOLE) reliability metric for this scenario, typically measured in days per year. Industry standard is 0.1 days/year (one day in ten years). Demonstrates resource adequacy.']
  renewable_capacity_addition_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned renewable generation capacity additions (solar, wind, hydro, biomass) over the planning horizon for this scenario, measured in megawatts (MW). Key metric for tracking renewable energy integration.']
  renewable_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target renewable energy percentage of total generation by the end of the planning horizon. Reflects state RPS requirements or utility commitments (e.g., 50.00 for 50% renewable by 2030).']
  reserve_margin_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planning reserve margin target for resource adequacy expressed as a percentage above forecasted peak demand (e.g., 15.00 for 15% reserve margin). Ensures sufficient capacity to meet reliability standards.']
  retirement_schedule_assumption varchar [note: 'Assumed retirement schedule for existing generation assets. Baseline follows planned retirements; accelerated models early coal/gas plant closures; delayed extends asset life; policy_driven reflects regulatory mandates.']
  scenario_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the scenario (e.g., BASE_2024, HIGH_GROWTH_2024, LOW_CARBON_2024). Used in regulatory filings and internal planning documents.']
  scenario_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the scenario including key assumptions, strategic rationale, and how it differs from other scenarios in the IRP. Used in regulatory filings and stakeholder presentations.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the IRP scenario (e.g., Base Case, High Load Growth, Accelerated Decarbonization, Low Gas Price). Human-readable label for reports and presentations.']
  scenario_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, caveats, or technical details about the scenario that do not fit in other structured fields. Used for model documentation and knowledge transfer.']
  scenario_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scenario. Draft indicates work in progress; under_review means internal stakeholder review; approved means executive sign-off; filed means submitted to PUC; archived means superseded by newer IRP.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scenario within the IRP framework. Base represents the most likely case; sensitivity tests single variable changes; alternative explores different strategic paths; stress_test examines extreme conditions; bookend defines planning boundaries.']
  sensitivity_variable varchar [note: 'For sensitivity scenarios, identifies the specific variable being tested (e.g., natural gas price, load growth, carbon price, technology cost). Null for base case scenarios.']
  stakeholder_feedback_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of stakeholder comments and feedback received on this scenario during public comment periods or technical conferences. Documents stakeholder engagement process required by many state PUCs.']
  technology_cost_assumption varchar [note: 'Assumed capital cost trajectory for new generation technologies (solar, wind, battery storage, gas turbines). Low assumes rapid cost declines; reference uses industry consensus; high assumes slower learning curves.']
  total_co2_emissions_million_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative CO2 emissions over the planning horizon for this scenario, measured in million metric tons. Key environmental metric for comparing scenarios and demonstrating compliance with carbon reduction goals.']
  total_resource_cost_npv_million decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net present value of total resource costs (capital, fixed O&M, variable O&M, fuel) over the planning horizon for this scenario, in millions of dollars. Key metric for comparing scenario economics.']
  transmission_expansion_assumption varchar [note: 'Assumed level of transmission system expansion over the planning horizon. None means no new transmission; limited means targeted upgrades; moderate means regional expansion; aggressive means major interstate projects.']
  weather_normalization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to normalize weather impacts in load forecasting. Historical_average uses 10-30 year average; climate_adjusted incorporates climate change projections; extreme_weather models increased frequency of heat waves and cold snaps.']

  Note: 'Master record for an Integrated Resource Plan scenario and its year-by-year planning data used in long-term capacity planning and resource adequacy analysis. Captures scenario parameters (load growth, fuel price trajectory, carbon policy, renewable targets, DR credits, retirement schedule) at the header level. Stores annual planning data for each year within the scenario including forecasted peak demand (MW), annual energy requirement (GWh), planned capacity additions/retirements (MW by resource type), reserve margin (%), renewable percentage, and CO2 emissions projection. Multiple scenarios per IRP filing (base, high growth, low carbon). Enables year-by-year resource adequacy gap analysis and investment planning. Submitted to state PUC as part of regulatory IRP process.'
}

Table forecast.irp_scenario_year {
  irp_scenario_year_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each annual planning data record within an IRP scenario. Primary key for the irp_scenario_year entity.']
  meter_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: IRP scenario projections are grounded in historical consumption patterns at service points. Planners trace meter points to validate load growth assumptions and customer class trends. Real business pro']
  primary_irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent IRP scenario that this annual planning record belongs to. Links to the irp_scenario master entity.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of this planning year data within the IRP scenario workflow. Tracks progression through planning review and approval process.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the planning authority who approved this planning year data. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planning year data was approved. Null if not yet approved. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  average_lcoe_usd_per_mwh decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weighted average levelized cost of energy in US dollars per megawatt-hour across the generation portfolio for this planning year. Represents all-in cost of generation.']
  capacity_factor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted average capacity factor as a percentage across the generation portfolio for this planning year. Represents actual generation relative to maximum potential generation.']
  co2_emissions_projection_tons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected annual carbon dioxide emissions in metric tons for this planning year. Critical for environmental compliance, carbon pricing analysis, and sustainability reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planning year record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  demand_response_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total demand response capacity in megawatts (MW) planned for this year. Represents load reduction capability from DR programs and customer participation.']
  der_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total distributed energy resource capacity in megawatts (MW) planned for this year. Includes behind-the-meter solar, microgrids, and customer-sited generation.']
  distribution_investment_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned distribution infrastructure investment in US dollars for this planning year. Includes distribution system upgrades, smart grid, and DER integration investments.']
  emissions_intensity_kg_per_mwh decimal(10,3) [note: 'Projected carbon emissions intensity in kilograms of CO2 per megawatt-hour of generation. Measures carbon efficiency of the generation portfolio.']
  energy_storage_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total energy storage capacity in megawatts (MW) planned for this year. Includes battery storage, pumped hydro, and other storage technologies.']
  forecasted_annual_energy_gwh decimal(15,3) [note: 'Projected total annual energy requirement in gigawatt-hours (GWh) for this planning year. Represents total electricity consumption across all customer classes.']
  forecasted_peak_demand_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Projected system peak demand in megawatts (MW) for this planning year. Critical metric for resource adequacy planning and capacity expansion decisions.']
  fossil_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total fossil fuel generation capacity in megawatts (MW) planned for this year. Includes coal, natural gas, and oil-fired generation.']
  fuel_cost_projection_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected total fuel costs in US dollars for fossil fuel and nuclear generation in this planning year. Based on forward fuel price curves and generation dispatch forecasts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this planning year record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  loss_of_load_probability decimal(8,6) [note: 'Calculated probability of loss of load events for this planning year. Reliability metric indicating likelihood of supply shortfall. Target typically <0.1 (one day in ten years).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this planning year record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  net_capacity_change_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net change in generation capacity (additions minus retirements) in megawatts (MW) for this planning year. Positive values indicate capacity growth, negative indicate reduction.']
  nuclear_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total nuclear generation capacity in megawatts (MW) planned for this year. Represents baseload nuclear power generation assets.']
  planned_capacity_additions_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total planned generation capacity additions in megawatts (MW) for this planning year across all resource types. Includes new builds, expansions, and acquisitions.']
  planned_retirements_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total planned generation capacity retirements in megawatts (MW) for this planning year. Includes decommissioning, mothballing, and asset sales.']
  planning_reserve_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target planning reserve margin percentage for this year as defined by regional transmission organization or internal planning criteria. Differs from actual reserve margin.']
  planning_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which this planning data applies within the IRP scenario horizon. Typically ranges from current year to 20-30 years forward.']
  renewable_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total renewable generation capacity in megawatts (MW) planned for this year. Includes solar, wind, hydro, biomass, and other renewable sources.']
  renewable_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total generation capacity from renewable sources for this planning year. Used to track compliance with state RPS mandates and corporate sustainability goals.']
  reserve_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned reserve margin as a percentage of peak demand for this planning year. Represents excess capacity above peak demand to ensure reliability. Typically 15-20% per NERC standards.']
  scenario_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting key assumptions, constraints, or special considerations for this planning year within the scenario. Captures planner commentary and decision rationale.']
  total_capital_expenditure_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned capital expenditure in US dollars for generation capacity additions, upgrades, and infrastructure investments in this planning year.']
  total_operational_expenditure_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned operational expenditure in US dollars for generation operations, maintenance, and fuel costs in this planning year.']
  transmission_investment_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned transmission infrastructure investment in US dollars for this planning year. Includes new lines, substations, and grid upgrades to support generation additions.']
  year_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this year within the IRP scenario planning horizon (1 for first year, 2 for second year, etc.). Enables chronological analysis.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing annual planning data for each year within an IRP scenario. Stores year-specific forecasted peak demand (MW), annual energy requirement (GWh), planned capacity additions (MW by resource type), planned retirements (MW), reserve margin (%), renewable percentage, and CO2 emissions projection. Enables year-by-year resource adequacy gap analysis and investment planning across the IRP planning horizon.'
}

Table forecast.capacity_requirement {
  capacity_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capacity requirement record. Primary key for the capacity requirement entity.']
  capacity_irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IRP scenario or planning case that generated this capacity requirement. Links capacity planning to long-term resource strategy.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Capacity requirements are calculated per control area for resource adequacy, planning reserve margin compliance, and NERC reliability standards. Control area operators use capacity requirements to ens']
  planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planning period for which this capacity requirement applies (e.g., annual IRP cycle, multi-year planning horizon).']
  primary_irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.irp_scenario.irp_scenario_id — Capacity requirements are derived from IRP scenarios. FK enables traceability from capacity obligations back to planning assumptions.']
  rto_iso_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RTO or ISO zone for which this capacity requirement is defined (e.g., PJM, MISO, CAISO, ERCOT, NYISO, ISO-NE).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this capacity requirement was formally approved by the regulatory authority (PUC, FERC) or RTO/ISO governing body.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority, commission, or governing body that approved this capacity requirement (e.g., \'FERC\', \'California PUC\', \'PJM Board\').']
  capacity_auction_clearing_price_per_mw_day decimal(18,2) [note: 'Market clearing price from the most recent capacity auction in dollars per megawatt-day ($/MW-day). Used for capacity procurement cost forecasting and budget planning.']
  capacity_deficiency_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated capacity deficiency in megawatts (MW), representing the shortfall between the capacity requirement and available capacity. Positive values indicate a need for additional procurement.']
  capacity_export_limit_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum capacity in megawatts (MW) that can be exported to external zones or regions, representing surplus capacity available for sale in external markets.']
  capacity_import_limit_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum capacity in megawatts (MW) that can be imported from external zones or regions to meet the local capacity requirement, based on transmission interface limits.']
  capacity_surplus_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated capacity surplus in megawatts (MW), representing the excess capacity above the requirement. Positive values indicate over-procurement or reserve margin cushion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity requirement record was first created in the system.']
  deficiency_penalty_rate_per_mw_day decimal(18,2) [note: 'Penalty rate assessed for capacity deficiency in dollars per megawatt-day ($/MW-day). Applied when a load-serving entity fails to meet its capacity obligation.']
  demand_response_capacity_requirement_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Capacity requirement allocated to demand response programs in megawatts (MW), representing load reduction capability that can be dispatched during peak periods.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this capacity requirement becomes effective and binding for planning and procurement activities.']
  energy_storage_capacity_requirement_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Capacity requirement allocated to energy storage resources (battery, pumped hydro, compressed air) in megawatts (MW). Supports grid flexibility and renewable integration.']
  expected_unserved_energy_mwh decimal(18,3) [note: 'Expected unserved energy limit in megawatt-hours (MWh), representing the maximum acceptable amount of load that may not be served due to capacity shortfalls. NERC reliability metric.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this capacity requirement expires or is superseded by a subsequent requirement. Null for open-ended requirements.']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory filing reference number or docket number associated with the approval of this capacity requirement (e.g., FERC docket number, PUC case number).']
  flexible_capacity_requirement_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Capacity requirement in megawatts (MW) for flexible ramping resources capable of responding to rapid load changes and renewable intermittency. Supports grid flexibility needs.']
  forecast_confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level for the peak demand forecast expressed as a percentage (e.g., 90.0% confidence interval). Indicates the reliability of the forecast used to set the capacity requirement.']
  installed_capacity_requirement_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total installed capacity requirement in megawatts (MW) calculated as peak demand plus planning reserve margin. This is the target capacity that must be procured or available.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity requirement record was last modified or updated.']
  local_capacity_requirement_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Capacity requirement in megawatts (MW) that must be met by resources located within a specific local reliability area or load pocket, due to transmission constraints.']
  loss_of_load_probability_target decimal(10,8) [note: 'Target loss of load probability expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1 represents one day in ten years). NERC reliability standard threshold for acceptable risk of load shedding.']
  net_qualifying_capacity_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Net qualifying capacity in megawatts (MW) representing the effective capacity contribution of resources after applying derating factors for availability, intermittency, and transmission constraints.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, assumptions, or special considerations for this capacity requirement (e.g., policy drivers, market conditions, planning assumptions).']
  peak_demand_forecast_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted peak demand in megawatts (MW) for the planning period, representing the maximum expected load that must be served.']
  planning_reserve_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planning reserve margin expressed as a percentage above peak demand, representing the target capacity cushion to ensure reliability (e.g., 15.0% means capacity must be 115% of peak demand).']
  renewable_capacity_requirement_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Capacity requirement allocated to renewable energy resources (wind, solar, hydro, biomass) in megawatts (MW). Driven by Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) mandates and clean energy targets.']
  requirement_name varchar [note: 'Business name or label for this capacity requirement (e.g., \'2025 Summer Peak Capacity Requirement\', \'PJM RPM 2024/2025\').']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity requirement: draft (under development), proposed (submitted for approval), approved (authorized by regulator), active (in effect), superseded (replaced by newer requirement), or archived (historical record).']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capacity requirement type: installed capacity (ICAP), planning reserve margin (PRM), net qualifying capacity (NQC), resource adequacy (RA), or reliability must-run (RMR).']
  thermal_capacity_requirement_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Capacity requirement allocated to thermal generation resources (coal, natural gas, nuclear) in megawatts (MW). Used for resource mix planning and diversification analysis.']
  transmission_constraint_adjustment_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Capacity adjustment in megawatts (MW) to account for transmission system constraints that limit the deliverability of generation capacity to load centers.']
  unforced_capacity_requirement_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Unforced capacity requirement in megawatts (MW), representing capacity adjusted for expected forced outage rates. Used in PJM and other markets to account for resource reliability.']
  weather_normalization_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether weather normalization adjustments were applied to the peak demand forecast (True) or not (False). Weather normalization removes extreme weather variability.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining resource adequacy capacity requirements and reliability targets for a planning period. Captures installed capacity reserve margin targets, planning reserve margin (PRM), net qualifying capacity (NQC) by resource type, and RTO/ISO-mandated capacity obligations (e.g., PJM RPM, MISO PRA). Stores NERC reliability standard thresholds including loss-of-load probability (LOLP) targets and expected unserved energy (EUE) limits. Drives capacity procurement triggers, deficiency penalties, and IRP resource selection. Updated annually per RTO/ISO capacity auction results.'
}

Table forecast.energy_price {
  energy_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for energy_price']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: LMP forecasts are control-area-specific for market operations, interchange pricing, and economic dispatch optimization. Control area boundaries define pricing zones and congestion management regions i']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or analyst who approved this forecast for publication and operational use. Supports accountability and governance.']
  energy_forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Energy price forecasts are spatially scoped to pricing nodes, which are located within forecast zones. The zone table defines geographic boundaries and RTO/ISO zone mappings. Adding forecast_zone_id F']
  energy_zone_id varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.zone.zone_id — Energy price forecasts are scoped to pricing nodes within forecast zones. FK enables zone-level price aggregation.']
  forecast_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.model. Business justification: Energy price forecasts are produced by registered forecasting models. The model table is the authoritative registry for model configuration and performance metrics. Adding forecast_model_id FK allows ']
  forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.run. Business justification: energy_price is the master record for energy price forecasts; energy_price_forecast_interval contains interval-level detail. Linking to run establishes the execution context. The run table captures ru']
  forecast_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the forecast scenario under which this price forecast was generated (e.g., base case, high load, low fuel cost).']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.irp_scenario. Business justification: Energy price forecasts are used in IRP economic analysis and LCOE calculations. The irp_scenario table defines planning scenarios with load growth, carbon policy, and renewable targets. Adding irp_sce']
  pricing_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific pricing node (PNode) or location where this energy price forecast applies. Critical for Locational Marginal Price (LMP) forecasting.']
  superseded_by_forecast_energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer forecast that supersedes this one. Nullable if this is the current active forecast. Supports forecast version lineage.']
  ancillary_services_price_usd_per_mwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'Forecasted price for ancillary services (regulation, spinning reserves, non-spinning reserves) in US dollars per megawatt-hour. Supports grid stability and frequency control cost modeling.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast was approved by an authorized user. Distinct lifecycle event from creation and modification.']
  capacity_price_usd_per_mw_day decimal(18,6) [note: 'Forecasted capacity price in US dollars per megawatt-day. Represents the cost of ensuring resource adequacy and grid reliability through capacity markets.']
  carbon_price_usd_per_ton decimal(18,6) [note: 'Forecasted carbon price or carbon tax in US dollars per ton of CO2 equivalent. Used for environmental compliance cost modeling and Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) analysis.']
  coal_price_usd_per_ton decimal(18,6) [note: 'Forecasted coal price in US dollars per ton. Used for modeling generation costs for coal-fired power plants.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this forecast (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations for international utilities.']
  forecast_confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level of the forecast expressed as a percentage (e.g., 80%, 90%, 95%). Indicates the probability that actual prices will fall within the forecasted range.']
  forecast_effective_end_date date [note: 'The last date for which this forecast is applicable. Defines the end of the forecast validity period. Nullable for open-ended forecasts.']
  forecast_effective_start_date date [note: 'The first date for which this forecast is applicable. Defines the beginning of the forecast validity period.']
  forecast_identifier varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier or code for this forecast run, used for tracking and referencing in reports and analysis.']
  forecast_interval_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise end timestamp of the interval for which this price forecast applies. Defines the interval boundary for time-series price data.']
  forecast_interval_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise start timestamp of the interval for which this price forecast applies. Used for interval-level LMP forecasting at hourly or sub-hourly resolution.']
  forecast_methodology varchar [note: 'The methodology used to generate this forecast: fundamental (supply-demand modeling), statistical (time-series analysis), machine learning (AI/ML models), hybrid (combination), or expert judgment (analyst-driven).']
  forecast_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or commentary from analysts regarding assumptions, limitations, or special considerations for this forecast. Supports transparency and decision-making context.']
  forecast_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system that generated this forecast (e.g., OpenLink Endur, proprietary forecasting tool, third-party vendor). Supports data lineage and audit.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast: draft (under development), approved (validated by analysts), published (released for operational use), superseded (replaced by newer forecast), or archived (historical record).']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast horizon: short-term (hours to days), mid-term (weeks to months), long-term (years), day-ahead market (DAM), real-time market (RTM), or seasonal planning.']
  hedging_strategy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast is used for developing hedging strategies and risk management in energy trading. True if used for hedging, False otherwise.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The duration of the forecast interval in minutes (e.g., 5, 15, 60). Supports configurable resolution for different market and planning needs.']
  iso_rto_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the ISO or RTO market region for this forecast (e.g., CAISO, PJM, ERCOT, MISO, NYISO, ISO-NE, SPP). Critical for market-specific price modeling.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was last updated or modified. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  lcoe_calculation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast is used for LCOE calculations and generation asset economic analysis. True if used for LCOE, False otherwise.']
  lmp_congestion_component_usd_per_mwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'Forecasted congestion component of the LMP in US dollars per megawatt-hour. Reflects transmission constraints and grid congestion costs at this pricing node.']
  lmp_energy_component_usd_per_mwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'Forecasted energy component of the Locational Marginal Price in US dollars per megawatt-hour. Represents the marginal cost of serving the next increment of load at this location.']
  lmp_loss_component_usd_per_mwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'Forecasted loss component of the LMP in US dollars per megawatt-hour. Accounts for electrical losses in transmission from generation to load.']
  lmp_total_usd_per_mwh decimal(18,6) [note: 'Total forecasted Locational Marginal Price in US dollars per megawatt-hour. Sum of energy, congestion, and loss components. Primary price metric for market operations and economic dispatch.']
  load_forecast_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted system load in megawatts for the interval or period. Key input assumption for price forecasting and economic dispatch modeling.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'The type of energy market this forecast applies to: day-ahead market (DAM), real-time market (RTM), bilateral contract, or forward market.']
  natural_gas_price_usd_per_mmbtu decimal(18,6) [note: 'Forecasted forward natural gas price in US dollars per million British thermal units. Critical input for generation cost modeling and LCOE calculations for gas-fired plants.']
  ppa_valuation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this forecast is specifically used for PPA economic valuation and contract negotiation. True if used for PPA analysis, False otherwise.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast was published and made available for operational decision-making, trading strategy, and planning activities.']
  renewable_energy_credit_price_usd decimal(18,6) [note: 'Forecasted price for Renewable Energy Certificates in US dollars per certificate. Used for RPS compliance cost modeling and renewable energy valuation.']
  renewable_generation_forecast_mw decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted renewable generation (wind, solar, hydro) in megawatts. Accounts for renewable intermittency and its impact on market prices.']
  transmission_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transmission constraints are expected to be binding during this forecast interval. True if constraints are active, False otherwise. Impacts congestion component of LMP.']
  weather_scenario varchar [note: 'The weather scenario assumption underlying this price forecast: normal (historical average), hot, cold, extreme heat, extreme cold, or mild. Weather is a key driver of load and renewable generation.']

  Note: 'Master record and interval-level values for forward energy price forecasts used in IRP economic analysis, PPA valuation, and trading strategy. Stores forecasted LMP by pricing node at the header level with fuel price trajectories, carbon price assumptions, and capacity price forecasts. Includes interval-level LMP ($/MWh) by PNode with energy, congestion, and loss components, forward natural gas price ($/MMBtu), and capacity price ($/MW-day) at configurable resolution. Supports LCOE calculations, economic dispatch optimization, hedging strategy development, bilateral contract valuation, and market participation decisions.'
}

Table forecast.energy_price_forecast_interval {
  energy_price_forecast_interval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the energy price forecast interval record. Primary key for this table.']
  energy_price_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent energy price forecast run that generated this interval-level forecast. Links this interval to the overall forecast execution context.']
  ancillary_services_price_forecast decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted price for ancillary services in $/MWh. Includes regulation, spinning reserves, non-spinning reserves, and other grid reliability services.']
  capacity_price_forecast decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted capacity price in dollars per megawatt-day ($/MW-day). Represents the cost of ensuring sufficient generation capacity is available to meet peak demand.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast interval record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or vendor that provided input data for this forecast interval. May reference ISO/RTO market data feeds, weather services, or internal systems.']
  forecast_accuracy_mape decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical mean absolute percentage error for this model and interval type. Provides a measure of expected forecast accuracy based on past performance.']
  forecast_confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level of the price forecast expressed as a percentage (0-100). Indicates the probability that actual prices will fall within the forecasted range.']
  forecast_lower_bound decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the forecasted LMP price range in $/MWh. Represents the low end of the confidence interval for this forecast.']
  forecast_upper_bound decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the forecasted LMP price range in $/MWh. Represents the high end of the confidence interval for this forecast.']
  interval_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the forecast interval in minutes. Common values include 5, 15, 30, or 60 minutes depending on market resolution.']
  interval_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The ending timestamp of the forecast interval. Represents the end of the time period for which this price forecast applies.']
  interval_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this interval within the parent forecast run. Used to maintain chronological order of forecast intervals.']
  interval_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The beginning timestamp of the forecast interval. Represents the start of the time period for which this price forecast applies.']
  lmp_congestion_component_forecast decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted congestion component of LMP in $/MWh. Represents the cost premium due to transmission constraints limiting power flow to this location.']
  lmp_energy_component_forecast decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted energy component of LMP in $/MWh. Represents the marginal cost of generating one additional MWh of electricity, excluding transmission congestion and losses.']
  lmp_loss_component_forecast decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted loss component of LMP in $/MWh. Represents the marginal cost of electrical losses in transmitting power to this location.']
  lmp_total_forecast decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted total Locational Marginal Price in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh). Represents the complete cost of energy at this location and time, including energy, congestion, and loss components.']
  load_forecast_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted electrical load demand in megawatts for this interval and location. Load is a primary driver of energy prices.']
  market_type varchar [note: 'The type of energy market for which this price forecast applies. Day-ahead market (DAM) forecasts next-day prices, real-time market (RTM) forecasts immediate dispatch prices, hour-ahead provides short-term forecasts, and forward represents longer-term contract prices.']
  market_zone varchar [note: 'The market zone or load zone in which this PNode is located. Used for aggregating prices across geographic regions.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Type of forecasting model used. Statistical models use time-series analysis, machine learning models use AI/ML algorithms, fundamental models use supply-demand economics, and hybrid models combine approaches.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the forecasting model used to generate this interval forecast. Enables tracking of model changes and performance over time.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast interval record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  natural_gas_price_forecast decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted natural gas price in dollars per million British thermal units ($/MMBtu). Natural gas prices are a key driver of electricity prices due to gas-fired generation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about this forecast interval, such as unusual market conditions, data quality issues, or special assumptions.']
  outage_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether planned or forced generation or transmission outages are expected to impact prices during this interval.']
  pnode_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the pricing node (PNode) at which this energy price forecast applies. PNodes represent specific locations in the transmission grid where prices are calculated.']
  pnode_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the pricing node. Provides business-friendly identification of the location.']
  price_interval_to_price_forecast varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.energy_price_forecast.energy_price_forecast_id — Every price forecast interval MUST reference its parent energy price forecast. Required for economic dispatch optimization and hedging.']
  published_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast interval has been published for use in trading, hedging, or operational decisions.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast interval was published and made available to business users. Critical for audit trails and understanding forecast timing.']
  renewable_energy_credit_price_forecast decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted price for Renewable Energy Credits in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh). RECs represent the environmental attributes of renewable generation and are traded separately from energy.']
  renewable_generation_forecast_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted renewable energy generation in megawatts for this interval. Includes wind, solar, and other renewable sources that impact price through supply availability.']
  temperature_forecast_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted ambient temperature in degrees Fahrenheit for this interval. Temperature is a key driver of electricity demand and therefore prices.']
  transmission_constraint_status varchar [note: 'Status of transmission constraints affecting this PNode during the forecast interval. Constraints drive congestion pricing.']
  weather_scenario varchar [note: 'Weather scenario classification used in this forecast. Different weather scenarios drive different load and price patterns.']

  Note: 'Interval-level energy price forecast values for a parent energy price forecast run. Stores forecasted LMP ($/MWh) by PNode and time interval, including energy, congestion, and loss components. Also captures forward natural gas price ($/MMBtu) and capacity price ($/MW-day) at interval or monthly resolution. Enables granular economic dispatch optimization, hedging strategy development, and bilateral contract valuation.'
}

Table forecast.zone {
