  zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for zone']
  parent_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent zone in a hierarchical zone structure. Enables roll-up aggregation from distribution feeder zones to load zones to transmission zones to RTO/ISO market zones.']
  area_square_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total geographic area of the forecast zone in square kilometers. Used for density calculations, load intensity analysis, and spatial planning.']
  centroid_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the zone\'s geographic centroid in decimal degrees. Used for distance calculations, weather station proximity analysis, and map display.']
  centroid_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the zone\'s geographic centroid in decimal degrees. Used for distance calculations, weather station proximity analysis, and map display.']
  climate_zone varchar [note: 'Climate classification for this zone based on Köppen climate classification or regional climate zones. Used for weather normalization and temperature sensitivity modeling. Examples: \'Humid Subtropical\', \'Mediterranean\', \'Continental\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this zone record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer accounts or service points within this forecast zone. Used for per-capita load analysis and customer density metrics.']
  der_penetration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total load served by Distributed Energy Resources (behind-the-meter solar, battery storage, microgrids) within this zone. Used for DER impact forecasting and grid planning.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this zone definition became effective for operational use. Used for temporal zone boundary changes and historical forecast reconciliation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this zone definition expires or was retired. Null for currently active zones. Used for zone lifecycle management and historical analysis.']
  feeder_count varchar [note: 'Number of distribution feeders that comprise or are contained within this forecast zone. Relevant for distribution feeder zones and load zones.']
  forecast_accuracy_mape decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical forecast accuracy for this zone measured as Mean Absolute Percentage Error. Lower values indicate better forecast performance. Used for zone-level accuracy tracking and model tuning.']
  forecast_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the forecast model or algorithm currently applied to this zone. Used for model performance tracking and version control in forecast accuracy analysis.']
  geographic_boundary_wkt varchar [note: 'Well-Known Text representation of the zone\'s geographic boundary polygon or multipolygon. Used for spatial queries, GIS integration, and zone overlap analysis. Conforms to OGC Simple Features specification.']
  installed_generation_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total installed generation capacity within this zone in megawatts. Includes conventional and renewable generation. Used for generation adequacy and renewable penetration analysis.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that last modified this zone record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this zone record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about the zone definition, boundary changes, special characteristics, or operational considerations.']
  peak_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Historical or design peak load for this zone in megawatts. Used as a reference point for forecast accuracy tracking and capacity adequacy assessment.']
  primary_weather_station_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary weather station used for weather data correlation and temperature-sensitive load forecasting for this zone. Typically a NOAA station identifier or internal weather station code.']
  renewable_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total installed renewable generation capacity within this zone in megawatts. Includes solar, wind, hydro, and other renewable sources. Used for Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) tracking and intermittency forecasting.']
  rto_iso_name varchar [note: 'Name of the RTO or ISO that governs this zone. Examples: \'PJM\', \'CAISO\', \'ERCOT\', \'MISO\', \'ISO-NE\', \'NYISO\', \'SPP\'. Used for regulatory reporting and market data integration.']
  rto_iso_zone_code varchar [note: 'External zone code used by the Regional Transmission Organization or Independent System Operator for market operations, pricing, and settlement. Maps internal zones to RTO/ISO market zones for Day-Ahead Market (DAM) and Real-Time Market (RTM) alignment.']
  substation_count varchar [note: 'Number of substations located within or serving this forecast zone. Used for infrastructure density analysis and capacity planning.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for this zone. Used for timestamp normalization and daylight saving time adjustments in forecast calculations. Examples: \'America/New_York\', \'America/Chicago\', \'America/Los_Angeles\'.']
  voltage_level_kv varchar [note: 'Primary voltage level(s) associated with this zone. For transmission zones, this is the transmission voltage (e.g., \'345\', \'500\'). For distribution zones, this is the distribution voltage (e.g., \'12.47\', \'34.5\'). Multiple levels may be comma-separated.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the forecast zone, used across operational systems and market reporting. Typically aligns with RTO/ISO zone naming conventions or internal geographic identifiers.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the forecast zone, used for reporting and display purposes. Examples: \'Metro East Load Zone\', \'Coastal Weather Zone\', \'Northern Transmission Zone\'.']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast zone. Active zones are in operational use, inactive zones are temporarily suspended, retired zones are no longer used, and planned zones are defined for future use.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast zone by its primary purpose. Load zones aggregate customer demand, weather zones group meteorological data, transmission zones define grid topology, distribution feeder zones represent circuit-level aggregation, market zones align with RTO/ISO pricing nodes, and planning zones support long-term capacity planning.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created this zone record. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining geographic forecast zones used to spatially aggregate all forecast products. Captures zone name, type (load zone, weather zone, transmission zone, distribution feeder zone), geographic boundary, associated substations, and RTO/ISO zone mapping. Provides the spatial hierarchy for load, generation, renewable, and price forecasts. Enables zone-level accuracy tracking, planning aggregation, and alignment with RTO/ISO market zones. Integrates with Esri ArcGIS Utility Network geographic boundaries.'
}

Table forecast.planning_assumption {
  planning_assumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planning_assumption']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Load growth and planning assumptions require analyst ownership for regulatory audit trails and IRP filings. Utilities must demonstrate who created and approved each assumption for PUC review. Critical']
  superseded_by_assumption_planning_assumption_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planning assumption record that supersedes this assumption. Used for version control and historical tracking of assumption changes over time.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the planning assumption was formally approved for use in Integrated Resource Plan (IRP), transmission planning, or capacity planning models. Critical for regulatory compliance and audit trail.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or committee that approved the planning assumption. Provides accountability and governance traceability for assumptions used in regulatory filings.']
  assumption_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the planning assumption type. Segments assumptions into load growth (customer class growth rates, Electric Vehicle (EV) adoption curves, electrification scenarios), fuel price (natural gas, coal, uranium, renewable fuel costs), carbon price trajectories, technology cost curves, economic indices, or policy scenarios.']
  assumption_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the planning assumption used in Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) and capacity planning models. Externally referenced in regulatory filings and planning documentation.']
  assumption_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the planning assumption including methodology, data sources, key drivers, and any caveats or limitations. Provides context for assumption interpretation and use in planning models.']
  assumption_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the planning assumption for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  assumption_source varchar [note: 'Source of the planning assumption. Examples include Energy Information Administration (EIA) Annual Energy Outlook (AEO), internal forecast, third-party consultant, industry benchmark, regulatory guidance, or historical trend analysis. Provides traceability for assumption provenance.']
  assumption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planning assumption. Tracks approval workflow and version control for assumptions used in regulatory filings and resource planning models.']
  assumption_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory within the assumption category. Examples include residential load growth, commercial load growth, natural gas price, coal price, uranium price, renewable fuel cost, carbon tax, renewable technology cost, fossil technology cost, Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth, employment rate, or renewable portfolio standard.']
  assumption_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value of the planning assumption. Interpretation depends on assumption_category and unit_of_measure. Examples include annual growth rate percentage, fuel price per Million British Thermal Unit (MMBtu), carbon price per ton Carbon Dioxide (CO2), or technology cost per Megawatt (MW).']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of confidence in the planning assumption based on data quality, source reliability, and historical accuracy. Used for risk assessment and scenario weighting in resource planning models.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning assumption record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  customer_class varchar [note: 'Customer class to which the load growth assumption applies. Used for customer class-specific growth rate assumptions in load forecasting and capacity planning models.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the planning assumption is no longer effective and should not be used in planning models. Nullable for assumptions with indefinite validity. Supports temporal validity and version control.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the planning assumption becomes effective and should be used in planning models. Supports temporal validity and version control for assumptions.']
  escalation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual escalation rate percentage applied to the assumption value over the planning horizon. Used for fuel price escalation, technology cost deflation, or inflation adjustments in long-term planning models.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Fuel type for fuel price assumptions. Applicable when assumption_category is fuel_price. Used in generation cost models and resource selection optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: natural_gas|coal|uranium|solar|wind|hydro|biomass|oil|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope to which the planning assumption applies. Examples include service territory, Regional Transmission Organization (RTO) region, Independent System Operator (ISO) zone, state, or national. Enables geographic segmentation of assumptions for transmission and capacity planning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning assumption record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking for regulatory compliance and data governance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the planning assumption. Used for documenting special considerations, stakeholder feedback, or planning team insights.']
  planning_horizon_year varchar [note: 'Target year for which this assumption applies within the long-term planning horizon. Typically ranges from current year plus one to current year plus twenty for Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) and transmission planning models.']
  planning_period varchar [note: 'Planning period designation within the planning horizon. Examples include near-term (years 1-5), mid-term (years 6-10), long-term (years 11-20), or specific multi-year blocks used in resource selection optimization.']
  planning_scenario varchar [note: 'Scenario identifier for multi-scenario sensitivity analysis. Examples include base case, high growth, low growth, high fuel price, low fuel price, aggressive decarbonization, or delayed policy implementation. Enables scenario-based resource adequacy and grid reliability planning.']
  policy_driver varchar [note: 'Policy or regulatory driver influencing the planning assumption. Examples include Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS), carbon cap, Clean Power Plan, state energy policy, or federal tax incentive. Used for policy scenario modeling and regulatory compliance planning.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory filing or docket number where this assumption was submitted or approved. Examples include Public Utility Commission (PUC) docket numbers, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) filing references, or Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) case numbers. Critical for regulatory compliance and audit trail.']
  sensitivity_high_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper bound value for sensitivity analysis. Represents the high-case scenario value used in multi-scenario planning and risk assessment.']
  sensitivity_low_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower bound value for sensitivity analysis. Represents the low-case scenario value used in multi-scenario planning and risk assessment.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the source document or publication from which the assumption was derived. Examples include EIA AEO 2023 Reference Case, Internal Load Forecast Report 2024-Q1, or Third-Party Market Analysis Report. Enables audit trail and assumption validation.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Generation or storage technology type for technology cost assumptions. Examples include Combined Cycle Gas Turbine (CCGT), solar photovoltaic, onshore wind, offshore wind, battery storage, pumped hydro, or nuclear. Applicable when assumption_category is technology_cost.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the assumption value. Examples include percent for growth rates, dollars per Million British Thermal Unit ($/MMBtu) for natural gas, dollars per ton ($/ton) for coal, dollars per pound Uranium-238 ($/lb U3O8) for uranium, dollars per Megawatt-Hour ($/MWh) for renewable fuel cost equivalent, dollars per ton Carbon Dioxide ($/ton CO2) for carbon price, or dollars per Kilowatt ($/kW) for technology cost.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the planning assumption. Incremented when assumption values or parameters are updated. Supports version control and change tracking for assumptions used in regulatory filings and planning models.']

  Note: 'Master record for long-term planning assumptions used as inputs to IRP, transmission planning, and capacity planning models. Consolidates load growth assumptions (customer class growth rates, EV adoption curves, electrification scenarios, economic indices), fuel price assumptions (natural gas $/MMBtu, coal $/ton, uranium $/lb U3O8, renewable fuel cost $/MWh equivalent by planning period and scenario), and other scenario parameters including carbon price trajectories and technology cost curves. Tracks assumption source (EIA AEO, internal forecast, third-party), approval date, regulatory filing reference, sensitivity ranges, and approval status. Enables multi-scenario sensitivity analysis across the planning horizon and feeds generation cost models and resource selection optimization.'
}

Table forecast.fuel_price_assumption {
  fuel_price_assumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel price assumption record. Primary key for the fuel price assumption entity.']
  baseline_assumption_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the baseline fuel price assumption against which this assumption is compared in sensitivity or scenario analysis. Supports variance analysis and risk quantification.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fuel price assumptions are critical inputs to IRP and rate cases requiring analyst ownership for regulatory scrutiny. Utilities must document who developed fuel price forecasts for PUC cross-examinati']
  superseded_by_assumption_fuel_price_assumption_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fuel price assumption record that supersedes this one. Maintains version history and assumption lineage for audit and regulatory purposes.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status indicating whether this fuel price assumption has been formally approved for use in generation dispatch modeling, IRP filings, and resource selection optimization.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or committee that approved this fuel price assumption for use in planning and economic analysis.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fuel price assumption was formally approved. Supports audit trail and version control for planning assumptions.']
  assumption_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this fuel price assumption within planning systems and Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) filings.']
  assumption_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for this fuel price assumption set, used for identification in reports and planning documents.']
  assumption_source varchar [note: 'Source of the fuel price assumption data. Identifies whether the assumption is derived from EIA Annual Energy Outlook (AEO), internal forecasting models, third-party consultants, market forward curves, historical averages, or regulatory guidance.']
  assumption_source_detail varchar [note: 'Additional detail about the assumption source, such as specific EIA AEO edition, consultant name, or market data provider. Supports audit trail and assumption validation.']
  assumption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fuel price assumption. Tracks progression from draft through approval and eventual supersession by updated assumptions.']
  carbon_intensity_kg_co2_per_mmbtu decimal(10,4) [note: 'Carbon dioxide emissions intensity of the fuel in kilograms CO2 per MMBtu. Used in emissions modeling, carbon cost calculations, and environmental compliance analysis.']
  carbon_price_per_ton_co2 decimal(12,4) [note: 'Assumed carbon price in US dollars per ton of CO2 emissions. Used to calculate carbon cost adder for generation dispatch and LCOE modeling under carbon pricing scenarios.']
  coal_price_per_ton decimal(12,4) [note: 'Assumed price of coal in US dollars per short ton. Supports coal-fired generation cost modeling and LCOE calculations for existing and planned coal assets.']
  confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level associated with the fuel price assumption, expressed as a percentage. Indicates the degree of certainty in the forecast based on historical volatility and market analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fuel price assumption record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all fuel price values in this assumption record. Supports multi-currency planning for utilities operating across jurisdictions.']
  delivery_point varchar [note: 'Specific delivery point or pricing node for the fuel assumption (e.g., pipeline interconnection, coal rail terminal, uranium enrichment facility). Used for transportation cost modeling and delivered fuel price calculation.']
  diesel_price_per_gallon decimal(12,4) [note: 'Assumed price of diesel fuel in US dollars per gallon. Used for backup generation, emergency peaking units, and distributed generation cost modeling.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this fuel price assumption becomes effective for use in generation dispatch modeling, procurement decisions, and infrastructure investment analysis.']
  escalation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual escalation rate applied to the fuel price assumption over the planning horizon, expressed as a percentage. Used to project future fuel costs in multi-year IRP and LCOE analysis.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this fuel price assumption is no longer valid for planning purposes. Supports assumption lifecycle management and prevents use of outdated data.']
  fuel_oil_price_per_gallon decimal(12,4) [note: 'Assumed price of fuel oil in US dollars per gallon. Supports cost modeling for oil-fired peaking units and backup generation assets.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Type of fuel for which the price assumption applies. Supports generation dispatch cost modeling and Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) calculations.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market area to which this fuel price assumption applies (e.g., Henry Hub for natural gas, Powder River Basin for coal, regional ISO/RTO market). Supports location-specific cost modeling.']
  heat_content_mmbtu_per_unit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average heat content of the fuel in MMBtu per physical unit (e.g., MMBtu per ton of coal, MMBtu per gallon of diesel). Used to convert physical fuel quantities to energy equivalents for dispatch modeling.']
  irp_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this fuel price assumption to a specific IRP filing or docket number. Supports regulatory compliance and assumption traceability in rate cases.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the forecasting model or methodology used to generate this fuel price assumption. Supports model governance and reproducibility.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fuel price assumption record was last modified. Tracks changes and updates to assumption data over time.']
  natural_gas_price_per_mmbtu decimal(12,4) [note: 'Assumed price of natural gas in US dollars per MMBtu. Used in generation dispatch cost modeling, combined cycle gas turbine (CCGT) economics, and peaking unit cost analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, caveats, or special considerations related to this fuel price assumption. Supports documentation and knowledge transfer.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning period for which this fuel price assumption is applicable. Defines the temporal boundary for economic analysis and dispatch modeling.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning period for which this fuel price assumption is applicable. Supports long-term capacity planning and resource adequacy analysis.']
  renewable_fuel_cost_per_mwh decimal(12,4) [note: 'Assumed fuel cost equivalent for renewable generation in US dollars per MWh. Typically zero for wind and solar; may reflect biomass or biogas fuel costs for renewable thermal generation.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of the planning scenario to which this fuel price assumption belongs (e.g., Base Case, High Gas Price, Low Gas Price, Renewable Acceleration). Used in IRP scenario modeling and sensitivity analysis.']
  sensitivity_high_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper bound fuel price for sensitivity analysis and risk assessment. Represents optimistic or high-price scenario for stress testing generation economics and resource selection.']
  sensitivity_low_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower bound fuel price for sensitivity analysis and risk assessment. Represents pessimistic or low-price scenario for stress testing generation economics and resource selection.']
  transportation_cost_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Assumed transportation cost per unit of fuel (e.g., dollars per MMBtu for gas pipeline, dollars per ton for coal rail). Added to base fuel price to calculate delivered cost at generation facility.']
  uranium_price_per_lb_u3o8 decimal(12,4) [note: 'Assumed price of uranium in US dollars per pound of U3O8. Used in nuclear generation cost modeling and fuel cycle economic analysis.']
  volatility_index decimal(8,4) [note: 'Measure of historical price volatility for the fuel type, used in risk assessment and stochastic modeling. Higher values indicate greater price uncertainty and risk exposure.']

  Note: 'Master entity capturing fuel price assumptions used in generation dispatch cost modeling, IRP economic analysis, and LCOE calculations. Stores natural gas price ($/MMBtu), coal price ($/ton), uranium price ($/lb U3O8), and renewable fuel cost ($/MWh equivalent) by planning period and scenario. Tracks assumption source (EIA AEO, internal forecast, third-party), approval status, and sensitivity range. Feeds generation cost models and resource selection optimization.'
}

Table forecast.forecast_run {
  forecast_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the forecast model run execution record. Primary key for the run entity.']
  baseline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a previous forecast run used as the baseline for comparison or delta analysis, supporting forecast accuracy assessment and model improvement.']
  forecast_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific forecast model configuration used for this run, linking to the model registry.']
  primary_forecast_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that initiated the forecast run for manual or event-triggered executions, supporting audit and accountability requirements.']
  superseded_by_run_forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a subsequent forecast run that supersedes this run, establishing forecast version lineage and supporting operational decision-making with the most current forecast.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast run output was approved for publication, establishing the approval audit trail.']
  compute_cluster_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the computational cluster or resource pool used to execute the forecast run, supporting performance analysis and resource allocation optimization.']
  configuration_parameters varchar [note: 'JSON or structured text representation of the model configuration parameters and hyperparameters used for this forecast run, enabling full reproducibility and parameter sensitivity analysis.']
  convergence_iterations varchar [note: 'Number of computational iterations required for the forecast model to reach convergence or the maximum iteration limit, used for performance tuning and model diagnostics.']
  convergence_status varchar [note: 'Indicator of whether the forecast model achieved mathematical convergence within acceptable tolerance thresholds, critical for assessing forecast reliability and model stability.']
  convergence_tolerance decimal(10,6) [note: 'The mathematical tolerance threshold used to determine model convergence, typically expressed as a percentage or absolute error metric.']
  cpu_hours_consumed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total computational resource consumption measured in CPU hours for this forecast run, used for cost allocation and capacity planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast run record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  duration_seconds decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds for the forecast run execution, calculated from start to end timestamp for performance monitoring and capacity planning.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast model run execution completed or terminated, used to calculate run duration and performance metrics.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error message or exception information captured if the forecast run failed or encountered critical issues, supporting troubleshooting and root cause analysis.']
  execution_environment varchar [note: 'The computational environment where the forecast run was executed, distinguishing between production forecasts and test or validation runs.']
  forecast_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The ending timestamp of the forecast period that this run produces predictions for, defining the last interval of the forecast output.']
  forecast_horizon_hours varchar [note: 'The time span in hours that this forecast run covers from the forecast start time, defining the look-ahead period for operational planning and dispatch decisions.']
  forecast_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The beginning timestamp of the forecast period that this run produces predictions for, defining the first interval of the forecast output.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast domain this run produces, distinguishing between load forecasting, generation forecasting, renewable intermittency prediction, price forecasting, and demand response forecasting.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic or electrical network level at which this forecast run operates, defining the spatial granularity of forecast outputs.']
  input_data_snapshot_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the specific snapshot of input data used for this forecast run, enabling full traceability and reproducibility of forecast results by linking to historical, weather, and operational data versions.']
  interval_resolution_minutes varchar [note: 'The temporal granularity of forecast intervals produced by this run, typically 5, 15, 30, or 60 minutes for operational forecasting aligned with market and dispatch intervals.']
  memory_peak_gb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Peak memory utilization in gigabytes during forecast run execution, used for resource sizing and performance optimization.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast run record was last modified, tracking updates to status, validation results, or metadata throughout the run lifecycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the forecast run, capturing operational context, special conditions, or analyst observations relevant to forecast interpretation.']
  output_record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of forecast interval records produced by this run, used for completeness verification and data quality monitoring.']
  output_validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of automated validation checks applied to forecast output data, including range checks, consistency checks, and business rule validation to ensure forecast quality before publication.']
  published_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this forecast run output has been published for operational use by dispatch, trading, and planning systems.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast run output was published and made available to downstream operational systems and users.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-friendly identifier for the forecast run, typically formatted as a human-readable code for operational reference and audit trails.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast run execution, tracking progression from initiation through completion or failure states.']
  run_to_model varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.forecast_model.forecast_model_id — Every forecast run execution must reference the model version used. Core lineage FK for auditability.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast run execution trigger mechanism, distinguishing between automated scheduled runs, manual operator-initiated runs, event-driven runs, historical backfills, reforecasts, and validation runs.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast model run execution began, marking the initiation of the computational process.']
  triggered_by_event varchar [note: 'Description or identifier of the specific event that triggered this forecast run for event-driven executions, such as significant weather changes, grid events, or market conditions.']
  validation_error_count varchar [note: 'Total number of validation errors detected in the forecast output, used to assess data quality and determine if manual review or rerun is required.']
  validation_warning_count varchar [note: 'Total number of validation warnings detected in the forecast output, indicating potential quality issues that do not prevent publication but require attention.']
  warning_message varchar [note: 'Warning messages or alerts generated during forecast run execution that do not prevent completion but indicate potential quality or performance issues.']
  weather_data_source varchar [note: 'Identification of the weather data provider or service used as input for this forecast run, such as NOAA, Weather Underground, or proprietary weather services.']
  weather_scenario varchar [note: 'Classification of the weather conditions or scenario used in the forecast model run, supporting sensitivity analysis and scenario planning for weather-dependent load and renewable generation forecasting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: actual|normal|hot|cold|extreme_heat|extreme_cold|high_wind|low_wind — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing the execution record of a forecast model run, including run timestamp, triggering event (scheduled, manual, event-triggered), input data snapshot reference, model version used, run duration, convergence status, and output validation flags. Provides operational lineage and auditability for all forecast outputs. Enables traceability from forecast interval values back to the specific model run and input data used, supporting regulatory and operational audit requirements.'
}

Table forecast.revision {
  revision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for revision']
  forecast_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent forecast that was revised. Links to the original forecast entity (load, generation, or other forecast type).']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Forecast revisions are spatially scoped to forecast zones. The zone table defines geographic boundaries and aggregation levels. Adding forecast_zone_id FK allows retrieval of zone_code, zone_name, and']
  revision_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier or username of the supervisor who approved the forecast revision, if approval was required. Null if no approval was needed.']
  revision_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier or username of the analyst or operator who authorized and executed the forecast revision. Required for accountability and audit compliance.']
  superseded_by_revision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a subsequent revision that supersedes this revision. Null if this is the current active revision. Enables complete version history tracking.']
  affected_interval_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the forecast interval affected by this revision. Defines the end of the time window impacted by the forecast change.']
  affected_interval_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the forecast interval affected by this revision. Defines the beginning of the time window impacted by the forecast change.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the revision required supervisory approval before being applied. True if approval was required, false if analyst had authority to revise independently.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the forecast revision was approved by a supervisor, if approval was required. Null if no approval was needed.']
  authorizing_analyst_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the analyst or operator who authorized the forecast revision. Supports human-readable audit trails and operational accountability.']
  confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level of the revised forecast value, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 for 95% confidence). Indicates forecast reliability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revision record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or data feed that triggered or provided input for the forecast revision (e.g., SCADA, weather service, market operator, EMS).']
  demand_response_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the revision was triggered by activation or curtailment of a demand response program. True if DR-related, false otherwise.']
  dispatch_adjustment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast revision requires immediate adjustment to generation dispatch schedules. True if dispatch change needed, false otherwise.']
  forecast_horizon_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours ahead from the revision timestamp to the affected forecast interval. Indicates whether this is a short-term operational forecast revision or longer-term planning revision.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Type of forecast being revised: load forecast, generation forecast, renewable intermittency forecast, demand response forecast, price forecast, or interchange forecast.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or operational scope of the forecast being revised: balancing authority area, control area, load zone, substation, or system-wide.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revision record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes to revision metadata or status.']
  market_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast revision has material impact on energy market operations, trading positions, or dispatch decisions. True if market-impacting, false otherwise.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the forecasting model or algorithm that generated the revised forecast value, if applicable. Supports model performance tracking and audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the forecast revision. Provides supplementary context for operational review and post-event analysis.']
  of_run varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.run_log.run_log_id — Revisions modify outputs of a specific forecast run. Must trace back to the original run for audit trail.']
  original_value_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'Original forecasted value in megawatts before the revision was applied. Preserved for audit trail and variance analysis.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the forecast revision: weather update, generation unit trip, unit return to service, demand response program activation, market price signal change, actual load deviation from forecast, forecast model update, or manual operator override. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather_update|unit_trip|unit_return|demand_response_activation|market_signal|load_deviation|forecast_model_update|operator_override — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation of the reason for the forecast revision. Provides context beyond the standardized reason code for post-event root cause analysis.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the forecast revision must be reported to regulatory authorities (NERC, FERC, or state PUC). True if reportable, false otherwise.']
  revised_value_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'Updated forecasted value in megawatts after the revision. Represents the new forecast value that supersedes the original.']
  revision_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the revision was generated: automated (system-generated based on rules or model updates), manual (analyst-initiated), or hybrid (system-suggested with analyst approval).']
  revision_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the forecast revision in its lifecycle: pending approval, approved, applied to operational forecast, superseded by later revision, or rejected.']
  revision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the forecast revision was made. Critical for intra-day forecast evolution tracking and real-time operations audit.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order of revisions for a given forecast. First revision is 1, second is 2, etc. Enables tracking of revision history chronology.']
  unit_outage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the revision was triggered by a generation unit outage (forced or scheduled). True if outage-related, false otherwise.']
  variance_mw decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between revised and original values in megawatts (revised minus original). Positive indicates upward revision, negative indicates downward revision.']
  variance_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage change from original to revised value. Calculated as (variance / original_value) * 100. Enables relative impact assessment.']
  weather_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the revision was triggered by a significant weather event or weather forecast update. True if weather-related, false otherwise.']

  Note: 'Transactional audit record capturing each revision to a published forecast value, including original value, revised value, revision timestamp, reason code (weather update, unit trip, demand response activation, market signal), and authorizing analyst. Maintains complete version history of forecast updates throughout the operating day or planning cycle. Required for NERC/FERC regulatory compliance, post-event root cause analysis, and real-time operations audit. Supports intra-day forecast evolution tracking critical for dispatch and trading decisions.'
}

Table forecast.resource_adequacy_assessment {
  resource_adequacy_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the resource adequacy assessment record. Primary key for this entity.']
  capacity_requirement_id varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.capacity_requirement.capacity_requirement_id — Resource adequacy assessments evaluate whether capacity meets requirements. This is the core RA compliance relationship.']
  control_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Resource adequacy assessments are performed at control area level for NERC compliance, reserve margin verification, and loss-of-load-expectation calculations. Balancing authorities must demonstrate ad']
  forecast_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Resource adequacy assessments evaluate capacity sufficiency for a geographic area. The zone table defines forecast zones with peak load, installed capacity, and renewable penetration. Adding forecast_']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.irp_scenario.irp_scenario_id — Resource adequacy assessments may be performed for specific IRP scenarios. FK enables scenario-specific adequacy evaluation.']
  resource_capacity_requirement_id varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.capacity_requirement.capacity_requirement_id — Resource adequacy assessments evaluate compliance against capacity requirements. FK is required for NERC reliability compliance tracking.']
  resource_ra_against_capacity_req varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.capacity_requirement.capacity_requirement_id — Resource adequacy assessments evaluate compliance against capacity requirement targets. FK enables compliance status determination.']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Resource adequacy assessments are owned by specific organizational units (resource adequacy department, planning department) for NERC and PUC compliance. Required for regulatory accountability, depart']
  superseded_by_assessment_resource_adequacy_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the newer resource adequacy assessment that supersedes this assessment. Used to maintain assessment version history and lineage. Null if this is the current active assessment.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or role who approved this resource adequacy assessment for internal use or regulatory submission. Typically a senior executive or planning director.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this resource adequacy assessment was formally approved by the designated authority for internal use or regulatory submission.']
  assessment_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resource adequacy assessment was completed and finalized for internal approval or regulatory submission.']
  assessment_identifier varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier or reference number for this resource adequacy assessment, used in regulatory filings and communications with NERC, RTO, and state PUC.']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the resource adequacy assessment: deterministic (fixed reserve margin approach), probabilistic (LOLE/EUE Monte Carlo simulation), hybrid (combination of both methods). Determines calculation approach and reliability metrics.']
  assessment_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning period covered by this resource adequacy assessment. Defines the conclusion of the forecast horizon for capacity evaluation.']
  assessment_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning period covered by this resource adequacy assessment. Defines the beginning of the forecast horizon for capacity evaluation.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the resource adequacy assessment: draft (in preparation), under_review (internal or regulatory review), approved (finalized internally), filed (submitted to regulatory bodies), superseded (replaced by newer assessment).']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type or category of resource adequacy assessment: seasonal (summer/winter peak), annual (full year), long-term (multi-year IRP), or special (event-driven or ad-hoc).']
  assessment_uses_peak_demand varchar [note: 'FK to forecast.peak_demand_forecast.peak_demand_forecast_id — RA assessments use peak demand forecasts as the demand-side input to reserve margin calculations.']
  available_generation_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total available generation capacity in megawatts (MW) from all owned and contracted generation resources, accounting for planned outages, derates, and seasonal availability.']
  capacity_market_purchases_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Generation capacity in megawatts (MW) procured through capacity markets, bilateral contracts, or power purchase agreements (PPA) to meet resource adequacy requirements. Supplements owned generation.']
  capacity_surplus_deficit_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net capacity surplus (positive value) or deficit (negative value) in megawatts (MW), calculated as the difference between available capacity and the capacity required to meet peak demand plus required reserves. Indicates whether additional capacity procurement or retirement is needed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the resource adequacy assessment against NERC, RTO, and state PUC reserve margin and reliability standards: compliant (meets all requirements), non_compliant (fails to meet one or more requirements), conditional (meets requirements under specific assumptions), pending_review (awaiting regulatory determination).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this resource adequacy assessment record. Audit trail for accountability and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this resource adequacy assessment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  demand_response_capacity_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total demand response capacity in megawatts (MW) available for peak load reduction, including interruptible load programs, direct load control, and voluntary curtailment programs.']
  der_penetration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of peak demand served by Distributed Energy Resources (DER) including rooftop solar, battery storage, and behind-the-meter generation. Impacts net load forecast and capacity credit calculations.']
  economic_scenario varchar [note: 'Economic growth scenario assumed in the load forecast for this resource adequacy assessment: base (expected growth), high_growth (accelerated economic expansion), low_growth (slower expansion), recession (economic contraction). Impacts demand forecast and capacity requirements.']
  electric_vehicle_load_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Incremental peak demand in megawatts (MW) from electric vehicle (EV) charging load forecasted for the assessment period. Increases total capacity requirement.']
  energy_efficiency_adjustment_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Peak demand reduction in megawatts (MW) attributed to energy efficiency programs and measures implemented during the assessment period. Reduces net capacity requirement.']
  expected_unserved_energy_mwh_per_year decimal(12,3) [note: 'Expected Unserved Energy (EUE) metric expressed in megawatt-hours per year (MWh/year), representing the expected amount of energy demand that cannot be served due to generation capacity shortfalls. Complements LOLE by quantifying the magnitude of potential shortfalls.']
  filed_with_nerc_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this resource adequacy assessment has been filed with NERC as part of mandatory reliability reporting requirements. True if filed, False if not filed.']
  filed_with_puc_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this resource adequacy assessment has been filed with the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) as part of integrated resource planning or rate case proceedings. True if filed, False if not filed.']
  forced_outage_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average forced outage rate percentage assumed for the generation fleet in this assessment, representing the probability that a generating unit is unavailable due to unplanned outages. Used in probabilistic reliability calculations.']
  forecasted_peak_demand_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Forecasted peak demand in megawatts (MW) for the assessment period, representing the highest expected load the utility must serve. Critical input for capacity adequacy calculations.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this resource adequacy assessment record. Audit trail for accountability and compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this resource adequacy assessment record was last updated or modified. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  loss_of_load_expectation_days_per_year decimal(8,4) [note: 'Loss of Load Expectation (LOLE) metric expressed in days per year, representing the expected number of days per year when available generation capacity is insufficient to meet demand. Industry standard reliability target is typically 0.1 days/year (one day in ten years).']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the resource adequacy model, simulation tool, or analytical framework used to perform this assessment. Enables traceability and reproducibility.']
  nerc_filing_date date [note: 'Date when this resource adequacy assessment was officially filed with NERC for compliance and reliability reporting purposes. Null if not yet filed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes, comments, or explanatory information about this resource adequacy assessment, including assumptions, limitations, special conditions, or regulatory feedback. Supports documentation and audit trail.']
  peak_demand_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Forecasted date and time when the peak demand is expected to occur during the assessment period. Used for operational planning and dispatch scheduling.']
  planned_capacity_additions_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total planned generation capacity additions in megawatts (MW) expected to come online during the assessment period, including new builds, repowering projects, and capacity purchases. Increases available capacity.']
  planned_capacity_retirements_mw decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total planned generation capacity retirements in megawatts (MW) expected during the assessment period, including plant closures, decommissioning, and end-of-life retirements. Reduces available capacity.']
  planning_reserve_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planning reserve margin expressed as a percentage, representing the amount of generation capacity above forecasted peak demand required to ensure reliability. Calculated as ((Available Capacity - Peak Demand) / Peak Demand) * 100. Regulatory requirement varies by RTO and state PUC.']
  puc_filing_date date [note: 'Date when this resource adequacy assessment was officially filed with the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) as part of regulatory proceedings. Null if not yet filed.']
  renewable_penetration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total generation capacity from renewable energy sources (wind, solar, hydro, biomass) in the resource mix for this assessment period. Impacts capacity factor assumptions and intermittency risk.']
  required_reserve_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum planning reserve margin percentage mandated by NERC, RTO, or state PUC for this assessment period. Benchmark against which actual reserve margin is evaluated for compliance.']
  rto_iso_region varchar [note: 'Regional Transmission Organization (RTO) or Independent System Operator (ISO) region for which this resource adequacy assessment applies (e.g., PJM, MISO, CAISO, ERCOT, SPP, NYISO, ISO-NE). Determines applicable reliability standards and reserve margin requirements.']
  weather_scenario varchar [note: 'Weather scenario or climate condition assumed in the resource adequacy assessment: normal (typical weather patterns), extreme_heat (high cooling demand), extreme_cold (high heating demand), drought (reduced hydro availability), high_wind (increased wind generation), low_wind (reduced wind generation). Critical for stress-testing capacity adequacy.']

  Note: 'Master entity capturing periodic resource adequacy assessments evaluating whether the utility has sufficient generation and demand response capacity to meet forecasted peak demand with required reserve margins. Stores assessment period, planning reserve margin (%), loss-of-load expectation (LOLE in days/year), expected unserved energy (EUE in MWh/year), capacity surplus/deficit (MW), and compliance status against NERC and RTO standards. Filed with NERC and state PUC.'
}

Table forecast.scenario_fuel_price {
  scenario_fuel_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this scenario-specific fuel price assignment. Primary key.']
  fuel_price_assumption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fuel price assumption record being applied to this scenario']
  irp_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the IRP scenario to which this fuel price assumption is applied']
  assigned_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the planner or analyst who assigned this fuel price assumption to the scenario. Supports audit trail and accountability for scenario configuration.']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fuel price assumption was assigned to the scenario. Supports audit trail and version control for scenario configuration.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining the rationale for this specific fuel price assignment or any scenario-specific adjustments made. Captures planning judgment and scenario design decisions.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this fuel price assignment within the scenario. Tracks whether this assignment is currently active in scenario modeling or has been superseded by updated assumptions.']
  escalation_rate_override_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Scenario-specific override for the annual escalation rate, expressed as a percentage. Enables testing different fuel price escalation trajectories across scenarios while preserving the base assumption.']
  scenario_effective_end_date date [note: 'End date when this fuel price assumption ceases to be effective within the context of this specific scenario. Supports scenario-specific temporal application of fuel price libraries.']
  scenario_effective_start_date date [note: 'Start date when this fuel price assumption becomes effective within the context of this specific scenario. Allows different scenarios to apply the same base assumption over different time periods.']
  scenario_price_override decimal(12,4) [note: 'Scenario-specific override price that replaces the base fuel price assumption for this particular scenario. Allows planners to test scenario-specific fuel price variations without modifying the master assumption record.']
  sensitivity_case_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this fuel price assignment represents a sensitivity case (high/low price variant) rather than the base case for the scenario. Supports sensitivity analysis and risk assessment.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of fuel price assumptions to IRP scenarios with scenario-specific overrides and effective periods. It captures the operational practice of applying reusable fuel price assumption libraries across multiple planning scenarios while allowing scenario-specific adjustments. Each record links one fuel_price_assumption to one irp_scenario with effective dates, price overrides, escalation adjustments, and sensitivity flags that exist only in the context of this scenario-specific application.. Existence Justification: In utility IRP planning, fuel price assumptions are maintained as a reusable library that planners actively assign to multiple scenarios with scenario-specific overrides, effective periods, and sensitivity variants. A single fuel price assumption (e.g., \'EIA AEO 2024 Reference Case Natural Gas\') is applied across multiple scenarios (Base, High Growth, Low Carbon), and each scenario uses multiple fuel price assumptions (natural gas, coal, uranium) with scenario-specific adjustments. The business explicitly manages these assignments as operational records, tracking which assumptions are active in which scenarios, with what overrides, and over what time periods.'
}

Table forecast.planning_period {
  planning_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planning_period']
  previous_planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on planning_period (previous_planning_period_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the planning period record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, assumptions, or special notes for the period.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the planning period.']
  forecast_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days ahead for which forecasts are generated within this period.']
  horizon_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days covered by the planning period.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is the default selection for new forecasts.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that performed the latest update.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the planning period (e.g., "2024 Summer Peak").']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or operational notes attached to the planning period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Category of the planning period indicating its planning horizon or scenario purpose.']
  planning_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Short code representing the planning cycle (e.g., "FY24", "Q2-2024").']
  planning_horizon_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the horizon follows calendar, fiscal, or operational definitions.']
  planning_horizon_years varchar [note: 'Number of years spanned by the planning period (useful for long‑term scenarios).']
  planning_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1‑12) of the planning period.']
  planning_quarter varchar [note: 'Quarter of the year for the planning period.']
  planning_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal or calendar year to which the planning period belongs.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the planning period.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planning period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the planning period record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for planning_period. Referenced by planning_period_id.'
}

Table forecast.pricing_node {
  pricing_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pricing_node']
  parent_pricing_node_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pricing_node (parent_pricing_node_id)']
  capacity_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum generation or consumption capacity represented by the node in megawatts.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned unique code that identifies the pricing node across systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the pricing node record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, or special characteristics of the node.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount applied to the base price, expressed as a percentage.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the pricing node becomes effective for pricing calculations.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the pricing node ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this node is the default pricing node for its hierarchy level.']
  market varchar [note: 'Market segment to which the pricing node belongs.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing node used in reports and dashboards.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the node within the pricing hierarchy.']
  price_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base price associated with the node expressed in the specified currency.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price amount.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data (e.g., market feed, contractual agreement, internal model).']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the price (e.g., USD per megawatt‑hour).']
  pricing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the price at this node.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Logical region identifier for the node; used for regional reporting.']
  settlement_period varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the pricing node settles transactions.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pricing node.']
  tax_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes are already included in the price amount.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tax rate applied to the price when tax_included is false, expressed as a percentage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the pricing node record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pricing_node. Referenced by pricing_node_id.'
}

Table forecast.weather_station {
  weather_station_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for weather_station']
  reference_weather_station_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on weather_station (reference_weather_station_id)']
  accuracy_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the station\'s measurement accuracy.']
  connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Network technology used to transmit data to the central system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing overall data quality for the station.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that provides raw sensor data.']
  data_update_frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'Interval in minutes between successive data pushes from the station.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the station was permanently retired or removed from service.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing location specifics, installation remarks, or operational considerations.']
  elevation_m double [note: 'Station elevation in meters above mean sea level.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the weather station was installed and became operational.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date the station\'s sensors were last calibrated.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the station in decimal degrees (WGS84).']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the station in decimal degrees (WGS84).']
  maintenance_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the maintenance contact.']
  maintenance_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or team responsible for station upkeep.']
  maintenance_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the maintenance contact.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that manufactured the weather station hardware.']
  measurement_capabilities varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of measurement types the station can capture.']
  measurement_units varchar [note: 'Standard units used for the station\'s measurements (e.g., °C, m/s, %).']
  model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the weather station equipment.']
  next_calibration_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next calibration activity.']
  owner_organization varchar [note: 'Business unit or subsidiary that owns the weather station.']
  power_source varchar [note: 'Primary energy source powering the station equipment.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the station\'s compliance with NERC, FERC, or other applicable regulations.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer.']
  station_code varchar [note: 'External business code or identifier assigned by the utility or regulatory body.']
  station_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the weather station used in reports and dashboards.']
  station_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the station based on deployment and data collection method.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the station within its lifecycle.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the station location (e.g., America/New_York).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for weather_station. Referenced by weather_station_id.'
}
